PT P
PN JP2022067388-A
TI Recovering carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide supply source by adsorbing carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide supply source into carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid and feeding to absorption liquid storage tank.
AU TAKAOKA H
   HIRAKI M
AE COSMO ENG KK (MAZN-C)
GA 202265863A
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide supply source is recovered by supplying gaseous carbon dioxide supply source containing carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid in carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid storage tank to absorption and regeneration tower (11), bringing gas-like carbon dioxide supply source (1) into contact with carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid (4) in absorption and regeneration tower, adsorbing carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide supply source into carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid to obtain a carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid (3), feeding carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid to a carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid storage tank (13) to be stored, supplying carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid in carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid storage tank in the absorption and regeneration tower, heating carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid, and desorbing carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid.
   USE - Recovery of carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide supply source.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide can be recovered by small number of reaction towers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide supply source is recovered by supplying gaseous carbon dioxide supply source containing carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid (4) in carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid storage tank to absorption and regeneration tower (11), bringing gas-like carbon dioxide supply source (1) into contact with carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid in absorption and regeneration tower, adsorbing carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide supply source into carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid to obtain a carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid (3), feeding carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid to a carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid storage tank to be stored, supplying carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid in carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid storage tank (13) in the absorption and regeneration tower, heating carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid, desorbing carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid, and obtaining carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid and carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid. The carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid and the carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid are composed of an absorbent liquid containing an absorbent. The absorption process and regeneration process are alternately repeated. The carbon dioxide concentration in gaseous carbon dioxide supply source is less than or equal to 50 vol.% and the pressure of gaseous carbon dioxide supply source is 90-300 kPa.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide recovery device for recovering carbon dioxide in a gaseous carbon dioxide supply source.
   Gas-like carbon dioxide supply source (1)
   Carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid (3)
   Carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid (4)
   Absorption and regeneration tower (11)
   Carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid storage tank (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent is an amine-based compound. Preferred Process: In the absorption step, in absorption-cum-regeneration tower, the gaseous carbon dioxide supply source is made to flow in the upward flow, and carbon dioxide-lean absorption liquid is made to flow in the downward flow. The carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid is brought into contact with gas-like carbon dioxide supply source in a counterflow. In the regeneration process, the carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid accumulated at the bottom of the absorption-cum-regeneration tower is heated, and the upward flow of the vapor of absorption liquid of the saturation temperature is generated in the absorption-cum-regeneration tower; and the absorption-cum-regeneration tower is heated. The absorption process and regeneration process are repeated by automatic control system. Preferred Parameters: The absorption rate of the carbon dioxide in the absorption step is 25-95%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E10-B03B2; E11-Q01B; E31-N05C; J01-E02B
IP B01D-053/14; B01D-053/18; B01D-053/62; C01B-032/50

PN CN114292748-A
TI Carbon dioxide control structure for carbon dioxide incubator, comprises carbon dioxide incubator, which includes box body and carbon dioxide inlet interface, and carbon dioxide inlet nozzle and carbon dioxide outlet nozzle are installed at inlet and outlet end of solenoid valve.
AU LI X
   GUAN C
   WANG Y
   LIU Q
   JIANG X

   ZHAO G
   QIU C
AE BINGSHAN SONGYANG BIOTECHNOLOGY DALIAN (BING-Non-standard)
GA 202254160P
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide control structure comprises carbon dioxide incubator, solenoid valve, carbon dioxide inlet nozzle, carbon dioxide outlet nozzle, controller and carbon dioxide concentration sensor. Carbon dioxide incubator includes a box body and a carbon dioxide inlet interface, and a carbon dioxide inlet nozzle (2) and a carbon dioxide outlet nozzle (3) are installed at the inlet end and the outlet end of the solenoid valve, respectively. The carbon dioxide concentration sensor is arranged in the box, and it is used to detect the carbon dioxide concentration inside the box. The controller collects the carbon dioxide concentration information in the incubator fed back by the carbon dioxide concentration sensor and controls the opening and closing time of the solenoid valve according to the algorithm.
   USE - Carbon dioxide control structure for carbon dioxide incubator.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide control structure solves the problem of carbon dioxide gas control precision in a carbon dioxide incubator and controls the carbon dioxide gas control precision of the carbon dioxide incubator at the set value, and has no overshoot and undershoot.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an assembly view of an electromagnetic valve and a nozzle.
   Electromagnetic valve (1)
   Inlet nozzle (2)
   Outlet nozzle (3)

PT P
PN CN112607738-A
TI Carbon dioxide purifying comprises placing carbon dioxide separating gas, adding organic solvent, removing sulfide in carbon dioxide, performing catalytic combustion, increasing pressure, and injecting carbon dioxide.
AU SHEN J
   DAI R
AE CHANGJIANG PLASTICIZING CO LTD ZHANGJIAG (ZJCJ-C)
GA 2021383922
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide purifying comprises (i) placing carbon dioxide separating gas in an exchange tank, adding organic solvent in the exchange tank, absorbing carbon dioxide, separating the carbon dioxide, (ii) carbon dioxide chemical purifying A, removing sulfide in carbon dioxide, adding the carbon dioxide obtained in the step (i) in the desulfurizing tower, (ii) performing catalytic combustion by placing carbon dioxide in the dealkylation tower, catalytically oxidizing combustible gas component in carbon dioxide to carbon dioxide and hydrogen dioxide, (iii) taking physical purified-chemical purified carbon dioxide in the variable pressure cylinder, ensuring the variable pressure cylinder into adsorbent, increasing the pressure in the variable pressure cylinder, realizing multiple separation of mixed gas in carbon dioxide and multiple use of the adsorbent, and injecting carbon dioxide into the sealing tank for storage.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon dioxide purifying.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: completes the first purification treatment of carbon dioxide; ensures pressure swing adsorption purification of carbon dioxide; improves the overall purity of carbon dioxide; is economical; ensures purity of carbon dioxide reaches more than 5 N; fully meet the laser gas preparation, electronics industry, reactor gas coolant fields; and has good market prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide purifying comprises (i) placing carbon dioxide separating gas in an exchange tank, adding organic solvent in the exchange tank, using the pressure and temperature between the alternating carbon dioxide and organic solvent, absorbing carbon dioxide, separating the carbon dioxide, adding the separated carbon dioxide into an absorption tower, reacting with the carbon dioxide in the absorption tower by chemical solvent, promoting the carbon dioxide into the solvent, introducing the formed pregnant solution into the absorption tower, heating and decomposing, absorbing and removing and absorbing to obtain the separated carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide gas passes through the film material to obtain the final separated carbon dioxide, (ii) carbon dioxide chemical purifying A, removing sulfide in carbon dioxide, adding the carbon dioxide obtained in the step (i) in the desulfurizing tower, hydrolyzing the COS to hydrogen sulfide under the catalytic action of the desulfurizing agent, taking the hydrogen sulfide in the carbon dioxide and the hydrogen sulfide generated by hydrolyzing and oxygen generate elemental sulfur, absorbing and removing the active carbon in the desulfurizing tower, (ii) performing catalytic combustion by placing carbon dioxide in the dealkylation tower, catalytically oxidizing combustible gas component in carbon dioxide to carbon dioxide and hydrogen dioxide, removing the total hydrocarbon in carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen when catalytic combustion, where the carbon dioxide catalytic reaction is 2H2+O2, 2H2O2CO+O2, 2CO2CH4+2O2, CO2+2H2O purified carbon dioxide into the drying tower for drying treatment, using 3 A molecular sieve to remove water, removing the impurities of carbon, water, oxygen, and nitrogen, firstly removing the nitrogen impurity in the carbon dioxide by distillation device, then carbon dioxide in the deoxidizing tower, using noble metal efficient deoxidizing catalyst to remove the rest oxygen, finishing the chemical purifying process of carbon dioxide, (iii) taking physical purified-chemical purified carbon dioxide in the variable pressure cylinder, ensuring the variable pressure cylinder into adsorbent, increasing the pressure in the variable pressure cylinder, and the mixed gas in the carbon dioxide, then reducing the pressure in the variable pressure cylinder, performing adsorbent regeneration, continuously increasing pressure in the variable pressure cylinder, separating the mixed gas in the carbon dioxide, repeating the steps, continuously pressurizing and reducing pressure to the variable pressure cylinder, realizing multiple separation of mixed gas in carbon dioxide and multiple use of the adsorbent, and injecting carbon dioxide into the sealing tank for storage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The film material comprises polyamide film, polysulfone film, cellulose acetate film or polyether sulfone film.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC J04-E01; L03-F02A1; A12-W11F

PT P
PN WO2019161420-A1
TI Improving performance characteristics of systems that utilize direct air capture of carbon dioxide comprises e.g. directing a flow of carbon dioxide-carrying air through a carbon dioxide capture contactor structure having channels.
AU EISENBERGER P
AE EISENBERGER P (EISE-Individual)
GA 201973271B
AB    NOVELTY - Improving performance characteristics of systems that utilize direct air capture of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-carrying air, comprises: directing a flow of carbon dioxide-carrying air through a carbon dioxide capture contactor structure having channels, where the contactor structure supports sorbent capable of binding carbon dioxide, to remove carbon dioxide from the air by binding carbon dioxide to the sorbent, causing the carbon dioxide capture contactor structure to be exposed to regeneration, where substantially saturated steam of a predetermined temperature is directed at the carbon dioxide bound to the sorbent, thus facilitating separation of the carbon dioxide from the sorbent and regenerating the sorbent, withdrawing the carbon dioxide that has been unbound from the sorbent together, and selectively then after again exposing the carbon dioxide capture structure to a flow of carbon dioxide-carrying air.
   USE - The method is useful for improving performance characteristics of systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The method optimizes and improves cost and performance of direct air carbon dioxide capture systems to lessen the threat of catastrophic climate change and optimizes lower cost direct air carbon dioxide capture system that removes carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving performance characteristics of systems that utilize direct air capture of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-carrying air, comprises: directing a flow of carbon dioxide-carrying air through a carbon dioxide capture contactor structure having channels, where the contactor structure supports sorbent capable of binding carbon dioxide, to remove carbon dioxide from the air by binding carbon dioxide to the sorbent, causing the carbon dioxide capture contactor structure to be exposed to regeneration, where substantially saturated steam of a predetermined temperature is directed at the carbon dioxide bound to the sorbent, thus facilitating separation of the carbon dioxide from the sorbent and regenerating the sorbent, withdrawing the carbon dioxide that has been unbound from the sorbent together, and selectively then after again exposing the carbon dioxide capture structure to a flow of carbon dioxide-carrying air, thus enabling the regenerated sorbent to be again used to bind carbon dioxide, to remove carbon dioxide from the newer flow of carbon dioxide-carrying air, the carbon dioxide capture contactor structure being formed with parallel channels of a predetermined geometry, utilizing the predetermined geometry to cause areas of laminar substantially non-turbulent flow of the carbon dioxide-carrying air, exhibiting characteristics where the proportionality of pressure drop with respect to the velocity of portions of carbon dioxide-carrying air moving through the channels is substantially linear as the velocity is increased. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cyclically removing carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-carrying ambient air, comprising steps as above per se where the saturated steam of a temperature is not greater than 120 degrees C is directed at the carbon dioxide bound to the sorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: As one increases the velocity to capture more CO2 per year it is best to decrease the cycle time for capture and regeneration to one half their previous values, to 15 minutes and 1.5 minutes, and higher velocities are used one is able to reduce those times further or when low cost heat is available one is able to increase TPY by going to even shorter times for both at 5 m/sec. Preferred Components: The channels are substantially parallel. The contactor material includes at least one of extruded ceramic, corrugated fiberglass sheets, corrugated metal sheets, extruded alumina, structured carbon and engineered plastics. The incoming velocity is increased to 5 m/sec while being dominated by relatively lower resistance laminar flow for a size S of 2 millimeters, and the wall thickness of the contactor W is relatively small W/S less than 1/4 (higher ratios raise the pressure drop), and where W is the size of the sum of the contactor substrate thickness and a coating, the substrate being of a predetermined thin dimension. The tradeoff between wall thickness and opening size S, with better performance with smaller S and thus smaller W, results in a higher surface area to volume SAv which will yield a higher carbon dioxide capture efficiency, where S and W yield 100-150 cpsi is best, where for smaller W the kinetics are improved, thus improving the overall process.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E11-Q02C; E31-N04D; E31-N05C; E34-C02; J01-D; J01-E03; J04-X; A12-W11; A12-W11F

PN KR2019063159-A; KR1997357-B1
TI Carbon dioxide processing system for industrial facilities, comprises a blower for supplying indoor air containing carbon dioxide to the absorber and an absorber absorbs carbon dioxide by the carbon dioxide absorbent.
AU PARK I G
   PARK Y S
   SIK P Y
AE PYEONG HWA ENG & CONSULTING CO LTD (PYEO-Non-standard)
GA 2019513222
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide processing system comprises a blower (100) for supplying indoor air containing carbon dioxide to the absorber. An absorber absorbs carbon dioxide by the carbon dioxide absorbent. An absorbent discharge line (10) supplies the absorbent absorbed with carbon dioxide to the desorbent portion. A desorbing unit separates the carbon dioxide absorbed by the absorbing unit into carbon dioxide. A carbon dioxide rejection unit (300) receives and stores the carbon dioxide separated from the desorbing unit. An absorbent recovery line (20) returns the absorbent separated from the desorbing unit to the absorbing unit. A carbon dioxide concentration detector (500) senses the concentration of carbon dioxide in real time. A controller (700) receives the signal from the carbon dioxide concentration detector and controls the operation of the air purifier (600).
   USE - Carbon dioxide processing system for industrial facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide removal efficiency is high and the carbon dioxide inevitably ejected with eutectic phase can be removed without the inflow of the outside, so the optimum production condition can be maintained.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of carbon dioxide processing system for industrial facilities.
   Absorbent discharge line (10)
   Absorbent recovery line (20)
   Blower (100)
   Carbon dioxide rejection unit (300)
   Carbon dioxide concentration detector (500)
   Air purifier (600)
   Controller (700)

PN US2019367878-A1; US11136550-B2
TI Purifying contaminated carbon dioxide from contaminated bone tissue involves collecting contaminated carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide treated contaminated bone tissue, and separating contaminants from collected contaminated carbon dioxide.
AU DECARO M
   WEI G
AE WARSAW ORTHOPEDIC INC (MEDT-C)
GA 2019A2373L
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying a contaminated carbon dioxide from a contaminated bone tissue involves collecting a contaminated carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide treated contaminated bone tissue, and separating contaminants from collected contaminated carbon dioxide to obtain a purified carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for purifying a contaminated carbon dioxide from a contaminated bone tissue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to purify a contaminated carbon dioxide from a contaminated bone tissue, which obtains purified carbon dioxide that is recycled to treat contaminated bone tissue, and retains original mineral content of bone tissue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for treating contaminated bone tissue with purified carbon dioxide, which comprises a bone tissue chamber which is configured for holding contaminated bone tissue and receiving purified carbon dioxide into bone tissue chamber and evacuating contaminated carbon dioxide from the bone tissue chamber, a purified carbon dioxide supply which is configured for supplying purified carbon dioxide to bone tissue chamber to decontaminate the bone tissue, a collection chamber which is configured to receive contaminated carbon dioxide from bone tissue chamber and evacuate contaminated carbon dioxide from collection chamber, and a purification chamber which is configured to receive contaminated carbon dioxide from collection chamber and to remove contaminants from contaminated carbon dioxide by a purification material to obtain purified carbon dioxide, where purification chamber is configured to evacuate purified carbon dioxide and supply it to bone tissue chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The contaminated carbon dioxide is separated from contaminants by bubbling contaminated carbon dioxide through water and/or an organic solvent to remove contaminants comprising lipids, disease causing pathogens, viruses or bacteria. The method further involves treating carbon dioxide bubbled through water and/or organic solvent with acid treatment, filtering and liquefying the carbon dioxide. The liquefied carbon dioxide is purified to 99.9% free of lipids, disease causing pathogens, viruses or bacteria. The method involves removing contaminated carbon dioxide, purifying contaminated carbon dioxide with a supercritical fluid to form a purified carbon dioxide, liquefying purified carbon dioxide to form a liquid purified carbon dioxide and using liquid purified carbon dioxide as a cooling fluid. The method involves treating contaminated bone tissue with an ethanol gradient dehydration. The method further involves flushing ethanol gradient dehydration treated contaminated bone tissue with a liquid carbon dioxide, subjecting contaminated bone tissue to critical point dehydration and/or supercritical carbon dioxide treatment, and releasing the carbon dioxide at a controlled rate. The temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide is raised to or above 31.1 degrees C and 1100 psi. The supercritical carbon dioxide treatment is carried out at 105 degrees C and 7000 psi. The method further involves generating processing charts including vanishing interfacial tension and carbon dioxide pressure. The bone tissue comprises bone fibers, bone chips, bone particles, bone matrices or its mixtures. The method further involves providing a delivery vehicle for bone tissue having contaminants, where delivery vehicle comprises a carrier or covering. The method of decontaminating the bone tissue is a multiple batch process.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN113432272-A
TI Method for regulating indoor carbon dioxide            concentration in air conditioner, involves determining            that time for collecting carbon dioxide is greater than            or equal to specific preset time threshold, and            discharging collected carbon dioxide to outside.
AU ZHANG F
   CHEN G
   WANG Q
AE HISENSE SHANDONG AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (HISG-C)
GA 2021B4790C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting (S1) indoor                carbon dioxide concentration. The concentration of                the indoor carbon dioxide reaches a preset                concentration threshold value is determined (S2),                and the carbon dioxide in the indoor air is                collected. The time of carbon dioxide collection is                recorded (S3). The time for collecting the carbon                dioxide is greater than or equal to a first preset                time threshold is determined (S4), and the                collected carbon dioxide is discharged to the                outside. The carbon dioxide collecting module for                collecting carbon dioxide is heated to release the                collected carbon dioxide and discharge the released                carbon dioxide to the outside. The time of carbon                dioxide release is recorded.
   USE - Method for regulating indoor carbon dioxide                concentration in air conditioner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces the indoor                carbon dioxide concentration, so that the indoor                air quality reaches the comfortable index of a                user. The carbon dioxide collection module is                controlled to discharge the collected carbon                dioxide to the outdoor, so that the purpose of                reducing the concentration of the indoor carbon                dioxide is achieved, and the problem that the                content of the carbon dioxide still rises due to                the closing of an indoor space is avoided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an apparatus for regulating carbon dioxide                concentration in a room; and
   (2) an air conditioner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                method for regulating indoor carbon dioxide                concentration in air conditioner. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   S1Step for collecting indoor carbon dioxide                concentration
   S2Step for determining the concentration of                the indoor carbon dioxide reaches a preset                concentration threshold value, and collecting the                carbon dioxide in the indoor air
   S3Step for recording the time of carbon                dioxide collection
   S4Step for determining that the time for                collecting the carbon dioxide is greater than or                equal to a first preset time threshold, and                discharging the collected carbon dioxide to the                outside

PN US2018106775-A1; US10145831-B2
TI Method for detecting recent human presence in space, involves measuring concentration of carbon dioxide by using carbon dioxide sensor within space to obtain carbon dioxide measurement, and then measuring another concentration.
AU ANGELI M
   DURST J
   LIDDIL G
   DISTASIO M M
AE SRC INC (SRCS-Non-standard)
GA 201829940N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring (12) concentration of carbon dioxide by using a carbon dioxide sensor within space to obtain a carbon dioxide measurement, and then measuring (14) another concentration of carbon dioxide within the space after a period of time elapses to obtain another carbon dioxide measurement. The carbon dioxide rate is calculated (16) for decay by using the former and the latter carbon dioxide measurements.
   USE - Method for detecting recent human presence in a space.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves measuring concentration of carbon dioxide by using a carbon dioxide sensor within space to obtain a carbon dioxide measurement, and thus enables to reduce energy consumption and costs or to improve the quality of the air within the space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves measuring (12) concentration of carbon dioxide by using a carbon dioxide sensor within space to obtain a carbon dioxide measurement, and then measuring (14) another concentration of carbon dioxide within the space after a period of time elapses to obtain another carbon dioxide measurement. The carbon dioxide rate is calculated (16) for decay by using the former and the latter carbon dioxide measurements. The time is extrapolated (20) by using the calculated rate of decay at which the concentration of carbon dioxide is approximately equivalent to a carbon dioxide concentration of interest. The carbon dioxide rate is correlated (22) for decay to a level of human presence in the space at the extrapolated time. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for detecting recent human presence in a space; and
   (2) a computer program stored on a computer useable storage medium, where the computer program is configured to implement the method for detecting recent human presence in a space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of the human detection method.
   Measuring carbon dioxide concentration (12)
   Measuring another carbon dioxide concentration (14)
   Calculating carbon dioxide rate (16)
   Inputting carbon dioxide concentration (18)
   Extrapolating time (20)
   Correlating carbon dioxide rate (22)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The space is a room. The carbon dioxide concentration of interest is predetermined by a user. The carbon dioxide sensor is an infrared gas sensor or a chemical gas sensor.

PT P
PN US2020338498-A1; US11207643-B2
TI Removing carbon dioxide from atmosphere and generating hydrogen by capturing carbon dioxide from atmosphere in alkaline capture solution, sending solution to series of electrolyzers in carbon dioxide-rich path, and removing carbon dioxide.
AU TORRES F E
   BEH E S M
   BAKER R J L
AE PALO ALTO RES CENT INC (PRCA-C)
GA 2020A5854R
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide from atmosphere and generating hydrogen, involves (a) capturing carbon dioxide from the atmosphere in an alkaline capture solution, (b) sending the capture solution to a series of electrolyzers in a carbon dioxide-rich path, where each electrolyzer cell raises the acidity of the input carbon dioxide-rich solution to produce an acidified carbon dioxide-rich solution, (c) removing carbon dioxide from the acidified carbon dioxide-rich solution at a carbon removal unit to produce a carbon dioxide-poor solution, (d) sending the poor solution to the series of electrolyzers in a return path, where each electrolyzer raises the alkalinity of the return carbon dioxide-poor solution to produce a basified carbon dioxide-poor solution, and a pH difference between the carbon dioxide-rich solution and carbon dioxide-poor solution within each electrolyzer is less than 3, and (f) returning the basified carbon dioxide-poor solution to the carbon dioxide capture unit operation.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon dioxide from atmosphere and generating hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and has high efficiencies and relatively low, or even negative emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves (i) mixing an output of at least one electrolyzer in a mixing vessel to allow more time for reactions before flowing the solution into an input of a next electrolyzer in the series, (ii) capturing oxygen from the electrolyzers in the carbon dioxide-rich path combined with at least one of minimizing or reabsorbing entrained carbon dioxide, (iii) controlling the pressures in the electrolyzers to be higher than a partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide-rich output solution, (iv) lowering pressure in the carbon dioxide removal unit operation to drive carbon dioxide out of the carbon dioxide-rich solution, (v) adding a salt that is not redox active to the alkaline capture stream, (vi) using the carbon dioxide removed by the carbon removal unit and hydrogen produced by at least one of the electrolyzers to synthesize liquid fuels, (vii) powering the electrolyzers using renewable electricity, (viii) completing a circuit by conducting at least one of potassium or sodium ions through a cation exchange membrane in at least one of the electrolyzers, (ix) sending the carbon dioxide-poor solution to the series of electrolyzers in the return path and sending the alkaline capture solution to the series of electrolyzers in the carbon dioxide-rich path by sending the solutions into the electrolyzers as countercurrent flows, (x) limiting bubbling of carbon dioxide from the solution anywhere but in the carbon dioxide removal unit, (xi) reducing pressure to degas the carbon dioxide and to drive electricity generation in tandem with reducing the pressure, (xii) sequestering at least some of the captured carbon dioxide, (xiii) venting the acidified stream under pressure to remove oxygen, (xiv) using at least part of the acidified stream for combustion of oxygen and carbon dioxide, and (xv) transferring heat from the carbon capture unit to the electrolyzers. Preferred Components: The base solution comprises potassium hydroxide, potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and/or sodium bicarbonate. The electrolyzers use catalysts comprising earth-abundant materials. Preferred Conditions: The pH of the system is 4-14.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN CN113307269-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide by purifying liquid carbon dioxide comprises transferring liquid carbon dioxide to high-purity liquid carbon dioxide purification device, subjecting to purification testing, pressurizing and filling in vacuum bottle.
AU LU X
   LIU G
   DENG X
AE YUNNAN ANFENG GAS CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A3873D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide by purifying liquid carbon dioxide comprises (i) transferring 99.5% liquid carbon dioxide in the cryogenic liquid storage tank to the high-purity liquid carbon dioxide purification device for the first purification, introducing purified carbon dioxide to the vaporizer for gasification, introducing vaporized carbon dioxide to the purifier for the secondary purification, and providing gas outlet pipe of the high-purity liquid carbon dioxide purification device with a gas-permeable membrane including 50-70 pts. wt. aromatic polyamide solution, 30-50 pts. wt. melamine derivatives, 1-2 pts. wt. nano-graphene, and 3-5 pts. wt. nano-silver; (ii) subjecting carbon dioxide to purification testing and testing, after passing the inspection, introducing purified carbon dioxide to the diaphragm compressor, pressurizing at 0-15 Mpa, and filling into a vacuum bottle; and (iii) testing carbon dioxide in the vacuum bottle, and qualifying and storing in the warehouse.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide by purifying liquid carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can meet the market's demand for carbon dioxide and realizes the requirement for the purity of carbon dioxide, so the purity of carbon dioxide reaches 99.999%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the preparation of carbon dioxide by purifying liquid carbon dioxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stirring paddle in the purification tank of the high-purity liquid carbon dioxide purification device is a magnetic stirring paddle. The purifier is a carbon dioxide purification device. The aromatic polyamide solution includes 50 mol.% 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether, 25 mol.% p-phenylenediamine, 25 mol.% o-dianisidine and dehydrated N-methyl-2 pyrrolidone and 100 mol.% isophthaloyl chloride and lithium carbonate. The melamine derivative includes 58 pts. wt. formaldehyde, 40 pts. wt. melamine, 29 pts. wt. ethylenediamine and pH regulator (proper amount). Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the purification detection test is performed after the carbon dioxide treated by the purifier is allowed to stand for 8 hours, and the purification detection test is performed; and the step is carried out in a vacuum bottle and allowed to stand for more than 8 hours before proceeding to step (iii). The diaphragm compressor is pressurized from 0-15 Mpa within 6 hours. In the step (iii), the test of the carbon dioxide filled in the vacuum bottle is to use a carbon dioxide analyzer to perform the test, and the carbon dioxide contained in the vacuum bottle is tested, and it reaches the standard of 99.999%.

PN CN114307513-A
TI Device useful for collecting and fixing escape            carbon dioxide in sewage treatment plant, comprises            carbon dioxide vacuum collecting system, carbon dioxide            separating and purifying system, carbon dioxide fixing            system, and carbon dioxide recycling system.
AU GAO X
   XING F
   MU Y
   ZHANG Z
   LI S
   DUAN L
   ZHANG H
AE CHINESE RES ACAD ENVIRONMENTAL SCI (CRHK-C)
GA 202256023W
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a carbon dioxide vacuum                collecting system, carbon dioxide separating and                purifying system, carbon dioxide fixing system,                carbon dioxide recycling system and product                collecting system, collecting the mixed gas                escaping from biochemical processing unit through                carbon dioxide vacuum collecting system, the mixed                gas enters the condenser through the connecting                pipe of the carbon dioxide vacuum capturing system,                the liquid carbon dioxide and the remaining mixed                gas formed by condensing are separated by the                gas-liquid separator, the separated liquid carbon                dioxide enters the evaporator to recover the gas,                the carbon dioxide separating and purifying system                enters the carbon dioxide fixing system through                soft pipe, generating the residual gas through the                carbon dioxide recycling system for recycling, the                organic matter generated by reaction and rest                biochemical sewage are stored and discharged                through the product collecting system set below the                carbon dioxide fixing system.
   USE - The device is useful for collecting and fixing                escape carbon dioxide in sewage treatment plant in                power-assisted sewage treatment industry                pollution-reducing plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes the fixation of carbon                dioxide, captures the carbon dioxide in the                biochemical treatment process, and generates                valuable chemical, and directly reduces the sewage                treatment industry carbon dioxide emission,                power-assisted sewage treatment industry                pollution-reducing and carbon-reducing target,                reduces the influence of the carbon dioxide gas                directly discharged by sewage treatment plant to                the greenhouse effect, generates value-added                chemicals, and uses the waste gas and biochemical                sewage in the treatment plant as raw material and                capable of quickly collecting and fixing carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                capturing and fixing carbon dioxide by using the                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural diagram of the                device for collecting and fixing escape carbon                dioxide.

PN US2018230366-A1; WO2018148617-A1; US10066155-B1; CA3053352-A1; CN110291174-A; EP3580298-A1; CN110291174-B
TI Viscosifying proppant used in hydraulic fracturing            fluid comprises proppant particles coated with carbon            dioxide-philic coating comprising carbon dioxide-philic            material e.g. partially fluorinated oxygen-containing            polymers.
AU LIANG F
   AL-MUNTASHERI G A
   LI L
   REDDY B R
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201863429V
AB    NOVELTY - Viscosifying proppant (140) comprises proppant                particles (110) coated with a carbon dioxide-philic                coating (120) comprising a carbon dioxide-philic                material consisting of cellulose acetates,                partially fluorinated oxygen-containing polymers,                oxygenated polymers, and/or crosslinked                oxygen-containing polystyrenes, where when the                viscosifying proppant is added to a carbon                dioxide-based fluid comprising at least 30 wt.%                carbon dioxide, a solvable portion of the carbon                dioxide-philic coating dissociates from the                proppant particles, associates with the carbon                dioxide in the carbon dioxide-based fluid, and                sufficiently viscosifies the carbon dioxide-based                fluid to cause the proppant particles to suspend in                the carbon dioxide-based fluid, and when the                viscosifying proppant is added to a fluid                comprising at least 30 wt.% water, the carbon                dioxide-philic coating does not dissociate from the                proppant particles.
   USE - The viscosifying proppant is used in hydraulic                fracturing fluid for treating subterranean                formations.
   ADVANTAGE - The viscosifying proppant: when added to                carbon dioxide-based fluid comprising carbon                dioxide, a solvable portion of the carbon                dioxide-philic coating dissociates from the                proppant particles, associates with the carbon                dioxide in the carbon dioxide-based fluid, and                sufficiently viscosifies the carbon dioxide-based                fluid to cause the proppant particles to suspend in                the carbon dioxide-based fluid, and when added to a                fluid comprising water, the carbon dioxide-philic                coating does not dissociate from the proppant                particles; and facilitates the subterranean                formations treatment in a simple and economical                manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of an                uncoated proppant and a viscosifying                proppant.
   Proppant particles (110)
   Carbon dioxide-philic coating (120)
   Carbon dioxide-based coating (121)
   Viscosifying proppant (140)
   Contacting step (320)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the viscosifying                  proppant, greater than or equal to 90 wt.% of the                  carbon dioxide-philic coating is not crosslinked.                  The proppant particles have a rough surface                  having an arithmetic average roughness (Ra) of                  greater than or equal to 1 nm. The proppant                  particles are particles of a material comprising                  oxides, silicates, sand, ceramic, resin, plastic,                  mineral, glass, graded sand, treated sand,                  resin-coated sand, other resin-coated particles,                  bauxite, sintered bauxite, glass particles and/or                  glass beads. The carbon dioxide-based fluid is a                  liquid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: When the viscosifying                  proppant is added to a fluid comprising at least                  75 wt.% water, the carbon dioxide-philic coating                  does not dissociate from the proppant particles.                  When the viscosifying proppant is added to a                  carbon dioxide-based fluid consisting of at least                  45 or 90 wt.% carbon dioxide and optionally at                  least one of nitrogen, dimethyl ether or                  hydrocarbons, the solvable portion of the carbon                  dioxide-philic coating dissociates from the                  proppant particles, associates with the carbon                  dioxide in the carbon dioxide-based fluid, and                  sufficiently viscosifies the carbon dioxide-based                  fluid to cause the proppant particles to suspend                  in the carbon dioxide-based fluid. When the                  viscosifying proppant is added to a carbon                  dioxide-based fluid consisting of carbon dioxide,                  the solvable portion of the carbon dioxide-philic                  coating dissociates from the proppant particles,                  associates with the carbon dioxide in the carbon                  dioxide-based fluid, and sufficiently viscosifies                  the carbon dioxide-based fluid to cause the                  proppant particles to suspend in the carbon                  dioxide-based fluid. When the viscosifying                  proppant is added to a carbon dioxide-based fluid                  consisting of at least 51 wt.% or 90 wt.%                  supercritical carbon dioxide and optionally at                  least one of nitrogen, dimethyl ether, or                  hydrocarbons, the solvable portion of the carbon                  dioxide-philic coating dissociates from the                  proppant particles, associates with the carbon                  dioxide in the carbon dioxide-based fluid, and                  sufficiently viscosifies the carbon dioxide-based                  fluid to cause the proppant particles to suspend                  in the carbon dioxide-based fluid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon                  dioxide-philic material comprises partially                  fluorinated oxygen-containing polymers of a                  monomer comprising                  2-(N-ethylperfluorooctane-sulfonamido)ethyl                  acrylate,                  2-(N-ethylperfluorooctane-sulfonamido)ethyl                  methacrylate,                  2-(N-methylperfluorooctane-sulfonamido) ethyl                  acrylate, 2-(N-methylperfluorooctane-sulfonamido)                  ethyl methacrylate, 1,1-dihydroperfluorooctyl                  acrylate, 1,1-dihydroperfluorooctyl methacrylate,                  1,1,2,2-tetrahydroperfluoroalkylacrylates,                  1,1,2,2-tetrahydroperfluoroalkyl-methacrylates,                  hexafluoropropylene oxide, perfluorocyclohexane                  oxide, perfluoro(propyl vinyl ether), or                  perfluoro(methyl vinyl ether).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114626637-A
TI Method for predicting and planning use of carbon            dioxide emissions, involves counting actual absorption            amount of carbon dioxide consumption system, and            judging whether actual absorption amount of carbon            dioxide consumption system satisfies regional carbon            dioxide emission planning target.
AU ZENG Y
   LI D
   SHI K
   CHEN X
AE UNIV HUNAN TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 202282447Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a carbon                dioxide statistical area and a regional carbon                dioxide emission planning target. Discharge amount                of carbon dioxide is predicted according to                regional economic data. Collected carbon dioxide                amount and a regional distribution set pattern in                the carbon dioxide statistical area are determined.                A carbon dioxide consumption system in the carbon                dioxide statistical area is established. Actual                absorption amount of the carbon dioxide consumption                system is counted. Judgment is made to check                whether actual absorption amount of the carbon                dioxide consumption system satisfies the regional                carbon dioxide emission planning target. The carbon                dioxide consumption system is connected to a carbon                dioxide emission unit to discharge.
   USE - Method for predicting and planning use of                carbon dioxide emissions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables establishing the carbon                dioxide consumption system, so that the later                consumption of carbon dioxide can be performed in                real time, thus improving economic data, and hence                reducing utility cost and satisfying economic                efficiency requirement in an effective manner. The                method enables widely popularizing the consumption                system with prolonged service life, so as to                satisfy requirement of carbon neutralization.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating a                method for predicting and planning use of carbon                dioxide emissions. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

PT P
PN US2018345207-A1; WO2018226368-A1; US10583387-B2
TI Removing carbon dioxide from gaseous source involves comprising contacting gaseous source with aqueous solution of carbon dioxide sorbent and then with carbon dioxide complexing compound, and removing precipitated salt.
AU CUSTELCEAN R
   WILLIAMS N J
   SEIPP C A
AE UT-BATTELLE LLC (UTBA-C)
   UT-BATTELLE LLC (UTBA-C)
GA 201896683R
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide from a gaseous source, comprises (i) contacting the gaseous source with an aqueous solution of a carbon dioxide sorbent that reacts with carbon dioxide to form an aqueous-soluble carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide sorbent, (ii) contacting the aqueous solution from step (i) with a carbon dioxide complexing compound, which is different from the carbon dioxide sorbent, to result in precipitation of a carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound and regeneration of the carbon dioxide sorbent and (iii) removing the precipitated carbonate or bicarbonate salt from the aqueous solution in step (ii) to result in a solid form of the carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound, where the carbon dioxide complexing compound (I), and the carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound (II).
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon dioxide from a gaseous source.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the sorbent that: has fast carbon dioxide absorption rates and thus quickly removes carbon dioxide from gaseous source, is easy to handle and has low maintenance with solid-state carbon dioxide desorption that avoids much of the energy penalty associated with heating and evaporating aqueous solutions, and minimizes sorbent degradation; and is environmentally friendly. Since the renewable energy source is solar energy, the method is energy efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing carbon dioxide from a gaseous source, comprises (i) contacting the gaseous source with an aqueous solution of a carbon dioxide sorbent that reacts with carbon dioxide to form an aqueous-soluble carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide sorbent, (ii) contacting the aqueous solution from step (i) with a carbon dioxide complexing compound, which is different from the carbon dioxide sorbent, to result in precipitation of a carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound and regeneration of the carbon dioxide sorbent and (iii) removing the precipitated carbonate or bicarbonate salt from the aqueous solution in step (ii) to result in a solid form of the carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound, where the carbon dioxide complexing compound of formula (I), and the carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound formula (II).
   A1 = ring-containing moiety or a bond;
   X1(m-) = carbonate or bicarbonate anion, where the m is 1 for bicarbonate and 2 for carbonate; and
   n = 0.5, 1 or 2.
   Provided that that nx m is 2 in the case of (I), and nx m is 1 in the case of (III).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide sorbent comprises organoamines, alkali hydroxides, alkali carbonates or alkaline earth hydroxides. The carbon dioxide sorbent is an organoamine, where the organoamine is an amino acid. The amino acid comprises glycine and N-alkylglycines, where alkyl comprises hydrocarbon groups containing 1-6 carbon atoms. The method further comprises (iv) regenerating the carbon dioxide complexing compound by subjecting the carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound to heat, vacuum or both, sufficient to result in release of carbon dioxide gas emanating from the carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound along with simultaneous restoration of the carbon dioxide complexing compound, where the released carbon dioxide gas is either quarantined for storage and/or converted into a useful product. The heat is provided by a renewable energy source. The renewable energy source is solar energy, where the solar energy is a direct solar heat concentrator. The carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide complexing compound is heated to a temperature of 30-250, (preferably 40-160) degrees C. The gaseous source is contacted intimately with the aqueous solution of a carbon dioxide sorbent by use of an air-liquid contact facilitator, where the air-liquid contact facilitator increases surface area of the aqueous solution to increase the air-liquid contact area between the gaseous source and the aqueous solution. The air-liquid contact facilitator is a humidifying device that contains a reservoir of the aqueous solution containing the carbon dioxide sorbent, and where the humidifying device increases the surface area of the aqueous solution by wicking the aqueous solution or forming a mist of the aqueous solution. The carbon dioxide released from the regenerating step (iv) is converted to a useful chemical substance or feedstock.
   Preferred Definitions:
   A1 = monocyclic ring which is a 5, 6, or 7-membered ring, carbocyclic ring which unsaturated, heterocyclyl which is a unsaturated and contains at least one N ring atom, where the A1 comprises benzene ring or pyridine ring.

The first emission error covariances, the carbon                dioxide emissions and the error covariances of the                preset atmospheric transport model are input (S130)                into the Bayesian inversion model. The first                emission error covariance corresponding to the                first carbon dioxide emission with the smallest                difference is determined (S140) from the carbon                dioxide simulated emission as the optimal emission                error covariance. The carbon dioxide monitoring                data and carbon dioxide background data of the                target region within a specified period of time are                input (S150) into the Bayesian inversion model by                combining the carbon dioxide emission, the optimal                emission error covariance, and the error covariance                of the preset atmospheric transport model. The                carbon dioxide emissions of the target region                within the specified period of time are obtained by                inversion.

   ADVANTAGE - The method makes the inversion result more                accurate by reducing the influence of the emission                error covariance.

   Step for determining the simulated carbon                dioxide emissions (S120)

   Step for determining the first emission error                covariance corresponding to the first carbon                dioxide emission with the smallest difference                (S140)
   Step for inputting the carbon dioxide                monitoring data and carbon dioxide background data                of the target region within a specified period of                time (S150)

TI Composition for recovering carbon dioxide from exhaust gas, comprises ionic liquid having preset melting point in carbon-dioxide-absorbed state, lower than melting point or decomposition temperature in carbon-dioxide-desorbed state.
AU UMEDA K
AE OSAKA GAS CO LTD (OSAG-C)
   OSAKA GAS CO LTD (OSAG-C)
GA 201845179S
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises an ionic liquid having a melting point of 50-110 degrees C in carbon-dioxide-absorbed state. The melting point or decomposition temperature of the ionic liquid in carbon-dioxide-desorbed state is higher than the carbon-dioxide-absorbed state melting point.
   USE - Composition is used for recovering carbon dioxide (claimed) from exhaust gas of engines, factories, power stations, boilers, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic liquid releases the heat of solidification, thereby improving the thermal absorption accompanying carbon-dioxide desorption and recovering carbon dioxide in terms of heat balance. The composition can be used conveniently for recovery of carbon dioxide from process gas of temperature higher than 100 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for recovering carbon dioxide, which involves contacting a process gas comprising carbon dioxide with the composition, to absorb carbon dioxide into the ionic liquid, at a temperature higher than the carbon-dioxide-absorbed state melting point.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of the temperature swing adsorption apparatus for recovering carbon dioxide using the composition.
   Temperature swing adsorption apparatus (1)
   Vapor supply line (4)
   Process-gas supply line (5)
   Carbon-dioxide recovery line (6)
   Blower (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ionic liquid comprises tetraethylammonium benzimidazolide, tetraethylammonium imidazolide or tetrabutylammonium carbazolide. Preferred Process: The method involves regenerating the composition by heating to desorb carbon dioxide after the contacting step, at a temperature higher than the contacting step temperature and lower than the melting point and decomposition temperature of the ionic liquid.

PN WO2020043833-A1; CA3109876-A1; AU2019332892-A1; CN112638502-A; IN202117007800-A; BR112021003608-A2; EP3843879-A1; US2021339188-A1; AU2019332892-B2
TI Capturing carbon dioxide from gas stream involves            providing carbon dioxide-containing gas stream,            contacting gas stream as provide in adsorption zone            with solid adsorbent particle, obtaining carbon            dioxide-enriched solid adsorbent particles.
AU BHALODI A K
   VAN PAASEN S
   INFANTINO M
   GRIGORIADOU A
   LEENDERS S H A
   LEENDERS S H A M
   PAASEN S V
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 202019379L
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide from gas stream                involves providing carbon dioxide-containing gas                stream, contacting the gas stream as provided in                adsorption zone with solid adsorbent particles,                obtaining carbon dioxide-enriched solid adsorbent                particles. The adsorption zone has two beds of                fluidized solid adsorbent particles. The solid                adsorbent particles are flowing downwards from bed                to bed and gas stream is flowing upwards. The                carbon dioxide-enriched solid adsorbent particles                are passed from the bottom of the adsorption zone                to the bottom of first desorption zone. The portion                of carbon dioxide is removed from carbon                dioxide-enriched solid adsorbent particles in the                first desorption zone. The carbon dioxide-depleted                solid adsorbent particles as obtained are passed                via riser to a second desorption zone. The carbon                dioxide (CO2) is removed from carbon                dioxide-depleted solid adsorbent particles in                second desorption zone.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from gas                stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to capture carbon dioxide                from gas stream that reduces cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing carbon dioxide from gas stream                involves providing carbon dioxide-containing gas                stream, contacting the gas stream as provided in                adsorption zone with solid adsorbent particles,                obtaining carbon dioxide-enriched solid adsorbent                particles. The adsorption zone has two beds of                fluidized solid adsorbent particles. The solid                adsorbent particles are flowing downwards from bed                to bed and gas stream is flowing upwards. The                carbon dioxide-enriched solid adsorbent particles                are passed from the bottom of the adsorption zone                to the bottom of first desorption zone. The portion                of carbon dioxide is removed from carbon                dioxide-enriched solid adsorbent particles in the                first desorption zone. The carbon dioxide-depleted                solid adsorbent particles as obtained are passed                via riser to a second desorption zone. The carbon                dioxide (CO2) is removed from carbon                dioxide-depleted solid adsorbent particles in                second desorption zone. The second desorption zone                has two beds of fluidized solid adsorbent                particles. The solid adsorbent particles are                flowing downwards from bed to bed and stripping gas                is flowing upwards. The regenerated solid adsorbent                particles as obtained are recycled to the                adsorption zone. The second desorption zone is                located above the adsorption zone. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for a apparatus for capturing                carbon dioxide from gas stream, which comprises                adsorption zone for contacting carbon                dioxide-containing gas stream with solid adsorbent                particles, and adsorption zone has two beds of                fluidized solid adsorbent particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The adsorption zone                  comprises two or more adsorption vessels, each                  adsorption vessel containing two beds of                  fluidized solid adsorbent particles and each                  adsorption vessel defining separate flow path for                  portion of the solid adsorbent particles and                  portion of the gas stream. The first desorption                  zone is located below the adsorption zone. The                  carbon dioxide-depleted solid adsorbent particles                  as passed via riser are separated in a gas/solids                  separator before entering the second desorption                  zone. The solids-enriched stream is passed to the                  second desorption zone. The portion of the                  gas-enriched stream obtained in the gas/solids                  separator is used as riser gas in the riser. The                  water is added to the regenerated solid adsorbent                  particles that are being recycled to the                  adsorption zone, before the regenerated solid                  adsorbent particles enter the adsorption                  zone.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2021252158-A1; US2021381758-A1
TI Method for use in utilizing ocean depths to separate carbon dioxide from gas mixture, involves liquefying carbon dioxide as result of depth in ocean to form liquid carbon dioxide which is not carbon dioxide dissolved in water.
AU DODSON J B
   MCCLURE R F
AE CARBON VAULT LLC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON VAULT LLC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021E3642K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves flowing a first mixture of gasses to a depth in an ocean, where the first mixture has carbon dioxide and other gasses (102). The carbon dioxide is liquefied as a result of the depth in the ocean to form liquid carbon dioxide (104), which is not carbon dioxide dissolved in water. The liquid carbon dioxide is separated from the other gasses (106) and a second mixture is flowed of other gasses upward to the ocean surface (108). The other gasses has products from combusting hydrocarbon fuel or molecular nitrogen. The liquid carbon dioxide is contacted with water in the ocean without dissolving the liquid carbon dioxide in the water.
   USE - Method for use in utilizing ocean depths to separate carbon dioxide from a gas mixture for use in industrial processes that combust hydrocarbons producing carbon dioxide as a byproduct.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid carbon dioxide is inert and can be discharged into the ocean at depths having sufficient pressures without dissolving in seawater and acidifying the ocean.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a system for separating carbon dioxide from other gases; and
   (2) a method of separating carbon dioxide from a gas mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method.
   Flowing a first mixture of gasses to a depth in an ocean, where the first mixture has carbon dioxide and other gases (102)
   Liquefying the carbon dioxide as a result of the depth in the ocean to form liquid carbon dioxide (104)
   Separating the liquid carbon dioxide from the other gasses (106)
   Flowing a second mixture of other gasses upward to the ocean surface (108)

PT P
PN CN215049786-U
TI System for co-producing carbon dioxide product by urea device, has guide mechanism for extracting portion of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide compressor set to satisfy liquefied pressure requirement into carbon dioxide liquefying system.
AU JI D
   LI M
AE LI M (LIMM-Individual)
GA 2022030703
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a system for co-producing carbon dioxide product by urea device, the system comprises a first guide mechanism, for guiding all carbon dioxide generated in the synthetic ammonia system to the carbon dioxide compressor set contained in the urea device; a second guide mechanism for extracting a portion of the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide compressor set to meet the requirement of liquefied pressure into the carbon dioxide liquefaction system. The system provided by the invention, using the carbon dioxide compressor set of urea device itself to pressurize the carbon dioxide formed by the synthetic ammonia process, the pressure of the carbon dioxide can reach the liquefied pressure requirement; in the process of liquefying the carbon dioxide, there is no need to provide the pressurizing device again. At the same time, the system does not need to introduce expensive electric machine driven volume type compressor, which can reduce the investment of the device, further reduce the production cost.

AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2022C2815X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a carbon dioxide                storage container filled with high concentration                salt water in seawater with depth not less than                3000 meters. Liquid carbon dioxide is injected into                the carbon dioxide storage container from a liquid                inlet pipe of the carbon dioxide storage container                through a liquid carbon dioxide conveying pipeline.                The high concentration of the salt water is                discharged from the liquid discharging pipe. The                liquid carbon dioxide is overflowed to a carbon                dioxide storing container. The carbon dioxide in                the drainage pipe and the seawater contact for                generating carbon dioxide hydrate to block the                liquid in-let pipe and a liquid discharge pipe of a                carbon dioxide storage container. A drain pipe is                located in a top unit of the carbon dioxide storing                container, where the total mass of the high                concentration water is 100% and the mass                concentration of salt in the high-concentration                water is 20%.
   USE - Method for carbon dioxide sequecbonding for                use in seabed carbon sealing environment-friendly                field. Can also be used in land seal and ocean                seal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables can effectively realize                large-scale storage and long-term isolation of the                seabed carbon dioxide. It can realize that the                liquid carbon dioxide is filled with storage                container to generate carbon dioxide hydrate to                effectively prevent diffusion of liquid carbon                monoxide in the storage container.

TI Carbon dioxide absorbent used for for carbon dioxide absorption and regenerating an absorbent, comprises 1,5-diazabicyclo (4.3.0) non-5-ene and alcohol.
AU LIU Y
   LU H
   LIU C
   WU K
   ZHU Y
   LIANG B
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2020242519
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprises 1,5-diazabicyclo (4.3.0) non-5-ene and alcohol.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbent used for for carbon dioxide absorption and regenerating an absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent has fast absorption rate, large circulation capacity, low regeneration energy consumption and low recycling loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for carbon dioxide absorption, which involves bring gas containing carbon dioxide into contact with any carbon dioxide absorbent; and
   (2) a method for regenerating an absorbent, which involves regenerating carbon dioxide-rich absorbent by heating, depressurization, flash evaporation, and steam stripping.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The alcohol is 1-5C alcohols. Preferred Conditions: The mass fraction of the 1,5-diazabicyclo (4.3.0) non-5-ene is 50-85%. The temperature during the contacting is 20-50 degrees C, and the pressure is 1-2 bar. The reaction time is 0.5-2 hours.

PN US2019060785-A1; CA2977305-A1; US10286336-B2; CA2977305-C
TI Method for extracting surface resident components from plant material in carbon dioxide extraction plant, involves transferring component-impregnated liquid-phase carbon dioxide into collection vessel with higher available volume.
AU DURWARD J
AE CRITICAL CO2 LTD (CRIT-Non-standard)
   CRITICAL CO2 LTD (CRIT-Non-standard)
   MEDXTRACTOR CORP (MEDX-Non-standard)
   CRITICAL CO2 LTD (CRIT-Non-standard)
GA 2019207378
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating an extraction vessel (10) to achieve supercritical-phase carbon dioxide at desired predetermined pressure and temperature. The supercritical-phase carbon dioxide is allowed to reside in the vessel at a predetermined temperature and pressure for a time sufficient to ensure impregnation of components (29) in the supercritical-phase carbon dioxide. The component-impregnated supercritical-phase carbon dioxide is cooled such that the component-impregnated supercritical-phase carbon dioxide in the vessel changes to component-impregnated liquid-phase carbon dioxide. The component-impregnated liquid-phase carbon dioxide is transferred into a collection vessel (24) at pressure lower than that of the vessel or into the collection vessel with higher available volume to decrease density of the liquid-phase carbon dioxide to convert the liquid-phase into gaseous-phase carbon dioxide to allow the components to be separated from the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for extracting components i.e. surface resident components, from a plant material in a carbon dioxide extraction plant using supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing simplified and cost effective way for controlling phase and density of extraction solvent during extraction of desired components from the plant material, while using the supercritical carbon dioxide. The method enables providing extraction of surface resident components from the plant materials using carbon dioxide effectively in absence of mechanical pumps by manipulation of density of carbon dioxide in the extraction vessel via creating a temperature differential between the liquid carbon dioxide supply and the extraction vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide extraction plant for extracting components from a plant material.
   Pressurized tank (2)
   Extraction vessel (10)
   Fluid bath (14)
   Collection line (20)
   Relief valve (22)
   Collection vessel (24)
   Components (29)

TI Agricultural greenhouse environment carbon dioxide            control system, has main control chip connected with            electromagnetic valve and carbon dioxide sensor for            controlling opening or closing of valve according to            concentration value detected by sensor.
AU ZHOU X
   LIN J
   LV Z
   ZHANG R
   WANG S
AE UNIV SHANDONG HUAYU TECHNOLOGY (USDH-C)
GA 2020B5645F
AB    NOVELTY - The invention claims an agricultural                greenhouse environment carbon dioxide control                system. The system comprises: The invention claims                a carbon dioxide gas storage steel bottle,                comprising an air outlet pipe for releasing the                carbon dioxide; a pressure reducer, connected with                the air outlet pipe of the carbon dioxide storage                steel bottle through a first pipeline, for reducing                the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas released by                the carbon dioxide storage steel bottle; an                electromagnetic valve arranged in the greenhouse                and connected with the first pipeline; a carbon                dioxide sensor for detecting the concentration                value of the carbon dioxide in the greenhouse; a                main control chip, respectively connected with the                electromagnetic valve and the carbon dioxide                sensor, for controlling the opening or closing of                the electromagnetic valve according to the                concentration value detected by the carbon dioxide                sensor, so as to control the concentration of the                carbon dioxide released to the greenhouse. The                invention accurately controls the carbon dioxide                release amount, ensures the carbon dioxide raw                material needed by greenhouse vegetable                photosynthesis, improves greenhouse greenhouse crop                yield; the gas release has good safety and is green                and environment-friendly.

AU CHEN M

   GAO S

   CHEN Z
   FU H
   QI N
AE GREAT WALL MOTORS CO LTD (GRWA-C)
GA 2020A4828M
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide monitoring unit outputting a monitoring signal that is matched with a first carbon dioxide concentration threshold value to act to execute alarm. An air conditioner control unit is connected with a control unit and the carbon dioxide monitoring unit, and outputs an engine starting signal output by the control unit. The carbon dioxide monitoring unit outputs the monitoring signal that is matched with a second carbon dioxide concentration threshold value, and controls external circulation mode opening operation of a vehicle air conditioner.
   USE - Carriage air conditioner carbon dioxide concentration control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system outputs a corresponding monitoring signal to the alarm unit and the air conditioner control unit by matching the monitored carbon dioxide concentration to the first carbon dioxide concentration threshold value and the second carbon dioxide concentration threshold value so as to fully exert double effect of manual control and automatic control and improve control efficiency of carbon dioxide concentration in the carriage.
