PN KR2019080804-A; KR2147432-B1
TI Method for measuring spatially resolved carbon dioxide flux using inelastic light detection and ranging (LIDAR), involves estimating carbon dioxide concentration of ground surface through linear regression.
AU YUN S T
   LEE H
   KIM D W
   YUN S
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV PUKYONG NAT (UYPN-C)
GA 201964059H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring the carbon dioxide concentration distribution in the horizontal direction using inelastic ladder, measuring the carbon dioxide concentration at the altitude while moving the inelastic ladder in a vertical direction on the ground, and estimating the carbon dioxide concentration of the ground surface through linear regression. The carbon dioxide flux is calculated through the estimated carbon dioxide concentration. The surface air velocity, surface temperature, surface air pressure, and surface humidity are measured. The estimated carbon dioxide concentration and the measured data are expressed by the following equations to calculate the carbon dioxide flux.
   USE - Method for measuring spatially resolved carbon dioxide flux using inelastic light detection and ranging (LIDAR).
   ADVANTAGE - A wide range of carbon dioxide flux is produced through telemetry using a single device, so that the carbon dioxide flux located on the optical path at a distance from the desired location can be calculated by a predetermined distance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide flux measurement system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the method for measuring spatially resolved carbon dioxide flux using inelastic LIDAR. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

Another path is connected with an inlet of a top portion of a vortex tube (4) through a cooler (3). An outlet of the vortex tube is divided into three paths for dividing medium-pressure Carbon dioxide fluid into three-ply low-pressure fluid. The path is connected with an inlet of the Carbon dioxide turbine after passing through a Carbon dioxide working substance pump (5) and a heater(6). PT P
PN WO2022165442-A1
TI Continuous carbon dioxide capture device comprises            carbon dioxide pump membrane comprising moisture-swing            material that absorbs and releases carbon dioxide when            dry and wet, and cavity comprising first fluid bound            and defined by pump membrane.
AU GREEN M
   LACKNER K
AE ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
GA 202298199U
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a carbon dioxide pump                membrane (102) comprising a moisture-swing material                (104) that absorbs carbon dioxide when dry and                releases carbon dioxide when wet. A cavity (106)                comprises a first fluid. The carbon dioxide pump                membrane separates a first fluid (108) from a                second fluid (110). A water concentration gradient                (116) within the moisture-swing material of the                carbon dioxide pump membrane creates a carbon                concentration gradient (122) across the carbon                dioxide pump membrane that decreases from a second                carbon concentration (120) to a first carbon                concentration (118) moving from outside the cavity                to inside the cavity. The water is continuously                transported from the first fluid to the second                fluid through the carbon dioxide pump membrane                because of the water concentration gradient, and                carbon dioxide is continuously captured from the                second fluid by the moisture-swing material of the                carbon dioxide pump membrane.
   USE - Continuous carbon dioxide capture                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The continuous carbon dioxide capture device                is simple, efficient, low-cost, and can be operated                in a batch process, gathering in one phase and                releasing in another. The device can reduce the                energy and financial cost, as well as the                mechanical complexity and fragility, to a practical                level. It can passively collect atmospheric carbon                dioxide from the ambient air using sorbent                materials that capture the carbon dioxide from the                atmosphere and subsequently release it for use or                sequestration. It does not require the use of                mechanisms to transition devices between a                collection phase and a release phase, and does not                provide numerous potential points of failure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a continuous carbon dioxide capture                system; and
   (2) a method for continuous carbon dioxide                capture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                continuous carbon dioxide capture device.
   Continuous carbon dioxide capture device                (100)
   Carbon dioxide pump membrane (102)
   Moisture-swing material (104)
   Cavity (106)
   First fluid (108)
   Second fluid (110)
   Water concentration gradient (116)
   First carbon concentration (118)
   Second carbon concentration (120)
   Carbon concentration gradient (122)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The moisture-swing material of the carbon                  dioxide pump membrane is a quaternary                  ammonium-functionalized polymer. The                  moisture-swing material of the carbon dioxide                  pump membrane is a quaternary                  ammonium-functionalized poly(arylene ether                  sulfone) copolymer. The moisture-swing material                  has a copolymerization unit based on diallyl                  bisphenal A (DABA).

PN CN113061475-A
TI Separating carbon dioxide from critical biogas            with adjustable carbon dioxide concentration by            liquefaction process comprises e.g. pre-treating            biogas, separating gas remaining after liquid carbon            dioxide pressurizing and purifying.
AU DOU M
   ZOU S
   LI H
   YANG J
   DENG F
   LI K
   HUANG F
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 202180358P
AB    NOVELTY - Separating carbon dioxide from critical biogas                with adjustable carbon dioxide concentration by                liquefaction process comprises (i) pre-treating                biogas, and pre-compressing to biogas pressure of                2.5 MPa, where the biogas pressure increases to 25                MPa when partial pressure of carbon dioxide in                biogas reaches 6.5 MPa and temperature is less than                or equal to 25 degrees C in critical state, cooling                biogas to less than 25 degrees C, adding carbon                dioxide into biogas to ensure the partial pressure                of carbon dioxide after pressurization reaches more                than 6.5 MPa when three-stage compressor is                pressurized but partial pressure of carbon dioxide                in biogas cannot be liquefied due to insufficient                partial pressure; (ii) introducing mixture of                liquid carbon dioxide and remaining gas in biogas                into gas-liquid separator to separate and                recovering liquid carbon dioxide; and (iii)                separating gas remaining after liquid carbon                dioxide and pressurizing to 25 MPa through methane                compressor, and purifying to obtain methane                product.
   USE - The method is useful for separating carbon                dioxide from critical biogas with adjustable carbon                dioxide concentration by liquefaction                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for large-scale                continuous production; and makes the content of                separated methane in the biogas to more than 98%,                purifies and enriches to a purity of more than 98%.                The device is simple and convenient to operate; and                saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                liquefying device for separating carbon dioxide                from critical biogas with adjustable carbon dioxide                concentration.

TI Treatment of biogas comprises passing biogas in            gaseous state through first membrane stage comprising            at least first separating membrane, where permeability            to methane of first separating membrane is greater than            carbon dioxide.
AU BRISSAUD M

   HALLIER F (HALL-Individual)
   BRISSAUD M (BRIS-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of biogas comprises passing biogas                in the gaseous state through a first membrane stage                comprising at least a first separating membrane,                where permeability to methane of first separating                membrane is greater than its permeability to carbon                dioxide, temperature and pressure conditions of                biogas is maintained to allow passage of methane                downstream of membrane, recovering gaseous mixture                enriched in carbon dioxide which accumulates                upstream of membrane, carrying out Joule-Thomson                expansion of carbon dioxide-enriched gas mixture to                obtain liquid carbon dioxide/methane gas mixture,                distilling liquid carbon dioxide/methane gas                mixture under temperature and pressure conditions                to obtain liquid carbon dioxide and recovering                liquid carbon dioxide, where carbon dioxide mixture                liquid carbon/methane gas to be distilled contains                at least 80%, remainder is predominantly methane                and in liquid carbon dioxide/methane gas to be                distilled that reaches a temperature greater than                or equal to -5 degrees C during distillation.
   USE - The method is useful for treatment of                biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, recovers the latent                heat from the separated carbon dioxide, and saves                energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treatment of biogas comprises passing biogas                in the gaseous state through a first membrane stage                comprising at least a first separating membrane,                where permeability to methane of first separating                membrane is greater than its permeability to carbon                dioxide, temperature and pressure conditions of                biogas is maintained to allow the passage of                methane downstream of membrane, recovering gaseous                mixture enriched in carbon dioxide which                accumulates upstream of membrane, carrying out                Joule-Thomson expansion of the carbon                dioxide-enriched gas mixture so as to obtain a                liquid carbon dioxide/methane gas mixture,                distilling liquid carbon dioxide/methane gas                mixture under temperature and pressure conditions                to obtain liquid carbon dioxide and recovering                liquid carbon dioxide, where carbon dioxide mixture                liquid carbon/methane gas to be distilled contains                at least 80%, preferably at least 90% mole of                carbon dioxide, remainder is predominantly methane                and in liquid carbon dioxide/methane gas to be                distilled that reaches a temperature greater than                or equal to -5 degrees C during distillation,                preferably at least 90% mole of carbon dioxide,                remainder is mainly methane and liquid carbon                dioxide/methane gas mixture to be distilled reaches                a temperature greater than or equal to -5 degrees C                during distillation, preferably at least 90% mole                of carbon dioxide, remainder being mainly methane                and liquid carbon dioxide/methane gas mixture to be                distilled reaches a temperature greater than or                equal to -5 degrees C during distillation. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for installation                for the treatment of biogas, comprising a source of                biogas. A methane separation unit is connected to                biogas source, which comprises at least a first                battery of separating membranes, which comprises at                least a first separating membrane whose                permeability to methane is greater than the                permeability to carbon dioxide. A distillation                column is configured for distillation of the                gaseous mixture enriched in accumulable carbon                dioxide upstream of the first membrane. A heat                exchanger is mounted upstream of a boiler of a                distillation column and a valve capable of                performing Joule-Thomson expansion so as to obtain                a liquid carbon dioxide/methane gas mixture. The                valve being mounted between distillation column and                boiler and downstream of the boiler on the pipe                which connects heat exchanger to distillation                column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The gaseous mixture                  enriched in carbon dioxide is cooled down to                  ambient temperature or a temperature below                  ambient temperature before carrying out the                  Joule-Thomson expansion. The gaseous mixture                  enriched in methane obtained is passed downstream                  of first separating membrane through a second                  membrane stage which comprises at least one                  second separating membrane whose permeability to                  methane is greater than the permeability to                  carbon dioxide, temperature and pressure                  conditions of gaseous mixture enriched in methane                  allows the passage of methane downstream of                  second membrane. The liquid carbon dioxide formed                  during distillation is used to produce cold and                  in particular to cool the biogas upstream of                  first membrane stage and/or second membrane stage                  is taken in combination. The liquid carbon                  dioxide is separated by gravity from the gaseous                  methane during the distillation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The heat exchanger                  makes it possible to cool biogas upstream of                  first battery of separating membranes with the                  liquid carbon dioxide present at the bottom of                  the distillation column.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of installation                for treatment of biogas that do not support the                claims.

PT P
PN CN109595878-A; CN109595878-B
TI Synthesizing ammonia, urea and liquid carbon dioxide comprises e.g. providing carbon dioxide, and subjecting raw material gas to pressure swing, temperature change adsorption dehydration and condensing to obtain liquid carbon dioxide.
AU QI S
   WANG Y
   GAO Z
   HUANG G
   WANG J
   WU Z
   SUN J
   CAO L
   BIAN T
AE INNER MONGOLIA BODA SHIDI CHEM CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
   INNER MONGOLIA BODA SHIDI CHEM CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 201934939N
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing ammonia, urea and liquid carbon dioxide comprises providing carbon dioxide which is desulfurized and dehydrogenated from the second stage outlet of the carbon dioxide compressor of the urea synthesis device as a co-production liquid carbon dioxide raw material gas, subjecting raw material gas to pressure swing, temperature change adsorption dehydration, and condensing to obtain liquid carbon dioxide product.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing ammonia, urea and liquid carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and environmentally friendly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of the synthesizing ammonia, urea and liquid carbon dioxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the temperature swing adsorption process, the regeneration gas is a synthetic ammonia flushing device, and molecular sieve regenerates the nitrogen gas, adsorbing moisture in the raw material gas which is desorbed by the hot regenerated nitrogen gas by adsorbent, and entering desorbed adsorbent in next round of adsorption, and venting nitrogen. In the temperature swing adsorption, the dew point of the raw material gas after dehydration is lower than -70 degrees C. In the condensation, condensing and liquefying carbon dioxide by liquid ammonia gasification using ammonia system refrigeration system. In the process, after the condensation separating gas and liquid, where liquid carbon dioxide meets the industrial carbon dioxide product standard and sending it to the carbon dioxide storage tank, venting non-condensable inert gas, and returning vaporized ammonia gas to the ammonia compressor of the ammonia plant. Preferred Components: The liquid carbon dioxide is co-produced by adding a carbon dioxide recovery process equipment and line on the basis of the existing synthetic ammonia and urea process lines, the carbon dioxide recovery process equipment comprises a pressure swing adsorber, a temperature change adsorber, a heat energy recovery device, a carbon dioxide liquefier, a carbon dioxide liquefier liquid level tank and a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The pressure swing adsorber and temperature change adsorber are connected to each other in the second stage outlet of the carbon dioxide compressor of the urea synthesis device. The outlet of the temperature change adsorber is connected to the feed gas inlet of the heat recovery device. The carbon dioxide gas outlet of the heat recovery unit is connected to the inlet of the carbon dioxide liquefier. The carbon dioxide liquefier outlet is connected to the carbon dioxide liquefier level tank and the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank in turn. The heat recovery unit and the ammonia unit refrigeration system forms a circuit, and the carbon dioxide liquefier and the ammonia unit refrigeration system forms a circuit. The heat energy recovery device is a heat exchanger for performing carbon dioxide cooling of the raw material gas and the non-condensable inert gas from the carbon dioxide liquefier to recover the cooling amount. The carbon dioxide liquefier is a U-tube heat exchanger which is provided with a gaseous carbon dioxide inlet, a liquid carbon dioxide outlet, a liquid ammonia inlet, and a gas ammonia export. The gaseous carbon dioxide inlet is connected to the carbon dioxide gas outlet of the heat recovery unit. The liquid carbon dioxide outlet is connected to the carbon dioxide liquid level tank. The liquefied carbon dioxide is introduced into the carbon dioxide liquefier for gas-liquid separation. The liquid ammonia inlet is connected to the ammonia cooler, and the gas ammonia outlet is connected with the ammonia cooler to recover the gas ammonia. The carbon dioxide liquefier is provided with a pressure gauge and a liquid level gauge interface to facilitate the operator to monitor the pressure of the equipment and the liquid level of the internal liquid at any time. The carbon dioxide liquefier liquid level tank is a vertical tank body, and the gas-liquid separation is performed by the gravity sedimentation principle.

TI Carbonazing minerals generated in air purification system of nuclear facilities, comprises injecting and maintaining high-pressure carbon dioxide in mixture of carbonation medium containing alkaline earth metal element and water are mixed.
AU HEE-CHUL E
   YANGHEECHUL
   KIMHYUNGJOO
   LEE K Y
   SEO B K
AE KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
GA 2020925608
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonazing minerals comprises (a) preparing a mixture in which carbonation medium containing alkaline earth metal element and water are mixed, and (b) injecting and maintaining high-pressure carbon dioxide into the mixture.
   USE - The method and system are useful for carbonazing minerals (all claimed) generated in air purification system of nuclear facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system treat carbon dioxide with high carbonation rate, and can be used in general treatment process through carbonation of carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for mineral carbonation device comprising a carbon dioxide gas compression system that compresses and stores carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide source, a burette for receiving carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide gas compression device and supplying the filled carbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide carbonation reactor and maintaining the pressure of the carbon dioxide carbonation reactor, and a carbon dioxide carbonation reactor (HP reactor) containing a mixture of a carbonation medium containing an alkaline earth metal element and water, and performing a carbonation reaction with carbon dioxide supplied from the burette.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the system for carbonazing minerals (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), pressure of high-pressure carbon dioxide is 10-55 bar; the high pressure is maintained until the carbonation reaction of the mixture ends; time point at which the reaction is terminated is the time point at which the temperature inside the reactor starts to drop; and initial temperature of injecting and maintaining high-pressure carbon dioxide into the mixture is 20-30 degrees C. Preferred Components: The carbonation medium is oxide or hydroxide of alkaline earth metal. The carbon dioxide is 14-carbon dioxide containing radioactive carbon C14. The solid-liquid ratio of the mixture is 0.18-0.22.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The burette is a temperature sensor and a pressure sensor. The carbon dioxide carbonation reactor comprises a stirrer, a temperature sensor and a pressure sensor. The mineral carbonation device further comprises a solid-liquid separation device for separating the product of the carbon dioxide carbonation reactor into carbonation products and aqueous solutions.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111013192-A; WO2021135149-A1; CN111013192-B
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method for pharmaceuticals, involves providing carbon dioxide as gas under normal temperature, and collecting gaseous carbon dioxide and returning to carbon dioxide storage tank for recycling.
AU HUANG X
   SHEN L
AE JIANGSU GAOKE PHARM EQUIP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU GAOKE PHARM EQUIP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202034085C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sending the carbon dioxide to an extraction tank (1) by a carbon dioxide storage tank (2). A medicine storage tank (3) sent a medicine liquid into the extraction tank. A pressurizer (4) pressurized the extraction tank. The carbon dioxide in the extraction tank reached the supercritical state with the temperature control device of the extraction tank. A supercritical carbon dioxide extracted the liquid medicine. A mixed liquid after extraction entered a separation tank (5) at normal temperature and pressure. The carbon dioxide is a gas under normal temperature and pressure conditions, which is easy to separate from the liquid medicine. The gaseous carbon dioxide is collected and returned to the carbon dioxide storage tank for recycling.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method for pharmaceuticals.
   ADVANTAGE - The recovery of gaseous carbon dioxide at normal temperature and pressure is realized. The extraction process and equipment composition are simplified and cost is reduced. The first shell is provided to sprinkle the medicine liquid. The mixing speed with supercritical carbon dioxide is accelerated, such that the extraction efficiency is improved. The separation tank is provided with wings to hit the falling mixture, such that the gas-liquid separation is completely separated. The reciprocating mechanism is provided to drive the two wings to incite back and forth, such that the impact is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method for pharmaceuticals.
   Extraction tank (1)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (2)
   Medicine storage tank (3)
   Pressurizer (4)
   Separation tank (5)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN107746039-A
TI Method for non-catalytic carbon dioxide reforming of methane to syngas, involves vacuumizing, supplying equimolar ratio of high purity methane and carbon dioxide to mixer, and pressurizing methane and carbon dioxide gas mixture.
AU NIE G
AE NIE G (NIEG-Individual)
GA 2018189322
AB    NOVELTY - The method for non-catalytic carbon dioxide reforming of methane to syngas, involves (A) vacuumizing, (B) opening all gas supply valves at bottom of mixer, supplying equimolar ratio of high purity methane and carbon dioxide to mixer and stirring, (C) pressurizing high purity methane and carbon dioxide gas mixture using high pressure pump, passing pressurized methane and carbon dioxide mixed gas to arc heater, (D) quickly heating high purity methane and carbon dioxide mixture at 1500-1900 degrees C and stirring, and (E) discharging mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
   USE - Method for non-catalytic carbon dioxide reforming of methane to syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables non-catalytic carbon dioxide reforming of methane to syngas with high purity by simple and economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for non-catalytic carbon dioxide reforming of methane to syngas, involves (A) closing all gas supply valves at the bottom of mixer and vacuumizing, (B) opening all gas supply valves at the bottom of mixer, supplying equimolar ratio of high purity methane and carbon dioxide to the mixer and evenly stirring the mixture, (C) pressurizing high purity methane and carbon dioxide gas mixture using high pressure pump at the top of mixer, passing the pressurized methane and carbon dioxide mixed gas to arc heater through transfer pipe and distributor, (D) quickly heating the high purity methane and carbon dioxide mixture at 1500-1900 degrees C and uniformly stirring, and (E) discharging a mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide through top outlet of reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for non-catalytic carbon dioxide reforming of methane to syngas.

PT P
PN CN114053824-A; CN114053824-B
TI Recovering carbon dioxide from petroleum            associated gas involves pressurizing associated gas,            separating heavy hydrocarbon, moisturizing associated            gas to obtain first gas rich in carbon dioxide, and            contacting first gas with adsorbent.
AU GAO Q
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG F
   WANG L
   SONG X
   HOU Y
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI PETROLEUM EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
GA 202232086A
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering carbon dioxide from petroleum                associated gas involves pressurizing associated                gas, separating heavy hydrocarbon, moisturizing                associated gas to obtain first gas rich in carbon                dioxide, contacting first gas with adsorbent,                contacting the first gas with an adsorbent to                remove carbon dioxide in the first gas to obtain a                second gas that does not contain carbon dioxide,                adsorbing carbon dioxide in the adsorbent,                contacting the adsorbent with a regeneration gas to                desorb carbon dioxide in the adsorbent to obtain a                mixed gas, separating regeneration gas in the mixed                gas from the carbon dioxide, and recovering the                carbon dioxide, where the adsorbent is prepared by                polymerizing a first monomer, a second monomer and                indole, the first monomer includes                3-butene-1-amine, maleic hydrazide and                4-pentene-1-amine, and the second monomer includes                tetravinyl tin and                N,N-methylenebisacrylamide.
   USE - Method for recovering carbon dioxide from                petroleum associated gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can adsorb carbon dioxide in the                first gas by adsorbent, so that the amount and the                recovery purity of the recovered carbon dioxide are                improved, the adsorbent can be repeatedly utilized,                and cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is                  selected from aluminium chloride, boron                  trifluoride, antimony pentachloride, iron                  bromide, ferric chloride, tin chloride, titanium                  tetrachloride, and zinc chloride. Preferred                  Process: The preparation method of the adsorbent                  involves dissolving the first monomer, the second                  monomer and the initiator into the solvent,                  stirring uniformly, heating up to 60-80degrees                  Celsius, reacting under a nitrogen atmosphere for                  2-5 hours to obtain a prepolymerized                  intermediate, where the degree of polymerization                  of the prepolymerized intermediate is 20-50, and                  the initiator includes azo, persulfuric or peroxy                  initiator, adjusting the temperature of the                  solution containing the prepolymerized                  intermediate to 70-110degrees Celsius, adding the                  indole and catalyst, stirring uniformly, reacting                  for at least 8 hours under nitrogen atmosphere,                  adding the cerium-zirconium composite oxide,                  stirring continuously the reaction for at least                  7.5 hours, purifying and drying to obtain                  adsorbent. Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio                  of 3-butene-1-amine, maleic hydrazide and                  4-pentene-1-amine is (3-10):(5-15):(0-4). The                  weight ratio of tetravinyl tin and                  N,N-methylenebisacrylamide is (2-5):1. The                  pressure of the associated gas pressurization is                  3-3.5 megapascal, and the temperature is                  15-25degrees Celsius. The pressure at which the                  first gas contacts the adsorbent is 0.2-0.5                  megapascal, and the temperature is 30-90degrees                  Celsius, and the inflow rate of first gas is                  100-200 L/hours. The regeneration gas is water                  vapor. The temperature at which the adsorbent is                  in contact with water vapor is 110-120degrees                  Celsius, and the pressure is 0.1-0.4 megapascal.                  The introduction speed of water vapor is 50-80                  L/hours, and the mixed gas is introduced into the                  water to separate the water vapor and carbon                  dioxide in the mixed gas.

PT P
PN CN110903331-A
TI Separating fatty alcohols in crude alkyl            glycosides comprises placing crude alkyl glycoside in            extraction kettle, starting supercritical carbon            dioxide extraction and separation system, and            recovering carbon dioxide in the extraction            kettle.
AU ZHAO Y
   SUN M
   GAO X
   HUANG S
   SUN R
AE NANTONG RUIZHI SUPERCRITICAL TECHNOLOGY (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 202024140W
AB    NOVELTY - Separating fatty alcohols in crude alkyl                glycosides comprises placing crude alkyl glycoside                in the extraction kettle, starting the                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and                separation system, setting the extraction                temperature and extraction pressure, and then                pumping carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide gas                source into the extraction kettle, (2) continuously                pumping carbon dioxide to extract fatty alcohol                through a carbon dioxide high-pressure pump                according to a certain flow rate when the                temperature and pressure in the extraction kettle                reach the set value, (3) separating carbon dioxide                containing fatty alcohol from carbon dioxide by                reducing the pressure in the separator, and                separating the carbon dioxide recycling of fatty                alcohol, and (4) recovering the carbon dioxide in                the extraction kettle when the extraction time                reaches a predetermined value, taking out the solid                pure alkyl glycoside from extraction kettle, and                separating fatty alcohol from the separator and                reusing.
   USE - The method is useful for separating fatty                alcohols in crude alkyl glycosides.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the movement path when                gaseous carbon dioxide overflows, and improves the                separation efficiency of gaseous carbon dioxide and                fatty alcohol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  extraction temperature is setting to 20-80                  degrees C, preferably 25-60 degrees C, and the                  extraction pressure is setting to 10-50 MPa. In                  the step (2), the constant flow rate of carbon                  dioxide is 3-10 kg/hour. The reducing pressure is                  the pressure of the separator of 3-7 MPa. In the                  step (4), the extraction time is 20-180 minutes.                  Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide gas                  source, carbon dioxide high-pressure pump,                  extraction kettle and separator together                  constitute a supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction and separation system, the carbon                  dioxide gas source, the carbon dioxide                  high-pressure pump, the extraction kettle and the                  separator are all connected by a pipeline, the                  output pipe of the separator communicates with                  the input pipe of the carbon dioxide                  high-pressure pump, the lower end of the                  separator is connected with a boiling gas pipe                  (21) communicating with the carbon dioxide gas                  source, the lower end of the separator is also                  connected with an auxiliary exhaust pipe (22)                  communicating with the output pipe of the                  separator, the he pipeline between the carbon                  dioxide gas source and the carbon dioxide                  high-pressure pump, the pipeline between the                  extraction kettle and the separator, and the                  valves are connected to the boiling auxiliary gas                  pipe and the auxiliary exhaust pipe. The                  semi-permeable suspended barrel is provided with                  an inner semi-permeable diaphragm (3), the inner                  semi-permeable diaphragm (3) is made of GORE-TEX                  (RTM: Fabrics) material, and the thickness of                  GORE-TEX (RTM: Fabrics) is 1-2 mm.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC E10-E04E4; E10-E04E5; E10-E04F; E11-Q01A; E31-N05C

PT P
PN CN109718205-A; CN109718205-B
TI Preparing medicinal liposome by mixing supercritical carbon dioxide in pipeline with core medicine and wall material solution, subjecting core medicine to granulating kettle from lower portion and granulating content to form granule.
AU HU Y
   FENG T
AE TAERPU BEIJING PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TAER-Non-standard)
GA 201944837H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing medicinal liposome, involves adjusting liquid carbon dioxide to supercritical state, mixing supercritical carbon dioxide in a pipeline with a core medicine, then mixing the supercritical carbon dioxide in another line with the wall material solution, and adjusting the temperature such that the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved is higher than the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core material is dissolved, the temperature-adjusted supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core medicine is dissolved is introduced into the granulating kettle from the upper portion, the temperature-adjusted supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved is introduced into the granulating kettle from the lower portion and granulating content to form the lipid system granules.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing medicinal liposome.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the agglomeration of the core material particles, the probability of multiple embedding of the liposome, and improves the encapsulation efficiency of the liposome.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing medicinal liposome, involves adjusting liquid carbon dioxide to a supercritical state, mixing supercritical carbon dioxide in a pipeline with a core medicine, then mixing the supercritical carbon dioxide in another line with the wall material solution, and adjusting the temperature such that the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved is higher than the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core material is dissolved, the temperature-adjusted supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core medicine is dissolved is introduced into the granulating kettle from the upper portion, the temperature-adjusted supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved is introduced into the granulating kettle from the lower portion and granulating content to form the lipid system granules, where the pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core material is dissolved and the pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved are greater than the pressure in the granulating kettle and collecting encapsulated liposomes from carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation system of medicinal liposome, which comprises granulating kettle, where the lower portion of the granulating kettle is connected to a piping system for transporting supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved, and the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved is adjusted, the upper portion of the granulating kettle is connected to a piping system for transporting supercritical carbon dioxide in which a core medicine is dissolved, and the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core medicine is dissolved is adjusted and the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved after adjusting the temperature is higher than the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core medicine is dissolved after adjusting the temperature, the pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core material is dissolved and the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved are both greater than the pressure in the granulating kettle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the preparation method, the core material solution is a mixture of menthol + acetone and medicine, and the ratio of menthol and acetone is (4-2):1. The wall material solution is a mixture of lecithin and hyaluronic acid, and the ratio of lecithin to hyaluronic acid is (0.1-1):1. The temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved is adjusted to 30-70 degrees C, and the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core material solution is dissolved is adjusted to 50-80 degrees C. The pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core material is dissolved is 26 MPa, the pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the wall material solution is dissolved is 22 MPa, and the pressure in the granulating kettle is 7 MPa. The lipid system granules in the granulating kettle are controlled. The temperature of the lipid granules is controlled to 65 degrees C. The supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core medicine is dissolved is introduced into the granulator through the nozzle, and the supercritical carbon dioxide in which the core medicine is dissolved is kept inside the nozzle. The supercritical carbon dioxide dissolved in the core material to be passed into the granulating kettle is vaporized, and after the core material particles is precipitated, the direction of movement of the core material particles is changed to make it move in an orderly manner.

PN CN115126562-A
TI Integrated compressed carbon dioxide            energy-storing coal-fired power generation system has            carbon dioxide low-pressure storage tank connected with            water return heating component, carbon dioxide to enter            carbon dioxide low-pressure storage tank through water            return heating component.
AU ZHAO Y
   YAN J
   LIU M
   SUN R
   JIANG H
   XU P
   SHI H
   WANG C
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
   HUANENG POWER INT INC (CHHN-C)
GA 2022C88741
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a carbon dioxide low-pressure                storage tank connected with a water return heating                component (13). First valve (20) and second valve                (21) are respectively set between the carbon                dioxide low-pressure storage tank and a compressor.                First generator (17) drives the compressor to                compress and store a carbon dioxide in the carbon                dioxide low-pressure storage tank to a carbon                dioxide high-pressure storage tank (9). The first                valve is closed, when the second valve is turn on.                Carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide high-pressure                storage tank exchanges heat with a boiler (1)                enters a carbon dioxide turbine (5) to do work. The                carbon dioxide enters the carbon dioxide                low-pressure storage tank through the water return                heating component.
   USE - Integrated compressed carbon dioxide                energy-storing coal-fired power generation                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The coal-fired power generation system is                capable of effectively using energy and high                operation efficiency. The steam is reduced,                realizing fast lifting load, and improving the                flexibility of the system. The carbon dioxide in                the carbon dioxide high pressure storage tank                exchanges heat with the boiler, and then enters the                water return heating component, and the second                generator generates electricity, so as to avoid the                de-nitration efficiency caused by the steam                recirculation rate (SCR) denitrification device                caused by heat exchange with the flue gas in the                boiler when the system is in the low load or high                load operation. The system is able to effectively                use the excess power of the steam turbine,                realizing the fast load and lower load operation,                and reducing the steam.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                operation method of integrated compressed carbon                dioxide energy-storing coal-fired power generation                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the                integrated compressed carbon dioxide energy-storing                coal-fired power generation system (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
   1Boiler
   5Carbon dioxide turbine
   13Water return heating component
   17First generator
   20First valve
   21Second valve

   SHANG X
AE HUADIAN NINGXIA LINGWU POWER GENERATION (HUAD-C)
   HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 2021917470
AB    NOVELTY - Fly ash-based carbon dioxide absorption system                comprises carbon dioxide capturing device and                carbon dioxide storage device. The carbon dioxide                capturing device comprises hydrophobic zeolite                molecular sieve absorption matrix obtained after                treatment with fly ash. The carbon dioxide storage                device comprises filter residue sealing matrix                obtained after treatment with fly ash.
   USE - Used as fly ash-based carbon dioxide                absorption system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: realizes the preparation process                of the hydrophobic zeolite molecular sieve                high-strength material with fly ash as the raw                material; realizes the disposal of carbon dioxide;                and at the same time, solves the problem of fly ash                storage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                recycling fly ash, comprising (1) subjecting the                fly ash to iron removal pretreatment and alkali                treatment, and obtaining mixture of filtrate and                solid, (2) subjecting mixture of the filtrate and                aluminosilicate obtained in the above steps to                hydrothermal reaction crystallization to obtain                zeolite molecular sieve, depositing the zeolite                molecular sieve and silicon tetrachloride in the                liquid phase, obtaining the hydrophobically                modified zeolite molecular sieve, curing the                hydrophobically modified zeolite molecular sieve,                silicate cement, polyacrylamide and water are                cured, and obtaining hydrophobic zeolite molecular                sieve absorption matrix, where the solid mixture                obtained in the above steps contains water, which                is used as filter residue sealing matrix, (3)                placing hydrophobic zeolite molecular sieve                absorption matrix in the carbon dioxide capturing                device, placing the filter residue sealing matrix                is placed in the carbon dioxide storage device,                obtaining carbon dioxide capture device containing                hydrophobic zeolite molecular sieve absorption                matrix and carbon dioxide storage device containing                a filter residue sealing matrix, and (4) capturing                the carbon dioxide in the flue exhaust gas by the                carbon dioxide capture device containing the                hydrophobic zeolite molecular sieve absorption                matrix obtained in the above steps, placing the                hydrophobic zeolite molecular sieve absorption                matrix that has captured carbon dioxide in the                dioxide desorption device, and sending to carbon                dioxide storage device containing filter residue                sealing matrix for storage after the captured                carbon dioxide is desorbed by hot steam.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the fly                ash recycling method (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorption matrix                  of the hydrophobic zeolite molecular sieve                  comprises 60-70 pts. wt. hydrophobically modified                  zeolite molecular sieve, 15-25 pts. wt. silicate                  cement, 1-2 pts. wt. polyacrylamide and water in                  a mass ratio of 5:(1-2). The hydrophobically                  modified zeolite molecular sieve comprises                  silicon dioxide surface hydrophobically modified                  zeolite molecular sieve. The metal oxide includes                  calcium oxide, aluminum oxide and/or magnesium                  oxide. The filter residue sealing matrix is                  specifically water-containing filter residue. The                  iron removal pretreatment comprises magnetic                  separation and/or acid solution removing iron.                  The acid solution comprises hydrochloric acid                  solution, sulfuric acid solution and/or nitric                  acid solution. The concentration of the acid                  solution is 2-4 mol/l. The temperature for                  removing iron from the acid solution is 60-70                  degrees C. The time for removing iron from the                  acid solution is 5-6 hours. The alkali liquor                  comprises sodium hydroxide solution and/or                  potassium hydroxide solution. The alkali liquor                  comprises treatment is specifically hydrothermal                  reaction. The temperature of the alkali liquor                  comprises treatment is 90-100 degrees C for 48-96                  hours.

PN CN111141051-A; CN211575584-U
TI Absorption compression ejection composite cascade supercooling trans-critical carbon dioxide cooling and heating combined supply system used in e.g. refrigerating has absorbing compression injection composite cascade cooling circulation.
AU DAI B
   DOU W
   QIAN J
   CAO Y
   YANG H
   HAO Y
   FENG Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN COMMERCE (UTJC-C)
GA 202042406F
AB    NOVELTY - Trans-critical carbon dioxide cooling and heating combined supply system has a trans-critical carbon dioxide refrigeration circulation, an absorbing compression injection composite cascade cooling circulation and a heating circulation. A carbon dioxide compressor (1) is connected with a carbon dioxide gas cooler (2). A primary cooler carbon dioxide side is connected with an inlet. A secondary cooler carbon dioxide side is connected with a throttle valve. A compressor is connected with the inlet. The outlet of the compressor and a first-stage cooler are connected with an absorber (4). A heat user is connected with a three-way valve one (3). A cooling fluid side outlet of the absorber is connected to the cooling fluid side inlet of a condenser. The other outlet of the three-way valve is connected to the inlet of the carbon dioxide gas cooler to cooling the fluid. A cooling fluid side outlet of the carbon dioxide gas cooler is connected to the three-way valve two (9).
   USE - The absorption compression ejection composite cascade supercooling trans-critical carbon dioxide cooling and heating combined supply system is useful for refrigerating, medium-high temperature hot water and heating and other occasions requiring cooling or heating.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: uses absorbing compression injection composite cascade cooling circulation, which reduces pressure through the step of throttling; achieves supercooling of two different evaporation pressures, which ensures the carbon dioxide fluid at the outlet of the carbon dioxide gas cooler of the stepped supercooled trans-critical carbon dioxide refrigeration cycle for two consecutive times; ensures the heat exchange process form a better temperature match; reduces the heat transfer temperature difference; reduces irreversible loss of heat transfer during the carbon dioxide supercooling process; reduces temperature of the carbon dioxide fluid at the outlet of the gas cooler; reduces the carbon dioxide throttling loss; reduces the compressor compression ratio; improves isentropic efficiency; improves the system COP.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the absorption compression ejection composite cascade supercooling trans-critical carbon dioxide cooling and heating combined supply system for refrigerating.

   Carbon dioxide gas cooler (2)
   Three-way valve one (3)
   Absorber (4)
   Three-way valve two (9)

PN CN114459691-A
TI Method for evaluating carbon dioxide leakage risk in carbon dioxide geological storage body, involves correlating change of sealing condition parameters with detected leakage, and storing correlated data, where correlated data is used as basis for predicting whether leakage occurs.
AU DU R
   WANG Z
   WANG Y
   ZHANG Q
   ZHANG Y
   LIU M
AE UNIV NORTHEAST PETROLEUM (UYPT-C)
GA 2022686689
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining change of sealing condition parameters of a carbon dioxide sealing layer. The change of the sealing condition parameters is stored according to a time sequence. Determination is made to check whether carbon dioxide in the sealing layer is leaked. A time point of detecting leakage of the carbon dioxide is obtained under the condition that a detection result is that the leakage has occurred. The change of the sealing condition parameters is correlated with the detected leakage. The correlated data is stored, where the correlated data is used as basis for predicting whether the leakage occurs according to the change of the sealing condition parameters.
   USE - Method for evaluating carbon dioxide leakage risk in a carbon dioxide geological storage body.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables estimating the leakage of the carbon dioxide according to change of a sealing environment, so that possibility of leakage of the carbon dioxide can be pre-estimated, thus improving safety of sealing the carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a system for evaluating carbon dioxide leakage risk in a carbon dioxide geological storage body;
   (2) a processor; and
   (3) a memory.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for evaluating carbon dioxide leakage risk in a carbon dioxide geological storage body. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

A control unit (11) controls air supply fan and ventilating fan based on carbon dioxide concentration acquired by carbon dioxide sensor. An arrival time required to reach predetermined carbon dioxide concentration is calculated based on carbon dioxide concentration variation information while decrease of carbon dioxide concentration is detected.

Un-purified hydrogen is guided                into a hydrogen purifying unit for purifying the                non-purification hydrogen to obtain purified                hydrogen. The air in the hydrogen supplying unit is                replced by carbon dioxide. The purified hydrogen is                replaced with carbon dioxide in the gas supply                unit. The hydrogen supplies unit is heated, when                carbon dioxide replaces hydrogen. The carbon                dioxide concentration in the hydrogen supply unit                is fixed lower than the first carbon dioxide                concentration value, when replacing carbon dioxide                in air.

The method further involves separating and recovering carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas, supplying the recovered carbon dioxide to carbon dioxide fixing. The fixing of carbon dioxide to the aggregate (A1) involves utilizing the waste heat. The method further involves deodorizing exhaust gas by a combustion deodorizer, and separating and recovering from the exhaust gas burned, and producing the recycled aggregate by crushing construction waste including asphalt pavement waste. Preferred Apparatus: The apparatus further comprises a drying furnace (B2) for drying a aggregate (A2) different from the aggregate (A1), a carbon dioxide fixing device (D2) for fixing the carbon dioxide to the aggregate (A2), a supply device (E2) for supplying the aggregate (A2) to which the carbon dioxide is fixed, and mixing the aggregate (A2) with the aggregate (A1) and the asphalt. The asphalt plant further comprises flue that collects the exhaust gas generated in the drying furnace (B1) and the exhaust gas generated in the drying furnace (B2) and supplies them to the carbon dioxide recovery device, and carbon dioxide separated from the exhaust gas by the carbon dioxide recovery device and recovered is supplied to the carbon dioxide fixing device (D2) through supply channels, a combustion deodorizer between the drying furnace (B1) and the carbon dioxide recovery device to deodorize exhaust gas, and carbon dioxide recovery device separates and recovers carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas burned by the combustion deodorizer. AU HANSEUNGWOO
   LEE M W
   NOH J
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 202223016F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring carbon dioxide emissions according to multiple field variables of a wheel type construction equipment. A database is built using the obtained carbon dioxide emission. The database is applied to non-linear regression analysis to generate an algorithm for predicting the carbon dioxide emissions according to an operating state of the construction equipment. Ranking is selected for each case according to emission of carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide emission is calculating in a working state when the operating state of the construction equipment is an excavate state.
   USE - Method for predicting carbon dioxide emissions of a wheel type construction equipment using nonlinear regression analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables predicting emission of carbon dioxide generated per hour according to type of the construction equipment by using the nonlinear regression analysis such that emission of the carbon dioxide can be minimized in planning or construction stage.

PN CN110683546-A; CN110683546-B
TI Performing dehydrocarbonation of carbon dioxide containing hydrocarbon impurities involves entering carbon dioxide raw material gas first in coiled preheater for preheating, then entering dehydrocarbon electric heater for heating.
AU ZHANG L
   SHEN J
   XU Y
AE HANGZHOU KUAIKAI HI TECH CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202007427M
AB    NOVELTY - Performing dehydrocarbonation of carbon dioxide containing hydrocarbon impurities involves entering carbon dioxide raw material gas first in coiled preheater for preheating, then entering the dehydrocarbon electric heater for heating, and entering the bottom of the dehydrocarbonation reaction tower after heating, performing dehydrocarbon reaction in the dehydrocarbonation reaction tower. The carbon dioxide raw material gas is entered in the diversion tube from the lower end inlet of the diversion tube, and after heat exchange through the tube heat exchanger, it entered in the cavity of the distributor through the upper end of the diversion tube. The carbon dioxide raw material gas is then guided downward through the guide tube and enters the reaction area between the catalyst packing layer and the tube-by-tube heat exchanger. The hydrocarbon impurities and oxygen in the carbon dioxide raw material gas occur.
   USE - Method for performing dehydrocarbonation of carbon dioxide containing hydrocarbon impurities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform dehydrocarbonation of carbon dioxide containing hydrocarbon impurities is simple process, convenient operation, low heat loss, high heat utilization rate, and the dehydrocarbonation equipment used has less area, cost-effective, higher economic benefits, and better dehydrocarbonation effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing dehydrocarbonation of carbon dioxide containing hydrocarbon impurities involves entering carbon dioxide raw material gas first in coiled preheater for preheating, then entering the dehydrocarbon electric heater for heating, and entering the bottom of the dehydrocarbonation reaction tower after heating, performing dehydrocarbon reaction in the dehydrocarbonation reaction tower. The carbon dioxide raw material gas is entered in the diversion tube from the lower end inlet of the diversion tube, and after heat exchange through the tube heat exchanger, it entered in the cavity of the distributor through the upper end of the diversion tube. The carbon dioxide raw material gas is then guided downward through the guide tube and enters the reaction area between the catalyst packing layer and the tube-by-tube heat exchanger. The hydrocarbon impurities and oxygen in the carbon dioxide raw material gas occur. The chemical reaction generates carbon dioxide and water, and the carbon dioxide mixed gas after the dehydrocarbonation reaction rises and is discharged from the top of the dehydrocarbonation reaction tower after being stabilized by the steady flow plate. The discharged carbon dioxide mixed gas enters the tube side of the preheater around the tube, and exchanges heat with the carbon dioxide raw material gas in the shell side. The decarbonated water cooler, the carbon dioxide mixed gas is cooled and cooled by circulating water, and the cooled carbon dioxide mixed gas enters the bottom of the adsorption tower. The carbon dioxide mixed gas gradually rises, flows through the baffle plate and the throttle plate, and adsorbed by the desiccant and activated carbon filler. The residual hydrocarbon impurities and water vapor in the carbon dioxide mixed gas are adsorbed, and the purified carbon dioxide gas is discharged from the top of the adsorption tower into a post-processing system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The residual hydrocarbon impurities and water vapor in the carbon dioxide mixed gas are adsorbed, and the purified carbon dioxide gas is discharged from the top of the adsorption tower into a post-processing system. The heat exchange process of the mixed gas in the coiled preheater. The control valve on the gas pipe is opened to allow the carbon dioxide raw material gas in the fixed loop to enter the coiled heat exchanger.

TI Assessing risk of carbon dioxide loss from current or potential carbon dioxide sequestration site, by obtaining geologic samples from site, measuring rock volatiles carbon dioxide level associated with samples, and determining risk of loss.
AU SMITH M
AE SMITH M (SMIT-Individual)
GA 2021B5940F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a number of geologic samples from the site that is sufficient for analyzing the rock volatiles carbon dioxide properties of one or more areas of the site. The method also involves measuring the rock volatiles carbon dioxide level associated with the samples, and determining if the one or more zones are associated with levels of rock volatiles carbon dioxide indicative of a risk of carbon dioxide loss from the site.
   USE - Method of assessing risk of carbon dioxide loss from current or potential carbon dioxide sequestration site to assess changes in ability of site to sequester carbon dioxide over time. Uses include but are not limited to, a petroleum well, a deep saline formation, a depleted oil or gas reservoir, deep un-mineable coal seam, or shale formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method assesses characteristics of geologic sites with respect to the site's overall carbon dioxide storage capability, changes in carbon dioxide content of the site over time, and other characteristics. It can be useful in assessing the suitability of site(s) for carbon sequestration, assessing the stability of a site during CCS/CCUS applications, and related applications.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a graph of carbon dioxide rock volatile levels obtained from two proximate oil well sites (about 0.75 miles apart in some places) (wells 1 and 2, respectively), reflecting areas of similarity and difference between the two sites in terms of carbon dioxide rock volatile levels.

PN WO2020115792-A1
TI Energy production system has heat source side heat exchange unit which is provided for converting low boiling point heat medium from liquid to low boiling point heat medium.
AU YASUTAKA S
AE USUI TECHNOS CO LTD (USUI-Non-standard)
GA 2020506822
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a liquefied carbon dioxide conversion unit (10) that compresses and liquefies carbon dioxide recovered from industrial discharge of a thermal power plant, a steel mill, a cement factory, a chemical factory or the atmosphere by electric power or surplus power. A storage unit (20) stores the liquefied carbon dioxide gas. A carbon dioxide side heat exchange unit (30) converts into expanded and evaporated carbon dioxide. A carbon dioxide turbine power generation unit (40) generates power by the expanded and vaporized carbon dioxide sent from the carbon dioxide side heat exchange unit. A buoyancy power generation unit (50) performs buoyancy power generation by residual pressure of carbon dioxide. A heat source side heat exchange unit (80) converts the low boiling point heat medium from a liquid to a low boiling point heat medium. A binary power generation unit (60) generates power by the low boiling point heat medium of the expanded and evaporated gas.
   USE - Energy production system.
   ADVANTAGE - Since prevention or reduction is attained in the increase in carbon dioxide levels, provision of a clean energy is attained. The cost reduction at the time of recovering carbon dioxides is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an energy production method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the energy production system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Carbon dioxide side heat exchange unit (30)
   Carbon dioxide turbine power generation unit (40)
   Buoyancy power generation unit (50)
   Binary power generation unit (60)
   Heat source side heat exchange unit (80)
   Energy production system (100)

TI Method for controlling the ambient temperature vaporization of carbon dioxide for use in a power plant, involves introducing a liquid carbon dioxide stream at a supply pressure into a pressure reduction valve, where stream is produced.
AU DOORE M J
   CANIGLIA G
   STEWART J A
   COLLINGE E L
   WILLIAMS C G
   SLAUGH A D
AE AIRGAS INC (AIRG-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIRGAS INC (AIRG-Non-standard)
   DOORE M J (DOOR-Individual)
   CANIGLIA G (CANI-Individual)
   STEWART J A (STEW-Individual)
   COLLINGE E L (COLL-Individual)
   WILLIAMS C G (WILL-Individual)
   SLAUGH A D (SLAU-Individual)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
GA 202029888S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing a liquid carbon dioxide stream at a supply pressure into a pressure reduction valve (103). The carbon dioxide stream is produced at a delivery pressure (107). The carbon dioxide stream is introduced at the delivery pressure into a heat exchange device (105). The heat is exchanged between a stream of ambient temperature air (106) and the liquid carbon dioxide stream. The vaporized carbon dioxide stream is produced at the delivery pressure. A flowrate totalizer (110) provides a running total of the carbon dioxide during a predetermined time period. The minimum delivery pressure is greater than deposition pressure.
   USE - Method for controlling the ambient temperature vaporization of carbon dioxide for use in a power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves introducing a liquid carbon dioxide stream at a supply pressure into a pressure reduction valve, where carbon dioxide stream is produced at a delivery pressure and carbon dioxide stream is introduced at the delivery pressure into a heat exchange device, and thus enables to prevents direct contact between the hydrogen and air to prevent a combustible mixture of hydrogen and oxygen and reduces solid carbon dioxide snow forming in the stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a controlled ambient temperature vaporization system.
   Controlled ambient temperature vaporization system (101)
   Liquid carbon dioxide (102)
   Pressure reduction valve (103)
   Heat exchange device (105)
   Ambient temperature air (106)
   Delivery pressure (107)
   Flowrate totalizer (110)

PN CN113149012-A
TI Extracting carbon dioxide from blast furnace gas            comprises pressurizing blast furnace gas, capturing            carbon dioxide in blast furnace gas by amine washing,            compressing carbon dioxide, desulfurizing, drying, and            storing in liquefaction.
AU WANG Z
   TANG W
   TAN S
   CONG Z
   LIANG X
   KE Z
   LI J
AE BENGANG STEEL PLATES CO LTD (BSTP-C)
GA 2021912824
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting carbon dioxide from blast furnace                gas comprises pressurizing blast furnace gas,                capturing carbon dioxide in blast furnace gas by                amine washing, compressing carbon dioxide,                desulfurizing, and drying, and storing in                liquefaction.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting carbon                dioxide from blast furnace gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, has higher added                value, flexible and efficient production, can                replace argon, and helps balance the gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The specific method                  comprises (i) performing pressurization and amine                  washing to capture carbon dioxide process blast                  furnace gas in blast furnace gas main network,                  where the pressure is 0.011 MPa, and the                  temperature is greater than or equal to 60                  degrees C, boosting blast furnace gas is 0.026                  MPa by blower and introducing bottom of                  decarburization tower, reversing contact with                  alcohol amine lean absorption liquid added at top                  of decarbonization tower, absorbing alcohol amine                  lean absorption liquid the carbon dioxide                  component in blast furnace gas to become a rich                  solvent, and returning unabsorbed gas to the                  blast furnace gas main network for secondary use,                  rich solvent after absorbing carbon dioxide is                  pressurized by the pump at the bottom of the                  decarburization tower and heating up with the                  alcohol amine lean absorbent heat exchanger and                  introducing regeneration tower, heating rich                  solvent in regeneration tower, removing carbon                  dioxide gas from the top of the regeneration                  tower to regenerate, where regenerated                  alcoholamine lean absorbent undergoes heat                  exchange to recover energy, cooling by bottom                  pump of the regeneration tower and returning to                  absorption tower; the carbon dioxide gas at the                  top of the regeneration tower contains water                  vapor and a small amount of rich solvent,                  condensing by condenser at top of regeneration                  tower, introducing reflux tank of the                  regeneration tower, and returning all the                  condensate to top of the decarburization tower by                  reflux pump of regeneration tower as reflux, and                  uncondensed gas is semi-product carbon dioxide                  gas, (ii) semi-product carbon dioxide gas of the                  compression and fine desulfurization process is                  at the second tap of the compressor, where                  pressure is 0.7 MPa, the temperature is greater                  than or equal to 40 degrees C, and introducing                  desulfurization tower to remove hydrogen sulfide                  in the semi-product carbon dioxide gas through                  the refined desulfurization process, and                  performing compression passing through third and                  fourth stages of the compressor to a pressure of                  2.6 MPa, (iii) introducing semi-product carbon                  dioxide gas of TSA (temperature swing adsorption)                  process at 2.6 MPa and a temperature of 40                  degrees C; where, the drying system comprises 1                  water separator, 1 drying heater, 1 drying                  cooler, and 3 drying units, tower composition,                  one drying tower works, the other two regenerate,                  Each tower undergoes the working steps of                  adsorption, reverse discharge, heating, cooling,                  and pressure increasing, thermally blowing                  desiccant saturated with adsorption and                  regenerating at 160 degrees C, and regeneration                  gas comes from flash steam, top of the drying                  tower obtains dry carbon dioxide gas, (iv)                  supplying dried carbon dioxide gas by                  refrigeration unit to provide refrigeration,                  after liquefaction, introducing purification                  tower for rectification and purification,                  obtaining qualified carbon dioxide liquid by                  purification tower kettle, which is stored in a                  low-temperature storage tank, non-condensable gas                  at the top of the purification tower is removed                  to TSA The process returns to the main network of                  blast furnace gas after making regeneration gas;                  (v) filling liquid carbon dioxide in                  low-temperature storage tank in the storage and                  transportation process and solding or                  pressurizing to 3 MPa by a liquid pump and                  introducing the gasifier for gasification and                  introducing gas spherical tank for storage.

A carbon-dioxide-gas water washing unit (6) cleans carbon dioxide gas from the regeneration tower with circulation cleaning water. A portion of circulation cleaning water of the waste gas water washing unit and the carbon dioxide gas water washing unit is added to the absorption liquid of absorption tower. The water temperature of the circulation cleaning water of the waste gas water washing unit is controlled more than the waste gas temperature that flows into absorption tower.

TI Maintaining syngas composition ratio during upset condition, comprises e.g. detecting reduction in import carbon dioxide flow rate with carbon dioxide import stream flow sensor and evaluating reduction in carbon dioxide flow rate or carbon dioxide pressure in controller.
AU ROESCH A
   GUERIF P
   QIAN Y
   YUPENGQIAN
   ROHESCH A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202268718F
AB    NOVELTY - Maintaining a syngas composition ratio during an upset condition, comprises: detecting a reduction in the import carbon dioxide flow rate with a carbon dioxide import stream flow sensor (301); evaluating the reduction in carbon dioxide flow rate or carbon dioxide pressure in a controller (108); performing at least one predetermined corrective actions as instructed by the controller, where the predetermined corrective actions comprise opening a carbon dioxide import stream flow valve; opening a hydrocarbon and steam stream feed valve (201); opening a carbon dioxide backup stream control valve; opening a syngas backup letdown valve (206); and starting a composition adjustment unit (105).
   USE - The method is useful for maintaining a syngas composition ratio during an upset condition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves gas phase; and is economical.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the control lines from the various sensors to the controller.
   Composition adjustment unit (105)
   Controller (108)
   Steam stream feed valve (205)
   Syngas backup letdown valve (206)
   Carbon dioxide import stream flow sensor (301)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide imports stream flow sensor, where the carbon dioxide imports stream flow valve, the hydrocarbon and steam stream feed valve, the carbon dioxide backup stream control valve, the syngas backup letdown valve, and the composition adjustment unit are operationally connected to the controller. The carbon dioxide backup stream control valve is opened first, then the composition adjustment unit is then started. A syngas stream generated by a syngas reactor bypasses the composition adjustment unit from the time that the composition adjustment unit is started, until the composition adjustment unit is on-line. The carbon dioxide buffer tank maintains the carbon monoxide inventory at supercritical conditions. The syngas backup letdown valve is opened first, then the composition adjustment unit is then started. A syngas stream generated by a syngas reactor bypasses the composition adjustment unit from the time that the composition adjustment unit is started, until the composition adjustment unit is on-line. The syngas buffer tank maintains the carbon monoxide inventory at supercritical conditions. Once the composition adjustment unit is on-line, the carbon dioxide backup stream control valve and the syngas backup letdown valve are closed.Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide buffer tank comprises a carbon monoxide pressure sensor, where the composition adjustment unit is then started based on the remaining carbon monoxide inventory. The composition adjustment unit comprises a membrane separation unit, where the composition adjustment unit is started when there is 10 minutes of remaining carbon monoxide inventory, and the composition adjustment unit is started when there is 10 minutes of remaining syngas inventory. A syngas buffer tank comprises a syngas pressure sensor, where the composition adjustment unit is then started based on the remaining syngas inventory.
