AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction system (1) comprises                an absorption tower (2) configured to bring a                source gas containing carbon dioxide into contact                with an absorption liquid composed of an aqueous                solution containing at least one amine compound so                that the carbon dioxide is absorbed in the                absorption liquid; an electrolysis apparatus (3)                for electrolyzing the carbon dioxide absorbed in                the absorption liquid in the absorption tower; and                a circulation line (13,14) for circulating the                absorption liquid between the absorption tower and                the electrolysis apparatus.
   USE - The system is useful forcarbon dioxide                reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - With the carbon dioxide reduction system, by                electrolyzing carbon dioxide absorbed in the                absorption liquid, it is possible to efficiently                produce carbon monoxide using carbon dioxide as the                source. With the configuration including the                impurity removal apparatus, since the absorption                liquid from which impurities have been removed by                the impurity removal apparatus is supplied to the                absorption tower, it is possible to improve the                absorption efficiency of carbon dioxide in the                absorption tower, and further it is possible to                improve the production efficiency of carbon                monoxide in the electrolysis apparatus. Through                pre-treatment of the source gas prior to the                introduction to the absorption tower, it is                possible to reduce factors of inhibiting absorption                of carbon dioxide into the absorption liquid in the                absorption tower and electrolysis of carbon dioxide                in the electrolysis apparatus. Thus, it is possible                to further improve the yield of the reduction                product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon                dioxide reduction which involves an absorption step                of bringing a source gas containing carbon dioxide                into contact with an absorption liquid composed of                an aqueous solution containing at least one amine                compound so that the carbon dioxide is absorbed in                the absorption liquid; an electrolysis step of                electrolyzing the carbon dioxide absorbed in the                absorption liquid in the absorption step; and a                circulation step of circulating the absorption                liquid between an absorption tower and an                electrolysis apparatus.

TI Processing method for making cellulose fibers by pre-swelling or pretreating cellulose fibers, adding additive to increase solubility of slimming drug in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and then immersing cellulose fiber in fluid.
AU ZHU W
   WANG H
   LONG J
   XIAO H
   SHI M
AE BINZHOU LOFTEX IND CO LTD (BINZ-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   NANTONG CELLULOSE FIBERS CO LTD (CNTA-C)
   SHANDONG CHUCAI ANHYDROUS FIBER DYEING (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201886338D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology for making cellulose fibers involves: (1) pre-swelling or pretreating the cellulose fibers; (2) adding an additive to increase the solubility of slimming drug in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and dissolving a hydrophilic drug, which is not soluble in supercritical fluid in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; (3) adding the slimming drug to a medicine tank of a high-pressure equipment, removing air, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure in the equipment to 8-30 MPa to obtain a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and immersing the cellulose fiber in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; and (4) introducing the slimming drug into the pre-swollen cellulose fiber, carrying out relative movement between the drug-loaded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and the cellulose fibers, and leaving still the slimming drug in the amorphous zone inside the cellulose fibers after pressure relief.
   USE - The processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology is useful for making cellulose fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The processing method is capable of making the cellulose fibers with excellent slimming function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology for making cellulose fibers involves: (1) pre-swelling or pretreating the cellulose fibers; (2) adding an additive to increase the solubility of slimming drug in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and using supercritical carbon dioxide/reverse micelle method to dissolve the hydrophilic drug, which is not soluble in supercritical fluid in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; (3) adding the slimming drug to a medicine tank of a high-pressure equipment, removing air, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure in the equipment to 8-30 MPa and temperature to 32-120 degrees C to obtain a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and immersing the cellulose fiber in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; and (4) introducing the slimming drug into the pre-swollen cellulose fiber by dissolving in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, continuously carrying out relative movement between the drug-loaded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and the cellulose fibers, and leaving still the slimming drug in the amorphous zone inside the cellulose fibers after pressure relief to form a drug-loaded cellulose fiber capable of sustained release.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is chosen from methanol, ethanol and/or acetone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The cellulose fibers include natural cellulose fibers and regenerated cellulose fibers. The natural cellulose fibers include cotton fibers and hemp fibers. The regenerated cellulose fibers comprise viscose fiber, high wet modulus viscose fiber and solvent spun regenerated cellulose fibers. Preferred Method: The pre-swelling method involves: (A) preparing 30-80 %mass N-methylmorpholine-N-oxide aqueous solution, immersing the cellulose fiber in the aqueous solution, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and aqueous solution is 1:15-1:35, and setting the temperature at 60-90 degrees C for 10-80 minutes; (2) preparing 10-20% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, immersing the cellulose fiber in the aqueous solution, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and aqueous solution is 1:25-1:40, and setting the temperature at -10 degrees C to 20 degrees C for 10-80 minutes; and (3) preparing an aqueous ionic liquid 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride solution with a water content of 2-5%, immersing the cellulose fiber in the ionic liquid, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and ionic liquid is 1:25-1:40, and setting the temperature at 60-120 degrees C for 10-80 minutes. The pretreatment involves carrying out active grafting on the cellulose fibers.

GA 202183097G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying water or electrolyte to an anode region, and immobilizing a carbonic anhydrase on a porous carbon support. A humidified carbon dioxide gas is supplied to a composite cathode area containing catalyst particles chosen from indium, tin, lead, mercury, palladium, bismuth, antimony, nickel and thallium. Hydrogen ions are passed through an electrolyte membrane, and are moved to a complex reduction electrode region to electrically reduce carbon dioxide. A gaseous electrical reduction reaction of the carbon dioxide is performed in a reduction electrode. The reduction product of carbon dioxide is formic acid, formaldehyde, formate, acetaldehyde, acetate, acetic acid, acetone, 1-butanol, 2-butanol, 2-butanone, ethanol, isopropanol, lactate, lactic acid, methanol, 1-propanal, 1-propanol, propionic acid, carbon monoxide, methane, ethylene and/or ethane.

PN WO2021028946-A1; IN201941032708-A
TI Sustainable fixation of carbon dioxide in organic molecule for producing cyclic carbonate, involves preparing marigold spinel bimetallic oxide hollow microsphere, using microsphere catalyst for coupling epoxide and carbon dioxide.
AU JADHAV A H
   NAGARAJA B M
   PRASAD D
   PATIL K N
   BHANUSHALI J T
   BHARI M N
AE UNIV JAIN (UYJA-Non-standard)
GA 2021179240
AB    NOVELTY - Sustainable fixation of carbon dioxide involves preparing marigold spinel bimetallic oxide hollow microsphere, using microsphere as a catalyst for coupling epoxide and carbon dioxide for sustainable chemical fixation of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide and epoxide under mild reaction condition.
   USE - Sustainable fixation of carbon dioxide in organic molecule for producing cyclic carbonate. Uses include but are not limited to aprotic solvent, electrolyte in lithium-ion battery, plastics, cosmetics and adhesives.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables sustainable fixation of carbon dioxide with excellent conversion efficiency of reactant and selectivity of desired product, and provides high yield of cyclic carbonate. The spinel bimetallic oxide hollow microsphere has high catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for catalytic fixation of carbon dioxide, which involves reacting epoxide of formula (I) with carbon dioxide to form cyclic carbonate of formula (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The sustainable fixation of carbon dioxide further involves dissolving cobalt and copper precursor in isopropanol and homogenizing the obtained solution under constant stirring, adding glycerol to the reaction mixture to obtain a pink colored solution, continuously stirring the pink solution for 10-60 minutes at room temperature, pouring the mixture and tightly sealing in a Teflon (RTM: polytetrafluoroethylene) lined autoclave and solvothermally heating for 1-6 hours at effective temperature, centrifuging and washing the resultant solid with water and ethanol after cooling for a number of times to obtain brown parent solid material, drying in vacuum for 12 hours at 90 degrees C and calcining at 420 degrees C with a heating rate of 2 degrees C minute-1 to obtain the pure phase of spinel bimetallic oxide hollow marigold microspheres, carrying coupling reaction in 100 mL stainless-steel high-pressure reactor fitted with a magnetic stirrer and an auto-programmed heating controller, in which charging the substrate epoxide, base and spinel bimetallic oxide hollow microsphere into the reactor under solvent free conditions and purging carbon dioxide gas thrice, and pressurizing the system to a constant carbon dioxide pressure of 5-25 bar, heating the reaction mixture at 25-120 degrees C for 1-5 hours under constant magnetic stirring of 600-1000 rpm, cooling the reaction mixture to ambient temperature after a set reaction time over, slowly depressurizing the reactor by venting the excess amount of carbon dioxide gas, diluting the reaction mixture using ethyl acetate and separating the spinel bimetallic oxide hollow microsphere catalyst by simple filtration, obtaining conversion and selectivity of the cyclic carbonate as product, identifying, quantifying and confirmed by using gas chromatography (GC) and GC-mass spectrometer (MS) analysis, washing the filtered catalyst with ethanol, and drying in the oven at 80 degrees C for 12 hours for reuse.

   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the purification speed and purification effect, prevents the air intake pipe from clogging, is reasonable, effective in usage of discharged carbon dioxide waste gas to produce liquid carbon dioxide, economical, environmentally-friendly and convenient to separate, purifies the reverse gas containing carbon dioxide, and liquefies to form liquid carbon dioxide production technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing high-purity liquid carbon dioxide, involves (a) injecting carbon dioxide exhaust gas with content more than 70%-85% to raw material gas buffer tank, (b) stabilizing carbon dioxide exhaust gas by raw material gas buffer tank, (c) after passing steady-pressure gas to compressor for compression, (d) passing feed gas from secondary outlet of compressor to desulfurization and pretreatment system under pressure of certain air pressure, (e) desulfurizing the gas of desulfurization and pretreatment system, (f) passing gas after desulfurization to purification system for purification, (g) passing purified gas to compressor, and compressing to certain air pressure by the compressor tertiary inlet to certain pressure, (h) passing compressed gas to evaporation condenser of condensation system for gas cooling, (i) passing gas after temperature reduction to purification kettle, and using purification kettle for purifying carbon dioxide gas to obtain purified high-purity liquid carbon dioxide, (j) passing purified high-purity carbon dioxide to liquid high-purity carbon dioxide storage tank, and (k) after discharging non-condensable gas from top of purification kettle to heat exchanger, cooling the temperature of non-condensable gas down to normal temperature and discharging to air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves passing gas containing carbon dioxide using decarburization separator for filtering out gas impurities in carbon dioxide gas, before passing gas to buffer tank, and filtering the dust in gas by dust filter system. The step (e) involves performing adsorption process after desulfurization, passing gas to adsorption system for adsorbing water and impurities in gas in carbon dioxide gas, and after adsorbing and filtering impurities, passing gas to purification system. The method involves using large amount of cold energy produced by conversion of liquid ammonia from liquid to gas for converting gaseous carbon dioxide to liquid. The principle of purification involves using principle of rectification for removing impurities in liquid carbon dioxide. In process for producing high-purity liquid carbon dioxide, the products comprise carbon dioxide waste gas, dust filter system, raw gas buffer tank, compressor, desulfurizer, pretreatment system, adsorption system, purification system, condensation system and purification system. Preferred Conditions: The certain air pressure in step (d) is 2-3.5 MPa, and the air pressure in step (g) is 5-6 MPa. The temperature drop in step (h) is state where the temperature drops from a normal temperature state to-13 to-20 degrees C.

The second stream having a pH of 12-14 and applying an electrical potential between the gas diffusion anode and the gas diffusion cathode to reduce the carbon dioxide and oxidize the glycerol or glucose.
   USE - Method for electroreducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to electroreduce carbon dioxide, which has improved economics of the overall process and activity for carbon dioxide electroreduction, and high sensitivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method further comprises withdrawing a liquid product stream from an outlet of the chamber, the liquid product stream comprising an oxidation product of glycerol or glucose or a reduction product of carbon dioxide and withdrawing a gaseous product stream comprising a reduction product of carbon dioxide selected from carbon monoxide, ethylene, methane, or any combination. The method further comprises purifying the liquid product stream to obtain a substantially pure stream of the oxidation product of glycerol or glucose or the reduction product of carbon dioxide, and purifying the gaseous product stream to obtain a substantially pure stream of the reduction product of carbon dioxide. The gas diffusion cathode divides the chamber into a gaseous region and a liquid region. The second stream comprises from 0.5-2.5M alkali metal hydroxide. The carbon dioxide is reduced to carbon monoxide, and where a partial current density for carbon monoxide is from 15-350 mA cm-2 at a cell potential of (-)1.0 V to (-) 2.5 V. The carbon dioxide is reduced to a reduction product of carbon dioxide and an onset cell potential for formation of the reduction product of carbon dioxide is from (-) 1.5 V to (-) 0.5 V. The chamber is divided into a cathode compartment and an anode compartment by an ion permeable membrane. The second stream comprises an anolyte stream and a catholyte stream, where the anolyte stream is fed into the anode compartment and the catholyte stream is fed into the cathode compartment. The chamber further comprises an ion permeable membrane in contact with the gas diffusion cathode. The method further comprises reducing the carbon dioxide to ethanol, formate, or combination. The chamber is defined inside a flow electrolyzer. The pH of the second stream is 12.5-14. The method of electroreducing carbon dioxide, which involves feeding a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide into chamber divided into a cathode compartment and an anode compartment by an ion permeable membrane, the anode compartment containing a gas diffusion anode and the cathode compartment containing a gas diffusion cathode. The gas diffusion cathode dividing the cathode compartment into a gaseous region and a liquid region, where the gas stream is fed into the gaseous region, and feeding a catholyte stream into the liquid region of the cathode compartment. The feeding of anolyte stream comprising glycerol into the anode compartment to contact the gas diffusion anode and applying an electrical potential between the gas diffusion anode and the gas diffusion cathode to reduce the carbon dioxide to a reduction product and oxidize the glycerol to an oxidation product, where an onset cell potential for formation of the reduction product is from (-) 1.5 V to (-) 0.5 V. The catholyte stream or the anolyte stream has a pH from 13-14. Preferred Components: The anolyte stream comprises an alkali metal hydroxide or an alkali metal carbonate. The gas diffusion cathode comprises an electrocatalytic cathode coating configured for reducing carbon dioxide. The electrocatalytic cathode coating comprises a metal selected from silver, gold, zinc, palladium, tin, lead, mercury, indium, copper, or combination. The gas diffusion anode comprises an electrocatalytic anode coating configured for oxidizing glycerol. The electrocatalytic anode coating comprises a platinum black catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Fertilizer used to enhance growth of plants, has nitrogen source, carbon dioxide source, acid source to increase retention of carbon dioxide released from the source in soil and increase availability to plant and carbohydrate source.
AU HAYES M C
   SHIRLEY A R
AE INNOVATIONS WORLD NUTRITION LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 2021B87532
AB    NOVELTY - Fertilizer comprises: a source of nitrogen; a source of carbon dioxide; a source of acid in an amount to increase retention of carbon dioxide released from the source in soil and increase the availability of carbon dioxide to a plant; and a source of carbohydrate, where the fertilizer provides increased plant crop yield, improve efficiency of nitrogen uptake by the plant, improved nitrogen levels in the plant, and increased plant uptake of carbon by the plant.
   USE - The fertilizer is useful for enhancing growth of plants (claimed).
No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer: utilizes the acid to provide increased retention of carbon dioxide in the soil; and enhances the growth of plants exhibiting enhanced growth from the combination of the nitrogen, carbon dioxide and carbohydrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for enhancing growth of plants comprising applying the fertilizer comprising the source of nitrogen, the source of carbon dioxide, the source of acid to increase retention of carbon dioxide released from the source in soil and increase the availability of carbon dioxide to the plant, and the source of carbohydrate to soil containing a plant and allowing the source of nitrogen to release nitrogen to the plant and soil, the source of carbon dioxide to release carbon dioxide to the plant and soil, and the source of acid to reduce the pH to increase retention of the carbon dioxide in the soil, the nitrogen, carbon dioxide and carbohydrate provide enhanced growth to the plant, increased crop yield of the plant, improved efficiency of nitrogen uptake by the plant, improved nitrogen levels in the plant, and increased plant uptake of carbon by the plant. The plant is a seed or seedling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The fertilizer comprises 10-96% the source of nitrogen, 1-40% the source of carbohydrate, 1-50% the source of carbon dioxide and up to 2% the source of acid. The source of acid is present in an amount to provide a pH of 4.5 and 6 measured by forming an aqueous solution of the source of nitrogen, the source of carbon dioxide, the source of acid and the source of carbohydrate. The fertilizer is in solid, semi-solid or liquid form. The source of carbon dioxide comprises at least one of dry ice, carbon dioxide gas, liquid carbon dioxide, a solution of carbon dioxide, a suspension containing carbon dioxide, or a slurry containing carbon dioxide. The source of carbon dioxide comprises dry ice. The source of carbohydrate comprises at least one of seed grind, starch or sugar. The source of carbohydrate is a seed grind formed from a same type of plant to be grown. The source of carbohydrate comprises brown rice seed grind. The source of acid comprises at least one of phosphoric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, hydrochloric acid or an organic acid. The ratio of a weight of the source of carbon dioxide to a weight of source of carbohydrate is 10:1-0.5:1.Preferred Method: The method further comprises: enhancing the growth of rice; using brown rice seed grind as the source of carbohydrate; applying the fertilizer beneath the soil from 0.5-10 inches deep to apply the fertilizer to roots of the plant, where the fertilizer is moved under the soil by use of water or mechanical incorporation; and conducting additional applications of the fertilizer after a first application. The fertilizer is applied to paddy rice seedlings at a carbon dioxide level of 10-100 g/m2 with or after planting the seed and prior to transplanting the plants. The fertilizer is applied to paddy rice in a field at a carbon dioxide level of 5-50 kg/hectare either just before, at, or after the paddy rice is transplanted.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN US2019126237-A1; WO2019089649-A1; CA3078968-A1; AU2018360565-A1; CN111225730-A; KR2020071091-A; EP3703842-A1; SG11202003119-A1; JP2021503366-W; US11014067-B2; KR2361888-B1; CN111225730-B; JP7145211-B2
TI Adsorption material used to abate carbon dioxide,            remove carbon dioxide and separating carbon dioxide,            comprises metal-organic framework comprising metal            cations and polytopic organic linkers, and many            polyamine ligands.
AU WESTON S C
   FALKOWSKI J M
   LONG J R
   KIM E J
   MARTELL J D
   MILNER P J
   SIEGELMAN R L
   KING E J
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 201937319S
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorption material comprises: a metal-organic                framework comprising many metal cations and many                polytopic organic linkers; and many polyamine                ligands, where each respective polyamine ligand in                the polyamine ligands comprises substituted amine                containing compounds (I).
   USE - The material is useful for: abating carbon                dioxide from a flue gas; removing carbon dioxide                from a biogas, natural gas or landfill gas; and                separating carbon dioxide from a gas produced by a                source.
   ADVANTAGE - The material exhibits strong ability to                capture carbon dioxie from various                compositions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Adsorption material comprises: a metal-organic                framework comprising many metal cations and many                polytopic organic linkers; and many polyamine                ligands, where each respective polyamine ligand in                the polyamine ligands comprises substituted amine                containing compounds of formula                ((R1)(X1)N-Z1(R2)(R3)-Q1(R4)(R5)-(Y1)-Q2(R6)(R7)-Z2(R8)(R9)-N(X2)(R10))                (I).
   X1, X2 = first and second metal cations in the                metal cations, where each metal cation is Mg                (preferred), Ca, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu or                Zn;
   Y1 = e.g.                -((Z3(R11)(R12))l-N(R15)-(Z4(R13)(R14))j)-;
   Z1-Z4, Q1, Q2 = C, Si, Ge, S or Se;
   i, j = 0-2; and
   R1-R15 = alkyl, heteroalkyl, aryl, heteroaryl,                heterocycloalkyl, alkoxy, arlyoxy or heteroaryloxy                (all optionally substituted), H or halo.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) abating (M1) carbon dioxide from a flue                gas, comprising (a) contacting the flue gas with                the adsorption material to reversibly adsorb carbon                dioxide from the flue gas, to generate an                adsorption material enriched for carbon dioxide,                and (b) stripping a major portion of the carbon                dioxide from the adsorption material enriched for                carbon dioxide using a regeneration process;
   (2) removing (M2) carbon dioxide from a                biogas, natural gas or landfill gas, comprising                contacting the biogas, natural gas or landfill gas                with the adsorption material to reversibly adsorb                carbon dioxide from the biogas thereby generating                an adsorption material enriched for carbon dioxide                and a residual gas that is greater than 98% pure                methane;
   (3) separating (M3) carbon dioxide from a gas                produced by a source, comprising exposing the                carbon dioxide within the gas to the adsorption                material, where the carbon dioxide is reversibly                separated into the adsorption material;
   (4) synthesizing (M4) the adsorption material,                comprising (A1) grafting the polyamine ligands onto                a metal-organic framework, where the grafting                comprises exposing a content of the metal-organic                framework to a solution comprising the polyamine                ligand diluted with a solvent, to form unactivated                adsorption material, and (B1) subjecting the                unactivated adsorption material to an inert                environment at a temperature of at least 165                degrees C, to activate the adsorption material;                and
   (5) removing (M5) carbon dioxide from a                multi-component gas mixture comprising carbon                dioxide and at least one of nitrogen gas, methane,                water and oxygen, comprising contacting the                multi-component gas mixture with the adsorption                material to reversibly adsorb carbon dioxide from                the multi-component gas mixture, to generate an                adsorption material enriched for carbon dioxide and                a residual gas that is depleted of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the material, at                  least 8% of the polyamine ligands in the                  polyamine ligands are (i) amine appended by a                  first amine to a first metal cation in the metal                  cations and (ii) amine appended by a second amine                  to a second metal cation in the metal cations of                  the metal-organic framework. The polytopic                  organic linker is                  4,4'-dioxidobiphenyl-3,3'-dicarboxylate                  (dobpdc(4-)),                  4,4''-dioxido-(1,1':4',1''-terphenyl)-3,3''-dicarboxylate                  (dotpdc(4-)),                  2,5-dioxidobenzene-1,4-dicarboxylate (dobdc(4-))                  or 3,3'-dioxide-biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate                  (para-carboxylate-dobpdc(4-)). Each polyamine                  ligand in the polyamine ligands is                  diethylenetriamine,                  N-(2-aminoethyl)-1,3-propanediamine,                  bis(3-aminopropyl)amine,                  N-(3-aminopropyl)-1,4-diaminobutane,                  triethylenetetramine,                  N,N'-bis(2-aminoethyl)-1,3-propanediamine,                  1,2-bis(3-aminopropylamino)ethane,                  N,N'-bis(3-aminopropyl)-1,3-propanediamine,                  N,N'-bis(3-aminopropyl)-1,4-diaminobutane or                  tetraethylenepentamine. The loading of the                  polyamine ligands to the metal-organic framework                  is 80-120%. The adsorption material exhibits: a                  step-shaped carbon dioxide adsorption profile or                  desorption profile; a single or                  multiple-step-shaped carbon dioxide adsorption or                  desorption profile; and a carbon dioxide                  adsorption profile that includes a carbon dioxide                  adsorption step above 140 degrees C. The                  polytopic organic linker has e.g. of formula                  (Ia)-(Ic).R19-R28 = H, halo, hydroxy, methyl or halo                  substituted methyl.The adsorption material: has a polyamine                  ligand: metal-organic framework metal cation                  loading ratio of less than or equal to 0.9 to                  1.5; adsorbs more than 3 mmol/g carbon dioxide at                  carbon dioxide pressures of greater than 100 mbar                  to less than 0.25 mmol/g nitrogen gas at nitrogen                  gas pressures of 100-1000 mbar at 40 degrees C;                  adsorbs more than 3 mmol/g carbon dioxide at                  carbon dioxide pressures greater than 100 mbar                  and less than 0.25 mmol/g oxygen gas at oxygen                  gas pressures of 100-1000 mbar at 40 degrees C;                  and adsorbs more than 3 mmol/g carbon dioxide at                  carbon dioxide pressures greater than 100 mbar                  and less than 0.25 mmol/g methane at methane                  pressures of 100-1000 mbar at 40 degrees C. In                  the method (M4): the solvent is toluene, water,                  methanol, dichloromethane, tetrahydrofuran,                  cyclohexane, pentane, 2-butanone, trichl                  oroethylene, methyl-t-butyl ether, heptane and/or                  diethyl ether; the inert environment is a stream                  of argon gas and/or nitrogen gas, blown on the                  unactivated adsorption material; and the                  activated adsorption material has a polyamine                  ligand loading of less than or equal to 110%,                  where the temperature is at least 190 degrees C.                  Preferred Method: In the method (M1): the                  regeneration process comprises a temperature                  swing adsorption method, a vacuum swing                  adsorption method, a pressure swing adsorption                  method and/or a concentration swing adsorption                  method; and the regeneration process comprises                  applying steam to the adsorption material                  enriched for carbon dioxide, to result in the                  desorption of the bound carbon dioxide for the                  reuse of the adsorption material. The method (M2)                  further comprises stripping a major portion of                  the carbon dioxide from the adsorption material                  enriched for carbon dioxide using a regeneration                  process. In the method (M2): the regeneration                  process comprises applying steam to the                  adsorption material enriched for carbon dioxide                  resulting in the desorption of the bound carbon                  dioxide for the reuse of the adsorption material;                  and the regeneration process is a temperature                  swing adsorption method, vacuum swing adsorption                  method, a pressure swing adsorption method and/or                  a concentration swing adsorption method. In the                  method (M3): the carbon dioxide is reversibly                  separated from the adsorption material by a                  regeneration process comprising applying steam to                  the adsorption material resulting in the                  desorption of the bound carbon dioxide for the                  reuse of the adsorption material, where the                  carbon dioxide is reversibly separated from the                  adsorption material by a regeneration process;                  and the regeneration process is a temperature                  swing adsorption method, vacuum swing adsorption                  method, a pressure swing adsorption method and/or                  a concentration swing adsorption method.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN KR2022099193-A
TI System for reducing carbon dioxide, contains            methanation process unit for converting carbon dioxide            into water and methane, and reliquefaction unit for            liquefying methane generated in the methanation process            unit and storing in storage tank.
AU AHN J H
   MYUNG K S
   KIM P G
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 202292073G
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction system comprises a                storage tank for storing liquefied natural gas                (LNG) and provided on a ship, a methanation process                unit for receiving carbon dioxide emitted from a                ship and converting into water and methane using                hydrogen, and a reliquefaction unit for receiving                and reliquefying boil-off gas generated in the                storage tank. The methane generated in the                methanation process unit is liquefied in the                reliquefaction unit and stored in the storage                tank.
   USE - System for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed)                in ships.
   ADVANTAGE - The system efficiently converts carbon dioxide                emitted from the ship into methane and liquefies in                an onboard reliquefaction unit, replenishes LNG in                the storage tank and supplies the required fuel to                the ship by supplying the methane converted from                carbon dioxide to the storage tank, ensures                environmentally-friendliness of the ship by                supplying the power required to convert carbon                dioxide into methane through renewable energy, and                minimizes carbon dioxide emitted from ships and                contributes to the reduction of greenhouse gas                emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reducing                carbon dioxide in ships, which involves supplying                carbon dioxide emitted from the ship to the                methanation process unit and converting into water                and methane using hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The system further                  contains a carbon dioxide separation unit for                  separating carbon dioxide from exhaust gas                  discharged from the ship and supplying to the                  methanation process unit, a hydrogen supply unit                  for supplying hydrogen produced by electrolysis                  of water to the methanation process unit, and a                  renewable energy generation unit provided in the                  ship. The carbon dioxide separation unit                  comprises a gas filter for receiving the exhaust                  gas and removing impurities including dust, a                  carbon dioxide separator for receiving the                  exhaust gas filtered by the gas filter and                  separating carbon dioxide, and a carbon dioxide                  tank for receiving and storing carbon dioxide                  separated by the carbon dioxide separator and                  supplying to the methanation process unit. The                  hydrogen supply unit comprises a water                  electrolyzer for electrolyzing water into                  hydrogen and oxygen, and a hydrogen tank for                  receiving and storing the hydrogen generated in                  the water electrolyzer and supplying to the                  methanation process unit. The water produced in                  the methanation process is supplied to the water                  electrolyzer for hydrogen generation. The energy                  generation unit generates electricity by                  renewable energy including wind power and solar                  power, and supplies power for hydrogen generation                  and carbon dioxide separation to the hydrogen                  supply unit and carbon dioxide separation unit.                  The methane generated in the methanation process                  unit is further supplied as a fuel to a gas-using                  place including an onboard engine. Preferred                  Process: The method further involves removing                  impurities including dust from the exhaust gas                  discharged from the ship, and separating carbon                  dioxide and supplying to the methanation process                  unit.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); W06 (Aviation, Marine and Radar Systems); 
MC E11-D02; E11-Q01B; E31-N05C; H04-E; H06-A02; H06-P; J03-B02; W06-C01A1; W06-C01C; X15-A02; X15-A10; X15-B01
IP B63J-003/00; B63J-003/04; C10G-002/00; C10L-003/08; C25B-001/04; C25B-015/08; F01N-003/021; F02M-021/02; F03D-009/32; H02S-010/12

PT P
PN CN212246844-U
TI Device for extracting Ganoderma lucidum triterpene and Ganoderma lucidum polysaccharide, comprises carbon dioxide steel bottle, carbon dioxide storage tank, extracting tank and separating tank, where carbon dioxide steel bottle is connected with storage tank.
AU MA Y
   HUANG Q
   HE H
   JIANG S
   ZHU C
   SUN Y
   ZHANG Q
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (ANHI-C)
GA 202103526D
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for extracting ganoderma triterpene and ganoderma lucidum polysaccharide on line, comprising a carbon dioxide steel bottle, a carbon dioxide storage tank, an extracting tank and a separating tank, wherein the carbon dioxide steel bottle and the carbon dioxide storage tank; between the separating tank and the carbon dioxide storage tank is provided with a carbon dioxide flow meter; between the extracting tank and the separating tank is provided with a fine adjusting valve; the carbon dioxide storage tank orderly passes through the carbon dioxide flow control valve, a first heat exchanger, a high pressure pump; the second heat exchanger and the valve are connected to the extraction tank; one side of the extraction tank is provided with a clean water storage tank; a water pump is set between the clean water storage tank and the extraction tank. AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorption enthalpy change measuring method involves using nitrogen as a heat balance medium for the calorimeter, converting to carbon dioxide at the same temperature and pressure for measurement, entering the nitrogen and carbon dioxide enter the pre-constant temperature system T1 and T2 of the thermostatic tank from the nitrogen gas tank and the carbon dioxide gas tank, moving through the three-way valve, quantitatively evacuating the gas by the mass flow meter, passing the gas through the constant temperature system of the calorimeter and the absorbent solution in the calorimeter is in a constant temperature environment, when the gas has reached the saturation state of the absorption solvent before entering the calorimeter, and recording the heat flow curve of carbon dioxide absorption by the absorbent solution, and integrating the heat flow curve to obtain absorbent solution to absorb the enthalpy change of carbon dioxide reaction.

AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
   KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202153807E
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide to formic acid                involves reacting among carbon dioxide and/or                carbon dioxide-derived inorganic carbonate with                hydrocarbon containing hydroxyl group in presence                of catalyst as active metal in catalyst complex,                noble metals and transition metals other than the                noble metals which are supported on support.
   USE - Method for converting carbon dioxide to formic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to convert carbon dioxide                to formic acid that improves process                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for converting carbon dioxide-derived metal                carbonate and glycerol, which involves performing                in presence of catalyst complex for carbon dioxide                conversion reaction, simultaneous conversion                reaction of carbon dioxide-derived metal carbonate                and glycerol to obtain reaction product, forming                mixture in which selected from 4-8C alcohols and                trialkyl phosphate (TAP) and adding to the obtained                reaction product, removing some moisture from the                mixed solution, performing carbonation and                esterification by adding carbon dioxide to the                mixed solution, performing esterification reaction                to synthesize alkyl lactate and alkyl formate,                separating alkyl lactate and alkyl formate produced                from each other through conventional methods such                as distillation, recovering metal carbonate                produced as reaction by-product, recycling metal                carbonate to the simultaneous conversion reaction                of glycerol and metal carbonate, catalyst complex                is catalyst in which one of noble metals and                transition metals other than the noble metals are                supporting as active metal on support.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components:                  The noble metals are selected from among                  ruthenium (Ru), iridium (Ir), rhodium (Rh),                  platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), and gold (Au). The                  transition metals other than the noble metals in                  catalyst complex comprises zinc (Zn), nickel                  (Ni), iron (Fe), cobalt (Co), manganese (Mn),                  magnesium (Mg), aluminum (Al), titanium (Ti),                  chromium (Cr), copper (Cu), zirconium (Zr),                  molybdenum (Mo), indium (In), barium (Ba),                  hafnium (Hf), and cerium (Ce). The support is                  selected from carbonaceous material, molecular                  sieve, ceramic material, and metal oxide. The                  hydrocarbon containing hydroxyl group is one                  selected from monohydric alcohol and polyhydric                  alcohol, or mixture. The hydrocarbon containing                  hydroxyl group is one selected from glucose,                  mannose, galactose, xylose, sorbitol, mannitol,                  galactitol, xylitol, glycerol, 1,4-butanediol or                  isomer, 1,4-pentanediol or isomer,                  1,2-propanediol or isomer, butanol, pentanol, and                  propanol, or mixture. The hydrocarbon containing                  hydroxyl group is glycerol and/or glucose. The                  alcohol is selected from 4-8C butanol, and inert                  organic solvent is tributyl phosphate (TBP).                  Preferred Conditions: The amount of the alcohol                  added is 1-50 in ratio of the number of moles to                  sum of the number of moles of metal lactate and                  metal formate, and molar ratio of the alcohol to                  trialkyl phosphate (TBP) is 1:5-5:1. The trialkyl                  phosphate (TAP) is added after the removing of                  moisture. Preferred Process: The method involves                  reacting hydrocarbon containing hydroxyl group                  with inorganic carbonate, reacting carbon dioxide                  with metal, metal salt, and ammonium salt to                  produce inorganic carbonate. The method involves                  hydrolyzing the alkyl lactate and alkyl formate                  to produce lactic acid and formic acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112697702-A
TI Optimizing carbon dioxide range based on density distribution characteristic involves obtaining a fast response curve of carbon dioxide, and analyzing the data characteristic distribution based on the carbon dioxide rapid response curve.
AU HU J
   CHEN D
   ZHANG J
   WANG Z
AE UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)
GA 2021448166
AB    NOVELTY - Optimizing carbon dioxide range based on density distribution characteristic involves obtaining a fast response curve of carbon dioxide (CO2), and analyzing the data characteristic distribution based on the CO2 rapid response curve. The width of the net photosynthetic rate is selected according to the fluctuation range of the CO2 rapid response curve, and divided the number of segments according to the amplitude/width of the net photosynthesis rate. The CO2 efficiency is analyzed. The enhancement efficiency of each unit of CO2 is calculted to photosynthesis, and used contribution analysis, and when the concentration of CO2 reaches the Nth segment, the cumulative efficiency of CO2 reaches an approximate value of 1. The continuous increase of the unit CO2 concentration is increase the net photosynthetic rate with an approximate value of 0. The Nth segment is selected as the suitable range of CO2 under the corresponding light temperature conditions.
   USE - The method is useful for optimizing carbon dioxide range based on density distribution characteristic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables analyzing distribution characteristic of carbon dioxide fast response curve, obtaining density distribution of carbon dioxide about net photosynthetic rate, selecting proper carbon dioxide range, providing basis for carbon dioxide supplement concentration control, which can promote the growth of plant, solving the problem that carbon dioxide in the facility environment is insufficient and the gas control precision in facility environment carbon dioxide fertilizing effect is limited.

TI Method for processing substrate, involves heating purified carbon dioxide liquid to target temperature above critical point to change liquid to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and utilizing fluid to clean substrate arranged in chamber.
AU DELMAS J
   VERHAVERBEKE S
AE APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
   APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
   APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
GA 201887114L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying heat to carbon dioxide liquid in a purification vessel with a distillation heater in the purification vessel. The carbon dioxide liquid is distilled in the purification vessel to form purified carbon dioxide gas in a single stage distillation process. The purified carbon dioxide gas is supplied from the purification vessel to a purification system condenser through a distillation fluid line. The purified carbon dioxide gas is condensed in the purification system condenser by heat exchange with a refrigerant from a refrigeration system to form purified carbon dioxide liquid. The purified carbon dioxide liquid is heated to target temperature above a critical point to change the purified carbon dioxide liquid to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is utilized to clean a substrate arranged in a processing chamber (101).
   USE - Method for processing a substrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables sweeping particles away from the substrate and preventing particles from re-depositing on the substrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for processing a substrate.

PN US2022143546-A1; EP4000713-A1; CA3135879-A1; AU2021257984-A1
TI Removing carbon dioxide from synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water comprises e.g. providing hygroscopic physical absorption medium and partially removing water from synthesis gas by cooling synthesis gas below dew point of water and separating condensed water.
AU CORBET S
   LINICUS M
   SCHMIDT S
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202266569U
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide from synthesis gas comprising at least hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water, comprises: (a) providing a hygroscopic physical absorption medium; (b) at least partially removing water from the synthesis gas by cooling the synthesis gas below the dew point of water and separating the condensed water; (c) cooling the absorption medium; (d) removing carbon dioxide from the cooled synthesis gas via the cooled absorption medium in a physical absorption step at elevated pressure, where the cooled synthesis gas and the cooled absorption medium are run in countercurrent, and synthesis gas at least partially freed of water and carbon dioxide is obtained and absorption medium laden with carbon dioxide and partially co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is obtained; (e) discharging the synthesis gas at least partially freed of water and carbon dioxide from the process; and (f) treating the laden absorption medium in many serially arranged flash stages.
   USE - The process is useful for: removing carbon dioxide from synthesis gas comprising at least hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water (claimed); and producing hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides a high rate of separated carbon dioxide and a pure and dry (anhydrous) carbon dioxide product; reduces operating costs of the plant; and improves the thermal integration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing carbon dioxide from synthesis gas comprising at least hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water, comprises: (a) providing a hygroscopic physical absorption medium; (b) at least partially removing water from the synthesis gas by cooling the synthesis gas below the dew point of water and separating the condensed water; (c) cooling the absorption medium; (d) removing carbon dioxide from the cooled synthesis gas via the cooled absorption medium in a physical absorption step at elevated pressure, where the cooled synthesis gas and the cooled absorption medium are run in countercurrent, and synthesis gas at least partially freed of water and carbon dioxide is obtained and absorption medium laden with carbon dioxide and partially co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is obtained; (e) discharging the synthesis gas at least partially freed of water and carbon dioxide from the process; (f) treating the laden absorption medium in many serially arranged flash stages, where co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is removed from the laden absorption medium in at least one first flash stage and carbon dioxide is removed from the laden absorption medium in a flash stage arranged downstream of the first flash stage; (g) compressing to absorption pressure and recycling to step (d) the carbon monoxide and hydrogen obtained in step (f); and (h) discharging the carbon dioxide obtained in step (f) from the process.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a plant for removing carbon dioxide from synthesis gas comprising the plant components in fluid connection with one another comprising a means for cooling the synthesis gas below the dew point of water, a means for separating water condensed out of the synthesis gas, a means for cooling a hygroscopic, physical absorption medium, an absorption column (T-01) for running the cooled synthesis gas freed of water and the cooled absorption medium in countercurrent at elevated pressure for removal of carbon dioxide from the synthesis gas, where synthesis gas at least partially freed of water and carbon dioxide is obtainable and absorption medium laden with carbon dioxide and partially co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is obtainable, a means for discharging the synthesis gas freed of water and carbon dioxide from the plant, many serially arranged flash stages comprising a first flash stage for removal of the co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen from the laden absorption medium and comprising a flash stage arranged downstream of the first flash stage for removal of carbon dioxide from the laden absorption medium, a compressor (C-02) for compressing carbon monoxide and hydrogen removed from laden absorption medium and for recycling the compressed carbon monoxide and hydrogen to the absorption column and a means for discharging carbon dioxide removed from laden absorption medium from the plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of plant for removing carbon dioxide from synthesis gas.
   Stripping medium via conduit (25)
   Compressor (C-02)
   Heat exchanger (HX-05)
   Absorption column (T-01)
   Rectification column (T-03)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The water is removed from the absorption medium in a distillation step arranged downstream of step (f). The distillation step for removal of water is operated temporarily. The absorption medium vapors obtained in the distillation step are utilized as a stripping medium for removal of carbon dioxide in the downstream flash stage according to step (f). The downstream flash stage has a lower pressure compared to the first flash stage. The absorption medium obtained in the downstream flash stage is recycled to the physical absorption step according to step (d) for reabsorption of carbon dioxide. The synthesis gas is in a process step upstream of the process subjected to a water gas shift reaction to increase the hydrogen yield, thus enriching the synthesis gas with water and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is not subjected to a drying step after discharging from the process according to step (h). The carbon dioxide is not subjected to a condensation step. The process comprises no hot regeneration step for regenerating the laden absorption medium. The water is removed by coded absorption medium in a further physical absorption step at elevated pressure. After discharging from the process to step (h) the carbon dioxide is compressed and subsequently stored or sent to a further use. An inert gas, preferably nitrogen, is utilized as stripping medium for removal of carbon dioxide in the downstream flash stage according to step (f).Preferred Components: The synthesis gas comprises: no sulfur compounds as impurities, preferably comprises no hydrogen sulfide and/or no carbonyl sulfide as an impurity; and no hydrogen cyanide and/or no ammonia as an impurity. The plant comprises means for withdrawing absorption medium vapors from the distillation apparatus and means for introducing the absorption medium vapors into the flash stage arranged downstream of the first flash stage for use of the absorption medium vapors as stripping medium. The serially arranged flash stages (f) has a distillation apparatus for removal of water from the absorption medium arranged downstream of it.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The provided hygroscopic, physical absorption medium comprises methanol. The provided hygroscopic, physical absorption medium comprises N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of parameters that do not support the claims.

The pressurizing unit is connected to a low-pressure steel cylinder that provides low-pressure carbon dioxide gas, and is suitable for pressurizing low-pressure carbon dioxide gas to form high-pressure carbon dioxide gas. The heat exchange unit is connected to the pressurizing unit and is suitable for heat exchange with high-pressure carbon dioxide gas. The output unit is connected to the heat exchange unit and is suitable for outputting a cooling fluid containing compressed gas and carbon dioxide particles. The cooling fluid and the carbon dioxide particles in it are used to take away the dust on the surface of the workpiece to achieve the effect of cooling and cleaning. In this way, the pressurizing unit pressurizes the low-pressure carbon dioxide gas to the required pre-pressure value, not only the pressure value can be controlled, but the low-pressure steel cylinder can be loaded with more carbon dioxide gas, which improves the supply time of carbon dioxide gas.

GA 2020985162
AB    NOVELTY - The gas heating system has a gas boiler unit (1), a flue gas waste heat primary recovery unit (2), a carbon dioxide heat pump waste heat deep recovery unit and the corresponding three-stage heating network of heat network return water. The carbon dioxide heat pump waste heat deep recovery unit includes a carbon dioxide evaporation device (5), a throttling device, a carbon dioxide gas cooling device and a compression device (6). The low-temperature flue gas exchanges heat with the carbon dioxide working fluid in the carbon dioxide evaporation device, and the flue gas temperature is further reduced to below 30 degrees C. The return water of the heating network after the first stage of heating is exchanged with the high temperature and high pressure carbon dioxide fluid in the carbon dioxide gas cooling device, and the return water of the heating network is sent to the gas boiler unit after the second heating.
   USE - Gas heating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes energy cascade utilization through the staged recovery of flue gas waste heat and staged heating of heat network return water, reduces the loss of gas energy, and can greatly improve the efficiency of gas heating systems, reduce gas heating costs and gas heating pollutant emissions.

PT P
PN CN115128244-A
TI Carbon dioxide injection storage potential            analysis method considering rock fluid action force,            involves calculating carbon dioxide saturation in            invading pores of pore network, and analyzing carbon            dioxide sequestration potential under maximum            displacement pressure.
AU SUN H
   YANG Y
   ZHANG K
   YAO J
   SONG W
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2022C7029L
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide injection storage potential                analysis method involves extracting pore network                model by the center axis method based on the                three-dimensional (3D) scanning image of the rock,                and considering both the rock-fluid force and the                capillary force of the gas-water interface. An                analysis method for the occurrence of carbon                dioxide and formation water under different                displacement pressures in pores with different                shapes is established. An intrusion percolation is                used to calculate the pores that carbon dioxide is                intruded on the pore network under different                displacement pressures according to the threshold                capillary force of each pore on the pore network                model. The carbon dioxide saturation in the                invading pores of the pore network is calculated,                and the carbon dioxide sequestration potential                under the maximum displacement pressure is analyzed                according to the single-pore gas-water occurrence                analysis method.
   USE - Carbon dioxide injection storage potential                analysis method considering rock fluid action                force.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcomes the defect that the                existing method can only consider the capillary                force of the gas-water interface and cannot                consider the influence of the rock fluid force on                the carbon dioxide injection and storage potential                of the underground rock, and can accurately predict                the carbon dioxide storage potential under                different rock pore structures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a carbon dioxide injection and                sequestration potential analysis device considering                the force of rock fluid;
   (2) an electronic device comprising a                controller, and a memory;
   (3) a computer readable storage medium stored                with computer readable instructions;
   (4) a computer program product comprising                computer instruction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a carbon                dioxide injection storage potential analysis method                considering rock fluid action force. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC S03-E14E7; T01-S03; T01-J04A

AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) includes: a first generation                device (60) for generating hydrocarbons having a                carbon count of two or more from a first raw                material containing hydrogen and at least one of                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; and a second                generation device (70) for generating methane from                a second raw material containing hydrogen and at                least one of the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide                in the first raw material discharged from the first                generation device. The second generation device is                provided with a nickel catalyst that produces                methane from the second raw material. The device                produces the products in a high yield, and                effectively uses excess heat of reaction. The                device efficiently generates hydrocarbons other                than methane from at least one of carbon monoxide                and carbon dioxide, and generates high-value-added                hydrocarbons other than methane. The methane is                produced in the second generating device with high                efficiency. The device improves the energy                efficiency of the entire process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                hydrocarbon generation method.

PN US2019366793-A1; DE102019111731-A1; CN110539609-A
TI Apparatus for controlling carbon dioxide level in            vehicle cabin, has controller that generates control            signal to switch recirculating module to open state in            response to estimation of carbon dioxide level            exceeding upper threshold value.
AU GIORDANO D
   TUMAS T M
   JAURDANO D
AE GM GLOBAL TECHNOLOGY OPERATIONS INC (GENK-C)
   GM GLOBAL TECHNOLOGY OPERATIONS INC (GENK-C)
GA 2019A1661Q
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a recirculation module (250)                that is provided for introducing outside air into                the vehicle cabin having a recirculation state and                an open state. A first sensor is provided for                determining the occupancy of the vehicle cabin. A                memory (245) is provided for storing a vehicle                cabin volume data. A controller is provided for                estimating the carbon dioxide level in response to                the duration of the recirculation state, the                occupancy of the vehicle cabin and the vehicle                cabin volume data. The controller is operative to                generate a control signal to switch the                recirculating module to the open state in response                to the estimation of the carbon dioxide level                exceeding an upper threshold value.
   USE - Apparatus for controlling carbon dioxide level                in vehicle cabin.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus does not require a costly carbon                dioxide sensor. The accurate estimation of carbon                dioxide concentration due to occupant respiration                without utilizing a carbon dioxide sensor is                achieved. The introduction of outside air is                minimized, while reducing strain on the heating                ventilation air conditioning system and reducing                interior build up of carbon dioxide. The system is                operative to estimate the carbon dioxide levels                within the vehicle cabin based on detectable                interior cabin factors, such as vehicle occupancy,                cabin size, vehicle location and the like. The time                duration facilitates the estimation of carbon                dioxide build up over time within the vehicle                cabin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of controlling carbon dioxide level in vehicle                cabin.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                apparatus for controlling carbon dioxide level in                vehicle cabin.
   Door state sensor (225)
   Vehicle speed sensor (230)
   Vehicle location sensor (240)
   Memory (245)
   Recirculation module (250)

PN DE102016116767-A1; DE102016116767-B4
TI Method for determining biodegradation of organic substances for e.g. controlling carbon in water system, involves calculating change in isotope ratios from samples taken before/after culturing medium containing carbon dioxide isotopes.
AU MECKENSTOCK R
AE MECKENSTOCK R (MECK-Individual)
GA 2018187969
AB    NOVELTY - A method for determining biodegradation of organic substances involves providing a biodegradation assay medium containing a microbial community, an electron acceptor, organic substances that can be degraded by the microbial community, and carbon dioxide isotopic mixture, culturing the medium to allow the progression of biodegradation of the organic substance, determining carbon dioxide isotope ratio of two samples from the container obtained before and after culturing, and calculating the change in carbon dioxide isotope ratios in the two samples.
   USE - Method for determining biodegradation of organic substances for controlling assimilable/biodegradable organic carbon in water systems such as drinking water, industrial wastewater and natural water, controlling metabolic activity in living organisms, tissues and cell cultures, and determining the absolute concentration of carbon dioxide in closed biodegradation vessel (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical, accurate and sensitive determination of biodegradation of organic substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A method for determining biodegradation of organic substances involves providing a biodegradation assay medium containing a microbial community, an electron acceptor, organic substances that can be degraded by the microbial community, and carbon dioxide isotopic mixture containing 13 carbon dioxide and 12 carbon dioxide in a ratio which is increased compared to the natural 13/12 carbon dioxide ratio, in a closed container, determining 13/12 carbon dioxide ratio of a sample (s1) taken from the container, culturing the biodegradation assay medium to allow the progression of biodegradation under conditions for biodegrading the organic substance to carbon dioxide, determining 13/12 carbon dioxide ratio of a sample (s2) taken from the container, and calculating the change in 13/12 carbon dioxide ratios in the two samples.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The microbial community is provided in form of a sample from an ecological source chosen from a drinking water supply system, an industrial wastewater treatment plant, and natural water. The microbial community comprises similar/different species of aerobic and anaerobic microorganisms capable of degrading the organic substances and being present in form of a microbial culture, autochthonous microorganisms, cell cultures, or other biota.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The biodegradation assay medium is obtained by adding the microbial community to an assay medium containing the electron acceptor, organic substance and carbon dioxide isotopic mixture. The usage amount of the carbon dioxide isotopic mixture is 1-50 atomic%, preferably 10-20 atomic%. The biodegradation assay medium comprises an aqueous phase and a gas phase. The samples are obtained from the same phase.

PN CN109089760-A
TI Ecological planting used e.g. to improve crop yield in greenhouse comprises using an anti-fog high-transparent film, fermenting to produce carbon dioxide and fermentation liquid, dehumidifying and applying fermentation broth to soil.
AU LIN X
AE DONGGUAN JIFENG AGRIC ECOLOGICAL PARK (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201903246F
AB    NOVELTY - Ecological planting comprises using an anti-fog high-transparent film as a greenhouse film, arranging a fermentation tank in the greenhouse, then arranging a carbon dioxide membrane separation device outside the greenhouse, fermenting in a fermentation tank to produce carbon dioxide and fermentation liquid, then separating the carbon dioxide from air as a supplement to the greenhouse carbon dioxide by starting the carbon dioxide membrane separation device when the concentration of carbon dioxide in the greenhouse is lower than the set value, carrying out dehumidification by heating, using the artificial dehumidification method to regulate the humidity when the humidity is still higher than the set value and uses organic waste as the raw material, breaking the organic waste as a raw material for fermenting in the fermentation tank after completing the artificial dehumidification, and applying fermentation broth to the soil to provide nutrients to the soil.
   USE - The planting is useful for improving crop yield in greenhouses and reducing pests and diseases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures normal photosynthesis of crops, always ensures optimal growth of crops, and achieves high output and high quality targets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ecological planting comprises (a) using an anti-fog high-transparent film as a greenhouse film, (b) arranging a fermentation tank in the greenhouse, then arranging a carbon dioxide membrane separation device outside the greenhouse, fermenting in a fermentation tank to produce carbon dioxide and fermentation liquid, then using carbon dioxide as a daily carbon dioxide supply in greenhouse, then separating the carbon dioxide from the air as a supplement to the greenhouse carbon dioxide by starting the carbon dioxide membrane separation device when the concentration of carbon dioxide in the greenhouse is lower than the set value, (c) carrying out dehumidification by heating, using the artificial dehumidification method to regulate the humidity when the humidity is still higher than the set value and uses organic waste as the raw material, breaking the organic waste as a raw material for fermenting in the fermentation tank after completing the artificial dehumidification, and (d) applying the fermentation broth fermented by the fermentation tank to the soil to provide nutrients to the soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The organic waste comprises 30-60 pts. wt. straw, 30-60 pts. wt. wheat bran, 1-5 pts. wt. biomass carbon, 5-10 pts. wt. wood residue and 1-5 pts. wt. waste newspapers. The nutrient material comprises 80-90 pts. wt. expanded alumina perlite, 30-40 pts. wt. algae clay, 20-30 pts. wt. fly ash, 5-10 pts. wt. lees, 10-20 pts. wt. grass peat, 1-3 pts. wt. microelement and 100-120 pts. wt. water. Preferred Components: The anti-fog high-transparent film is a polyethylene film having an inner layer coated with colloidal silicon dioxide coating layer. Preferred Method: The method comprises setting the fermentation tank by digging a pit in the greenhouse, spreading tarpaulin in the pit, placing broken organic waste and decomposing agent, watering, covering with a tarpaulin with an opening for fermentation to produce carbon dioxide and fermentation broth. The artificial dehumidification method comprises covering the soil layer of greenhouse with a covering composed of organic waste, where the thickness of the cover is 1-3 cm. The step (d) comprises while applying fermentation broth to the soil simultaneously apply the same weight portion of nutrient material.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN113121865-A; CN113121865-B
TI Preparing polyurethane foaming mixture used for polyurethane foaming, involves adding polyethyleneimine and/or grafted modified polyethyleneimine to storage tank, passing mixture of absorbable carbon dioxide to pipeline reactor, adding supercritical carbon dioxide, and reacting.
AU XIE X
   YUAN S
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2021839836
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing polyurethane foaming mixture, involves adding 100 pts. wt. polyurethane foam raw material, 0.1-100 pts. wt. polyethyleneimine and/or 0.1-100 pts. wt. grafted modified polyethyleneimine to storage tank, uniformly mixing and stirring to obtain mixture for absorbing carbon dioxide, reacting by passing the mixture of absorbable carbon dioxide to pipeline reactor using pressurizing pump, adding liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide to pipeline reactor using metering pump, uniformly mixing and reacting two reaction raw materials in mixer, spraying the mixture to polyurethane foaming mixture storage tank using discharge port, passing obtained product to polyurethane foaming mixture reservoir by pressurized pump or even multiple times to pipe reactor and reacting with liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide until the reaction of polyurethane foaming mixture to carbon dioxide reaches saturation.
   USE - Method for preparing polyurethane foaming mixture used as raw material for polyurethane foaming (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing polyurethane foaming mixture, involves adding 100 pts. wt. polyurethane foam raw material, 0.1-100 pts. wt. polyethyleneimine and/or 0.1-100 pts. wt. grafted modified polyethyleneimine to storage tank, uniformly mixing and stirring to obtain mixture for absorbing carbon dioxide, where the polyurethane foam material does not contain isocyanate, and comprises polyol as polyurethane soft segment, and reacting by passing the mixture of absorbable carbon dioxide to pipeline reactor using pressurizing pump, adding liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide to pipeline reactor using metering pump, uniformly mixing and reacting two reaction raw materials in mixer, spraying the mixture to polyurethane foaming mixture storage tank using discharge port, where the temperature of material in pipeline reactor is 0-50 degrees C, and the pressure is a state at which carbon dioxide can be kept in liquid state or supercritical state, the concentration of carbon dioxide added is 0.01-1.2 times amount of polyethyleneimine and/or grafted modified polyethyleneimine saturated absorption in above-mentioned mixture of absorbable carbon dioxide, when the amount of carbon dioxide added is less than saturation absorption amount, passing obtained product to polyurethane foaming mixture reservoir by pressurized pump or even multiple times to pipe reactor and reacting with liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide until the reaction of polyurethane foaming mixture to carbon dioxide reaches saturation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyol is selected from polyether polyols, polyester polyols, polybutadiene diols, castor oil-based polyols, vegetable oil-derived polyols, vinyl polymer grafted polyether polyols and/or flame-retardant polyether polyols. The polyurethane foam raw material comprises catalyst, foam stabilizer, flame retardant, chain extender, cell opener, water and/or physical blowing agent. The side chain of graft-modified polyethyleneimine comprises chain comprising alkyl chain, polypropylene glycol chain and/or polysiloxane chain, where the alkyl chain comprises 1-16C atoms, preferably 3-8C atoms, the polypropylene glycol chain comprises structure of formula (I), and the polysiloxane chain comprises structure of formula (II). The polysiloxane chain and main chain of graft-modified polyethyleneimine has structure of formula (III).m = 1-15;n = 1-6;p = 0-10;andT = linking group between polysiloxane chain and main chain of graft-modified polyethyleneimine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyethyleneimine comprises linear polyethyleneimine, branched polyethyleneimine and/or hyperbranched polyethyleneimine, where the molecular weight of polyethyleneimine is 200-80000 daltons. Preferred Conditions: The degree of grafting of graft-modified polyethyleneimine is 0-45 mol.%, preferably 1-8 mol.% based on N atom of polyethyleneimine.
