PT P
PN WO2018025197-A1
TI Separation of carbon dioxide from mixture of synthesis gases coming from shift and reforming sections in plants for production of ammonia involves gravimetric separation of gases and recovery of flow of carbon dioxide and synthesis gases.
AU CAPONE G
   DE RINALDIS S
   LEPORE A
   SALA M
   ZAMBIANCO A
AE SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
GA 201811692Y
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide is separated from mixture of synthesis gases coming from shift and reforming sections in plants by carrying out (1) gravimetric separation of gases of mixture; and (2) separate recovery of a flow of carbon dioxide and a flow of synthesis gases purified from carbon dioxide, where mixture of synthesis gases subjected to gravimetric separation is in supercritical phase.
   USE - Separation of carbon dioxide from mixture of synthesis gases coming from shift and reforming sections in plants for production of ammonia (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Purification rate of synthesis gases (syngas) from carbon dioxide is improved, and overall separation efficiency is improved. Process for the purification of syngas from carbon dioxide allows a considerable energy saving, since the hiqh energy cost for regenerating the solvent is avoided. The gravimetric separation leads to the recovery of carbon dioxide under conditions which allow it to be ready for use for the urea synthesis, where subjecting carbon dioxide to a low pressure to a compression step of 140-200 bar is unnecessary. Purified synthesis gases can also be used directly for ammonia production by utilizing the outlet pressure from the purification step, without the need for a subsequent compression step.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of urea which involves carrying out the separation process of carbon dioxide from mixture of synthesis gases;
   (2) conversion/modification of conventional ammonia synthesis plant, comprising reforming and shift section, from which a flow of synthesis gases (syngas) is obtained, removing section of carbon dioxide through washing with suitable solvents, obtaining a flow of carbon dioxide and a flow of purified synthesis gases, section of use of carbon dioxide separated in a urea synthesis process, possibly after suitable compression, section of further purification of the synthesis gases, obtaining a flow of further purified synthesis gases, and section of use of further purified synthesis gases in an ammonia synthesis process, which involves modifying the plant so that greater than or equal to 1 portion of the synthesis gases (syngas) obtained from the reforming and shift section is subjected to a removal step of carbon dioxide in supercritical phase through a gravimetric separation process; and
   (3) ammonia and/or urea production plant comprising greater than or equal to 1 devices for gravimetric separation in supercritical phase of carbon dioxide from a flow of synthesis gases.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the separation process of carbon dioxide from mixture of synthesis gases coming from shift and reforming sections in plants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Mixture of synthesis gases comprises contains elements and compounds selected from hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, water, and/or carbon dioxide. Mixture of synthesis gases further contains elements and compounds selected from nitrogen, argon, helium, and other incondensable inerts. Preferred Processes: Before gravimetric separation, a pre-treatment step of the mixture of synthesis gases is carried out to obtain a dry flow. Pre-treatment step is a dehydration step. Pre-treatment step is preceded by a compression step, possibly followed by a cooling step. Pre-treatment step is followed by a further compression step, possibly followed by a further cooling step. Gravimetric separation includes (1a) gravimetric separation at intermediate pressure of less than or equal to 75 bar, preferably 100-200 bar, obtaining a head flow (310) predominantly of synthesis gases purified of carbon dioxide and a bottom flow (320) predominantly of carbon dioxide. Head flow (310) is subjected to (1b) gravimetric separation (HPS2) at a pressure of less than or equal to 75 bar, preferably 200-300 bar, obtaining a head flow (330) predominantly of synthesis gases purified of carbon dioxide and a bottom flow (340) predominantly of carbon dioxide. After step (1a) and before step (1b), a compression step (HPC1) of the head flow (310) is carried out at a pressure of less than or equal to 75 bar, preferably 200-300 bar. After step (1a) and before step (1b), and preferably after the compression step of the head flow (310), a cooling step HEX3 is carried out at - 50-50 degrees C, preferably 0-50 degrees C. Step (3b) is carried out at a pressure of 120-240 bar. Head flow is used in an ammonia preparation step (3). Head flow, before being used in ammonia preparation step (3), is subjected to (3a) heating, (3b) purification from carbon dioxide and from carbon monoxide, and (3c) cooling. Head flow, before being subjected to steps (3a)-(3c), is subjected to expansion step at 120-240 bar. In step (3a), the head flow is heated to 250-350 degrees C. Step (3b) produces a flow of purified synthesis gases with a content of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide of less than or equal to 10 ppm. Bottom flow is subjected to high-pressure gravimetric separation step carried out at a pressure of less than or equal to 75 bar, preferably 200-300 bar, obtaining a head flow predominantly of synthesis gases purified of carbon dioxide and a bottom flow predominantly of carbon dioxide. Head flow (350), possibly together with the head flow (330) forming a flow (370), is subjected to ammonia preparation step (3) and to steps (3a)-(3b). Before high-pressure gravimetric separation step (HPS1), the bottom flow (320) is subjected to compression step (HPC2) at a pressure of less than or equal to 75 bar, preferably 200-300 bar. After compression step (HPC2) and before separation step (HPS1), cooling step (HEX4) is carried out at - 50-50 degrees C, preferably of 0-50 degrees C. Bottom flow (360), before being used in the urea preparation step (4), is subjected to step (4a) of heating (HEX6) at 90-150 degrees C. Before step (4a), the flow (360) is subjected to expansion step (EX2), preferably, at a pressure of 140-200 bar. Bottom flow (340), possibly together with bottom flow (360) forming a flow (380), is used in the urea production step (4). Purification step (3b) is carried out through high-pressure methanation. Purification step (3b) is carried out through cryogenic washing with liquid nitrogen. Purification step through cryogenic washing is preceded by a step of passage through greater than or equal to 1 molecular sieves (HMPS) for removal of carbon dioxide. Recycling of greater than or equal to 1 flows is carried out for bottom flow (340) obtained from high-pressure gravimetric separation (HPS2) comprising predominantly carbon dioxide and head flow (350) obtained from high-pressure gravimetric separation (HPS1) comprising predominantly synthesis gases purified from carbon dioxide (purified syngas), at the step of gravimetric separation at intermediate pressure (LPS). Recycling is preceded by greater than or equal to 1 pre-treatment steps. Preferred Conditions: Compression step is carried out up to a pressure of 50-60 bar. Cooling and further cooling steps are carried out, independently from one another, to a temperature of 20-50 degrees C. Preferred Device: Devices for gravimetric separation in supercritical phase of carbon dioxide from a flow of synthesis gases are in fluid connection with each other in series or in parallel.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E35; H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide foaming material used as military materials, medical materials, aviation materials and ship materials, comprises polyethylene terephthalate, carbon dioxide blowing agent, chain extender, and nucleating agent.
AU QU J
   ZHANG X
AE JIANGSU BEITELI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202111895X
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide foaming material comprises 80-100 pts. wt. polyethylene terephthalate (PET), 1-10 pts. wt. carbon dioxide blowing agent, 0.1-4 pts. wt. chain extender, and 0.1-10 pts. wt. nucleating agent.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide foaming material used as military materials, medical materials, aviation materials, ship materials, electronic materials, automotive materials, instruments and power supplies (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing supercritical carbon dioxide foaming material, which involves:
   (A) drying the PET, the chain extender, and the nucleating agent into a twin-screw extruder for extrusion;
   (B) inject carbon dioxide blowing agent into the sleeve after plasticization, on the twin-screw extruder sleeve; and
   (C) forming the mold to obtain the supercritical carbon dioxide foam material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide blowing agent is supercritical carbon dioxide. The chain extender includes one or a combination of homoisocyanates, bisimide esters, bicyclic carboxylic acid anhydrides, dioxirane compounds, bicyclic imine ether compounds, and lactam compounds. The nucleating agent includes one or a combination of talc, calcium carbonate, silicon dioxide, alum, titanium dioxide, calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, carbon black, and mica. Preferred Conditions: The 12 temperature zones arranged in sequence during twin-screw extrusion, the temperature of one zone is 30-80 degrees C. The temperature in the second zone is 240-280 degrees C, and the temperature in the third zone is 240-280 degrees C. The temperature in zone four is 240-280 degrees C, the temperature in zone five is 240-280 degrees C, The temperature in the six zone is 240-280 degrees C, and the temperature in the seven zone is 230-260 degrees C. The temperature in the eight zones is 230-260 degrees C, the temperature in the nine zones is 230-260 degrees C, and the temperature in the ten zones is 230-260 degrees C. The temperature in zone 11 is 230-260 degrees C, and the temperature in zone 12 is 230-260 degrees C. The mold temperature is 220-280 degrees C during twin-screw extrusion, and the screw speed is 30-100 r/min.

TI Lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode material for lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode, comprises aminated graphene oxide prepared by mixing graphene oxide, an amination reagent and water, and undergoing a hydrothermal reaction.
AU LIU D
   GAO Z
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202084544L
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode material comprises aminated graphene oxide. The aminated graphene oxide is prepared by mixing graphene oxide, an amination reagent and water, and undergoing a hydrothermal reaction.
   USE - Lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode material for lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method of aminated graphene oxide in the positive electrode material of the lithium carbon dioxide battery is simple, and solves the problems of large charge-discharge potential difference and poor cycle performance of high-capacity batteries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode, which involves mixing lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode material, binder and organic solvent to obtain a slurry, coating the slurry on the surface of carbon paper, and drying at 70-90 degrees C to obtain the desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Conditions: The mass-volume ratio of graphene oxide, amination reagent and water is 80-120 mg: 20-40 ml: 5-20 ml, preferably 100 mg: 30 ml: 10 ml. The hydrothermal reaction is performed by heating the mixture at 130-150 degrees C, preferably 140 degrees C and keeping at constant temperature for 8-12 hours, preferably 10 hours for hydrothermal reaction, The mass ratio of the lithium carbon dioxide battery cathode material and the binder is 5-7:1, preferably 6:1. Preferred Components: The amination reagent is ammonia or hydrazine hydrate solution. The binder is polyvinylidene fluoride, and the organic solvent is N-methyl pyrrolidone. Preferred Process: The preparation method of aminated graphene oxide involves mixing graphene oxide, amination reagent and water for hydrothermal reaction. The product of the hydrothermal reaction is washed and dried to obtain aminated graphene oxide.

PN CN110624273-A; CN211189182-U
TI Improving the extraction rate of supercritical            carbon dioxide oil sludge, comprises passing carbon            dioxide into a compressor, adjusting the pressure to a            critical pressure, and obtaining compressed carbon            dioxide.
AU PEI X
   WANG M
   LIU M
   ZHOU Y
   LI W
AE BEIJING HERON ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020026501
AB    NOVELTY - Improving the extraction rate of supercritical                carbon dioxide oil sludge, comprises passing carbon                dioxide into a compressor, adjusting the pressure                to a critical pressure, and obtaining compressed                carbon dioxide. The compressed carbon dioxide and                entrainer enter the preheating kettle for                preheating respectively, and the preheating is                above the critical temperature of carbon dioxide.                The sludge is dehydrated and placed in the                extraction kettle. The supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid carrying the entrainer is passed from the                bottom of the extraction kettle to the extraction                kettle. The oil in the sludge is extracted by the                supercritical fluid, and the fluid is extracted                from the extraction kettle upper outflow. The                supercritical fluid containing oil flows out of the                extraction kettle, and is depressurized by a                pressure reducing valve at the inlet of the                gas-liquid separation tank.
   USE - Method for improving the extraction rate of                supercritical carbon dioxide oil sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve the extraction                rate of supercritical carbon dioxide oil sludge,                which solves the problems of high cost and high                energy consumption in the conventional extraction                method, and achieves the effects of reducing the                extraction cost, reducing energy consumption and                increasing the extraction rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving the extraction rate of supercritical                carbon dioxide oil sludge, comprises passing carbon                dioxide into a compressor, adjusting the pressure                to a critical pressure, and obtaining compressed                carbon dioxide. The compressed carbon dioxide and                entrainer enter the preheating kettle for                preheating respectively, and the preheating is                above the critical temperature of carbon dioxide.                The sludge is dehydrated and placed in the                extraction kettle. The supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid carrying the entrainer is passed from the                bottom of the extraction kettle to the extraction                kettle. The oil in the sludge is extracted by the                supercritical fluid, and the fluid is extracted                from the extraction kettle upper outflow. The                supercritical fluid containing oil flows out of the                extraction kettle, and is depressurized by a                pressure reducing valve at the inlet of the                gas-liquid separation tank, and then enters the                gas-liquid separation tank for gas-liquid                separation to obtain an oil-water mixture and a                normal temperature and pressure carbon dioxide gas.                The oil-water mixture enters the oil-water                separation tank for oil-water separation. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for                improving supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                sludge extraction rate, which comprises a                compressor, a preheating kettle, a high-pressure                metering device, an extraction kettle, a gas-liquid                separation tank, and an oil-water separation tank,                which are sequentially arranged, and there is also                a dewatering device and an electric degreaser, the                dehydration device is connected to the extraction                kettle, the feed port of the electric degreaser is                connected to the gas-liquid separation tank outlet,                and the outlet of the electric degreaser is                connected to the compressor inlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The entrainer is one                  or more of methanol, ethanol, acetone, ethyl                  acetate, n-pentane and the like.

AU RIBEIRO LIMA P C
AE RIBEIRO L P C (RIBE-Individual)
GA 201909994E
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises separator of fluids is produced at high pressure, porgas is produced with low concentration of contaminants and high concentration of methane by a liquid stream with a high concentration of carbon dioxide and segregated in the separator of fluids produced (105) at high pressure by a production of gasses produced which transported them to a maritime production unit by a pipeline of produced liquids which transported them to the production unit of production by a maritime unit of stationary production and installed near the surface of the sea (101), where a processing unit is installed for all gasses produced and a compressing unit for the gasses produced by export pipelines.
   USE - System for promoting segregation of contaminants as carbon dioxide from stream of fluids produced by oil reservoir.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for promoting segregation of contaminants such as carbon dioxide from a stream of fluids produced by an oil reservoir, which involves:
   (A) separating the produced fluids at high pressure for generating a stream of gasses produced with low concentration of contaminants and high concentration of methane;
   (B) generating a liquid stream with high concentration of carbon dioxide dissolved in the oil by transporting the stream of gasses produced with low concentration of contaminants and high concentration of methane to a marine unit of production;
   (C) treating by compressing and exporting the current ofgas produced with low concentration of contaminants and high concentration of methane;
   (D) transporting the liquid stream with high concentration of carbon dioxide dissolved in the oil to the maritime unit of production;
   (E) depressurising the liquid stream with high concentration of carbon dioxide dissolved in the oil along the upward flow of the oil and desorbing the carbon dioxide;
   (F) generating a gaseous stream rich in carbon dioxide and an oil stream with low concentration of carbon dioxide;
   (G) separating the low-pressure carbon dioxide-rich gas stream from the oil with low concentration of carbon dioxide; by compressing the gaseous stream rich in carbon dioxide;
   (H) mixing a fraction of the carbon dioxide rich gas stream with injection water by dissolving carbon dioxide in the injection water for generating a stream of carbonated water;
   (I) generating a low-carbon stream of carbon dioxide to place carbonated water in oil or aquifer reservoirs;
   (J) dehydrating the gaseous stream with low concentration of carbon dioxide by exporting or injecting gaseous stream with low concentration of carbon dioxide; and
   (K) transporting a fraction of the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream to a point below sea level using this fraction for pneumatic lifting of the liquid stream exiting the fluid separator at high pressure to the off shore unit of production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of system.
   Sea (101)
   Fluids produced (105)
   Oil produced (108)
   Mixing pipe (111)
   Check valve (114)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The gas stream is used with low concentration of carbon dioxide to assist in the pneumatic lifting of the liquid phase leaving the separator of fluids produced at high pressure, and installed below sea level, followed by pumping the liquid phase leaving the separator of fluids produced at high pressure to replace or supplement the pneumatic lift, controlling the flow rate of the pneumatic lifting gas to allow a pressure in the separator at a lower pressure which is equal to or greater than that which would be sea bottom pressure of a conventional production system. The partial segregation of the gas phase from the liquid phase in the fluid separator is produced at high pressure to promote a gas-liquid two-phase flow in the pipeline which is used to transport oil produced by separator to the separation vessel of the oil at low pressure and installed in the marine production unit, and reduced the specific gravity and pressure gradient. The gasses produced and liquids produced are produced by annular spaces formed from the installation of pipes installed inside one another, desulphurising both the produced gasses which are segregated in the fluid separator produced at high pressure and the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream before proceeding for export or injection. The contaminants are removed from the carbon dioxide rich stream by conventional technologies such as permeation through degassing membrane which is used to diffuse the carbon dioxide in the absorption liquids. The catalyst is used to accelerate the dissolution process of the contaminants in the adsorbing liquids. The hydrogen sulfide in the seawater and treated that water with possible reduction of the acidity and treated, before discarded. The method further involves transporting all or fraction of the acarbonate and the gas streams produced to other producing areas, injecting into other aquifers or reservoirs, or transporting to other consumption units, mixing or pressurizing venturis, gas lift valves, flet pump pumps, membranes or any other type of similar equipment in order to increase the area of contact of the contaminants with the liquid, dissolving and reducing the slip between the phases in the pipe, where the pneumatic elevation of the liquid phase occurs, dehydrating the gaseous phases, before unsealing for export or injection separately from the injection water into the reservoir or aquifers, dissolving the hydrogen sulfide in the injection vessel to the carbon dioxide, dissolving the carbon dioxide in any other type of oily or aqueous solution, dissolving it in the water of injection by using a porous matrix including catalyst, immobilizing on a solid support and controlling thecorrosion and forming deposits and hydrates.

PT P
PN CN111875808-A; CN111875808-B
TI Carbon dioxide assisted preparation of            nano-zeolite imidazole framework material for            catalyzing photodegradation of organic dyes,            introducing carbon dioxide, introducing and removing            carbon dioxide to make zeolitic imidazolate            framework.
AU ZHAN G
   ZENG X
   CHEN B
   XIAO J
   ZHOU S
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 2020B2258C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for carbon dioxide assisted preparation                of nano-zeolite imidazole framework material by                introducing carbon dioxide during the ZIFs                synthesis process, introducing and removing the                carbon dioxide to make the zeolitic imidazolate                frameworks (ZIFs) undergo structural transformation                during the process of dissolution and                recrystallization to prepare nano-zeolite imidazole                framework materials, which are recorded as nano                ZIFs, and introducing and removing the carbon                dioxide gas by mixing the metal salt and the                organic ligand to produce precipitation, where the                carbon dioxide gas with a pressure of 1-50 bar is                introduced for 10-120 minutes, removing the carbon                dioxide gas and stirring the reaction for 10-120                minutes.
   USE - The nano-zeolite imidazole framework material                is used for catalyzing the photodegradation of                organic dyes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is short in time, simple in                operation, excellent in repeatability, economical                and environmentally-friendly in the synthesis                process, and the prepared nano ZIFs has small                particle size, large specific surface area, high                crystallinity, excellent thermal stability and high                purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves (a)                  dissolving the metal salt in an alcohol solvent,                  and stirring until completely dissolved to obtain                  solution (A), dissolving the organic ligand in an                  alcohol solvent, adding lye, and stirring until                  it is completely dissolved to obtain solution                  (B), and mixing solution (A) and solution (B) to                  obtain turbid liquid with white solid                  precipitation, and (b) pouring carbon dioxide gas                  into the turbid liquid and waiting until the                  turbid liquid turns into a clear solution,                  removing the carbon dioxide gas and stirring to                  make the clear solution gradually produce milky                  white solid products, recovering the solid                  product by centrifugation, washing and drying to                  obtain nano ZIFs. The method involves washing the                  solid product recovered by centrifugation for 3-5                  times with ethanol, and drying in an oven at                  55-65 degrees C for 12-14 hours to obtain nano                  ZIFs. Preferred Components: The metal salt                  comprises anhydrous zinc acetate, zinc acetate                  dihydrate, zinc nitrate anhydrous, zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate, zinc sulfate anhydrous, zinc sulfate                  heptahydrate, cobalt acetate anhydrous, cobalt                  acetate tetrahydrate, cobalt nitrate anhydrous,                  cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt sulfate                  anhydrous and/or cobalt sulfate heptahydrate.                  Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the                  metal salt to the organic ligand is 1:(1-8), and                  the molar ratio of the alkali solution to the                  organic ligand is 1:(1-5). The Brunauer, Emmett                  and Teller (BET) specific surface area of                  nano-scale ZIFs particles is 1180-1190 m2/g and                  pore diameter is not greater than 2 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solvent                  comprises methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol,                  n-propanol, isopropanol and/or propylene glycol.                  The organic ligand comprises 1-methylimidazole,                  2-methylimidazole, 1,2-methylimidazole,                  2,4-methylimidazole, 2-formylimidazole,                  1-ethylimidazole and/or 2-ethyl imidazole. The                  lye comprises ethylenediamine, triethylamine,                  propylamine, n-butylamine, tert-butylamine,                  ammonia, sodium hydroxide and/or potassium                  hydroxide.

TI Capturing carbon dioxide produced from fluid catalytic cracker regenerator flue gas involves operating crossflow membrane at vacuum permeate pressure to remove bulk of carbon dioxide followed by membrane which operates with counter-current air sweep.
AU CHINN D
   BHUWANIA N
   HE A Z
AE CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
   CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
GA 202250922M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves operating a crossflow membrane at vacuum permeate pressure to remove the bulk of the carbon dioxide, followed by a membrane which operates with a counter-current air sweep on the permeate side. The two stage compression of permeate and liquefying carbon dioxide using a carbon dioxide liquefaction unit that liquefies carbon dioxide out the bottom and produces carbon dioxide-rich vapor and recycling permeate of polishing membrane. The crossflow membrane and polishing membrane are hollow fiber membranes selected from the group consisting of polypropylene, polyethylene, polytetrafluoroethylene , polyvinylidene difluoride, polysulfone, PEBAX, PDMS, polyimide, carbon molecular sieve, graphene oxide, mixed matrix, zeolites, metal organic frameworks and polyetherimide.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide produced from a fluid catalytic crack regenerator (FCC) regenerator flue gas in a refinery-based combustion system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the input of energy required to capture carbon dioxide from the fluid catalytic cracker (FCC) regenerator using select membranes. The method controls carbon dioxide emissions from combustion processes and streams, and eliminates the use of a polishing membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a membrane process may be integrated with a FCC regenerator.

PT P
PN CN112679322-A; CN112679322-B
TI Preparation of 2,6-dihydroxytoluene involves            adding 2,6-diaminotoluene and phosphoric acid aqueous            solution in reactor, stirring, heating, hydrolyzing            under supercritical carbon dioxide, cooling,            extracting, and concentrating.
AU PAN Z
   YE J
   ZHENG X
   LI L
   WANG L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202145716X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 2,6-dihydroxytoluene method                involves (1) adding 2,6-diaminotoluene and                phosphoric acid aqueous solution in reactor,                stirring and mixing uniformly, using carbon dioxide                to completely replace the air in the reactor, and                then pressurizing the carbon dioxide to the reactor                until reaches certain pressure, closing the reactor                and stopping the input of carbon dioxide into the                reactor, (2) heating the reactor containing the                reaction liquid materials in step (1) to the                reaction temperature, the carbon dioxide in the                reactor forms a supercritical state, and the                co-thermal catalysis of carbon dioxide and using                phosphoric acid to make 2,6-diaminotoluene undergo                a hydrolysis to obtain 2,6-dihydroxytoluene, (3)                completing the reaction in step (2), cooling,                recovering carbon dioxide in reactor for repeated                recycling, discharging the reactor, extracting the                reacted materials and separating using organic                extractant, evaporating the organic phase and                concentrating to remove solvent.
   USE - Preparation of 2,6-dihydroxytoluene catalyzed                by supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical carbon dioxide                and phosphoric acid to co-catalyze the preparation                of 2,6-dihydroxytoluene. The carbon dioxide is                recovered and recycled, and the amount of inorganic                acid is greatly reduced, which reduces                environmental pollution and manufacturing                cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  the phosphoric acid aqueous solution is 10-20                  %mass, and the molar ratio of 2,6-diaminotoluene                  to phosphoric acid is 1:1.5-4 in step (1). The                  molar ratio of 2,6-diaminotoluene to the water in                  the phosphoric acid aqueous solution is 1:50-100                  in step (1). The reaction kettle containing the                  reaction liquid materials in step (1) is closed.                  The pressure in the reaction kettle is 4-26 MPa,                  the reaction temperature is 180-240 degrees C,                  and the reaction time is 2.0-6.0 hours, and                  diaminotoluene is hydrolyzed into                  2,6-dihydroxytoluene under the co-thermal                  catalysis of carbon dioxide and phosphoric acid                  aqueous solution. The reaction temperature in the                  reaction kettle is 220 degrees C, and the                  reaction time is 6 hours. Preferred Process: The                  specific process of recovering carbon dioxide in                  the reactor is an exhaust valve is provided on                  the top of the reactor, and the exhaust valve is                  connected to a gas collection device through a                  pipe, the reactor is cooled to below 20 degrees C                  to convert carbon dioxide from supercritical                  state to gaseous state, and then open the exhaust                  valve on the top of the reactor to make gaseous                  carbon dioxide enter the gas collection device                  through the pipeline for recovery, and the                  recovered carbon dioxide is reused recycling in                  the reactor. Preferred Components: The organic                  extractant is ethyl acetate.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN111672505-A
TI Silver-based carbon dioxide reduction catalyst is used in electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction, comprises silver oxide nanoparticles which are supported on the surface of the electrode, carbon powder is activated carbon.
AU ZHOU W
   WU X
   SUN Z
   XIE F
   GUAN D
   GUO Y
   SHAO Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202093735G
AB    NOVELTY - Silver-based carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises silver oxide nanoparticles which are supported on the surface of the electrode.
   USE - Silver-based carbon dioxide reduction catalyst is used in electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The silver-based carbon dioxide reduction catalyst has faraday efficiency of nearly 100% for the production of carbon monoxide, simple synthesis method, certain industrial value, and alleviates current energy and environmental problems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing silver-based carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, which involves stirring silver nitrate aqueous solution and sodium hydroxide aqueous solution rapidly at room temperature to produce silver oxide precipitate, obtaining resulting precipitate by centrifugation or suction filtration, and then drying to get silver oxide powder, mixing above-mentioned silver oxide powder, carbon powder, organic solvent, activated carbon, and binder ultrasonically to form uniform ink-like slurry, dripping slurry on surface of electrode and air-drying naturally as cathode working electrode; and
   (2) a method for using silver-based carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, which involves constructing three-electrode system to form electrochemical cell, silver oxide quickly forming metallic silver nanocatalyst in situ when negative potential is applied, conducting electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction reaction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon powder is activated carbon. The organic solvent is alcohol solvent. The alcohol solvent is ethanol or isopropanol. The adhesive is nafion solution, polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) adhesive or polyvinylidene fluoride adhesive. The electrode is glassy carbon electrode. Preferred Conditions: The size range of the silver oxide nanoparticles is 50-500 nanometer. The electrode refers to carbon glass electrode. The specific surface area of the activated carbon is 50-300m2/g, and particle size is 10-60 nanometer. The mass ratio of silver oxide powder and carbon powder is 1:(0.2-3). The ratio of silver oxide powder, organic solvent and binder is 10mg:(0.8-1.5)mL:(80-150 L). The silver-silver chloride is the reference electrode, and platinum sheet is the counter electrode. The potassium bicarbonate is used as electrolyte in electrochemical cell.

PT P
PN CN113856632-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent with imitated core-shell structure, comprises e.g. adding stabilizing agent into de-ionized water, heating and stirring, filtering, washing, drying to obtain carbon dioxide adsorbent precursor D and spraying solution B on carbon dioxide adsorbent precursor D.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHAO Z
   ZHANG S
   WANG F
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202210787K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent with imitated core-shell structure, comprises (i) adding the stabilizing agent into the de-ionized water to heat and stirring to obtain solution A; (ii) adding the active agent into the de-ionized water, heating and stirring to obtain solution B; (iii) adding the adsorbent to the solution A to obtain solution C; (iv) adding the carrier material into the solution C, soaking for 5-24 hours, filtering, washing, drying to obtain carbon dioxide adsorbent precursor D; (v) spraying the solution B on carbon dioxide adsorbent precursor D, drying for 2-12 hours (preferably 2-6 hours) at 60-110 degrees Celsius, preferably 80-110 degrees Celsius.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent with imitated core-shell structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has double adsorption effect of surface shell layer and hole inner core layer, large carbon dioxide adsorption capacity and ensures carbon dioxide absorption amount can 4.2-8.6 mmol/g adsorbent under the condition of low temperature and normal pressure, which is a carbon dioxide high-efficiency adsorption material with very industrial prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide adsorbent with imitated core-shell structure prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stabilizer is disodium edetate and sodium thiosulfate, and its mass concentration in solution C is 0.5-1.5%, preferably 1.2%. The active trapping agent is monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, piperazine and/or piperidine, and its mass concentration in solution B is 15-30%, preferably 25%.    ADVANTAGE - The system is highly desirable to remove carbon dioxide from aquacultural water and prevent or reduce emission of biogenic carbon dioxide based on both economic and environmental considerations.

TI Carbon dioxide blasting hole wall impact pressure time estimation method, involves establishing carbon dioxide blasting wall impact pressure-time curve equation, and drawing carbon dioxide blasting hole wall impact pressure-time curve.
AU LI H
   WANG B
   LI X
   LIU L
   WU D
AE INST ROCK & SOIL MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD (CRSM-C)
GA 202103868Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves manufacturing a batch of cement mortar blasting test component (100) with a drilling hole by using sets of different cement mortar matching ratio. Density of the cement mortar blasting test component is measured. The cement mortar blasting test component is cracked by using a small carbon dioxide gas explosion device (300). A statistical relationship between a radial main crack number experiment value and a radial main crack number theoretical value is established. Radial main crack number experiment values are counted by using a carbon dioxide blasting cartridge to crack a rock to be excavated. A carbon dioxide blasting hole wall impact pressure-time curve equation is established. A corresponding carbon dioxide blasting hole wall impact pressure-time curve is drawn.
   USE - Carbon dioxide blasting hole wall impact pressure time estimation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing difficulty of measuring impact pressure and boosting time of the drilling wall by an in-situ test and a large volume concrete model test, and estimating the carbon dioxide blasting hole wall impact pressure time with low test cost, a small danger coefficient and high success rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a small carbon dioxide gas explosion device.

TI Recovering flue gas carbon dioxide and urea by ammonia method comprises absorbing carbon dioxide in the flue gas to form a solution of ammonium bicarbonate, and sending to a urea production unit for urea production.
AU LI Y
   WU K
   ZHANG X
   SHI F
   LI B
   WANG S
   XU H
   ZHANG Y
AE JINXI NATURAL GAS CHEM ENG CO LTD (CHNI-C)
GA 201939967Q
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering flue gas carbon dioxide and urea by ammonia method comprises absorbing carbon dioxide in the flue gas to form a solution of ammonium bicarbonate, and sending to a urea production unit for urea production.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering flue gas carbon dioxide and urea by ammonia method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the analysis and compression of carbon dioxide and reduces energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ammonium bicarbonate is delivered to a low pressure, medium pressure or high pressure system in a urea production unit for urea production. The flue gas is cooled to 40 degrees C by the cooling scrubber, pressurized by the flue gas fan and sent to the lower part of the absorption tower, countercurrent contact with the ammonium carbonate liquid with high ammonia content in the absorption section of the absorption tower, recovering the carbon dioxide in the flue gas, to obtain solution of ammonium carbonate having a lower ammonia content, the flue gas is subjected to carbon dioxide absorption by the absorption section of the absorption tower, countercurrent contact with the condensate added in the washing section of the absorption tower, and the ammonia is recovered and discharged to the atmosphere, the obtained ammonium carbonate liquid having a lower ammonia content, contact with the ammonia added at the bottom of the absorption tower to form a carbon ammonium liquid solution liquid having a higher ammonia content, kettle liquid is pumped and a part is sent to the urea, the production unit is returned to the absorption section of the absorption tower for recycling. The concentration of ammonia in the ammonium carbonate solution having high ammonia content is 30% or more. The water cooler is arranged at the outlet of the kettle liquid pump, and the kettle liquid returning to the upper part of the absorption tower is cooled by a water cooler.

PN CN107673351-A; CN107673351-B
TI Preparing high-purity carbon dioxide involves compressing combustible carbon dioxide gas containing impurities such as methane, ethylene, benzene, and mechanical water, and oxygen with excessive chemical reaction go through water separator.
AU ZHU E
   ZHONG J
   LI D
AE HUIZHOU KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018145310
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity carbon dioxide involves compressing combustible carbon dioxide gas containing impurities such as methane, ethylene, benzene, and mechanical water, and oxygen with excessive chemical reaction go through the water separator and mechanical water and entering compression process. The pressure is increased to 0.1-0.35 by the primary compressor, secondary compressor boost to 0.8-1.2MPa and three-stage compressor boost to 2.3-2.8MPa, compressed carbon dioxide feedstock gas is transferred to the dehydrocarbonation process.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-purity carbon dioxide which improves purity of the raw material to 99.95%, purifying tower the purity is improves to 99.999%, through the secondary purifying process, reduces the consumption of raw materials and improves the yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-purity carbon dioxide involves compressing combustible carbon dioxide gas containing impurities such as methane, ethylene, benzene, and mechanical water, and oxygen with excessive chemical reaction go through the water separator and mechanical water and entering compression process. The pressure is increased to 0.1-0.35 by the primary compressor, secondary compressor boost to 0.8-1.2MPa and three-stage compressor boost to 2.3-2.8MPa, compressed carbon dioxide feedstock gas is transferred to the dehydrocarbonation process. The dehydrocarbonation process compressed carbon dioxide feedstock gas containing methane, ethylene and benzene impurities and excess chemical oxygen entered dehydrocarbonization process. The methane, ethylene and excess chemical oxygen in the dehydrocarbonation reactor at temperature of 480-520 degrees C, pressures of 2.3-2.8 MPa, platinum (Pt) and palladium act as active components, and catalysts containing alumina as catalysts, generate carbon dioxide and water, removed carbon dioxide from methane and ethylene, and removed hydrocarbons. The device discharges carbon dioxide gas containing traces of methane, ethylene, benzene and other impurities from the compression process and joins tube process, then heat exchanged through a heat exchanger, cooled by a water cooler and separated by a water separator to removed mechanical water. The purification process adopts the variable temperature adsorption process when multiple adsorption towers are switched, when multiple adsorption towers are in the adsorption state, other adsorption towers are in the regeneration state. The adsorption tower adsorbed impurities at room temperature, desorption impurities at high temperature. The alumina and silica gel adsorbents are filled in the adsorption tower are traces of PPM-grade traces of water vapor and PPB-grade traces from carbon dioxide feedstock gasses containing traces of methane, ethylene, oxygen, benzene and water vapor impurities from the dehydrocarbonation. The benzene impurities are selectively adsorbed, carbon dioxide gas containing trace amounts of methane, ethylene, and oxygen impurities is transferred to a liquefaction process and a distillation purification. The portion of the carbon dioxide gas containing impurities from the purification process is passed as a heat source gas through the orifice flow meter to the inlet of the crude purification column of the distillation purification process and the tower reactor coil of the refined purification column to provide product liquid carbon dioxide distillation purification. The required heat source is discharged from the outlet of the tower drum coil, and after the other portion of the carbon dioxide gas containing impurities from the purification process flows out of the orifice valve control valve, and two parts converge. The evaporative condenser entering the liquefaction process is liquefied, most of the carbon dioxide gas containing impurities is liquefied, and transferred distillation purification process together with uncondensed carbon dioxide gas and trace amounts of methane, ethylene, and oxygen. The liquefied carbon dioxide containing uncondensed carbon dioxide gas and trace amounts of methane, ethylene, and oxygen from the liquefaction step is first subjected to distillation purification in a crude purification column of a distillation purification process, and crude purification column is top-down. The top-column tube heat exchangers, nozzles, single-layer corrugated structured packing and multiple tower-coil tubes.
TF Full Definitions: The spray head is connected to the evaporative condenser of the liquefaction process, containing uncondensed carbon dioxide gas and traces of methane, ethylene and oxygen. The liquefied carbon dioxide is sprayed from the showerhead to the bottom of the crude purification tower. The tower reactor coil tube is immersed in the liquefied carbon dioxide. The temperature of the liquefied carbon dioxide is -21-23 degrees C, and the tower still flows through the distillation in the tower drum coil. The temperature of the carbon dioxide gas is 29-30 degrees C. The liquefied carbon dioxide is heated and volatilizes. The mixed gas of volatile carbon dioxide gas and hydrocarbon impurities passes through a single layer of corrugated structured packing and the liquefied carbon dioxide sprayed by the showerhead, heat exchanged, partial volatilization of the mixed gas liquefy and fall back to the bottom of the crude purification tower, another portion of the mixed gas continues to rise to the tower top tube heat exchanger, and pressure of mixed gas is 2.3-0.8Mpa through pressure reducing valve, its temperature drops to -43-47 degrees C, heat exchanged with rising mixture gas, cooling up mixed gas. The carbon dioxide gas is preferentially condensed and recovered, hydrocarbon impurity gas continues to rise, and the liquefied carbon dioxide containing uncondensed carbon dioxide gas and trace amounts of methane, ethylene, and oxygen is completely purified from the liquefaction process, and bottom of the crude purified column is liquefied. The carbon dioxide is passed into the refined purification tower. The liquefied carbon dioxide contains trace amounts of methane and ethylene, and the refined purification tower is provided with a lower layer tube heat exchanger, an upper layer tube heat exchanger, and an upper layer ripple from top to bottom. Plate structured packing, nozzle, lower structured packing of corrugated plate, and multiple tower reactor coils, the number of tower reactor coils in the refining tower is greater than the number of tower reactor coils in the crude purification tower, and the liquefied carbon dioxide is sprayed from the shower nozzle to the fine at the bottom of the purifying tower, multiple tower drum coils are immersed in liquefied carbon dioxide liquid, and multiple tower drum coils are passed into the heat source gas, and the liquefied carbon dioxide is heated to form traces containing mixed gas of alkane and ethylene, mixed gas volatilizes and rises, and the liquefied carbon dioxide that is sprayed downwards exchanges heat with the structured packing of the lower corrugated plate. The portion of the carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is condensed back to the bottom of the tower, and rest of the mixed gas rises to the upper corrugated plate with structured packing, portion of carbon dioxide is condensed back to the bottom of the tower, and the remaining mixed gas rises through the lower layer tubular heat exchanger to exchange heat, portion the carbon dioxide gas condenses and liquefies, and the other portion of the mixed gas rises into the upper row tubular heat exchanger, upper heat exchange The refrigerant of the device is liquid ammonia, liquid ammonia is stored in the shell side of the upper-row tubular heat exchanger. The mixed gas exchanges heat with the liquid ammonia, gaseous carbon dioxide condenses and liquefies.

PN CN114984725-A
TI Copper(II) oxide-KIT-6 catalyzed desorption of            carbon dioxide in amine solvent, comprises e.g.            absorbing carbon dioxide in monoethanolamine solution,            titrating and analyzing amount of absorbed carbon            dioxide, after absorbed carbon dioxide reaches            saturation and becomes a rich amine solution.
AU LI C
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG Y
   LI T
   ZHANG R
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 2022B8186K
AB    NOVELTY - Copper(II) oxide-KIT-6 catalyzed desorption of                carbon dioxide in amine solvent, comprises (1)                absorbing carbon dioxide in monoethanolamine                solution, titrating and analyzing the amount of                absorbed carbon dioxide, after absorbed carbon                dioxide reaches saturation and becomes a rich amine                solution, adding copper(II) oxide-KIT-6 catalyst to                amine-rich solution, desorbing the adsorbed carbon                dioxide by passing nitrogen gas at 100 &#8451;,                desorption time is 2 hours, cooling desorbed lean                amine solution to room temperature, titrating                residual carbon dioxide absorption, re-introducing                carbon dioxide for absorption, so cycle absorption                and desorption of carbon dioxide for 5 times,                desorption energy consumption only increased by                6.57%, indicating that the copper(II) oxide-KIT-6                catalyst still maintains a good catalytic                desorption effect.
   USE - The method is useful for copper(II)                oxide-KIT-6 catalyzed desorption of carbon dioxide                in amine solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: used catalyst is simple and                economical, state of catalyst is relatively stable                and it can be better mixed with amine solvent; and                used catalyst has good cycle stability and has no                effect on the CO2 absorption performance of the                amine solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Copper(II) oxide-KIT-6 catalyzed desorption of                carbon dioxide in amine solvent, comprises (1)                absorbing carbon dioxide in 5 mol/l                monoethanolamine solution at 40&#176;C for 10                hours, titrating and analyzing the amount of                absorbed carbon dioxide , after absorbed carbon                dioxide reaches saturation and becomes a rich amine                solution, adding 0.5-1.25 wt.% copper(II)                oxide-KIT-6 catalyst to amine-rich solution,                desorbing the adsorbed carbon dioxide by passing                nitrogen gas at 100 &#8451;, desorption time is                2 hours, cooling desorbed lean amine solution to                room temperature, titrating residual carbon dioxide                absorption, re-introducing carbon dioxide for                absorption, so cycle absorption and desorption of                carbon dioxide for 5 times, desorption energy                consumption only increased by 6.57%, indicating                that the copper(II) oxide-KIT-6 catalyst still                maintains a good catalytic desorption effect; the                preparation of copper(II) oxide-KIT-6 catalyst                comprises supporting metal oxide copper(II) oxide                on the hydrothermally synthesized KIT-6 molecular                sieve by impregnation process, the specifically:                adding template agent P123 and HCl (hydrochloric                acid) solution into the reactor, stirring and                dissolving at 35&#176;C for 4 hours, adding                BuOH (n-butanol) solution, stirring and dissolving                for 1 hour, adding TEOS (ethyl orthosilicate)                solution, stirring and dissolvin for 24 hours,                forming mixed solution A, where the molar ratio of                TEOS:P123:HCl:H2O:BuOH=1:0.017:1.83:195:1.31;                hydrothermally reacting the mixed solution A in                polytetrafluoroethylene reactor at                100-110&#176;C for 24 hours, cooling, carrying                suction filtration to obtain precipitate B, drying                the precipitate B at 80-110&#176;C for 12-48                hours, calcining at 500-550&#8451; for 3-6                hours, obtaining the KIT-6 molecular sieve of                hydrothermal synthesis; mixing 0.1-1 mol/l copper                nitrate solution with molecular sieve according to                the solid-liquid ratio of 1:10-20, stirring and                immersing at 40&#176;C for 12 hours, heating to                80&#176;C and evaporating for 3 hours,                transferring to the oven and drying at                60-100&#8451; for 8 hours, calcining at                350-550&#8451; for 3-6 hours, obtaining                copper(II) oxide-KIT-6 catalyst.

TI Printing and dyeing pulsed supercritical carbon dioxide comprises pressurizing the liquid carbon dioxide, heating and converting into supercritical carbon dioxide and mixing supercritical carbon dioxide with three primary colors of dyes.
AU YANG J
AE SHANGHAI FULUDI FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI FULUDI FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020339005
AB    NOVELTY - Printing and dyeing pulsed supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (i) pressurizing the liquid carbon dioxide, heating processing, and converting into supercritical carbon dioxide, (ii) mixing supercritical carbon dioxide with three primary colors of dyes in a certain proportion in a dye tank to form dyeing solution, (iii) injecting the dye liquor into the dyeing kettle from the top and/or bottom of the dyeing kettle using a pulse injector, dyeing the spindle from the feed end under the action of ultrasonic generator and bidirectional circulation pump and sent from the end of the spindle after dyeing, and (iv) returning the dye liquor discharged from the dye kettle to the dye tank for recycling; or separating and purifying the discharged dye liquor into liquid carbon dioxide and dye, it is used as the liquid carbon dioxide raw material in step (i) and the dye raw material in step (ii) for recycling or storage for use.
   USE - The method is useful for printing and dyeing pulsed supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes pulse conveying can realize the best dyeing liquid conveying status; improves the uniformity of printing and dyeing and the efficiency of printing and dyeing; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for pulsed supercritical carbon dioxide printing and dyeing device comprising carbon dioxide storage tank, three primary colors tank, dye tank, pulse generator, dyeing kettle, dye collection tank, decompression cooling unit and extraction separation unit, the carbon dioxide storage tank is connected to the dye tank through the three primary color tanks through the filter unit, the drying unit, the heating unit and the pressurizing unit through the pipeline in order to convert the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank after pressurization and heating treatment is supercritical carbon dioxide, and the supercritical carbon dioxide is mixed with the three primary color dyes in the three primary color tanks respectively in a certain proportion to form a dye liquor. The dye tank is sequentially connected to the dyeing kettle through a pipeline through an auxiliary tank, a flow meter, and a number of pulsed syringes connected in parallel, and each pulsed syringe is connected to the top and/or bottom of each dyeing kettle in turn, and the number of liquid inlet holes are arranged for feeding the dyeing liquid into the dyeing kettle of each section through the pulsed injector in a pulsed injection manner to dye the yarn spindle from the spindle end. Each section of the dyeing kettle is provided with a dual-core ultrasonic generating device and a bidirectional circulation pump. The two ends of the bidirectional circulation pump are respectively connected to the pulse injector and the dyeing kettle with a Y1-shaped interface, and the two Y1-shaped ports are respectively located at the liquid inlet holes at both ends of the bottom of the dyeing kettle, and the bottom of the dyeing kettle is connected to the dye tank through a pipe, so that the remaining dye liquor discharged from the dyeing kettle is returned to the dye tank for recycling or the bottom of the dyeing kettle is connected to the dye collection tank through a pipe to collect the remaining dyeing liquid in the dyeing kettle after being dyed in the dye collection tank, after being separated and purified into liquid carbon dioxide and dye, the liquid carbon dioxide raw material and the dye raw material are respectively transported to the carbon dioxide storage tank and the dye tank through a pipeline for recycling and stored for use by pipeline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (i), a pressurizing unit is used to increase the pressure of carbon dioxide to more than 16 MPa; and a heating unit is required to heat the temperature of carbon dioxide to more than 80 degrees C. The step (ii) includes adding a dye aid to the dyeing solution. In step (iii), the dyeing kettle is divided into at least one section along its length, and each section of the dyeing kettle is equipped with the pulse injector, ultrasonic generator, and bidirectional circulation pump. The pulse injectors are connected to many liquid inlet holes arranged in sequence on the top and/or bottom of each section of the dyeing kettle. The two ends of the bidirectional circulation pump are respectively connected to the pulse injector and the dyeing kettle with a Y1-shaped interface, and the two Y1-shaped interfaces are located at the liquid inlet holes at both ends of the bottom of each dyeing kettle. The step (iii) includes thermal insulation treatment of the dyeing kettle during the dyeing process. The bottom of the dyeing kettle is connected to the reduced-pressure cooling unit through the dye collection tank through a pipe to collect the remaining dyeing liquid after dyeing in the dyeing kettle in the dye collection tank, and decompress and separate the remaining dye liquor by the decompression cooling unit, the top of the reduced-pressure cooling unit is connected to the carbon dioxide storage tank through a purification unit, a filtration unit, and a drying unit through a pipeline in order to send the separated carbon dioxide gas into the carbon dioxide storage tank for recycling, and the bottom end of the reduced-pressure cooling unit is connected to the extraction and separation unit through a pipeline to extract the separated dye liquor, and the extracted dye is sent to the dye tank for recycling and reuse and/or the extracted auxiliary agent is sent to the auxiliary agent tank for recycling.

AB    (CN217246078-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a non-invasive                ventilation mask based on artificial intelligence,                comprising a mask main body, the mask main body is                provided with an air suction channel and an                exhaling channel, the air suction channel is                connected with an air suction valve, the exhaling                channel is connected with an expiratory valve; the                mask main body is further provided with a carbon                dioxide collecting pipe for extracting the gas in                the mask main body, the tail end of the carbon                dioxide collecting pipe is connected with a carbon                dioxide detecting device for detecting the carbon                dioxide concentration of the gas in the mask main                body, the carbon dioxide detecting device is                connected with an opening control unit for                increasing the expiration valve when the measured                carbon dioxide concentration is greater than the                preset carbon dioxide concentration threshold                value. The utility model claims a non-invasive                positive pressure ventilation mask, capable of                monitoring carbon dioxide concentration of gas in                the mask main body in real time, the control unit                adjusts the opening degree of the exhalation valve                according to the carbon dioxide concentration of                the gas in the mask, so as to adjust the carbon                dioxide concentration of the gas in the mask main                body in real time.

The mask main body is connected with a                suction valve. The expiration passage is connected                with an expiration valve. GA 202076736H
AB    NOVELTY - Gas streams production system (100) comprises a carbon dioxide capture device (110) configured to receive a carbon dioxide-containing stream and comprising an aqueous alkaline solution comprising hydroxide ions and/or carbonate ions, and an electrolyzer fluidically coupled to the device (110) defining a volume comprising an anode region (150) and cathode region (130). The volume comprises an electrolyte solution having a pH gradient generated by the application of an electric current between the electrodes, causing the electrolyte solution in the anode region to be acidic and the electrolyte solution in the cathode region to be alkaline. The device (110) generates a carbon-rich solution when the aqueous alkaline solution absorbs carbon dioxide received from the carbon dioxide-containing stream. The carbon-rich solution is received into the volume of the electrolyzer and the electrolyzer generates a hydrogen product stream, an oxygen product stream and a carbon dioxide product stream.
   USE - System for producing gas stream (claimed) used in synthetic fuel production.
   ADVANTAGE - The system produces fuel having high purity, and is economical and has thermodynamic favorability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gas streams production system comprises a carbon dioxide capture device configured to receive a carbon dioxide-containing stream and comprising an aqueous alkaline solution comprising hydroxide ions and/or carbonate ions, and an electrolyzer fluidically coupled to the carbon dioxide capture device defining a volume comprising an anode region having an anode, a cathode region having a cathode. The volume comprises an electrolyte solution having a pH gradient generated by the application of an electric current between the anode and the cathode, causing the electrolyte solution in the anode region to be acidic and the electrolyte solution in the cathode region to be alkaline. The carbon dioxide capture device generates a carbon-rich solution when the aqueous alkaline solution absorbs carbon dioxide received from the carbon dioxide-containing stream. The carbon-rich solution comprises carbonate ions and/or bicarbonate ions are received into the volume of the electrolyzer. The electrolyzer generates a hydrogen product stream, an oxygen product stream, and a carbon dioxide product stream. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) electrolyzer; and
   (2) manufacture of gas streams.

TI Plant for production of methane by anaerobic fermentation, has plant portion for production of biogas, and for producing process energy required by plant portions regenerative sources, and carbon dioxide produced in system is liquefied or permanently bound in solid.
AU 
AE TERRA MATER RENEWABLES INVESTMENTS AB (TERR-Non-standard)
GA 2021C02791
AB    NOVELTY - The plant has a plant portion for the production of biogas, for drying and cleaning the biogas, for separating the biogas into methane and carbon dioxide, for liquefying carbon dioxide and for producing the process energy required by the plant portions regenerative sources. All of the carbon dioxide produced in the system is liquefied or permanently bound in a solid. The plant has the plant portion for the production of dry ice. The waste heat from the plant portion for separating the biogas into methane and carbon dioxide and the plant portion for liquefying the carbon dioxide and the plant portion for the production of dry ice as process heat, in the plant portion for the production of biogas, is used.
   USE - Plant for production of methane by anaerobic fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant is considered to be achieved when 90% of the carbon dioxide produced is liquefied or bound in solids, or 90% of the process energy is provided from renewable sources, with electrical energy and heat being weighted in a ratio of 2-1. The systems have system portions for cooling and cleaning the hydrogen sulfide, and heat pump with which the temperature level of the waste heat from the system portions for biogas separation and liquefaction of carbon dioxide is increased. The system advantageously has a pipeline connection in order to return the methane separated in the system portion to liquefy the carbon monoxide to another system portion. The liquefied carbon dioxide replaces carbon dioxide from fossil sources in industrial processes, but does not itself contribute to increasing the carbon dioxide concentration in the atmosphere, since it is produced from regenerative substrates. The system has system portion which enables the separated carbon dioxide to be liquefied. The plant for the production of methane by anaerobic fermentation does not emit carbon dioxide, but instead provides all of the carbon dioxide produced by the plant in liquefied form or permanently bound in a solid such as calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for production of methane by anaerobic fermentation.

AE MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
GA 2020C7065W
AB    NOVELTY - Vacuum container (10) comprises an airtight container (2), and a carbon dioxide absorbent (C) and a water absorbent (W) arranged in the space (4) within the airtight container, where the carbon dioxide absorbent (C) and the water absorbent (W) are isolated in the space respectively by a membrane through which carbon dioxide and water vapor permeate and by a membrane through which water vapor permeates, and the pressure measured within the container at 25 degrees C is 3 kPa or less.
   USE - As vacuum container.
   ADVANTAGE - The container stably maintains a high internal vacuum state.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a precursor of the vacuum container, comprising at least an airtight container in which carbon dioxide and water are sealed, a carbon dioxide absorbent (C) and a water absorbent (W) arranged in the space of the container, where the absorbent (C) is isolated in the space by a membrane that permeates carbon dioxide and water vapor, and the absorbent (W) is isolated by a membrane that permeates water vapor, and where the absorbent (C) exists in an amount capable of absorbing a larger amount of carbon dioxide than the amount of carbon dioxide in the container, and the absorbent (W) exists in an amount capable of absorbing a larger amount of water than the amount of water that can exist in the container;
   (2) a method for manufacturing a vacuum container, involving (a) preparing the precursor, (b) absorbing carbon dioxide in the precursor in the absorbent (C), and (c) absorbing water in the precursor in the absorbent (W); and
   (3) a kit for forming a decompressed state, comprising a combination of carbon dioxide absorber (C) and water absorber (W), where the absorber (C) is packaged by a membrane that allows carbon dioxide and water vapor to pass through, and the absorber (W) is packaged by a membrane that allows water vapor to pass through.

   Airtight container (2)
   Space (4)
   Vacuum container (10)
   Carbon dioxide absorbent (C)
   Water absorbent (W)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vacuum container further comprises a porous body in the space, where the pressure in the void of the porous body is 3 kPa or less. The absorbent (C) is a substance that absorbs carbon dioxide by a chemical reaction. The absorbent (C) contains a metal hydroxide or an amine compound. The absorbent (W) is a metal oxide. The absorbent (C) is a substance that generates water when it absorbs carbon dioxide by the chemical reaction. Preferred Composition: The absorbent (C) contains 2-40 %mass of water with respect to the total mass of the absorbent (C). The absorbent (W) exists in an amount capable of absorbing a larger amount of water than the total amount of water generated by the chemical reaction and water existing in the space other than the water. The absorbent (C) contains X (%mass) of water with respect to the total mass of the absorbent (C), and at least one of the membrane (C1) and the membrane (W1) having a water vapor permeability T (38 degrees C, for 24 hours) that satisfies: T = less than 350x X (g/m2). Preferred Method: The step (c) includes absorbing the water generated in the carbon dioxide absorption in step (b) in the absorbent (W). Preferred Conditions: The following relationship is satisfied by the method: the rate at which carbon dioxide is absorbed in absorbent (C) less than the rate at which water is absorbed in absorbent (W).

TI Photo-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction method            involves taking carbon dioxide and cerium oxide nano,            generating carbon dioxide reduction, stirring, reacting            and carrying out photo catalytic reduction.
AU WANG F
   HOU T
   LUO N
   ZHANG J
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202011764K
AB    NOVELTY - Photo-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction                method involves taking carbon dioxide and cerium                oxide nano cluster vulcanized portions, vulcanized                indium-zinc surface catalyst and water reducing                agent, generating carbon dioxide reduction under                illumination condition to obtain carbon monoxide                product; preparing catalyst by adding zinc salt                precursor, indium salt precursor, cerium salt                precursor and surfactant into stainless steel                reaction kettle with polyfluortetraethylene inside                lining, adding solvent, stirring for more than 10                minutes to dissolve, adding 2-4 times mole zinc                salt thioacetamide, crystallizing and drying at                60-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain                catalyst; and adding catalyst and water or reaction                solvent into photo-reaction tubes with sacrificial                agent, introducing carbon dioxide under mild                conditions, reacting and carrying out photo                catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide for 1-50                hours, preferably for 10-24 hours to obtain carbon                monoxide.
   USE - Method for photo-catalytic reducing carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy and                environment-friendly, and has sustainable                development of energy strategy and wide application                prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Photo-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction                method comprises taking carbon dioxide and cerium                oxide nano cluster vulcanized portions, vulcanized                indium-zinc surface catalyst and water reducing                agent, generating carbon dioxide reduction under                illumination condition to obtain carbon monoxide                product; preparing catalyst by adding zinc salt                precursor, indium salt precursor, cerium salt                precursor and surfactant into stainless steel                reaction kettle with polyfluortetraethylene inside                lining, adding solvent, stirring for more than 10                minutes to dissolve, adding 2-4 times mole zinc                salt thioacetamide, continuously stirring for more                than 10 minutes, sealing polyfluortetraethylene                lining into stainless steel reaction kettle,                placing into oven, crystallizing, washing with                absolute ethyl alcohol and de-ionized water for 2-4                times, washing with ethanol for 1 time, placing                into vacuum drying box and drying at 60-120 degrees                C for 12-24 hours to obtain catalyst; and adding                catalyst and water or reaction solvent into                photo-reaction tubes with sacrificial agent, using                LEDs bead and/or xenon lamp as light source,                introducing carbon dioxide under mild conditions,                reacting and carrying out photo catalytic reduction                of carbon dioxide for 1-50 hours, preferably for                10-24 hours to obtain carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  crystallizing at 60-350 degrees C, preferably at                  140-180 degrees C for 6-48 hours, preferably for                  10-24 hours, and reacting at 10-80 degrees C,                  preferably at 30-60 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The zinc salt precursor is zinc                  nitrate or zinc sulfate. The indium salt                  precursor is indium chloride and/or indium                  nitrate. The cerium salt precursor is cerium                  nitrate, cerium chloride and/or cerium. The                  surface active agent is sodium dodecyl sulfate,                  polyethylene glycol and/or hexadecyltrimethyl                  ammonium chloride. The solvent is glycol, water,                  ethanol and/or methanol. The reaction solvent is                  acetonitrile and/or DMF. The sacrificial agent is                  triethylamine and/or triethanolamine. The light                  source is ED lamp and/or xenon lamp with 200-900                  nm, preferably 300-700 nm different wave band or                  constant wavelength that are based on selected                  simulating natural light. The surfactant is                  sodium dodecyl sulfate, polyethylene glycol                  and/or hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride,                  preferably polyethylene glycol or cetyl trimethyl                  ammonium chloride. The zinc salt precursor is                  0-10 mmol. The indium salt precursor is 0-20                  mmol. The cerium salt precursor is less than 0                  mmol. The catalyst is 1-50 mg catalyst/mL                  solvent.
