   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide based flameproof device.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide based flameproof device is simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for working supercritical carbon dioxide-based flameproof device, which involves charging the gas storage tank through an inflation valve carbon dioxide in the process of inflation, closing the high-speed solenoid valve, detecting the pressure in the gas storage tank by a pressure gage, monitoring the temperature in the gas storage tank and adjusting by the temperature control device, so that the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas in the gas storage tank is greater than 7.4. MPa at 32 degrees C, when the pressure and temperature in the gas storage tank reach the set value and the inflation valve is closed to make the supercritical carbon dioxide in the gas storage tank internal pressure, when an explosion accident occurs in the equipment or pipeline, transmitting the signal detector to the controller, controlling the controller to control the high-speed solenoid valve to open and opening the high-speed solenoid valve to store the gas, spraying the supercritical carbon dioxide in the tank into the equipment or pipeline through the connecting pipe A, high-speed solenoid valve and connecting pipe B, injecting the supercritical carbon dioxide into the equipment or pipeline is absorbed and rapidly expanded, controlling the high-speed solenoid valve to close and refilling the gas storage tank with carbon dioxide gas.

TI Carbon dioxide capture and release process of coupled heat recovery has using direct combustion type absorption type heat pump to replace traditional heat exchanger, facilitating transportation and storage, and regenerating absorbent.
AU MU S
AE BEIJING MINLI ENERGY STORAGE TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202246683G
AB    NOVELTY - The process comprises using direct combustion type absorption type heat pump to replace a traditional heat exchanger. A spraying device is sent into a spraying washing heat exchange tower through a circulating water pump. A spray head is uniformly sprayed into the cavity of spraying washing heat exchanger. The flue gas is discharged into a main flue. The circulating water pump is pumped into the evaporator of direct combustion type absorption heat pump. The flue gas from a flue gas condensing section main flue enters the lower part of absorption tower through a draught fan. The carbon dioxide combined in the rich liquid is released under the action of heat, and the released carbon dioxide gas stream is compressed through condensation and gas-liquid separation in turn, so as to facilitate transportation and storage. The reaction between carbon dioxide and the absorbent is exothermic reaction. The reaction will proceed in the reverse direction to regenerate the absorbent.
   USE - The process is useful for capturing and releasing carbon dioxide of coupled heat recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: relies on the Aspen Plus software; imports the physical data required for carbon dioxide capture in the pro-II software; realizes system optimization design and high-efficiency, reliable and accurate simulation analysis of the whole process; obtains the real-time physical property data of carbon dioxide; improves the accuracy of simulation; and reduces the running cost of carbon dioxide coupling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a carbon dioxide capture and release device;
   (2) a simulation method for carbon dioxide capture and release process; and
   (3) a carbon dioxide capture absorbent.

GA 202123525A

A gas-liquid separator is used for separating water and carbon dioxide. A liquid oxygen tank is used for storing liquid oxygen, and a carbon dioxide tank for storing the carbon dioxide separated by the gas-liquid separator. A carbon dioxide fuel controller is used for controlling supply of carbon dioxide to mixing chamber between the carbon dioxide tank and mixing chamber. PT P
PN CN111266000-A; CN111266000-B
TI Treating flue gas containing carbon dioxide by using microalgae comprises capturing and purifying flue gas containing carbon dioxide to obtain purified flue gas, transporting into microalgae cultivation tank to form carbon dioxide solution.
AU YANG X
   LIU Z
   HAN S
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 2020550612
AB    NOVELTY - Treating flue gas containing carbon dioxide by using microalgae comprises (i) capturing and purifying flue gas containing carbon dioxide to obtain purified flue gas, where the concentration of carbon dioxide in the purified flue gas is greater than 10%, the concentration of sulfide in the gas is less than 50 mg/m3, and the concentration of nitride is less than 200 mg/m3; (ii) transporting and distributing the purified flue gas to the microalgae cultivation tank to form carbon dioxide solution; (iii) controlling the pH value of the carbon dioxide solution in the microalgae cultivation tank, so that it is within the range suitable for microalgae carbon fixation, and then the carbon fixation by the microalgae reaches the balance of microalgae carbon fixation and oil growth.
   USE - The method is useful for treating flue gas containing carbon dioxide by using microalgae.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device, comprising: a gas-liquid separator for removing water vapor in the flue gas containing carbon dioxide, a pump to receive the gas after removing the water vapor from the gas-liquid separator, and pressurizes it, membrane separator, which desulfurizes and denitrifies the gas from the pump, and treats the purified carbon dioxide concentration in the flue gas above 10%, the concentration of sulfide in the gas is less than 50 mg/m3, the concentration of nitride is less than 200 mg/m3, and the concentration of nitrogen oxide is less than 60 ppm; the microalgae culture tank, which receives the purified flue gas from the membrane separator, and the flue gas is distributed to the microalgae cultivation tank to form a carbon dioxide solution, and the microalgae are used to fix carbon and generate grease.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device comprises a pretreatment tower which deashes flue gas containing carbon dioxide from a fixed coal-fired power plant, dehydration, desulfurization, denitrification, and demetallization to purification of flue gas; the first-stage separator, the second-stage separator, and the third-stage separator, which are connected in sequence, accept the purified flue gas from the pretreatment tower and carry out further processing to obtain liquid carbon dioxide and mixed gas; a liquefaction rectification tower, which receives liquid carbon dioxide from three stages of separation and rectification and purification to obtain food-grade liquid carbon dioxide with a concentration higher than 99.5%; microalgae cultivation tank, which receives food-grade liquid carbon dioxide from the liquefaction rectification tower, and distributes the food-grade liquid carbon dioxide into a microalgae cultivation tank to form carbon dioxide solution, and uses the microalgae in it to fix carbon and generate oil and fat.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The microalgae are Nannochloropsis, including edible-grade Nannochloropsis and non-edible-grade Nannochloropsis. The carbon dioxide solution is supplied by reverse swirl aeration, where the aeration hole of the aeration head is controlled to be 20-45 degrees included angle. The pH value is controlled by adding a slow-release agent to the carbon dioxide solution. The sustained-release agent is a diamine hydrogen phosphate and/or amine phosphate solution. The initial 3-5 days of the growth of the microalgae, the nitrogen fertilizer supplement is 100-220 mg/m2/days, and the phosphate fertilizer supplement is 50-90 mg/m2/days; 5-7 days in the middle growth period of the microalgae, nitrogen fertilizer supplementation according to 75-150 mg/m2/days, phosphate fertilizer supplementation according to 35-45 mg/m2/days; 7-12 days during the microalgae harvest period, nitrogen fertilizer supplementation according to 30-50 mg/m2/day, phosphate supplementation according to 15-25 mg/m2/day; Nannochloropsis growth rate at 5.5-12 g/m2/day; thus the oil and fat is obtained as an oil and fat composition with a concentration of 25-40%. The microalgae is food-grade Nannochloropsis. Preferred Method: The method for treating flue gas containing carbon dioxide by using microalgae includes: (i) purifying flue gas containing carbon dioxide from a coal-fired power plant to obtain purified flue gas, where the concentration of carbon dioxide in the purified flue gas is higher than 10%, the concentration of sulfide in the gas is less than 50 mg/m3, the concentration of nitride is less than 200 mg/m3, the concentration of nitrogen oxide is less than 60 ppm; (ii) liquefying the purified flue gas to obtain a liquid carbon dioxide that meets the requirements for consumption; (iii) converting the liquid carbon dioxide, transporting and distributing to the microalgae cultivation tank; (iv) controlling the pH value of the carbon dioxide solution in the microalgae cultivation tank, so that it is within the range suitable for microalgae carbon fixation, and then the carbon fixation by the microalgae finally reaches carbon fixation to balance with fat growth. In step (iii), the liquid carbon dioxide is first distributed to a carbon dioxide dissolution tank for dissolving the liquid carbon dioxide in water to obtain carbon dioxide solution; then the carbon dioxide solution is further distributed into the micrococcus algae cultivation pool. The liquid carbon dioxide is dissolved in at least one solvent in a carbon dioxide dissolution tank to obtain carbon dioxide solution, and when the carbon dioxide concentration in the carbon dioxide solution reaches 40-80 mg/l, dissolved when the oxygen concentration reaches 3-4 mg/l, the carbon dioxide solution is supplied to the microalgae. The purification treatment in step (ii) includes desulfurization, denitrification, demetallization treatment, and liquefaction rectification treatment. The liquefaction rectification treatment includes using three-stage compression and three-stage fractional liquefaction to obtain liquid carbon dioxide having a concentration higher than 99.5%.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN109507357-A; CN109507357-B
TI Device for testing high-temperature carbonate calcining reaction characteristic under alternating heating mode, has de-dusting and power system for feeding carbon dioxide, and data collecting control system for collecting parameter data.
AU LI M
   TANG S
   YU Y
   LIU Z
   TAO Y
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2019285653
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cylindrical-shaped test section (1) horizontally arranged for performing particle bed reaction. An alternating heating system (2) is placed with the test section for performing alternating heating process. A carbon dioxide pre-heating system (10) is connected with an end of the test section for heating carbon dioxide entering from the end into the test section. A cooling system (14) is connected with another end of the test section for cooling the carbon dioxide entering from the end into the test section. A de-dusting and power system is connected between the carbon dioxide cooling system and the carbon dioxide pre-heating system for feeding the carbon dioxide after heating and cooling the carbon dioxide. A data collecting control system (9) collects environment parameter data in reaction process.
   USE - Device for testing high-temperature carbonate calcining reaction characteristic under an alternating heating mode.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can test carbonate high-temperature calcining reaction characteristic of a burning section under different alternating heating modes, and calcine circulating working substance at high-temperature to generate carbon dioxide, and realize carbon dioxide recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for testing high-temperature calcining reaction characteristic of carbonate under an alternating heating mode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device for testing high-temperature carbonate calcining reaction characteristic under an alternating heating mode. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Cylindrical-shaped test section (1)
   Alternating heating system (2)
   Data collecting control system (9)
   Carbon dioxide pre-heating system (10)
   Cooling system (14)

PN US2018036681-A1; US10532317-B2
TI Carbon dioxide removal system, comprises carbon            dioxide absorber configured to receive treated flue gas            from treatment system to produce carbon dioxide-lean            treated flue gas, and metal removal system fluidly            coupled to solvent circuit.
AU REDDY S
   YONKOSKI J
AE FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
GA 201813614R
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide removal system comprises a                carbon dioxide absorber that is fluidly coupled via                a solvent circuit to a regenerator. The absorber is                configured to receive a treated flue gas (1) from a                treatment system and to produce a carbon                dioxide-lean treated flue gas, a metal removal                system (5) fluidly coupled to the solvent circuit,                comprising a metal ion chelator chemically bonded                to a solid phase and is configured to receive a                metal containing amine solvent from the solvent                circuit and to provide a purified amine solvent to                the solvent circuit.
   USE - Carbon dioxide removal system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide removal system comprises a                carbon dioxide absorber that is fluidly coupled via                a solvent circuit to a regenerator. The absorber is                configured to receive a treated flue gas from a                treatment system and to produce a carbon                dioxide-lean treated flue gas, a metal removal                system fluidly coupled to the solvent circuit,                comprising a metal ion chelator chemically bonded                to a solid phase and present in an amount to reduce                ionic metals in the amine solvent flowing through                the solvent circuit 1 mg/l or less, and is                configured to receive a metal containing amine                solvent from the solvent circuit and to provide a                purified amine solvent to the solvent                circuit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                carbon dioxide capture plant.
   Flue gas (1,3)
   Capture plant (2)
   Metals (4)
   Metal removal system (5)
   Purified solvent (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal containing                  amine solvent contains dissolved metals. The                  metal ion chelator selectively binds iron,                  nickel, and/or chromium ions. The flue gas                  treatment system comprises a treatment system                  selected from a particulate filtration unit, a                  direct contact condenser, a SOx reduction unit,                  or a NOx reduction unit. Preferred Composition:                  The concentration of ionic metals in the purified                  amine solvent is 1 mg/L or more. The treated flue                  gas comprises fly ash in a concentration of 1                  mg/Nm3 or less (STP) having a metal ion content                  of 10 wt.% or less.

PT P
PN WO2022006230-A1; US2021403335-A1; US11377363-B2; US2022306483-A1
TI Zero carbon dioxide emission process comprises            calcining limestone in electric kiln, treating            resulting mixture comprising calcium oxide with            nitrogen-containing salt solution, and contacting with            gaseous stream containing carbon dioxide.
AU GILLIAM R J
   WEISS M J
AE ARELAC INC (AREL-Non-standard)
   ARELAC INC (AREL-Non-standard)
   ARELAC INC (AREL-Non-standard)
GA 2022026742
AB    NOVELTY - A zero carbon dioxide emission process                comprises calcining limestone in a cement plant in                an electric kiln to form a mixture comprising                calcium oxide and a first gaseous stream comprising                clean carbon dioxide; treating the mixture with                nitrogen-containing salt solution under dissolution                condition(s) to produce a first aqueous solution                comprising calcium salt; and contacting the first                aqueous solution with the first gas stream under                precipitation conditions to produce precipitation                material comprising vaterite, aragonite, and/or                calcite. The clean carbon-dioxide comprises no                gaseous or non-gaseous components from combustion                of fuel.
   USE - A zero carbon dioxide emission for producing                precipitation material for use in forming a                cementitious product, i.e. a formed building                material selected from masonry unit, construction                panel, conduit, basin, beam, column, slab, acoustic                barrier, and/or insulation material (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces clean carbon dioxide                devoid of pollutants that are emitted from the                combustion of fuel, such as fossil fuel; and the                sequestration of the clean carbon dioxide into                commercially useful cementitious products or                disposal underground. The process reduces potential                risks of climate change requires sequestration and                avoidance of carbon dioxide from various                anthropogenic processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for zero                carbon dioxide emission system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Calcining limestone in                  the electric kiln produces less than or equal to                  20% of at least one component selected from SOx;                  thermal NOx; carbon monoxide; metal; volatile                  organic matter; and/or particulate matter.                  Preferred Compositions: First gaseous stream                  comprises less than or equal to 20%, preferably                  less than or equal to 5% of at least one                  component selected from SOx; thermal NOx; carbon                  monoxide; metal; volatile organic matter; and/or                  particulate matter, which as a result comprises                  clean carbon dioxide. First gaseous stream                  comprises 20-95 vol.% clean carbon dioxide.                  Mixture comprising calcium oxide and/or first                  aqueous solution comprising calcium salt further                  comprises 1-40 wt.% solids selected from                  silicate, iron oxide, aluminum oxide, and/or                  other particulate matter. Mixture comprises                  greater than or equal to 40 wt.% calcium oxide.                  Calcium oxide is an underburnt lime, low reactive                  lime, and/or high reactive lime.                  Nitrogen-containing inorganic salt is selected                  from ammonium halide, ammonium acetate, ammonium                  sulfate, ammonium sulfite, ammonium nitrate,                  and/or ammonium nitrite. Ammonium halide is                  ammonium chloride. Nitrogen-containing salt                  solution is ammonium chloride solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Nitrogen-containing                  organic salt is a nitrogen-containing organic                  compound selected from aliphatic amine, alicyclic                  amine, and/or heterocyclic amine. Molar ratio of                  nitrogen-containing salt:mixture is 0.5:1-3:1 by                  weight.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN CN113586020-A
TI Radial wells combined with carbon dioxide fracturing vertical well volume fracturing method, involves performing carbon dioxide fracturing operation on radial wells, and performing hydraulic fracturing operation on radial wells after carbon dioxide fracturing operation.
AU LIU C
   PAN L
   ZHOU T
   WANG H
   HE J
   LI X
   LI F
AE SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021C65992
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drilling multiple radial wells on a same reservoir of a vertical well. Carbon dioxide fracturing operation is performed on the radial wells. Hydraulic fracturing operation is performed on the radial wells after the carbon dioxide fracturing operation.
   USE - Radial wells combined with carbon dioxide fracturing vertical well volume fracturing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables drilling the radial wells in the same reservoir of the vertical well, realizing change to original in-situ stress during fracturing operation by a radial well stress field formed by radial well inter-well interference and avoiding complication of the formed stress field to the stress so as to realize carbon dioxide fracturing operation, open micro-fractures in the radial well stress field based on characteristics of easy diffusion of carbon dioxide, zero interfacial tension and complex fractures and form a preliminary complex fracture network, and hence performing hydraulic fracturing operation, expanding micro-fractures opened in a carbon dioxide fracturing stage and forming a supporting fracture network to realize volume fracturing effect, and hence forming main fractures at ends of the radial wells during hydraulic fracturing operation, increasing an oil drainage area and improving recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a radial wells combined with carbon dioxide fracturing vertical well volume fracturing system.

TI Underwater unmanned aerial vehicle power system,            has carbon dioxide turbine whose outlet is connected            with hot side inlet of heat regenerator, where hot side            outlet of heat regenerator is connected with inlet of            carbon dioxide condenser.
AU BAI W
   ZHANG X
   QIAO Y
   ZHANG Y
   YANG Y
   GU Z
   LI H
   YAO M
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202113321N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a generator (8) connected with                a carbon dioxide pump (11). An outlet of a carbon                dioxide turbine (7) is connected with a hot side                inlet of a heat regenerator (9). A hot side outlet                of the heat regenerator is connected with a hot                side inlet of a carbon dioxide condenser (10). A                hot side outlet of the carbon dioxide condenser is                connected with an inlet of the carbon dioxide pump.                An outlet of the carbon dioxide pump is connected                with a cold side inlet of the heat regenerator,                where a cold source of the carbon dioxide condenser                is sea water around an underwater unmanned                aircraft.
   USE - Underwater unmanned aerial vehicle power                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively combines the fuel                system based on metal fuel lithium and sulfur                hexafluoride (SF6) as oxidant and the circulating                power system by using carbon dioxide Rankine cycle.                The system is easy to store and discharge the the                combustion product to the outer side, and has high                circulating efficiency and compactly                arranged.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for working underwater unmanned aerial vehicle                power system.

AU WU Y
   HU J
   MA W
   HU X
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 202266519L
AB    NOVELTY - Producing formic acid by hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide based on cyclic conversion of                imidazole formate and imidazole bicarbonate                involves using imidazole bicarbonate compound (I)                as base, and performing carbon dioxide                hydrogenation in presence of catalyst to produce                formic acid.
   USE - Method for producing formic acid by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide based on cyclic                conversion of imidazole formate and imidazole                bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce formic acid by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide with high                efficiency, environmental protection and energy                saving, and has cost-effective and social benefits                and is suitable for popularization and use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing formic acid by hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide based on cyclic conversion of                imidazole formate and imidazole bicarbonate                involves using imidazole bicarbonate compound of                formula (I) as base, and performing carbon dioxide                (CO2) hydrogenation in presence of catalyst to                produce formic acid.
   R1and R2=1-6C straight-chain or branched-chain                alkyl group.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  selected from water, tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl                  sulfoxide and acetonitrile. The catalyst is                  selected metal iridium complex PNP-Ir(III),                  cycloalkylaminocarbene iridium and                  triphenylphosphine iridium.Preferred Process: The step of                  CO2hydrogenation to formic acid involves adding                  solvent and imidazole bicarbonate to the reaction                  kettle, filling the reaction kettle with CO2 and                  hydrogen (H) at room temperature, raising the                  temperature of the reaction kettle to a preset                  reaction temperature, reacting for 12-24 hours to                  obtain imidazole formate, filling CO2into the                  reactor, stirring, using CO2 to replace formic                  acid from imidazole formate at 12 hours to obtain                  formic acid and regenerated imidazole                  bicarbonate, performing multiple                  CO2pressurization and depressurization operation                  on the reactor during the reaction process after                  the reaction is completed, cooling the reaction                  kettle to room temperature to release the                  pressure, and measuring the content of formate,                  where the temperature in the reaction kettle is                  25-160&#8451; and the pressure is 1-10                  megapascal, and the pressure ratio of CO2and H2                  charged into the reactor is 2:5-2:6.Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the                  imidazole bicarbonate added to the reactor to the                  solvent is 5:1-1:5.

AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to power supply systems using renewable energy sources. Geothermal-carbon dioxide power complex comprises turbine with electric generator, working medium receiver connected to turbine outlet, compressor with heat removal, cooling heat exchanger and working medium accumulator. Carbon dioxide gas from production well of carbonic mineral water deposit is compressed in compressor with heat removal made in form of oil-free carbon-dioxide compressor, and then cooled in heat exchanger-cooler. Part of carbon dioxide gas is condensed in cooling heat exchanger by means of refrigerating machine, and part is mixed with carbon dioxide gas from turbine with electric generator and supplied to working medium receiver made in form of pressure geothermal well, where it is heated in underground geothermal header and from production geothermal well it is supplied to turbine to produce electric power and to provide it with oil-free carbon dioxide compressor, compressor of refrigerating machine, electric motors of water pumps and consumers.
   USE - Power supply systems.
   ADVANTAGE - Higher economic efficiency of operation of geothermal-carbon dioxide power complex.

TI Method for cleaning pipelines of hydraulic systems from oil and operational contamination with supercritical carbon dioxide, involves cooling carbon dioxide in supply tank with help of refrigeration unit, and turning into liquid state.
AU SOSNINA YU N
   POSPELOVA A V

AE ONEGA PROJECT NO TECHNO OFFICE SUIT (ONEG-Non-standard)
GA 2020A87356
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves directing the working medium along the pressure line to the pipeline being washed. The pipeline is washed using the working medium. The working medium is returned to the supply tank. The carbon dioxide is used as the working medium, and is provided in the supply tank with the liquid state. The working medium is separated from contaminants on the separator with the transition of carbon dioxide to a gaseous state. The working medium is returned to the supply tank. The carbon dioxide is cooled in the supply tank with the help of a refrigeration unit. The carbon dioxide is again turned into a liquid state. The residual carbon dioxide from the flushed pipeline after flushing is removed to the atmosphere.
   USE - Method for cleaning pipelines of hydraulic systems from oil and operational contamination with supercritical carbon dioxide.

GA 202151091T
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization method of polytetrafluoroethylene waste residue containing paraffin, comprises (i) continuously introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into polytetrafluoroethylene waste residue containing paraffin to extract, and obtaining supercritical carbon dioxide and polytetrafluoroethylene recovery material containing paraffin, (ii) continuously performing primary separation to supercritical carbon dioxide containing paraffin to obtain paraffin recovery material and supercritical carbon dioxide in a small amount of paraffin, and (iii) continuously performing secondary separation to the supercritical carbon dioxide in a small amount of paraffin to obtain paraffin recovery material and the liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive utilization of polytetrafluoroethylene waste residue containing paraffin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high separation efficiency and large operational elasticity; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (i): flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide is 300 to 800 l/h; and ratio between polytetrafluoroethylene waste residue containing paraffin and supercritical carbon dioxide is 1:60-240 kg/l; and extraction pressure is 20-40 MPa, preferably 25-35 MPa, temperature is 40-90 degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C, and time is 0.5-8 hours, preferably 2-6 hours. In step (ii): primary separation pressure is 7.5-10 MPa, and temperature is 50-70 degrees C. In step (iii): secondary separation pressure is 4.5-5.5 MPa, and temperature is 10-30 degrees C. The content of paraffin in polytetrafluoroethylene waste residue containing paraffin is 4-70 wt.%. The moisture content in polytetrafluoroethylene waste residue containing paraffin is 3-40 wt.%.

PT P
PN WO2021060929-A1; KR2021036013-A; KR2286472-B1
TI Functionalized metal-organic structure used for adsorbent used in selective separation of carbon dioxide, comprises hydroxyl group, and is functionalized with amine-based polymer.
AU BAE Y
   KANG J H
   YOON T
   BAE Y S
   OONG Y T
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 202133014J
AB    NOVELTY - A functionalized metal-organic structure comprises a hydroxy group and is functionalized with an amine-based polymer.
   USE - Functionalized metal-organic structure used for adsorbent used in selective separation of carbon dioxide (all claimed) from combustion gas of fossil fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The functionalized metal-organic structure has excellent selectivity, stability, and adsorption performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of the functionalized metal-organic structure, which involves preparing a functionalized metal-organic structure by impregnating the degassed metal-organic structure with an amine-based polymer;
   (2) a carbon dioxide adsorbent, which comprises the functionalized metal-organic structure; and
   (3) selective separation of carbon dioxide, which involves adsorbing carbon dioxide by contacting the carbon dioxide adsorbent with a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The degassed metal-organic structure is produced by heating the metal-organic structure at 80-200 degrees C for 8-24 hours. The selective separation of carbon dioxide further involves heating and activating the carbon dioxide adsorbent, adsorbing carbon dioxide by contacting the activated carbon dioxide adsorbent with a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide, and regenerating the carbon dioxide adsorbent by desorbing the adsorbed carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The functionalized metal-organic structure is a functionalized metal-organic in which the central metal comprises zirconium, cobalt, nickel, zinc, magnesium, manganese, copper, vanadium, chromium, iron, aluminum, ytterbium, scandium, yttrium, calcium, silver or terbium. The functionalized metal-organic structure is zirconium-based metal-organic framework (NU-1000) in which the central metal is zirconium. The mixed gas contains carbon dioxide and nitrogen. Preferred Composition: The content of the amine-based polymer is 10-70 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. of the metal-organic structure before functionalization. Preferred Conditions: The regeneration is performed at 80-200 degrees C for 1-5 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine-based polymer includes an amine group in the repeating unit, and the amine group of the amine-based polymer is primary amine, secondary amine, tertiary amine or quaternary amine. The functionalized metal-organic structure includes an organic ligand of formula: Y'A-(Y')n.Y' = -OH or -C(O)OH;A = 6-30C aryl or 6-30C polyvalent radical of heteroaryl (both optionally substituted);andn = 1-10.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN111185071-A; CN111185071-B
TI Carbon dioxide absorbent used in carbon dioxide capture for flue gas, comprises component A having alkali metal carbonates and organic secondary amine, tertiary amine or polyamine, component B having basic amino acid salt and primary amine.
AU ZHAO X
   WANG B
   CUI Q
AE CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
GA 202046607K
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprises component A having one or more of alkali metal carbonates and organic secondary amine, tertiary amine or polyamine, component B having one or more of basic amino acid salts and organic primary amines, and component C having weak acid and weak acid salt.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbent used in carbon dioxide capture for flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent increases regeneration rate of absorbent, reduces regeneration temperature and regeneration energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a carbon dioxide absorbent, which involves mixing component A and component C to obtain a mixture, and then mixing obtained mixture with component B to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of weak acid and weak acid salt in component C is (0.5-10):1, preferably (1-7):1. The mass ratio of component A and component C is (2-20):1, preferably (5-15):1. The mass ratio of component B and component C is (2-15):1, preferably (3-12):1. The mass ratio of alkali metal carbonates and organic secondary amine, tertiary amine or polyamine in component A is (0.01-10):1, preferably (0.5-5):1. The mass ratio of basic amino acid salts and organic primary amines in component B is (0.01-8):1, preferably (1-6):1. Preferred Components: The weak acid is selected from one or more of inorganic weak acids and 2-20C organic weak acids, preferably one or more of boric acid, citric acid, adipic acid, glutaric acid, aminotriacetic acid, and ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid. The weak acid salt is selected from one or more of inorganic weak acid salt and 2-20C organic weak acid salts, preferably one or more of borate, citrate, adipate, glutarate, ammonia triacetate and ethylenediaminetetraacetate, more preferably one or more of potassium borate, sodium borate, potassium citrate, sodium citrate, potassium adipate, sodium adipate, potassium glutarate, sodium glutarate, potassium triacetate, sodium triacetate, ethylenediamine, potassium tetraacetate and sodium edetate. The alkali metal carbonate is selected from one or more of potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate and lithium carbonate. The secondary organic amine is selected from one or more of 2-10C secondary organic amine, preferably one or more of methyl monoethanolamine, N-ethylethanolamine, N,N-diethylaniline, 3-methylamino-1,2-propanediol and piperazine and its derivative. The secondary organic amine is selected from one or more of 2-10C tertiary organic amines, preferably one or more N,N-dimethylethanolamine, triethanolamine, or N-methyldiethanolamine. The polyamine is selected from one or more of diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine and tetraethylenepentamine. The basic amino acid salt is selected from one or more of glycine salt, alanine salt, valine salt, leucine salt, isoleucine salt, phenylalanine salt, proline salt, tryptophan salt, serine salt, tyrosine, cysteine, methionine, asparagine, threonine, aspartate, glutamate, lysine, arginine or histidine, preferably one or more of potassium glycinate, sodium glycinate, potassium alanine, sodium alanine, potassium asparagine, sodium asparagine, potassium threonine, sodium threonine, potassium aspartate, sodium aspartate, potassium glutamate, sodium glutamate, potassium lysine, sodium lysine, potassium arginine, sodium arginine, potassium histidine or sodium histidine. The primary organic amine is selected from one or more of 2-10C primary organic amine, preferably monoethanolamine, 3-aminopropanol, 1-amino-2-propanol, 2-amino-1-butanol, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, and 2-amino-2-methyl-1,3-propanediol. Preferred Compositions: The absorbent comprises 10-40 wt.% component A, 0.5-25 wt.% component B and 0.01-20 wt.% component C based on the total weight of absorbent.

PT P
PN TW201900263-A; TW660770-B1
TI Device and treatment method for carbon dioxide capture and utilization has barium carbonate for capturing carbon dioxide.
AU LU M
   HUANG Y
   VU X
AE UNIV CHIA NAN PHARMACY & SCI (UYCH-Non-standard)
GA 201927887Q
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention relates to a treatment method for carbon dioxide capture and utilization, which utilizes the high solubility of carbon dioxide in KOH solution. The carbon dioxide is firstly absorbed in a solution containing KOH to form potassium carbonate in an absorption tower and then introduced into a fluidized bed crystallization tank to react with calcium, magnesium or barium ions to produce crystalline particles of calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, or barium carbonate for capturing the carbon dioxide. Furthermore, potassium ions in the crystallization tank are recycled to the absorption tower for reuse. Therefore, the present invention can avoid the additional environmental pollution, and can produce chemicals for reused, which increases additional benefits. In addition, the present invention further provides a device for carbon dioxide capture and utilization for the treatment method of the present invention.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   LIU Y
   JIA A
   KOU Z

AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 201905471U
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide/R170 azeotrope refrigerant comprises mass ratio of carbon dioxide is 78-80.6%, where the temperature slip is 0 degrees C.
   USE - The carbon dioxide/R170 azeotrope refrigerant is useful in single or dual stage refrigeration/heating systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerant has low GWP value, low flammability, zero temperature slip value, higher COP heat value, lower theoretical specific work, lower discharge temperatures and lower compression ratios.

PT P
PN CN110817795-A; CN211998802-U
TI Hydrogen separation and water gas reforming hydrogen-integrated pressing system comprises reformer, three-phase heat exchanger, steam trap, refrigerator, carbon dioxide liquefaction device and hydrogen separated from water gas.
AU YUE X
   HAN D
   ZHAO J
   LI J
   CHEN F
   ZHANG Y
   YUE Y
AE ZHONGKE LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN (ZHON-Non-standard)
   CAS LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN TECH (CASL-Non-standard)
GA 202018156K
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen separation and water gas reforming hydrogen-integrated pressing system comprises a reformer, three-phase heat exchanger, a steam trap, a refrigerator (6), a carbon dioxide liquefaction device and hydrogen separated from the water gas reforming integral device. The reformer is connected with a methanol steam inlet pipe and mixed gas outlet pipe. The methanol steam inlet pipe is connected with a three-phase heat exchanging (2), the reformer (5) is adapted to methanol vapor to prepare hydrogen, mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide. The water gas reforming integrated device (3) comprises a reaction cavity. The reaction cavity is set outside the heating chamber, which is adapted to the reaction chamber provides the reaction temperature. The reaction cavity is set in the hydrogen separating cavity and water gas reforming chamber, the hydrogen separation chamber is located at the water gas reforming chamber.
   USE - Hydrogen separation and water gas reforming hydrogen-integrated pressing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen separation and water gas reforming hydrogen-integrated pressing system realizes the gas system for circulating and purifying, theoretical yield reaches to 100% and realizes the hydrogen yield is to 95%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrogen separation and water gas reforming hydrogen-integrated pressing system comprises a reformer, three-phase heat exchanger, a steam trap, a refrigerator (6), a carbon dioxide liquefaction device and hydrogen separated from the water gas reforming integral device. The reformer is connected with a methanol steam inlet pipe and mixed gas outlet pipe. The methanol steam inlet pipe is connected with a three-phase heat exchanging (2), the reformer (5) is adapted to methanol vapor to prepare hydrogen, mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide. The water gas reforming integrated device (3) comprises a reaction cavity. The reaction cavity is set outside the heating chamber, which is adapted to the reaction chamber provides the reaction temperature. The reaction cavity is set in the hydrogen separating cavity and water gas reforming chamber, the hydrogen separation chamber is located at the water gas reforming chamber above the hydrogen separation chamber is connected with the water gas reforming chamber (31). The hydrogen separation chamber is opened with mixed gas inlet and a carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet. The hydrogen separating cavity (32) is inserted into the hydrogen suction pipe, and suitable for separating the pure hydrogen. The hydrogen absorption pipe is connected with the pure hydrogen outlet pipe. The water gas reforming chamber is provided with catalyst filler. The water gas reforming chamber is provided hydrogen mixed gas inlet. The mixed gas outlet pipe is connected with the mixed gas inlet connected with the carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet is connected with the carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe, and the carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe is connected with steam trap. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for medium-pressing, which involves:
   (A) transferring liquid pump and methanol water into the methanol water vapor tube, where the pump pressure is 7-18 MPa, heating methanol water and vaporizing into methanol water vapor and entering the reformer;
   (B) reforming methanol water vapor in the reformer to generate hydrogen, transferring mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and then the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide into the hydrogen separation chamber of the water gas reforming integrated device, where the gas phase composition of the mixed gas of 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide and 0.3-3% carbon monoxide. The hydrogen absorption tube in the hydrogen separation chamber are used to separate the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and the separated pure hydrogen is collected from the output of the hydrogen absorption tube. The remaining carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is output from the hydrogen separation chamber, and the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas. The pressure is controlled by a liquid pump, the temperature of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is controlled by the refrigerator, and then the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is transferred to a carbon dioxide separation device for carbon dioxide liquefaction separation. The gas phase components of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are 25-45% hydrogen, 55-75% carbon dioxide, 0-3% water, and 0.3-3% carbon monoxide, the pressure controlled by the liquid pump is 7-18 MPa, and the refrigerator controls temperature is -25-18 degrees C. The carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is made into a liquid carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed residual gas in a carbon dioxide separator, and the liquid carbon dioxide is output and collected. The components of the hydrogen mixed residual gas are 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide, and 3-9% carbon monoxide. The residual hydrogen mixed gas is sent to the water gas reforming cavity of the water gas reforming integrated device, and the water is made into a reforming mixed gas. The water is distributed according to the content of carbon monoxide, and the water distribution ratio (carbon monoxide: water) is 1:1-20. The supplied hydrogen mixed gas is reformed into a reformed mixed gas by water distribution in a water gas reforming cavity, where the gas phase components of the reformed mixed gas are 62-77% hydrogen, 22-27% carbon dioxide, and 0.5-1.5% carbon monoxide, where the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the reformed mixed gas is close to the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide; and
   (C) transferring reformed mixed gas the hydrogen separation chamber and mixing with a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide, and the reforming mixed gas and the mixed gas of hydrogen, subjecting carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide to hydrogen separation operation in the hydrogen separation chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of system.
   Three-phase heat exchanging (2)
   Water gas reforming integrated device (3)
   Reformer (5)
   Refrigerator (6)
   Water gas reforming chamber (31)
   Hydrogen separating cavity (32)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The output pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are all output after heat exchange and temperature reduction in a three-phase heat exchange device, and the methanol water undergoes three-phase exchange. The heat device vaporizes into methanol water vapor. The methanol water is replaced by natural gas.

The purified gas is supplied to the adsorption tower where the adsorbent is filled, in the drying process, after heating the purified gas. The adsorption towers have the first adsorption tower and second adsorption tower which are connected so that the source gas distributes one by one. The purified gas is distributed toward upper portion from the lower portion of the adsorption tower. PT P
PN WO2022030203-A1
TI Manufacture of hydrocarbon compound e.g. methane, involves contacting absorption conversion catalyst comprising oxide carrier, component comprising metal e.g. alkali metal, and component comprising metal e.g. nickel and iron with carbon dioxide-containing gas, and contacting with reducing gas.
AU KOSAKA F
   KURAMOTO K
   MOCHIZUKI T
   LIU Y
   CHEN S
   TAKAGI H
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 202221666N
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hydrocarbon compound from carbon dioxide involves (i) preparing an absorption conversion catalyst comprising an oxide carrier, and a component (a1) comprising at least one type of metal chosen from alkali metal and alkaline earth metal, and a component (a2) comprising at least one type of metal chosen from nickel, iron, copper, cobalt and ruthenium, supported on the oxide carrier, (ii) contacting the catalyst with a carbon dioxide-containing gas at a pressure higher than atmospheric pressure to store carbon dioxide in the catalyst, and (iii) contacting the catalyst in which carbon dioxide is stored with a reducing gas at a pressure higher than atmospheric pressure.
   USE - Manufacture of hydrocarbon compound e.g. 1-5C hydrocarbon e.g. methane and/or ethane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method directly separates and recovers carbon dioxide at concentrations including low concentration range, and efficiently converts carbon dioxide into hydrocarbon compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus, which is a circulating fluidized bed-type reaction apparatus comprising a carbon dioxide recovery layer, a hydrocarbon compound synthesis reaction layer, and a catalytic circulation path connecting the carbon dioxide recovery layer and the hydrocarbon compound synthesis reaction layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of integrated carbon dioxide capture and conversion system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide concentration in the carbon dioxide-containing gas in the process (ii) is 0.01-5%. The component (a1) preferably comprises sodium, calcium and/or potassium, more preferably sodium. The component (a2) preferably comprises nickel. The oxide carrier comprises an alumina carrier. The reducing gas is a hydrogen-containing gas. Preferred Property: The average particle size of the catalyst is 0.1-500 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The pressure is more than 1 atm to 9 atm. Carbon monoxide is obtained together with the hydrocarbon compound in the process (iii).
   EXAMPLE - gamma -Alumina having particle size of 74-125 mu m was immersed in 50 ml aqueous solution comprising 17.6 %mass (with respect to amount of gamma -alumina) potassium carbonate, dried at 110 degrees C overnight and baked in air at 550 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain potassium- gamma -alumina carrier. Potassium- gamma -alumina carrier was immersed in 50 ml aqueous solution comprising 13 %mass (with respect to amount of carrier) nickel nitrate, dried at 110 degrees C overnight and baked in air at 550 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain nickel/potassium-alumina catalyst. 1 g Nickel/potassium-alumina catalyst was placed in an electric furnace. The catalyst was pretreated at 500 degrees C for 1 hour in hydrogen atmosphere. Carbon dioxide stream and hydrogen stream were supplied to the furnace. Nitrogen was supplied for 5 minutes as a purge gas between the carbon dioxide stream and hydrogen stream. The reaction was performed at 1-9 atm and at 450 degrees C to obtain outlet gas including methane, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide.

PN CN113110030-A; CN113110030-B
TI Carbon dioxide capturing type dynamic matrix control-proportional-integral-derivative cascade system for use in thermal power station, has carbon dioxide capture control module fixed at front end of carbon dioxide capture absorption module.
AU AN A
   ZHANG W
   LI Q
   MA C
   ZHENG Y
   WEN Y
   WANG Q
   WANG R
   LI J
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV LANZHOU TECHNOLOGY (UYLZ-C)
GA 202181738Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide capture control module fixed at a front end of a carbon dioxide capture absorption module. The carbon dioxide capture control module is provided with an outer ring module and an inner ring module. The outer ring module is connected with the inner ring module in series. The outer ring module is provided with a dynamic matrix control (DMC) controller. The inner ring module is provided with a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controller. A flue gas pipeline is installed with a flue gas flow sensor. A smoke gas flow sensor detects smoke gas flow rate of coal power plant smoke and connected with the PID controller.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capturing type DMC-PID cascade system for use in a thermal power station.
   ADVANTAGE - The system maintains excellent performance of the controllers, and has structure characteristic of the cascade system. The system finishes control of the controlled object large delay strong interference so as to improve adaptability and flexibility of the carbon dioxide capture system to the variable load operation of the thermal power station.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide capturing type DMC-PID cascade system controlling method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a carbon dioxide capturing type DMC-PID cascade system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN US2021039044-A1; WO2021029866-A1
TI Sequestering carbon, comprises e.g. providing source of carbon dioxide in gaseous or dissolved form, introducing dioxide to calcium-containing source water and mixing dioxide in water in presence of calcium hydroxide or calcium carbonate.
AU ALAMOUDI A S M
   AYUMANTAKATH M F
   VOUTCHKOV N
   IHM S
AE SALINE WATER CONVERSION CORP (SALI-Non-standard)
   SALINE WATER CONVERSION CORP (SALI-Non-standard)
GA 202121543T
AB    NOVELTY - Sequestering carbon, comprises: providing a source of carbon dioxide in gaseous or dissolved form; introducing the carbon dioxide into calcium-containing source water; mixing the carbon dioxide in the calcium-containing source water in an engineered reactor in the presence of calcium hydroxide or calcium carbonate, the carbon dioxide is converted into dissolved calcium bicarbonate; and discharging the dissolved calcium bicarbonate-containing source water to a natural surface water body having a pH 7.5-9, or to a man-made facility for storage or further use.
   USE - The method is useful for sequestering carbon in applications including drinking water, irrigation water, power plant cooling water, industrial water, horticultural water or municipal water or wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides multiple discharge streams from desalination plant to sequester carbon dioxide; is cost-effective and reliable sequestration of carbon dioxide from anthropologic origin into a permanently soluble form of calcium bicarbonate, which then can be stored practically indefinitely in waters of surface water bodies i.e. the world's oceans, seas, and rivers; and enables reduction of corrosively soft water streams, i.e. desalinated water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for sequestering carbon, comprising providing source of carbon dioxide, and contacting the carbon dioxide in calcium-containing source water with a solid calcium source in an engineered reactor, the carbon dioxide is converted into dissolved and chemically bound calcium bicarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pH of the discharged calcium bicarbonate-containing water is adjusted to pH 7.5-9 before the act of discharging. The calcium-containing source water is a retentate discharge or brine discharge from a desalination process or wastewater reclamation process, and the calcium-containing source water has a calcium concentration higher than a calcium concentration of feed water to the desalination process. The calcium-containing source water is brackish water, seawater, cooling water from an electric power generation plant, treated or untreated wastewater, and/or reclaimed water.Preferred Components: The natural surface water body has a pH of 7.5-9, and the natural surface water body is ocean, bay, estuary, river, lake or ground water. The source of the carbon dioxide is internal combustion engine exhaust, gas generated by degasification from thermal desalination, burning of fossil fuel, ethanol production, food and beverage production, pharmaceutical production, chemical production and/or natural carbon dioxide emissions. The calcium-containing source water is permeating produced by membrane desalination process including membrane wastewater treatment process, and/or distillate produced by thermal desalination process. The calcium-containing source water is desalinated water, treated wastewater, untreated wastewater or reclaimed wastewater. The solid calcium source comprises calcite, dolomite, calcium hydroxide, calcium sulfate and/or calcium hypochlorite.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

AE QINGDAO HAIER AIR-CONDITIONER CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAIER CO LTD (HIER-C)
GA 2020836988
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining indoor Carbon                dioxide concentration, indoor temperature, indoor                humidity and current active number of people.                Target Carbon dioxide concentration is determined                according to the indoor temperature, the indoor                humidity and the current active number of the                people. Main control strategy of a fresh air system                is established according to the indoor Carbon                dioxide concentration and the target Carbon dioxide                concentration. The fresh air system is controlled                according to first control strategy when the indoor                Carbon dioxide concentration is greater than the                target Carbon dioxide concentration. The fresh air                system is controlled according to second control                strategy when the indoor Carbon dioxide                concentration is less than or equal to the target                Carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - Method for controlling a fresh air system                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables integrating multiple                parameters to establish the main control strategy                of the fresh air system so as to integrate number                of the people in an indoor activity space to adjust                indoor environment parameters, thus improving                accuracy of adjusting the indoor environment                parameters by the fresh air system and improving                comfort and user experience of an indoor                environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a device for controlling a fresh air                system; and
   (2) a storage medium including computer                program for controlling a fresh air system.

PT P
PN KR2289069-B1
TI Device for inferring concentration of algae in hydroponic culture medium by analysis of dissolved oxygen concentration and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration, has analysis unit that inferring algae concentration in nutrient solution.
AU HYUN B B
   LEE M W
AE IMAGINATION GARDEN INC (IMAG-Non-standard)
GA 202194490X
AB    NOVELTY - Algae concentration inference device has a sensor unit (130) that is provided with sensors for obtaining dissolved oxygen concentration and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration in nutrient solution. An analysis unit (140) is provided for inferring the algae concentration in the nutrient solution by analyzing the amount of change in the dissolved dioxide concentration. The change amount is greater than the initial dissolved oxide concentration, when the dissolved carbon-dioxide concentration is not changed back to initial dissolved acid concentration, and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration after it has decreased, is inferred that the algae concentration in the nutrient solution is increased, and the sensor unit is connected with the analysis unit in which each of the dissolved oxygen concentration and the dissolved carbon dioxide concentration are further obtained from different standard nutrient solutions.
   USE - Device for inferring algae concentration in hydroponic nutrient solution through dissolved oxygen concentration and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces environmental pollution by reducing the amount of disinfectant used and amount of waste nutrient solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Algae concentration inference device has a sensor unit that is provided with sensors for obtaining dissolved oxygen concentration and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration in nutrient solution. An analysis unit is provided for inferring the algae concentration in the nutrient solution by analyzing the amount of change in the dissolved dioxide concentration. The change amount is greater than the initial dissolved oxide concentration, when the dissolved carbon-dioxide concentration is not changed back to initial dissolved acid concentration, and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration after it has decreased, is inferred that the algae concentration in the nutrient solution is increased, and the sensor unit is connected with the analysis unit in which each of the dissolved oxygen concentration and the dissolved carbon dioxide concentration are further obtained from different standard nutrient solutions, and the analysis unit is learned with each of the dissolved oxygen concentration and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration obtained by the sensor unit from the standard nutrient solutions. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for inferring concentration of algae in a nutrient solution, which involves obtaining a dissolved oxygen concentration and a dissolved carbon dioxide concentration in the nutrient solution, and inferring the concentration of algae in the nutrient solution by analyzing the amount of change in the obtained dissolved oxygen concentration and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration by an analysis unit, where the step of obtaining the dissolved oxygen concentration and the dissolved carbon dioxide concentration include acquiring the dissolved oxygen concentration and the dissolved carbon dioxide concentration from standard nutrient solutions having different algae concentrations by the sensor unit, and the analysis unit learning from each of the dissolved oxygen concentration and the dissolved carbon dioxide concentration obtained by the sensor unit from the standard nutrient solutions, and dissolved oxygen concentration and dissolved carbon dioxide concentration in hydroponic culture nutrient solution through analysis; and
   (2) an algae reduction system using the method of inferring the concentration of algae in the hydroponic nutrient solution (100) through the analysis of the dissolved oxygen concentration and the dissolved carbon dioxide concentration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an algae concentration inference device `(Drawing includes non-English language text)`.

   Nutrient solution tank (110)
   Hydroponics bed (120)

PN WO2018119542-A1
TI Photochemical transformation of carbon dioxide to acetaldehyde involves reacting carbon dioxide inside a photoreactor with light, where reactor contains titanium dioxide photocatalyst with copper oxide nanoparticles.
AU OYARCE CANALES H E
   SAEZ NAVARRETE C A
   GUZMAN DE LA CERDA D J
   RUIZ LEON D A
   ISAACS CASANOVA M A
AE UNIV PONTIFICIA CATOLICA CHILE (UYPO-Non-standard)
   UNIV SANTIAGO CHILE (UYSA-Non-standard)
GA 201852572M
AB    NOVELTY - Photochemical transformation of carbon dioxide to acetaldehyde is carried out by reacting carbon dioxide (CO2) inside a photoreactor with light, where the reactor contains a titanium dioxide (TiO2) photocatalyst with copper oxide (Cu2O) nanoparticles.
   USE - Photochemical transformation of carbon dioxide to acetaldehyde.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is highly selective in terms of the products that are obtained and performs efficient photoreaction with high conversion of CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Reactor includes sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3)/sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) buffer with bicarbonate concentration of 0.9-1.1 M and carbonate concentration of 0.09-0.11 M.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Method includes using light having wavelength within the solar spectrum. Preferred Conditions: The pH inside the reactor is 8-12, preferably 9 to allow greater stability of Cu2O nanoparticles present in the photocatalyst and in turn, improve the distribution of electrons formed by photocatalysis due to load stabilizing effect of hydroxide ions (OH-). Preferred Compositions: Reactor includes solution of triethanolamine with triethanolamine concentration of 0.5-3 wt.%. Acetaldehyde is obtained as a main product, in which proportion of acetaldehyde with respect to the rest of sub-products is greater than or equal to 90%. Formaldehyde and formic acid are obtained as by-products.

TI Biomethanol production system used to remove            carbon dioxide, comprises feed stream, reformer            reactor, methanol reactor, wash column having e.g.            biomethanol product to absorb carbon dioxide from            syngas mixture, and flash column.
AU MOOS K
AE UNION ENG AS (UNEN-Non-standard)
   UNION ENG AS (UNEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A4435E
AB    NOVELTY - Biomethanol production system (10) comprises:                (a) a feed stream comprising methane, water and                carbon dioxide; (b) a reformer reactor (12) capable                of reacting the feed stream to form a syngas                mixture comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and                carbon dioxide; (c) a methanol reactor (14) capable                of reacting the syngas mixture to form biomethanol                product; (d) a wash column (16) comprising the                syngas mixture and the biomethanol product, where                the biomethanol product is capable of absorbing                carbon dioxide from the syngas mixture; and (e) a                flash column (18) comprising a vent capable of                venting the absorbed carbon dioxide.
   USE - The systems and method are useful for removing                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The systems and method remove excess carbon                dioxide without requiring the addition of hydrogen                to the process streams, thus increasing the                efficiency and cost effectiveness as compared to                the conventional systems and methods.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a biomethanol production system; and
   (2) removing carbon dioxide from the                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the                biomethanol production system.
   Biomethanol production system (10)
   Reformer reactor (12)
   Methanol reactor (14)
   Wash column (16)
   Flash column (18)

PT P
PN TW202229715-A
TI Dry ice cycle power generation facility for            loading carbon dioxide, has carbon dioxide tank that            converts low-pressure gaseous carbon dioxide into            liquid carbon dioxide and transports liquid carbon to            dry ice maker through closed pipeline.
AU ZHONG Q
AE YELLOW OLD CO LTD (YELL-Non-standard)
GA 2022C7508M
AB    NOVELTY - This invention creates a dry ice cycle power                generation device, which includes a closed                pipeline, a dry ice maker, a dry ice jet, a                generator, and a carbon dioxide tank. The closed                pipeline is annular and has a circulation                direction, and carbon dioxide can flow in the                closed pipeline. The dry ice maker, the dry ice                jet, the generator, and the carbon dioxide tank are                arranged on the closed pipeline and are                sequentially arranged in a ring along the                circulation direction. In this way, the carbon                dioxide can be made into dry ice by the dry ice                maker and sent to the dry ice jet, and then                sublimated into a high-pressure gas by the dry ice                jet, which is ejected to the generator, and pushes                the power generation components of the generator so                that the generator can generate electricity, and                finally flows to the generator. The carbon dioxide                is stored in the tank for the next cycle to be sent                to the dry ice maker. This creation uses the dry                ice cycle to drive the generator without causing                environmental pollution.
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))
MC Q51-C
IP F01K-025/10

PN CN114702397-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide absorbent i.e.            2-((3-aminopropyl)(ethyl)amino)ethanol comprises            reacting acrylonitrile with ethanolamine while            stirring, dissolving with catalyst in solvent,            reacting, quenching the reaction, and evaporating the            solvent.
AU WANG J
   YUE C
   TONG H
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202290881A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide absorbent i.e.                2-((3-aminopropyl)(ethyl)amino)ethanol comprises                (1) reacting acrylonitrile with ethanolamine for                period of time while stirring at a certain                temperature, heating the reaction mixture,                distilling off the unreacted material under reduced                pressure at 100-120&#8451; to obtain 3-[ethyl                (2-hydroxyethyl)amino]propionitrile (II), and (2)                dissolving catalyst and (II) in solvent, adding                dropwise (II) into the catalyst solution at low                temperature, reacting for period of time, quenching                the reaction, and evaporating the solvent to obtain                final product.
   USE - The carbon dioxide absorbent is useful for                carbon dioxide absorption liquid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces product with yield of                75-80%. The carbon dioxide absorbent has large                absorption capacity, fast absorption rate, high                desorption rate, high cycle desorption capacity,                and low energy consumption for regeneration                compared with the existing common amine carbon                dioxide absorbers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of carbon dioxide absorbent in carbon dioxide                absorption liquid, where the concentration of                2-((3-aminopropyl)(ethyl)amino)ethanol in the                carbon dioxide absorption liquid is 0.5-3 mol/l,                preferably 2 mol/l.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (1), the reaction                  time of ethanolamine and acrylonitrile is                  performed at 20-35&#8451; for 3-6 hours; and                  the 3-[ethyl (2-hydroxyethyl) amino]                  propionitrile temperature of reaction is                  60-90&#8451;. In step (2), the reaction time                  of catalyst and (II) is 1-3 hours, preferably 2                  hours; and low temperature is 0-5&#8451;. In                  the use method, the absorption temperature of                  described carbon dioxide absorption liquid is                  10-80&#8451;; the regeneration temperature of                  carbon dioxide absorbent is 80-120&#8451;;                  the pressure of the absorbed gas is 0.1-3 MPa;                  the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the gas                  is 0-99%.Preferred Components: In step (1), the mol                  ratio of acrylonitrile to ethanolamine is                  1.1-1.5; and the catalyst is sodium borohydride,                  lithium aluminum hydride or borane complex,                  preferably lithium aluminum hydride. In step (2),                  the molar ratio of catalyst to (II) is 1.5-3; the                  solvent is tetrahydrofuran, ether or ethyl                  acetate, preferably tetrahydrofuran.

PN CN112683838-A
TI Concentration detection method for infrared carbon            dioxide sensor, involves constructing quadratic fitting            function formula, and substituting actual output value            of sensor as U value into fitted quadratic function            curve.
AU LV J
   MAO K
   QIU J
   CHI K
AE HANGZHOU MULTIIR TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202145589D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating the                temperature values of the three temperature nodes                according to the applied ambient temperature range                for a carbon dioxide concentration detection sensor                node based on infrared technology. The carbon                dioxide concentration values of the three nodes of                the three nodes are calculated according to its                carbon dioxide concentration measurement range. The                output voltage values of the three sensing nodes                are collected under different carbon dioxide                concentrations respectively under the ambient                temperature. The quadratic fitting function formula                for carbon dioxide concentration is constructed                based on the three data points. The quadratic                fitting function formula is constructed. The actual                output value of the sensor as the value is                substituted into the fitted quadratic function                curve. The obtained value is the estimated value of                carbon dioxide concentration at ambient                temperature.
   USE - Concentration detection method for infrared                carbon dioxide sensor based on nine-point                fitting.
   ADVANTAGE - The concentration value of the infrared carbon                dioxide sensor is accurately estimated that avoids                the high cost caused by the real-time detection                method of large amounts of data. The time cost for                data collection is small, and the material cost for                gas distribution is also low, which has a strong                use effect.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide hydrogenation involves                introducing gaseous water to a positive electrode                (108) of an electrolysis cell (106) comprising                positive electrode, negative electrode (112) and a                proton-conducting membrane (110) between the                electrodes, introducing carbon dioxide to the                negative electrode and applying a potential                difference between the electrodes to generate                hydrogen ions from the gaseous water that diffuse                through the proton-conducting membrane and                hydrogenate carbon dioxide at the negative                electrode.    KIM J M

AE UNIV DONGGUK IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYGG-C)

GA 201868718R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves discharging a gas existing in a head space (22) other than the space occupied by the kimchi in the internal space of the storage container (20) in which the kimchi is accommodated, so that the pressure of the head space becomes negative. The carbon dioxide is injected into the headspace alternately and repeatedly. The carbon dioxide partial pressure of the head space is formed or maintained at 0.7 atmosphere or higher.
   USE - Kimchi storage method.
   ADVANTAGE - The flavor and storage period of kimchi can be improved by providing the high concentration of carbon dioxide. The high concentration of carbon dioxide is efficiently formed in the head space other than the space in which the kimchi is stored in the Kimchi storage container, and thus, the carbon dioxide used for storing the kimchi can be saved. The gas discharge-carbon dioxide injection in the storage container can be facilitated. The volume of the head space in the storage container can be easily calculated to efficiently perform the gas discharge-carbon dioxide injection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a kimchi storage device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of Kimchi storage device.
