AU ZHANG T
AE OUSHANGYUAN TIANJIN CO LTD (OUSH-Non-standard)
GA 2022678147
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-based polylactic acid                copolymer modified cellulose material comprises (i)                taking carbon dioxide polypropylene carbonate,                oxalic acid and lactic acid, performing                polycondensation reaction to obtain polycondensate                (I), (ii) subjecting the condensation polymer (I)                and ethyl phosphoryl chloride (II) to substitution                reaction, (iii) obtaining substituent (II), (iv)                reacting the substituted substituted substituted                (II)-substituent with cellulose, and (v) mixing the                obtained cellulose product obtained in step (iii),                and (vi) reacting with the substituted (III)                substituted cellulose to obtain a carbon dioxide                based polylacto-polypropylene-based cellulose                derivative.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide-based polylactic                acid copolymer modified cellulose material. Uses                include but are not limited to industry, medicine,                building, transportation, agriculture, electronic                and electric, automobile and environment-friendly                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based polylactic acid                copolymer modified cellulose material has good                physical and chemical properties, mechanical                property, and biodegradable performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparing cellulose material by carbon                dioxide-based polylactic acid copolymer; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide-based polylactic acid                copolymer modified cellulose material.

TI Preparing 4H-pyrido(1,2-a)pyrimidin-4-one            compounds by using carbon dioxide, comprises mixing            substituted (Z)-N-ethylidenepyridin-2-amine compounds            with medium strong base, and carbonylating in solvent            under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
AU ZHANG Y
   TAN M
   ZHANG Z
   ZHAO J
   PENG L
   ZHAO G
AE UNIV CHENGDU (UYUD-C)
GA 202018136L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 4H-pyrido(1,2-a)pyrimidin-4-one                compounds (I) by using carbon dioxide, comprises                mixing substituted (Z)-N-ethylidenepyridin-2-amine                compounds (II) with medium strong base, then adding                solvent, and carrying out carbonylation reaction at                80 degrees C under carbon dioxide atmosphere to                obtain (I).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                4H-pyrido(1,2-a)pyrimidin-4-one compounds by using                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts carbonylation reaction                under carbon dioxide atmosphere having standard                atmospheric pressure; does not use transition                metals; and does not adopt neutral redox                conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 4H-pyrido(1,2-a)pyrimidin-4-one                compounds of formula (I) by using carbon dioxide,                comprises mixing substituted                (Z)-N-ethylidenepyridin-2-amine compounds of                formula (II) with medium strong base, then adding                solvent, then carrying out carbonylation reaction                at 80 degrees C under carbon dioxide atmosphere to                obtain (I).
   R1-R3 = optionally substituted alkyl or                aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The medium strong base                  includes tert-butanol salt, methanol salt,                  ethanol salt, carbonate, phosphate, potassium                  hydroxide, sodium hydroxide or organic base,                  preferably tert-butoxide. The molar ratio between                  tert-butanol salt and (II) is 1-6:1, preferably                  4.5:1. The tert-butanol salt includes lithium                  tert-butoxide, potassium tert-butoxide and/or                  potassium tert-butoxide, preferably lithium                  tert-butoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  passing carbon dioxide atmosphere before adding                  solvent, or passing carbon dioxide gas bubbles                  after adding solvent. The pressure of carbon                  dioxide atmosphere is 1 atmosphere, the reaction                  time is 24 hours, and the reaction temperature is                  130 degrees C. The method further comprises                  purifying by silica gel flash chromatography to                  obtain (I). The eluent used during purification                  treatment is mixed solvent of petroleum ether and                  ethyl acetate in a volume ratio of 10:1.                  Preferred Components: The solvent includes                  N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone,                  acetone, acetonitrile, diethylene glycol dimethyl                  ether, 1,4-dioxane, tetrahydrofuran,                  2-methyltetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane,                  1,2-dichloroethane and/or ethylene glycol                  dimethyl ether, preferably                  N,N-dimethylformamide.

PT P
PN CN110132493-A
TI Method for identifying geological storage carbon dioxide leakage by using plant stable carbon isotope, involves selecting experimental material to select seven typical plants, collecting experimental soil from farmland in storage area.
AU XUE L
   KANG F
   LI Q
AE UNIV YULIN (UNYL-C)
GA 201972654R
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide leakage geological storage identifying method involves selecting an experimental material to select seven typical plants, including 3 carbon atom plants and 4 carbon atom plants, collecting the experimental soil from the 0-20 cm soil of the farmland in the storage area. Experiment and simulation using the carbon dioxide intelligent artificial climate chamber to simulate the concentration of carbon dioxide. The collected samples are washed with distilled water and dried at 70 degrees C for 24 hours, the dried plant leaves are ground in a grinder and passed through a 200 mesh sieve. The carbon dioxide produced by the complete combustion of the plants collected by the combustion method, and the carbon dioxide collected is analyzed by a MAT-251 mass spectrometer and significant analysis is performed.

TI Apparatus for separating carbon dioxide from e.g. landfill gas in e.g. sewage disposal plant, has temporary discharge outlet summarily ejecting blast furnace gas when carbon dioxide is separated into main body side and inserted with vent.

   PIAO S
   XIE J
AE PARK S E (PARK-Individual)
   PIAO S (PIAO-Individual)
   XIE J (XIEJ-Individual)
GA 2019307444
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a main body (10) formed as a form of a partition part. A connection part (11) is formed as sheet form in a bolt hole (12) that is formed on a separated part to a bolt. An inlet port (20) flows blast furnace gas into an upper side and outer side of the main body. A separation film pipe (30) is formed with a polymer silicone film of porosity to separate carbon dioxide among the blast furnace gas. Multiple simplicity collecting tubes (40) summarily collect carbon dioxide, which is separated in a lower part of the separation film pipe. A flow tube (50) flows the carbon dioxide, which is sampled to the collecting tubes. A discharge pipe (60) ejects the flown carbon dioxide in the flow tube. A vent (70) is connected to a lower part outer side of the main body and ejects the carbon dioxide to the outer side. A temporary discharge outlet (80) summarily ejects the blast furnace gas when the carbon dioxide is separated into the main body side and is inserted with the vent.
   USE - Apparatus for separating carbon dioxide from blast furnace gas e.g. landfill gas and combustion gas in industrial facilities and production facility such as sewage disposal plant, waste water disposal plant and reclaimed place.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus prevents the separation film pipe and flow tube from being contracted in pressure by the operation of the vacuum pump and maintains a form with the separation film pipe so as to separate readily the carbon dioxide due to the separation film pipe, prevent the flow tube from being affected through the partial pressure of the carbon dioxide in the processing and emission quantity of the carbon dioxide and maximize separation and recovery efficiency of the carbon dioxide. The separation film pipe is fixed in an upper portion of the spring so as to maintain the form of the separation film pipe and spring to the fixing ring in operation of the vacuum pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective view of a carbon dioxide separating apparatus.
   Main body (10)
   Connection part (11)
   Bolt hole (12)
   Inlet port (20)
   Separation film pipe (30)
   Simplicity collecting tubes (40)
   Flow tube (50)
   Discharge pipe (60)
   Vent (70)
   Temporary discharge outlet (80)

PN CN114262430-A
TI Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol for solid tire, is prepared by adding propylene oxide and carbon dioxide to carry out polymerization reaction under the effect of double metal cyanide catalyst.
AU GONG W
   ZHANG D
   WANG T
   YU T
   SUN Z
AE SHANDONG INOV NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202251383W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol is prepared by adding propylene oxide and carbon dioxide to carry out polymerization reaction under the effect of double metal cyanide catalyst, obtaining intermediate polycarbonate ether polyol alcohol, and then under the action of phosphazene catalyst, the intermediate polycarbonate ether polyol is polymerized with propylene oxide, and ethylene oxide is terminated to obtain the polycarbonate ether polyol; The functionality is 2.0-3.0, and the hydroxyl value is 18-23 mgKOH/g.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol for solid tire.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol uses carbon dioxide as raw material, reduces the cost, green and environment-friendly, the polyurethane solid tire prepared by it has good mechanical property and wear-resistant property and good hydrolysis resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of polycarbonate ether polyol and carbonate group is 10-20 wt.%. The composite starting agent molecular polyether polyol with high functionality and low functionality, functionality is 2.0-6.0, the molecular weight is 200-3000g/mol. The DMC catalyst is 0.02-0.10 wt.% of the total mass of the intermediate polycarbonate ether polyol. The mass of the propylene oxide for initiating the aggregated reaction is 8-16 wt.% of the mass of the composite initiator. The adding amount of the phosphazene catalyst is 0.08-0.40 wt.% of the mass of the crude polycarbonate ether polyol. The mass of the ethylene oxide of the terminated polymerization is 8-30 wt.% of the total mass of the crude polycarbonate ether polyol. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol is prepared by adding the composite agent and DMC catalyst into the high pressure reaction kettle, replacing with carbon dioxide, dehydrating, introducing propylene oxide to initiate polymerization, then adding carbon dioxide and propylene oxide to react to obtain intermediate polycarbonate ether polyol, adding the intermediate polycarbonate ether polyol obtained and the phosphazene catalyst into a pressure-resistant reaction kettle, replacing with nitrogen, heating, adding propylene oxide for polymerization, adding ethylene oxide to carry out end-capping polymerization, obtaining crude polycarbonate ether polyol, after processing the crude polycarbonate ether polyol obtained and obtaining the polycarbonate ether polyol. The crude polycarbonate ether polyol is added with acid, water and adsorbent for post-processing, filtering to obtain the polycarbonate ether polyol. Preferred Components: The DMC catalyst is zinc-cobalt double metal cyanide complex catalyst.

   WANG T
   FENG H
AE PETROLEUM ENG TECHNOLOGY RES INST JIANGS (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2022107174
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide solubilizing viscosity-reducing agent i.e. methoxybenzene compound (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in the production of low-efficiency heavy oil reservoirs, and in preparing carbon dioxide solubilizing viscosity-reducing system for oil flooding and exploitation in low-efficiency heavy oil reservoirs (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide solubilizing viscosity-reducing agent i.e. methoxybenzene compound of formula (I) is new.
   n = 0-20;
   x and y = 0-20; and
   x+y = greater than or equal to3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for
   1. preparation of (I); and
   2. carbon dioxide solubilizing and viscosity-reducing system comprising 5-50 wt.% carbon dioxide solubilizing and viscosity-reducing agent and 50-95 wt.% auxiliary agent, where the auxiliary agent is straight chain or branched chain 2-10C alkanol and/or 3-10C alkane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises (i) reacting ethylenediamine with benzyl chloride under alkaline conditions to obtain N,N'-dibenzylethylenediamine, (ii) reacting alkylphenol polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether with thionyl chloride under heating to obtain chlorinated alkylphenol polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether, and (iii) reacting N,N'-dibenzylethylenediamine with chlorinated alkylphenol polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether under alkaline conditions, under solvent and heating to obtain final product.Preferred Components: The number average molecular weight of the carbon dioxide solubilizing and viscosity reducing agent is 600-4000 g/mol, preferably 800-3000 g/mol. The alkylphenol polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether is compound of formula (II). The chloroalkylphenol polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether is compound of formula (III). In step (i), the sodium hydroxide is used to form an alkaline environment, the molar ratio between benzyl chloride and ethylenediamine is 2-2.4:1. In step (ii), the molar ratio between alkylphenol polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether and thionyl chloride is 1:1.6-2. The auxiliary agent is ethanol, propanol, n-hexane, cyclohexane, and/or n-heptane. The (I) and carbon dioxide are used in a mass ratio of 0.8-5:1, preferably 1-3:1.Preferred Method: The step (iii) adopts the mixture of water and alcohol as solvent, and then reacting under pH of 10-12, and the molar ratio between water and alcohol is 1:2.2-2.5.

PN CN111675609-A
TI Preparing acetic acid by one-step conversion of methane and carbon dioxide, comprises using synergistic effect of low-temperature plasma and supported copper-based catalyst to activate methane and carbon dioxide.
AU WANG L
   DU X
   XU H
   ZHU Y
AE UNIV DALIAN MARITIME (UYDM-C)
GA 202093663M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing acetic acid by one-step conversion of methane and carbon dioxide, comprises using the synergistic effect of low-temperature plasma and supported copper-based catalyst to activate methane and carbon dioxide to selectively generate acetic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing acetic acid by one-step conversion of methane and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realizes the conversion of methane and carbon dioxide into high value-added acetic acid in a one-step process under low temperature and normal pressure conditions; is simple and environmentally-friendly; can solves the problem of high temperature (usually not greater than 700 degrees C) required for the conventional catalytic methane and carbon dioxide reaction, at the same time, improves added value of two conversion products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supported copper-based catalyst includes Cu(x)O and carrier; the value of x = 1-2; the carrier is titanium dioxide, fumed silicon dioxide, S-1, TS-1 and/or HZSM-5; the weight of the Cu(x)O in the supported copper-based catalyst is 0.1-40 wt.%. The supported copper-based catalyst includes second active component and/or an auxiliary agent; the second active component is gold, cobalt, iron, rhodium, iridium and/or ruthenium; the auxiliary agent is alkali metal and/or rare earth metal. When the supported copper-based catalyst includes Cu(x)O, carrier, and second active component; the molar ratio of the Cu(x)O to the second active component is 1-10:1; the weight of the Cu(x)O and the second active component in the supported copper-based catalyst is 0.1-40 wt.%. When the supported copper-based catalyst includes Cu(x)O, carrier, and auxiliary agent; the molar ratio of the Cu(x)O to the auxiliary agent is 1-10:1; the weight of the Cu(x)O and the auxiliary agent in supported copper-based catalyst is 0.1-40 wt.%. When the supported copper-based catalyst includes Cu(x)O, carrier, a second active component, and auxiliary agent; the molar ratio of the Cu(x)O to the second active component of the doping aid is 1-10:1; the weight of the Cu(x)O and the second active component of the doping auxiliary agent to supported copper-based catalyst is 0.1-40 wt.%. The molar ratio of methane and carbon dioxide is 0.5-2:1. Preferred Method: In the method, the supported copper-based catalyst is filled in the dielectric barrier discharge area. In the method, reaction temperature is 25-200 degrees C. In the method, residence time of the methane and carbon dioxide in the dielectric barrier discharge area is 4-8 seconds.

PN CN111514694-A
TI Recovering glass flue gas comprises e.g. carrying out desulfurization and denitrification treatment on glass flue gas, recycling sulfides and nitrogen oxides in glass flue gas, adsorbing the carbon dioxide gas, liquefying and storing.
AU PENG S
   CHANG Z
AE CHINA TRIUMPH INT ENG CO LTD (CHNB-C)
GA 202078764U
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering glass flue gas comprises (i) carrying out desulfurization and denitrification treatment on glass flue gas to obtain desulfurization and denitrification gas, and recycling sulfides and nitrogen oxides in the glass flue gas, (ii) adsorbing the carbon dioxide gas in the desulfurization and denitration gas to obtain solid carbon dioxide, (iii) desorbing and enriching the solid carbon dioxide to obtain first purified gas, (iv) compressing the first purified gas, and then performing adsorption treatment to obtain second purified gas, (v) rectifying the second purified gas to obtain rectified carbon dioxide gas, and(vi) liquefying and storing the rectified carbon dioxide gas to obtain carbon dioxide liquefied gas.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering glass flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the recycling of waste gas; and protects the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) includes (i.i) using induced draft fan, introducing the glass flue gas into a water washing tower, and filling the water washing tower with ozone and oxidizing the glass flue gas to obtain oxide glass flue gas, (i.ii) passing the oxide glass flue gas into the lye in the water washing tower for desulfurization and denitration to obtain desulfurization and denitration gas and desulfurization and denitration solution, and(i.iii) adding air to the desulfurization and denitration liquid, oxidizing and crystallizing the sulfite and nitrite in the desulfurization and denitration solution to obtain sulfate and nitrate, and realizing the recycling of sulfide and nitrogen oxide in glass flue gas. In the step (ii), using pressure swing adsorption device for adsorbing the carbon dioxide gas in the desulfurization and denitration gas to obtain solid carbon dioxide. The step (iii) comprises (iii.i) desorbing the solid carbon dioxide in the pressure swing adsorption device to obtain carbon dioxide gas, and(iii.ii) enriching the carbon dioxide gas to obtain first purified gas. The step (v) includes (v.i) passing the second purified gas into a desulfurization bed for desulfurization to obtain desulfurization gas, (v.ii) passing the desulfurization gas into a drying bed for drying to obtain dry desulfurization gas, and(v.iii) passing the dry desulfurized gas into an adsorption bed for adsorption to obtain rectified carbon dioxide gas. The method further includes (ai) continuously feeding the external residual gas into the drying bed, heating, and discharging the residual gas from the exhaust valve of the drying bed, (aii) performing temperature measurement of the gas discharged from the exhaust valve, and stopping passing the residual gas into the drying bed when the measured temperature is greater than the first set temperature. The method comprises (bi) continuously passing the heated residual gas into the adsorption bed, and discharging the residual gas from the discharge valve of the adsorption bed, and(bii) measuring the temperature of the gas discharged from the discharge valve, and stopping the residual gas from passing into the drying bed when the measured temperature is greater than the second set temperature. The drying bed adsorbs moisture and grease in the desulfurized gas to obtain the dry desulfurized gas. The adsorption bed adsorbs nitrogen oxides, mercury, arsenic and phosphides in the dry desulfurization gas to obtain rectified carbon dioxide gas. Preferred Components: The residual gas is carbon dioxide, water and/or nitrogen oxide.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110568035-A; CN110568035-B
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction material by constructing electrochemical system and loading isoreticular metal-organic framework-8/isoreticular metal-organic framework-1 dispersion liquid on working electrode.
AU LIN P
   CAO Y
   XU Z
   WU T
   ZENG X
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 2019A6563V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction material involves constructing electrochemical system including reference electrode, counter electrode, working electrode and electrolyte, loading isoreticular metal-organic framework-8 or isoreticular metal-organic framework-1 dispersion liquid on the working electrode, applying voltage to the working electrode and the reference electrode, breaking zinc acetate bond in isoreticular metal-organic framework-8 or isoreticular metal-organic framework-1, depositing zinc in situ on the surface of the working electrode and generating zinc nanoparticles for carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction. The working electrode is glassy carbon.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction material with excellent chemical stability. The carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction material improves efficiency of carbon dioxide reduction and selectivity of carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conductive filler is conductive carbon black, conductive graphite and/or conductive mica. The reference electrode is silver/silver chloride electrode. The counter electrode is platinum sheet electrode. Preferred Property: The particle diameter of the zinc nanoparticles is greater than 3 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The isoreticular metal-organic framework-8 dispersion liquid is prepared by dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid in N,N-dimethylformamide to obtain mixed solution, heating the mixed solution to covalently combine zinc in zinc nitrate hexahydrate with carboxyl group in 2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid to form isoreticular metal-organic framework-8, dispersing the isoreticular metal-organic framework-8 and conductive filler in mixed solution of Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer copolymer) and ethanol and ultrasonically processing for 30-60 minutes. The isoreticular metal-organic framework-1 dispersion liquid is prepared by dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate and terephthalic acid in N,N-dimethylformamide to obtain mixed solution, heating the mixed solution to covalently combine zinc in zinc nitrate hexahydrate with carboxyl group in terephthalic acid to form isoreticular metal-organic framework-1, dispersing the isoreticular metal-organic framework-1 and conductive filler in mixed solution of Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer copolymer) and ethanol and ultrasonically processing for 30-60 minutes.

   USE - An adsorption agent for electrode for carbon dioxide reduction used in carbon-dioxide reducing device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption agent has excellent carbon dioxide retention and electroconductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An adsorption agent comprises porous metal complex which has center metal and imidazole-based ligand of formula (I) coordinated to center metal.
   Ar1-Ar3=aromatic hydrocarbon ring optionally substituted by 1-4C alkyl, provided that COOH and OH bonded to aromatic hydrocarbon ring are bonded with 2 adjacent C atoms in the aromatic hydrocarbon ring.

AU WANG J
   ZHANG S
   WANG H
   CHEN J
   SONG J
   XU J
   WANG W
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)
GA 201905057P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for propagating poplar tree, involves using sugar-free medium to culture tissue culture seedlings, using carbon dioxide instead of sugar as a carbon source, and autotrophically allowing to grow by photosynthesis.
   USE - The method is useful for propagating poplar tree.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves survival rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for tissue culture propagation device, which has carbon dioxide generator and a valve for controlling flow rate of carbon dioxide at exit, the carbon dioxide generator is used to generate, store and release carbon dioxide gas, culture box containing sugar-free medium, an inlet of valve connected to an outlet of the carbon dioxide generator, an outlet of the valve in communication with a gas inlet of incubator through a conduit, LED tube arranged above the culture box, and a light source for photosynthesis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The sugar-free medium is a modified WPM medium without sugar. The WPM medium comprises an inorganic salt and an organic substance. The inorganic salt comprises 400 mg/l ammonium nitrate, 556 mg/l calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, 990 mg/l potassium sulfate, 96 mg/l calcium chloride dihydrate, 170 mg/l monopotassium phosphate, 0.25 mg/l sodium molybdate dihydrate, 370 mg/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 22.4 mg/l manganese(II) sulfate monohydrate, 8.6 mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.25 mg/l copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate, 27.8 mg/l ferrous sulfate heptahydrate and 37.3 mg/l disodium EDTA. The organic matter comprises 100 mg/l inositol, 1 mg/l vitamin B1, 0.5 mg/l niacin, 0.5 mg/l vitamin B6, 2 mg/l glycine, 5 mg/l glutamic acid and 6.5 g/l agar and has pH of 5.8. Preferred Device: The carbon dioxide is provided at air inlet speed of 0.015-0.035 ml/second. The LED lamp tube has illumination intensity 15000-20000 lx, the illumination is performed for 10-13 hours a day. The culture box temperature is 25-30 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN111534371-A
TI Producing walnut oil using supercritical carbon            dioxide extraction comprises drying washed walnut            kernels, crushing, adjusting the temperature,            liquefying, extracting oleic acid, and controlling the            separation pressure.
AU LI H
AE GUANGYUAN HAINUO FOOD CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202080700H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing walnut oil using supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction comprises washing the                fresh walnut kernels, drying in a drying box,                crushing, adding the pretreated walnut kernel                powder into extraction kettle, then adding acetone                solution to extraction kettle, adjusting the                temperature in the extraction kettle to 30-35                degrees C, allowing to stand, turning on carbon                dioxide cylinder, liquefying the carbon dioxide gas                released from the carbon dioxide cylinder, then                passing to a preheater for preheating, then                increasing the pressure to a preset value to make                carbon dioxide a supercritical liquid, extracting                the oleic acid in the walnut kernel, then releasing                the pressure in the extraction kettle to normal                pressure to obtain a mixture, adding the mixture                into oil-liquid separator, and controlling the                separation pressure for separating the walnut oil                from oil-liquid separator.
   USE - The method is useful for producing walnut oil                using supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the oil yield of walnuts                and the dissolution rate of unsaturated fatty                acids.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing walnut oil using supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction comprises (a) washing the                fresh walnut kernels, drying in a drying box, and                crushing through 30-35 mesh sieve, (b) adding the                pretreated walnut kernel powder into extraction                kettle, then adding 1.5-2 mol/l acetone solution to                extraction kettle, adjusting the temperature in the                extraction kettle to 30-35 degrees C, and allowing                to stand for 0.5-1 hour, (c) turning on carbon                dioxide cylinder, liquefying the carbon dioxide gas                released from the carbon dioxide cylinder, then                passing to a preheater for preheating, passing to                the extraction kettle, then increasing the pressure                to a preset value to make carbon dioxide a                supercritical liquid, extracting the oleic acid in                the walnut kernel for 4 hours, and then releasing                the pressure in the extraction kettle to normal                pressure to obtain a mixture, (d) adding the                mixture into oil-liquid separator, and controlling                the separation pressure of 10-12 Mpa for separating                the walnut oil from oil-liquid separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The walnut kernel is                  dried until the moisture content is less than 3%.                  Preferred Method: The drying in the step (a) is                  carried out at 35-40 degrees C. The pressure and                  temperature inside the extraction kettle in the                  step (c), respectively is 30 MPa, and 30 degrees                  C. The separation in the step (d) is carried out                  at 35 degrees C.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN107937276-A; CN107937276-B
TI Mixing and controlling carbon dioxide and acetic acid for promoting carbon fixation growth of Chlorella, involves regulating and culturing Chlorella using both carbon dioxide and acetic acid during culture period and reduces production cost.
AU GE B
   YU Q
   HUANG F
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2018327372
AB    NOVELTY - Mixing and controlling carbon dioxide and acetic acid involves regulating and culturing Chlorella using both carbon dioxide and acetic acid during the culture period.
   USE - Method for mixing and controlling carbon dioxide and acetic acid for promoting carbon fixation growth of Chlorella.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to mix and control carbon dioxide and acetic acid, which significantly increases the biomass production of the Chlorella and the fullness of the algae cells, increases the carbon fixation efficiency of the Chlorella, reduces the production cost, and reduces the contamination contamination probability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The regulated cultivation of Chlorella using both carbon dioxide and acetic acid involves adjusting carbon dioxide during the day, and the pH is adjusted by sodium carbonate/carbon dioxide buffer system for photosynthetic autotrophic culture. During night, acetic acid is added to adjust the pH, and heterotrophic culture is performed using acetic acid as a carbon source. During the culture cycle, the first 2/3 cycle is fed with carbon dioxide and the latter is followed by acetic acid. The regulated cultivation of Chlorella using both carbon dioxide and acetic acid involves adjusting algae culture medium pH to 6.5-7.5, the light intensity is 13200 Lux, the culture temperature is 25 plus minus 1 degrees C, and passage is performed every 10 days. An air/carbon dioxide mixture with a carbon dioxide concentration of 5% is introduced during the day in the algae culture medium, and acetic acid is added at night to maintain the pH of the algal fluid at 6.5-7.5. The 1.5% agar is added to algae culture medium, adjusting the pH to 6.5-7.5, sterilizing and plating, the light intensity is 13200 Lux, the culture temperature is 25 plus minus 1 degrees C, every other month, passaging once. Preferred Condition: The culture period is 4-12 days. The carbon dioxide is used to regulated the culture of Chlorella, carbon dioxide is introduced into the culture medium through nanometer aeration tube aeration. During conditioned culture of Chlorella with acetic acid, the acetic acid is added to the culture medium. The flow acceleration is controlled at pH 6.5-7.5. The stand still culture or shake culture at 100-120 rpm in a horizontal shaking shaker is used. Preferred Composition: The algal fluid medium is BG-11 medium, which comprises 1-2g/l sodium nitrate, 0.03-0.05g/l potassium hydrogen phosphate trihydrate, 0.07~0.08g/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.035~0.038g/l calcium chloride hexahydrate, 0.0005-0.0015g/l disodium ethylenediaminetetraacetate dihydrate,0.01-0.03g/l sodium carbonate, 0.005-0.007g/l citric acid, 0.005-0.007g/l ferric ammonium citrate, 2.5-3mg/l boric acid, 1.5-2mg/l manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate, 0.1-0.3mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.3-0.4mg/l sodium molybdate dihydrate, 0.07-0.08mg/l copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate , 0.03-0.05mg/l cobalt(II) chloride hexahydrate.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A04A; D05-H08A
IP C12N-001/12; C12R-001/89

PT P
PN EP4071274-A1; CN115198300-A; JP2022161022-A; US2022323900-A1
TI Carbon dioxide capture device for solar-driven            direct air carbon dioxide capture and separation,            comprises first reactor comprising anion exchange            membrane, and second reactor comprising proton exchange            membrane.
AU JOHNSON H
   KINGE S
   TSAMPAS M
   ZAFEIROPOULOS G
   JOHANSSON H
AE TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
   DUTCH FUNDAMENTAL ENERGY RES INST (DUFU-Non-standard)
   TOYOTA MOTOR CORP (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
GA 2022C8902N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capture device comprises a                first reactor comprising an anion exchange membrane                placed between a porous anode and porous cathode,                and a second reactor comprising a proton exchange                membrane. The anode contains or is connected                physically or electrically to oxygen evolution                and/or carbon dioxide evolution catalyst(s), and                the cathode contains/is connected                physically/electrically to an oxygen reduction                catalyst. The cathode of the first reactor has at                least a fluid inlet (214) able to carry carbon                dioxide, air and water, and the anode of first                reactor has at least a fluid inlet (215) able to                carry water and oxygen. The porous cathode of the                second reactor has at least a fluid outlet able to                carry carbon dioxide and water, and the porous                anode of the second reactor has at least a fluid                outlet able to carry water and oxygen.
   USE - The device is useful: for capturing for carbon                dioxide (claimed); and for solar-driven direct air                carbon dioxide capture and separation of carbon                dioxide from ambient air or from point                (concentrated) sources.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: can capture carbon dioxide from                ambient air or from point (concentrated) sources in                an efficient manner; and is an easy-to-implement,                stand-alone device and/or a new method for                separation of oxygen and carbon dioxide by                converting oxygen to water (gas to liquid);                provides a mechanism for a simple, solid-state                device design which captures CO2 and produces                electricity simultaneously - incentivizing its                use.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates combined device                configurations where capture and separation                functions are combined into one device.

PT P
PN CN112266475-A; CN112266475-B
TI Carbon dioxide-based polyester polyol used for preparing fully biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyurethane, is prepared from poly(carbonate-ether) polyol, sodium sulfonate-containing dibasic acid, dicarboxylic acid and catalyst.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHOU Q
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202112261T
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide-based polyester polyol is prepared from 800-2300 pts. wt. poly(carbonate-ether) polyol, 3.5-20 pts. wt. sodium sulfonate-containing dibasic acid (ester), 35-52 pts. wt. dicarboxylic acid and 0.12-1.6 pt. wt. catalyst. The sodium sulfonate-containing dibasic acid (ester) is sodium isophthalic acid-5-sulfonate, sodium terephthalate-2-sulfonate, sodium dioctyl sulfosuccinate, sodium diethyl sulfosuccinate, 1,3-dimethyl phthalate-5-sodium sulfonate and/or sodium diisobutyl sulfosuccinate.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based polyester polyol is used for preparing fully biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyurethane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based polyester polyol improves biodegradability of the polyurethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the carbon dioxide-based polyester polyol, which involves mixing poly(carbonate-ether) polyol, sodium sulfonate-containing dibasic acid (ester) and dicarboxylic acid, reacting at 110-160 degrees C for 1-3 hours in nitrogen protection atmosphere, then heating the reaction product to 180-260 degrees C, adding the catalyst, vacuuming to 1 kPa, and reacting for 2-5 hours;
   (2) a fully biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyurethane, which is prepared from 80-150 pts. wt. carbon dioxide polyester polyol, 25-40 pts. wt. aliphatic diisocyanate, 0.01-0.05 pt. wt. catalyst, 0.15-1.1 pt. wt. antioxidant and 2.4-6.5 pts. wt. chain extender; and
   (3) preparation of the fully biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyurethane, which involves heating the carbon dioxide polyester polyol to 90-120 degrees C, reducing pressure for 1-2 hours, adding aliphatic diisocyanate, reacting for 1-3 hours, adding catalyst and antioxidant to the reaction product, reacting for 1-2 hours, heating the reaction product to 150-180 degrees C, adding chain extender, and reacting for 2-5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The dicarboxylic acid is 1,3-malonic acid, 1,4-succinic acid, 1,6-adipic acid, 1,7-pimelic acid, 1,8-suberic acid, 1,9-azelaic acid, 1,10-sebacic acid, 1,11-undecane dibasic acid and/or 1,12-dodecane dibasic acid. The catalyst is p-toluenesulfonic acid, isopropyl titanate, n-butyl titanate, ethylene glycol titanium, antimony trioxide, antimony acetate, ethylene glycol antimony, germanium dioxide, dibutyltin dilaurate, 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone p-toluenesulfonate, N-methylimidazolium p-toluenesulfonate and/or 1-(3-sulfonic acid)propyl-3-methylimidazolium p-toluenesulfonate. The aliphatic diisocyanate is 1,6-hexamethylene diisocyanate, methylcyclohexyl diisocyanate, dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate and/or isophorone diisocyanate. The catalyst is stannous octoate, dibutyltin dilaurate, bismuth neodecanoate, bismuth laurate, bismuth isooctanoate and/or bismuth naphthenate. The antioxidant is 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol, triethylene glycol bis(3-tert-butyl-4-hydroxy-5-methylphenyl)propionate, pentaerythritol tetrakis( beta -(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate), n-octadecyl beta -(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl) propionate and/or N,N'-bis-(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionyl)hexamethylenediamine. The chain extender is ethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,7-heptanediol, 1,8-octanediol and/or 1,4-cyclohexanediol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The molecular weight of poly(carbonate-ether) polyol is 1000-5000 g/mol. The acid value of carbon dioxide polyester polyol is less than 0.8 mg potassium hydroxide/g. Preferred Composition: The poly(carbonate-ether) polyol has carbonate content of 30-80 wt.%.

TI Hot air device for e.g. greenhouse, comprises e.g. carbon dioxide generator provided at lower side of multi-stage heat transfer tube and provided on bottom side of case to generate carbon dioxide and installation hole formed on side of case.
AU JAEBOK K
AE JAEBOK K (JAEB-Individual)
GA 202027700T
AB    NOVELTY - Hot air device comprises a discharge pipe connected to an end portion of a multi-stage heat transfer pipe located in the direction of movement of the hot air to discharge hot air to the outside of a case (10); a carbon dioxide generator which is provided at a lower side of the multi-stage heat transfer tube and is provided on the bottom side of case to generate carbon dioxide; a discharge side end (60a) of a carbon dioxide discharge passage (60) is positioned at the upper end side of at least one blowers (40); an observation member is provided to allow a user to observe inside case located on the upper side of a through hole (15); an installation hole (17) formed on one side of case; an insertion communication pipe provided with carbon dioxide emission passage; a controller (70) provided to selectively uses warm air and carbon dioxide; a protective cover member (92) for protecting carbon dioxide generator; and a heat discharging passage (45) for discharging hot air.
   USE - The device is useful for indoors e.g. greenhouses for growing horticultural or agricultural crops, and glass greenhouses.
   ADVANTAGE - The device promotes cultivation of high quality and high yield crops; can stably supply carbon dioxide necessary for photosynthesis of plants; and selectively uses warm air and carbon dioxide according to working conditions and used by manual operation or automatic setting.

PN KR1904835-B1
TI Integrated control module for carbon dioxide safe fire extinguishing system in building, has control unit controlling lock so as to lift door from locked state if carbon dioxide concentration inside fire area is above preset concentration.
AU RYU S H
   HONG Y E
   HAN H C
   JO D H
AE FIRE ENG & SCI TECHNOLOGY (FIRE-Non-standard)
   KOREA CERTIFIED TEST & RES INST (KOCE-Non-standard)
GA 2018823343
AB    NOVELTY - The module (300) has a control unit (390) for controlling fire in the fire area based on a signal from a fire sensor or human sensing signal from a human body access sensor (190). A concentration measuring unit includes a carbon dioxide sensor (131) installed at the fire area and measures the carbon dioxide concentration remaining inside the fire area and delivers the signal corresponding to measured carbon dioxide concentration and an oxygen sensor (132) installed at the fire area to measure the oxygen content remaining inside the fire area and deliver the signal corresponding to measured oxygen content (130). A door lock (161) is installed at door (160) of the fire area. The control unit controls the door lock so that the lock lifts the door of the fire area from a locked state in case the concentration of the carbon dioxide remaining inside the fire area is above pre-set safe concentration or less, where the module is implemented in exterior wall of fire area in the form of panel.
   USE - Integrated control module for a carbon dioxide safe fire extinguishing system in a building.
   ADVANTAGE - The module enables controlling the carbon dioxide safe fire extinguishing system in a stable manner to prevent the carbon dioxide-induced suffocation of on-site personnel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an integrated control module for carbon dioxide safe fire extinguishing system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Measured oxygen content (130)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (131)
   Oxygen sensor (132)
   Door (160)
   Door lock (161)
   Human body access sensor (190)
   Integrated control module (300)
   Control unit (390)

PN CN114958274-A
TI Carbon dioxide carbonyl reaction type polyurethane            hot melt adhesive useful for bonding low-polarity            material comprises carbon dioxide dihydric alcohol,            carbon-dioxide based tetrahydric alcohol,            polyphosphoric acid polylol, diisocyanate, tackifying            resin, antioxidant and catalyst.
AU LI S
   LIN H
   CAO Y
AE WELDTONE XIAMEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WELD-Non-standard)
GA 2022B53896
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide carbonyl reaction type                polyurethane hot melt adhesive comprises 60-80 pts.                wt. carbon dioxide dihydric alcohol, 5-10 pts. wt.                carbon-dioxide based tetrahydric alcohol, 1-2 pts.                wt. polyphosphoric acid polylol, 16-24 pts. wt.                diisocyanate, 8-15 pts. wt. tackifying resin, 1-2                pts. wt. antioxidant, 0.01-0.05 pts. wt. catalyst.                The preparation of the polyphosphoric acid polyol                comprises: polyphosphoric acid is obtained by                polymerizing with carbon dioxide and an epoxy                compound under a double metal cyanide catalyst;                where the catalyst is a complex of                tris(1-phenyl-isoquinoline) iridium and an                organotin compound in a mass ratio of (0.8-1.2):                1.
   USE - The carbon dioxide carbonyl reaction type                polyurethane hot melt adhesive is useful for                bonding a low-polarity material.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide carbonyl reaction type                polyurethane hot melt adhesive has low polarity of                polypropylene material having high bonding force,                under the condition of not passing any flame                treatment, performing direct bonding, and at low                temperature. It has high bonding strength, has very                fast surface drying speed, cross-linked cured hot                melt colloid is easy to be removed from the surface                of the adhesive component and has no residue, and                has good heat resistance, storage stability and                environment aging resistance. It will not cause                damage of the valuable electronic device, and is                good for recycling and reusing the electronic                precision instrument device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                carbon dioxide carbonyl reaction type polyurethane                hot melt adhesive.

TI New non-naturally occurring carbonic anhydrase            comprising mutation that results in substitution of            cysteine for amino acid in naturally occurring carbonic            anhydrase, used for performing carbon dioxide            separation and carbon dioxide capture.
AU ZHANG M
   LUNIN V V
   WEI H
AE ALLIANCE SUSTAINABLE ENERGY LLC (ALLU-C)
GA 2022A8577S
AB    NOVELTY - A non-naturally occurring carbonic anhydrase                comprising mutation that results in the                substitution of cysteine for amino acid in a                naturally occurring carbonic anhydrase, is new. The                non-naturally occurring carbonic anhydrase has                increased activity at a temperature of greater than                60&#176; C when compared to the naturally                occurring carbonic anhydrase.
   USE - The non-naturally occurring carbonic anhydrase                is useful for performing carbon dioxide separation                and carbon dioxide capture (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The non-naturally occurring carbonic anhydrase                has increased activity at a temperature of greater                than 60&#176; C, preferably 90&#176; C when                compared to the naturally occurring carbonic                anhydrase for more than 30 minutes, preferably 92                hours, has enhanced specific activity, thermal                stability, chemical stability, robustness,                catalytic activity, thermostability and solvent                compatibility, performs carbon dioxide separation                and carbon dioxide capture with reduced energy                consumption, cost and energy requirement, and                utilizes environmentally friendly solvents to                improve process sustainability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   method for performing carbon dioxide                separation and carbon dioxide capture involving                reacting carbon dioxide with a non-naturally                occurring carbonic anhydrase comprising mutation                that results in the substitution of cysteine for                amino acid in a naturally occurring carbonic                anhydrase; and
   ystem comprising the non-naturally occurring                carbonic anhydrase, where the system further                comprises a support in which the non-naturally                occurring carbonic anhydrase is immobilized to the                support, and the non-naturally occurring carbonic                anhydrase is contacted with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given.                  Preferred Biomolecule: The nucleotide sequence                  encoding the non-naturally occurring carbonic                  anhydrase comprises a base pair sequence having                  greater than 70% identical to one of 5 (762-768)                  base pair sequences (SEQ ID NOs: 6, 8, 22, 24 and                  26) all fully defined in the specification. The                  non-naturally occurring carbonic anhydrase                  comprises an amino acid sequence having greater                  than 70% identical to one of 5 (253-255) amino                  acid sequences (SEQ ID NOs: 5, 7, 21, 23 and 25)                  all fully defined in the specification. The                  to-be-separated and captured carbon dioxide                  results from the combustion of fossil fuels or                  biomass. The carbon capture unit comprises an                  immobilized biocatalyst comprising the                  above-mentioned amino acid sequence. Preferred                  Method: The method involves reacting carbon                  dioxide with non-naturally occurring carbonic                  anhydrase at a temperature greater than                  65&#176; C, preferably 90&#176; C for                  more than 30 minutes, preferably 92 hours.

GA 2022860831
AB    NOVELTY - Ultra-low carbon emission blast furnace                ironmaking system for carbon dioxide resource                utilization comprises blast furnace body having                multiple tuyere air supply devices evenly                distributed on the cross section of the blast                furnace cylinder, pure oxygen is blown into the                blast furnace cylinder and the blast furnace                outputs nitrogen-free or ultra-low nitrogen blast                furnace metallurgical gas, after the blast furnace                gas is pressurized, carbon dioxide is removed, the                removed carbon dioxide is pressurized and then                transported into the plasma rectangular body for                heating through pipelines, the high temperature                carbon dioxide heated by the plasma moment enters                the reforming chamber through the plasma moment                nozzle, and after removing carbon dioxide, the                high-pressure blast furnace gas is injected into                the reforming chamber together with the pulverized                coal through the pulverized coal nozzle as the gas                carrying the pulverized coal.

   ADVANTAGE - The system provides carbon dioxide resource                utilization of ultra-low carbon discharge blast                furnace iron making system, capable of solving the                problem in the background technology, and reducing                carbon emission.

AU JIANG W
   GUO Z
   YUE J
   ZHOU D
   YANG Y
   YIN Z
   GUO H
   FAN B
   LI Z
   WU G
   WU Z
   XING X
AE BEIJING RES CENT CHINA NAT OFFSHORE OIL (CNOO-C)
   CNOOC CHINA CO LTD (CNOO-C)
   BEIJING RES CENT CNOOC CHINA LTD (CNOO-C)
   CNOOC CHINA LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 202246341P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide corrosion resistant                material comprises (S1) mixing forsterite,                manganese olivine and calcium forsterite and ball                milling, (S2) successively drying ball-milled                material and pressing to obtain a test piece, (S3)                pre-sintering the test piece, cooling and then                pulverizing to obtain a powder, (S4) performing                calcination of the powder at a high temperature,                and quenching to obtain the carbon dioxide                corrosion resistant material.
   USE - The carbon dioxide corrosion resistant                material is useful in the preparation of carbon                dioxide corrosion-resistant cementation caement                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is reliable, and has high yield,                and low requirements on the quality of raw                materials. The prepared product has high                uniformity, good chemical stability and strong                hydration ability, improves the anti-corrosion                performance of the cement stone through the                admixture of olivine, and can also enhance the                resistance of the oil well cement; ensures that the                cement sheath has better integrity; and and is                suitable for large-scale industrial                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a carbon dioxide corrosion resistant                material;
   (2) a use of carbon dioxide corrosion                resistant material in the preparation of carbon                dioxide corrosion-resistant cementation cement,                where the cementing cement stone is Portland cement                stone; and
   (3) a carbon dioxide corrosion cementation                cement stone comprising Portland cement stone and                carbon dioxide corrosion resistant material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (S1), the                  average particle diameters of the forsterite, the                  manganese olivine and the forsterite are all less                  than or equal to 50 mum. The mass ratio of the                  forsterite, the manganese olivine and the                  forsterite is 80: 4-15: 5-20. In the                  manufacturing method, the tablet pressing                  conditions are as follows: the pressure is                  50-65KN, the pressure increasing rate is 0.2KN/s,                  the pressure is released after maintaining the                  pressure for 185-200 seconds, and the pressure is                  less than or equal to 4.5KN during pressure                  release. The mass percentage content of the                  carbon dioxide corrosion resistant material in                  the Portland cement of the Portland cement stone                  is 4-7%Preferred Method: In step (S3), the                  pre-sintering steps are: pre-sintering to room                  temperature of 850degrees Celsius, where the                  heating time is 45-55 minutes. The holding time                  at 850degrees C is 5-20 minutes. The heating time                  at 850-1250degrees Celsius is 30-45 minutes. The                  heating time at 1250-1350degrees Celsius is 30                  minutes, and the holding time is 40-120 minutes.                  The cooling method is natural cooling or wind                  cooling. The pulverizing method is airflow                  pulverization, and the particle size of the                  powder after the airflow pulverization is 15-35                  mum. In step S4, the conditions of the                  high-temperature calcination are: the temperature                  is 1500-1600degrees Celsius, and the time is                  20-35 seconds. and the quenching method is liquid                  nitrogen quenching.

PN CN113236201-A
TI Dry ice sublimation gas pressure injection process, involves transporting dry ice to gas injection site, heating, sublimating, reheating, compressing to form gas phase carbon dioxide, and injecting carbon dioxide into gas injection well.
AU CAO S
   GU F
   LIAO S
   CUI Y
   LIU F
   LI Y
   DING J
   YANG X
AE CHINA PETRO-CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROCHEMICAL CORP EAST CHINA PET (SNPC-C)
   JIANGSU HUAYANG LIQUID CARBON CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202194949D
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves transporting the dry ice to the gas injection site, heating, sublimating, reheating, compressing to form gas phase carbon dioxide, and injecting carbon dioxide into the gas injection well. The dry ice is transported to the gas injection site. The dry ice is converted to the pre-feeding box through the automatic conveyor. The dry ice in the pre-feeding box is put into the dry ice sublimation reactor through the spin-in feeder for sublimation. The gas-phase carbon dioxide after sublimation is discharged from the upper outlet of the dry ice sublimation reactor, which enters the gasifier for heating. The heated gas-phase carbon dioxide is entered into the carbon dioxide buffer tank for buffering. The buffered gas phase carbon dioxide is entered into the compressor used for oilfield associated gas recovery and injection, and is injected into the gas injection well after four-stage boosting and four-stage cooling.
   USE - Dry ice sublimation gas pressure injection process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process solves the problem of oil fields with wide area, ineffective matching of sources and sinks, and long transportation distance of liquid carbon dioxide. The liquid carbon dioxide injection technology can be used for on-site dry ice sublimation injection, and the water content of oil wells is significantly reduced, and the effect of increasing production is obvious.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow diagram of a dry ice sublimation gas pressure injection process. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN KR2021072960-A
TI Carbon dioxide cleaning system for glass substrate used in mobile device, has carbon dioxide cleaning nozzle that sprays snow jet mixed with carbon dioxide particles and compressed gas on surface of received substrate.
AU CHOI W C
   LEE J S
   PARK J
   YOON C N
AE KCTECH CO LTD (KCTE-C)
GA 202171280U
AB    NOVELTY - The cleaning system includes a glass substrate (G) receiving unit (111). A carbon dioxide cleaning nozzle (155,165) is used for dry cleaning the substrate by spraying a snow jet mixed with carbon dioxide particles and compressed gas on the surface of the substrate received by the substrate receiving unit.
   USE - Carbon dioxide cleaning system for dry cleaning a glass substrate used in a mobile device.
   ADVANTAGE - Performs dry cleaning on a substrate by spraying compressed air and carbon dioxide particles together on the substrate, thus increasing the cleaning process efficiency. Uses an inverter to turn the substrate in 180 degrees to reliably prevent errors due to foreign matter when both surfaces of the substrate are being cleaned. Prevents re-contamination as the surface of the already cleaned substrate comes into contact with the inverter by holding only the edge of the substrate by the inverter. Reduces the time required for cleaning the entire substrate by pre-inspecting the foreign substances remaining on the substrate and cleaning only the area where the foreign substances remaining above the reference value are located. Performs the substrate cleaning process even if the thickness of the substrate is reduced as the substrate is transferred in a state seated on the tray, thus reliably preventing damage to the substrate during the transfer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram illustrating the configuration of the carbon dioxide cleaning system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Carbon dioxide cleaning unit (100)
   Substrate receiving unit (111)
   Carbon dioxide cleaning nozzle (155,165)
   Receiver (190)
   Substrate (G)

PN WO2021101780-A1; US2022265164-A1
TI Method for generating capnographic waveform,            involves measuring carbon dioxide in exhaled gas            flowing in second flow path distinct from first flow            path by controller of device, and creating capnographic            waveform by controller of device.
AU AYLSWORTH A C
AE INCOBA LLC (INCO-Non-standard)
   INCOBA LLC (INCO-Non-standard)
GA 202157284X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring (602) carbon                dioxide in exhaled gas flowing in a first flow path                by a controller of a device. The measuring creates                a first set of values which is indicative of carbon                dioxide. The carbon dioxide in exhaled gas flowing                in a second flow path distinct from the first flow                path is measured (604) by the controller of the                device. The measuring creates a second set of                values indicative of carbon dioxide. A capnographic                waveform is created (606) using selected from the                group comprising the first set of values indicative                of carbon dioxide, the second set of values                indicative of carbon dioxide and both the first and                second sets of values of carbon dioxide by the                controller of the device.
   USE - Method for generating capnographic                waveform.
   ADVANTAGE - The flow sensors can couple to the common                ports of the three-port valves, respectively, if                the flow sensors can withstand the pressure of the                therapeutic gas during bolus delivery without                damage. The three-port valves change valve                positions, thus enabling the flow sensors to again                sense airflow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for Capnography.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                method for generating capnographic waveform.
   Step for starting the method (600)
   Step for measuring carbon dioxide in exhaled                gas flowing in a first flow path (602)
   Step for measuring the carbon dioxide in                exhaled gas flowing in a second flow path distinct                from the first flow path (604)
   Step for creating a capnographic waveform                (606)
   Step for ending the method (608)

PN DE202020104484-U1
TI Storage system for containers utilized for storing carbon dioxide, has goods dispensing machine for dispensing of containers filled with carbon dioxide and goods return machine to which empty containers are fed.
AU 
AE MAYER M (MAYE-Individual)
GA 202158225K
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a goods dispensing machine (2) for dispensing of containers filled with carbon dioxide and goods return machine (3) to which empty containers are fed. The goods dispensing machine and the goods return machine are spatially assigned and arranged side by side. The containers are designed in form of soda bottles. Each bottle has a valve and a threaded head with an external thread in the area of its removal opening. The goods dispensing machine has a carbon dioxide input unit (8), where each container is dispensed on the goods return machine by entering a predetermined amount of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Storage system for containers utilized for storing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system particularly and advantageously stores different types of containers in the machine. The containers are stored in the machine, so that the user can recognize the different types and select them accordingly. The system realizes simple, efficient logistics for containers filled with carbon dioxide and performs provision of containers filled with carbon dioxide in a simple manner, so that the user can remove the containers from the machine in desired number against payment. The system can purchase the containers filled with carbon dioxide via the machine and return empty containers to the machine, so that the user does not have to worry about disposal or refilling of the containers. The system allows the user can use both the machines directly and without detours.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the storage system.
   Storage system (1)
   Goods dispensing machine (2)
   Goods return machine (3)
   Compartments (7)
   Carbon dioxide input unit (8)

PN WO2021067220-A1; EP4037807-A1
TI Method for automatically maintaining level of            carbon dioxide in closed structure e.g. greenhouse,            involves directing effluent stream to closed structure,            where stream has flow rate selected to automatically            maintain level of carbon dioxide.
AU ALDRIDGE J F
   SHULENBERGER A M
   CORMIA R D
AE DAC CITY INC (DACC-Non-standard)
   DAC CITY INC (DACC-Non-standard)
GA 202135852M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inputting air into an                adsorption unit, where air comprises oxygen (O2),                nitrogen (N2) and carbon dioxide (CO2). The                adsorption unit is utilized to separate a portion                of CO2 from O2 and N2 to generate an effluent                stream. The effluent stream is directed to the                closed structure, where the effluent stream has a                flow rate or a CO2 content that is selected to                automatically maintain the level of CO2 in closed                structure. The sensors are used to measure the                temperature, pressure, or humidity of input air,                and a controller is used to adjust the temperature,                pressure or humidity of input air.
   USE - Method for automatically maintaining level of                carbon dioxide in closed structure e.g. greenhouse                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process control system ensures that                temperature, humidity, and flow rate are precisely                controlled during loading and unloading to maximize                the throughput and efficiency of carbon dioxide                capture and enrichment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a device for automatically maintaining a                level of carbon dioxide in a closed                structure;
   (2) a method for enriching a content of carbon                dioxide in a closed structure;
   (3) a device for enriching a content of carbon                dioxide in a closed structure;
   (4) a method for generating a continuous                stream of carbon dioxide in a closed structure;                and
   (5) a device for generating a continuous                stream of carbon dioxide in a closed                structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                sample device.

AB    NOVELTY - Total organic carbon measuring device (1) comprises a water reservoir (10) in which water, sewage, and/or wastewater to be measured total organic carbon is stored; a reactor (20) for heating water flowing from the water reservoir to the reaction tube to generate a gas containing steam and carbon dioxide; a condenser (30) for separating water vapor and carbon dioxide from gas flowing from reaction tube; a preprocessor (40) for removing at least one interfering substance that causes an error in measurement of total organic carbon among carbon dioxide introduced from condenser by using a filter unit in which multiple different catalysts are disposed; a primary carbon dioxide flow rate, which is a flow rate of carbon dioxide when installed in the pre-processor and flows from condenser, and a secondary carbon dioxide flow rate, which is a flow rate of carbon dioxide from which the interfering substance has been removed more than once by filter unit.
   USE - Used as total organic carbon measuring device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device generates carbon dioxide easily generated from water, prevents errors in total organic carbon for waste water, completely removes interfering substances of carbon dioxide, improves efficiency of removing interfering substances of carbon dioxide and determines replacement timing of catalyst easily.

PT P
PN US2020263079-A1; WO2020165635-A1; US10961433-B2; EP3924445-A1
TI Recovering hydrocarbon gas from subterranean reservoir comprising wellbore, comprises introducing solid carbon dioxide into wellbore, and recovering hydrocarbon gas from subterranean reservoir.
AU BULEKBAY A
   KARADKAR P B
   ALKHOWAILDI M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020798620
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering (M1) hydrocarbon gas from a subterranean reservoir comprising a wellbore, where hydrocarbon gas flow from the subterranean reservoir is impeded due to the collection of fluid in the wellbore, comprises: introducing solid carbon dioxide into the wellbore, where the solid carbon dioxide sublimates to gaseous carbon dioxide in the presence of the fluid, thus displacing the fluid from the wellbore and restoring hydrocarbon gas flow from the subterranean reservoir; and recovering hydrocarbon gas from the subterranean reservoir.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering hydrocarbon gas from a subterranean reservoir comprising a wellbore.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a system for introducing solid carbon dioxide into a wellbore and receiving hydrocarbon gas from the wellbore, the system being fluidly coupled to the wellbore and comprising an insertion chamber (103), and a wellhead (or christmas tree) (101) comprising a flow line, where the wellhead (or christmas tree) is configured to receive solid carbon dioxide from the insertion chamber and introduce the solid carbon dioxide into the wellbore, and the flow line is configured to receive hydrocarbon gas from the wellbore; and
   (2) deliquifying (M2) a hydrocarbon gas well comprising introducing solid carbon dioxide into a hydrocarbon gas well, subliming the solid carbon dioxide in the hydrocarbon gas well to thus deliquify the hydrocarbon gas well.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic view of the system for introducing solid carbon dioxide into a wellbore and receiving hydrocarbon gas from the wellbore.
   Wellhead (101)
   Valve (102)
   Insertion chamber (103)
   Pressure gauge (104)
   Casing (105)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The insertion chamber comprises a first pressure gauge (104). The flow line comprises a second pressure gauge. The wellhead (or christmas tree) is configured to receive the solid carbon dioxide from the insertion chamber once a pre-determined pressure is measured within the insertion chamber by the first pressure gauge. The insertion chamber is configured to be sealed after solid carbon dioxide is inserted in the insertion chamber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The foaming agent is introduced to the wellbore. The foaming agent is a liquid soap or a soap stick. The foaming agent comprises an alkyl ether sulfate, an alpha -olefin sulfonate, an alkyl betaine, or an alkyl amidopropyl betaine. In the method (M2) the solid carbon dioxide is: introduced into the wellbore using the system; and introduced into the hydrocarbon gas well using the system.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solid carbon dioxide is encapsulated in a polymer. The polymer is a self-degrading polymer. The polymer comprises polyester, a polylactide and/or a polyanhydride.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN KR2020031819-A; KR2213331-B1
TI Method for removing trace amines generated in capturing process of combustion flue gas carbon dioxide in greenhouse, involves washing combustion flue gas in washing tower, and separating carbon dioxide from absorbent containing gas.
AU YUN Y I
   IL-SU C
   HEE Y S
   HO C J
   JEONG J Y
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202023946U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves washing combustion flue gas in a washing tower (110). Carbon dioxide is collected from the combustion flue gas through contact of cleaned combustion flue gas with an absorbent in an absorption tower (120). The carbon dioxide is separated from absorbent containing gas. Amine is removed from the absorbent containing gas using cooling water (130). The cooling water containing the amine is recovered and supplied to the absorption tower. Secondary removal of the amine is performed through a water adsorption process.
   USE - The method is useful for removing trace amines generated in capturing process of combustion flue gas carbon dioxide in a greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving removal efficiency of trace amines by increasing life of the adsorbent, simultaneously collecting high-purity carbon dioxide and providing removal step of trace amines generated in carbon dioxide capture process. The method enables supplementing amine loss in the carbon dioxide capture process after separately recovering cooling water containing amine generated when the amine is removed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for removing trace amines generated in capturing process of combustion flue gas carbon dioxide in a greenhouse.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the apparatus for removing trace amines generated in capturing process of combustion flue gas carbon dioxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Washing tower (110)
   Absorption tower (120)
   Cooling water (130)
   Cooling water storage tank (170)
   Absorber (193)
