PN CN108060130-A; CN108060130-B
TI Inducing amplification of immune cells involves starting time by controller when mononuclear cell is inoculated into bioreactor, where controller measures oxygen source when oxygen concentration exceeds ten percent from first to ninth day.
AU XU Z
   ZHANG X
AE GUANGZHOU SHAAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201841763D
AB    NOVELTY - Inducing amplification of immune cells involves starting time by controller when mononuclear cell is inoculated into bioreactor, where controller measures oxygen source when oxygen concentration exceeds 10% from the first day to the 9th day. The controller starts up when the oxygen concentration is less than 8%. The oxygen source supplies oxygen to the culture space. The controller turns off the carbon dioxide source when the carbon dioxide concentration exceeds 7%. The controller starts the apparatus when the carbon dioxide concentration is less than 2%. The carbon dioxide source supplies carbon dioxide to the culture space. The timer is on the 10th day to the 22nd day. The controller turns off the oxygen source when the oxygen concentration exceeds 18%. The controller starts when the oxygen concentration is lower than 12%. The oxygen source supplies oxygen to the culture space. The controller turns off the carbon dioxide source when the carbon dioxide concentration exceeds 7%.
   USE - Method for inducing amplification of immune cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to activate and enhance immunity of organism, both double functions of treatment and health care.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The controller starts the apparatus when the carbon dioxide concentration is less than 2%. The carbon dioxide source supplies carbon dioxide to the culture space. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a immune cell, which is obtained by inducing amplification of a mononuclear cell in an incubator, where incubator is limited.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mononuclear cells are seeded into the bioreactor and on the first day of the time incubation. The mononuclear cells are in the first culture medium. One medium is T cell expansion medium containing 10% autologous plasma, 1000 IU/ml IL-2, and 0.01 KE/ml Shapalin. The mononuclear cells are in a second culture medium on the 2nd to 9th days of the timed culture, and the second culture medium is a T cell expansion medium containing 10% autologous plasma and 1000 IU/ml IL-2 or contained 1000 IU/ml IL-2 T cell expansion medium. The mononuclear cells are in a third culture medium on day 10-22 of the timed culture, which is SuperCultureTM L500 medium containing 1000 IU/ml IL-2. Mononuclear cells are inoculated into the bioreactor and cultured for 13-15 days in order to obtain the immune cells. Mononuclear cells cultured for 14 days are recovered on day 15 of the timed culture and resuspended to obtain the immune cells, and the resuspension is performed in physiological saline containing 5% human serum albumin. The T cell expansion medium is OpTmizer(R) CTS(R) T-cell expansion SFM medium. The IL-2 is purchased from Pepro Tech. The mononuclear cells are derived from peripheral blood. The bioreactor is pre-coated with anti-human CD61 antibody, and the pre-coated anti-human CD61 antibody is prepared by adding physiological saline containing 2.5 microgram/mL of anti-human CD16 monoclonal antibody to the bioreactor. The bioreactor is incubated in the dark at 4 degrees C overnight. The incubated bioreactor is washed with physiological saline and the T cell expansion medium in sequence. Preferred Process: The peripheral blood mononuclear cells are obtained by performing first centrifugation treatment of rapidly-sensed peripheral blood for 15 minutes at speed of 2500 revolution per minute. Second centrifugation is performed on the upper plasma obtained by the first centrifugation, which is performed at 3500 revolution per minute for 15 minutes to obtain supernatant plasma and blood cell precipitates. The supernatant plasma is inactivated and third centrifugation is performed to remove complement. The inactivation treatment is performed at a temperature of 56 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, and the third centrifugation treatment is performed at 3500 revolution per minute for 15 minutes. The blood cell sediment are resuspended using physiological saline containing a volume fraction of 50%, and total amount of 1/3 hydroxyethyl starch is added to the resuspension. The resuspension obtained is subjected to a static treatment to sediment red blood cells. The resuspension is subjected to a fourth centrifugation to obtain leukocyte pellets, and the fourth centrifugation is performed at 1800 revolution per minute for 5 minutes. The leukocyte pellet is resuspended with physiological saline to obtain a suspension, an equal volume of the obtained resuspension is added to a human peripheral blood lymphocyte separation liquid, and centrifuging resulting mixture at 2000 revolution per minute for 25 minutes to obtain an intermediate cloud mist white blood cells. The middle cloud white blood leukocyte is washed with physiological saline twice, and the middle cloud leukocyte washed with physiological saline is the mononuclear cell. The human peripheral blood lymphocyte separation liquid comprises dextran, diatrizoate meglumine.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN DE202017106158-U1
TI Device for producing dry ice pellets, has feed unit which is provided with pressure reducing valve in which carbon dioxide exiting at gas cylinder is expanded so that cryogenic liquid carbon dioxide is present at outlet.
AU 
AE MAYER M (MAYE-Individual)
GA 2019169001
AB    NOVELTY - Device has an extruder die (3) and a punch (7) movable against the extruder die. A feed unit is provided by which liquid, cryogenic carbon dioxide in one of the extruder die and the compression chamber (2) is entered. The dry ice snow is pressed into a dry ice pellet, by passing the punch against the extruder die. A supply unit is associated with a storage unit in the form of a gas cylinder (10), in which carbon dioxide is stored liquefied under ambient pressure. The feed unit is provided with a pressure reducing valve (11) in which carbon dioxide exiting at the gas cylinder is expanded so that cryogenic liquid carbon dioxide is present at the outlet of the pressure reducing valve, which is fed to the pressing chamber or supply unit with an expansion valve (21). The carbon dioxide leaving the gas cylinder with the supply unit is released so that dry ice snow is generated, which is introduced into the compression chamber.
   USE - Device for producing dry ice pellets used in various applications for cooling purposes, such as in food industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry ice pellets can be produced with little effort and the production of dry ice pellets with high quality is possible. The time-consuming storage of cryogenic carbon dioxide in tanks is eliminated. The storage and logistics to provide the required carbon dioxide for the production of dry ice pellets is thus considerably simplified, by which the manufacturing cost of the entire device are reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The device has a dry ice snow which is pressed into dry ice pellet, by passing the die against the extruder die. The liquid carbon dioxide expands and is converted into dry ice snow, when introduced into the compression chamber. The carbon dioxide is expanded from a pressure in the range of 50-70bar to a pressure in the range of 15-20bar, in the pressure reducing valve. The carbon dioxide is cooled to a temperature in the range of -15 degrees C to -20 degrees C by relaxing in the pressure reducing valve. The carbon dioxide which is expanded in the pressure-reducing valve is fed to a buffer unit in the form of pump circuit and kept in the cryogenic state. The carbon dioxide from the pump circuit of the pressing chamber is fed through the removal unit. The removal unit is provided with a valve. The expansion valve is followed by an injection nozzle, by which the liquid carbon dioxide is converted into dry ice snow, which is fed to the pressing chamber. The carbon dioxide is depressurized to atmospheric pressure, by the expansion valve and the downstream injection nozzle. The expansion valve is controlled by the control unit. The expansion valve is a pulse modulated expansion valve. An inlet opening (8) for the carbon dioxide is provided in the pressing chamber into which an injection ring (9) is inserted. The injection nozzle generated dry ice snow is supplied to the injection ring. The degassing zone, a sieve (17) is provided, through which gaseous carbon dioxide from the pressing chamber is diverted. The sieve is associated with an annular element by which the sieve is cleaned. The annular element in the form of a slotted plastic ring (19) is formed. A degassing bore (20) is provided in the degassing zone, through which gaseous carbon dioxide is diverted.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the structure of device for producing dry ice pellets with press unit.
   Compression chamber (2)
   Extruder die (3)
   Punch (7)
   Gas cylinder (10)
   Pressure reducing valve (11)
   Sieve (17)
   Slotted plastic ring (19)
   Degassing bore (20)
   Expansion valve (21)

PN CN108554370-A; CN108554370-B
TI Preparation of spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent by pressing hydrophobic mold having hemispherical cavity on hydrophobic powder layer, placing precursor slurry into hemispherical cavity to obtain slurry ball, and drying.
AU YANG Y
   LIU W
   XU M
   HU Y
   TONG X
   CHEN Q
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201877001J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises uniformly paving hydrophobic powder to obtain hydrophobic powder layer; pressing hydrophobic mold having hemispherical cavity on hydrophobic powder layer; placing precursor slurry into hemispherical cavity to obtain slurry ball, in which precursor slurry is mixed solution of 1 pts. wt. silica sol and 2.0-2.5 pts. wt. lithium carbonate powder; drying slurry ball to obtain green ball; separating hydrophobic mold from green ball; and calcining green ball in aerobic environment until dried precursor in green ball is completely converted to lithium orthosilicate, i.e. spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - Preparation of spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple and easy operation and produces spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent with good sphericity and outstanding ability to adsorb carbon dioxide, thus suitable for use as lithium-base adsorbent in practical industrial fluidized circulation system. The spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent with particle diameter of 1.5-2.5 mm.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for preparing spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprising powder silo, conveyor belt, molding machine, grouting machine, and dryer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a process for preparing spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The silica sol is 20-40 wt.% silica sol containing 1 pts. wt. silica. The precursor slurry contains 2.0-2.5 pts. wt. lithium carbonate and 3-5 pts. wt. water. The weight ratio of silica to lithium carbonate is 1:2.1. The hydrophobic powder comprises graphite powder, silicone hydrophobic powder, nano hydrophobic coating, and/or hydrophobic zinc oxide powder. Preferred Method: The calcining step is performed at 600-1200 degrees C, preferably 850-950 degrees C for 4-5 hours.

PN US2020385274-A1; US11097952-B2
TI Recovering carbon dioxide from acid gas stream involves e.g. feeding first oxygen stream comprising oxygen and acid gas stream having carbon dioxide and sulfur-containing compounds to sulfur recovery unit.
AU CHOW T K T
   CHOW R Y D
   ALJAZZAR G
   LI D
AE FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
   FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
GA 2020C3368J
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering carbon dioxide from an acid gas stream, comprises: e.g. feeding a first oxygen stream comprising at least 90 vol.% oxygen and the acid gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and sulfur-containing compounds to a sulfur recovery unit; recovering a sulfur product stream and a tail gas stream from the sulfur recovery unit; receiving the tail gas stream in a tail gas treatment unit (200); contacting the sulfur-containing compounds and hydrogen received from the tail gas stream with a hydrogenation catalyst in the tail gas treatment unit to produce a hydrogenated product stream comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, water, and hydrogen; contacting the hydrogenated product stream with a solvent in an absorber to produce a treated tail gas stream (202) and a hydrogen sulfide-loaded solvent stream; and converting hydrogen of the treated tail gas stream to water via thermal oxidation or catalytic oxidation to produce an oxidation product stream comprising carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - The process is useful for recovering carbon dioxide from an acid gas stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering carbon dioxide from an acid gas stream, comprises: feeding a first oxygen stream comprising at least 90 vol.% oxygen and the acid gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and sulfur-containing compounds to a sulfur recovery unit; recovering a sulfur product stream and a tail gas stream from the sulfur recovery unit, where the tail gas stream comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen, sulfur-containing compounds, water, and hydrogen sulfide; receiving the tail gas stream in a tail gas treatment unit (200); contacting the sulfur-containing compounds and hydrogen received from the tail gas stream with a hydrogenation catalyst in the tail gas treatment unit to produce a hydrogenated product stream comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, water, and hydrogen; contacting the hydrogenated product stream with a solvent in an absorber to produce a treated tail gas stream (202) and a hydrogen sulfide-loaded solvent stream, where the treated tail gas stream comprises carbon dioxide, water, and hydrogen, the hydrogen sulfide-loaded solvent stream comprises hydrogen sulfide and the solvent; converting hydrogen of the treated tail gas stream to water via thermal oxidation or catalytic oxidation to produce an oxidation product stream comprising carbon dioxide and water; cooling and optionally increasing a pressure of the oxidation product stream; and separating water from the oxidation product stream to produce a carbon dioxide product stream. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a system for recovery of a carbon dioxide product stream from acid gas, comprising an oxygen source configured to produce an oxygen stream comprising at least 90 vol.% oxygen, a sulfur recovery unit configured to receive the oxygen stream and an acid gas stream and configured to produce a tail gas stream and a sulfur product stream, where the tail gas stream comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen, sulfur-containing compounds, water, and hydrogen sulfide, a tail gas treatment unit comprising a hydrogenation reactor and an absorber, where the hydrogenation reactor is configured to convert the sulfur-containing compounds received from the tail gas stream to hydrogen sulfide, and produce a hydrogenated product stream, the absorber is configured to receive the hydrogenated product stream, selectively absorb hydrogen sulfide, produce a treated tail gas stream comprising carbon dioxide, water, and hydrogen, and produce a hydrogen sulfide-loaded solvent stream comprising hydrogen sulfide and a solvent and a carbon dioxide recovery unit comprising an oxidation reactor and a dehydration unit, where the oxidation reactor is configured to receive the treated tail gas stream and to convert hydrogen to via thermal oxidation or catalytic oxidation to produce an oxidation product stream comprising carbon dioxide and water, where the dehydration unit is configured to receive the oxidation product stream and to separate the water from the carbon dioxide to produce the carbon dioxide product stream; and
   (2) retrofitting a sulfur recovery unit and a tail gas treatment unit to recover a carbon dioxide product stream, comprising connecting the sulfur recovery unit to an oxygen source configured to provide an oxygen stream comprising greater than or equal to 90 vol.% oxygen to a burner of the sulfur recovery unit, disallowing a flow of air to the burner of the sulfur recovery unit, adding a desuperheater/direct contact condenser upstream of an absorber in the tail gas treatment unit, where the desuperheater/direct contact condenser is configured to at least partially condense water for removal from the tail gas treatment unit at a location upstream of the absorber and adding a carbon dioxide recovery unit that is configured to produce the carbon dioxide product stream from a treated tail gas stream received from the tail gas treatment unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic view of tail gas treatment unit.
   Tail gas treatment unit (200)
   Treated tail gas stream (202)
   Acid gas recycle stream (203)
   Heat exchanger (210)
   Heated tail gas stream (211)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrogenation reactor of the tail gas treatment unit uses hydrogen formed in the sulfur recovery unit and does not use hydrogen from another source. The sulfur recovery unit is operated at a temperature so that hydrogen is formed in the sulfur recovery unit. The process further comprises: increasing a pressure of the carbon dioxide product stream to an on-spec pressure; receiving a second oxygen stream in the tail gas treatment unit; and contacting hydrogen received from the tail gas stream and oxygen received from the second oxygen stream with an oxidation catalyst in the tail gas treatment unit to produce water; heating the tail gas stream prior to the steps of contacting in the tail gas treatment unit. The no portion of the treated tail gas stream is vented to the atmosphere or sent to an incinerator. The no fuel gas is required for converting hydrogen of the treated tail gas stream to water. Preferred Components: The treated tail gas stream comprises carbon dioxide of 30-70 vol.%, hydrogen of 10-30 vol.%, and hydrogen sulfide of 2-10 ppmv. The oxidation catalyst comprises titanium dioxide. The hydrogenation catalyst comprises cobalt and/or molybdenum. The carbon dioxide product stream comprises greater than 95 vol.% carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide recovery unit further comprises: a heat exchanger (210) configured to cool the oxidation product stream upstream of the dehydration unit; a first blower or compressor configured to increase a pressure of the oxidation product stream upstream of the dehydration unit; and a second blower or compressor configured to increase a pressure of the carbon dioxide product stream. The hydrogenation reactor comprises a first catalyst bed containing an oxidation catalyst and a second catalyst bed containing a hydrogenation catalyst, where the tail gas treatment unit further comprises a splitter configured to split the tail gas stream into a first portion and a second portion and a heat exchanger configured to heat the first portion of the tail gas stream upstream of the hydrogenation reactor, and the hydrogenation reactor is configured to receive the heated first portion upstream of the first catalyst bed, and the hydrogenation reactor is configured to receive the second portion in a middle of the first catalyst bed. Preferred Method: The method further comprises: converting hydrogen received from the treated tail gas stream to water via thermal oxidation or catalytic oxidation in an oxidation reactor of the carbon dioxide recovery unit to produce an oxidation product stream comprising carbon dioxide and water; and separating water from carbon dioxide received from the oxidation product stream in a dehydration unit of the carbon dioxide recovery unit to produce the carbon dioxide product stream; cooling the oxidation product stream upstream of the dehydration unit; increasing a pressure of the oxidation product stream upstream of the dehydration unit; and increasing a pressure of the carbon dioxide product stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN111453703-A; CN212269460-U
TI Production of hydrogen sulfide involves washing methanol gas with alcohol, heating, regenerating alcohol washed sulfur-rich methanol, cooling, alcohol washing, dividing sulfur-rich methanol which releases carbon dioxide into two paths.
AU WANG J
AE SHAANXI JUNENG XINCHUANG COALIFICATION (SHAA-Non-standard)
   SHAANXI JUNENG XINCHUANG COALIFICATION (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2020727341
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen sulfide by purification of low temperature methanol involves (A) washing the methanol gas with alcohol, heating by heat exchanger to obtain alcohol washed sulfur-rich methanol, regenerating alcohol washed sulfur-rich methanol to obtain sulfur-rich methanol which releases carbon dioxide, cooling the carbon dioxide, continuously regenerating the condensed alcohol liquid, discharging the carbon dioxide rich gas, passing to alcohol washing, and (B) dividing the sulfur-rich methanol which releases carbon dioxide into two paths, in which one is hydrogen sulfide released after hydrogen sulfide regeneration and concentrated to obtain high purity hydrogen sulfide gas and other generates gas phase and water phase after methanol thermal regeneration. The gas phase is used for carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide regeneration. The water phase is separated from alcohol and water to obtain thermal regeneration qualified methanol and wastewater.
   USE - Production of hydrogen sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces hydrogen sulfide with high concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of high concentration hydrogen sulfide by purification of low temperature methanol involves (A) washing the methanol gas with alcohol, heating by heat exchanger to obtain alcohol washed sulfur-rich methanol, regenerating alcohol washed sulfur-rich methanol to obtain sulfur-rich methanol which releases carbon dioxide, cooling the carbon dioxide, continuously regenerating the condensed alcohol liquid, discharging the carbon dioxide rich gas, passing to alcohol washing, and (B) dividing the sulfur-rich methanol which releases carbon dioxide into two paths, in which one is hydrogen sulfide released after hydrogen sulfide regeneration and concentrated to obtain high purity hydrogen sulfide gas and other generates gas phase and water phase after methanol thermal regeneration. The gas phase is used for carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide regeneration. The water phase is separated from alcohol and water to obtain thermal regeneration qualified methanol and wastewater. The qualified methanol for thermal regeneration and methanol gas are heat-exchanged and cooled as lean methanol for de-alcohol washing. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device, which consists of low-temperature methanol purification device for producing high-concentration hydrogen sulfide comprising partition tower, a reflux tank and a reboiler. The partition is arranged in the upper portion of the partition tower, and divides the upper space in the partition tower into two left and right regions. The two regions are carbon dioxide regeneration section and hydrogen sulfide regeneration section. The reboiler is connected to the carbon dioxide regeneration section. The outlet of the carbon dioxide regeneration section is located at the top of the partition tower, and is connected with the inlet of the carbon dioxide regeneration section. The outlet of the hydrogen sulfide regeneration section is located at the top of the partition tower, and is connected to the inlet of the reflux tank. The outlet of the reflux tank is connected to the inlets of the carbon dioxide regeneration section and hydrogen sulfide regeneration section.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The device further consists of tower top condenser, heat exchanger, and bottom condenser. The top outlet of the carbon dioxide regeneration section is connected to the inlet of the carbon dioxide regeneration section after passing through the tower top condenser. The methanol thermal regeneration section is located below the carbon dioxide regeneration section and hydrogen sulfide regeneration section. The outlet of the methanol thermal regeneration section is connected with a reboiler. The inlet of the methanol thermal regeneration section is connected with a heat exchanger. The outlet of the hydrogen sulfide regeneration section is connected with the inlet of the reflux tank through a heat exchanger. The inner portion of the partition tower is provided with an alcohol-water separation section. The alcohol-water separation section is located below the methanol thermal regeneration section. The bottom outlet of the alcohol-water separation section returns to the alcohol-water separation section after passing through the bottom condenser.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (A) preferably involves regenerating the alcohol-washed sulfur-rich methanol by the carbon dioxide regeneration section in the partition tower, in which the obtained rich carbon dioxide comes out of the top of the carbon dioxide regeneration section of the partition tower, cooling, refluxing the condensed alcohol to the carbon dioxide regeneration section in the partition tower, discharging the carbon dioxide-rich gas and alcohol washing. The process (B) preferably involves dividing the sulfur-rich methanol which releases carbon dioxide into paths (P1) and (P2), sending path (P1) to the hydrogen sulfide regeneration section in the partition tower to regenerate hydrogen sulfide, heat exchanging and cooling the top gas of the hydrogen sulfide regeneration section and methanol thermal regeneration section, returning the liquid phase to the bottom of the carbon dioxide regeneration section and top of the hydrogen sulfide regeneration section, concentrating the gas phase to obtain high purity hydrogen sulfide, sending path (P2) to the methanol thermal regeneration section in the partition tower for methanol thermal regeneration, feeding the dissolved gas into the carbon dioxide regeneration section and hydrogen sulfide regeneration section respectively, feeding the water phase into the alcohol-water separation section to separate water, sending the wastewater out of the boundary area from the bottom of the partition tower, and qualified methanol for thermal regeneration and methanol gas are heat-exchanged and cooled as lean methanol for de-alcohol washing. Preferred Property: The molar ratio of hydrogen sulfide to carbon dioxide in the methanol gas is (1:6)-(2:1).

Preferred                  Method: The reaction environment provided by the                  plasma catalytic reactor is 60&#176;C, 0.1                  MPa, and the residence time of carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen in the discharge area is 4 seconds;                  output power of the plasma power supply is 12 W,                  and the discharge frequency is 9.5kHz; molar                  ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is                  1:3.

The high-voltage electrode and ground electrode                  are made of copper, iron, tungsten, aluminum or                  stainless steel; the barrier is made of quartz                  glass, hard glass, alumina ceramic,                  polytetrafluoroethylene or non-metallic composite                  materials.

AU LU Y

AE CHINA RAILWAY ENG EQUIP GROUP CO LTD (CREN-C)
GA 202193949V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drilling a palm surface by using an advanced drilling machine. The microwave of a microwave generating device is inserted into an advanced drilling hole. A rock burst inoculation area is determined. Microwave irradiation of a rock body is performed by using the microwave generating device through a microwave coupling rod. Supercritical carbon dioxide is injected into the advanced drilling hole through the microwave coupling rod. A crack grid is generated by the rock body. Gas vibration is generated in a supercritical carbon dioxide rapid expansion process. Rock explosion is prevented.
   USE - Microwave gain type carbon dioxide phase change pressure relief rock explosion preventing and treating method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing enhanced carbon dioxide for pressure relief rockburst prevention, performing microwave irradiation in the construction process, generating micro-crack, increasing the effect of supercritical carbon dioxide explosion gas vibration, entering supercritical carbon dioxide into the pre-crack generated by the microwave, quickly expanding the crack, and reaching the high stress relief effect.

PT P
PN CN108409260-A
TI Producing mine filling slurry, comprises e.g. introducing carbon dioxide in de-ionized water for saturating carbon dioxide to obtain saturated aqueous solution of carbon dioxide, crushing and sieving coal ore and adding in aqueous solution.
AU HU C
   ZHANG G
   LI J
AE FOSHAN GAOMING ZHUAHE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2018668161
AB    NOVELTY - Producing mine filling slurry, comprises (i) continuously introducing carbon dioxide into de-ionized water for saturating carbon dioxide to obtain saturated aqueous solution of carbon dioxide, (ii) crushing and sieving coal ore, adding into aqueous solution of carbon dioxide, stirring, incubating, and filtering to obtain filtrate as dissolved solution, (iii) adding coal gasification slag into vibrating screen machine to screen out coal gasification slag of various particle sizes, and batching to obtain coal gasification slag coarse material, (iv) adding fly ash and natural sand into ball mill, ball milling and sieving to obtain fine sand material, (v) feeding ordinary Portland cement-32.5, coal gasification slag coarse material and fine sand material through feeding belt to symmetric feeding bucket of screw feeder, adding carbon dioxide solution into first mixing tank, stirring, discharging slurry into into double-grade enhanced stirring barrel and stirring.
   USE - The method is useful for producing mine filling slurry.
   ADVANTAGE - The mine filling slurry controls overburden and surface deformation, and prevents separation of the slurry during the pipeline transportation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the weight ratio of the coal mine stone and saturated carbon dioxide aqueous solution is 1:5-1:20. In the step (ii), the stirring is carried out for 1-2 hours at stirring speed of 100-200 revolutions/minute, and incubating for 24-48 hours. The step (iii) is carried out to screen 3 to 4 pts. wt. coal gasification slag having a particle size of 0.075-0.15 mm, 8-10 pts. wt. coal gasification slag having a particle size of 0.15-0.30 mm, 16-18 parts of coal gasification slag having a particle size of 0.3-0.6 mm, 26-28 pts. wt. coal gasification slag having a particle size of 0.6-1.18 mm, 14-16 pts. wt. coal gasification slag having a particle size of 1.18-2.36 mm, 25-27 pts. wt. coal gasification slag having a particle size of 2.36-4.75 mm and 3-4 pts. wt. coal gasification slag having a particle size of 4.75-5.12 mm. In the step (iv), weight ratio of fly ash and natural sand is 4:1-1:1. The step (vi) is carried out using ordinary Portland cement 32.5, 15 pts. wt. coal gasification slag coarse material, fine sand material and dissolved liquid, ordinary silicate cement-32.5, 40-50 pts. wt. coal gasification slag, 28-38 pts. wt. fine sand and 8-10 pts. wt. saturated aqueous solution of carbon dioxide.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN CN114534452-A
TI Separating and capturing carbon dioxide in exhaust            gas involves preprocessing exhaust gas emitted by            factory, subjecting preprocessed exhaust gas to first            carbon dioxide gas separation through gas separation            membrane after first separation, passing exhaust gas            into solid-liquid two-phase absorbent.
AU LI H
   YANG S
   DONG H
   HUANG D
   XUE Y
   DENG T
   ZHAO Z
   ZHOU D
   SUN H
AE ZIBO HIGH-TECH IND DEV ZONE ADVANCED CER (ZIBO-Non-standard)
   LUOYANG JUN JIANG JIANCAI TECHNOLOGY CO (LUOY-Non-standard)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2022748294
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and capturing carbon dioxide in                exhaust gas involves preprocessing exhaust gas                emitted by factory, subjecting the preprocessed                exhaust gas to first carbon dioxide gas separation                through gas separation membrane after first                separation, and passing exhaust gas into                solid-liquid two-phase absorbent to second carbon                dioxide (CO2) gas separation.
   USE - Method for separating and capturing carbon                dioxide in exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses solid-liquid two-phase                absorbent to treat the exhaust gas treated by the                membrane separator, reduces the loss of the gas                separation membrane, and improves the reuse life of                the membrane. The solid-liquid two-phase absorbent                greatly increases the absorption efficiency of                carbon dioxide, which is convenient for recycling,                and at the same time, and it can be reused after                heating the solid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The gas separation                  membrane is hollow fiber membrane, which is                  polyethylene, polypropylene,                  polytetrafluoroethylene, polyvinylidene fluoride,                  polysulfone, silicone rubber, polyethersulfone,                  and/or cellulose acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid absorbent                  is 3-methylaminopropylamine,                  triethylenetetramine, anhydrous piperazine,                  ethylenediamine, divinyltriamine, and/or                  diethylaminopropylamine. The solid adsorbent is                  activated carbon fiber. The solvent is                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide,                  methanol, and/or ethanol.Preferred Process: The solid-liquid                  two-phase absorbent is prepared by dissolving the                  liquid absorbent in a solvent and mixing with the                  solid absorbent.Preferred Conditions: The solid-liquid                  two-phase absorbent comprises 0.1-0.5 mol/l                  liquid adsorbent, and 5-12 wt.% solid adsorbent                  content. The exhaust gas comprises 20-60 vol.%                  CO2, 10-40 vol.% nitrogen, 5-15 vol.% water, and                  0-10 vol.% oxygen. The preprocessing exhaust gas                  includes dust removal, condensation and                  filtration. The temperature after passing through                  the condenser is below 35&#8451;. The                  concentration of CO2gas in the exhaust gas                  separated for the first time in the second carbon                  dioxide (CO2) gas separation is increased to                  50-70 mol.%. The second carbon dioxide (CO2) gas                  separation is completed in a glass bubble column                  reactor, and the temperature is maintained at                  40-80&#8451;.

PT P
PN CA3061855-A1
TI Hydrodynamic cavitation-based system used for regeneration of carbon dioxide rich solvent, comprises process liquid pump to elevate carbon dioxide rich solvent pressure to desired value, preheater to heat up fluid to desired temperature, and hydrodynamic device to create supercavitation flow regime.
AU RADFARNIA H R
   MOSADEGHSEDGHI S
   BEHESHTI F H
   MOSADEGH S B
AE RADFARNIA H R (RADF-Individual)
   MOSADEGHSEDGHI S (MOSA-Individual)
   BEHESHTI F H (BEHE-Individual)
   MOSADEGH S B (MOSA-Individual)
GA 202159474R
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrodynamic cavitation-based system comprises a process liquid pump (107) to elevate the carbon dioxide rich solvent pressure to desired value, a preheater to heat up the fluid to desired temperature, and a hydrodynamic device to create supercavitation flow regime at outlet of cavitation device.
   USE - Hydrodynamic cavitation-based system used for regeneration of carbon dioxide-rich solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces the time of regeneration that adds value to the economy of carbon dioxide capture system for post combustion application, and needs a lower energy demand than traditional approach.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the hydrodynamic cavitation device for treating carbon dioxide rich solution coming from absorption unit.
   Absorption tower (101)
   Heat exchanger (104)
   Carbon dioxide degassing vessel (106)
   Pump (114)
   Absorbent stream (117)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The hydrodynamic cavitation device is any of flow orifice, or venturi nozzles, or high-velocity rotation, rotor-stator siren-like device. The carbon dioxide trapped in the supercavitational bubbles are released from the liquid bulk at degassing vessel (106) working at a lower pressure than upstream of cavitation device, preferably vacuum to atmospheric pressures. The water carryover with the released carbon dioxide is condensed at the overhead condensed and the carbon dioxide is sent out by a blower or vacuum pump for using at downstream unit. The combination of preheater, cavitation device and degassing vessel is enough to meet the desired specification of carbon dioxide loading at regenerated solvent mixture. The hydrodynamically-driven cavitation solvent regeneration system regenerates the carbon dioxide rich solvent at a faster rate and a less energy intensive environment at the expense of a reasonable additional liquid pumping power. The hydrodynamic based cavitation system consumes a less energy for the regeneration of rich in carbon dioxide solvent in comparison to the ultrasound driven apparatus. The cavitation device is the supplementary to the downstream stripper column, and the degassed semi-regenerated carbon dioxide absorbent is pumped to the stripper column for the completion of carbon dioxide regeneration process. The released carbon dioxide and water carryover from degassing vessel is sent to the stripper column overhead. The stripper column is preferentially working at vacuum to atmospheric pressures. The reboiler duty of stripper column and the dimension of stripper itself is reduced since the considerable portion of the carbon dioxide is released after treating the rich solvent by the cavitation device. The solvent degradation rate decreases due to the lower solvent exposure time to superheated steam from reboiler, a reduction in regeneration temperature of carbon dioxide rich methyl ethanolamine solvent by 17 degrees C results in the methyl ethanolamine degradation four times less. The carbon dioxide rich solvent splits to two branches at downstream of fed pump and prior to enter to preheater followed by the cavitation device. The one branch of the carbon dioxide rich solvent is heated and directed to the cavitation device, and another branch of the solvent which is cold fluid is routed to the top of the stripper column. The heat exchanged between the hot gases in the stripper column and nonregenerated cold solvent stream at the top section of tower helps to recover the large portion of waste heat of leaving gases from the top of stripper and adds more energy efficiency to the process by lowering the reboiler duty.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide gas in the feed stream is absorbed at the absorption unit by chemical solvents comprising but not limited to alkanolamines such as monoethanolamine, methyl diethanolamine, diethanolamine, amino acid salt such as potassium or sodium derivatives of glycine, L-alanine, taurine, sarcosine, single or their mixtures with other amines such as piperazine, and their sterically hindered amine.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Automobile parts precision cleaning device, has supercritical carbon dioxide circulation system provided with carbon dioxide storage tank, purifying tank, cooling mechanism, high pressure pump and heating mechanism, and separating tank connected with tank.
AU XU J
   QIN H
   LI Q
AE AOLIN AUTO PARTS CHANGSHU CO LTD (AOLI-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Production of chabazite-type zeolite membrane                involves forming a seed layer by depositing                particles having a chabazite structure on a                support, and preparing the membrane by hydrothermal                synthesis of the support in a precursor solution                comprising an alkali metal hydroxide and                silica.
   USE - Production of chabazite-type zeolite membrane                used in separation of carbon dioxide (all                claimed).

   (1) chabazite-type zeolite membrane, which is                free of an organic structural guide; and

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal in                  alkali metal hydroxide is chosen from lithium,                  sodium and/or potassium. Preferred Process: The production process further                  involves drying the membrane. Preferred                  Condition: The hydrothermal synthesis is carried                  out at 100-250 degrees C for 12-120 hours. The                  separation process is carried out at 30-200                  degrees C under dry condition, and at 75-200                  degrees C under humid condition.

GA 2022372893
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing methane-enriched gas                involves (a) providing a bioreactor, (b) providing                a device for determining the proportion of carbon                dioxide in the methane-enriched gas removed from                the bioreactor, (c) determining a target value S,                (d) feeding gas containing carbon dioxide into the                bioreactor, (e) supplying hydrogen-containing gas                into the bioreactor, (f) forming methane-enriched                gas in the bioreactor, (g) removing                methane-enriched gas, (h) determining an actual                value for the proportion of carbon dioxide, (i)                comparing the target value S, and (j) regulating                amount of carbon dioxide-containing gas supplied in                step (d).
   USE - The method is useful for producing                methane-enriched gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method minimizes proportions of unreacted                reactant gases, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and is                performed in an efficient and economical                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing methane-enriched gas                involves (a) providing a bioreactor having at least                one device for the supply of a gas, at least one                outlet for the extraction of the methane-enriched                gas produced in the bioreactor, (b) providing a                device for determining the proportion of carbon                dioxide in the methane-enriched gas removed from                the bioreactor, (c) determining a target value S                for the proportion of carbon dioxide in the                methane-enriched gas removed from the bioreactor,                (d) feeding gas containing carbon dioxide into the                bioreactor, (e) supplying hydrogen-containing gas                into the bioreactor, (f) forming methane-enriched                gas in the bioreactor, (g) removing                methane-enriched gas formed in the bioreactor from                the bioreactor, (h) determining an actual value for                the proportion of carbon dioxide in the                methane-enriched gas removed from the bioreactor,                (i) comparing the target value S specified in step                (c) with the actual value determined in step (h),                and (j) regulating amount of carbon                dioxide-containing gas supplied in step (d) and/or                regulating amount of hydrogen-containing gas                supplied in step (e) in such a way that the actual                value determined in step (h) corresponds to the                setpoint value S specified in step (c), where the                target value S specified in step (c) for the                proportion of carbon dioxide in the                methane-enriched gas removed from the bioreactor                satisfies the condition 0 vol.% less than or equal                to S less than or equal to 5 vol.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The regulation in step (j)                  is performed exclusively on the basis of the                  comparison performed in step (i). The comparison                  in step (i) and the regulation in step (j) are                  performed by a computer unit. The target value S                  specified in step (c) for the proportion of                  carbon dioxide in the methane-enriched gas                  removed from the bioreactor satisfies the                  condition 0 vol.% less than or equal to S less                  than or equal to 5 vol.%, 0 vol.% less than or                  equal to S less than or equal to 2 vol.%, 0 vol.%                  less than or equal to S less than or equal to 1.5                  vol.%, 0.1 vol.% less than or equal to S less                  than or equal to 5 vol.%, 0.1 vol.% less than or                  equal to S less than or equal to 4 vol.%, 0.1                  vol.% less than or equal to S less than or equal                  to 2 vol.%, 0.1 vol.% less than or equal to S                  less than or equal to 1.5 vol.%, 0.5 vol.% less                  than or equal to S less than or equal to 5 vol.%,                  0.5 vol.% less than or equal to S less than or                  equal to 4 vol.%, 0.5 vol.% less than or equal to                  S less than or equal to 2 vol.%, or 0.5 vol.%                  less than or equal to S less than or equal to 1.5                  vol.%. Preferred Device: The two devices are                  provided for the supply of gases, which are at                  least one device for the supply of a carbon                  dioxide-containing gas and at least one device                  for the supply of a hydrogen-containing gas. The                  device for supplying a carbon dioxide-containing                  gas and/or the device for supplying a                  hydrogen-containing gas is a flow control                  device.

TI Method for analyzing shale supercritical carbon            dioxide fracturing test, involves performing computed            tomography scan on cubic core after supercritical            carbon dioxide fracturing test, and calculating            distribution uniformity index.
AU PENG P
AE UNIV BEIJING CIVIL ENG & ARCHITECTURE (UBCE-C)
GA 201995473L
AB    NOVELTY - A shale supercritical carbon dioxide                fracturing test analyzing method involves                performing computed tomography scan on the cubic                core after the supercritical carbon dioxide                fracturing test, and comparing the computed                tomography images before and after the fracturing,                identifying the fracturing induced crack,                digitizing the spatial position information of the                crack, and calculating the distribution uniformity                index to evaluate the effect of fracturing.
   USE - Method for analyzing a shale supercritical                carbon dioxide fracturing test.

PT P
PN CN113842885-A
TI Preparing metal-anchored organic amine carbon dioxide adsorbent used in power plant exhaust carbon dioxide capture device, exhaust gas carbon dioxide removal device, involves adding metal salt into deionized water with heating.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHANG C
   ZHANG S
   WANG F
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202209556B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-anchored organic amine carbon dioxide adsorbent involves adding metal salt into deionized water with heating and stirring to dissolve to obtain metal salt aqueous solution, where the mass concentration of the metal salt solution is in the range of 1-5%, preferably, 2-8%, adding carrier to the obtained metal salt aqueous solution, soaking, drying to remove water to obtain a precursor containing the metal anchoring site
   USE - Method for preparing metal-anchored organic amine carbon dioxide adsorbent used in power plant exhaust carbon dioxide capture devices, exhaust gas carbon dioxide removal device, air purification device or confined space carbon dioxide removal device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances the stability of the organic amine adsorbent, so as to improving the cycle stability of the carbon dioxide adsorbent, and is an ultra-stable carbon dioxide adsorbent material with industrial application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing metal-anchored organic amine carbon dioxide adsorbent involves adding metal salt into deionized water with heating and stirring to dissolve to obtain metal salt aqueous solution, where the mass concentration of the metal salt solution is in the range of 1-5%, preferably, 2-8%, adding carrier to the obtained metal salt aqueous solution, soaking, drying to remove water to obtain a precursor containing the metal anchoring site, where the mass ratio of the carrier to the obtained metal salt aqueous solution is 1: 3-20, preferably, 1:3-10, adding organic amine into an ethanol solution, stirring uniformly to obtain an organic amine ethanol solution, where the mass ratio of the organic amine to the ethanol is 1:2-50, preferably, 1:4-20, adding the obtained precursor containing the metal anchoring site to the obtained organic amine ethanol solution after organic amine ethanol solution is anchored to precursor containing the metal anchoring site, drying, and removing ethanol to obtain metal-anchored organic amine carbon dioxide adsorbent, where the mass ratio of the obtained precursor containing the metal anchoring site and the obtained organic amine ethanol solution is 1:2-100, preferably, 1:2-40.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt is selected from nickel nitrate hexahydrate, nickel chloride hexahydrate, cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt chloride hexahydrate, copper nitrate trihydrate, copper chloride dihydrate, zinc nitrate hexahydrate, zinc chloride, and magnesium chloride hexahydrate. The carrier is selected from mesoporous alumina, SAB-15 molecular sieve, porous resin, coconut shell activated carbon, coal-based activated carbon, and silica porous material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pore volume of the carrier is greater than 0.45 cm3/g. The carrier contains pores with a pore size greater than 2 nanometer, and the pore account for more than 5% based on the total pore volume. The metal salt is added into deionized water, and heating at 5-80degrees Celsius for 0.5-12 hours. The carrier is added to the metal salt aqueous solution, soaking for 2-8 hours, removing water by drying at 80-120degrees Celsius for 2-12 hours. The obtained precursor containing the metal anchoring site is adding to the obtained organic amine ethanol solution after organic amine ethanol solution is anchored to precursor containing the metal anchoring site, drying, and removing ethanol, where the drying is vacuum drying, and the vacuum drying temperature is 45-100degrees Celsius and the time is 2-12 hours. The organic amine is selected from polyethyleneimine of weight average molecular weight 600-20000, polyvinylamine with a weight average molecular weight of 600-10000, polypropylene imine, pentaethylene hexamine with weight average molecular weight of 1000-20000, tetraethylenepentamine, triethylenetetramine, diethylenetriamine, and diethanolamine.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.)
MC A12-W11D; A12-W11F; J01-E02B; J01-D; E31-N05C; E11-Q02B; E31-N04C; E31-P03; E34-B03; E34-C03; E35-W; E35-C03; E35-V; E35-C04; E10-B04D; E10-B04A2

TI Treating starting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen and water by forming product fraction containing carbon dioxide in which starting gas mixture is enriched in carbon dioxide and hydrogen and depleted in water.
AU CHI J
   GUZMANN M
   MANIUT C
   MERCER J
   NEUHAUS O

   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2020A2693C
AB    NOVELTY - A starting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen and water, and is produced by Claus process, is treated by forming greater than or equal to 1 product fraction predominantly or exclusively containing carbon dioxide, where starting gas mixture is enriched in carbon dioxide and hydrogen and depleted in water in a 1st group of method of forming an intermediate gas mixture including compressing, cooling, condensing and drying, and in that the intermediate gas mixture is partially submitted to a 2nd group of method including subjecting greater than or equal to 1 portion of intermediate gas mixture to a partial liquefaction forming a liquid fraction and gaseous fraction, where liquid fraction is partially used in forming greater than or equal to 1 product fraction.
   USE - Treatment of starting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas downstream a gas treatment unit is less diluted with nitrogen from the combustion air classically used in the reaction furnace of the Claus process and potentially in the reducing gas generator of the gas treatment unit. No cryogenic separation of nitrogen and carbon dioxide from each other is necessary to provide fraction when oxygen enrichment is performed. Due to oxygen enrichment of desulfurization process, hydrogen concentration in carbon dioxide raw gas is increasing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus for treating a starting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen and water, comprising unit which forms greater than or equal to 1 product fraction predominantly or exclusively containing carbon dioxide, and enriches the starting gas mixture in carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, depletes starting gas mixture in water forming an intermediate gas mixture in a 1st group of method including compressing, cooling, condensing and drying, and partially submits the intermediate gas mixture to 2nd group of method including subjecting greater than or equal to 1 portion of intermediate gas mixture to a partial liquefaction forming a liquid fraction and a gaseous fraction, where liquid fraction is partially used in forming greater than or equal to 1 product fraction.

TI Adsorption material comprises metal-organic            framework which comprises multiple of metal ions and            multiple of polytopic organic linkers, where each            polytopic organic linker in multiple of polytopic            organic linker connected to metal ions.
AU LONG J R
   WESTON S C
   MILNER P J
   SIEGELMAN R L
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL TECHNOLOGY & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 201913825P
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorption material comprises metal-organic                framework which comprises multiple of metal ions                and multiple of polytopic organic linkers, where                each polytopic organic linker in multiple of                polytopic organic linker which is connected to two                metal ions in multiple of metal ions, and multiple                of ligands. The each respective ligand in multiple                of ligands is amine appended to metal ion in                multiple of metal ions of metal-organic framework.                The each respective ligand in the multiple of                ligands are represented by (I-II).
   USE - Adsorption material.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption material has high capture rate                and thermodynamic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Adsorption material comprises metal-organic                framework which comprises multiple of metal ions                and multiple of polytopic organic linkers, where                each polytopic organic linker in multiple of                polytopic organic linker which is connected to two                metal ions in multiple of metal ions, and multiple                of ligands. The each respective ligand in multiple                of ligands is amine appended to metal ion in                multiple of metal ions of metal-organic framework.                The each respective ligand in the multiple of                ligands are represented by group of formulae                (I-II).
   X=metal ion in multiple of metal ions of                metal-organic framework;
   Z=carbon, silicon, germanium, sulfur, or                selenium; and
   R1-R10=hydrogen, halogen, methyl, halogen                substituted methyl, and hydroxyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for abating carbon dioxide from                flue gas, which involves contacting flue gas with                adsorption material to reversibly adsorb carbon                dioxide from flue gas by generating adsorption                material enriched for carbon dioxide, and stripping                major portion of carbon dioxide from adsorption                material enriched for carbon dioxide using                temperature swing adsorption method, vacuum swing                adsorption method, pressure swing adsorption                method, concentration swing adsorption                method;
   (2) a method for removing carbon dioxide from                biogas, which involves contacting biogas with                adsorption material to reversibly adsorb carbon                dioxide from biogas contacting by generating                adsorption material enriched for carbon dioxide and                residual gas that contains purified methane with                greater than or equal to 2% carbon dioxide;                and
   (3) a method for sequestering carbon dioxide                produced by source, which involves exposing carbon                dioxide to adsorption material, where carbon                dioxide is reversibly sequestered into adsorption                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The each metal ion (X)                  in multiple of metal ions is magnesium, calcium,                  manganese, chromium, iron, cobalt, Nickel,                  Copper, or Zinc. The polytopic organic linker is                  4,4'-dioxidobiphenyl-3,3'-dicarboxylate (dobpdc4                  ). The adsorption material exhibits multiple of                  carbon dioxide adsorption upon carbon dioxide                  adsorption. Preferred Process: The flue gas                  contains greater than or equal to 4% (40 mbar)                  carbon dioxide. The adsorption process removes                  more than 90% of carbon dioxide from flue gas.                  The method further involves adsorption of more                  than 90% of the carbon dioxide from the target                  gas stream. The carbon dioxide is stripped from                  adsorption material enriched for carbon dioxide                  using temperature swing adsorption method, a                  vacuum swing adsorption method, a pressure swing                  adsorption method, a concentration swing                  adsorption method. The method further involves                  regenerating adsorption material enriched for                  carbon dioxide.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1-R10=hydrogen; and
   Z=carbon.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2022116739-A
TI Liquefied air energy storage system for capturing            and storing carbon dioxide in atmosphere, has liquefied            carbon dioxide separator that is arranged between heat            exchanger for first compressor and first air compressor            of multi-stage air compressor.
AU IK L J
   PARK J H
   HUH J
   SUNGMIN S
   AQIL J
   YEOL B
   KIM N
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2022A9723K
AB    NOVELTY - The liquefied air energy storage system (100)                has an air compressor (104) with a multi-stage                structure for compressing air introduced from the                outside. Multiple compressor heat exchangers                (105,107,108) are provided for absorbing                compression heat generated in the multi-stage air                compressor, placed between a heat exchanger (106)                for a first compressor among the heat exchangers                for multiple compressors (102,103) and the first                air compressor of the multi-stage air compressor to                separate liquefied carbon dioxide from the air                introduced from the heat exchanger for the first                compressor. A liquefied carbon dioxide tank (117)                is provided for storing the liquefied carbon                dioxide separated through the liquefied carbon                dioxide separator.
   USE - Liquefied air energy storage system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system completely separates the carbon                dioxide in the atmosphere without using a                conventional separation filtration membrane by                simply collecting carbon dioxide from the                atmosphere using the multi-stage air compressor and                a heat oil storage system, so that global warming                caused by air pollution can be effectively                prevented. The system is eco-friendly, and can                efficiently store energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of a                liquefied air energy storage system. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   100Liquefied air energy storage system
   102,103Compressor
   104Air compressor
   105,107,108Compressor heat exchangers
   106Heat exchanger
   117Liquefied carbon dioxide tank

The carbon dioxide concentration detection test paper is adhered to the middle of the upper end surface of the gas diversion cylinder to seal the detection port. The circumference of the upper end surface of the gas guide tube surrounds the carbon dioxide concentration detection test paper and is provided with a micro gas guide groove penetrating the inside of main portion.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a corrosion damage control device which is provided for controlling the corrosion damage of a cooling pipeline (1). The corrosion damage control device is provided with a dry ice transportation pipeline (6) and a monitoring device for real-time acquisition of the corrosion damage rate of the cooling pipeline. The monitoring device and the dry ice delivery pipeline are respectively connected to the cooling pipeline. The cooling pipeline is provided to flow through users of supercritical carbon dioxide (10).
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide cooling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The corrosion damage rate of the cooling pipeline can be reduced by injecting dry ice into the cooling pipeline when the corrosion damage rate of the dry ice transportation pipeline exceeds the allowable range. The supercritical carbon dioxide cooling system effectively controls the development of corrosion damage of the supercritical carbon dioxide cooling pipeline and prolong the service life of the pipeline, which is of great significance for improving the reliability of the supercritical carbon dioxide cooling pipeline.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling corrosion damage of supercritical carbon dioxide cooling system pipelines.

PN WO2020159148-A1; KR2020093411-A; KR2020093474-A; KR2020104838-A; KR2148613-B1; CN113329781-A; US2021283366-A1; US11338108-B2; JP2022519525-W; KR2450531-B1
TI System for inducing sleep by temporarily            increasing the carbon dioxide concentration of air            inhaled by a user, comprises a discharge unit for            injecting the mixed gas including carbon dioxide at a            position spaced apart from the face of user.
AU KIM D S
   JIN D
AE NYX INC (NYXN-Non-standard)
   NYX INC (NYXN-Non-standard)
GA 202073968D
AB    NOVELTY - The sleep induction system comprises a mixed                gas generating unit for generating a mixed gas                having carbon dioxide concentration satisfying a                set range by mixing supplied carbon dioxide and                air. A discharge unit injects the mixed gas at a                position spaced apart from the face of user. A                concentration measurement sensor (320) measures the                carbon dioxide concentration of the injected mixed                gas, and a control unit (400) adjusts the carbon                dioxide concentration of the mixed gas generated by                the mixed gas generating unit within a set range                based on the measured carbon dioxide concentration.                A sleep state detection sensor detects the sleep                state of user after the mixed gas is sprayed for a                set time.
   USE - System for inducing sleep by temporarily                increasing the carbon dioxide concentration of air                inhaled by a user having difficulty in                sleeping.
   ADVANTAGE - The sleep induction system solves the problems                such as insomnia persist, fatigue, decreased                concentration, mood disorders, pain, and taking                sleeping pills.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for inducing sleep by temporarily                increasing the carbon dioxide concentration of air                inhaled by a user having difficulty sleeping.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a sleep                induction system. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
   Supply unit (110)
   Mixing unit (120)
   Storage unit (130)
   Concentration measurement sensor (320)
   Control unit (400)

   (A) weighing 90-97 pts. wt. Low-density polyethylene (LDPE) raw materials, 2-7 pts. wt. sheet-modified masterbatch, and 1-3 pts. wt. food-grade talcum powder, mixing in proportion and adding to the hopper;
   (B) feeding the semi-finished product through the first stage screw of the tandem single-screw extruder, then melting, mixing and plasticizing;
   (C) injecting the semi-finished product into the supercritical carbon dioxide foaming agent through the second screw, and then mixing, plasticizing and cooling, and extruding the semi-finished product through the die;
   (D) calendering and cooling by a calender, pulling, and winding to form a coil; and
   (E) using a die-cutting machine to make a gasket.

PN WO2022183107-A1
TI Converting carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide,            involves providing reaction mixture comprising quantum            dots, cobalt(III) porphyrin compound, and reducing            agent in water, adding carbon dioxide to reaction            mixture, and illuminating reaction mixture with            light.
AU ARCUDI F
   DORDEVIC L
   WEISS E A
   NAGASING B
   STUPP S I
AE UNIV NORTHWESTERN (NOUN-C)
GA 2022B2434W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for converting carbon dioxide to carbon                monoxide, involves (a) providing a reaction mixture                comprising quantum dots, a cobalt(III) porphyrin                compound, and a reducing agent in water, (b) adding                carbon dioxide to the reaction mixture, and (c)                illuminating the reaction mixture with light.
   USE - The method is used for converting carbon                dioxide to carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves photoreduction of carbon                dioxide to carbon monoxide in pure water with                excellent performance parameters.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                composition comprising quantum dots comprising a                copper indium sulfide core, a zinc sulfide shell,                and a capping molecule comprising an amino group, a                cobalt(III) porphyrin compound, a reducing agent,                and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quantum dots are                  core/shell quantum dots comprising a copper                  indium sulfide core and a zinc sulfide shell. The                  quantum dots further comprises a capping molecule                  on the surface of the quantum dots. The capping                  molecule comprises a thiol moiety and an amine                  moiety. The capping molecule is                  2-aminoethanethiol. The cobalt(III) porphyrin                  compound is chosen from                  [{meso-tetra(4-sulfonatophenyl)porphyrinato}cobalt(III)]                  and                  [{meso-tetra(4-carboxyphenyl)porphyrinato}cobalt(III)].                  The reducing agent is chosen from sodium                  ascorbate and/or tris(carboxyethyl)phosphine. The                  reaction mixture does not comprise an organic                  solvent or a buffer. The composition further                  comprises carbon dioxide. Preferred Composition:                  The quantum dots are present in the mixture at a                  concentration of 1-5 &#956; m. The                  cobalt(III) porphyrin compound is present in the                  reaction mixture at a concentration of 0.1-5                  &#956; m. The reducing agent is present in                  the reaction mixture at a concentration of 5-100                  mM. Preferred Property: The reaction mixture has                  a pH of 6-7. Preferred Method: The method                  involves illuminating the reaction mixture with                  light at a wavelength of 450 nm. The method                  involves illuminating the reaction mixture with a                  450 nm light-emitting diode. The method involves                  illuminating the reaction mixture for 18-96                  hours. The reaction mixture is contained within a                  reaction vessel, and carbon dioxide is added to                  the reaction vessel at a pressure of 1 atm.

A                product collecting and measuring component collects                and measures a product through the pipeline. An                axial pressure transmitter is arranged between a                pipeline between a compression pressure loading                device and a shaft pressure loading injection hole.                A pipeline is provided with a gas pressure                transmitter between a second gas storage tank and a                carbon dioxide injection pipe. The pipeline is                connected with the carbon dioxide bottle. A first                gas pressure transmitter, a second gas pressure                transmitter and a product collecting measuring                component are electrically connected with a data                collecting control device.
   USE - System for testing and replacing natural gas                hydrate by carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The test system is capable of performing                carbon dioxide sustainable replacement test of                natural gas hydrate in the laboratory, providing                guidance for carbon dioxide replacement                exploitation of the hydrate. The pressure in the                methane hydrate sample sealing space can be                precisely controlled, so as to ensure the                sustainability of the carbon dioxide displacement                test. The methane gas injection pipeline through                along the sample height direction is arranged with                multiple air holes to accelerate the synthesis                process of methane hydrate. The carbon dioxide                injection pipeline is injected with carbon dioxide                gas in the sample to realize the continuity of the                replacement reaction process, aiming at the change                of the gas amount and pressure change in the whole                test process, using the air inlet pipeline and the                gas pipeline of the sealing sample arranged with                multiple automatic pressure and gas amount sensor                to monitor the change process of gas flow and                pressure.

TI Method for detecting mixed phase front edge of carbon dioxide driven mixed phase in oil field development carbon dioxide flooding field, involves calculating moving distance of mixed phase front edge in long rock core, and determining carbon dioxide driving mixed phase front edge position.
AU YUAN J
   WANG Z
   LI C
   WANG M
   ZHU W
   XU X
   LI Z
   TIAN W
AE SINOPEC ZHONGYUAN OILFIELD CO EXPLORATIO (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202212097L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a target reservoir with a representative rock core according to reservoir geological features of the target reservoir. An environment of the target reservoir is simulated. A generated oil gas sample is collected. Conversion volume of stratum crude oil manufactured in a corresponding time period is calculated. Volume of the oil gas sample in a long rock core is combined by overlapping the conversion volume and the correction volume of the stratum crude oil produced in the time period. Moving distance of a mixed phase front edge in a long rock core is calculated. A carbon dioxide driving mixed phase front edge position is determined.
   USE - Method for detecting a mixed phase front edge of a carbon dioxide driven mixed phase in an oil field development carbon dioxide flooding field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables characterizing a motion rule of the mixed phase front edge.

PN CN112964666-A
TI Method for calculating atmospheric carbon dioxide content based on surface bidirectional reflection, involves constructing atmosphere carbon dioxide initial value profile line and covariance prior matrix and calculating average mixing ratio.
AU WU H
AE CHINA ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY GROUP CORP (CETC-C)
GA 202172374R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inputting observation spectrum of three wave bands. Judgment is made to check whether an observation position is located in a city area. Three parameter change of a roses-Li surface reflection model is counted according to a time sequence. A RPV surface reflection model is constructed. A kernel function parameter is set. A surface reflectivity value of an observation point is obtained according to a resolution imaging spectrometer MODIS data set. The atmosphere carbon dioxide initial value profile line and the prior covariance matrix is constructed. Atmosphere carbon dioxide column average dry air mixing ratio is calculated.
   USE - The method is useful for calculating atmospheric carbon dioxide content based on surface bidirectional reflection.
   ADVANTAGE - The inversion error of the surface reflection and the ground gas coupling is effectively corrected. The inversion precision of the atmospheric carbon dioxide content is improved. The high precision algorithm for the current and next generation of satellite load data processing mainly for monitoring city atmosphere carbon dioxide emission source is provided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for calculating atmospheric carbon dioxide content based on surface bidirectional reflection (Drawing includes non-English language text).
