PN WO2021094447-A1; NL2024243-B1; CA3158990-A1; IN202247027283-A; KR2022088932-A; CN114761390-A; EP4058440-A1
TI Continuous preparation of cyclic carbonate product            e.g. ethylene carbonate, involves reacting epoxide            compound with carbon dioxide in presence of            heterogeneous catalyst, supplying reactor carbon            dioxide and epoxide compound, and discarding.
AU VERGUNST F
   LAURENTIUS S
AE NEW GREEN WORLD BV (NEWG-Non-standard)
   NEW GREEN WORLD BV (NEWG-Non-standard)
GA 2021539854
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous preparation of cyclic carbonate                product involves reacting an epoxide compound with                carbon dioxide in the presence of a heterogeneous                catalyst which is activated by an activating                compound, performing in at least a reactors (A)-(B)                comprising a slurry of the heterogeneous catalyst                and cyclic carbonate product as present as a                liquid, continuously supplying the reactor carbon                dioxide (i) and the epoxide compound, discarding                the liquid cyclic carbonate as a product stream                (12) and unreacted carbon dioxide and epoxide as a                gaseous effluent stream (3), continuously supplying                the gaseous effluent in the reactor (B), discarding                the liquid cyclic carbonate as a product stream                (12) and unreacted carbon dioxide and epoxide as a                gaseous effluent stream (4) while all of the                heterogeneous catalyst remains in the reactor (B),                and added an activating compound in the reactor (C)                to activate the heterogeneous catalyst by obtaining                a reactor comprising activated heterogeneous                catalyst.
   USE - Continuous preparation of cyclic carbonate                product such as ethylene carbonate from ethylene                oxide and carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow diagram of                preparation of cyclic carbonate product.
   Stream (2)
   Gaseous effluent stream (3,4)
   Product stream (12,13)
   Cleaned liquid product stream (14)
   Carbon dioxide stream (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The reactor (A) and (B)                  is carried out at temperature of 20-150 degrees C                  and absolute pressure of 0.1-0.5 MPa, where the                  temperature is below the boiling temperature of                  the cyclic carbonate product at the chosen                  pressure. Preferred Components: The heterogeneous                  catalyst comprises an organic compound containing                  one or more nucleophilic groups. The nucleophilic                  group is a quaternary nitrogen halide. The                  heterogeneous catalyst is a supported dimeric                  aluminum salen complex and the activating                  compound is a halide compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supported dimeric                  aluminum salen complex is of formula (I), is                  activated by a halide compound. The halide                  compound is benzyl halide comprising benzyl                  bromide. The epoxide compound contains 2-8 carbon                  atoms, and comprises ethylene oxide, propylene                  oxide, butylene oxide, pentene oxide, glycidol or                  styrene oxide.S' = solid support connected to the nitrogen                  atom via alkylene group;X1 = tertiary butyl;andX2 = H.The support (S') comprises particles having                  an average diameter of 10-2000 mu m and                  comprising silica, alumina, titania, siliceous                  MCM-41 or siliceous MCM-48. Preferred Process:                  The cyclic carbonate product as present in                  cleaned product stream is separated from the                  activating compound as present in the combined                  product stream in a distillation step, where a                  purified cyclic carbonate product is obtained as                  a bottom product of the distillation step. The                  activating compound obtained in the distillation                  step is used to activate the deactivated catalyst                  in the reactor (C). The product stream (12) and                  the product stream (13) and/or the combined                  stream pass a buffer vessel upstream of the                  distillation step. The volume of the buffer                  vessel or vessels expressed in relative to the                  amount of dimeric aluminum salen complex is 5-50                  m3/kmol. The gaseous effluent (3) is recycled to                  the reactor (A) and portion of the gaseous                  effluent (4) is purged from the process.

PN US2020340665-A1; WO2020223391-A1; CA3138179-A1; EP3963269-A1; IN202117050798-A; JP2022530635-W
TI Method for operating a combustion boiler within a            building, involves providing air and fuel to a            combustion burner, the air and fuel are combusted            within the combustion burner, and the amount of free            oxygen in the burner is monitored.
AU FUGLEVAND W A
   VOLBERDING A T
AE NEXTWATTS INC (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTWATTS INC (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTWATTS INC (NEXT-Non-standard)
GA 2020A5786D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing air and fuel to                a combustion burner. The air and fuel are combusted                within the combustion burner. The amount of free                oxygen in the burner is monitored. The amount of                air and fuel provided to the burner to maintain a                free oxygen amount is controlled. The carbon                dioxide is separated from the flue gas. The carbon                dioxide is liquefied after separating the carbon                dioxide from the flue gas. The carbon dioxide is                stored after separating the carbon dioxide from the                flue gas. The carbon dioxide is transported after                storing the carbon dioxide. The fluid communication                are provided between the operating burner of boiler                and the plenum while restricting fluid                communication between the plenum and an idle burner                of the other operating boiler.
   USE - Method for operating a combustion boiler                within a building.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is used to reduce on-off cycles by                extending boiler run time at a reduced flame rate,                while increasing the life on the boilers.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a carbon                dioxide capture method.
   Combustion (11)
   Separation (14)
   Liquefaction (16)
   Storage (18)
   Transfer (19)

PN US2020055020-A1; US10994259-B2
TI Metal-organic framework is used in electrochemical            carbon dioxide reduction system, and in carbon dioxide            adsorbent medium.
AU MORRIS A
   ZHU J
AE VIRGINIA TECH INTELLECTUAL PROPERTIES (VIRG-C)
GA 202014451N
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic framework (MOF) is represented                by linear formula.
   USE - Metal-organic framework used in                electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction system, as                light-induced nitric oxide (NO) release and as                magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) agents, and as                catalyst for the transformation of carbon dioxide                and epoxides to cyclic carbonates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal-organic framework (MOF) is M6( psi                3-OH)8(OH)8( psi 2, nu 2-(O2C)2cyclam)8 cluster and                metal atom is coordinated to the one or more cyclam                of the cluster, where M is zirconium and hafnium                and metal atom is copper, nickel, chromium,                cobalt,and gadolinium. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                included for the following:
   (1) a electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction                system, which comprises a conductive substrate and                a film which is coated on a surface of the                conductive substrate, and film comprises an                metal-organic framework;
   (2) a carbon dioxide adsorbent medium,                comprises metal-organic framework;
   (3) a method for removing carbon dioxide from                a mixture of gases, which involves reacting the                mixture of gasses with an adsorbent medium;
   (4) a method of synthesizing a cyclic                carbonate, which involves reacting carbon dioxide                and an epoxide in the presence of a catalyst, where                the catalyst is metal-organic framework; and
   (5) a magnetic resonance imaging (MRI)                contrast agent, which comprises metal-organic                framework.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves recovering the catalyst after cyclic                  carbonate synthesis is complete, and reusing the                  recovered catalyst in a subsequent cyclic                  carbonate synthesis procedure. Preferred                  Components: The M is zirconium. The metal atom is                  gadolinium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic corrosion inhibitor is potassium or sodium salts containing metavanadic acid, chromic acid, dichromic acid, nitrous acid, nitric acid and phosphate ions. The organic corrosion inhibitor is 4-amino-N,N-bis-(2-pyridylmethyl)aniline which involves treating 1,4-phenylenediamine in the presence of di-tert-butyl dicarbonate to obtain aniline derivative of formula (I). The aniline derivative of formula (I) is treated in the presence of 2-(chloromethyl)pyridine to obtain N,N-bis(2-pyridylmethyl)aniline derivative of formula (II). The N,N-bis(2-pyridylmethyl)aniline derivative of formula (II) is treated in the presence of hydrochloric acid to obtain 4-methyl-N,N-bis(2-pyridylmethyl)aniline derivative of formula (IV). The carbon dioxide capture solvent includes alcohol amine and other solvents, where the alcohol amine includes ethanol amine, diethanol amine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol (AMP) and compound alcohol amine solution and other solvents refer to water or one or more mixed solvents of methylpyrrolidone, dimethylsulfoxide, and N-formylmorpholine. The carbon dioxide capture solvent is an alcohol amine solution with a concentration of 3-5 M. The gas source is flue gas. Preferred Conditions: The relative molecular mass of the organic corrosion inhibitor 4-amino-N,N-bis-(2-pyridylmethyl)aniline is 290.36. The corrosion inhibitor is added so that the concentration is 0.1-2 wt.% solution mass, the amount of inorganic corrosion inhibitor added is 0.15-1.5 wt.% solution mass, and the organic corrosion inhibitor dosage of the agent is 0.1-1 wt.% of the mass of the solution. The temperature of the carbon dioxide capture system is 35-50 degrees C..

TI Preparing pure alkali by carbon dioxide in flue gas and recycling ammonia gas by magnesium oxide involves using traditional alkali method to absorb carbon dioxide in flue gas, mother liquor after separating heavy alkali.
AU DENG Q

   ZHENG H
   REN Z
   ZHAO D
   LI M
   ZHONG G
   ZHENG C
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2022158779
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pure alkali by carbon dioxide in flue gas and recycling ammonia gas by magnesium oxide involves using traditional alkali method to absorb carbon dioxide in the flue gas, the mother liquor after separating the heavy alkali, using modified magnesium oxide or hydroxyl magnesium chloride to steam ammonia, evaporating and concentrating the magnesium chloride solution obtained by ammonia evaporation to obtain solid sodium chloride.
   USE - Method for preparing pure alkali by carbon dioxide in flue gas and recycling ammonia gas by magnesium oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recycling ammonia gas when absorbing carbon dioxide by using combined alkali production principle, using the product sodium bicarbonate and calcined calcium carbonate to prepare caustic soda, thus avoiding the ammonia in ammonium chloride cannot be recycled and using high energy consumption of the calcining furnace. The method greatly reduces the cost and energy consumption and reduces the carbon dioxide in the flue gas and obtains the light sodium carbonate with high added value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves decomposing the magnesium chloride after separating the salt at 350-450degrees Celsius to obtain hydrogen chloride and magnesium oxide or hydroxyl magnesium chloride, the latter is recycled. The grain diameter of the grain diameter nano-grade magnesium oxide crystal grain is controlled to be 20-500 nanometer. The method involves recycling device for recycling carbon dioxide in the flue gas, connected with the compressed flue gas source through pipeline, ammonium chloride low temperature evaporation device for magnesium oxide adsorption mother liquid, the pipeline is connected with the magnesium oxide feeding port, the ammonia gas outlet is connected with the ammonia absorbing device through the pipeline, the ammonia gas is recycled, the magnesium oxide from high temperature roasting is connected with the low temperature evaporating device through pipeline, the outlet of the low temperature evaporating device is connected with the high temperature baking through the pipeline, high temperature roasting device for roasting basic magnesium chloride at high temperature, the device is connected with the low temperature evaporation device through pipeline, the generated magnesium oxide is continuously returned to the low temperature roasting device for recycling and the generated chlorine is collected by the discharge port. The obtained caustic soda and part of heavy alkali obtained by filtering and separating enter into the caustic soda carbonizing device to react, the mol ratio is 0.1-2, finally generating light soda for the next process.

PT P
PN WO2021219644-A1; EP3953021-A1; EP3988523-A1
TI Producing 1-3C functionalized organic molecules including ethanol and/or acetone, involves contacting carbon dioxide as only gas or gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide and methane in presence of water with catalyst comprising permanently polarized hydroxyapatite.
AU TURON DOLS P
   SANZ BELTRAN V
   RODRIGUEZ RIVERO A M
   ALEMAN LLANSO C E
   PUIGGALI BELLALTA J
   REVILLA-LOPEZ G
   SANS J
AE BRAUN SURGICAL SA B (BINT-C)
   UNIV CATALUNYA POLITECNICA (UYCL-C)
   BRAUN SURGICAL SA B (BINT-C)
   UNIV CATALUNYA POLITECNICA (UYCL-C)
GA 2021C23333
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing 1-3C functionalized organic molecules including at least two of ethanol, methanol, formic acid, acetic acid, malonic acid, and/or acetone, involves contacting carbon dioxide as only gas or a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide and methane in presence of water with a catalyst comprising permanently polarized hydroxyapatite.
   USE - The process is useful for producing 1-3C functionalized organic molecules including at least two of ethanol (preferred), methanol, formic acid, acetic acid, malonic acid, and/or acetone, and removing carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The process facilities conversion of carbon dioxide into high-value chemicals, ensures high crystallinity degree, increases electrochemical activity and accumulation of charge per unit mass and surface, ensures electrochemical activity and ionic mobility, which do not disappear over time, increases conversion of carbon dioxide and methane into ethanol, maximizes selective synthesis of ethanol as major reaction product, and increases efficiency of reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The permanently polarized hydroxyapatite has a crystallinity of greater-than 65%, preferably 65-99%, a proportion of amorphous calcium phosphate of less than 18 wt.%, preferably 0.1-17 wt.%, a proportion of b-tricalcium phosphate of less than 36 wt.%, preferably 0.1-35 wt.%, a bulk resistance of 107-105 W cm2, preferably 106-105 W cm2, and increases by 4-73%, preferably 4%, after 3 months, and/or a surface capacitance decreasing of less than 8%, preferably 5-3%, after 3 months. Preferred Method: The permanently polarized hydroxyapatite is obtained by (a) preparing samples of hydroxyapatite, (b) sintering the samples at 700-1200 degrees C, (c) applying a constant or variable direct current voltage between 250-2500 V in at least 1 minute at 900-1200 degrees C to the samples obtained in step (b) to its shaped bodies; applying an equivalent electric field of 1.49-15 kV/cm for at least 1 minute at 900-1200 degrees C to the samples obtained in step (b) or to its shaped bodies; applying an electrostatic discharge of 2500-1500000 V for less than 10 minutes at 900-1200 degrees C to the samples obtained in step (b) or to its shaped bodies; or applying an equivalent electric field of 148.9-8928 kV/cm for less than 10 minutes at 900-1200 degrees C to the samples obtained in step (b) or to its shaped bodies, and (d) cooling the samples obtained in step (c) maintaining the direct current voltage or the equivalent electric field; or cooling the samples obtained in step (c) maintaining or without maintaining the electrostatic discharge or the equivalent electric field; or (i) preparing samples of hydroxyapatite, (ii) sintering the samples prepared in step (i) at 1000 degrees C for 2 hours, (iii) applying an equivalent electric field of 3 kV/cm at 1000 degrees C for 1 hour to the samples obtained in step (ii) or to its shaped bodies and (iv) cooling the samples obtained in step (iii) maintaining the equivalent electric field for 30 minutes. The step of contacting is carried out in the presence of liquid water. The step of contacting is carried out with a volumetric ratio of the permanently polarized hydroxyapatite to water of 1000:1-0.01:1, preferably 300:1-350:1. The step of contacting is carried out with a volumetric ratio of carbon dioxide to methane of 200:1, preferably 1:1. The step of contacting is carried out under a total pressure of 0.1-100 bar, preferably 6 bar. The step of contacting is carried out under a pressure of carbon dioxide of 0.035-100 bar, preferably 6 bar. The step of contacting is carried out under a partial pressure of carbon dioxide of 0.035-90 bar, preferably 3 bar, and/or under a partial pressure of methane of 0.00017-5 bar, preferably 3 bar. The step of contacting is carried out with a molar ratio of carbon dioxide to permanently polarized hydroxyapatite of 0.1-0.5, preferably 0.3-0.5, and/or a molar ratio of methane to permanently polarized hydroxyapatite of 0.1-0.5, preferably 0.3-0.5. The step of contacting is carried out under UV irradiation or UV-Vis irradiation having a wavelength of 200-850 nm, preferably of 253.7 nm. The step of contacting is carried out under UV irradiation and or visible light irradiation having an irradiance of 0.1-200 W/m2, preferably 2-10 W/m2. The step of contacting is carried out at a temperature of 25-250 degrees C, preferably 95 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI olid carbon dioxide sorbent with structural integrity used for adsorbing carbon dioxide, comprises modified polyamine reaction product of excess of amine containing diethylenetriamine, which is supported upon and within solid support.

   OLAH G A

   GOEPPERT A
AE UNIV SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA (USCA-C)
GA 202176470R
AB    NOVELTY - A solid carbon dioxide sorbent with structural integrity comprises a modified polyamine which is supported upon and within a solid support. The modified polyamine is a reaction product of an excess of an amine chosen from tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine, hexaethyleneheptamine, triethylenetetramine, diethylenetriamine, polyethylenimine and/or a mixture, and a diepoxide, a triepoxide, a polyepoxide, and a polymeric epoxide to provide a material with amine functionalities. The solid support is (a) a nanostructured support of silica, silica-alumina, alumina, titanium oxide, calcium silicate, carbon nanotubes, carbon, and/or mixture and having a primary particle size of less than 100 nm, or (b) a natural or synthetic clay and/or a mixture.
   USE - Solid carbon dioxide sorbent with structural integrity used for adsorbing carbon dioxide from gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid carbon dioxide sorbent has excellent structural integrity, high selectivity and increased capacity for efficiently capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixtures, including the air. The sorbent is regenerative, and can be used through multiple cycles of adsorption-desorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The solid carbon dioxide sorbent comprises 1-90 wt.%, preferably 1-40 wt.% modified polyamine. The solid carbon dioxide sorbent further comprises 1-25 wt.% polyethylene oxide or polyol and their mixtures
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyol is glycerol, oligomers of ethylene glycol or polyethylene glycol, and the polyethylene oxides may be present as their corresponding ethers.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred properties: The carbon dioxide sorbent has a high surface area for solid-gas contact, and regenerative for capturing and separating carbon dioxide for at least one cycle at temperatures of 85degrees Celsius or less, preferably 55degrees Celsius from a gas containing 1 vol.%, preferably 200-5000 ppm by volume carbon dioxide. The solid support has a primary particle size of 3-50 nm, preferably 3-15.Preferred Component: The natural or synthetic clay and/or mixture comprise montmorillonite.

   NIU J
   WU L
   ZHAO L
   CAO S
AE WU R (WURR-Individual)

A backend component is coupled to the probe and configured to be connected to a source of carbon dioxide gas (14). TI Carbon dioxide methanation-based nickel-based multimetal catalyst comprises titania carrier component and active component comprising nickel, cobalt, iron and its oxides.
AU DING Z

AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 201948879A
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide methanation-based nickel-based multimetal catalyst comprises 80-95 wt.% titania carrier component and 5-20 wt.% active component comprising nickel, cobalt, iron and its oxides. The active component comprises 60-95 wt.% nickel and its oxide, 3-35 wt.% cobalt and its oxide, and 2-35 wt.% iron and its oxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide methanation-based nickel-based multimetal catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low reaction temperature, good reaction effect and has 99% methane selectivity with active attenuation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of carbon dioxide methanation-based nickel-based multimetal catalyst comprising taking carrier powder, carrying out solution impregnation coprecipitation method, drying, and reducing; and
   (2) application of carbon dioxide methanation-based nickel-based multimetal catalyst comprising taking 0.2 g carbon dioxide methanation-based nickel-based multimetal catalyst, reacting at 150-300 degrees with carbon dioxide, hydrogen and argon gasses according to volume percentage of 3%, 12% and 85%, and performing reaction using batch reaction device at reaction volume of 200-300 mL and reaction space velocity 3000 mL.gcat-1.h-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide methanation-based nickel-based multimetal catalyst is prepared by sintering titania carrier at 400-550 degrees C for 2-5 hours at rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute to obtain carrier powder dispersing together with 50 mg/mL nickel, 5 mg/mL cobalt and 50 mg/mL iron active component in 150 mL deionized water for 10-60 minutes at rate of 500 revolutions/minute, ultrasonicating 1-4 times for 10-60 minutes at frequency of 60 kHz, immersing for 0.5-4 hours, adding 0.1-2 mol?l sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, adjusting pH value to 7-14, precipitating, drying precipitate, sampling, and performing carbon dioxide reduction reaction under hydrogen atmosphere at 300-500 degrees C at rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute at reaction airspeed of 7500 mL.gcat-1.h-1.

PN CN109731578-A
TI Carbon dioxide hydro-conversion catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to produce carbon monoxide, methanol or ethanol comprises copper-indium alloy as core and porous silicon dioxide as shell.
AU WU D
   DAN Z
   TAN Q
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201945250J
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide hydro-conversion catalyst is CuIn@SiO2 core-shell structure comprising copper-indium (CuIn) alloy as core and 50-80 wt.% porous silicon dioxide (SiO2) as shell wrapped by CuIn alloy.
   USE - Carbon dioxide hydro-conversion catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to produce carbon monoxide, methanol or ethanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves carbon dioxide hydrogenation activity, suppresses sintering of active components, has excellent stability, simple preparation and low cost, produces little pollution and is ideal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the carbon dioxide hydro-conversion catalyst comprising mixing material, agitating uniformly to obtain mixed solution; reacting in kettle at 150-200 degrees C for 3-8 hours, cooling naturally to room temperature to obtain suspension, adding into de-ionized water, absolute ethyl alcohol and ammonia water, stirring uniformly, adding hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide and ethyl orthosilicate, reacting at 120-180 degrees C for 8-15 hours and cooling naturally to obtain reaction liquid; centrifuging, washing, drying, roasting and reducing.

AB    NOVELTY - Bag-shaped carbon dioxide sustained release                pack is formed in the shape of bag by using at                least a resin sheet (A). The resin sheet has (a)                carbon dioxide permeability of 20                l/(m2.day.atmosphere) or more when measured using                JIS K7126-1 at 23&#176; C and with relative                humidity of 0%, and (b) Oken type air permeability                of less than 5 &#956; m/(Pa.second) when                measured using JIS P8117:2009 at 23&#176; C.                The pack releases carbon dioxide encapsulated                inside it while in contact with the skin.
   USE - The pack is useful for sustained release of                carbon dioxide to the skin, preferably face (all                claimed), which is useful for promoting blood                circulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The pack provides prolonged effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for sustainedly releasing carbon dioxide to the                skin using the pack, which involves contacting the                resin sheet closely with the skin, and releasing                carbon dioxide from the inside of the pack between                the resin sheet and the skin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Product: The resin sheet (A) has                  (c) Young's modulus (E) of 10-500 MPa at                  23&#176; C, and (d) tensile elongation of 30%                  or more at 23&#176; C. The resin sheet (A) is disposed on the skin side                  and the resin sheet (B) is disposed on the                  atmosphere side. The                  pack is enclosed with at least one chosen from                  carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polyolefin film or                  sheet is at least one chosen from polyethylene,                  ethylene copolymer, propylene copolymer and                  4-methyl-1-pentene polymer. The resin sheet (A) comprises a copolymer                  having constitutional unit derived from                  4-methyl-1-pentene and a &#945; -olefin                  having 2-20&#176; C atoms other than                  4-methyl-1-pentene.

GA 2019286070
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving the effect of                self-generated carbon dioxide in multi-layer rounds                involves (1) injecting starch gel system into water                injection well, subjecting to one or more layers of                self-generated carbon dioxide and condensing, (2)                self-generating carbon dioxide regulating agent                system in layered plug injection layer, (3)                recovering measures for water injecting                wells.
   USE - Method for improving the effect of                self-generated carbon dioxide in multi-layer rounds                using starch glue (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances the profile control                capability, effectively blocks high-permeability                zone. The liquid flow steering is achieved, so that                most of the carbon dioxide chemicals generated in                subsequent layers enter the middle and low                permeability layers, further starting the crude oil                of the low permeability layer and improves the                effect of the regulation and drive. The recovery                factor is improved by 6.11-14.05% and oil recovery                rate is 10.50%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is                  chosen from one or more of potassium persulfate                  and sodium persulfate. The sulfate is chosen from                  one or more of sodium sulfate and potassium                  sulfate. The mineral acid is hydrochloric acid.                  Preferred Composition: The self-generated carbon                  dioxide regulating agent system comprises gas                  generating agent, separating agent, air releasing                  agent and stabilizer from bottom to top in a                  volume ratio 10:1:10:1-4. The gas generating                  agent is aqueous solution of one or two of 8-15                  wt.% carbonate or bicarbonate where carbonate is                  one or more of potassium carbonate, sodium                  carbonate or ammonium carbonate, and bicarbonate                  is one or more of sodium bicarbonate, potassium                  bicarbonate or ammonium bicarbonate. The gas                  release agent comprises 8-15 wt.% mineral acid,                  0.5-2.0 wt.% surfactant and 0.5-2.0 wt.%                  corrosion inhibitor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent                  is N,N-methylenebisacrylamide. The surfactant is                  alpha -olefin sulfonate. The corrosion inhibitor                  is one or more of a fused ring aromatic                  hydrocarbyl quaternary ammonium salt or an oleic                  acid hydroxyethyl imidazoline.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The injection amount                  of the starch gel (1) is 0.1 to 0.3 PV. The                  starch gel comprises 2-6 %mass hydroxypropyl                  starch, 2-6 %mass acrylamide, 0.01-2.0 %mass                  cross linking agent, 0.001-0.015 %mass initiator,                  0-0.005 %mass sulfate and balance water.                  Preferred Composition: The stabilizer having                  average molecular weight 1200-1600 million is                  0.1-0.5 wt.% partially hydrolyzed polyacrylamide                  aqueous solution preferably seawater-soluble                  type. The degree of hydrolysis is 3-5%.

PN CN108675274-A
TI Producing high purity phosphorus pentachloride involves feeding liquid carbon dioxide and chlorine through closed pipe containing desiccant in physical drying first column and second column sulfate and allowing to stand.
AU LI H
AE SUZHOU HANTECH CHEM CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201884598D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high purity phosphorus pentachloride involves feeding liquid carbon dioxide and chlorine through a closed pipe containing a desiccant within a physical drying first column and second column sulfate, by physical desiccant liquid carbon dioxide and chlorine water and allowing to stand liquid carbon dioxide dry chlorine and vaporizing chlorine dioxide from the liquid, such that the liquid carbon dioxide into gaseous chlorine and and two were stored in a buffer tank to obtain chlorine and carbon dioxide, adding liquid phosphorus trichloride to the reaction kettle and passing chlorine gas into the reaction kettle chlorine reaction, where reaction temperature is controlled at 110-12 degrees C. The chlorine gas is introduced into the autoclave from the bottom until the inner reactor turned into a solid mass by the liquid crystal, chlorine is stopped to obtain crystals. The crystals are poured into the filter bag, and purged carbon dioxide in a clean environment process.
   USE - Method for producing high purity phosphorus pentachloride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The filter is heated to 165 degrees C and kept at this temperature until the end of the extraction process. The liquid carbon dioxide and chlorine are fed to the heat exchanger in the heat, external heating using a water bath is heated to 40-50 degrees C, such that sufficient liquid carbon dioxide and vaporized liquid chlorine. The chlorination reaction is performed in which autoclave stirring rate of the stirring shaft is 40-50 revolutions/minute, until the material the solid phase from the liquid phase becomes completely until the crystals. The purge pressure of carbon dioxide is 3-6 megaPascals. Preferred Components: The drying agent is a physical fiber sepiolite, bentonite, mixture of psyllium husk powder and pine pollen. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of fibrous sepiolite, bentonite, psyllium husk powder and pine pollen is 2:1:1:1.

TI Low energy consumption air direct carbon capturing            system, has heater which heats carbon dioxide in            desorption medium and sends carbon dioxide to adsorbent            material of adsorption/desorption/regeneration device,            where portion of carbon dioxide captured by adsorbent            material is enriched.

   JING X

AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2022B36015
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an                adsorption/desorption/regeneration device (1)                through which the original air passes through a                suction device to form purified air. The carbon                dioxide in desorption medium is heated by a heater                (2) and sent to the adsorbent material of the                adsorption/desorption/regeneration device. The                carbon dioxide in desorption medium carries portion                of the carbon dioxide captured by the adsorbent                material through a cooler (6) and is enriched.                Portion of the carbon dioxide continues to be                heated by the heater and sent to the adsorbent                material of the adsorption/desorption/regeneration                device.
   USE - Low energy consumption air direct carbon                capturing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The direct air carbon capture process is                simplified to CO2 adsorption, CO2 desorption, and                absorption, which eliminates the CO2 separation                process and effectively reduces the energy                consumption in the CO2 separation stage. Air is                used as a cooling medium to directly cool the                adsorbent material in the regeneration stage, thus                effectively reducing the energy consumption in the                regeneration stage of the adsorbent material. The                system generally reduces the energy consumption of                direct air capture carbon dioxide by more than 80%                through the comprehensive consumption reduction                process, thus significantly reducing the cost of                direct air capture carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                suction tower. (Drawing includes non-English                text)

GA 2022A9220S
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide by grinding solid                waste involves (s1) crushing solid waste in blocks                with the size &#8804; 50 mm, (s2) taking the                total amount of solid waste as reference, adding                1-10.0% amino acid salt and/or 1-10.0% amino                plasma, 1-10.0% organic amine, 0.5-3.0% initiator                and 0.5-3.0% cationic surfactant of the total                amount of the solid waste, and grinding the mixture                to obtain recycled aggregate with the particle size                of 6.5 &#181;m-20 mm, (s3) placing the recycled                aggregate in high-concentration carbon dioxide                environment to absorb carbon dioxide to obtain                carbonized recycled aggregate, and (s4) mixing and                stirring carbonized recycled aggregate and cement                slurry or cement mortar uniformly to prepare the                mineralized lightweight concrete test piece.
   USE - Removing carbon dioxide by grinding solid                waste used in industrial and mining solid wastes                and construction waste recycled aggregate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove carbon dioxide by                grinding solid waste, overcomes carbon dioxide                adsorption and analysis in the existing technology,                the sealing cost is high and the existing carbon                dioxide maintenance concrete influence factor is                more, not easy to control, easy to cause large area                cracking quality of the concrete building block.                The physical performance of the recycled aggregate                is improved, because carbon dioxide reacts with                calcium hydroxide and carbon-sulfur-hydrogen in the                slurry to increase the solid phase volume and                reduce the porosity.

AU WU F
   GAN S
   ZHANG L
   HU H
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2021652870
AB    NOVELTY - Use of titanium oxyhydroxide as a biogas purifier.
   USE - The titanium oxyhydroxide is useful as a biogas purifier (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The titanium oxyhydroxide: removes the sulfur element in the biogas mixed gas, obtaining the sulfur-free biogas, post compression, has low temperature and drying process, adsorbing carbon dioxide by hydroxy titanium oxide, and realizes the purification of biogas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for:
   (1) biogas purifying device comprising a desulfurization device for removing sulfur element in methane mixed gas to obtain sulfur-free marsh gas, the desulfurizing device comprises a methane mixed gas inlet and a sulfur-free methane outlet, a compression low temperature drying device, for compressing the sulfur-free biogas, and performing low temperature and drying treatment, obtaining high pressure low temperature anhydrous sulfur-free biogas, the compression low-temperature drying device comprises an inlet and an outlet, the inlet of the compression low-temperature drying device is connected with the sulfur-free biogas outlet of the desulfurization device, adsorption device, for using hydroxy titanium oxide to absorb carbon dioxide in the high pressure low temperature anhydrous sulfur-free marsh gas, obtaining hydroxy titanium oxide with high pressure and pure methane carbon dioxide, the adsorption device comprises an inlet, a methane outlet and a carbon dioxide outlet, the inlet of the adsorption device is connected with the outlet of the compression low temperature drying device, a methane recycling device for collecting the high pressure pure methane the methane recycling device is connected with the methane outlet of the absorption device, a carbon dioxide recovery device for desorbing and collecting the hydroxy titanium oxide adsorbing carbon dioxide by vacuumizing treatment to obtain carbon dioxide, the carbon dioxide recycling device is connected with the carbon dioxide outlet of the adsorption device; and
   (2) biogas purifying method comprising removing the sulfur element in the methane mixed gas to obtain the sulfur-free marsh gas, compressing the sulfur-free biogas, and performing low temperature and drying treatment, obtaining high pressure and low temperature anhydrous sulfur-free biogas, using hydroxy titanium oxide to absorb carbon dioxide in the high pressure low temperature anhydrous sulfur-free marsh gas, obtaining hydroxy titanium oxide with high pressure and pure methane carbon dioxide, the hydroxy titanium oxide adsorbing carbon dioxide is desorbed by vacuumizing so as to obtain the carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural diagram of a biogas purifying device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The titanium oxyhydroxide selectively adsorbs the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide/methane component of the biogas mixture gas to realize the purification of the biogas. In the purifying biogas method comprises, in the compression, the pressure is increased to 5-15 bar, the low temperature is 20-28 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The compressed low-temperature drying device includes a compressor for compressing the sulfur-free biogas to obtain high-pressure sulfur-free biogas. The compressor has an inlet and an outlet, and the inlet of the compressor is in communication with the sulfur-free biogas outlet of the desulfurization device. A low-temperature dryer is used for low-temperature and drying treatment of the high-pressure sulfur-free biogas to obtain high-pressure, low-temperature, anhydrous, sulfur-free biogas. The low-temperature dryer has an inlet and an outlet, and the inlet of the low-temperature dryer is in communication with the outlet of the compressor. The buffer container is used to collect the high-pressure, low-temperature, anhydrous, and sulfur-free biogas. the buffer container is in communication with the outlet of the low-temperature dryer. The adsorption device includes N adsorption tanks, where the N greater than or equal to 1, N adsorption tanks all contain an inlet, a methane outlet, and a carbon dioxide outlet. The inlets of the N adsorption tanks are all communicated with the outlets of the compression low-temperature drying device. The methane outlets of the N adsorption tanks are all connected to the methane recovery device, and the carbon dioxide outlets of the adsorption device are all connected to the carbon dioxide recovery device. The carbon dioxide recovery device includes a vacuum pump and a carbon dioxide collection container. The carbon dioxide collection container is communicated with the carbon dioxide outlet of the adsorption device through the vacuum pump. A first valve is provided on the connecting pipe between the adsorption device and the vacuum pump. The connecting pipeline between the compression low-temperature drying device and the adsorption device is provided with a second valve, and the connecting pipeline between the adsorption device and the methane recovery device is provided with a third valve.

PT P
PN CN112973434-A
TI Phase-change solvent-enhanced microalgae fixation of coal-fired flue gas carbon dioxide and resource conversion, by adding microalgae to culture solution, injecting carbon dioxide gas, pouring carbon dioxide fumes, and extracting products.
AU CHEN S
   BAI L
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV QINGDAO (UNQD-C)
GA 2021718982
AB    NOVELTY - Phase-change solvent-enhanced microalgae fixation of coal-fired flue gas carbon dioxide and resource conversion, comprises (1) adding the microalgae to a culture solution, and injecting carbon dioxide flue gas to acclimate for a period of time, so that it adapts to the carbon dioxide environment, (2) adding n-heptane to the culture solution, placing n-heptane on the upper layer of the culture solution, and pouring carbon dioxide fumes into the culture solution to culture until the microalgae mature stage, and (3) separating the phase change solvent and the microalgae culture solution, and extracting value-added products from the phase change solvent.
   USE - Method for phase-change solvent-enhanced microalgae fixation of coal-fired flue gas carbon dioxide and resource conversion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maintains the n-heptane and the microalgae culture system in a layered state, so that they do not directly contact the microalgae cells, which will cause less damage to the microalgae cells, and will not affect the photosynthesis of the cells, thus improving efficiency. The method separates n-heptane from the microalgae system through liquid-liquid phase separation, and extracts low-oil and other value-added products from it, thus realizing the recycling of n-heptane, and reducing costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In step (1), the microalgae are logarithmic phase microalgae, where the initial concentration of the microalgae is 0.2 g/l, and the concentration of the microalgae after the completion of the domestication is 0.25 plus minus 0.02 g/l. In step (1), the carbon dioxide flue gas is a mixed gas of 2% carbon dioxide and 98% nitrogen, the 2% carbon dioxide and 98% nitrogen simulated flue gas is aerated into the culture medium intermittently at a flow rate of 100 ml/minute, the acclimation time is 2 days, the acclimation temperature is 25 plus minus 2 degrees C, and the acclimation process pH is 6.5-9, where during the domestication process, the lighting method adopts the light-dark alternate mode, the duration of light and darkness are both 12 hours, and the light intensity is 4000 Lux. In step (2), n-heptane is slowly added to the pre-culture system. The carbon dioxide flue gas in step (2) is a mixture of 15% carbon dioxide and 85% nitrogen, the simulated smoke ventilation rate is 50 ml/minute, the ventilation time is 2 hour after the start of light, and the ventilation time is 15 minutes, where during the cultivation process, the lighting method adopts the mode of alternating light and dark, the duration of light and darkness are both 12 hours, and the light intensity is 4000 Lux. In step (3), the liquid-liquid separation method is used to separate the n-heptane from the microalgae culture solution, and the value-added products include oils, proteins or sugars. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of n-heptane in step (2) to microalgae in step (1) is 0.05-0.15.

Stream (11) is preferably derived from an air-separation device (12) used for obtaining a nitrogen stream (13). Synthesis of hydrogen-containing compound further involves obtaining a reformed recycling stream (14) having a higher methane content than stream (5), from hydrogen separator, and feeding stream (14) to synthesis gas reactor to obtain gas stream (1), obtaining a reform recycling stream (15) having higher methane content than stream (3), from carbon dioxide remover, combining stream (15) with stream (14), and feeding to synthesis gas reactor. Gas stream (1) is at least partially fed, before feeding to methanol reactor, to a shift conversion reactor (38) for water-gas shift reaction so that the molar content of hydrogen in gas stream (1) is increased.

PN US2018126326-A1
TI Apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide generated in thermoelectric power plants, has first heat exchanger preheating saturated absorbent, and second heat exchanger secondarily preheating primarily preheated saturated absorbent.
AU LEE K
   KIM H
   KIM J
   HWANG S
AE UNIV SOGANG RES FOUND (USOG-C)
GA 2018359605
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has an absorption tower (210) filled with an absorbent (112) that absorbs carbon dioxide (150) contained in exhaust gas (110) to form a saturated absorbent (120). A stripping tower (220) strips the carbon dioxide from the saturated absorbent transferred from the absorption tower and the absorbent is regenerated. A first heat exchanger (310) preheats the saturated absorbent while the saturated absorbent is being transferred from the absorption tower to the stripping tower. A second heat exchanger (320) secondarily preheats the primarily preheated saturated absorbent.
   USE - Apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide generated in combustion facilities i.e. thermoelectric power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus recovers thermal energy present in unreacted gas discharged to an upper portion of the absorption tower through a third heat exchanger such that a generator generates extra energy, thus increasing energy efficiency of the entire carbon dioxide capture process. The apparatus removes droplets of the absorbent or steam by using circulating washing water or makeup water so as to maintain water balance in the process using a low-energy absorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for capturing carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide.
   Exhaust gas (110)
   Absorbent (112)
   Saturated absorbent (120)
   Carbon dioxide (150)
   Absorption tower (210)
   Stripping tower (220)
   Heat exchangers (310, 320)

PN WO2022180544-A1
TI Manufacturing steel product in steelmaking units,            by performing calculation step where expected level of            carbon dioxide emissions is calculated, considering all            carbon dioxide contributions associated to raw            materials, energy sources and processes used, and            manufacturing product in steelmaking unit.
AU DA GAMA CAMPOS H
   VAN DER HOEVEN J
AE ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
GA 2022B24988
AB    NOVELTY - Method for manufacturing a steel product P in                at least two steelmaking units Si, involves                performing a calculation step where an expected                level of carbon dioxide emissions Eexpiis                calculated to manufacture P in each steelmaking                unit Si, such calculation being done considering                all carbon dioxide contributions associated to raw                materials, energy sources and processes used for                manufacturing the steel product P in each                respective steelmaking unit Si, and manufacturing                the product P in the steelmaking unit Siwhere the                calculated expected level of carbon dioxide                emissions Eexpiis the lowest.
   USE - The method is used for manufacturing steel                product P in at least two steelmaking units                Si.
   ADVANTAGE - The method determines and reduces carbon                dioxide footprint of steel products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw materials are                  chosen among coal, coke, iron ore, biomass,                  sintered ore, agglomerates, pellets,                  direct-reduced iron (DRI), scrap, mineral                  additions, alloying elements, oxygen or hydrogen.                  The scrap is of different types and are chosen                  among old scrap, new scrap, prime scrap, home                  scrap, pit scrap, shredded, plates and structure                  scrap, heavy melting scrap, cast scrap, coil                  scrap or busheling scrap. The energy sources are                  chosen among renewable electricity, electricity                  produced by internal recycling of exhaust gas                  from the steel manufacturing process or by                  capture of heat released by products from the                  steel manufacturing process. Preferred Method:                  The processes are chosen among coking, sintering,                  ironmaking, steelmaking, casting, and finishing,                  preferably direct reduction processes,                  hydrogen-based ironmaking, steel electrolysis,                  blast furnace with top-gas recycling, blast                  furnace with top-gas conversion,                  electric-arc-furnace steelmaking, converter                  steelmaking, and scrap melting. The method                  involves after manufacturing step of establishing                  a certificate for the manufactured steel                  indicated the level Eexpiof carbon dioxide                  emissions associated to its manufacturing route.                  Preferred Conditions: In the method, for a given                  steelmaking unit Si, at least two different                  manufacturing routes MR(i, x) for the steel                  product P are defined and the calculation of                  expected level of carbon dioxide emissions Eexpi,                  where x is also performed for each defined                  manufacturing route MR(i, x) and the                  manufacturing of the product P is performed                  according to the manufacturing route having the                  lowest calculated expected level of carbon                  dioxide emissions of both Eexpiand Eexp(i,                  x).

PN IN202111040017-A
TI Preparing ammonium bicarbonate in a plant used as chemical/nitrogen fertilizer by reacting ammonium carbonate with carbon dioxide in presence of demineralized water in bicarbonation towers to form aqueous slurry, centrifuging and drying.
AU SINGH G
   SINGH R
AE SINGH G (SING-Individual)
   SINGH R (SING-Individual)
GA 2021B1113C
AB    NOVELTY - An ammonium bicarbonate in a plant is prepared by reacting ammonia, carbon dioxide and demineralized (DM) water to form ammonium carbonate in a carbonation tower; reacting the ammonium carbonate with carbon dioxide in presence of DM water in bicarbonation towers to form aqueous slurry containing ammonium bicarbonate crystals and mother liquor; centrifuging the aqueous slurry to separate ammonium bicarbonate crystals from mother liquor; drying the crystals in a rotary drier to obtain the finished product; and, packaging the finished product. The carbon dioxide is sourced from steam reforming of hydrocarbon.
   USE - Preparation of ammonium bicarbonate in a plant used as chemical/nitrogen fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses carbon dioxide and converts it into useful product such as ammonium bicarbonate to protect the environment from harmful effects of carbon dioxide when released into the atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The conversion efficiency to ammonium bicarbonate crystals is 25-40% under strict temperature control. Preferred Process: The dust of salt escaping from rotary dryer is separated and recovered in a cyclone separator where the residual dust along with air from cyclone separator is passed through a wet venturi scrubber for complete recovery of salt from air exhausted to atmosphere. The method is prepared in grass root green field plant further comprising the main plant with carbonation tower, bicarbonation tower, drying section, product bunker, end product in bagging section, and, storage of raw material. The finished product is packed in 25 kg packs in the bagging and storing section of grassroot green field plant. The grassroot green field plant is designed to produce 25-60 MT/day. The method uses only DM water in the process reaction. The method is a continuous process and runs round the clock. The ammonia is obtained from an ammonia storage tank and carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide (CO2) storage tank.

PN CN109396353-A; CN109396353-B
TI Blowing carbon dioxide hardening method for casting water glass sand, involves placing product of water glass sand to be hardened into sealed box, vacuumizing, introducing carbon dioxide for blowing, and introducing air into sealed box.
AU WEI J
   YIN X
   JIN G
AE SHENYANG HYATON FOUNDRY MATERIAL CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201922780E
AB    NOVELTY - The blowing carbon dioxide hardening method for casting water glass sand, involves placing product of water glass sand to be hardened into sealed box, vacuumizing (I) at 3-20 kPa, introducing carbon dioxide for blowing until the pressure reaches 20-60 kPa, maintaining pressure for 5-120 seconds, introducing air into the sealed box until normal pressure is restored, vacuumizing (II) until the pressure reaches 2-19 kPa, maintaining pressure for 1-120 seconds, introducing air until normal pressure is restored and pushing out article.
   USE - Blowing carbon dioxide hardening method for casting water glass sand.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables casting of water glass sand, removes residual carbon dioxide in the product and avoids the mechanical property degradation caused by over-blowing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The water glass sand comprises 1.2-5 %mass, preferably 1.8-3 %mass casting water glass, 0.01-2.5 %mass, preferably 0.01-1.5 %mass reinforcing agent and remaining of raw sand. The raw sand is foundry sand, ceramic foundry sand, forsterite sand, zircon sand and chromite sand. The casting water glass is water glass for blowing carbon dioxide process, water glass for ester hardening process and/or water glass for vacuum replacement hardening process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reinforcing agent is silica fume, zirconium fumed silica, precipitated silica, fumed silica and/or fly ash. The carbon dioxide content in the carbon dioxide for blowing is more than 50 wt.%. Preferred Conditions: The pressure holding time after introduction of carbon dioxide for blowing, is 5-100 seconds, preferably 10-20 seconds. The carbon dioxide is blown into air until the pressure reaches to 20-40 kPa, preferably 20-30 kPa. The vacuumizing (II) is performed until the pressure reaches to 2-4 kPa, preferably 3-4 kPa. The dwell time after vacuuming (II) is 1-20 seconds, preferably 10-20 seconds. The vacuumizing (I) is performed until the pressure reaches to 5-20 kPa, preferably 5-10 kPa.

PN WO2019013705-A1; CN110997120-A; US2020129930-A1; SG11202000139-A1; EP3651884-A1; EP3651884-A4; US11325075-B2
TI Membrane used for forming membrane module, comprises polymeric support layer comprising spatially ordered uniform sized pores, and carbon dioxide capture layer comprising solid porous material with carbon dioxide adsorption site(s).
AU TAN M C
   LOW H Y
   ZHANG Y
   WONG H C
   CHEN M
   LIU F
   HUANG Q
AE UNIV SINGAPORE TECHNOLOGY & DESIGN (UYSI-Non-standard)
   UNIV SINGAPORE TECHNOLOGY & DESIGN (UYSI-Non-standard)
   UNIV SINGAPORE TECHNOLOGY & DESIGN (UYSI-Non-standard)
GA 201907000W
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon capture-mixed matrix membrane comprises a polymeric support layer comprising spatially ordered uniform sized pores, and a carbon dioxide capture layer comprising solid porous material with at least one carbon dioxide adsorption site, and in contact with the polymeric support layer.
   USE - Carbon capture-mixed matrix membrane used for forming membrane module (claimed) used as spiral-wound module for separating carbon dioxide from nitrogen in industrial, submarine, spacecraft and underground parking applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The thin and light weight membrane can be molded and adapted easily to fit with existing infrastructure or process lines, and has improved carbon dioxide capture ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) membrane module; and
   (2) formation of carbon capture-mixed matrix membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymeric support layer comprises a patterned surface and spatially ordered through-holes. The polymeric support layer comprises polyvinylidene difluoride, polyethersulfone, polysulfone, polyether block amide or its copolymers. The solid porous material comprises amine-functionalized material, and a porous particle template with particles having an average particle size of 5-100 nm. The carbon dioxide capture layer comprises polyethyleneimine (PEI)-silica, PEI-graphene oxide, polyaniline-graphene oxide, PEI-nanoporous carbon, PEI-metal-organic framework, PEI-oxidized nanofibrillated cellulose, PEI-porous organic polymer, PEI-mesoporous molecular sieve, tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA)-graphene oxide, TEPA-silica, zeolite imidazolate framework-8, and/or amine modified or unmodified copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate. Preferred Properties: The polymeric support layer has pores having diameter of 100 nm to 100 mu m, and thickness of 50-500 mu m. The solid porous material comprises pores having an average diameter of 1-100 nm. The membrane has a thickness of 50-2500 mu m.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); ; E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Use of riboflavin cathode catalyst to enhance            power generation performance of carbon dioxide            mineralized battery by placing cation exchange membrane            in carbon dioxide mineralized battery container and            dividing container to anode and cathode.
AU WANG Y
   XIE H
   LIU T
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201887742P
AB    NOVELTY - Utilization of riboflavin cathode catalyst to                enhance power generation performance of carbon                dioxide mineralized battery comprises placing                cation exchange membrane in carbon dioxide                mineralized battery container, dividing container                to anode and cathode, adding Glauber's salt and                calcium hydroxide solution to cathode, adding                flavin mononucleotide (FMN,                riboflavin-5'-phosphate) catalyst, applying direct                current, feeding sodium bicarbonate to anode side,                ionizing, recycling reduced FMN, neutralizing and                precipitating.
   USE - Method for utilizing riboflavin derivative FMN                as cathode catalyst to enhance power generation                performance of carbon dioxide mineralized                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method employs proton-coupled electron                transfer-reaction properties to avoid higher HER                potential. The battery has improved electric                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon                dioxide mineralized battery comprising anode and                cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction is done at                  25-100 degrees C.

PN CN108288738-A; CN108288738-B
TI Method for using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid recycling electrolyte of lithium ion battery, involves separating solvent of electrolyte from electrolyte, and removing entrainer and component analysis according to analysis result.
AU ZHENG X
   CHEN Y
   WEI M
   WANG J
   ZOU Z
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
   UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 201856965Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves crushing waste lithium ion battery discharge. An electrolyte after disassembling is transferred to ethyl butyl ketone as entrainer in carbon dioxide supercritical extracting device. A temperature of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is adjusted. A solvent of electrolyte is separated from electrolyte, and the entrainer and component analysis is removed according to analysis result.
   USE - Method for using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid recycling electrolyte of lithium ion battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The usage of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid recycling electrolyte of lithium ion battery is performed in simple and easier manner with improved extraction separating speed and without complex post-processing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical view of the process for using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid recycling electrolyte of lithium ion battery. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN US2022037691-A1; WO2022173617-A1
TI Interconnected photosynthesis matrix and            bio-energy production system for reducing carbon            dioxide, comprises interconnected system comprising            bio-energy production system and photosynthesis matrix            comprising carbon dioxide, and chloroplast.
AU KOHANE M
AE KOHANE TECHNOLOGIES LLC (KOHA-Non-standard)
   KOHANE TECHNOLOGIES LLC (KOHA-Non-standard)
GA 202218394M
AB    NOVELTY - Interconnected photosynthesis matrix and                bio-energy production system, comprises an                interconnected system comprising bio-energy                production system comprising a selection process,                where the selection process is applied to a first                organism strain for a multiple of generations to                create a second organism strain with enhanced                energy availability, and an extraction process,                where the extraction process creates an energy rich                homogenate from the energy enhanced organism                strain, and a photosynthesis matrix comprising                carbon dioxide, and a chloroplast solution having                homogenized plant material and an adenosine                triphosphate (ATP) solution, where the ATP solution                is derived from the energy rich homogenate.
   USE - Interconnected photosynthesis matrix and                bio-energy production system for reducing carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system can be used to reduce atmospheric                carbon dioxide levels by improving the                naturally-occurring photosynthesis capabilities of                plant material to absorb carbon dioxide and produce                glucose, oxygen, and water, when used on a large                scale, the photosynthesis matrix can improve                greenhouse gas levels by accelerating rates of                carbon dioxide reduction and, therefore,                ameliorating environmental damage that has occurred                due to global climate change.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method of reducing carbon dioxide, which                involves:
   (1) creating an energy enhanced organism                strain by using a selection process applied to a                first organism strain for a multiple of                generations;
   (2) creating an energy rich homogenate from                the energy enhanced organism strain;
   (3) pumping the energy rich homogenate from an                energy rich homogenate chamber into a filter                chamber to produce a filtered material;
   (4) pumping the filtered material through a                first biomolecule sensor to a photosynthesis                chamber;
   (5) adding a predetermined quantity of carbon                dioxide to an inferior end of the photosynthesis                chamber;
   (6) combining the filter material with the                carbon dioxide and a chloroplast solution in the                photosynthesis chamber;
   (7) reducing the quantity of carbon dioxide                within the photosynthesis chamber through                photosynthesis;
   (8) creating a glucose product within the                photosynthesis chamber;
   (9) pumping the glucose product from a                superior end of the photosynthesis chamber through                a second biomolecule sensor and to the energy rich                homogenate chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The bio-energy                  production system creates electrons and protons                  for human use. The photosynthesis matrix consumes                  the carbon dioxide and produces glucose. The                  photosynthesis matrix is housed on at least one                  drone, the drone including wires that attract                  additional carbon dioxide molecules. The glucose                  from the photosynthesis matrix is used as a food                  source for the bio-energy production system. The                  ATP solution is used by the photosynthesis matrix                  at the same rate that the glucose is used by the                  bio-energy production system. The glucose is used                  by the bio-energy production system during the                  selection process. The interconnected system is                  incorporated into a passive cellular array. The                  cell in the passive cellular array includes both                  the energy rich homogenate solution and the                  chloroplast solution.
