AU HUANG Z
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG Y
   LU Y
   ZHU L
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 2021016486
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capture system comprises carbon                dioxide capture and separation system and heat pump                system; the carbon dioxide capture and separation                system includes absorption tower, desorption tower,                lean liquid pump, rich liquid pump, lean and rich                liquid heat exchanger, and lean liquid condenser;                the carbon dioxide capture and separation system                and heat pump system are connected through                reboiler/condenser, the reboiler/condenser includes                reboiler and condenser, the reboiler is connected                with carbon dioxide capture and separation system,                condenser is connected with heat pump system; the                lower end of the absorption tower is connected to                the upper end of the desorption tower through rich                liquid streamline, the upper end of absorption                tower is connected to lower end of the desorption                tower through the lean liquid streamline, the rich                liquid pump is provided on the rich liquid                streamline, and the lean liquid condenser and the                lean liquid pump are provided on the lean liquid                streamline.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide capture system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is based on recovery and                utilization of waste heat from blast furnace slag                washing water; has good capture effect, strong                processing air volume; can uses mature process to                treat blast furnace gas, achieves 90% CO2 removal                rate of blast furnace gas, reduces the carbon                emission coefficient of blast furnace gas, can                increases the calorific value of blast furnace gas                from 3500 kJ/Nm3 to 4400 kJ/Nm3.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide capture system comprises carbon                dioxide capture and separation system and heat pump                system; the carbon dioxide capture and separation                system includes absorption tower, desorption tower,                lean liquid pump, rich liquid pump, lean and rich                liquid heat exchanger, and lean liquid condenser;                the carbon dioxide capture and separation system                and the heat pump system are connected through a                reboiler/condenser, the reboiler/condenser includes                a reboiler and a condenser, the reboiler is                connected with the carbon dioxide capture and                separation system, and the condenser is connected                with the heat pump system; the lower end of the                absorption tower is connected to the upper end of                the desorption tower through a rich liquid                streamline, the upper end of the absorption tower                is connected to the lower end of the desorption                tower through the lean liquid streamline, the rich                liquid pump is provided on the rich liquid                streamline, and the lean liquid condenser and the                lean liquid pump are provided on the lean liquid                streamline, the rich liquid streamline and the lean                liquid streamline are connected by the lean and                rich liquid heat exchanger; the lean liquid                streamline is provided with a chemical absorbent                streamline, and the chemical absorbent streamline                is connected to the lower end of the desorption                tower, the reboiler is arranged on the chemical                absorbent flow line. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for carbon dioxide capture process based                on recovery and utilization of waste heat from                blast furnace slag flushing water, comprising using                carbon dioxide capture system; carrying circulation                steps of carbon dioxide capture and separation                system by: passing blast furnace gas after                dedusting, desulfurization and denitration into the                absorption tower from bottom to top from the bottom                of the absorption tower to make countercurrent                contact with the lean liquid state chemical                absorbent sprayed from the top to the bottom of the                absorption tower, absorbing carbon dioxide in blast                furnace gas by chemical absorbent, changing it from                the lean liquid state to the rich liquid state,                transporting by the rich liquid pump to the lean                and rich liquid heat exchanger, purified blast                furnace gas leaves from the top of the absorption                tower; passing rich liquid state chemical absorbent                heated by the heat exchange of the lean and rich                liquid heat exchanger into the upper end of the                desorption tower to make countercurrent contact                with the bottom-up steam generated by the reboiler                at the bottom of the tower, separating the vapor                containing carbon dioxide, under the action of the                gas-liquid separator, refluxing condensed liquid to                the upper end of the desorption tower, discharging                high-purity carbon dioxide from the desorption                tower, reduction of carbon dioxide content in the                chemical absorber changes from a rich liquid state                to a lean liquid state, transporting by the lean                liquid pump to the lean and rich liquid heat                exchanger for heat exchange and cooling, and then                returning to the top of the absorption tower                through the lean liquid condenser to further cool                down for cyclic absorption; carrying heat pump                system circulation steps by: passing the blast                furnace slag flushing water of the steel plant into                the evaporator after precipitation and removal of                impurities, transferring the heat of blast furnace                slag flushing water to the refrigeration working                fluid, the refrigerant becomes a high-temperature                and high-pressure medium under the action of the                compressor, entering the condenser to release heat,                exchanging heat in the condenser to cool down,                depressurizing refrigerant by the throttle valve                and entering the evaporator to complete the heat                exchange cycle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide capture system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chemical absorbent                  is alcohol amine absorbent composed of inorganic                  amine and/or organic amine. The refrigerant is                  R123, R114, R141b, R142b, R245fa, R600, and/or                  R744.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The heat pump system                  includes compressor, a throttle valve, and                  evaporator; the compressor, the throttle valve,                  the evaporator, and the condenser are connected                  in sequence to form a closed circuit. The lower                  end of the absorption tower is provided with a                  lower inlet of the absorption tower and a lower                  outlet of the absorption tower, blast furnace gas                  enters the inside of the absorption tower from                  the lower inlet of the absorption tower, the                  lower outlet of the absorption tower is connected                  to the rich liquid pump through the rich liquid                  streamline; the upper end of the absorption tower                  is provided with an upper inlet of the absorption                  tower and an upper outlet of the absorption                  tower, the purified coal gas purified by the                  absorption tower is transported from the inside                  of the absorption tower through the upper outlet                  of the absorption tower, the upper inlet of the                  absorption tower is connected to the lean liquid                  condenser through the lean liquid streamline. The                  upper end of the desorption tower is provided                  with a first inlet, a second inlet and a first                  outlet, and the rich liquid streamline is                  connected with the first inlet, the first outlet                  is connected to the gas-liquid separator through                  a steam flow line, the second inlet is connected                  with the liquid streamline, and a third inlet and                  a second outlet are provided at the lower end of                  the desorption tower, the second outlet is                  connected to the lean liquid streamline, and the                  third inlet is connected to the reboiler. The                  inlet of the gas-liquid separator passes through                  the steam streamline and the first outlet, the                  outlet of the gas-liquid separator is provided                  with a gas streamline for high-purity carbon                  dioxide discharge, and the outlet of the                  gas-liquid separator is connected with the second                  inlet through the liquid streamline. The solution                  inlet of the lean and rich liquid heat exchanger                  is connected to the rich liquid pump through the                  rich liquid streamline, and is connected to the                  lean liquid pump through the lean liquid                  streamline; the solution outlet of the lean-rich                  liquid heat exchanger is connected to the first                  inlet of the desorption tower through the rich                  liquid streamline, and is connected to the lean                  liquid condenser through the lean liquid                  streamline. The inlet of the lean liquid                  condenser is provided with a cooling water                  streamline inlet and a lean liquid streamline                  inlet connected to the lean-rich liquid heat                  exchanger, the outlet of the lean liquid                  condenser is provided with a cooling water                  streamline outlet and a lean liquid streamline                  outlet connected to the absorption tower.

PN CN114392723-A
TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent for adsorption removal of            carbon dioxide in low or high humidity environment,            comprises cement, amine substance, carrier e.g.            graphene, auxiliary agent e.g. sodium carboxymethyl            cellulose, and water.
AU YUAN L
   ZHAO B
   LI L
   WANG C
   QIN H
   LI S
   CHENG Y
   TAN G
   CHENG S
AE CHIA TAI ENERGY MATERIALS DALIAN LTD (CHIA-Non-standard)
GA 202262854R
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises 1 pts. wt.                cement, 0.25-1 pts. wt. amine substance, 0.25-1                pts. wt. carrier, 0.01-0.03 pts. wt. auxiliary                agent and 0.5-2 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Carbon dioxide adsorbent used for adsorption                removal of carbon dioxide in a low humidity                environment of &#8804;50% or less, or high                humidity environment greater than 50% (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple and easy to                implement, eco-friendly and suitable for mass                production and industrial application. The prepared                carbon dioxide adsorbent has good mechanical                strength, and applied to adsorption and removal of                carbon dioxide in a high-humidity environment, is                easy to activate and regenerate and recycled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   A method for producing a carbon dioxide                adsorbent, which involves: carrying out dry                dispersion loading on an amine substance on a                carrier by adding amine substances into a carrier                material, and then uniformly mixing the materials                by adopting equipment to obtain a mixed material 1,                where apparatus comprises crusher or a stirring                mixer or a kneader. The mixed material and cement                are uniformly mixed by adopting the equipment to                obtain a mixed material 2. The auxiliary agent and                water are introduced, adopted the equipment,                uniformly mixed the mixed material 2 and the                auxiliary agent powder, and then introduced water,                where water is introduced before the formation of                the adsorbent or in the formation process of the                adsorbent according to a specific forming mode. The                auxiliary agent powder is dissolved in water to                form an auxiliary agent aqueous solution, and                introduced the mixed material 2 into the auxiliary                agent aqueous solution before or during the forming                process. The rolling ball or extruding strip or                tabletting adsorbent forming method is used to form                a granular adsorbent, where granules comprise                balls, strips or tablets, or the concrete is molded                by adopting a concrete molding method of mold                entering and compaction, so that the integral,                honeycomb or granular adsorbent is formed. The                hydration maintenance is carried out on the formed                adsorbent, and sealed the adsorbent or opening the                adsorbent in the environment in the process,                preferably sealing the adsorbent. The humidity of                the curing environment is maintained to be more                than 95% in the curing process, maintained for 1-10                days to obtain a material after maintenance, and                drying the cured material to obtain the carbon                dioxide adsorbent.
   A carbon dioxide adsorbent produced by the                production method.
   A method for activating and regenerating the                carbon dioxide adsorbent, which involves                heat-treating fresh or using carbon dioxide                adsorbent in an air stream or an inert gas stream                at a temperature of 80-120&#8451; for 0.5                hour-1 day.
   Use of the carbon dioxide adsorbent for                adsorption removal of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The cement is portland                  cement, aluminate cement, and phosphate cement.                  The amine substance is triethylene tetramine,                  tetraethylene pentamine, pentaethylene hexamine,                  polyethyleneimine, diethanolamine and                  triethanolamine, preferably tetraethylene                  pentamine, pentaethylene hexamine,                  polyethyleneimine and triethanolamine. The                  carrier is powdered activated carbon, graphene,                  nano silicon dioxide, white carbon black and                  powdered carbon molecular sieve. The auxiliary                  agent is sesbania powder, sodium carboxymethyl                  cellulose and starch. Preferred Conditions: The                  powdered activated carbon has a specific surface                  area of more than 1000 m2/g. The powdered                  activated carbon with a water extract pH value                  greater than 7. The nano-silicon dioxide has                  specific surface area greater than 250 m2/g. The                  white carbon black has specific surface area                  greater than 250 m2/g. The dry dispersion load                  process of amine substance does not introduce any                  other solvent, where the maintaining temperature                  is 10-50&#8451;, preferably 20-40&#8451;.                  The adsorbent is exposed to air to dry naturally,                  or dried at low temperature in air or inert gas                  at 40 -80&#8451; for 1 hour-5 days. The fresh                  or used carbon dioxide adsorbent is heat treated                  in a certain temperature of air flow or inert gas                  flow at 80-120&#8451; for 0.5 hour-1                  day.

PN CN113956225-A
TI Preparing amorphous apigenin nanoparticles by supercritical enhanced solution dispersion technology comprises e.g. using miscible solvent to dissolve apigenin, reducing carbon dioxide flow rate to preset flow rate when all solution entered kettle, and taking out apigenin granules after dropping.
AU FANG W
   WU B
   LU X
   BIAN J
   WANG S
   HAN X
   SONG X
   YAN T
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 202218918A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing amorphous apigenin nanoparticles by supercritical enhanced solution dispersion technology comprises (1) opening carbon dioxide inlet valve, closing other valves at same time, checking air tightness of high pressure crystallization system, setting pressure and temperature parameters in kettle, starting heating system, opening carbon dioxide outlet valve to maintain certain flow rate of the carbon dioxide outlet gas, and maintaining the pressure and temperature in the kettle, and (2) using miscible solvent to dissolve apigenin to obtain 3-5 mg/ml apigenin solution, increasing the flow rate of carbon dioxide to the specified flow rate, at the same time, pumping the apigenin solution at certain flow rate, reducing the carbon dioxide flow rate to the preset flow rate when all the solution entered the kettle, closing the carbon dioxide inlet valve, closing the heater, and opening the lid of the kettle and taking out the apigenin granules after dropping to normal pressure.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing amorphous apigenin nanoparticles by supercritical enhanced solution dispersion technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple operation, short production period, green environmental protection; and is suitable for heat-sensitive medicine; can improve the supersaturation of the solution; improves the yield and purity of the medicine; adjusts the process parameters; obtains amorphous apigenin nanoparticles with high purity, narrow particle size distribution, high yield and controllable morphology; significantly improves the dissolution rate and pharmacological activity of amorphous apigenin nanoparticles; and expands its application in injection medicine delivery, pulmonary medicine delivery, etc.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing amorphous apigenin nanoparticles by supercritical enhanced solution dispersion technology comprises (1) opening the carbon dioxide inlet valve, closing other valves at the same time, checking the air tightness of the high pressure crystallization system, setting the pressure and temperature parameters in the kettle, starting the heating system, opening the high-pressure pump to pump carbon dioxide into the kettle until the preset pressure after reaching the preset temperature, opening the carbon dioxide outlet valve to maintain certain flow rate of the carbon dioxide outlet gas, and maintaining the pressure and temperature in the kettle, and (2) using miscible solvent to dissolve apigenin to obtain 3-5 mg/ml apigenin solution, increasing the flow rate of carbon dioxide to the specified flow rate, at the same time, pumping the apigenin solution at certain flow rate, reducing the carbon dioxide flow rate to the preset flow rate when all the solution entered the kettle, maintaining for certain period of time until the solvent in the kettle is completely drained, closing the carbon dioxide inlet valve, closing the heater, and opening the lid of the kettle and taking out the apigenin granules after dropping to normal pressure, where the miscible solvent is that dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) and acetone are miscible according to the volume ratio of 1:10 when configuring the apigenin solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the pressure range in the kettle is 5-40 MPa, and the temperature range is 30-60degrees Celsius. The flow rate of carbon dioxide when the apigenin solution is not passed was 1 LPM. In the step (2), the carbon dioxide is sprayed into the still through the outer shaft of the coaxial nozzle, the apigenin solution is sprayed into the still through the inner shaft, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 5-10 LPM, and the apigenin solution flow rate is 1 ml/minute, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is reduced to 1 LPM after the solution is pumped, and the infusion time is 1 hours.

PN CN110422844-A
TI Preparing electronic grade carbon dioxide by using flue gas as raw material comprises introducing flue gas, passing oxygen, mixing with crude carbon dioxide, cooling, adsorbing, extracting heavy impurities and passing to three waste system.
AU ZENG X
AE TIANJIN ZHONGKE TUOXIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201996269C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing electronic grade carbon dioxide by using flue gas as raw material comprises (i) introducing flue gas after bleaching and dust removal, extracting light components, crude carbon dioxide gas and passing into a mixer and extracting heavy components and passing it into a three waste system, (ii) passing oxygen into the mixer and mixing with crude carbon dioxide, applying pressure, allowing small amount of sulfur-containing, nitrogen-containing and hydrocarbon compounds to fully react, cooling the top of the catalytic burner using a cooler, (iii) adsorbing acid gas and water using an adsorbent, introducing into the low pressure tower, (iv) extracting light impurities from low pressure tower and transferring to a high pressure tower and (v) extracting from the top of the high pressure tower for heat exchange, taking part as high pressure tower reflux liquid and using part as carbon dioxide product, extracting heavy impurities and passing to the three waste system.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing electronic grade carbon dioxide by using flue gas as raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces 7N electronic-grade carbon dioxide; can save greater than or equal to 30% of energy consumption; is economical; and effectively reduces carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing electronic grade carbon dioxide by using flue gas as raw material comprises (i) introducing flue gas after bleaching and dust removal into a pretreatment tower (T101), extracting the light components in the flue gas from the top of the pretreatment tower, extracting crude carbon dioxide gas by the lower side line and passing into a mixer (M101) and extracting the heavy components from the bottom and passing it into a three waste system, (ii) passing oxygen into the mixer, and mixing uniformly with crude carbon dioxide, applying pressure using a compressor (C101) and entering into a catalytic burner (R101), allowing a small amount of sulfur-containing, nitrogen-containing and hydrocarbon compounds contained in the crude carbon dioxide to fully react in the catalytic burner, cooling the top of the catalytic burner using a cooler (E101) and entering into an adsorption tower (T102), (iii) adsorbing the acid gas and water in the reaction product using an adsorbent in the adsorption tower, extracting from the top of the adsorption tower and introducing into the low pressure tower (T103), (iv) extracting light impurities from the top of the low pressure tower and transferring the tower kettle material to a high pressure tower (T104) through a tower kettle pump (P101) and (v) extracting from the top of the high pressure tower into a heat exchanger for heat exchange, taking part of it as high pressure tower reflux liquid and using part of it is as the carbon dioxide product, extracting heavy impurities from the tower kettle and passing it to the three waste system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst used in the catalytic burner (R101) is supported cobalt molybdenum or cobalt molybdenum tungsten catalyst and/or supported nickel molybdenum or nickel molybdenum tungsten catalyst. The amount of sulfur and nitrogen in the raw material gas adsorbed by the adsorption tower is less than or equal to 0.5 ppm and the water content is less than or equal to 10 ppm. The adsorbent of the adsorption tower is at least one of activated carbon, alumina molecular sieve and silica gel. The amount of carbon dioxide content in the flue gas after bleaching and dust removal is 10-90%. Preferred Method: The temperature of the catalytic burner is 300-350 degrees C and the pressure is 1-3 MPa. The high-pressure tower and low-pressure column are operated at a pressure of 2-4 MPa and 1-2.5 MPa. The temperature difference between the high pressure tower and the low temperature tower is greater than or equal to 12 degrees C.

TI Method for measuring carbon dioxide in carbon            sequestrate formation water diffusion coefficient,            comprises e.g. adding fluorescent agent into stratum            water sample to stir to obtain mixed solution sample,            and adding carbon dioxide and mixed liquid sample.
AU WANG Y
   XU L
AE JINAN YINGCHUANG ZHILIAN TECHNOLOGY CONS (JINA-Non-standard)
GA 2022B0936N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding a fluorescent agent                into a stratum water sample to stir to obtain a                mixed solution sample. The carbon dioxide and the                mixed liquid sample are placed into a microfluidic                chip micro-channel diffusion. The microchannel                fluorescence picture is recorded different time                through a fluorescence microscope. The fluorescent                picture is introduced into an ImageJ for                fluorescence intensity analysis. The pH value of                the mixed solution is calculated corresponding to                the x position in the microchannel. The x position                is the distance of the initial coordinate of the                carbon dioxide in the mixed fluid sample.
   USE - Method for measuring carbon dioxide in carbon                sequestrate formation water diffusion coefficient                by using micro-fluid used in carbon dioxide                sequestrations.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates the error caused by the                convection quality transmission, at the same time,                microfluidic can bear the experiment condition of                high temperature and high pressure, improve the                accuracy of experiment and calculation                result.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                method for measuring carbon dioxide in carbon                sequestrate formation water diffusion                coefficient.

GA 202284268W
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises a contaminant remover,                and an organic solvent comprising a fluoroalkane                compound (I).
   USE - The composition is used as a liquid carbon                dioxide-based cleaning detergent composition, and                as a detergent composition for liquid carbon                dioxide-based cleaning, preferably laundry cleaning                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has excellent usability for                high-pressure liquid carbon dioxide cleaning and                excellent solubility of detergent components in                liquid carbon dioxide, excellently removes both                oil-based and water-based contaminants, has                excellent cleaning efficiency for water-based                contaminants and oil-based contaminants, has good                friendliness with a non-polar solvent such as                liquid carbon dioxide and water-based and oil-based                contaminants removing substances to dissolve those                contaminants removing substances in the liquid                carbon dioxide easily, and is safe for human body                and the environment and eco-friendly with no                flammability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composition comprises a contaminant remover,                and an organic solvent comprising a fluoroalkane                compound of formula: R1-(CH2)m(CRfF)n-R2 (I).
   Rf = H or F, preferably F;
   R1, R2 = H, F, CH3, CH3O and CF3;
   m = 0-4;and
   n = 1-5.
   An INEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of cleaning using liquid carbon dioxide, involving                using above-mentioned composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 80-99 wt.% organic solvent, and 1-20                  wt.% contaminant remover. The composition                  comprises 80-90 wt.% organic solvent, 1-10 wt.%                  oil-based contaminant remover, and 1-10 wt.%                  water-based contaminant remover. Preferred                  Components: The organic solvent comprises                  perfluoroheptane, pentafluorobutane,                  decafluoropentane and methoxynonafluorobutane.                  The contaminant remover comprises an oil-based                  contaminant remover, and/or a water-based                  contaminant remover. The oil-based                  contaminant remover comprises dimethyl carbonate,                  diethyl carbonate, dipropyl carbonate and/or                  dibutyl carbonate. The water-based contaminant                  remover is an ether compound of formula:                  CaH2a+1-O-(CH2CH2O)b-H (III). The water-based                  contaminant remover comprises polyoxyethylene                  dodecanyl ether, polyoxyethylene tetradecanyl                  ether, polyoxyethylene hexadecanyl ether and/or                  polyoxyethylene heptadecanyl ether.R3, R4 = 1-4C alkyl, where R3, R4 may be                  equal to or different from each other;a = 5-21, preferably 10-18;andb = 1-10.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 89 wt.%                pentafluorobutane; 1 wt.% : dimethyl carbonate; and                10 wt.% polyoxyethylene (7) dodecyl ether.

   WEISS R
AE GROHE AG (GRHE-C)
GA 2020796151
AB    NOVELTY - Decalcifying water comprises enriching water with carbon dioxide, and adding carbon dioxide to water at least as a function of: a temperature of the water, a degree of hardness of the water and a pressure or a flow rate of the water, in a fitting or downstream of a valve. The method is carried out immediately upstream of a valve, where the water exiting via the valve or via a line connected to the valve from a line network to environment.
   USE - The method is useful for decalcifying water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out in simple and efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for decalcifying water by enriching the water with carbon dioxide comprising a storage tank for carbon dioxide and has an impregnation device for introducing carbon dioxide into the water, where the impregnation device is arranged in or downstream or directly upstream of a fitting, and the water exiting via the fitting or via a line connected to the fitting from a pipe network to an environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The enrichment takes place downstream of a mixing device which is provided for mixing water volumes of different temperatures. The enrichment takes place upstream of a mixing device which is provided for mixing differently tempered water volumes, each volume of water supplied to the mixing device being enriched with carbon dioxide. The addition of carbon dioxide takes place only with flowing water. The addition of carbon dioxide takes place in a proportion of not greater than 3 g/l. The metered addition of carbon dioxide takes place in a proportion which exceeds a saturation limit of the water into which the carbon dioxide is metered by not greater than 0.5 g/l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device comprises a control unit for regulating the addition of carbon dioxide to the water and a temperature sensor for determining a temperature of the water and at least one pressure sensor for determining a pressure or a flow sensor for determining a flow rate of the water. The device has a degree of hardness sensor for determining a degree of hardness of the water. The impregnation device is arranged downstream of a mixing device, which is provided for mixing water volumes of different temperatures. The impregnation device is arranged upstream of a mixing device which is provided for mixing differently tempered water volumes, an impregnation device being arranged in each feed line of the mixing device via which a volume of water can be fed to the mixing device.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - A methane/carbon dioxide continuously separating method involves forming a hydrate of the mixed gas methane/carbon dioxide in the fluidized bed through the gas distributor and the atomized droplets, combining heat pump technology with the formation and decomposition of hydrates, connecting the heat pump cold source to the variable diameter conduit of the fluidized bed reactor and providing the required low temperature environment for hydrate formation. The heat pump heat source is connected to the cone of the cyclone separator to provide the required high temperature environment for hydrate dissociation, which improves energy utilization efficiency and achieves continuous separation of methane/carbon dioxide mixed gas.
   USE - Method for continuously separating methane/carbon dioxide.

The atomized droplets comprises pure water, amino acid solution or quaternary ammonium salt solution. Preferred Composition: The amino acid solution or quaternary ammonium salt solution are present in the concentration of 0-5 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The circulating heat transfer medium is 50-80 wt.% ethylene glycol solution.

The first circuit and the second circuit are provided for exchanging heat through the sodium-sodium heat exchanger to transfer the high temperature heat generated by the reactor to a liquid sodium of the second circuit, while the second cycle and the third cycle exchange heat through the supercritical carbon dioxide-sodium heat exchanger to transfer the heat to the supercritical carbon dioxide of the third cycle.
   USE - Highly efficient supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system with sodium-cooled fast reactor and two-stage branching.
   ADVANTAGE - The low temperature regenerator and the high temperature regenerator use a printed circuit board heat exchanger (PCHE) to achieve the compactness, high efficiency and low resistance of the regenerator under the condition of large heat recovery of the supercritical carbon dioxide joule cycle with the main compressor operating near a critical point of carbon dioxide to ensure that the recompression joule cycle of the third cycle has high cycle efficiency. The good coordination of the heat exchange temperature window of the second circuit and the third circuit are realized on top of each other and the problem of efficient cascading use of the energy of the supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system is solved with a sodium-cooled fast reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the highly efficient supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and wax removal system for powder metallurgy molded parts, comprises a supercritical carbon dioxide injection module, that includes an injection pump, and carbon dioxide storage tank.
AU MA F
AE MA F (MAFF-Individual)
GA 2020954201
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and wax removal system comprises a supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) injection module (1), a supercritical CO2 extraction cycle module (2), and a dewaxing separation module (3). The supercritical CO2 injection module includes an injection pump (11), a CO2 storage tank (12), and a condenser (13). The injection pump is driven by a motor to achieve forward drive and reverse drive. The bottom end of the CO2 storage tank is connected to the forward output end of the injection pump through a pipe. The condenser is connected to the top end of the CO2 storage tank through a pipe, and the output end of the condenser is connected to a supercritical CO2 extraction cycle module. The supercritical CO2 extraction circulation module includes a recovery pump (21), a circulation tank (22), a filter kettle (23) and an extraction kettle group (24). The recovery pump, the circulation tank and the filter kettle are connected in series in sequence.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and wax removal system for powder metallurgy molded parts.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses pollution-free supercritical CO2 medium for removing wax. The cost is low. The wax can be removed quickly by extraction, and CO2 can be directly discharged by separating the wax. Each extraction kettle unit can realize independent extraction and can also perform extraction at the same time. The provided electric heating box can avoid the problem of wax getting solid and causing blockage during the cooling process.

PN WO2019051609-A1; US2020208278-A1
TI Electrocatalytically reducing carbon dioxide by            delivering gas having carbon dioxide to cathode,            maintaining electrical potential difference between            cathode and anode, and delivering water or aqueous            electrolyte solution to support layer.
AU BERLINGUETTE C
   SALVATORE D
   LI Y C
   YAN Z
   MALLOUK T E
AE UNIV BRITISH COLUMBIA (UYBR-C)
   PENN STATE RES FOUND (PSRF-C)
GA 2019261505
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide is electrocatalytically reduced                by delivering gas comprising carbon dioxide to                cathode comprising gas diffusion layer and cathode                catalyst of membrane electrode assembly, which                further has anode comprising anode catalyst,                bipolar membrane between anode and cathode and                comprising anion exchange layer facing anode and                cation exchange layer facing cathode, and liquid                permeable support layer between bipolar membrane                and cathode; maintaining electrical potential                difference between cathode and anode of membrane                electrode assembly; and delivering water or aqueous                electrolyte solution to support layer so that                support layer contains liquid water and allowing                liquid water of support layer to hydrate cation                exchange layer and carry cations from bipolar                membrane toward cathode, where electrical potential                between anode and cathode electrochemically reduces                carbon dioxide in gas and causes current density at                cathode of greater than or equal to 100                mA/cm2.
   USE - Electrocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The support layer facilitates stable operation                at higher current densities. The presence of                aqueous support layer inhibits carbonate-forming                reactions between hydroxide ions and carbon dioxide                at cathode by ensuring the availability of water at                the cathode. The water can undergo a                hydrogen-forming reaction at the cathode which                competes with reactions that create carbonates. The                hydrogen-forming reaction can also beneficially                cause the cell to produce a mixture of carbon                monoxide and hydrogen gas (syngas), which is useful                as a fuel. Furthermore, the support layer retains                water for hydration of the bipolar membrane,                achieving stable performance of the                electrocatalytic cell, reducing gaseous carbon                dioxide at a reasonable Faradaic efficiency (FE)                while operating at high current densities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) electrolytic cell operable for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide,                comprising the membrane electrode assembly;                and
   (2) system for electrochemically reducing                gaseous carbon dioxide comprising a cell and a                power supply connected to supply a potential                difference between the anode and the cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method includes                  humidifying the gas before delivering gas to                  cathode, where delivering water to support layer                  comprises allowing water from humidified gas to                  enter support layer and collect in support layer                  as liquid water. Delivering gas to cathode                  comprises passing flow of gas through flow plate                  that is in fluid communication with gas diffusion                  layer of cathode. The method comprises delivering                  basic anolyte to anode. Anode catalyst is                  operable to promote oxygen evolution reaction at                  the anode. The method comprises, at the cathode,                  electrochemically reducing the carbon dioxide to                  yield carbon monoxide. The method comprises                  collecting the carbon monoxide. The method                  comprises, at the cathode, generating hydrogen                  gas and collecting a mixture of the carbon                  monoxide and the hydrogen gas. Preferred                  Compositions: Support layer comprises a solid                  porous support. Solid porous support comprises                  glass fibers. Water in aqueous support layer is                  in a solution comprising dissolved electrolyte.                  Electrolyte comprises sodium bicarbonate                  (NaHCO3). Cathode comprises metals selected from                  silver (Ag), gold (Au), copper (Cu), cobalt (Co),                  zinc (Zn), cadmium (Cd), lead (Pb), palladium                  (Pd), iron (Fe), manganese (Mn), tungsten (W),                  and/or tin (Sn). Anode comprises a foamed metal.                  Anode comprises nickel foam that serves as gas                  diffusion layer and as anode catalyst. Anode                  comprises material selected from nickel (Ni),                  iron (Fe), cobalt (Co), manganese (Mn), tungsten                  (W), and/or iron nickelate (FeNiOx). Anode                  comprises late transition metal selected from                  iridium (Ir), platinum (Pt), and/or ruthenium                  (Ru). The gas comprises air, flue gas, and/or                  process gas. The gas has carbon dioxide content                  of 1-2%. Anode is in contact with basic anolyte.                  Preferred Properties: Solution has a pH of 7 plus                  minus 1.5. Preferred Device: Membrane electrode                  assembly is between 1st flow plate engaging the                  cathode and 2nd flow plate engaging the anode.                  Electrolytic cell comprises a source of water                  connected to deliver water or an electrolyte                  solution to the support layer. Electrolytic cell                  comprises conduit arranged to carry a gas                  containing carbon dioxide to cathode, where                  source of water comprises humidifier connected to                  humidify the gas before the gas is carried to the                  cathode. Source of water comprises a pump                  connected to deliver water directly to the                  support layer. The cell comprises greater than or                  equal to 2 passages in fluid communication with                  the support layer, where pump is operable to flow                  water continuously or intermittently through the                  support layer by way of the passages. The cell                  comprises a pump connected to circulate the basic                  anolyte past the anode.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Support layer                  comprises a mat of glass fibers, hydrophilic                  PTFE, expanded PTFE, porous PTFE, silica beads,                  ceramic beads, and/or porous sheets. Preferred                  Properties: Support layer has a thickness of less                  than or equal to 1 mm.

PN CN108006684-A; CN108006684-B
TI Method for removing high temperature carbon dioxide circulation based on rotary fixed bed, involves carrying out next carbonation reaction to capture carbon dioxide so as to complete carbon dioxide removal cycle.
AU RONG N
   FANG T
   ZHU S
   WANG Y
   MA J
   WANG G
   HU H
   MA X
   TIAN X
   PAN W
   WANG J
   LI W
AE UNIV ANHUI JIANZHU (UYAH-C)
GA 201837842L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding flue gas with the temperature of 500-700 degrees C by a flue gas side inlet passage (1). The carbon dioxide in the flue gas (7) is removed by calcium-based absorbent filler located on the flue gas side and forms calcium carbonate, when the flue gas flows through a fixed bed rotor (3). The fuel gas is natural gas, methane, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, synthesis gas, which is discharged from a flue gas side outlet passage (2), after removing carbon dioxide, and calcium oxide in the filler is converted into calcium carbonate by carbonation reaction. The packing on the flue gas side is rotated as rotor rotates around a rotating shaft. The combustion reaction occurs after the fuel gas and oxygen are fed through the fuel gas side inlet passage. The calcium-based absorbent filler is regenerated from calcium carbonate and regenerated into calcium oxide. The next carbonation reaction is carried out to capture carbon dioxide so as to complete a carbon dioxide removal cycle.
   USE - Method for removing high temperature carbon dioxide circulation based on rotary fixed bed.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide cycle removal process based on the rotary fixed bed is made simple, and the fluidization transport and gas-solid separation of the absorbent is not carried out so as to reduce the difficulty of operation. The collision and wear of the particles caused by the fluidization operation are eliminated so as to prolong the service life of the absorbent and reduce the replacement amount of the absorbent. The scale of flue gas treatment is enlarged by parallel connection of multiple systems.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the method for removing high temperature carbon dioxide circulation based on rotary fixed bed.
   Flue gas side inlet passage (1)
   Flue gas side outlet passage (2)
   Fixed bed rotor (3)
   Flue gas (7)
   Oxygen (10)

PN CN112680559-A; CN112680559-B
TI Extracting vanadium by blowing carbon dioxide at            stepped bottom of converter involves adding            vanadium-containing molten iron into converter, blowing            carbon dioxide at bottom of converter after shaking,            using oxygen lance top blowing oxygen.
AU LI L
   WANG J
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 2021492556
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting vanadium involves adding                vanadium-containing molten iron into the converter,                blowing carbon dioxide (CO2) at bottom of the                converter after shaking, using oxygen (O2) lance                top blowing oxygen lance blowing for 2 minutes,                adding coolant, and blowing to the endpoint.
   USE - Method for extracting vanadium by blowing                carbon dioxide at step bottom of the                converter.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide gas discharged by the steel                production can be recycled. The effect of                energy-saving and emission reduction can be                achieved. The residual vanadium content can be                effectively reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the                  vanadium-containing molten iron is 1260-1320                  degrees C. The dosage of the cooling agent is 20                  to the power of 20-27 kilograms/time (kg/t) of                  the iron. Blowing to the endpoint, the semi-steel                  temperature is controlled at 1360-1400 degrees C.                  Preferred Process: The bottom of the converter is                  paved ventilation brick according to the I area,                  II area, and III area, where the ventilation                  brick I area, and III area are symmetrically                  distributed. The ventilation brick II area is                  symmetrically distributed, the included angle of                  II area is 80-100 degrees . Preferred Parameters:                  The single ventilation brick bottom blowing CO2                  flow in the I area is 100-120                  newton-meter-cube/minute (Nm3/min), the II area                  in single ventilation brick bottom blowing CO2                  flow is 140-160 Nm3/minute, the III area single                  ventilation brick bottom blowing CO2 flow is                  180-200 Nm3/min. The oxygen supply intensity of                  the oxygen lance is 1.5-3.0 Nm3/minutes (Nm3.t)                  iron, the gun position is controlled at 1.7-2.0                  meters (m). The oxygen lance top blowing O2 time                  is 5-6 minutes. Preferred Components: The coolant                  is selected from pellet ore, pig iron block, or                  cold pressing block for the converter.

AU WANG T
   GUO H
   MAI S
AE JIANGSU HENGCHUANG PACKING MATERIAL LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202252239A
AB    NOVELTY - Biaxially stretched polypropylene (BOPP) film,                comprises an upper surface layer, a core layer, a                lower surface layer, where the surface layer and                lower surface layer comprises 91-98 pts. wt.                homopolymerized polypropylene, 1-5 pts. wt.                anti-blocking agent and 1-4 pts. wt. carbon dioxide                reducing agent master batch. The core layer                comprises 90-98 pts. wt. homopolymerized                polypropylene, 0.5-3 pts. wt. antistatic agent and                0.5-3 pts. wt. slip agent and 1-4 pts. wt. carbon                dioxide reducing agent masterbatch, where the                carrier of the carbon dioxide reducing agent master                batch is a PP homopolymer. The component of the                carbon dioxide reducing agent master batch is a                carbon dioxide reducing agent. The carbon dioxide                reducing agent is a carbonized catalyst liposome.                The average grain diameter of the carbonization                catalyst liposome is nanometre level.
   USE - The biaxially stretched polypropylene film is                useful in packaging and printing industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The BOPP film: does not reduce the original                physical performance and keeps excellent                appearance; and the method is simple and economical                does not need special production device and can                reduce the carbon dioxide emission rubbish                burning.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biaxially stretched polypropylene (BOPP) film,                comprises an upper surface layer, a core layer, a                lower surface layer, where the surface layer and                lower surface layer comprises 91-98 pts. wt.                homopolymerized polypropylene, 1-5 pts. wt.                anti-blocking agent and 1-4 pts. wt. carbon dioxide                reducing agent master batch. The core layer                comprises 90-98 pts. wt. homopolymerized                polypropylene, 0.5-3 pts. wt. antistatic agent and                0.5-3 pts. wt. slip agent and 1-4 pts. wt. carbon                dioxide reducing agent masterbatch, where the                carrier of the carbon dioxide reducing agent master                batch is a PP homopolymer. The component of the                carbon dioxide reducing agent master batch is a                carbon dioxide reducing agent. The carbon dioxide                reducing agent is a carbonized catalyst liposome.                The carbonization catalyst liposome is a spherical                carrier preparation formed by enveloping the                carbonization catalyst in the single-layer                phospholipids capsule. The average grain diameter                of the carbonization catalyst liposome is nanometre                level.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                a biaxially stretched polypropylene film,                comprising (i) premixing homo-polypropylene and                carbon dioxide reducing agent master batches in the                raw materials of the upper surface layer, core                layer and lower surface layer and then respectively                mixing the uniformly mixed homo-polypropylene and                carbon dioxide reducing agent master batches with                an anti-bonding agent of the upper surface layer,                an antistatic agent and a slip agent of the core                layer and an anti-bonding agent of the lower                surface layer to obtain a raw material of the upper                surface layer, a raw material of the core layer and                a raw material of the lower surface layer, (ii)                adding the upper surface layer raw material, core                layer raw material and lower surface layer raw                material into different extruders, separately                feeding the materials in each layer and performing                melting plasticization to obtain an upper surface                layer melt, a core layer melt and a lower surface                layer melt, (iii) converging and extruding the                upper surface layer melt, core layer melt and lower                surface layer melt in a three-layer structure die                head to obtain a membrane, (iv) cooling the                membrane by attaching a chilling roller and water                bath to obtain a casting sheet, and (v)                longitudinally stretching the casting sheet,                transverse stretching, carrying out corona                treatment on the cast sheet and then rolling to                obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carrier of the                  anti-blocking agent is PP homopolymer. The                  component is silicon dioxide. The concentration                  is 5 wt.% and the grain diameter is 4-7                  &#181;m. The concentration of the carbon                  dioxide reducing agent in the carbon dioxide                  reducing agent master batch is 5 wt.% and the                  carbon dioxide reducing agent is prepared by                  supercritical reverse evaporation method. The                  carrier of the antistatic agent is PP                  homopolymer. The component is ethoxy amine and                  mono glyceride and the concentration is 40 wt.%.                  The carrier of the slip agent is PP homopolymer.                  The component is high molecular amine and the                  concentration is 25 wt.%. The thickness of the                  upper surface layer is 1.0-1.5 &#181;m. The                  thickness of the middle layer is 16-36                  &#181;m and the thickness of the lower                  surface layer is 1.0-1.5 &#181;m.Preferred Method: In the method, the melting                  and plasticizing temperatures are as follows; the                  feeding section is 210-240&#8451;, the                  melting section is 245-255&#8451; and the                  metering section is 250-255&#8451;. The                  method comprises the following steps; the                  longitudinal stretching ratio is 4.50-5.50 and                  the transverse stretching ratio is 8.0-10.0, in                  the longitudinal stretching process, the                  preheating temperature is 120-145&#8451;, the                  stretching temperature is 110-135&#8451;, and                  the setting temperature is 140-145&#8451; and                  in the transverse stretching process, the                  preheating temperature is 165-175&#8451;, the                  stretching temperature is 150-160&#8451; and                  the setting temperature is 165-170&#8451;,                  where the temperature of the chilling roller is                  22-27&#8451; and the temperature of the water                  bath is 20-25&#8451;.

TI Direct preparation of sine phase grating on            surface of fused quartz by carbon dioxide laser by            emitting carbon dioxide laser beam, focusing and            irradiating surface of fused quartz, ablating,            detecting and forming Gaussian ablation pit.
AU ZHANG C
   WANG H
   CHEN J
   JIANG X
   FANG Z
   LIAO W
   ZHANG L
   ZHU Q
AE LASER FUSION RES CENT CHINA ACAD ENG PHY (CAEP-C)
GA 2021A0274V
AB    NOVELTY - Direct preparation of sine phase grating on                surface of fused quartz by carbon dioxide laser                comprises setting parameter of carbon dioxide laser                according to parameter of sine phase grating to be                formed, emitting carbon dioxide laser beam by                carbon dioxide laser, focusing and irradiating                surface of fused quartz for one time, ablating on                surface of fused quartz to form Gaussian ablation                pit with smooth surface, detecting if surface                topography and profile of Gaussian ablation pit                formed has no ablation deposition, if not,                adjusting parameter of carbon dioxide laser,                re-emitting laser beam to form another Gaussian                ablation pit, repeatedly detecting newly formed                Gaussian ablation pit until surface topography and                outline of Gaussian ablation pit has no ablation                deposition, orderly forming adjacent Gaussian                ablation pit on fused quartz surface on upper step                of ablation formed by Gaussian ablation pit, and                repeating step until each Gaussian ablation pit                forms a preset sine phase grating.
   USE - The method is for direct preparation of sine                phase grating on surface of fused quartz by carbon                dioxide laser.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing one-dimensional                sine phase grating, conveniently and flexibly                realizing preparation of two-dimensional sine phase                grating of multiple structure, high prepared sine                phase grating laser damage threshold, strong laser                beam splitting and important application in the                multi-beam interference micro-structure laser                processing field.

PN CN108854139-A; CN208726791-U
TI Supercritical extraction device has filter that is set between carbon dioxide storage tank and high pressure pump, and material handling device that loads or unloads material cartridge in extraction kettle.
AU ZHAO Y
AE NANTONG RUIZHI SUPERCRITICAL TECHNOLOGY (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2018957170
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical extraction device has a carbon dioxide storage tank (1) that is connected to lower portion of an extraction kettle (3) through a high pressure pump (2). The high pressure pump is provided for transporting carbon dioxide provided in the carbon dioxide storage tank to the extracting kettle. The raw material tank (7) is connected to upper portion of the extraction kettle through the material pump (6). The material pump is provided for transporting raw materials provided in the raw material tank into the extraction kettle. The extraction kettle is connected to a separator, and whose upper portion is connected to a carbon dioxide storage tank. A filter (5) is arranged between carbon dioxide storage tank and the high pressure pump. The material handling device is provided for loading or unloading the material cartridge in the extraction kettle.
   USE - Supercritical extraction device.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide is filtered by the filter to reduce impurities and improve the extraction effect, so that the material cylinder in the extraction kettle can be loaded or taken out through the material cylinder pick-and-place device. Thus the production efficiency of the device can be improved and the labor cost can be reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical extraction device.
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   High pressure pump (2)
   Extraction kettle (3)
   Filter (5)
   Material pump (6)
   Raw material tank (7)

PN CN109704280-A
TI Steam reforming gas separation system combined with pressure swing adsorption, absorption and membrane separation comprises pressure swing adsorption device, carbon dioxide absorption device and hydrogen membrane separation device.
AU XIAO W
   ZHUANG C
   HUANG W
   JIANG X
   RUAN X
   HE G
   LI X
   WU X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201942323X
AB    NOVELTY - Steam reforming gas separation system combined with pressure swing adsorption, absorption and membrane separation comprises a pressure swing adsorption device, carbon dioxide absorption device and hydrogen membrane separation device, where the mixture of coal synthesis gas or natural gas obtained by steam reforming, the main components are hydrogen, carbon dioxide, methane, carbon monoxide and nitrogen, first, the mixed gas is purified by a pressure swing adsorption device to obtain a high-purity hydrogen product, separating desorbed gas into a carbon dioxide absorption device to obtain a carbon dioxide product, the desorbed gas for removing carbon dioxide through the carbon dioxide absorption device enters the hydrogen membrane separation device to obtain a concentrated hydrogen return pressure swing adsorption device on the membrane permeate side to obtain a high purity hydrogen product.
   USE - Used as steam reforming gas separation system combined with pressure swing adsorption, absorption and membrane separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes separation of hydrogen and carbon dioxide steam in the reformed gas, has high hydrogen yield, multiple separation units of synergistic coupling and synergistic advantages, and good economic and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Steam reforming gas separation system combined with pressure swing adsorption, absorption and membrane separation comprises a pressure swing adsorption device, carbon dioxide absorption device and hydrogen membrane separation device, where the mixture of coal synthesis gas or natural gas obtained by steam reforming, the main components are hydrogen, carbon dioxide, methane, carbon monoxide and nitrogen, first, purifying mixed gas by a pressure swing adsorption device to obtain a high-purity hydrogen product, separating desorbed gas into a carbon dioxide absorption device to obtain a carbon dioxide product, the desorbed gas for removing carbon dioxide through the carbon dioxide absorption device enters the hydrogen membrane separation device to obtain a concentrated hydrogen, returning pressure swing adsorption device on the membrane permeate side to obtain a high purity hydrogen product, where the mixed gas containing 50-80 mol.% hydrogen and 10-50 mol.% carbon dioxide, entering pressure swing adsorption device to obtain high-purity hydrogen gas, entering desorbed gas to bottom of the absorption tower (1), where the carbon dioxide absorption device comprises an absorption tower, no.1 circulation pump (2), no.1 heat exchanger (3), desorption column (4), no.2 circulation pump (5), and no.2 heat exchanger, no.3 heat exchanger and no.4 heat exchanger, entering desorbed gas of the pressure swing adsorption device to the carbon dioxide absorption device through the bottom of the absorption tower, the mixed gas after removing carbon dioxide is obtained from the top of the absorption tower, proceeding to a subsequent separation and purification process to remove absorbent, supplemented absorbent and water from the top of the absorption tower into the carbon dioxide absorption unit, the absorption tower absorbs carbon dioxide, an absorbent rich liquid containing a higher carbon dioxide concentration is obtained from the bottom of the absorption tower, the absorbent rich liquid is subjected to heat exchange between first heat exchanger, desorbing tower bottom lean liquid under the action of the firs circulation pump, dripping rich liquid from the top of the desorption tower, the rich liquid is in countercurrent contact with the absorbent vapor generated by the heating medium of the no.4 heat exchange in the desorption column, desorbing carbon dioxide in the rich liquid from the liquid phase absorbent at a high temperature, partially condensed from the top of the desorption column into the no.3 heat exchanger to obtain a carbon dioxide product having a molar concentration of more than 99 mol% in a gas phase, returning liquid phase absorbent to the desorption column, the bottom of the desorption column is obtained to obtain a replenishing agent containing a lower concentration of carbon dioxide, under the action of the no.2 circulating pump, the absorbent lean liquid divided into two paths, one way passes through the heat exchange of the no.4 heat exchanger and enters from the bottom of the desorption tower, the other way is cooled by the no.1 heat exchanger and the no.2 heat exchanger, mixing with the supplementary absorbent and water, returning to the top of the absorption tower, removing mixed gas stream obtained by removing carbon dioxide from the top of the absorption tower by the no.1 defogger, pressurizing by the no.1 compressor at a pressure 2-5 MPa, the outlet of the stream is higher after compression, in the no.5 heat exchanger, using utility cooling water to remove part of the heat, reducing temperature to 30-80 degrees C to meet the entry into the no.2 defogger and the precision filter requirements, removing process stream from the droplets and minute solid particles through no.2 defogger and precision filter, then concentrating hydrogen in a hydrogen membrane separator through a membrane separation device to obtain membrane retentate gas having a lower molar concentration of hydrogen on the membrane retentate side as a fuel gas, the concentrated hydrogen concentration of 85-95 mol% of permeate gas is obtained on the permeate side of the membrane and the pressure is 0.1-0.5 MPa, cooling to 35-45 degrees C by the no.6 heat exchanger, increasing pressure to 2.5-3 MPa by the no.2 compressor, after compressing, the temperature of the stream is high, and after cooling to 40 degrees C using the no.7 heat exchanger, returning permeate gas to the steam reforming gas produced by the hydrogen production unit and mixing into the pressure swing adsorption device for pressure swing adsorption treatment to purify hydrogen, and the product hydrogen molar concentration is 98.5-100%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the steam reforming gas separation system combined with pressure swing adsorption, absorption and membrane separation (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Absorption tower (1)
   Circulation pump (2)
   Heat exchanger (3)
   Desorption column (4)
   Circulation pump (5)

TI Chemical sequestration used in space craft,            involves providing mixture of sulfur dioxide, nitrogen            oxide, nitrogen dioxide, carbon dioxide and water in            chambers, reacting, adding alcohol and dialkyl ketone            solvent, and reacting mixture.
AU RICHARDSON R G
   RICHARDSON G R
AE RICHARDSON R G (RICH-Individual)
   RICHARDSON R G (RICH-Individual)
   RICHARDSON R G (RICH-Individual)
   RICHARDSON G R (RICH-Individual)
GA 202073958R
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical sequestration of sulfur dioxide,                nitrogen oxide, nitrogen dioxide and carbon dioxide                by loop sequence involves (A) providing a mixture                of sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxide, nitrogen                dioxide, carbon dioxide, sodium hypochlorite,                sodium hydroxide and water in reaction chambers                (R2), (B) reacting sulfur dioxide with sodium                hypochlorite and water, (C) reacting nitrogen oxide                and nitrogen dioxide with sodium hypochlorite, (D)                reacting carbon dioxide with sodium hypochlorite                and water, (E) reacting nitrogen oxide and nitrogen                dioxide with sodium hydroxide and sodium                hypochlorite, (F) reacting carbon dioxide with                sodium hydroxide, (G) adding an alcohol solvent to                the mixture, (H) removing solvent from the mixture,                (I) adding dialkyl ketone solvent to the mixture,                (J) removing ketone solvent from the mixture, (K)                adding sodium hypochlorite to the mixture, (L)                adding sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxide, nitrogen                dioxide and carbon dioxide to the mixture in                chambers (R2), and reacting mixture.
   USE - Chemical sequestration used in space craft,                ships, submarines, and anesthesia.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical sequestration of sulfur dioxide,                nitrogen oxide, nitrogen dioxide and carbon dioxide                by loop sequence involves (A) providing a mixture                of sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxide, nitrogen                dioxide, carbon dioxide, sodium hypochlorite,                sodium hydroxide and water in the one or more                reaction chambers (R2), (B) reacting sulfur dioxide                with sodium hypochlorite and water to generate                sodium chloride and sulfuric acid in the mixture,                (C) reacting nitrogen oxide and nitrogen dioxide                with sodium hypochlorite to generate sodium nitrate                and hydrochloric acid in the mixture, (D) reacting                carbon dioxide with sodium hypochlorite and water                to generate hypochlorous acid and sodium                bicarbonate in the mixture, (E) reacting nitrogen                oxide and nitrogen dioxide with sodium hydroxide                and sodium hypochlorite to generate sodium nitrite                and water in the mixture, (F) reacting carbon                dioxide with sodium hydroxide to provide sodium                bicarbonate in the mixture, (G) adding an alcohol                solvent to the mixture, forcing the generated                sodium chloride, sodium bicarbonate and sodium                sulfate to precipitate, removing the precipitate                from the mixture, (H) removing the alcohol solvent                from the mixture, (I) adding a dialkyl ketone                solvent to the mixture, forcing the generated                sodium nitrate, sodium nitrite to precipitate,                removing the precipitate from the mixture, (J)                removing dialkyl ketone solvent from the mixture,                (K) optionally adding sodium hypochlorite, sodium                hydroxide and/or water to the mixture, (L) adding                sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxide, nitrogen dioxide                and carbon dioxide to the mixture in reaction                chambers (R2), and reacting mixture according to                process (B) to complete the loop sequence.                INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) chemical sequestration of carbon dioxide                by loop sequence; and
   (2) system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solvent is                  methanol or tert-butanol. The dialkyl ketone                  solvent is acetone. Preferred Property: The                  mixture in reaction chambers is in an aqueous                  solution, an aqueous mist, or a gas phase.

The breeding waste of the livestock farm is sent to the microalgae system to realize low-pollution treatment of agricultural cultivation waste water. TI Test device for carbon dioxide rheology, has rotational rheometer that communicates with phase change system and observes phase state of system, and data processing system that records test data collected by rotational rheometer.
AU LIU C
   LI F
   ZHOU T
   LI X
   WANG H
   HE J
AE SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021C0253K
AB    NOVELTY - The test device has a phase change system that communicates with the gas source (1), and is used to receive the carbon dioxide supplied by the gas source and change the temperature and pressure of the carbon dioxide system. A rotational rheometer (12) communicates with the phase change system, tests the rheological properties of carbon dioxide, and observes the phase state of the system in real time. A data processing system (16) records the test data collected by the rotational rheometer. The rotational rheometer includes a visible window is arranged on the wall of the measuring cup. A rotor is arranged in the inner cavity of the measuring cup, and is driven by magnetic force.
   USE - Test device for carbon dioxide rheology used in field of unconventional shale oil and gas production enhancement and transformation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has simple structure, convenient operation and high accuracy of measurement data, which provides a theoretical basis for further research on rheological laws and carbon dioxide fracturing design and construction. The test device can evaluate the rheological properties of carbon dioxide. The rotor in the magnetic drive mode can be completely enclosed in the measuring cup, and the need to open a connection port on the measuring cup is avoided, so that the measuring cup can be better sealed to avoid heat exchange between the liquid carbon dioxide system and the outside world, thus ensuring the accuracy of the test. The rotation speed of the rotor can be changed by changing the size of the magnetic field, so that the test parameters can be easily set.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a test device for carbon dioxide rheology.
   Gas source (1)
   Refrigerator (3)
   Intermediate container (7)
   Rotational rheometer (12)
   Data processing system (16)

TI Method for extracting carbon dioxide from            atmospheric air through pressure swing absorption,            involves transferring mixture from turbine into            low-pressure vessel, and releasing carbon dioxide from            low-pressure vessel for collection.
AU LEE W A
AE AEOLUS WORKS LLC (AEOL-Non-standard)
   AEOLUS WORKS LLC (AEOL-Non-standard)
GA 202142685M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing ambient air                including carbon dioxide and a set of secondary                gases with a working fluid from a low-pressure                vessel (130) to generate a first mixture including                a volume of air dispersed throughout the working                fluid. The first mixture is conveyed through a                compressor configured to pressurize the first                mixture from a first pressure within a first                pressure range at an inlet of the compressor to a                second pressure within a second pressure range at                an outlet of the compressor. The second mixture is                conveyed from the high-pressure vessel through a                turbine configured to extract energy from the                second mixture and reduce pressure of the second                mixture to promote desorption of carbon dioxide                from the working fluid. The second mixture from the                turbine is transferred into the low-pressure                vessel. The carbon dioxide desorbed from the                working fluid is released from the low-pressure                vessel for collection.
   USE - Method for extracting carbon dioxide from                atmospheric air through pressure swing                absorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The system minimizes opportunities for                breakage of different components of the system,                minimizes a number of components required for                assembly of the system and increases compactness                and space efficiency of the system. The power                required to maintain operation of the system is                decreased and the operating costs is                decreased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT claim is included for a system                for extracting carbon dioxide from atmospheric air                through pressure swing absorption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                system for extracting carbon dioxide from                atmospheric air through pressure swing                absorption.
