PN CN112375246-A; CN112375246-B
TI Modifying surface of polymer by discharging carbon dioxide plasma at atmospheric pressure comprises e.g. using plasma generator based on atmospheric pressure discharge, generating plasma in carbon dioxide gas flow and modifying surface.
AU ZHANG J
   JIA Q
   ZHANG B
   GAO K
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 202120460P
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying surface of polymer by discharging carbon dioxide plasma at atmospheric pressure comprises using plasma generator based on atmospheric pressure discharge, generating plasma in carbon dioxide gas flow and modifying the surface of polymer to obtain polymer with low friction coefficient surface.
   USE - The method is useful for modifying surface of polymer by discharging carbon dioxide plasma at atmospheric pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: bombards the polymer surface by the produced carbon dioxide high-energy particles; completes the breaking of the surface bond and the synthesis reaction by only few atomic layers; has excellent adhesion while maintaining the properties of the polymer itself; reacts with the polymer; reduces its friction coefficient; obtains surface-modified polymer with good tribological properties; and contributes to sustainable development path that meets the needs of contemporary people without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their needs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) placing the polymer in deionized water and cleaning it for 5-10 minutes, and repeating 3-5 times to remove the contaminants on the polymer surface, (2) placing the above-mentioned cleaned polymer into plasma generator based on atmospheric pressure discharge, opening the direct current power supply, and adjusting the current 60-100 mA, working voltage 2500 V, and average power 100 W, and (3) introducing in the working gas carbon dioxide, controlling the gas flow at 1-5 l/minute, and plasma density generated is 1011/cm-3, performing plasma treatment on the surface of the polymer takes 20-40 minutes, and obtaining the plasma modified polymer. Preferred Components: The polymer is fluorosilicone rubber or silicone rubber.

PN CN111847999-A; CN111847999-B
TI Reinforced mortar comprises cement mortar, carbon            dioxide releasing resin balls, and calcium carbonate            powder, where carbon dioxide releasing resin balls and            calcium carbonate powder are respectively mixed in            cement mortar.
AU FANG J
   PAN W
   HE Y
   ZHU Z
AE SHENZHEN QIXIN GROUP CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   JIANGXI QIXIN GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A9602R
AB    NOVELTY - Reinforced mortar comprises cement mortar,                carbon dioxide releasing resin balls, and calcium                carbonate powder. The carbon dioxide releasing                resin balls and calcium carbonate powder are                respectively mixed in the cement mortar according                to a predetermined weight ratio.
   USE - Reinforced mortar.
   ADVANTAGE - The reinforced mortar is able to enhance the                mechanical strength of the mortar and improving the                crack resistance of the mortar, effectively                accelerate the hydration of cement, increase the                strength of the mortar. The preparation method of                the reinforced mortar is simple, quick and easy to                use, and can be widely processed, prepared and used                in various construction environments and processing                plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing reinforced mortar, which                involves:
   preparing carbon dioxide releasing resin                balls; and
   preparing the cement mortar by mixing the                carbon dioxide releasing resin ball and calcium                carbonate powder into the cement mortar, stirring,                after a predetermined time to obtain the reinforced                mortar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  releasing resin ball comprises super absorbent                  resin particles and an aqueous solution                  containing carbon dioxide absorbed in the super                  absorbent resin particles. Preferred                  Compositions: The addition amount of the carbon                  dioxide releasing resin ball is 6-8 wt.% cement                  mortar. The super absorbent resin particles are                  cross-linked acrylic acid/sodium acrylate                  copolymer, and the absorption capacity of the                  water absorbent resin particles to the aqueous                  solution containing carbon dioxide is 30-200                  times the weight of the particles themselves. The                  added amount of the calcium carbonate powder is                  4-10 wt.% cement mortar. The bicarbonate is                  calcium bicarbonate, and the weight ratio of                  calcium bicarbonate, acetic acid, and water in                  the mixed aqueous solution is (10-30):15:50.                  Preferred Process: The bicarbonate, acetic acid,                  and water are uniformly mixed according to a                  predetermined weight ratio to form a mixed                  aqueous solution. The super absorbent resin                  particles are put into the mixed aqueous                  solution, stirred and soaked, and the soaking is                  stopped, after the water absorbent resin                  particles expand to a predetermined particle                  size.

PT P
PN CN111763137-A
TI Preparing ethanol from carbon dioxide and hydrogen comprises reacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen to liberate ethanol, methanol and water in presence of multifunctional catalyst.
AU GAO X
   ZHANG J
   MA Q
   FAN S
   LU P
   ZHAO T
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2020A19021
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethanol from carbon dioxide and hydrogen comprises reacting 3 moles carbon dioxide and 9 moles hydrogen to liberate ethanol, methanol and 4 moles water in presence of multifunctional catalyst which consists of five parts, where first part is palladium-based catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation and carbon monooxide; second part is the copper-based catalyst for carbon monooxide hydrogenation to methanol; third part is gamma -aluminum oxide catalyst for methanol dehydration to dimethyl ether; fourth part is H-MOR or H-FER catalyst for carbonylation of dimethyl ether to methyl acetate; fifth part is copper-based catalyst for the hydrogenation of methyl acetate to ethanol; and above five parts of catalysts are packed in five layers from top to bottom in catalytic reactor, and packing mass ratio between five kinds of catalysts is 0.5-1:0.5-1:0.5-1:0.5-1:0.5-1.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ethanol from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The palladium-based catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to carbon monooxide is palladium catalyst supported on aluminum oxide, catalyst uses aluminum oxide as carrier, palladium as active component, and palladium loading is 0.1-5 wt.%; and catalyst uses commercial gamma -aluminum oxide as carrier, and prepared by dipping method using palladium tetrammine dinitrate as precursor. The steps of impregnation method comprises (i) dissolving quantitative amount of palladium tetrammine dinitrate in deionized water to form precursor solution having total concentration of 0.1-2 M, (ii) calculating based on palladium loading amount of 0.1-5 wt.%, dropping the above-mentioned precursor solution onto the quantitative gamma -aluminum oxide carrier, then drying at 60-120 degrees C for 6-24 hours, calcining at 250-550 degrees C for 1-6 hours to obtain palladium/ gamma -aluminum oxide catalyst. The copper-based catalyst for carbon monooxide hydrogenation to methanol is copper-zinc-aluminum oxide catalyst for carbon monooxide hydrogenation to methanol, and mass content of each component of catalyst: copper oxide, zinc oxide and aluminum oxide is 20-75%:15-50%:50%, and catalyst is prepared by the co-precipitation method. The preparation method of copper-based catalyst for carbon monooxide hydrogenation to methanol includes adding mixed solution of copper nitrate, zinc nitrate and aluminum nitrate into urea aqueous solution, stirring constantly, then heating mixture at 90 degrees C and maintaining it at this temperature for 2 hours until the mixture settles completely, aging resulting slurry at room temperature overnight, filtering and washing with deionized water, drying resulting filter cake at 120 degrees C and calcining at 350 degrees C for 2 hours. The gamma -aluminum oxide catalyst for production of dimethyl ether by methanol dehydration is a commercial gamma -aluminum oxide catalyst having catalytic surface area of 200-500 m2/g. The dimethyl ether carbonylation to methyl acetate catalyst is commercial H-MOR or H-FER molecular sieve having eight-membered ring structure, and the ratio of silicon to aluminum silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide is between 10-50. The copper-based catalyst for hydrogenation of methyl acetate to ethanol is copper-zinc-aluminum oxide catalyst, and preparation method is the same as the second part of the catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon monoxide to methanol. The catalytic reaction conditions are: gas composition: hydrogen/carbon dioxide is 2-8 volume ratio, space velocity is 500-5000, reaction temperature is 180-300 degrees C, and reaction pressure 0.5-5 MPa. The catalytic reaction conditions are: preferably hydrogen/carbon dioxide is 3-6 volume ratio, space velocity of 500-3000, reaction temperature is 200-280 degrees C, reaction pressure 1.5-3 MPa, carbon dioxide conversion rate is greater than 12%, ethanol selectivity greater than 30%, and methanol selectivity is greater than 35%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

Gas permeable film is triple-layer hollow fiber membrane in which innermost layer has porous support, middle layer has separation membrane, and outermost layer has triple-layer structure of porous support. Porous support has polyolefin-type polymer as main component. Preferred Process: Melt-molding is melt-extrusion. Stretching process is performed after stress relaxation process. TI Method for preventing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of oleoresin of Chinese prickly ash involves distilling Chinese prickly ash oleoresin obtained by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to remove water, and performing tubular centrifuging.
AU YE D
   LI B
   DENG J
   GUO C
   MA Q
AE SICHUAN TIANWEI FOOD GROUP CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202234691X
AB    NOVELTY - A method for preventing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of oleoresin of Chinese prickly ash comprises distilling Chinese prickly ash oleoresin obtained by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to remove water, performing tubular centrifuging, collecting outlet material liquid, obtaining high-purity oleoresin, putting into sealed tank body, pumping air, forming negative pressure, instantly recovering the normal pressure, and repeating operation for 1-2 times to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preventing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of oleoresin of Chinese prickly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively solve the problem of short storage time of the prickly ash oleoresin treated by the prior method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preventing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of oleoresin of Chinese prickly ash comprises taking Chinese prickly ash, crushing, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction for 2-4 hours at 35-45degrees Celsius under 25-35 MPa at carbon dioxide flow rate of 12-14 mL/minute to obtain Chinese prickly ash oleoresin, performing molecular distillation dehydration at 68-72degrees Celsius, obtaining Chinese prickly ash oleoresin with a temperature of 58-62degrees Celsius after removing the water, performing tubular centrifuging at centrifugal rotation speed of 14000-18000 revolutions/minute, putting into sealed tank body at 40-55degrees Celsius, pumping air, forming negative pressure until reaching -0.05 MPa, instantly recovering the normal pressure, and repeating operation for 2 times to obtain product.

PN CN113332981-A
TI Carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material useful in dehydrogenation materials to photocatalytic system comprises binary complex material composed of cuprous oxide and titanium dioxide.
AU XING M
   ZHU Q
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2021A45049
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material comprises binary complex material composed of cuprous oxide and titanium dioxide, and the binary complex material is composed of cuprous oxide supported on titanium dioxide.
   USE - The carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material is useful in dehydrogenation materials to the photocatalytic system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic material is economical; and has high photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction performance and good selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material comprising (i) adding titanium dioxide to water and dispersing, (ii) adding copper chloride metal salt precursor and sodium borohydride in sequence, and preparing cuprous oxide/titanium dioxide complex material, and (iii) centrifuging the product, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The loading amount of the cuprous oxide on the titanium dioxide is 1-4%. In the step (i), the titanium dioxide is a commercial titanium dioxide, and the mass ratio of the commercial titanium dioxide to water is 0.01-0.3:30. In the step (ii), the copper chloride metal salt precursor is added first, then sodium borohydride is added, and the mixture is stirred for 30 minutes. The sodium borohydride is an aqueous sodium borohydride solution, the concentration of sodium borohydride in the sodium borohydride aqueous solution is 1-2 M, and the excess is maintained during the reaction. The copper chloride metal salt precursor is copper chloride dihydrate. In the step (iii), the product of (ii) is washed and dried in vacuum. The carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material is added to the photocatalytic system.

PN CN112877166-A
TI Quickly clarifying blueberry wine based on high            density carbon dioxide treatment, comprises using high            density carbon dioxide to process blueberry wine.
AU FENG P
   XUN H
   DONG H
AE UNIV LUDONG (UNLD-C)
GA 2021643722
AB    NOVELTY - Quickly clarifying blueberry wine based on                high density carbon dioxide treatment, comprises                using high density carbon dioxide to process                blueberry wine.
   USE - The method is useful for quickly clarifying                blueberry wine based on high density carbon dioxide                treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problems of long                clarification period and poor stability that were                common in the production process of blueberry wine;                quickly settles the unstable compounds e.g. protein                and colloid in the wine body; combines rapid cold                treatment, so that the blueberry wine can be                quickly clarified; improves the stability of the                product; and does not have a negative impact on the                product index.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing blueberry wine.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-E

ER

PT P
PN US2019211463-A1; US10975480-B2
TI Electrochemical device for converting carbon            dioxide to reaction product, comprises electrolyzer            stack comprising electrochemical cells having anode            electrocatalyst, cathode electrocatalyst, source of            carbon dioxide and membrane.
AU MASEL R I
AE DIOXIDE MATERIALS INC (DIOX-Non-standard)
GA 201960294F
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical device for converting carbon                dioxide to a reaction product, comprises an                electrolyzer stack, where the stack comprises at                least two electrochemical cells, each cell                comprises (a) an anode comprising a quantity of                anode electrocatalyst, (b) a cathode comprising a                quantity of cathode electrocatalyst, (c) a source                of carbon dioxide directed to the cathode, (d) a                membrane interposed between the anode and the                cathode, the (i) cathode electrocatalyst is in the                form of unsupported particles or particles                supported on an electrically conductive support,                (ii) particles have an average size of 0.6-100 nm                and (iii) cathode electrocatalyst converts carbon                dioxide to a carbon-containing reaction                product.
   USE - Used as electrochemical device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrocatalyst                  comprises at least one of vanadium, chromium,                  manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, tin,                  zirconium, niobium, molybdenum, ruthenium,                  rhodium, palladium, silver, cadmium, hafnium,                  tantalum, tungsten, rhenium, iridium, platinum,                  gold, mercury, aluminum, silicon, indium,                  thallium, lead, bismuth, antimony, tellurium,                  uranium, samarium, terbium, lanthanum, cerium or                  neodymium. The catalytically active element                  comprises platinum, palladium, gold, silver,                  copper, nickel, iron, cobalt, ruthenium or                  rhodium. The reaction product comprises at least                  one of carbon monoxide, aldehyde ion,                  formaldehyde, formate ion, formic acid, methanol,                  methane, ethene, ethanol, acetate ion, acetic                  acid, ethane, oxalic acid, (carboxylate ion)2 or                  trifluoroacetic acid. The reaction product is one                  of formic acid and carbon monoxide. The membrane                  comprises positively charged amines. The                  positively charged amines are imidazoliums or                  pyridiniums.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The membrane has an                  average thickness and the shortest ion conduction                  path between anodes of different cells in the                  stack is at least 10 times the average membrane                  thickness. The shortest ion conduction path                  between anodes of different cells in the stack is                  at least 100 times the average membrane                  thickness. The shortest ion conduction path                  between anodes of different cells in the stack is                  at least 3 cm. The electrochemical device further                  comprises: a conduit fluidly connecting the anode                  of one cell in the stack to the anode of a                  different cell in the stack, and a source of                  liquid electrolyte directed to the conduit; an                  electrical connection between the anode of one                  cell in the stack and the cathode of a different                  cell in the stack; a source of potential between                  the cathode of the one cell in the stack and the                  anode of a different cell in the stack; and a                  conduit fluidly connecting the cathode of one                  cell in the stack to the cathode of a different                  cell in the stack, the source of carbon dioxide                  directed to the conduit. The carbon dioxide is in                  gaseous form. The membrane is a polymer                  electrolyte membrane. The membrane is an anion                  exchange membrane. The average particle size of                  the particles is 0.6-40 nm.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC A12-E09; A12-M01; A12-M02; A12-W11A; E10-C02D1; E10-C04F; E10-C04J1P; E10-C04J2P; E10-D01C; E10-E04E1; E10-E04E2; E10-J02B3; E10-J02C3; E10-J02D1; E11-A01; E11-D02; E11-E03; E11-F03; E11-F08; E11-G05; E11-N; E31-G; E31-N05B1; E31-P06A; J03-A; J03-B03; L03-J; N06-C02; N07-G; X16-C; X16-E06A5A

PN CN109576017-A; CN109576017-B
TI Preparation of low-sulfur diesel fuel lubricity improver under supercritical condition by taking unsaturated carboxylic acid ester, vegetable oil fatty acid and catalyst, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting and reusing carbon dioxide.
AU LI L
   CHENG J
   LI X
   WANG Y
   LUO Y
   WANG L
   QIAO K
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
GA 2019341722
AB    NOVELTY - A low-sulfur diesel fuel lubricity improver is prepared under supercritical condition by adding unsaturated carboxylic acid ester, vegetable oil fatty acid and catalyst into reactor, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor, making carbon dioxide in supercritical state, starting to stir, reacting, recovering carbon dioxide and reusing, filtering remaining liquid, removing biological catalyst, and vacuum distilling.
   USE - The method is used for low-sulfur diesel fuel lubricity improver preparation under supercritical condition.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has low product solidifying point and low blending ratio of low-sulfur diesel oil, and meets national V standard lubricity and solidifying requirement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biological catalyst is linoleic acid isomerase or linoleic acid isomerase producing bacteria. The producing bacteria is Lactobacillus plantarum, Lactobacillus acidophilus and/or Lactobacillus delbrueckii.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vegetable oil fatty acid is non-conjugated double-bond fatty acid of vegetable oil, preferably corn oil fatty acid, cottonseed oil fatty acid, linseed oil fatty acid, soybean oil fatty acid and/or peanut oil fatty acid. The vegetable oil fatty acid is vegetable oil fatty acid having iodine value of greater than 85 mg KOH/g. The unsaturated carboxylic acid ester is esterification product of maleic anhydride. The molar ratio of unsaturated carboxylic acid esters and vegetable oil fatty acid is 0.5:1-3:1. The unsaturated carboxylic acid ester is dimethyl maleate, diethyl maleate, dipropyl maleate and/or dibutyl maleate. The improver has acid value of 80-160 mg KOH/g, solidifying point of less than or equal to - 18 degrees C and 60 degrees C correction crack diameter of not greater than 460 mu m when the dosage is 80-250 ppm. The low-sulfur diesel fuel has sulfur content of less than 10 ppm and spot diameter of greater than 580 mu m. Preferred Method: The method involves adding unsaturated carboxylic acid ester, vegetable oil fatty acid and catalyst into reactor, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor, making carbon dioxide in supercritical state, starting to stir, reacting at 30-60 degrees C for 5-10 hours at 5-10 MPa, recovering carbon dioxide and reusing, filtering remaining liquid, removing biological catalyst, and vacuum distilling at 180-220 degrees C at 30-150 Pa, preferably 65-120 Pa.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN KR2018006771-A; KR1930740-B1
TI Preparing carbon dioxide absorbent involves reacting mixture with an aminosilane compound and epoxide in solvent, reacting product with porous support and solvent without stirring, and drying product in oven to obtain final product.
AU KO Y S
   JEON J W
   PYO S W
   CHOI M K
AE UNIV KONGJU NAT IND ACAD COOP GRO (UYKN-C)
GA 201809178L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide absorbent involves reacting mixture with an aminosilane compound and epoxide in solvent, reacting the product with a porous support and a solvent without stirring, and drying the product in an oven to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon dioxide absorbent in cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide adhesive, which comprises porous support, and aminosilane compound immobilized in the pores of the porous support, where aminosilane compound is reacted with the epoxide to convert the primary amine of the aminosilane compound to a secondary amine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method for preparing carbon dioxide absorbent further involves polymerizing the aminosilane compound hrough the in-situ method of polymerization to bound to the porous support and epoxide reaction product in the form of polyaminosilane. The epoxide convertes primary amine in the aminosilane compound to a secondary amine. The method for preparing carbon dioxide absorbent involves immersing the aminosilane compound in an amount of 70% or more of the pore volume of the porous support in the epoxide reaction product or the reaction product polymer. The ratio of solvent, aminosilane compound, and epoxide is 1:1:1. The reaction is carried out at 30-70 degrees C for 2-5 hours. Preferred Composition: The aminosilane compound comprises one amine which is selected from primary amine and a secondary amine. Preferred Components: The aminosilane compound is N-(3-(trimethoxysilane)propyl ethylenediamine, and (N-(3- (trimethoxysilyl) propyl) ethylenediamine, 2NS). The he epoxide is selected from propylene oxide (1,2-epoxypropane), 1,2-epoxybutane, and 1,2-epoxypentane. The porous support is amorphous silica. The epoxide is selected from propylene oxide (1,2-epoxypropane), 1,2-epoxybutane, and 1,2-epoxypentane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2022130212-A1
TI Generating anhydrous hydrogen fluoride, involves thermally decomposing fluorinated material into gaseous effluent comprising hydrogen fluoride and carbon dioxide, contacting gaseous effluent with carbon to convert carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and condensing hydrogen fluoride.
AU HIRSCHBERG M E
   HINTZER K
   SCHOETTLE T W
   THALER A
   SCHMIDT-RODENKIRCHEN A
   GERDES T
   MIERDEL K
AE 3M INNOVATIVE PROPERTIES CO (MINN-C)
GA 202281631N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for generating anhydrous hydrogen fluoride, involves (i) thermally decomposing a fluorinated material into a gaseous effluent comprising hydrogen fluoride and carbon dioxide, (ii) contacting the gaseous effluent with carbon at a temperature (TQ) of at least 830 degrees C to convert the carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and producing a gaseous product comprising the hydrogen fluoride and carbon monoxide, where if the gaseous product further comprising carbon dioxide, a molar ratio of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide is not greater than 0.05 and (iii) condensing the hydrogen fluoride from the gaseous product to form liquid anhydrous hydrogen fluoride.
   USE - The method is used for generating anhydrous hydrogen fluoride as a fuel to provide energy to one or both of thermal decomposition reactor and carbon treatment reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method delivers high yields of anhydrous hydrogen fluoride, is cost-effective, removes or reduces concentration of such gases to avoid contamination of desired end products, recycles in-process fluorinated waste materials and post-use fluorinated products, reduces amount of carbon dioxide, ensures very high to complete conversion of water into hydrogen and carbon monoxide, and provides without excessive consumption of carbon or risk of introducing contaminants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The temperature is at least 1000 degrees C. The fluorinated material is thermally decomposed at a temperature of at least 500 degrees C. The temperature of the gaseous effluent contacting the carbon (TQ) is at least 300 degrees C. The TQ is at least 800 degrees C, preferably at least 500 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide is not greater than 0.01. The liquid anhydrous hydrogen fluoride has a water content of less than 1000 ppm by weight. Preferred Components: The carbon comprises activated carbon or graphite. Preferred Method: The method further comprises using microwave radiation to heat the carbon to the temperature. The gaseous effluent further comprises water vapor, and contacting the gaseous effluent with carbon at a temperature, converts at least 99 wt.% of the water to hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The method further comprises adding water to the gaseous effluent before or while contacting the gaseous effluent with carbon at a temperature. The method further comprises collecting the hydrogen and carbon monoxide from the gaseous product as syngas. The method further comprises separating the hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The thermally decomposing the fluorinated material comprises subjecting the fluorinated material to microwave irradiation in the presence of microwave active particles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fluorinated material comprises fluorinated polymers. The fluorinated polymer is a perfluorinated polymer.
   EXAMPLE - The example section were described the general preparation of anhydrous hydrogen fluoride so it does not support the claims.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN CN113822420-A
TI Method for establishing model of dead cavity fraction based on volume carbon dioxide graph, involves establishing hidden layer state transmission of adjacent carbon dioxide graph, and establishing loss function equation to determine MBRRNN model.
AU CHEN H
   CHENG H
   XIE L
   YAN P
AE YAN P (YANP-Individual)
GA 2022063206
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing an LSTM model. An LSLM network is stacked in positive and negative directions to construct a BRNN model. A MBRNN model is constructed by a multi-layer superposition BRNN network. A relationship between each dimension in a sequence point is established based on the MBRNN network. Hidden layer state transmission of adjacent carbon dioxide graph is established based on the MBRNN network. Loss function equation is established to finally determine a MBRRNN model. Carbon dioxide partial pressure curve is projected to an area of a transverse shaft.
   USE - Method for establishing model of dead cavity fraction based on volume carbon dioxide graph.

PN CN108246790-A
TI Method of repairing phenolic organic material contaminated soil involves introducing carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide carrier into reactor comprising soil, adding oxidant and catalyst into reactor and performing gasification reaction.
AU TIAN S
   ZHANG Y
   LI Y
   HUANG J
   HU X
   NING P
   GU J
   GUAN Q
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2018546069
AB    NOVELTY - Method of repairing phenolic organic material contaminated soil involves placing phenol organic material contaminated soil in a high-temperature high-pressure reactor (A), introducing carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide carrier into high-temperature high-pressure reactor (A), heating and pressurizing carbon dioxide carrier to supercritical state of carbon dioxide at 32-85 degrees C at 7.5-25 MPa, contact extracting for 10-90 minutes to obtain remediation soil and extraction gas, adding water, oxidizing agent and catalyst into high temperature high pressure reactor (B), sealing the high-temperature high-pressure reactor (B), heating and pressurizing the water in the high-temperature high-pressure reactor (B) to supercritical state of the water at 375-600 degrees C at 22.1-35 MPa, passing obtained extraction gas into high-temperature high-pressure reactor (B) for oxidative gasification reaction for 1-60 minutes to obtain harmless gas.
   USE - Method of repairing phenolic organic material contaminated soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and carried out at low temperature. The carbon dioxide used in the method can be recycled and reused. The phenolic organic material used in the process is detoxified and resourced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The oxidizing agent is hydrogen peroxide, sodium peroxide, sodium percarbonate, potassium peroxide, potassium peroxycarbonate or an oxidizing gas. The oxidizing gas is oxygen and/or ozone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is an alkali metal hydroxide, alkali metal carbonate, alkali metal bicarbonate, transition metals and precious metals alloy, transition metal alloy, and/or precious metals alloy. The alkali metal hydroxide is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. The alkali metal carbonate is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate or potassium bicarbonate. The transition metal is zinc, cobalt, nickel or copper. The precious metal is palladium, platinum or ruthenium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The phenolic organic substance is phenol, p-cresol, m-cresol, hydroquinone, catechol, resorcin, and/or pyrogallol. The carbon dioxide carrier is methanol and/or ethanol. Preferred Composition: The liquid-solid ratio of carbon dioxide carrier to phenolic organic material contaminated soil is (0-0.1):1. The molar ratio of oxidizing agent to phenolic organic material is (1-16):1. The mass ratio of catalyst to phenolic organic material is (0.1-1):1.

TI Graphene-based carbon dioxide capture agent comprises silicon carbide, copper oxide, carbon dioxide, iron hydroxide, manganese, zinc, silver, Wu, ammonia, and processed carbon dioxide.
AU XIAO Z
   WANG M
AE NANJING CHEM IND PARK ENVIRONMENTAL PROT (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020388790
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene-based carbon dioxide capture agent comprises 20-23.5 wt.% silicon carbide (SiC), 0.1-0.3 wt.% copper oxide (CuO), 20-30 wt.% carbon dioxide (CO2), 20-34 wt.% iron hydroxide (Fe(OH)2), 1.03-0.14 wt.% manganese (Mn), 0.03-0.04 wt.% zinc (Zn), 0.003-0.004 wt.% silver (Ag), 100-150 ppm Wu, ammonia (NH3) and processed CO2.
   USE - A graphene-based carbon dioxide capture agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene-based carbon dioxide capture agent can satisfy greenhouse gas requirement. It can be used multiple times without by-products, which can well meet the needs of domestic and foreign markets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing a graphene-based carbon dioxide capture agent which involves: (a) charging newly-configured 200m1 ammonia solution into a reaction volume of 600 ml, placing the reactor in a constant temperature water bath and heating at 40 degrees C, dissolving silver ion salt in an organic solvent of ethanol to obtain an ammonia ion salt solution, adding distilled water to the reaction to obtain carbon dioxide that can react with carbon dioxide solution, taking the clear liquid, and finally preparing a homogeneous and stable absorption solution; (b) dissolving 2.0 mol graphene powder in 2.0 L tetrahydrofuran to prepare a solution at 0.5 mol/L, and mixing the graphene powder and tetrahydrofuran solution and dropping into a 50 L glass reactor containing anhydrous ether, removing the organic carrier solution, and adding 200 ml to the beaker, maintaining slow stirring, and measuring 2 g sodium bisulfite and 0.3 g methyl group were measured in sequence, adding silicone oil to the carrier solution, stirring the reaction at 25 degrees C for 12 hours, and keep stirring, when the amine solution absorbs to half saturation, stopping the absorption reaction and obtaining a precipitate to prepare the capture agent; and (c) placing treated carbon dioxide capture agent in a reactor equipped with the treated solution and placing in a thermostatic water bath for heating at 80 degrees C, and maintaining liquid phase stirring speed at 1400 revolutions per minute (r/min); introducing 0.2 L/min CO2 and 0.8 L/min nitrogen gas; when the exhaust gas concentration is less than or equal to 2 kPa of the simulated flue gas concentration (20%), stopping the desorption process to obtain refined solid products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The control time of the deposition process of the carbon dioxide gas is 1-50 minutes, preferably 5-10 minutes. The external temperature is maintained at 10-100 degrees C, preferably 25 degrees C.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pumping device is disposed in the return connection. The pumping device is arranged in the gas space. The pressure difference between gas space and cathode space of 10-100 hPa is maintained. The outlet in the gas space is arranged on the bottom side. The outlet is connected to an overflow container. The overflow container is connected via a throttle to the electrolyte circuit, where the throttle is configured to provide a definable pressure difference between the gas space and the cathode space at a flow of a mixture of product gases and to effect liquid electrolyte. The throttle comprises a pipe arranged at an angle of 0-80 degrees to the vertical. The tube is rotatably arranged. The gas space comprises turbulence promoters. The turbulence promoters are designed such that an air gap of at least 0.1 mm remains between them and the surface of the cathode. The pressure difference between gas space and cathode space is maintained by throttle between the outlet and an electrolyte circuit in the interval of 10-100 hPa.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide                induced cracking device convenient for coal layer                to increase penetration and preventing fire,                relating to the technical field of carbon dioxide                induced cracking device. comprising a cracking                device main body and a reinforcing unit set at the                lower end of the cracking device main body, the                reinforcing unit comprises a mounting ring and a                fixing buckle foot set in the mounting ring, the                inner part of the mounting ring is provided with a                mounting piece fixedly mounted, the inner part of                the mounting piece is provided with an elastic                piece, the inner part of the elastic piece is                provided with a shot bead, The utility model solves                the problem that the penetration of carbon dioxide                when using carbon dioxide cracking device is not                good, the penetrating power of carbon dioxide in                the coal layer is not enough, the technical problem                of not enough air permeability in the coal bed is                not enough, the carbon dioxide is exploded from the                air outlet hole, at the same time, the elastic ball                in the air outlet hole is extruded and popped out,                the impact force ejeces the ejection ball, the                impact force of the carbon dioxide gas is enhanced,                the penetration force in the coal layer is                enhanced, and the air permeability in the coal                layer is enhanced.

AB    NOVELTY - Fault detection method of electric device involves collecting the fluorinated nitrile/carbon dioxide mixed gas in the electric device, injecting the fluorinated nitrile/carbon dioxide mixed gas into the gas chromatograph to detect, obtaining the component of the fluorinated nitrile/carbon dioxide mixed gas, when detecting that the component does not contain the decomposition product, judging that there is no fault in the electric device, when detecting that the component comprises octafluorocyclobutane or i-octafluorocyclobutane, judging that the internal portion of the electric device is locally overheating fault, when detecting that the component does not comprise octafluorocyclobutane or i-octafluorocyclobutane.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fault detection method of electric device involves collecting the fluorinated nitrile/carbon dioxide mixed gas in the electric device, injecting the fluorinated nitrile/carbon dioxide mixed gas into the gas chromatograph to detect, obtaining the component of the fluorinated nitrile/carbon dioxide mixed gas, when detecting that the component does not contain the decomposition product, judging that there is no fault in the electric device, when detecting that the component comprises octafluorocyclobutane or i-octafluorocyclobutane, judging that the internal portion of the electric device is locally overheating fault, when detecting that the component does not comprise octafluorocyclobutane or i-octafluorocyclobutane, detecting whether the component comprises hexafluoro-1,3-butadiene and perfluoroacrylonitrile, obtaining the detection result, judging the discharge fault in the electric device, where the discharge failure is a power frequency breakdown failure, partial discharge failure or spark discharge failure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves judging the discharge fault in the electric device according to the detection result includes when the detection result is yes, judging the electric device internal power frequency breakdown fault, when the detection result is negative, extracting the ion peak area of octoctafluoropropane and trifluoromethylisocyanide by quantitative analysis, and according to the ion peak area, judging whether the discharge fault occurring in the electric device is partial discharge fault or spark discharge fault. The preset threshold value is 1.

PT P
PN CN111138955-A; CN111138955-B
TI Preparing graphene aqueous epoxy composite coating useful as corrosion-resistant coating for high-chlorine saturated carbon dioxide environment comprises adding dispersant and anti-settling agent, adding graphene and curing.
AU HU H
   XU H
   LI Y
   SHEN Z
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202043462R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene aqueous epoxy composite coating suitable for high-chlorine saturated carbon dioxide environment comprises (a) adding dispersant and anti-settling agent to aqueous -based epoxy emulsion, mixing them evenly, preparing compound emulsion, centrifuging the compound emulsion, taking clear liquid, (b) adding a few layers of graphene and surfactant to the clear solution, using ultrasound to disperse evenly to obtain a premixed solution, (c) adding the aqueous epoxy curing agent to the premix, mixing evenly to obtain a composite slurry and (d) evenly coating the composite slurry on the surface of the substrate and curing to obtain a graphene aqueous epoxy composite coating suitable for a high-chlorine saturated carbon dioxide environment.
   USE - The coating is useful as a corrosion-resistant coating suitable for high-chlorine saturated carbon dioxide environment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The coating: has a protection efficiency on the substrate reaches 99.98% in a high chloride saturated carbon dioxide aqueous solution with 10% sodium chloride, when the amount of added few layers of graphene is 0.5% of the total mass of the composite slurry; improved corrosion resistance, waterproof performance and stability in high chlorine saturated carbon dioxide environment; is suitable for high-chlorine saturated carbon dioxide environment, which can be applied to high-chlorine saturated carbon dioxide aqueous solution environment as a pipeline protective layer in the production environment of oil and gas fields and provides reliable long-term corrosion protection for the base metal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous epoxy emulsion is an E44 waterborne epoxy emulsion. The dispersant is sodium carboxymethyl cellulose. The anti-settling agent is fumed silica. The surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfonate. The added amount of the dispersant is 0.05-0.2% and the anti-settling agent is 0.1-0.3%. The amount of surfactant used is 5-10%. The addition amount of the low-layer graphene is 0.25-1% of the total mass of the composite slurry and the thickness of the low-layer graphene is 1-3 nm, and the number of layers is 1-2 layers. The mass ratio of aqueous epoxy curing agent to aqueous epoxy emulsion is 1:(1.5-3). The substrate used is 20 steel metal substrate. The surface of the graphene aqueous epoxy composite coating is dense and smooth, the color is transparent gray-black and the thickness is uniform. Preferred Method: The curing is performed in a vacuum environment at 60-90 degrees C for 1.5-4 hours. The method further comprises cleaning and pretreatment of the 20 steel metal substrate before coating including cleaning is to use deionized water and absolute ethanol to clean the oil stains in sequence and the pretreatment is to use 180-1500 sandpaper in order, and then polish to the mirror surface.

PN CN110553559-A; CN110553559-B
TI Method for controlling properties of explosion reactor by using phase change of liquid carbon dioxide in mines, involves designing carbon dioxide blasting experiment in waste rock area.
AU SHI X
   YU Z
   GAO C
   HU D
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG W
   CHEN X
   RAO D
   HUO X
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
   CHINA ALUMINUM MINING CO LTD (CNAL-C)
GA 2019A5744J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves designing a carbon dioxide blasting experiment in the waste rock area. The blasting parameter is taken as the independent variable and the blasting attribute parameter as the dependent variable. The regression equation of the blasting attribute parameters and blasting parameters is established in the waste rock area. The regression equations are established for blast area attribute parameters and blast parameters in the ore area. The selection of blasting parameters is guided in the ore area and waste rock area. The explosive blastholes (5) are arranged in the ore area of the ore and rock boundary line for explosive blasting, and liquid carbon dioxide blasters are arranged in the waste rock area for carbon dioxide blasting. The blasting energy of liquid carbon dioxide in the waste rock area is lower than that in the ore area, and the waste rock area detonates before the ore area.
   USE - Method for controlling properties of explosion reactor by using phase change of liquid carbon dioxide in mines.
   ADVANTAGE - The degree of ore mixing in the blast pile is effectively reduced. The poverty and damage for mines with complicated ore body storage conditions, high grade control requirements, and complex surrounding environment are reduced, and control measures are provided for open-pit mines with special explosive heap property requirements.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a top view of a medium and large ore mixed explosion zone.
   Free space (1)
   Hole (5)
   Liquid carbon dioxide blaster connection line (6)
   Deferred detonator between rows (10)
   Detonator detonation (11)

PN CN108557821-A; CN208732627-U
TI Carbon dioxide generating device with low oxygen protection, used in modern agriculture field, has controller that controls execution module to stop generating gas when oxygen concentration value reaches preset threshold value.
AU XIAO Y
   ZHENG Y
AE BABALA XIAMEN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BABA-Non-standard)
GA 201877035L
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a machine housing portion, and a control module, a data collecting module and an execution module which are set in the inner portion of housing portion. The data collecting module comprises an oxygen concentration sensor to detect the oxygen concentration of a surrounding environment and output an oxygen concentration value. The execution module is configured to generate the carbon dioxide gas. The control module comprises a controller which is electrically connected with the output end of the data collecting module to read the oxygen concentration value. The controller is electrically connected with the executing module to control the execution module to generate carbon dioxide gas or to stop generating carbon dioxide gas when the oxygen concentration value reaches a preset low oxygen alarm threshold value. The power supply module is configured to supply power to the control module, data collecting module and execution module.
   USE - Carbon dioxide generating device with low oxygen protection, used in modern agriculture field such as greenhouse cultivation.
   ADVANTAGE - The real-time detection of the oxygen concentration is increased and the automatic closing of the carbon dioxide generating device is realized under low oxygen state. Thereby the safety of the carbon dioxide generating device is greatly improved in the confined space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of the carbon dioxide generating device.
   Machine housing portion (1)
   Control module (3)
   Data collecting module (4)

AU CHEN J
AE CHEN J (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202156896F

PN CN107602475-A; CN107602475-B
TI New 1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium taurine ionic            liquid useful in adsorbing carbon dioxide and sulfur            dioxide.
AU TONG J
   ZHENG X
   LI H
   JIANG L
   JING L
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
   UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 2018086101
AB    NOVELTY - 1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium taurine ionic                liquid (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in adsorbing carbon dioxide and                sulfur dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium taurine ionic                liquid of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS                are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use of (I) in adsorbing carbon dioxide and                sulfur dioxide comprising passing carbon dioxide                and/or sulfur dioxide gas through                1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium taurinate ionic                liquid, and magnetically stirring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) taking chloro-n-propane and                  N-methylimidazole, heating to reflux at 70                  degrees C for 70-75 hours, sealing, freezing at                  -10 to 0 degrees C, crystallizing liquid as white                  solid, removing upper liquid and resulting white                  solid is 1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium chloride                  crude product, (ii) purifying crude                  1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium chloride, adding                  mixed solution of ethyl acetate and acetonitrile,                  heating to reflux at 70 degrees C to dissolve,                  sealing, freezing solution to -10 to 0 degrees C                  and crystallizing again to obtain white solid,                  removing upper liquid, repeating operation until                  upper liquid is colorless, crushing resulting                  white solid, filtering, and vacuum drying to                  obtain purified 1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium                  chloride, (iii) taking purified                  1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium chloride, adding                  deionized water to dissolve, dissolving                  chlorinated 1-methyl-3-propyl imidazole, then                  adding into OH-type anion exchange resin,                  adjusting pH to 12-14, and detecting chloride ion                  to obtain 1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium hydroxide                  solution, and (iv) adding taurine into solution                  of 1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium hydroxide,                  stirring and reacting under ice bath for 70-75                  hours, rotary evaporating to remove moisture, and                  vacuum drying at 50 degrees C to obtain                  1-methyl-3-propyl imidazole taurine ionic liquid.                  Preferred Components: In step (i), molar ratio                  between chlorinated n-propane and n-methyl                  imidazole is 1.5:1-2:1. In step (ii), volume                  ratio between ethyl acetate and acetonitrile is                  2:1. In step (iii), ratio between purified                  1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium chloride and                  deionized water is 1 g:4-6 ml. In step (iv),                  molar ratio between 1-methyl-3-propylimidazolium                  hydroxide and taurine is 1:1. Preferred Method:                  The flow rate of carbon dioxide and sulfur                  dioxide gas is 70-90 ml/minute.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

Preferred Components: The porous material is a porous material comprising activated carbon, aluminosilicate, hydrotalcite, porous clay mineral, porous silica and activated alumina.

AU HONG C S
   CHOE J H
   CHAE Y S
   CHOI D S
   JONGHYEOK C
   CHAE Y
   CHOI D
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2021903276
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises a porous metal-organic framework, a polyvalent amine introduced into an open metal site of the porous metal-organic framework, and an alkanolamine introduced into an open metal site of the porous metal-organic framework and coexisting with the polyvalent amine.
   USE - Adsorbent is used in fluidized bed for capturing carbon dioxide from thermal power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent can effectively reduce renewable energy generated in the process of adsorption and desorption of carbon dioxide and maintain structural stability against moisture present in exhaust gas and against changes in adsorption/desorption temperature and moisture in the fluidized bed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkanolamine is formed through a ring opening reaction of the polyvalent amine and an epoxide compound is introduced into the open metal site of the porous metal-organic framework. The porous metal-organic framework further comprises aluminum trioxide bonded to the metal ions, and a hydrophobic silane coated on a surface of a composite formed by bonding the metal ions of the organic framework and aluminum trioxide. The porous metal-organic framework is chosen from compounds of formulae: M2(D1), M2(D2), M2(D3), M2(D4) and M2(D5). The polyvalent amine is of formula (I), preferably chosen from compounds of formulae (I-1)-(I-6). The alkanolamine is of formula (II). The epoxide compound is of formula (III), preferably chosen from 7 compounds, e.g. compounds of formulae (III-1)-(III-5). The hydrophobic silane is of formula (IV), preferably chosen from compounds of formulae (IV-1)-(IV-6).M = Mg, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu or Zn;D1 = 4,4'-dioxido-3,3'-biphenyldicarboxylate;D2 = 2,5-dioxido-1,4-benzenedicarboxylate;D3 = 4,6-dioxido-1,3-benzenedicarboxylate;D4 = 1,5-dioxide-2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylate;D5 = 4,4'-dioxido-3,3'-triphenyldicarboxylate;R1,R2,R4,R5,R7,R8 = H, -F, -Cl, -Br, -CN, -NO2, -OH, -CN, optionally substituted 1-30C alkyl, or -(CH2)pNR'aR'b;R3,R6,R7 = H, hydroxy, optionally substituted 1-30C alkyl, or -(CH2)pNR'aR'b';l,n = 1-10;r,s,p,q = 0-10;m = 0 or 1;R'a-R'd = H or optionally substituted 1-30C alkyl;R9 = H, hydroxy, optionally substituted 1-30C alkyl, phenyl, aryl, -(CH2)pNR'aR'b, or -(A1-B2-)qCR'aR'bCR'cR'dO;A1,B2 = O, NR'a, (CR'bR'c)r, or (C6H4)s;n' = 1-20;R1' = H, hydroxy, optionally substituted 1-30C alkyl, phenyl or aryl (when n is 0), or O, NR'a, (CR'bR'c)r, or (C6H4)s (when n is 1);R2' = O, NR'a, (CR'bR'c)r1, or (C6H4)s1;R3'-R8' = H, hydroxy, optionally substituted 1-30C alkyl, phenyl, aryl, -(CH2)p1NR'aR'b, -(A1-B2-)q1CR'aR'bCR'cR'dO;p1,q1,r1,s1 = 0-20;R11-R13,R11'-R13' = H or (CH2)m1-CH3;andm1,n1 = 0-20.

GA 2021793403
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high-purity cross-linked hyaluronic                acid using supercritical carbon dioxide, comprises                performing cross-linking reaction by mixing                hyaluronic acid, a cross-linking agent and water,                and supplying supercritical carbon dioxide to the                prepared cross-linked hyaluronic acid.
   USE - Used as high-purity cross-linked hyaluronic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The cross-linked with hyaluronic acid produced                has excellent biocompatibility and                biodegradability, which is degraded over time in                the living body and eventually disappears; and is                prepared rapidly, conveniently and by completely                removing the residual or unstable cross-linking                agent in the production process using supercritical                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                high-purity cross-linked hyaluronic acid produced                by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon                  dioxide is supplied at 30-60 degrees C under                  pressure of 7-45 MPa. The step of supplying                  supercritical carbon dioxide to the prepared                  cross-linked hyaluronic acid is performed in a                  high-temperature and high-pressure reactor in                  which the temperature and pressure above the                  critical point of carbon dioxide are maintained.                  The method further comprises removing carbon                  dioxide at a temperature and pressure below the                  critical point from the reactor to remove the                  crosslinking agent dissolved in carbon dioxide                  from the crosslinked hyaluronic acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112341324-A; CN112341324-B
TI Preparing aryl propionic acid compounds by carboxylation of carbon dioxide without metal catalysis, comprises stirring mixed solution of diselenide catalyst and styrene compound, then adding hydrogen peroxide, and reacting.
AU JING S
   CHENG F
   JI W
   GU R
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202117609V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aryl propionic acid compounds by carboxylation of carbon dioxide without metal catalysis, comprises stirring mixed solution of diselenide catalyst and styrene compound under carbon dioxide atmosphere, then adding hydrogen peroxide, and reacting to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aryl propionic acid compounds by carboxylation of carbon dioxide without metal catalysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes styrene as raw material and diselenide as catalyst to ensure carboxylation reaction of olefin and carbon dioxide; produces only water as by-product compared with metal catalyst residue or generation of halogen-containing by-products in existing synthesis methods; and utilizes small molecules of diselenide as catalysts to replace existing metal catalysts and avoids metal residues.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio between diselenide catalyst and styrene compound is 0.02-0.08:0.2-0.4 mmol. The ratio between diselenide catalyst and solvent in the mixed solution is 0.02-0.08:1-2 mmol/ml. The diselenide catalyst is diphenyl diselenide, dibenzyl diselenide, bis(4-bromobenzyl) diselenide (I), bis(2-bromobenzyl) diselenide or dipropyl diselenide. The styrene compound is 6-methoxy-2-naphthalene ethylene, 3,4-dimethoxystyrene, 2,4-dimethylstyrene, 2-methylstyrene, 3-methylstyrene, 4-methylstyrene, 4-bromostyrene, 4-chlorostyrene, 4-methoxystyrene, 4-hydroxystyrene, 4-vinyl biphenyl or 4-tert-butylstyrene and styrene. The solvent of the mixed solution of diselenide catalyst and styrene compound is acetonitrile, ethanol or dimethyl sulfoxide. The ratio between diselenide catalyst and hydrogen peroxide is 0.02-0.08:0.1-0.5 mmol/ml. Preferred Method: The controlling amount of carbon dioxide to make pressure of the reaction system to 80-120 KPa. The method comprises stirring the mixed solution of diselenide catalyst and styrene compound at room temperature for 1-3h under carbon dioxide atmosphere. The method comprises adding hydrogen peroxide, and stirring at room temperature for 8-16 hours under carbon dioxide atmosphere.

   JOO E
   SHIN D
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 201828428X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing aromatic vinyl-vinyl cyanide                copolymer, comprises (a) extracting volatile                organic compounds copolymer by contacting                pellet-type aromatic vinyl-vinyl cyanide copolymer                with carbon dioxide in supercritical fluid                condition, and (b) separating carbon dioxide in                supercritical fluid condition containing volatile                organic compound extracted pellet-type aromatic                vinyl-vinyl cyanide copolymer and volatile organic                compounds. The carbon dioxide has a density of                0.1-0.8 g/cm3. The vinyl-vinyl cyanide copolymer                has volatile organic compound content of less than                1700 ppm.
   USE - The method is useful for producing aromatic                vinyl-vinyl cyanide copolymer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the residual amount of the                volatile organic compound remaining in the pellet                of the aromatic vinyl-cyanide vinyl                copolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The co-solvent is                  methanol, ethanol, 1-propanol, 2-propanol,                  1-butanol, 2-butanol, 1-heptanol, 2-heptanol,                  n-hexane, Furan, tetrahydropyran, acetone,                  propanone, 2-butanone and/or methyl ethyl                  ketone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The pellet-type                  aromatic vinyl-vinyl cyanide copolymer comprises                  a repeating unit derived from an aromatic vinyl                  compound and a repeating unit derived from a                  vinyl cyanide compound. The pellet-type aromatic                  vinyl-vinyl cyanide copolymer has a pellet length                  or an average particle diameter of 0.3-4 mm. The                  pelletized aromatic vinyl-vinyl cyanide copolymer                  has a bulk density of 0.7-1.5 g/cm3. Preferred                  Method: The step (a) is carried out using an                  entrainer under pressure of 74 bar and the                  temperature of 31.1&#176; C. The method                  further comprises separating the carbon dioxide                  in the supercritical fluid state including the                  volatile organic compound into volatile organic                  compounds and gaseous carbon dioxide, and reusing                  the gaseous carbon dioxide as carbon dioxide in a                  supercritical fluid state for extracting volatile                  organic compounds. The steps (a) and (b) are                  performed in a continuous process.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing cyclic carbonate by converting carbon dioxide under the action of chromium-based catalyst comprises e.g. taking carbon dioxide and epoxide as raw materials under normal temperature and pressure without solvent, and then reacting.
AU CAO Y
   ZHANG X
   LI S
   WANG Y
   XU Z
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201823083W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate by converting carbon dioxide under the action of chromium-based catalyst comprises taking carbon dioxide and epoxide as raw materials, conjugated chrome-porphyrin microporous polymer material as heterogeneous catalyst, tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as cocatalyst under normal temperature and pressure without solvent, and reacting for 12-48 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate by converting carbon dioxide under the action of chromium-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has low cost, mild reaction conditions and is suitable for large-scale industrial production; and the product has high catalytic activity and stable structure performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexeneoxide. The amount of active component of heterogeneous catalyst is 0.2-0.5 mol% the amount of epoxy compound. The amount of co-catalyst is 3.6-7.2 mol% the amount of epoxy compound. Preferred Method: The conjugated chrome porphyrin microporous polymer material of formula (I) is prepared by using Scholl coupling reaction and taking tetraphenylporphyrin as monomer and anhydrous aluminum chloride as catalyst. The conjugated chrome porphyrin microporous polymer material can be replaced with conjugated chrome porphyrin bibenzylamine microporous polymer material, and conjugated chrome porphyrin bibenzylamine microporous polymer material of formula (II) is prepared by using Scholl coupling reaction, and taking tetraphenylporphyrin and benzylamine as monomer and anhydrous aluminum chloride as catalyst.

PN CN113083367-A
TI New metal phthalocyanine-based photocatalytic            material used as catalyst in reduction of monoatomic            carbon dioxide.
AU ZANG S
   WANG S
   WANG Q
   DONG X
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202182602R
AB    NOVELTY - A metal phthalocyanine-based photocatalytic                material (I), is new.
   USE - New metal phthalocyanine-based photocatalytic                material is used as catalyst in reduction of                monoatomic carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal phthalocyanine-based porous organic                polymer material has improved photocatalytic                efficiency by changing its coordination                environment. The material provides more direct                opportunity to identify the activity of the metal                center. In the results of photo-reduction of carbon                dioxide, the introduction of nickel-dinitrogen                dioxide catalytic center into the original                phthalocyanine-based nickel-tetranitrogen framework                can achieve excellent carbon monoxide generation                capacity (7.77 mmol/g), and the relative hydrogen                selectivity is as high as 96%. Combining controlled                experiments and theoretical studies, the material                has high carbon dioxide reduction activity compared                to the existing metal-nitrogen and metal-dinitrogen                dioxide sites.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal phthalocyanine-based photocatalytic                material of formula (I), is new.
   M = Ni or Co.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of the material (I); and
   (2) use of the material (I) as catalyst in                reduction of monoatomic carbon dioxide while                satisfying the conditions of light, carbon dioxide                saturation, photosensitizer and sacrificial                agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Preparation of the                  material (I) involves adding                  2,3,9,10,16,17,23,24-octamino phthalocyanine                  nickel and 2,6-diformylphenol to a mixed solvent                  comprising N,N-dimethylacetamide and mesitylene,                  ultrasonically-processing, adding aniline and                  acetic acid aqueous solution, sealing in a                  polytetrafluoroethylene reactor, reacting by                  solvothermal method, cooling to room temperature,                  centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain a                  single-center monoatomic material (a), adding the                  material (a) to a saturated methanol solution of                  metal acetate tetrahydrate (M(OAc)2.4H2O),                  preferably nickel(II) acetate tetrahydrate or                  cobalt(II) acetate tetrahydrate, stirring at room                  temperature, centrifuging, washing and drying.                  The reduction of monoatomic carbon dioxide is                  performed at visible light wavelength of 390-780                  nm, using 10-99.99 %mass carbon dioxide, 1-2 mg                  catalyst, and solvent having water content of                  0-20 %mass. Preferred Composition: The material                  (I) has a two-dimensional network structure, and                  simultaneously two types of monoatomic sites                  including metal (M)-nitrogen (N4) and metal                  (M)-dinitrogen dioxide exists in the material                  (I).

TI Preparing bio-based polycarbonate useful in e.g.            building board, and electronic appliance, comprises            e.g. introducing carbon dioxide in reactor for placing            isosorbide and catalyst, introducing supercritical            carbon dioxide fluid, and carrying out            prepolymerization reaction.
AU CHENG J
   LI X
   CAO C
   WANG Y
AE SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202273005K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bio-based polycarbonate comprises                (1) placing isosorbide and catalyst in                high-pressure reaction vessel, open air inlet valve                and the air release valve, passing carbon dioxide                to purge replacement air, and closing air release                valve, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid into reactor, adjusting temperature and                pressure in reactor, maintaining carbon dioxide in                a supercritical state in the reactor, and carrying                out prepolymerization reaction of each reactant,                (2) open inlet valve and air release valve,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide from air                inlet valve, and make carbon dioxide continue to                flow out from air release valve to maintain a                constant pressure in reaction system, continuing                each reactant to termination reaction, and (3)                after completing reaction, cooling, adding                dichloromethane to dissolve product, filtering,                adding filtrate into ethanol, and precipitate                obtained by filtration is bio-based                polycarbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bio-based                polycarbonate, which is useful in building board,                electronic appliance, office machine, packaging and                sports equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is more easily dissolving and                bringing out by-product water, and good for growth                of molecular chain. The polycarbonate: has large                molecular weight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                bio-based polycarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is a                  metal-supported molecular sieve catalyst. The                  molecular sieve is H-Zeolite Socony Mobil-5                  molecular sieve or titanium-silicon molecular                  sieve. The metal is platinum, silver, cobalt,                  copper and manganese, preferably platinum, or                  silver/copper bimetal.Preferred Method: The metal loading in the                  catalyst is 0.5-10 wt.% of carrier based on mass                  of the metal. The preparation method of                  metal-supported molecular sieve catalyst is                  impregnation method, chemical vapor deposition                  method, hydrothermal method or deposition                  precipitation method. The preparation method of                  metal-supported molecular sieve catalyst includes                  dipping molecular sieve into a metal soluble salt                  solution, drying, and calcining in an inert                  atmosphere to obtain catalyst. The consumption of                  the catalyst is 0.1-10 wt.% isosorbide. In the                  step (1), the temperature of the                  prepolymerization reaction is 32-70degrees                  Celsius, the pressure is 7.3-10 MPa, and the time                  is 2-5 hours. In the step (2), after opening the                  gas release valve, the flow rate of carbon                  dioxide outflow is maintained at 1-20 ml/minute.                  In the step (2), the temperature of the final                  polymerization reaction is 40-80degrees Celsius,                  and the time is 4-7 hours. In the step (3), the                  carbon dioxide purge is maintained during the                  cooling process. In the step (3), the mass ratio                  of the amount of dichloromethane added to the                  crude product is 1:1-4:1. In the step (3), the                  ethanol and the filtrate are mixed in a mass                  ratio of 1:1-4:1. The bio-based polycarbonate                  having molecular weight of greater than or equal                  to 45000, and the glass transition temperature is                  greater than or equal to 120degrees                  Celsius.

   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly; ensure the high heat and recycling rate of carbon dioxide; realize the low carbon target; and the product has high purity and excellent quality performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) Preparing high-purity magnesium oxide by low carbon circulation comprises (i) calcining the magnesite in a tower-type reheating shaft kiln to generate light-burned magnesium and carbon dioxide, removing dust, heat-exchanging and cooling through the kiln gas purification device and the treated carbon dioxide enters the carbonization tower through the air compressor for carbonization reaction, (ii) crushing the light burned magnesium prepared in the resultant step and then allowing to enter a digestion tank for digestion reaction, where the digestion temperature is 50-70degrees Celsius and the digestion time is 30-60 minutes, (iii) entering the digestion product into the carbonization tower, carrying out carbonization reaction together with carbon dioxide and the carbonization temperature is 30-40degrees Celsius, (iv) placing the carbonized solution into a carbonized liquid storage tank, conveying the carbonized solution to a plate-and-frame filter press for solid-liquid separation and placing the separated heavy magnesium water into a heavy magnesium water storage tank, preheating the heavy magnesium water with the waste heat after the kiln gas purification device and carbon dioxide heat exchange and injecting the preheated heavy magnesium water into a pyrolysis tower for pyrolysis to generate suspended basic magnesium carbonate and carbon dioxide, and (v) placing basic magnesium carbonate into a magnesium carbonate storage pool, dehydrating the basic magnesium carbonate by a filter, sending the dehydrated product into a tower-type reheating shaft kiln II and introducing a kiln gas purification device and carbon dioxide, generating high-purity magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide, sending the carbon dioxide to the kiln gas purifying device to remove dust, after the heat exchange cooling treatment used for carbonization reaction, filtering the filtrate by filtering device and placing into the filtrate storage pool; and
