TI Method for calculating real-time road vehicle tail            gas carbon dioxide emission based on time-space deep            learning model, involves.
AU ZHANG P
   JIA T
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2022A2069D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting vehicle Global                Positioning System (GPS) track data. Training data                is divided from the collected vehicle GPS track                data. End to end of a spatio-temporal deep learning                model is constructed. The space-time deep learning                model is provided with a history module, a current                module, an external module, a fusion module and a                full connection network layer. The history module                is used for extracting vehicle history driving                model historical driving condition data by using                long-short term memory (LSTM) network. The current                module is used for extracting a current driving                model and space feature from current driving                condition data by multi-level full-communicate. The                external module is used for extracting the external                feature by using the full connection network. The                fusion module is used for fusing output of the                history module. The full connection network layer                is used for estimating a current carbon dioxide                (CO2) emission value according to result of the                fusion module. Vehicle driving condition variables                and related variables are derived from training                data to minimize measured vehicle per second carbon                dioxide emission and estimated vehicle per second                carbon dioxide emission as target. The trained                space-time deep learning model is obtained.                Real-time calculation of tail gas carbon dioxide                emission of the road vehicle is performed by using                the trained time-space deep learning model.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables deriving the vehicle                driving condition variables and related variables                from the training data as input data input                constructed space-time deep learning model to                minimize the measured vehicle per second carbon                dioxide emission and estimated vehicle carbon                dioxide emissions as target, training the model,                obtaining the trained space time deep learning                models, and performing real-time calculation of                tail gas carbon dioxide emission of the road                vehicle by using the trained time-space deep                learning model.

AE NINGXIA ZIGUANG TIANHUA METHIONINE CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2022301277
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methionine based on carbon dioxide acidification comprises (i) separating 5-(2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin hydrolyzate into crude methionine, crystallizing mother liquor and carrying out acidification with carbon dioxide, (ii) adding crude methionine with water for mixing and beating, separating and drying to obtain methionine, (iii) concentrating crystallization mother liquor, dividing part of the concentrated solution, and using the remaining concentrated solution for 5-(2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin hydrolysis, (iv) separating concentrated solution of the diversion into crude methionine and impurity enrichment solution through carbon dioxide acidification, and (v) returning methionine crude product into the crystallization mother liquor, extracting the secondary mother liquor without methionine solids, returning remaining secondary mother liquor containing methionine solids to the acidification of the hydrolyzate in the step (i).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methionine based on carbon dioxide acidification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: removes impurities in the saponification solution in the production process of DL methionine; does not produce a large accumulation during the continuous production of methionine, and affecting the quality of methionine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing methionine based on carbon dioxide acidification comprises (i) separating 5-(2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin hydrolyzate into crude methionine, crystallizing mother liquor and carrying out acidification with carbon dioxide, (ii) adding crude methionine with water for mixing and beating, separating and drying to obtain methionine, combining returned beating water to step (i) with the crystallization mother liquor, (iii) concentrating crystallization mother liquor, dividing part of the concentrated solution, and using the remaining concentrated solution for 5-(2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin hydrolysis after regeneration, (iv) separating concentrated solution of the diversion into crude methionine and impurity enrichment solution through carbon dioxide acidification, (v) returning methionine crude product into the crystallization mother liquor of step (i) to obtain secondary mother liquor, extracting the secondary mother liquor without methionine solids and repeating step (iii)-(v), returning remaining secondary mother liquor containing methionine solids to the acidification of the hydrolyzate in the step (i).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the mass ratio of crude methionine and water is 0.1-1:1.Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the concentration temperature of the crystallization mother liquor is 100-180degreesC. In the step (iii), dividing the concentrated solution is 10-30 wt.% of the total concentrated solution. In the step (iii), the remaining concentrated solution is thermally decomposed at 160-200 degreesC, pyrolysis of potassium bicarbonate into potassium carbonate and potassium methionine, and returning to hydrolysis of 5-(2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin. The method comprises adjusting the impurity enrichment solution to acid, removing the oil phase impurities by static layering or organic solvent extraction, and the formic acid by vacuum distillation, separating methionine and potassium sulfate from the deacidified aqueous phase by electrodialysis, chromatography or concentration. The impurity enrichment solution is left to stand for stratification after acid adjustment. The oil phase was adjusted to pH greater-than 10 by adding calcium oxide, using solidified material as a soil conditioner or feed additive, adjusting aqueous phase of allowing to stand and stratifying to pH 4-7 with calcium oxide, and using filtrate for 5-(2-methylthioethyl)-hydantoin hydrolysis. The electrodialysis method is bipolar membrane electrodialysis. The impurity enrichment solution is adjusted to pH less-than 3. In the step (v), the secondary mother liquor extracted is 70-90 wt.% of the total secondary mother liquor.

AB    NOVELTY - Terpene-enhanced cannabinoid concentrate is                produced and contaminants are removed by extracting                terpene oil from Cannabis to yield residual                Cannabis and terpene oil by pressurizing carbon                dioxide to 1000-1300 psi to form supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid and exposing Cannabis to                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid at 80-140                degrees C for 15 minutes to 6 hours to obtain                carbon dioxide extraction solution consisting of                terpene oil and residual Cannabis; obtaining                Cannabis oil from residual Cannabis; distilling                Cannabis oil to obtain cannabinoid distillates;                separating cannabinoid distillates into purified                cannabinoid distillates and residual contaminants                by mixing cannabinoid distillates with greater than                or equal to 1 contaminant attractant consisting of                hydrophilic compound; and blending purified                cannabinoid distillates with terpene oil to obtain                a terpene oil enhanced cannabinoid                concentrate.
   USE - Production of terpene-enhanced cannabinoid                concentrate and removal of contaminants.
   ADVANTAGE - Blending terpenes at the end of extraction may                enhance the flavor and effectiveness of the                purified cannabinoid concentrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Terpene-enhanced cannabinoid concentrate is                produced and contaminants are removed by extracting                terpene oil from Cannabis to yield residual                Cannabis and terpene oil by pressurizing carbon                dioxide to 1000-1300 psi to form supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid and exposing Cannabis to                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid at 80-140                degrees C for 15 minutes to 6 hours to obtain                carbon dioxide extraction solution consisting of                terpene oil and residual Cannabis; obtaining                Cannabis oil from residual Cannabis; distilling                Cannabis oil to obtain cannabinoid distillates;                separating cannabinoid distillates into purified                cannabinoid distillates and residual contaminants                by mixing cannabinoid distillates with greater than                or equal to 1 contaminant attractant consisting of                hydrophilic compound to yield cannabinoid                contaminant attractant solution, heating, adding                distilled water to dissolved cannabinoid                contaminant attractant solution, heating,                separating cannabinoid contaminant attractant                aqueous solution into purified cannabinoid                contaminant attractant aqueous solution and                residual contaminants, and removing contaminant                attractant and water from purified cannabinoid                contaminant aqueous solution to yield purified                cannabinoid distillates; and blending purified                cannabinoid distillates with terpene oil to obtain                a terpene oil enhanced cannabinoid                concentrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Addition of contaminant                  attractant to cannabinoid distillates comprises                  adding cannabinoid distillates at a ratio of                  2:1-4:1 of contaminant attractant by volume in ml                  to cannabinoid distillates by mass in grams, to                  yield cannabinoid contaminant attractant                  solution. Heating cannabinoid contaminant                  attractant solution comprises heating to 40-80                  degrees C to yield dissolved cannabinoid                  contaminant attractant solution. Addition of                  distilled water to dissolved cannabinoid                  contaminant attractant solution comprises adding                  distilled water to dissolved cannabinoid                  contaminant attractant solution at a ratio of                  1:4-1:10 of distilled water by volume in ml to                  cannabinoid distillates by mass in grams, to                  yield cannabinoid contaminant attractant aqueous                  solution. Heating the cannabinoid contaminant                  attractant aqueous solution comprises heating                  cannabinoid contaminant attractant aqueous                  solution to 40-60 degrees C. Separation of                  cannabinoid contaminant attractant aqueous                  solution into purified cannabinoid contaminant                  attractant aqueous solution and residual                  contaminants separately comprises allowing                  residual contaminants to settle out of the                  cannabinoid contaminant attractant aqueous                  solution. Allowing the residual contaminants to                  settle out of the cannabinoid contaminant                  attractant aqueous solution consists essentially                  of cooling cannabinoid contaminant attractant                  aqueous solution to - 30-10 degrees C, yielding a                  cooled cannabinoid contaminant attractant aqueous                  solution; and allowing the residual contaminants                  to settle out of the cooled cannabinoid                  contaminant attractant aqueous solution for 2                  hours to 7 days. The method further comprises                  freezing residual Cannabis to obtain a frozen                  Cannabis; washing frozen Cannabis with cold                  ethanol to obtain an ethanol oil solution of                  Cannabis; and separating ethanol oil solution of                  Cannabis into ethanol and Cannabis oil by                  distilling ethanol oil solution of Cannabis at                  80-200 degrees F under a vacuum measuring between                  - 10 inches Hg and - 28 inches Hg. The method                  further comprises reusing ethanol for a                  subsequent washing step of 2nd batch of frozen                  Cannabis. Distilling Cannabis oil to obtain                  cannabinoid distillates consists essentially of                  generating vacuum in a distillation vessel with a                  pressure at or below - 29 inches Hg; heating                  Cannabis oil to 90-157 degrees C; collecting 1st                  condensed vapor to yield terpene distillates;                  heating Cannabis oil to 153-230 degrees C; and                  collecting 2nd condensed vapor to yield                  cannabinoid distillates. The method further                  comprises redistilling purified cannabinoid                  distillates to obtain higher concentration of                  purified cannabinoid distillates; and blending                  higher concentration of purified cannabinoid                  distillates with terpene oil to obtain a higher                  concentration of terpene oil enhanced cannabinoid                  concentrate. Extracting terpene oil further                  consists essentially of separating a terpene                  hydrosol from carbon dioxide extraction                  product.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2019164956-A1; EP3737831-A1; US2021079760-A1; BR112020016316-A2; JP2021514035-W; US11225853-B2; EP3737831-A4; JP2022119987-A
TI Method for rehabilitation of water well, involves            distributing energy throughout well structure by            controlling carbon dioxide phase changes during carbon            dioxide injection to cause surging agitation within            well bore.
AU MANSUY N
   CATANIA S
   ORLANDO J
AE SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
   SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
   SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
   SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
   SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
GA 201974664E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a more effective                distribution of effective energy throughout an                entire well structure (10,11) that includes                portions of the producing interval of the well and                a surrounding formation (13). The energy is                distributed throughout the entire well structure by                controlling carbon dioxide phase changes during                carbon dioxide injection to cause surging agitation                within the well bore and surrounding formation and                allowing carbon dioxide mechanical energy and                optionally chemical energy to reach all area of the                well. A volume displacement pipe (7) is set into                the well before carbon dioxide injection.
   USE - Method for rehabilitation of water well such                as water supply well, injection well, aquifer                storage and recovery (ASR) well, recovery well,                monitor well, collector well, slant well,                horizontal well, and directional well.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy is more effectively directed, which                results in carbon dioxide volume reduction and less                residual carbonation and carbon dioxide that needs                to be pumped during the development of a well. The                chemical energy is delivered more effectively to                all portions of the well by placing chemistry in                the well and utilizing the agitation of the carbon                dioxide phase change. The volume displacement pipe                reduces the amount of energy needed to clean the                entire well with less energy penetrating into the                surrounding formation. The vortex allows the                combination energies to be distributed from the                injection point represented by diversion elbow and                upward inside the well allowing the entire well and                near well to be cleaned more effectively. The                annular space between well casing and screen or                open hole and volume displacement pipe is                reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for well rehabilitation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of a                screened well with filter pack for energy                injection.
   Flange (2)
   Grout (6)
   Volume displacement pipe (7)
   Well structure (10,11)
   Surrounding formation (13)

TI Sensor apparatus for detecting carbon dioxide in skin of earlobe of human or animal patient, has radiation filter configured such that radiation in detection radiation path has wavelengths that extend over region other than absorption band.
AU FEHR J
   ROULET J
   WEBER P
   FINCI J
   SCHERER L
AE RADIOMETER BASEL AG (DANH-C)
   RADIOMETER BASEL AG (DANH-C)
   RADIOMETER BASEL AG (DANH-C)
   RADIOMETER BASEL AG (DANH-C)
GA 201954073J
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a radiation filter (70) positioned relative to a gas measuring chamber (30) such that an interaction radiation path is defined between a radiation source (40) through the gas measuring chamber to the radiation filter. A first radiation detector (50) is positioned relative to the radiation filter such that a detection radiation path is defined that is in addition to an inter-action radiation path. A second radiation detector (60) is positioned relative to the radiation filter. The radiation filter is configured such that radiation in the detection radiation path has wavelengths that extend over a region other than an absorption band of carbon dioxide, where signals from the first and second radiation detectors are useable to determine partial pressure of carbon dioxide in a skin of a patient.
   USE - Sensor apparatus for detecting carbon dioxide in a skin of an earlobe of a patient i.e. human or animal patient, by a hospital staff.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus provides gas sensor with minimal components, thus providing reduction in size and increased signal intensities to increase signal to noise and reduce detection times. The apparatus is compact, simple to assemble and not overly expensive, and exhibits high signal intensities. The apparatus uses an off-the-shelf filter to save costs. The apparatus diffuses blood gases from the patient skin to penetrate a membrane, while blocking dust and moisture from penetrating the membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for detecting carbon dioxide
   (2) a computer program comprising a set of instructions for detecting carbon dioxide
   (3) a computer readable medium comprising a set of instructions for detecting carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a sensor apparatus for detecting carbon dioxide.
   Sensor apparatus for detecting carbon dioxide (10)
   Gas measuring chamber (30)
   Radiation source (40)
   Radiation detectors (50, 60)
   Radiation filters (70, 80)

TI Water treatment system for separating water, comprises osmotic pressure generator including first treatment container having osmosis membrane, concentrator, phase separating unit and carbon dioxide introducing unit.
AU SUZUKI A
   IMADA T
   KUBOKI T
   SANO K
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
GA 2018755397
AB    NOVELTY - A water treatment system comprises an osmotic pressure generator (1) including a first treatment container (11) having an osmosis membrane (12) to partition a first chamber for accommodating water to be treated and a second chamber (14) for accommodating a working medium to induce osmotic pressure, and a concentrator (2) including a carbon dioxide releasing unit for releasing carbon dioxide from the working medium, a phase separating unit for separating the phase-separated working medium, and a carbon dioxide introducing unit for absorbing carbon dioxide into the working medium, where the working medium contains an amine compound (I) or (II).
   USE - Water treatment system for separating water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system can be operated at low cost and energy consumption, and can efficiently perform a desalination treatment of seawater (recovery of pure water).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A water treatment system comprises an osmotic pressure generator including a first treatment container having an osmosis membrane to partition a first chamber for accommodating water to be treated and a second chamber for accommodating a working medium to induce osmotic pressure, and a concentrator including a carbon dioxide releasing unit for releasing carbon dioxide from the working medium, a phase separating unit for separating the phase-separated working medium, and a carbon dioxide introducing unit for absorbing carbon dioxide into the working medium, where the working medium contains an amine compound of formula (I) or (II).
   R11, R14 = 1-5C alkyl or optionally tertiary amine, a cyclic tertiary amine, a carboxyl group, and a hydroxyl group;
   R12, R13, R15, R16 = H, 1-3C alkyl or optionally hydroxyl group, or halogen;
   R21 = 2-10C alkyl or optionally tertiary amines;
   R22, R23 = CH2 , O, or CH-COOR24; and
   R24 = 0-4 linear or branched carbon atoms.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a working medium comprising an amine compound.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a water treatment system.

   Concentrator (2)

PN JP2018103071-A; JP6559644-B2
TI Regeneration soil manufacturing system, has carbon-dioxide-gas contact unit which provides carbon dioxide gas to construction sludge in which hydration reaction is stimulated and to neutralize construction sludge.
AU SUZUKI T
   YAMAUCHI H
   MORIYA T
   HAYANO K
AE DOMI KANKYO KK (DOMI-Non-standard)
   DOMI ENVIRONMENTAL SOLUTIONS KK (DOMI-Non-standard)
   SUZUKI T (SUZU-Individual)
   MORIYA T (MORI-Individual)
GA 201853630Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a stirring unit which is provided to stir the alkaline construction sludge in which the water absorbing modified material is added. A first curing area cures the construction sludge stirred in the stirring unit until it becomes semi-solid form. A loosening granulation unit is provided to loosen the fine granules. A second curing area cures the construction sludge formed into fine granules by the loosening the granulation unit and promotes hydration reaction. A carbon-dioxide-gas contact unit provides the carbon dioxide gas to the construction sludge in which the hydration reaction is stimulated and to neutralize the construction sludge. A cement-type solidification material is added to the construction sludge. The flow path into which the carbon dioxide gas is made to flow is provided in the pit from the upper direction. A supply pipe supplies the carbon dioxide gas to the space. A throwing-in portion throws the construction sludge provided in the second curing area.
   USE - Regeneration soil manufacturing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen ion concentration index is neutralized and the high quality regeneration soil is manufactured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a regeneration soil manufacturing method. The method involves adding and mixing the water absorbing modified material with alkaline construction sludge. The construction sludge processed in the modification solidification process is stirred until it becomes semi-solid form. The fine granules are formed from the construction sludge and the hydration reaction is promoted. The carbon dioxide gas contacts with the construction sludge in which the hydration reaction is stimulated. A cement-type solidification material is added to the construction sludge. The construction sludge in which the hydration reaction is stimulated is made to directly-contact with the carbon dioxide gas and is neutralized. The construction sludge in which the hydration reaction is stimulated is thrown in on the space where the carbon dioxide gas is filled and is cured.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of regeneration soil manufacturing system.
   Mixer (2)
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).)

   USE - The gismondine-type aluminosilicate zeolite useful for carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gismondine-type aluminosilicate zeolite utilizes environmentally-friendly and economical sodium metal element.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:

   (2) carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising the gismondine-type aluminosilicate zeolite; and
   (3) manufacturing gismondine-type aluminosilicate zeolite, comprising heating the composition including 1-3 pts. wt. sodium oxide, 1 pts. wt. alumina, 2-10 pts. wt. silica and 100-1000 pts. wt. water.

The gismondine-type aluminosilicate zeolite is a dehydrated zeolite having a stepped carbon dioxide adsorption isotherm. The gismondine zeolite comprises 0.1-10 wt.% metal oxide of formula (MxO) (I), 1 wt.% aluminum oxide and 4-100 wt.% silicon dioxide in molar ratio of 0.5-5:1:6-10. Preferred Method: The composition is heated at greater than or equal to 150 degrees C.M = inorganic cation of monovalent alkali metal cations and divalent alkaline earth metal cations; andx = 1 or 2.

PN CN114180748-A
TI Device for reducing carbon dioxide by heavy metal            ions in waste water, comprises inlet and outlet which            are respectively connected with reaction tubes, and            inlet and outlet communicate with each other and            reaction tube is provided with photocatalyst-loaded            adsorption material.
AU CHEN H
   DENG Q
   ZHANG H
AE SHANGHAI INST CERAMICS CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGU-C)
GA 202242761T
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises a reactor, and the reactor                comprises several reaction tubes. Multiple reaction                tubes are connected in series and communicate with                each other, and the reactor is provided with an                inlet and an outlet. The inlet and the outlet are                respectively connected with multiple reaction                tubes, and the inlet and outlet communicate with                each other through multiple reaction tubes. The                reaction tube is provided with a                photocatalyst-loaded adsorption material. When                catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide is required,                the S2-containing solution is introduced into the                reaction tube and the adsorbed heavy metal ions are                in situ to generate metal sulfide semiconductor                nanomaterials. The carbon dioxide gas is introduced                into the reaction tube, and carbon dioxide is                catalyzed and reduced by the synergistic effect of                illumination, photocatalyst and metal sulfide                semiconductor nanomaterials.
   USE - Device for reducing carbon dioxide by heavy                metal ions in waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The device utilizes the sensitization effect                of metal sulfide to photocatalyst to catalyze                reduction of carbon dioxide. The use of a tubular                shape is beneficial to the improvement of the                reaction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A device comprises a reactor, and the reactor                comprises several reaction tubes. Multiple reaction                tubes are connected in series and communicate with                each other, and the reactor is provided with an                inlet and an outlet. The inlet and the outlet are                respectively connected with multiple reaction                tubes, and the inlet and outlet communicate with                each other through multiple reaction tubes. The                reaction tube is provided with a                photocatalyst-loaded adsorption material. When the                heavy metal ions in the waste water need to be                removed, the waste water enters the reaction tube                through the inlet, and the heavy metal ions in the                waste water are adsorbed by the adsorption                material. When catalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide is required, the S2-containing solution is                introduced into the reaction tube and the adsorbed                heavy metal ions are in situ to generate metal                sulfide semiconductor nanomaterials. The carbon                dioxide gas is introduced into the reaction tube,                and carbon dioxide is catalyzed and reduced by the                synergistic effect of illumination, photocatalyst                and metal sulfide semiconductor                nanomaterials.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for reducing carbon dioxide by utilizing heavy                metal ions in wastewater.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the device for reducing carbon dioxide                by using heavy metal ion in waste water.
   Light source irradiation reaction tube                (1)
   Shell (4)
   Inner shell (5)
   Light source (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent material                  is made of porous adsorbent material. The surface                  of the porous material contains hydrophilic                  functional groups. The S2-containing solution is                  sodium sulfide solution.

PN EP3766950-A1; WO2021009337-A1; US2022267697-A1; CA3163124-A1; JP2022541516-W
TI Separate extraction of rice bran oil and rice bran            wax by adopting extraction vessel containing rice bran,            percolating supercritical carbon dioxide, transferring            extract comprising oil and waxes and reducing pressure            to precipitate.
AU JEROME A
   ABRAHMI J
AE RB PROCESS SA (RBPR-Non-standard)
   RB PROCESS SA (RBPR-Non-standard)
   RB PROCESS SA (RBPR-Non-standard)
GA 202109108K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separate extraction of rice bran                oil and rice bran wax involves (a) performing an                extraction by adopting an extraction vessel                containing rice bran and percolating supercritical                carbon dioxide through the extraction vessel at an                extraction pressure (p1) of 100-1000 bar and an                extraction temperature (T1) of 35-120 degrees C,                (b) performing a first separation by transferring                the supercritical carbon dioxide and an extract                comprising oil and waxes from step (a) to a first                separation vessel and reducing the pressure to a                first separation pressure (p2) that is lower than                the extraction pressure (p1) to precipitate a first                product comprising rice bran oil from supercritical                carbon dioxide and (c) performing a second                separation by transferring supercritical carbon                dioxide and remaining extract from step (b) to a                second separation vessel and reducing the pressure                to a second separation pressure (p3) to precipitate                a second product comprising rice bran wax from a                gaseous carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for separate extraction                of rice bran oil and rice bran wax.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the extracted products                which are free from toxic residues, have high                concentration of nutritionally valuable components                and are obtainable in a yield comparable to the                conventional techniques and obtains the yield of                the collected rice bran oil and rice bran wax after                decantation and water removal of 14.7% and 1.1%,                respectively and total yield of rice bran oil and                wax of 15.7%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separate extraction of rice bran                oil and rice bran wax involves (a) performing an                extraction by adopting an extraction vessel                containing rice bran and percolating supercritical                carbon dioxide through the extraction vessel at an                extraction pressure (p1) of 100-1000 bar and an                extraction temperature (T1) of 35-120 degrees C,                (b) performing a first separation by transferring                the supercritical carbon dioxide and an extract                comprising oil and waxes from step (a) to a first                separation vessel and reducing the pressure to a                first separation pressure (p2) that is lower than                the extraction pressure (p1) to precipitate a first                product comprising rice bran oil from supercritical                carbon dioxide and (c) performing a second                separation by transferring supercritical carbon                dioxide and remaining extract from step (b) to a                second separation vessel and reducing the pressure                to a second separation pressure (p3) that is lower                than the first separation pressure (p2) to                precipitate a second product comprising rice bran                wax from a gaseous carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  adopting the extraction pressure (p1) for the                  extraction step (a) of 100-400 bar, preferably                  280 bar. The method involves adopting the                  extraction temperature (T1) for the extraction                  step (a) of 50-80 degrees C, preferably 60                  degrees C. The method involves adopting the first                  separation which is conducted at a first                  separation temperature (T2) that is higher than                  the extraction temperature (T1) of extraction                  step (a). The method involves adopting the first                  separation temperature (T2) is at least 65                  degrees C, preferably 75 degrees C, where the                  first separation pressure (p2) for the first                  separation step (b) is set below 230 bar,                  preferably 180 bar. The method involves adopting                  the second separation pressure (p3) for the                  second separation of below 73.8 bar, preferably                  60 bar. The method involves conducting the second                  separation at a second separation temperature                  (T3) that is lower than the extraction                  temperature (T1) of the extraction step (a). The                  method involves setting the second separation                  temperature (T3) below 70 degrees C, preferably                  50 degrees C. The method involves conducting the                  extraction step (a) with a solvent/feed ratio of                  25-45, preferably 36. The method further involves                  (d) performing recovery, in which the temperature                  of the gaseous carbon dioxide after the second                  separation is decreased to a recovery temperature                  (T4), at which the gaseous carbon dioxide changes                  to a liquid state, where the recovery temperature                  (T4) is at most 30 degrees C, preferably 5-15                  degrees C and after the recovery step (d), the                  pressure and the temperature of the liquid carbon                  dioxide is increased to the extraction pressure                  (p1) and extraction temperature (T1) to change                  the state of the carbon dioxide to supercritical                  for re-use in extraction step (a).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110311161-A; CN110311161-B
TI Controlling cathode potential in electrochemical hydrogen pump carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor by using membrane method involves using positive charge-modified proton exchange membrane instead of liquid buffer layer.
AU WU X
   HE G
   WANG Y
   ZHANG S
   CHENG H
   FAN S
   JIN R
   WANG X
   LI X
   XIAO W
   JIANG X
   FENG M
   FANG Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
   UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019949661
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling a cathode potential in an electrochemical hydrogen pump carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor by using a membrane method involves: (A) using a positive charge-modified proton exchange membrane instead of a liquid buffer layer, when electric energy is applied to the electrochemical hydrogen pump carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor, passing the hydrogen protons generated by the hydrogen dissociation of the anode through the proton exchange membrane, and generating in-situ adsorption hydrogen and carbon dioxide at the cathode for hydrogenation reaction; and (B) introducing the positive charges into the proton exchange membrane through an ion replacement method or a layer-by-layer self-assembly method according to the proton exchange membrane, where the positive charges in the membrane migrate and accumulate towards the cathode side under the action of an electric field to form a double electric layer with the cathode.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling a cathode potential in an electrochemical hydrogen pump carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor by using a membrane method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the modified membrane instead of the liquid buffer layer; avoids the liquid phase environment; eliminates the interface problem; realizes higher cathode potential; simultaneously utilizes the Donnan repulsion effect of the positive charge on the hydrogen proton; stably suppresses the hydrogen evolution reaction for a long time; and obtains higher carbon dioxide hydrogenation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Controlling a cathode potential in an electrochemical hydrogen pump carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor by using a membrane method involves: (A) using a positive charge-modified proton exchange membrane instead of a liquid buffer layer, when electric energy is applied to the electrochemical hydrogen pump carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor, passing the hydrogen protons generated by the hydrogen dissociation of the anode through a positive-charged modified proton exchange membrane, and generating in-situ adsorption hydrogen and carbon dioxide at the cathode for hydrogenation reaction; (B) introducing the positive charges into the proton exchange membrane through an ion replacement method or a layer-by-layer self-assembly method according to the proton exchange membrane, where the positive charges in the membrane migrate and accumulate towards the cathode side under the action of an electric field to form a double electric layer with the cathode, regulating the cathode potential of the carbon dioxide hydrogenation, and promoting the carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction; and (C) simultaneously using Donnan repulsion effect of positive charge on hydrogen protons to suppress the hydrogen evolution reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The positive charge-modified proton exchange membrane is prepared by ion displacement method. The preparation method involves placing the proton exchange membrane in an aqueous solution of potassium chloride, sodium chloride or magnesium chloride at a concentration of 1.5-3 mol/l, soaking at 75-85 degrees C for 1-12 hours, displacing some of the hydrogen protons into metal cations by soaking, and then using deionized water to clean the surface of the film. The positive charge-modified proton exchange membrane is prepared by layer-by-layer self-assembly method. The preparation method involves: alternately immersing the proton exchange membrane in a negatively charged polymer aqueous solution and positively charged polymer aqueous solution, soaking for 20-40 minutes each time and finally cleaning the membrane surface with deionized water to obtain a positively charged modified proton exchange membrane, where the number of self-assembled layers is 1-3 layers, and each layer has a thickness of 0.2-0.3 mu m; and utilizing the electrostatic attraction between the negatively charged sulfonated polyetheretherketone film and the positively charged polymer material to attach the positive charge to the surface of the membrane, and forming an electric double layer with the cathode to regulate the hydrogenation potential of carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The proton exchange membrane is made of a non-fluorosulfonic acid polymer having a sulfonation degree of 40-80%. The negatively charged polymer is a sulfonated polyetheretherketone having a sulfonation degree of greater than 70%, and the aqueous solution has a mass concentration of 50-88%. The positively charged polymer is a polyquaternium or a polyethylenimine, and the aqueous solution has a mass concentration of 50-88% or 3-10%, respectively.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

GA 2019A7422R
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying raw synthesis gas by gas scrubbing                with detergent, comprises e.g. providing and                supplying the raw synthesis gas to a pre-scrubber                for removing trace components including ammonia and                hydrogen cyanide, feeding the gaseous top product                of the pre-scrubber to an hydrogen sulfide scrubber                to remove hydrogen sulfide, feeding the gaseous                overhead product of the hydrogen sulfide scrubber                into a carbon dioxide scrubber to remove carbon                dioxide, discharging a purified synthesis gas                stream as the gaseous top product of the carbon                dioxide scrubber, supplying the liquid sump product                of the prewash to a medium pressure prewash flash                container, supplying the gaseous top product of the                medium pressure prewash flash container to a                re-compressor, supplying the liquid sump product of                the medium pressure prewash flash container to a                low pressure prewash flash container, and supplying                the gaseous overhead product the low-pressure                prewash flash container to the re-compressor.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying raw                synthesis gas by gas scrubbing with                detergent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying raw synthesis gas by gas scrubbing                with detergent, comprises (a) providing and                supplying the raw synthesis gas to a pre-scrubber                for removing trace components including ammonia and                hydrogen cyanide, (b) feeding the gaseous top                product of the pre-scrubber to an hydrogen sulfide                scrubber to remove hydrogen sulfide, (c) feeding                the gaseous overhead product of the hydrogen                sulfide scrubber into a carbon dioxide scrubber (6)                to to a                re-compressor, supplying the liquid sump product of                the medium pressure prewash flash container to a                low pressure prewash flash container, supplying       the hydrogen sulfide scrubber to a medium pressure                hydrogen sulfide flash container, supplying the                gaseous top product of the medium pressure hydrogen TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a)                  providing and supplying the raw synthesis gas to                  a pre-scrubber for removing trace components                  including ammonia and hydrogen cyanide, (b)                  supplying the gaseous top product of the                  pre-scrubber to an hydrogen sulfide scrubber for                  removing Hydrogen sulfide, (c) feeding the                  gaseous overhead product of the hydrogen sulfide                  scrubber to a carbon dioxide scrubber for                  removing carbon dioxide, (d) introducing at least                  part of the gaseous overhead product of the                  carbon dioxide scrubber into a preferably                  multi-stage expander, mechanical work and cold                  being obtained and an expanded, purified                  synthesis gas stream being discharged from the                  expander, (e) supplying the liquid sump product                  of the prewash to a medium pressure prewash flash                  container, supplying the gaseous top product of                  the medium pressure prewash flash container to a                  re-compressor, supplying the liquid sump product                  of the medium pressure prewash flash container to                  a low pressure prewash flash container, supplying                  the gas shaped top product of the low-pressure                  prewash flash container to the re-compressor, and                  supplying the liquid bottom product of the                  low-pressure prewash flash container to at least                  one device for flash regeneration and/or hot                  regeneration of the detergent, (f) feeding the                  liquid sump product of the hydrogen sulfide                  scrubber to a medium pressure hydrogen sulfide                  flash container, supplying the gaseous top                  product of the medium pressure hydrogen sulfide                  flash container to the first or a subsequent                  stage of the multi-stage expander or into the                  line downstream of the expander, supplying the                  liquid sump product of the medium pressure                  hydrogen sulfide flash container a low-pressure                  hydrogen sulfide flash container, supplying the                  gaseous top product of the low-pressure hydrogen                  sulfide flash container to the re-compressor,                  supplying the liquid bottom product of the                  low-pressure hydrogen sulfide flash container to                  at least one device for flash regeneration and/or                  hot regeneration of the detergent, (g) feeding                  the liquid bottom product of the carbon dioxide                  scrubber to a medium-pressure co2 flash                  container, feeding the gaseous top product of the                  medium-pressure carbon dioxide flash container to                  the first or a subsequent stage of the the low-pressure hydrogen sulfide                  flash container and/or to the re-compressor,                  supplying the liquid bottom product of the                  low-pressure carbon dioxide flash container to at                  least one device for flash regeneration and/or                  hot regeneration of the detergent, and (h)                  returning the gas stream compressed by the                  re-compressor to the pre-scrubber for the raw                  synthesis gas. Preferred Components: The                  detergent contains components including methanol,                  N-methylpyrrolidone, secondary amines,                  diethanolamine, tertiary amines, preferably                  methyldiethanolamine, polyethylene glycol dialkyl                  ether, preferably polyethylene glycol dimethyl                  ether. The re-compressor is designed in multiple                  stages, the gaseous overhead products from the                  low-pressure flash containers of the first stage                  and the gaseous overhead products from the                  medium-pressure flask containers being fed to a                  subsequent stage of the multi-stage                  re-compressor.

TI High-burned magnesium oxide, pure carbon dioxide powder producing system comprises grinding system, a combustion system, a filtering system and a cooling system, the system further comprises a double calcinations system.
AU ZHANG S
   LIU J
   WANG Y
   YAO T
AE ZHENJIANG SOBUTE NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
   SUBOTE NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SUBO-Non-standard)
GA 2018779956
AB    NOVELTY - High-burned magnesium oxide, pure carbon dioxide powder producing system comprises grinding system, a combustion system, a filtering system and a cooling system. The system further comprises a double calcinations system for rotary kiln and which is divided into an inner cylinder and outer cylinder. The inner cylinder and outer cylinder are completely isolated. The two ends of the outer cylinder are respectively connected with the grinding system and filtering system. The two ends of the inner cylinder are respectively connected with the burning system and chimney, combustion system fully burning in the inner cylinder, and the combustion heat generated is connected with inner cylinder to outer cylinder, magnesite outer cylinder is heated and decomposed to obtain light burned magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as high-burned magnesium oxide, pure carbon dioxide powder producing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves burning magnesium oxide grade of the product, recycles the high purity carbon dioxide gas and solves resource waste and environmental pollution problem of burned magnesium oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Processing high-burned magnesium oxide, pure carbon dioxide powder by using production system, comprising taking magnesite with different grain size as raw material, heating the vertical mill grinding the magnesite raw ore powder into fineness of 200 meshes of magnesite powder, feeding the magnesite raw powder into the outer cylinder of the rotary kiln from the kiln tail, gradually transmitting magnesite raw powder along with the rotation of the outer cylinder from the inner cylinder, moving the kiln head, absorbing magnesite powder from the kiln tail cylinder is out of the rotary kiln, gradually decomposing into light-burned magnesium oxide powder and carbon dioxide gas, transferring heat into the rotary kiln cylinder from the combustion system provides using coal powder preparing equipment of the coal powder grinding to fineness is 0.08 mm screen residue less than or equal to 10% of coal powder, adding the coal powder through burner of from the kiln into the rotary kiln cylinder, under the action of the combustion fan, the fully burning the coal powder, combustion heat in the rotary kiln cylinder through the rotary inner cylinder to the outer cylinder of the rotary kiln, calcining magnesite in the outer cylinder of the rotary kiln in the raw powder, discharging from the kiln tail chimney of high temperature smoke gas after burning and the inner cylinder, full heat exchanging magnesite powder, heating and decomposing to generate burned magnesium oxide powder and carbon dioxide gas, discharging from the outer tube head of the rotary kiln, entering burned magnesium oxide powder into the cooling system and monocular cooling machine, cooling to obtain light burned magnesium oxide powder product, discharging carbon dioxide gas from the tail of the outer cylinder of the rotary kiln, where the rotary kiln outer cylinder is connected with the filtering system, the kiln discharging carbon dioxide gas dust collector after purification, washing tower washing and filtering with water to obtain high purity carbon dioxide gas, discharging burned magnesium oxide powder from outer cylinder head, collecting to obtain the light-burned magnesium oxide product, carbon dioxide gas escapes from the tail part of the outer cylinder, purifying and dust removing to obtain high purity carbon dioxide gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The rotary outer cylinder comprises heat-resistant steel. The head and tail end is provided with support and can rotate slowly under the action of the transmission mechanism. The rotary kiln cylinder comprises heat-resistant ceramic pipe. The heat-resistant ceramic pipe is installed outside the rotary kiln cylinder, and the outer cylinder is arranged in parallel. The inclined angle of the double-cylinder rotary kiln is 3.5 degrees . The system comprises grinding system, a burning system, a combustion system, a filtering system and a cooling system is orderly connected. The grinding system is a vertical mill to a tail high head low manner obliquely arranged. The burning system is composed of coal powder preparation device, the pulverized coal burner and a combustion-supporting fan and is connected with the rotary kiln cylinder head system composed of the purifying dust collector, the filter and the water washing tower and the tail part of the outer cylinder of the rotary kiln. The cooling system is a monocular cooling machine. The magnesite raw ore is mined magnesite fine powder shape produced in the crushing process.

TI Ship ballast water treatment system comprises a main treatment means for destroying the organisms contained in the ballast water, pretreatment means and post-treatment means for neutralizing the ballast water.
AU PARK Y S
AE WATERFIN CO LTD (WATE-Non-standard)
GA 2018541276
AB    NOVELTY - Ship ballast water treatment system comprises a main treatment means (100) for destroying the organisms contained in the ballast water flowing into the ballast tank through the ballast piping from the outside of the ship, a pretreatment means (200) for destroying the organisms contained in the ballast water before the ballast water introduced into the vessel flows into the main treatment means, provided at a front end of the main treatment means and a post-treatment means (300) for neutralizing the ballast water discharged from the ballast tank to outside of the ship. The pretreatment means is provided with a physical treatment device for crushing and destroying the organisms contained in the ballast water introduced into the ballast pipe, and a carbon dioxide storage tank for storing high-pressure carbon dioxide, an injector for injecting carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank into the ballast pipe, and a carbon dioxide injection device.
   USE - Used as ship ballast water treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system preserves the produced treated water for long time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ship ballast water treatment system comprises a main treatment means (100) for destroying the organisms contained in the ballast water flowing into the ballast tank through the ballast piping from the outside of the ship, a pretreatment means (200) provided at a front end of the main treatment means for destroying the organisms contained in the ballast water before the ballast water introduced into the vessel flows into the main treatment means, and a post-treatment means (300) for neutralizing the ballast water discharged from the ballast tank to outside of the ship. The pretreatment means is provided with a physical treatment device for crushing and destroying the organisms contained in the ballast water introduced into the ballast pipe, and a carbon dioxide storage tank for storing high-pressure carbon dioxide, an injector for injecting carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank into the ballast pipe at the front end of the physical treatment apparatus, and a carbon dioxide injection device for injecting carbon dioxide into the ballast water to reduce the pH of the ballast water. The physical processing apparatus includes a body connected to the ballast pipe and having a flow passage for allowing the ballast water to pass, a stator built in the main body, a crushing unit at one side and multiple stator housings, and a rotor having a crushing means corresponding to the crushing means of the stator. The crushing means is composed of a device integrated with the carbon dioxide injection device. The crushing means of the stator and the crushing means of the rotor are rotatable with each other to rotate and kill the organisms of the ballast water by the shear force and the pressure difference during rotation and to mix and dissolve the carbon dioxide and the ballast water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the ship ballast water treatment system.

TI Producing organic compound in subterranean            environment involves providing e.g. carbon dioxide            feedstock and converting carbon dioxide feedstock            portion into organic compound by adding the feedstock            to subterranean environment.
AU KARIMI T
   KARIMI M
AE CEMVITA FACTORY INC (CEMV-Non-standard)
   CEMVITA FACTORY INC (CEMV-Non-standard)
   CEMVITA FACTORY INC (CEMV-Non-standard)
   CEMVITA FACTORY INC (CEMV-Non-standard)
GA 202119516W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing at least one organic compound in a                subterranean environment comprises providing a                carbon dioxide feedstock, a microbial culture, and                a microbial growth medium and converting a portion                of the carbon dioxide feedstock into the organic                compound by adding the carbon dioxide feedstock,                the microbial culture, and the microbial growth                medium to the subterranean environment, where the                carbon dioxide feedstock contains 1-100 vol.%                carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing at least                one organic compound in a subterranean environment,                where the subterranean environment includes a                wellbore, a well formation containing a                hydrocarbon, a natural cavern, a subterranean                formation and/or an underground storage tank.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: converts a carbon dioxide                feedstock stored in a subterranean environment into                organic compounds, thus reducing excess carbon                dioxide from the environment; is cost effective;                create renewable hydrocarbon wells by pumping                existing carbon dioxide into subterranean                environments along with microbes designed to                transform carbon dioxide into hydrocarbons; and is                economically profitable for an oil or natural gas                company to remove carbon dioxide from the                environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1)                a system for producing at least one organic                compound in a subterranean environment comprising a                microbial culture vessel connected to a microbe                injection port and a microbial growth medium vessel                connected to a microbial growth medium injection                port, or a culture mixture vessel connected to a                culture mixture port, a supply pipeline connected                to at least one of the microbe injection port, the                microbial growth medium injection port, the culture                mixture port, and to a pumping station, and an                injection pipeline connected to the pumping                station, where a portion of the injection pipeline                is connected to the subterranean environment; and                (2) increasing the production of a hydrocarbon from                a wellbore comprising collecting a sample from a                wellbore environment at a time point, analyzing the                sample to obtain a sample microbiome information,                selecting a microbial culture and a microbial                growth medium based on the sample microbiome                information, where the sample microbiome                information comprises an adaptation of the                microbial culture, a resistance of the microbial                culture to at least one property of the wellbore                environment and/or at least one property of the                wellbore environment, where the property includes                temperature, pressure, permeability, porosity,                salinity and/or acidity, providing a biosurfactant                feedstock, converting a portion of the                biosurfactant feedstock into at least one                biosurfactant and degrading at least one petroleum                hydrocarbon and/or at least one paraffin present in                the wellbore environment by adding the                biosurfactant feedstock, the microbial culture, and                the microbial growth medium to the wellbore                environment, where the microbial culture comprises                a surfactant producing bacterium, a petroleum                hydrocarbon degrading bacterium, a paraffin                degrading bacterium, Gordina sp, Pseudomonas sp,                Bacillus sp, Bacillus subtilis, a rock degrading                bacterium and/or Rhodothermus marinus and                increasing production of the hydrocarbon from the                wellbore.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microbial culture:                  includes at least one bacterial population native                  to the subterranean formation, at least one                  exogenous bacterial population, at least one                  anaerobic bacterial population and/or at least                  one genetically modified bacterial population;                  includes a Geobacter bacterium, Clostridium                  bacterium, Bacillus bacterium, Geobacillus                  bacterium, Petrobacterbacterium, Desulfotomaculum                  bacterium, Bacteroides bacterium,                  Thermoanaerobacter bacterium, Thermococcus                  bacterium, Thermotogales bacterium, Petrotoga                  bacterium, Thermotoga bacterium, Caminicella                  bacterium and/or Geosporobacter bacterium, a                  methanotrophic bacterium, a methanogenic                  bacterium, archaebacterium, a chemotrophic                  bacterium, iron oxidizing bacterium, sulfur                  oxidizing bacterium, extremophile bacterium,                  thermophilic bacterium, halophilic bacterium,                  hydrogen producing bacterium, surfactant                  producing bacterium and/or an acetogenic                  bacterium; and is an aqueous liquid comprising                  1-50 wt.% at least one bio-based material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: harvesting the organic compound from                  the subterranean environment after a time period                  sufficient for the microbial culture to convert                  the portion of the carbon dioxide feedstock into                  the compound or adding a second portion of carbon                  dioxide feedstock to the subterranean                  environment; collecting a first sample from the                  subterranean environment at a first time point,                  analyzing the first sample to obtain a first                  sample microbiome information and selecting the                  microbial culture and the microbial growth medium                  based on the microbiome information of the first                  sample; selecting the microbial culture and the                  microbial growth medium based on the property of                  the subterranean environment; collecting a second                  sample from the subterranean environment at a                  second time point, analyzing the second sample to                  obtain a second sample microbiome information,                  selecting an adjustment dose based on the second                  sample microbiome information, where the                  adjustment dose includes a second microbial                  culture and/or a second microbial growth medium                  and injecting the adjustment dose into the                  subterranean environment; measuring an amount of                  the organic compound produced by the first                  microbial culture and the second microbial                  culture; adding the carbon dioxide feedstock, the                  microbial culture, and the microbial growth                  medium to the subterranean environment,                  simultaneously, or in any order or injecting or                  pumping at least one of the carbon dioxide                  feedstock, the microbial culture and the                  microbial growth medium into the subterranean                  environment, simultaneously or in any order;                  forming a culture mixture by mixing the microbial                  culture and the microbial growth medium together                  and adding the culture mixture and the carbon                  dioxide feedstock to the subterranean                  environment, simultaneously or in any order;                  injecting the microbial culture and the microbial                  growth medium together into the subterranean                  environment in a combined volume at an injection                  rate and an injection pressure sufficient to                  convert the portion of carbon dioxide feedstock                  into the organic compound; and introducing at                  least one of an electrolysis apparatus and a                  hydrogen feedstock into the subterranean                  environment, where the hydrogen feedstock                  contains 1-80 vol.% hydrogen. The carbon dioxide                  feedstock is added to a first subterranean                  environment, where the microbial culture and the                  microbial growth medium are added to a second                  subterranean environment, and where the first                  subterranean environment is connected to the                  second subterranean environment. Preferred                  Components: The bio-based material includes                  glucose, fructose, glycerol, sucrose,                  maltodextrin, sodium chloride, a yeast extract, a                  malt extract, a casein peptone, sodium acetate,                  sodium lactate, a com steep liquor, a watermelon                  rind, a com cob, molasses and/or a dry bio-based                  material. The organic compound includes a 1-12C                  alkane, 1-4C alcohol, 1-3C organic acid and/or                  1-120C hydrocarbon, alkane, methane, butane,                  pentane, hexane, heptane, octane nonane, decane,                  dodecane, tetradecane, octadecane, alkene,                  alcohol, methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanediol,                  organic acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid, olefin,                  ethylene and/or bio-surfactant
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108144324-A; WO2019126994-A1
TI Multifunctional supercritical particle preparation system comprises cylinders for storing high purity carbon dioxide set with carbon dioxide coolers, pumps and valves, entrainer container for storing entrainer, and reactors.
AU HE D
   LIN L
   CHEN C
   XU S
   JIN C
AE NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
   SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 201847427Y
AB    NOVELTY - Multifunctional supercritical particle preparation system comprises cylinders (C) for storing high purity carbon dioxide comprising carbon dioxide coolers, carbon dioxide pumps (B) and valves (K1-K5); entrainer container for storing entrainer including entrainer pump; first reactor equipped with pressure gauge and temperature gauge; and second reactor set with upper and lower covers. The high-purity carbon dioxide in cylinders is first cooled by carbon dioxide cooler to reach temperature range required for supercritical conditions, entered and passed through carbon dioxide pump, pressurized to bring carbon dioxide to supercritical state, and controlled through valve to enter subsequent reaction process. The entrainer container is pressurized by entrainer pump. The generated high-pressure fluid is mixed with supercritical carbon dioxide controlled by valve.
   USE - Multifunctional supercritical particle preparation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has simple operation, adjustable experimental device parameter range, strong stability, and high production efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of multifunctional supercritical particle preparation system.
   Reactors (A1, A2)
   Carbon dioxide pump (B)
   Cylinder (C)
   Valves (K1, K2, K3, K4, K5)
   Separator (S)
EA    (WO2019126994-A1)   NOVELTY - The system has an entrainer container (CS) for storing the entrainer, an entrainer pump (U), and the entrainer in the entrainer container are pressurized by the entrainer pump, and enter the first and second reactors (A1,A2) through a pipeline. The first reactor is equipped with a first pressure gauge and a first temperature gauge. The temperature control and pressure in the first reactor are controlled by first pressure gauge and first thermometer. The temperature control and pressure in the second reactor are shown by second pressure gauge and second thermometer. The reactants are passed through the valve three from the top into the second reactor. The reaction product in the second reactor is controlled from the bottom pipe to the separator (S) or collector by the valve. The reactants are passed through the valve from the bottom to the second reactor, and the reaction product in second reactor is controlled from the top line to the separator or collector by the valve.
   USE - Multifunctional supercritical particle preparation system used in material research, preparation, and application, and excellent industrial fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The multifunctional supercritical particle preparation system realizes substance extraction, organic micro/nanometer material preparation, inorganic micro/nanometer material preparation. The device is easy to operate and permits a wide range of adjustment of parameters of an apparatus in experimentation, and ensures superior stability and good manufacturing efficiency.

PN KR2022117784-A
TI Production of carbon dioxide reduction product            comprises e.g. supplying carbon dioxide into reduction            electrode, supplying water or aqueous alkali metal salt            solution into oxidation electrode, and generating            formate solution by applying voltage.
AU LEECHANWOO
   KIM H R
   WIJAYA D
AE UNIV KOOKMIN IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYKO-C)
GA 2022A99269
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a carbon dioxide reduction                product involves supplying carbon dioxide to the                reduction electrode, supplying water or an aqueous                alkali metal salt solution to the oxidation                electrode, applying voltage between the reduction                electrode and oxidation electrode to generate a                formate solution by moving formate ions and                hydroxide ions generated by the reduction reaction                at the reduction electrode to the solid electrolyte                through the anion-exchange membrane, and moving one                or more hydrogen ions generated by oxidation at the                oxidizing electrode and alkali metals present in                aqueous alkali metal salt solutions to the solid                electrolyte through the cation-exchange membrane to                generate a formate solution in the solid                electrolyte, and passing a fluid through the solid                electrolyte to collect the formate solution.
   USE - Production of carbon dioxide reduction                product.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide reduction product is                produced with high efficiency even at a low voltage                by controlling the operating temperature of the                device, the type and concentration of the solution                injected into the oxidizing electrode. The                deterioration of durability and stability of the                electrode due to the electrolyte is                prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of a carbon dioxide reduction                product involves supplying carbon dioxide to the                reduction electrode, supplying water or an aqueous                alkali metal salt solution to the oxidation                electrode, applying voltage between the reduction                electrode and oxidation electrode to generate a                formate solution by moving formate ions and                hydroxide ions generated by the reduction reaction                at the reduction electrode to the solid electrolyte                through the anion-exchange membrane, and moving one                or more hydrogen ions generated by oxidation at the                oxidizing electrode and alkali metals present in                aqueous alkali metal salt solutions to the solid                electrolyte through the cation-exchange membrane to                generate a formate solution in the solid                electrolyte, and passing a fluid through the solid                electrolyte to collect the formate solution. The                carbon dioxide reduction device has a reduction                electrode (cathode), an oxidation electrode                (anode), a solid-state electrolyte positioned                between the reduction electrode and oxidation                electrode, an anion-exchange membrane positioned                between the reduction electrode and solid                electrolyte, and a cation-exchange membrane                positioned between the oxidation electrode and                solid electrolyte. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included                for the carbon dioxide reduction device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reduction                  electrode and oxidation electrode include a                  gas-diffusion layer and a catalyst. The                  gas-diffusion layer comprises a carbon paper,                  carbon cloth, metal foam or metal mesh. The                  catalyst contained in the reduction electrode                  comprises bismuth, tin, indium, lead, antimony,                  nickel, and/or its alloy or oxide. The catalyst                  contained in the oxidation electrode is at least                  one of iridium, platinum, ruthenium, palladium,                  and its alloy or oxide. The carbon dioxide                  supplied to the reduction electrode is humidified                  carbon dioxide. The alkali metal salt aqueous                  solution has a concentration of 1-10M. The alkali                  metal salt aqueous solution comprises at least                  one of hydroxides, halides, carbonates, hydrogen                  carbonates, and nitrates of alkali metals.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The resulting formate                  solution contains at least one of formic acid and                  an alkali metal salt of formic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solid electrolyte                  comprises an ion-conductive polymer, an inorganic                  electrolyte or a ceramic.
