PT P
PN CN113481381-A
TI Copper fire refining method based on carbon dioxide, involves continuously adding reducing agent to copper liquid after slagging for reduction, and smelting and shaping copper liquid to obtain refined copper, after reduction is completed.
AU WANG H
   GAO D
   WANG Q
AE ZHANGJIAGANG UNITED COPPER CO LTD (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B9957E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding the copper raw material into the refining furnace (12) for heating and melting, and at the same time passing the first gas into the copper raw material and stirring continuously. The molten copper liquid is obtained, where the first gas is a mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide. The input flow rate of carbon dioxide is 600 to 1200Nm/h, and the input flow rate of oxygen accounts for 0-25% of the total flow rate of the first gas input. The second gas includes oxygen and carbon dioxide, the input flow rate of carbon dioxide is 600-1200 Nm/h, and the input flow rate of oxygen accounts for 0-25% of the total input flow rate of the second gas. The reducing agent is continuously added to the copper liquid after the slagging for reduction, and the copper liquid is smelted and shaped to obtain the required refined copper, after the reduction is completed.
   USE - Copper fire refining method based on carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The copper fire refining process avoids the copper liquid in the reduction process of reducing edge oxidation, and also reduces the super-standard discharge risk of the nitrogen oxide. The input oxygen flow is adjustable through the mixed input of oxygen and carbon dioxide, on one hand, avoiding excessive oxidation of copper liquid, and on the other hand, using carbon dioxide as melting material stirring and conveying the process medium.

   Temperature transmitter (6)

TI Apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide from an acid gas stream, comprises an absorber having a first packing segment, a second packing segment and a third packing segment.
AU LIU K
   NIKOLIC H
   ZHEN F
   THOMPSON J
   WARRINER A
AE UNIV KENTUCKY RES FOUND (KENT-C)
GA 2020539595
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises an absorber (32) having a first packing segment (64), a second packing segment (66) and a third packing segment (68). The first packing segment includes a first structured packing having a first specific surface area (SA1), the second packing segment includes a second structured packing having a second specific surface area (SA2) and the third packing includes a third structured packing having a third specific surface area (SA3), where SA1 not less than SA2 not less than SA3.
   USE - Apparatus used for capturing carbon dioxide from an acid gas stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus have a discretized packing arrangement, as well as to a related method, that are both adapted to modify this temperature profile, effectively moves the temperature bulge down the absorber, hence results in as much as a 5-11% increase in rich loading, depending upon solvent lean loading.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of capturing carbon dioxide from an acid gas stream, which involves:
   (A) providing an absorber tower with a first packing segment, having a first specific surface area SA1, a second packing segment, having a second specific surface area SA2, and a third packing segment, having a third specific surface area SA3;
   (B) subjecting the acid gas stream to a countercurrent flow of a carbon dioxide lean CO2 absorbent in the absorber tower;
   (C) discharging treated acid gas and a carbon dioxide rich CO2 absorbent from the absorber tower; and
   (D) recovering the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide rich CO2 absorbent in a stripper and returning carbon dioxide lean CO2 absorbent to the absorber tower.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of absorber.
   Absorber (32)
   First packing segment (64)
   Second packing segment (66)
   Third packing segment (68)
   Liquid/gas distributor (72)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Conditions: The apparatus further including at least one in-situ liquid/gas distributor in at least one packing section of the first packing segment, the second packing segment and the third packing segment. The in-situ liquid/gas distributor is a section of random packing. The section of random packing is characterized by a random packing pressure drop per height greater than any pressure drop per height characteristic of the first structured packing, the second structured packing and the third structured packing. The first specific surface area SA1 is less than 34 feet squared/feet cubed, the second specific surface area SA2 is between 34 feet squared/feet cubed and 129 feet squared/feet cubed and the third specific surface area SA3 is above 152 feet squared/feet cubed. The first packing segment includes multiple layers of the first structured packing separated by a first layer of the random packing. The multiple layers of the first structured packing have a first thickness (T1) of 121.92-182.88 cm and the layer of random packing has a thickness (T2) of 7.62-15.24 cm. The third packing segment includes multiple layers of the third structured packing having the first thickness separated by a third layer of the random packing having the thickness T2. The absorber is adapted to remove carbon dioxide from a flue gas stream using a carbon dioxide (CO2) absorbent and the apparatus further includes a stripper adapted to remove carbon dioxide from the CO2 absorbent. The secondary stripper includes a top packing section including the first structured packing having the first specific surface area SA1 and a lower packing section including a fourth structured packing having a fourth specific surface area SA4 where SA4 is equal to SA2 or SA3.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Attapulgite-based carbon dioxide solid amine adsorbent used in decarbonization and purification of biogas, comprises organic liquid amine modified attapulgite.
AU CHEN X
   GUO H
   LI Q
   ZHANG H
   LI H
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI GUANGZHOU ENERGY RES IN (CAEC-C)
GA 201974055P
AB    NOVELTY - An attapulgite-based carbon dioxide solid amine adsorbent comprises organic liquid amine modified attapulgite. The organic liquid amine is accounted for 10-50% of weight of attapulgite.
   USE - Attapulgite-based carbon dioxide solid amine adsorbent used in decarbonization and purification of biogas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The attapulgite-based carbon dioxide solid amine adsorbent has high carbon dioxide saturation adsorption amount and high recycling performance, by simple, economical, easy and industrial process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: preparation of attapulgite-based carbon dioxide solid amine adsorbent, which involves carrying out attapulgite modification and organic liquid amine modification process, (1) carrying out attapulgite modification by crushing attapulgite ore through 200 mesh standard sieve, adding 15-30 wt.% acid solution to vessel and heating at 80-120 degrees C, adding attapulgite ore powder in ratio of 5-10 mL/g, sealing container and stirring for 24-48 hours, leaving still for 10-30 minutes, filtering upper layer slurry and washing to neutrality, drying obtained filter cake, pulverizing, and passing through 200 mesh standard sieve to obtain acid-modified attapulgite powder, (2) carrying out organic liquid amine modified attapulgite by adding 50 pts. wt. organic solvent to vessel, adding 0.5-5 pts. wt. organic liquid amine, mixing, adding 5-10 pts. wt. acid-modified attapulgite powder, and immersing the container seal for 12-24 hours, recovering organic solvent by rotary evaporation, and drying in vacuum drying oven; and #use of attapulgite-based carbon dioxide solid amine adsorbent in decarbonization and purification of biogas, which involves loading prepared solid amine adsorbent into fixed bed reactor, adsorbing carbon dioxide in biogas at 10-50 degrees C to increase methane content in biogas, desorbing solid amine adsorbent when methane content in biogas is detected to be less than 90%, and regenerating at 80-150 degrees C and reusing for biogas purification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid in step (1) is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, sulfuric acid, and/or phosphoric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent in step (2) is methanol, ethanol, and/or cyclohexane. The organic liquid amine in step (2) is monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, diisopropanolamine, triethanolamine, methyldiethanolamine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine, and/or mixed organic amine of polyethyleneimine. Preferred Properties: The adsorption effect of carbon dioxide is related to loading amount of organic liquid amine. The adsorption temperature, inlet flow rate and carbon dioxide partial pressure, low temperature, low intake flow rate and high carbon dioxide partial pressure are more favorable for carbon dioxide adsorption removal.

TI Large marine algae acidification adaptability research system comprises cultivating workshop and long pipeline system, where cultivating workshop is set to natural sea area within one km from workshop roof with semi-transparent glass steel.
AU XU D
   YE N
   ZHANG X
   FAN X
   WANG Y
AE CAFS YELLOW SEA FISHERIES RES INST (CAFS-C)
GA 2019159885
AB    NOVELTY - The large marine algae acidification adaptability research system comprises cultivating workshop (1) and long pipeline system (2), where the cultivating workshop is set with natural sea area within 1 km from the workshop roof with semi-transparent glass steel tile. The long pipeline system comprises a reservoir (3), carbon dioxide enriched pool and algae culturing pool and a drainage pipe is set with disposed valve for amount of controlling water displaced by water inlet. The carbon dioxide enriched pool is provided with carbon dioxide, which is used for adjusting water pH value of the three drainage pipe.
   USE - Large marine algae acidification adaptability research system.
   ADVANTAGE - The large marine algae acidification adaptability research system allows to establish relative stability of large algae seawater acidification culture system, and has large algae-adaptability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating large marine algae acidification adaptability research system involves:
   (A) placing the algae to be tested in the algae culture pond;
   (B) setting different gradient pH in the carbon dioxide enrichment tank, and maintaining pH by the carbon dioxide richer inflation method; and
   (C) controlling the flow rate of the reservoir, carbon dioxide enrichment tank and algae culture tank by adjusting the size of the drainage tube, and controlling the culture density of the algae to maintain the pH in the algae culture pond and ensuring stable carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide enrichment tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a large marine algae acidification adaptability research system
   Cultivating workshop (1)
   Long pipeline system (2)
   Reservoir (3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The reservoir has length, width, and height of 4 m, 2 m, and 2 m, respectively, and a submersible pump is used to remove seawater from the natural sea area and pumping into the reservoir. The carbon dioxide enrichment tank has length, width, and height of 4 m, 2 m, and 1 m, respectively. The seawater is introduced into the carbon dioxide enrichment tank in the reservoir, and seawater pH is adjusted by carbon dioxide richer inflation method. The length, width and height of the algae culture pond are 1m, 4m and 0.6m respectively, and the seawater has stable pH value in the carbon dioxide enrichment tank, that enters the algae culture pond through the drainage pipe. The water flow rate between the reservoir, carbon dioxide rich pool and algae culture pond is 2.5L/minutes, and at the same time, and the effects of algae respiration and photosynthesis are reduced based on seawater pH, and algae culture density does not exceed 5g FW L-1.

PN DE102017005681-A1; WO2018228718-A1; CA3065580-A1; CN110730830-A; IN201917048955-A; EP3638828-A1; US2020165732-A1; JP2020525641-W
TI Production of gas product comprising carbon monoxide involves electrolyzing carbon dioxide, adsorbing to obtain gas product enriched in carbon monoxide, and residual mixture enriched in carbon dioxide, adsorbing and electrolyzing mixture.
AU HENTSCHEL B
   PESCHEL A
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 2018A1485U
AB    NOVELTY - Production (100,200) of gas product (D) comprising carbon monoxide involves electrolyzing (10) carbon dioxide to obtain a raw gas (A) comprising carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, partially or completely recycling carbon dioxide contained in the raw gas to electrolysis and performing adsorption (20) to obtain gas product (D) enriched in carbon monoxide and depleted in carbon dioxide, and a residual mixture (E) enriched in carbon dioxide and depleted in carbon monoxide, performing membrane separation (30) of the mixture (E) to obtain a gas mixture (B) as a retentate and a gas mixture (H) as a permeate, performing adsorption of the mixture (B) with the raw gas or with portion(s) of the raw gas, and partially recycling the mixture (H) to electrolysis.
   USE - Production of gas product comprising carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, easy and economical production of gas product comprising carbon monoxide with improved yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production plant for gas product comprising carbon monoxide, which comprises an electrolysis unit, a unit adapted to return the carbon dioxide contained in the raw gas to electrolysis, a unit adapted to supply the raw gas to adsorption, a unit adapted to supply the mixture (E) to membrane separation, a unit adapted to return the mixture (B) to electrolysis, and a unit adapted to return the mixture (H) to adsorption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show a schematic view of the production plant for gas product comprising carbon monoxide.
   Electrolysis process (10)
   Adsorption process (20)
   Membrane separation process (30)
   Production of gas product comprising carbon monoxide (100,200)
   Raw gas (A)
   Gas mixtures (B,H)
   Gas product (D)
   Residual mixture (E)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixture (B) is enriched in carbon monoxide and depleted in carbon dioxide. The mixture (H) is enriched in carbon dioxide and depleted in carbon monoxide. The gas product (D) comprises 20-100% carbon monoxide and 0-80 hydrogen, and is lean or free of carbon dioxide. The raw gas comprises 0-60% hydrogen, 10-90% carbon monoxide, and 10-80% carbon dioxide. Preferred Process: The adsorption involves pressure swing adsorption and/or temperature swing adsorption. The membrane separation involves at least two membrane separation steps, where the mixture (B) each in the at least two membrane separation steps is formed as retentate, and the mixture (H) each in the at least two membrane separation steps is formed as permeate. The electrolysis is at an electrolysis pressure level. The adsorption is at an adsorption pressure level. The adsorption pressure level differs by not more than 1, 2, 3 or 5 bar from the electrolysis pressure level, and the mixture (E) and/or the mixture (B,H) are compressed to the electrolysis pressure level. The adsorption pressure level is 5-30 bar above the electrolysis pressure level, where the raw gas or portion(s) of the raw gas subjected to the adsorption is compressed to the adsorption pressure level. The electrolysis is performed in the form of high-temperature electrolysis using solid oxide electrolysis cells and/or low-temperature co-electrolysis using a liquid electrolyte.

The weight ratio of water and raw material is 30-35:60-65. The concentration of carbon dioxide is 1-1.5 times and 3-4 times of concentration of carbon dioxide in air. Preferred Method: Cooling is done to 25-28 degrees C. The humidity is 85-90% and 20-30%.

PN CN112794327-A; CN112794327-B
TI Preparing carbonyl sulfide from mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide involves placing alkaline earth metal modified diatomaceous earth in reaction chamber of bipolar plate reactor, and introducing nitrogen to remove air.
AU ZHAO L
   FANG K
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202156643D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbonyl sulfide from mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide involves placing the alkaline earth metal modified diatomaceous earth in the reaction chamber of the bipolar plate reactor, and introducing nitrogen to remove the air in the reactor, and then introducing a mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, where the volume percentage of carbon dioxide in the mixture is 50-99%, the volume percentage of hydrogen sulfide is 1-50%. The flow rate of the mixed gas is 50-9000h-1, and the reaction pressure is 0.1-3 MPa, and the reaction temperature 120-150 degrees C. The plasma power supply connected to the high-voltage electrode and the ground electrode is turned on, the voltage is adjusted to 1-100kV, and the frequency is adjusted to 1-30kHz to obtain carbonyl sulfide gas.
   USE - Method for preparing carbonyl sulfide from mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbonyl sulfide from mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide in an eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a bipolar plate reactor to carry out method for preparing carbonyl sulfide from mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, which comprises an air inlet, high-voltage electrode, ground electrode, shell and air outlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline earth metal is beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium or barium. The material of the shell is glass, quartz glass, ceramic or corundum.

PT P
PN KR2017761-B1
TI Biomass filler for manufacturing polymer matrix composite, is obtained by forming dialdehyde cellulose with aldehyde functional groups using biomass including lignin, and bonding amine group of polymer to aldehyde functional group.
AU YOUNG L J
   SUB H K
   KWON H J
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 201978326D
AB    NOVELTY - A biomass filler is obtained by treating biomass containing complex of lignin, hemicellulose and cellulose, and component(s) chosen from soybean hull, wheat, chaff, rice straw, sugar cane, herbal and wood with periodate reagent to break off the glucose ring structure in the cellulose chain, forming 2,3-dialdehyde cellulose having two aldehyde functional groups, and covalently bonding an amine group of a polymer having carbon dioxide adsorption capacity to the aldehyde functional group. Polymer with carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, has weight average molecular weight of 800-100000. The weight ratio of biomass with respect to polymer with carbon dioxide adsorption capacity is 1:0.5-2. The addition amount of polymer with carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, is 10-50 mg with respect to 1 g of filler.
   USE - Biomass filler is used for manufacturing polymer matrix composite for carbon dioxide adsorption molded article e.g. wrapping paper, container, sheet and laminate used for food packaging material for adsorbing carbon dioxide generated from food (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) polymer matrix composite, which comprises gas permeable polymer resin and biomass-based filler;
   (2) manufacture of polymer matrix composite, which involves mixing gas permeable matrix resin and biomass filler in the volume ratio of 0.05-1:1; and
   (3) carbon dioxide adsorption molded article, which is prepared using polymer matrix composite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The biomass-based filler is manufactured using biomass which is in powder form. The polymer having carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, is polyethyleneimine. The periodate reagent is treated with 0.5-7 times the amount of glucose in the biomass. The gas permeable matrix resin is a moldable resin. The molded article is recyclable by heating or filling with an inert gas.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q32 (Container/closure types, special packaging features and transit packaging (B65D)); Q33 (Packaging container/closure material (B65D)); Q34 (Types of goods packaged/bottled/bound/labelled/unpacked (B65B, C, D))
MC A03-A05; A03-C02; A05-J07; A08-M10; A08-R07; A08-R08; A11-A03; A12-P01; A12-W09; E11-Q02C; E31-N05C; J01-E02B; J01-E03C; Q32-D01; Q33-B; Q33-C; Q34-C
IP B01J-020/34; B29B-007/92; B29B-009/12; B65D-081/26; C08J-003/20; C08L-001/08; C08L-079/02

   MORIMOTO S

TI Carbon-molecular gasification recycling carbon dioxide gas producing furnace, has purifier that is added to clean gas in carbon dioxide recycling system according to requirement, and small furnace is not provided with outer fire grate.
AU ZHANG J
   CHEN Z
   CHEN C
   CHEN J
   CHEN T
   CHEN K
AE CHEN T (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2022090089
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-molecule gasification recycling carbon dioxide gas producing furnace, wherein the main advantages are as follows: recycling carbon dioxide tail gas to increase carbon and making gas + high-efficient combustion, and it does not need to purify carbon dioxide tail gas, the technique system is simple, it reduces the investment (about half of the existing technology at home and abroad), It reduces the energy consumption (only a few of the domestic and foreign capturing and sealing technology), and the operation cost is very low (about several times of collecting and sealing at home and abroad), so as to scientifically realize the carbon-increasing and gas-generating + high-efficiency combustion circulation, and making the whole system near zero carbon discharge, environment-friendly and great economic benefit. The application surface of the technology is very wide, almost all coal-producing countries have market, almost the fuel and the need to reduce carbon can be used. The technology correctly reduces carbon dioxide, can lead the global scientific to realize carbon neutralization, preventing and controlling climate change.

GA 2021A24720
AB    NOVELTY - Method involves obtaining a meteorological element information of a meteorological station. Friction speed is calculated by a multi-point non-iterative algorithm according to a wind profile line and a full boundary layer flux model. The full boundary layer flux model is determined by a near stratum turbulence characteristic scale and a turbulence characteristic scale on an upper part of a boundary layer. A carbon dioxide concentration gradient is calculated according to a carbon dioxide concentration profile. A turbulence diffusion coefficient is multiplied with different heights by the carbon dioxide concentration gradient to obtain carbon dioxide flux.
   USE - The method is useful for calculating carbon flux.

PN CN111871152-A; CN111871152-B
TI Functionalized ionic liquid used for capturing or separating carbon dioxide in mixed gas, comprises tetraethylenepentamine-5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide salt.
AU LI W
   LIU F
   LI S
   SHEN Y
   SHEN L
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2020B2202H
AB    NOVELTY - A functionalized ionic liquid comprises tetraethylenepentamine-5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide salt (I).
   USE - Functionalized ionic liquid used for capturing or separating carbon dioxide in mixed gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The functionalized ionic liquid has excellent solubility in solvents such as water and alcohol, high efficiency of carbon dioxide absorption capacity, excellent stability and reusability, low regeneration energy consumption and is easy to regenerate when used as carbon dioxide absorbent, and effectively overcomes the defect of organic amines and ionic liquids in absorbing carbon dioxide. The method enables simplified production of the functionalized ionic liquid and is easy to operate and implement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A functionalized ionic liquid comprises tetraethylenepentamine-5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide salt of formula (I). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the functionalized ionic liquid, which involves mixing tetraethylenepentamine, 5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide, ethanol and deionized water, reacting the mixed solution at a constant temperature for 18-30 hours under stirring at a speed of 1000-2000 rpm, removing unreacted raw materials, excess deionized water and ethanol in the ionic liquid by vacuum distillation, and vacuum drying; and
   (2) application of the functionalized ionic liquid, in capturing or separating carbon dioxide in mixed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process of capturing or separating carbon dioxide involves passing the mixed gas comprising carbon dioxide into the solution comprising the functionalized ionic liquid to obtain a saturated carbon dioxide absorption solution, and regenerating the saturated solution by heating at a temperature of 100-120 degrees C for 30-60 minutes. Preferred Property: The molar ratio of tetraethylenepentamine to 5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide is (2:1)-(1:2). Preferred Conditions: The reacting process is carried out at a constant temperature of 20-50 degrees C. The vacuum distillation is carried out at a temperature of 60-80 degrees C for 2-6 hours to constant weight. The vacuum drying is carried out at a temperature of 60-80 degrees C for 12-36 hours. The solution comprising functionalized ionic liquid absorbs carbon dioxide at a temperature of 30-60 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The mixed gas comprises 10-15 vol.% carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN KR2018057947-A
TI Producing supercritical carbon dioxide-mediated carbon nanotube/cobalt oxide nanocomposite used in electrode for supercapacitor, comprises e.g. preparing carbon nanotubes and cobalt precursor in solvent and firing them to prepare solution.
AU SHIM J J
   VAN HOA N
AE UNIV YEUNGNAM RES COOP FOUND (UYYE-C)
GA 2018441856
AB    NOVELTY - Producing supercritical carbon dioxide-mediated carbon nanotube/cobalt oxide nanocomposite, comprises: preparing carbon nanotubes and a cobalt precursor in a solvent; firing them to prepare a solution; loading the prepared solution into a high-pressure reactor and treating with supercritical carbon dioxide; adding the supercritical carbon dioxide-treated high-pressure reactor to an oven and cooling the carbon nanotube nanocomposite to room temperature to synthesize a carbon nanotube nanocomposite immersed with cobalt oxide nanoparticles; recovering the synthesized nanocomposite after decompressing the high-pressure reactor; and washing and drying the recovered nanocomposite.
   USE - The nanocomposite is useful in electrode for supercapacitor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is eco-friendly; and utilizes the supercritical carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the carbon nanotube/cobalt oxide nanocomposite produced the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first step is carried out by firing the carbon nanotube and the cobalt precursor in solvent for 10-20 minutes, where the cobalt precursor comprises cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt acetate 4-hydrate, cobalt chloride hexahydrate, cobalt sulfate heptahydrate and/or cobalt sulfate heptahydrate. The second step is carried out loading the prepared solution into a high-pressure reactor, and pressurizing supercritical carbon dioxide treatment at 30-40 degrees C and pressure of 800-1200 psi. The third step is performed by maintaining the mixture at 140-180 degrees C for 4-8 hours. The fifth step comprises washing the recovered nanocomposite with anhydrous ethanol, and drying the nanocomposite at 50-80 degrees C for 10-14 hours. Preferred Components: The cobalt oxide nanoparticles have average diameter of 3-7 nm. The weight ratio of the carbon nanotubes to the cobalt precursor is 1:0.3-3.
   EXAMPLE - 30 mg Carbon nanotubes and 30 mg cobalt nitrate hexahydrate were added to 5 ml ethanol at a ratio of 1:1 and then fired for 15 minutes to obtain a solution, 25 ml of the solution was loaded into a high-pressure reactor and then carbon dioxide was added at 35 degrees C until it reached 1000 psi. Then, the high-pressure reactor was placed in an oven, maintained at 160 degrees C for 6 hours, and naturally cooled to room temperature to give supercritical carbon dioxide-mediated carbon nanotube/cobalt oxide nanocomposite.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V01 (Resistors and Capacitors)

   CHEN Y

   YAN Y
AE XIAN HONGJUN RUIZE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018413627
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of resin-based carbon dioxide                adsorbent comprises adding D201 macroporous                styrenic anion exchange resin particles into                deionized water, stirring for 2-4 hours, filtering,                soaking into 0.5 mol/L phosphate solution, stirring                while replacing phosphate solution every 2-4 hours,                continuously reacting for 3 times, repeatedly                washing ion-exchanged resin with deionized water                until washing solution is neutral, and oven-drying                for more than 24 hours.
   USE - Method for preparation of resin-based carbon                dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, replaces chlorine ion                with phosphate radical ion which is capable of                absorbing carbon dioxide in dry environment and                desorbing carbon dioxide in moist environment,                realizes adsorbent regeneration at low cost, and                has low raw material cost.

The resin particles and                  phosphate solution have volume ratio of 1:2-1:4.                  Preferred Method: The oven-drying is done at                  25-60 degrees C.

PN CN110317835-A
TI Enhancing methanogenic bacteria to convert carbon dioxide to methane, by obtaining microbial electrolysis system, producing cathode end of microbial electrolysis system, forming potential difference and obtaining methane gas.
AU QI Y
   LV C
   LUN Z
   XU T
   WANG H
   ZHOU X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 201987320N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for enhancing methanogenic bacteria to convert carbon dioxide to methane, involves obtaining microbial electrolysis system, producing cathode end of microbial electrolysis system and target reservoir methane bacteria, forming potential difference between the anode and the cathode of the microbial electrolysis system and obtaining methane gas at the cathode end of microbial electrolysis system.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing methanogenic bacteria to convert carbon dioxide to methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves carbon dioxide reduction rate of methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The anode of the microbial electrolysis system is chosen from carbon cloth, carbon brush, carbon felt, and metal. The cathode of the microbial electrolysis system is a metal conductive material chosen from platinum, iron, aluminum and zinc. Preferred Method: The method involves forming a potential difference between the anode and the cathode of the microbial electrolysis system by solar photovoltaic method and/or providing an external power source. The method involves obtaining dual-chamber microbial electrolytic cell device comprising cell body, an anode, cathode and external circuits. The cell body of dual-chamber microbial electrolysis cell device is cathode-anode cell of an H-type microbial electrolysis cell. The cathode of the double-chamber microbial cell device is provided with gas collecting device, and the methane gas obtained by the gas collecting device is collected. The method further involves analyzing and obtaining methanogenic single bacteria or a flora in the target reservoir. The methanogen comprises Methanomicrobiales, Methanococcus, Methanobacteriales, Methanococcales, and Methanocellales. The carbon monoxide, the carbon brush, and the carbon felt electrode are pretreated before use, and the pretreatment step involves pickling, organic solvent washing and water washing.

TI Extraction of tobacco source spice used for            aromatizing tobacco filter tip involves crushing dried            tobacco source material, carbon dioxide fluid            extracting, separating, desolventizing, dissolving in            ethanol, and mixing oils.
AU DAI Y
   HE A
   CHEN W
AE CHINA TOBACCO HEBEI IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 2020C1388B
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of tobacco source spice comprises                crushing dried tobacco source material, sieving,                putting into extraction kettle, and vacuumizing;                introducing carbon dioxide fluid into extraction                kettle, extracting, adding carbon dioxide                extraction liquid into separating kettle, and                desolventizing; introducing butane entrainer and                carbon dioxide extracting agent into filter residue                in the extracting kettle, extracting, putting                extracting solution into the separating kettle, and                desolventizing; adding ethanol entrainer,                introducing carbon dioxide fluid extracting agent,                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extracting,                separating, and concentrating; adding distilled                water entrainer in filter residue, extracting,                putting extracting solution into the separating                kettle, and desolventizing; and dissolving third                and fourth tobacco extracts in 75% ethanol and                mixing first tobacco essential oil, second tobacco                essential oil, third tobacco absolute oil, and                fourth tobacco absolute oil at volume ratio.
   USE - The method is used for extraction of tobacco                source spice for aromatizing tobacco filter tip                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The spice has low extraction time, is suitable                for the actual need of cigarette product flavoring                and feeding, provides natural spice homolog with                the cigarette, increases tobacco fragrance, reduces                irritation, and improves cigarette quality. The                method has low carbon dioxide critical fluid                boiling point, reduces waste, is simple, has high                safety and high extracting efficiency,                comprehensive extracts fragrance substance, and                avoids the loss of fragrance substance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extraction of tobacco source spice                comprises:
   (A) crushing dried tobacco source material,                sieving by 80-mesh sieve, putting into extraction                kettle, and vacuumizing to - 0.085 MPa, where                tobacco source material is low-order tobacco leaf,                waste tobacco leaf, tobacco powder, and/or tobacco                stalk;
   (B) introducing carbon dioxide fluid into the                extraction kettle, extracting for 1-3 hours at                0.5-5.0 MPa and 25-35 degrees C, adding carbon                dioxide extraction liquid into separating kettle,                and desolventizing at - 0.1 MPa and 60 degrees C,                where mass-volume ratio of tobacco source material                to carbon dioxide fluid is 1:1-6;
   (C) orderly introducing butane entrainer and                carbon dioxide extracting agent into filter residue                in the extracting kettle, extracting at 0.5-5.0 MPa                and 25-35 degrees C for 1-3 hours, putting                extracting solution into the separating kettle, and                desolventizing at 60 degrees C, where mass-volume                ratio of the filter residue and butane is 100:1-5                and mass-volume ratio of e filter residue and                carbon dioxide fluid is 1:1-6;
   (D) adding 75% ethanol entrainer, introducing                carbon dioxide fluid extracting agent,                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extracting at                15-30 MPa and 30-60 degrees C for 1-3 hours,                separating at 8.5 MPa and 60 degrees C, and                concentrating to 1.2-1.4 g/mL;
   (E) adding distilled water entrainer in filter                residue, extracting at 0.5-5.0 MPa and 25-35                degrees C for 1-3 hours, putting extracting                solution into the separating kettle, and                desolventizing at 60 degrees C, where mass-volume                ratio of the filter residue and butane is 100:1-5                and mass-volume ratio of filter residue and carbon                dioxide fluid is 1:1-6; and
   (F) dissolving third and fourth tobacco                extracts in 1-9 volume 75% ethanol and mixing first                tobacco essential oil, second tobacco essential                oil, third tobacco absolute oil, and fourth tobacco                absolute oil at volume ratio of                2.2-4:2.4-4:0.9-4:0.6-2.1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the preparation of the use method of                tobacco source spice comprising diluting tobacco                source spice by 1-10 volume 75% ethanol and                uniformly spraying on cigarette tobacco shred at                dosage of 0.3-0.8 wt.%; diluting prepared tobacco                source spice by 1-5 volume of octanoic acid                glyceride, placing explosion bead with dosage of                1-50 mu L, and aromatizing filter tip; or diluting                tobacco source spice by 1-5 volumes of propylene                glycol, placing in microcapsule with dosage of                10-100 mu L, and perfuming filter tip.

PN CN110110774-A
TI Method for predicting solubility of carbon dioxide in ionic liquids e.g. 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, involves utilizing input variables followed by inputting into fusion models and obtaining solubility values.
AU XIA L
   WANG J
   PAN H
   LIU S
   LI Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201970555M
AB    NOVELTY - A solubility of carbon dioxide prediction method involves selecting temperature (T), pressure (P), critical temperature (Tc), critical pressure (Pc), molecular weight (M) and eccentricity factor (w) as input variables of solubility prediction model of carbon dioxide in ionic liquids, utilizing solubility of carbon dioxide in ionic liquids as output variable of the model, creating a sample data set, dividing the sample data set into training set X1 and test set X2 according to scale, ensuring that the training set covers all ionic liquid types, utilizing the training set X1 to establish artificial neural network sub-model 1, support vector machine sub-model 1 and extreme learning machine sub-model 1 followed by calculating weight coefficient of each sub-model, establishing a first linear fusion model, establishing a second linear fusion model, imputing input variables into the first and second linear fusion models and obtaining solubility values of carbon dioxide in ionic liquids.
   USE - Method for predicting solubility of carbon dioxide in ionic liquids e.g. 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium ethyl sulfate, 1-hexyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluormethylsulfonyl)imide, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium trifluoromethanesulfonate, 1-hexyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, 1-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate- methylsulfate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and 1-hexyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables predicting solubility of carbon dioxide with high accuracy by providing effective and feasible modeling method for predicting physical and chemical parameters of materials and providing important guidance for evaluating and screening highly selective ionic liquids.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A solubility of carbon dioxide prediction method involves selecting temperature (T), pressure (P), critical temperature (Tc), critical pressure (Pc), molecular weight (M) and eccentricity factor (w) as input variables of solubility prediction model of carbon dioxide in ionic liquids, utilizing solubility of carbon dioxide in ionic liquids as output variable of the model, creating a sample data set, dividing the sample data set into training set X1 and test set X2 according to scale, ensuring that the training set covers all ionic liquid types, utilizing the training set X1 to establish artificial neural network sub-model 1, support vector machine sub-model 1 and extreme learning machine sub-model 1, calculating weight coefficient of each sub-model using method of minimizing error sum of squares, establishing a first linear fusion model, calculating weight coefficients of each sub-model by information entropy method, establishing a second linear fusion model, testing predicted performance of the linear fusion models using test set X2, imputing the temperature (T), pressure (P), critical temperature (Tc), critical pressure (Pc), molecular weight (M) and eccentricity factor (w) into the first and second linear fusion models and obtaining solubility values of carbon dioxide in ionic liquids.

AE FUJIAN BILLION POLYMERIZATION FIBER TECH (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2022244552
AB    NOVELTY - Performing waterless printing process involves                grinding disperse dyes, adding into dye kettle,                injecting supercritical fluid carbon dioxide into                dye kettle, heating up to 45-55degrees Celsius,                pressuring in dye kettle is 2.5-3.5 megapascal,                keeping temperature for 30-40 minutes. All disperse                dyes are dissolved in dye kettle. In supercritical                fluid carbon dioxide, dyeing solvent is made. After                fabric is pretreated, fabric is wound on first                roll, beginning of fabric is unrolled and wound on                second roll, and second roll drives movement of                fabric. The dyeing solvent in dye kettle is                injected into digital inkjet printing machine on                dyeing device through pressurized pump, and nozzles                on digital inkjet printing machine are used to                spray on flat fabric for printing and dyeing.
   USE - Method for performing waterless printing                process used for polyester fiber fabrics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing waterless printing process involves                grinding disperse dyes, adding into dye kettle,                injecting supercritical fluid carbon dioxide into                dye kettle, heating up to 45-55degrees Celsius,                pressuring in dye kettle is 2.5-3.5 megapascal,                keeping temperature for 30-40 minutes. All disperse                dyes are dissolved in dye kettle. In supercritical                fluid carbon dioxide, dyeing solvent is made. After                fabric is pretreated, fabric is wound on first                roll, beginning of fabric is unrolled and wound on                second roll, and second roll drives movement of                fabric. The dyeing solvent in dye kettle is                injected into digital inkjet printing machine on                dyeing device through pressurized pump, and nozzles                on digital inkjet printing machine are used to                spray on flat fabric for printing and dyeing. The                supercritical fluid carbon dioxide after sprayed                through nozzle, is quickly vaporized, so that                disperse dye is fixed inside fiber, and vaporized                carbon dioxide is output through ventilation hole                on dyeing device, and is recovered by recovery                mechanism to complete printing. The recovery                mechanism comprises recovery kettle connected to                vent hole for purifying recovered gas, and                transported to condenser through pressurized pump,                re-pressurized and cooled carbon dioxide of gas to                liquid, and transported and stored in outlet                storage tank. The storage tank is connected with                dye kettle, so that supercritical fluid carbon                dioxide is injected into dye kettle.

The dispersant is sodium lignin                  sulfate.

TI Gas-making apparatus for gas-making system for manufacturing generated gas containing carbon monoxide has source gas and reducing-gas supply units and reaction unit which can switch between source gas and reducing gas supplied to reactor.

AE SEKISUI CHEM IND CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 202135720X

When predetermined amount of source gas is supplied to reactor, or when conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide falls below a predetermined value, the gas is supplied to each of the reactors, and it is configured to switch between source gas and reducing gas.
   USE - A gas-making apparatus for gas-making system for manufacturing generated gas containing carbon monoxide by contacting source gas containing carbon dioxide and reducing agent containing metal oxide for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Generated gas containing carbon monoxide can be efficiently manufactured continuously from the source gas containing a carbon dioxide. This advantage can be improved more by adjusting a switching timing with the source gas and the reducing gas which are supplied to a reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) gas-making system comprising source gas production unit for producing source gas containing carbon dioxide, and the gas-making apparatus connected to the source gas production unit through the source gas supply unit; and
   (2) gas-making method which involves preparing reactor which has reducing agent inside, contacting source gas containing carbon dioxide with reducing gas containing a reducing substance which reduces carbon dioxide by switching the reactor that supplies source gas and reducing gas, converting carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, reducing oxidized reducing agent, merging gas after passing the reactor to generate mixed gas, and recovering mixed gas as generated gas or purifying mixed gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the gas-making system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   Connection unit (2)
   Reducing-gas supply unit (3)

PN CN111218403-A; CN111218403-B
TI Utilizing totally enclosed cell culture system for fully enclosed cell culture involves setting the amount of infusion and injecting culture medium into the culture tank, presetting the concentration values of oxygen and carbon dioxide.
AU YU X
   XU P
   FANG Y
   LI L
   LIANG H
AE HUADAO SHANGHAI CAR-TCELL MEDICINE CO (HUAD-Non-standard)
GA 202051441B
AB    NOVELTY - Utilizing totally enclosed cell culture system for fully enclosed cell culture involves setting the amount of infusion and injecting culture medium into the culture tank, presetting the concentration values of oxygen and carbon dioxide, inject oxygen and carbon dioxide into the incubator separately, and measuring the gas concentration values of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the incubator in real time, comparing with the set value respectively, when the oxygen and carbon dioxide values in the incubator reach the standard, inject the gas in the incubator into the incubator and setting the incubator temperature and preheat the incubator. The cells, factors are injected, start agitator, inject culture medium into culture tank, filter metabolic products and discharge waste liquid during continuous cultivation. After the cell culture is completed, the medium is first replaced with physiological saline.
   USE - Method for utilizing totally enclosed cell culture system for fully enclosed cell culture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to utilize totally enclosed cell culture system for fully enclosed cell culture, where equipment culture can greatly save labor costs and significantly improve the culture efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Utilizing totally enclosed cell culture system for fully enclosed cell culture involves setting the amount of infusion and injecting culture medium into the culture tank, presetting the concentration values of oxygen and carbon dioxide, inject oxygen and carbon dioxide into the incubator separately, and measuring the gas concentration values of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the incubator in real time, comparing with the set value respectively, when the oxygen and carbon dioxide values in the incubator reach the standard, inject the gas in the incubator into the incubator and setting the incubator temperature and preheat the incubator. The cells, factors are injected, start agitator, inject culture medium into culture tank, filter metabolic products and discharge waste liquid during continuous cultivation. After the cell culture is completed, the medium is first replaced with physiological saline. After the replacement, the medium is concentrated and the waste liquid is continuously discharged to reduce the volume of the liquid in the culture tank. The agitator is stopped and recover the finished cells in the culture tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The totally enclosed cell culture system includes an incubator, cultivation tank, used for cell culture, set in the incubator. The culture tank is provided with a stirrer, and the stirrer is provided in the culture tank. A gas flow component communicates with the incubator and used for adjusting the oxygen and carbon dioxide concentrations in the incubator. The liquid flow component communicates with the culture tank and is used to adjust the liquid flow rate in the culture tank, filtering the metabolic products produced by cell culture and recovering the finished cells. A temperature regulating component adjusts the temperature in the incubator. A central controller connects and controls the air flow component, the liquid flow component and the temperature adjustment component. The airflow component comprises separate air passages, carbon dioxide passages and oxygen passages are respectively communicated with the incubator and used to transport gas into the incubator to form a mixed gas. A mixed gas suction passage connecting the incubator and the incubation tank are provided for inputting the mixed gas in the incubator into the incubation tank. Exhaust gas discharge channel communicates with the culture tank, is used to discharge the waste gas generated in the process of cell culture. Gas concentration sensing module includes oxygen gas concentration sensor and carbon dioxide gas concentration sensor. The gas concentration sensing module provides detection information to the central controller. The carbon dioxide pathway includes a carbon dioxide storage device, a carbon dioxide pressure relief valve, a carbon dioxide pathway switch, and a carbon dioxide pipeline. The carbon dioxide storage device is connected to the carbon dioxide pipeline, and the carbon dioxide pipeline is driven by a carbon dioxide pressure reducing valve. The carbon dioxide pipeline communicates with the incubator. A carbon dioxide path switch is provided on the carbon dioxide path, and the carbon dioxide path switch is controlled by the central controller. The oxygen passage includes an oxygen storage device, an oxygen pressure relief valve, an oxygen passage switch, and an oxygen pipeline. The oxygen storage device is connected to the oxygen pipeline. The oxygen pipeline is driven by an oxygen pressure reducing valve, and the oxygen pipeline communicates with the incubator. An oxygen path switch is provided on the oxygen path, and the oxygen path switch is controlled by the central controller. The mixed gas suction passage includes a mixed gas suction pump and a mixed gas suction line. The mixed gas suction line is driven by the mixed gas suction pump. The mixed gas suction line is used to inject the mixed gas from the incubator into the culture tank. The mixed gas suction pump is controlled by the central controller. The incubator is provided with a gas discharge path for discharging the gas in the box and keeping the air pressure in the box stable. The carbon dioxide passage and the oxygen passage are provided with a carbon dioxide passage incubator inlet and an oxygen passage incubator inlet on the incubator. The carbon dioxide pathway incubator inlet and the oxygen pathway incubator inlet are both located in the upper portion of the incubator. The mixed gas passage incubator outlet is provided on the incubator. The mixed gas passage incubator outlet, oxygen gas concentration sensor and carbon dioxide gas concentration sensor are provided in the lower portion of the incubator. A fan is provided in the incubator for agitating the airflow, accelerating the mixing, making the gas mixing more uniform, and accelerating the heat exchange inside the incubator. The central controller includes gas concentration comparison unit to compare the real-time carbon dioxide gas concentration value and the real-time oxygen gas concentration value sent by the gas concentration sensing module with the preset carbon dioxide concentration value and the preset oxygen gas concentration value. According to formulas C CO2= C 0CO2-C tCO2, C O2=C 0O2-C tO2, required concentration difference is obtained, that is, the concentration difference and the gas concentration switch control unit used to control the opening and closing of the carbon dioxide channel, oxygen channel, and mixed gas suction channel, according to the value of, adjust the on and off time of the oxygen channel. According to the value of, adjust the on-off time of the carbon dioxide pathway, when both and satisfy the set threshold range, open the mixed gas suction channel and suck the gas in the incubator into the incubator, when at least one of and does not satisfy the set threshold range, the mixed gas suction passage is closed. The flow component comprises inlet channel, circulation channel, waste channel and recovery channel. The liquid inlet passage and the circulation passage communicate with the culture tank. The liquid inlet channel is used to feed liquid into the culture tank, and the circulation channel is used to filter metabolic products produced by cells. The waste liquid passage communicates with the circulation passage for discharging the metabolic products, and the recovery passage communicates with the culture tank for recovering the finished cells. The flow control components of the liquid inlet path, circulation path, waste liquid path and recovery path are controlled by the central controller. The liquid inlet channel includes a liquid storage bag, a liquid inlet pipeline and a liquid inlet pump. The liquid storage bag is in communication with the liquid inlet line. The liquid inlet pipe is driven by the liquid inlet pump, and the liquid inlet pipe communicates with the culture tank. The liquid inlet pump is controlled by the central controller. The circulation channel includes a circulation pipeline, a circulation pump and a filter. The filter is provided on the circulation line and communicates with the circulation line. The circulation pump is controlled by the central controller. The waste liquid passage includes a waste liquid pump, a waste liquid barrel and a waste liquid pipeline. The waste liquid pipeline communicates with the circulation path, and the waste liquid barrel is connected with the waste liquid pipeline. The waste liquid pipeline is driven by the waste liquid pump , and the waste liquid pump is controlled by the central controller. The recovery channel includes a recovery pipeline, a recovery pump and a recovery bag. The recovery line communicates with the cultivation tank, and the recovery line is driven by the recovery pump. The recovery bag is in communication with the recovery line, and the recovery pump is controlled by the central controller. The liquid flow assembly includes a weighing sensor, which is used to determine the real-time culture tank weight, is located at the bottom of the culture tank and provides detection information to the central controller. The temperature adjustment assembly includes a heating device and a temperature sensor. The heating device and the temperature sensor are provided in the incubator. The temperature sensor is used to measure the real-time temperature value in the incubator and provide detection information to the central controller. The heating device is controlled by the central controller. The agitator includes a middle shaft, at least two blades that are rotationally symmetrical about the axis of the central shaft and connected to the central shaft, and each of the blades includes a blade body. The blade body extends in a spiral shape, and the axial length of the blade body accounts for 20% to 35% of the length of the central shaft. The rotation angle of the blade body is 15 degrees to 50 degrees , the maximum radial length is 20 mm to 54 mm, and the radial length gradually decreases from the bottom to the top. The method uses the oxygen channel and the carbon dioxide channel of the totally enclosed cell culture system to inject oxygen and carbon dioxide into the incubator. The real-time oxygen gas concentration value CtO2 and the real-time carbon dioxide gas concentration value CtCO2, compare with the preset oxygen gas concentration value C0O2 and the preset carbon dioxide concentration value C0CO2 respectively, and obtain the difference between the required concentrations according to formulas CO2=C 002-C tO2 and C CO2=C OCO2-C tCO2, when both CO2 and CCO2 meet the set threshold range, open the mixed gas suction channel to suck the gas in the incubator into the culture tank, when at least one of CO2 and CCO2 does not satisfy the set threshold range, the mixed gas suction passage is closed. According to the value of CO2, the on-off time of the oxygen channel is adjusted in a stepwise manner, and/or, according to the value of CCO2, the on-off time of the carbon dioxide path switch is adjusted in a way of the staged control. The method uses a circulation path of a totally closed cell culture system to filter metabolic products, and a filter and a circulation pump are provided on the circulation path. The circulating pump is also reversely operated to recover the finished cells in the circulating path.

GA 202222126D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon                dioxide involves contacting, grinding reaction raw                material gas and catalyst to making reaction raw                material gas, and reacting under action of catalyst                to obtain methanol.
   USE - Method for producing methanol by hydrogenation                of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce methanol by                using mechanical grinding and a common catalyst to                carry out catalytic reaction, which significantly                reduces the reaction temperature, improves the                conversion rate of carbon dioxide and the                selectivity of methanol, and prolongs the service                life of the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction raw                  material gas includes hydrogen and carbon dioxide                  gas. The catalyst is a carbon dioxide                  hydrogenation catalyst for methanol production.                  Preferred Compositions: The added amount of the                  catalyst is 10-20 wt.% based on the total volume                  of the reaction vessel. Preferred Process: The                  method for preparing methanol specifically                  involves replacing air in the reaction container                  with reaction raw material gas, introducing                  continuously the reaction raw material gas into                  the reaction container until the reaction                  container reaches a preset reaction pressure,                  sealing the reaction container, and placing the                  sealed reaction container on a grinding device                  for grinding reaction by heating the reaction                  vessel to a preset reaction temperature to obtain                  the methanol, where the preset reaction pressure                  is 0.1-2 aqueous-soluble, the preset reaction                  temperature is 60-120degrees Celsius, the                  grinding speed is 100-1000 revolutions per                  minute, and the reaction time is 0.5-24                  hours.

TI Method of performing catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to cyclic carbonate at atmospheric pressure, involves performing reaction by using tetrabutylammonium halide as catalyst, and using organic amine as solvent and co-catalyst.
AU LI L
   CUI C
   WANG R
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 201820945R
AB    NOVELTY - The method of performing catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to cyclic carbonate at atmospheric pressure, involves performing reaction by using tetrabutylammonium halide as a catalyst, and using organic amine as a solvent and a co-catalyst.
   USE - Method of performing catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to cyclic carbonate at atmospheric pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes high-efficiency catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic carbonate under normal pressure conditions with mild catalytic reaction conditions and high yield while conforming to actual production requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The tetrabutylammonium halide is composed of tetrabutylammonium cation and halogen anion. The organic amine is a tertiary amine containing an alkyl chain. Preferred Conditions: The reaction is carried out under the pressure of 0.1 MPa at a reaction temperature of 25-100 degrees C for 1-24 hours.

PN WO2021146678-A1
TI Producing cannabinoid-containing crystals involves exposing cannabigerolic acid-containing plant material to fluid in supercritical state to extract components of plant material, and coalescing fraction of extracted components.
AU MCMICHAEL C K
   GALPIN J A G
AE CALDERA GROUP LLC (CALD-Non-standard)
GA 2021838640
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) cannabinoid-containing crystals comprises providing a cannabigerolic acid (CBGA)-containing plant material that comprises at least 2 wt.% CBGA, exposing the CBGA-containing plant material to a fluid in a supercritical state to extract components of the CBGA-containing plant material, and coalescing a fraction of the extracted components, where the coalesced fraction of the extracted components including cannabinoid-containing crystals containing at least one of CBGA and a cannabinoid derivative of CBGA from the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing cannabinoid-containing crystals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing (M2) cannabinoid-containing crystals, comprising placing CBGA-containing plant material that comprises 2 wt.% CBGA into an extractor, providing carbon dioxide in a subcritical liquid state to the extractor containing the CBGA-containing plant material, transforming the carbon dioxide within the extractor from the subcritical state to a supercritical state to extract components from the CBGA-containing plant material, flowing the carbon dioxide containing the extracted components of the CBGA-containing plant material to a first separator, decreasing a pressure of the carbon dioxide flowed into the first separator to coalesce a fraction of the extracted components from the carbon dioxide, where the coalesced fraction of the extracted components including cannabinoid-containing crystals containing at least one of CBGA and a cannabinoid derivative of CBGA, flowing the carbon dioxide containing an uncoalesced fraction of the extracted components out of the first separator, and collecting the cannabinoid-containing crystals from the first separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further comprises crystallizing CBGA within the fluid while in the supercritical state. The step of exposing the CBGA-containing plant material to the fluid in the supercritical state comprises exposing the CBGA-containing plant material to the fluid in a subcritical liquid state and transforming the fluid to a supercritical state. In the method (M1), transforming the fluid to a supercritical state comprises increasing the pressure of the fluid, or increasing the temperature of the fluid. The method (M1) further comprises increasing a temperature of the fluid over time while the fluid is in the subcritical liquid state, increasing a temperature of the fluid over time while the fluid transforms to the supercritical state, and increasing a temperature of the fluid over time while the fluid is in the supercritical state. The step of coalescing a fraction of the extracted components comprises reducing a pressure of the fluid, or transforming the fluid from the supercritical state to a subcritical state. The method (M1) further comprises chemically converting CBGA extracted from the CBGA-containing plant material to at least one cannabinoid derivatives of CBGA, where the coalesced cannabinoid-containing crystals contain the cannabinoid derivatives converted from the CBGA extracted from the CBGA-containing plant material. In the method (M1), chemically converting the extracted CBGA from the CBGA-containing plant material to at least one cannabinoid derivatives of CBGA includes reacting the extracted CBGA with a cannabinoid synthase in the fluid. The cannabinoid-containing crystals coalesced from the fluid at a first time have a first cannabinoid concentration ratio and the cannabinoid-containing crystals coalesced from the fluid at a second time, which is later than the first time, have a second cannabinoid concentration ratio, which is different from the first cannabinoid concentration ratio. The first cannabinoid concentration ratio has a higher concentration of CBGA than the second cannabinoid concentration ratio. The method (M1) further comprises segregating the cannabinoid-containing crystals coalesced at the first time from the cannabinoid-containing crystals coalesced at the second time. In the method (M2), the step of transforming the carbon dioxide within the extractor from the subcritical state to a supercritical state includes increasing a pressure of the carbon dioxide within the extractor, increasing a temperature of the carbon dioxide over time, increasing a temperature of the carbon dioxide over time while the carbon dioxide is in the subcritical liquid state, increasing a temperature of the carbon dioxide over time while the carbon dioxide transforms to the supercritical state, or increasing a temperature of the carbon dioxide over time while the carbon dioxide is in the supercritical state. The CBGA crystals are formed within the extractor. The step of decreasing a pressure of the carbon dioxide flowed into the first separator includes decreasing a temperature of the carbon dioxide flowed into the first separator, or transforming the carbon dioxide flowed into the first separator from the supercritical state to a subcritical liquid state. The method (M2) further comprises chemically converting CBGA extracted from the CBGA-containing plant material to cannabinoid derivatives of CBGA, where the coalesced cannabinoid-containing crystals contain the cannabinoid derivatives converted from the CBGA extracted from the CBGA-containing plant material. The cannabinoid-containing crystals collected from the first separator at a first time have a first cannabinoid concentration ratio and cannabinoid-containing crystals collected from the first separator at a second time, which is later than the first time, have a second cannabinoid concentration ratio, which is different from the first cannabinoid concentration ratio. The first cannabinoid concentration ratio has a higher concentration of CBGA than the second cannabinoid concentration ratio. The method (M2) further comprises segregating the cannabinoid-containing crystals collected at the first time from the cannabinoid-containing crystals collected at the second time. Preferred Components: The CBGA-containing plant material comprises cannabis, or industrial hemp. The CBGA-containing plant material comprises at least 5 wt.%, preferably 8 wt.% CBGA. The coalesced cannabinoid-containing crystals include at least 50 wt.% CBGA, preferably at least 90 wt.% CBGA. The coalesced cannabinoid-containing crystals collected from the first separator includes cannabinoid-containing crystals containing at least 50 wt.%, preferably 90 wt.% CBGA.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113013422-A
TI Preparation method of metal-air battery positive electrode film for adsorbing carbon dioxide, involves rolling current collector after spraying, and placing in argon atmosphere furnace for sintering to obtain metal-air battery cathode film.
AU WU J
   HU J
   CHEN D
   LIN Z
AE CAERULEUM GUANGDONG NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOG (CAER-Non-standard)
GA 202176021P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing the carbon dioxide absorbent with the solvent and stirring to obtain the absorption liquid. PTFE is added to the absorption liquid and stirred to obtain an emulsion. The emulsion is sprayed on one side of the current collector, and the catalyst is sprayed on the other side of the current collector. The current collector is rolled after spraying, and then placed it in an argon atmosphere furnace for sintering to obtain a metal-air battery cathode film. The carbon dioxide absorbent is selected from one of calcium oxide, sodium peroxide or soda lime. The solvent is selected from one of absolute ethanol, isopropanol, or acetone. The catalyst is a manganese lanthanum series catalyst.
   USE - Preparation method of metal-air battery positive electrode film for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-air battery anode film has high porosity and good hydrophobicity, and has carbon dioxide capture ability, effectively isolates carbon dioxide from entering the battery to react with the electrolyte, reduces the occurrence of carbonation, reduces the content of carbonate in the electrolyte, slows down the electrolyte aging. The method can improve the secondary starting performance of the metal-air battery and prolong the service life of the metal-air battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a metal-air battery positive electrode film for adsorbing carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram illustrating the results of the hydrophobicity test of the metal-air battery cathode film.

   Catalyst structure for carbon dioxide reduction (1)
   Carrier (10)
   Channel (11)
   Pores (11a)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Enlarged diameter portion connects the pores which comprise the one-dimensional pore, the two-dimensional pore, or the three-dimensional pores. The catalyst structure further comprises greater than or equal to 1 other metal complex held at the outer surface of the carrier. Amount of greater than or equal to 1 metal complex inherent in the carrier is greater than amount of greater than or equal to 1 other metal complex held at the outer surface of the carrier. Zeolite-type compound is a silicate compound. Metal complex is a complex containing nickel (Ni), ruthenium (Ru), or rhenium (Re). Metal complex is Ru-Re supramolecular complex or Ru-Re porous coordination polymer. Catalyst structure is dispersed in a dispersion medium. Preferred Properties: Average particle diameter of metal complex is larger than average bore diameter of channel, and smaller than internal diameter of enlarged diameter portion. Average bore diameter of channel is 0.1-1.5 nm, and internal diameter of enlarged diameter portion is 0.5-50 nm. Preferred Processes: Before sintering process, 50-500 mass% nonionic surfactants are added with respect to precursor material (A). Precursor material (A) is made to impregnate the metal-containing solution by dividing metal-containing solution multiple times, and adding to the precursor material (A) before sintering process. When making the precursor material (A) impregnate the metal-containing solution before the sintering process, the amount of metal-containing solution to be added to precursor material (A) and the amount of metallic element (M) contained in the metal-containing solution to be added to precursor (A) are adjusted to be 10-1000 in terms of atomic ratio of silicon constituting precursor material (A) to metallic element (M). In the hydrothermal treatment process, mixed solution of precursor material (B) and structure-defining agent is hydrothermally treated. Preferred Condition: Hydrothermal treatment process is performed in basic atmosphere.

TI Carbonic acid engine e.g. gasoline engine, pumps carbon dioxide from engine into steam portion of secondary tank which is comprised with lower temperature than primary tank, in specific stroke.
AU STRUNK H
   CONRAD M
AE STRUNK H (STRU-Individual)
   CONRAD M (CONR-Individual)
GA 201816241C
AB    NOVELTY - The carbonic acid engine has expansion tubes whose end portion is closed except for a lockable vent. The engine cooling and cooling of expansion tubes allow the pressure and temperature at the end of the second cycle to be lower than in the tank and in near the saturation line. The carbon dioxide is pumped from the engine into the steam portion of a secondary tank which has a lower temperature than the primary tank, in the fourth stroke, with the provision that the secondary tank is cooled, after the expansion stroke in the third cycle.
   USE - Carbonic acid engine such as gasoline engine and diesel engine.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the carbon dioxide engine requires no internal combustion and no external combustion for operation, the heat must enter engine in a different way. The carbon dioxide-filled engine can also burn with pure oxygen. Hence, the carbon dioxide in the engine is split into carbon monoxide and oxygen or Carbon for which the pressure from the compression and also the heat supplied can be used. The process can be promoted through the external supply of pure oxygen, in which case, the gases leaving the engine are either mixed with pure hydrogen to form methanol or fed to an after burning with the provision that the carbon dioxide resulting is returned to the engine. The fuels are supplied through the direct injection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbonic acid engine has a primary tank that introduces warm carbon dioxide from the steam portion at the beginning of the first cycle in a four-stroke reciprocating engine through an inlet valve.
