PN WO2021244872-A1
TI Producing hydrogen and/or methane by absorption of carbon dioxide involves e.g. introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen-producing bacteria, acetogenic bacteria and methanogenic microorganisms in first, second and third reactors respectively.
AU GALIANO M
   REVERSO R
AE BIOREWEAL SRL (BIOR-Non-standard)
   BIORENOVA SPA (BIOR-Non-standard)
GA 2021E0289B
AB    NOVELTY - Biological production of hydrogen and/or methane by absorption and biological conversion of carbon dioxide, involves: (i) introducing carbon dioxide in first reactor containing up to 95 vol.% first culture medium comprising hydrogen-producing bacteria and continuous stirring in anaerobic conditions until stationary phase of growth of hydrogen-producing bacteria is achieved to get first fermented culture medium and gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide; (ii) optionally separating hydrogen from gaseous mixture; and (iii) e.g. introducing gaseous mixture in (a) second reactor comprising up to 95 vol.% second culture medium comprising acetogenic bacteria and continuous stirring in anaerobic conditions to get second fermented culture medium and hydrogen and (b) third reactor comprising up to 95 vol.% third culture medium comprising methanogenic microorganism and continuous stirring in anaerobic conditions to get third fermented culture medium and methane.
   USE - The process is useful for biological production of hydrogen and/or methane by absorption and biological conversion of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: facilitates the generation of hydrogen at relatively low costs; has reduced energy consumption, is convenient and sustainable from an economic and environmental point of view; provides methane with a higher yield and efficiency having a high degree of purity by reducing content of unwanted gases as compared to conventional processes; and is highly reliable and flexible in application, is relatively easy to provide and has competitive costs and almost no process waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biological production of hydrogen and/or methane by absorption and biological conversion of carbon dioxide, comprises: (i) introducing carbon dioxide in at least one first reactor containing up to 95 vol.% first culture medium comprising at least one hydrogen-producing bacteria and keeping under continuous stirring in anaerobic conditions until a stationary phase of the growth of the hydrogen-producing bacteria is achieved, obtaining a first fermented culture medium and a gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide, where the hydrogen-producing bacteria comprises Clostridium beijerinckii, Clostridium butyricum, Clostridium bifermentans, Clostridium sporogenes, Rhodobacter sphaeroides, Rhodobacter capsulatus, Enterobacter cloacae, Thermotoga neapolitana or Hungateiclostridium thermocellum; (ii) optionally separating the hydrogen from the gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide; and (iii) introducing the gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide in at least one of (a) at least one second reactor comprising up to 95 vol.% second culture medium comprising at least one acetogenic bacteria and keeping under continuous stirring in anaerobic conditions, obtaining a second fermented culture medium and hydrogen, and (b) at least one third reactor comprising up to 95 vol.% third culture medium comprising at least one methanogenic microorganisms and keeping under continuous stirring in anaerobic conditions, obtaining a third fermented culture medium and a gaseous mixture comprising methane, or introducing the residual carbon dioxide separated from the hydrogen in step (ii) in the at least one second reactor which comprises up to 95 vol.% second culture medium comprising at least one acetogenic bacteria and keeping under continuous stirring in anaerobic conditions, obtaining a second fermented culture medium, where the acetogenic bacteria comprises Acetoanaerobium noterae, Acetoanaerobium pronyense, Acetoanaerobium sticklandii, Acetobacterium carbinolicum, Moorella thermoacetica, Butyribacterium methylotrophicum, Eubacterium limosum, Moorella thermoautotrophica, Desulfosporosinus orientis or Blautia producta and the methanogenic microorganisms comprises Methanolacinia paynteri, Methanothermobacter wolfeii, Methanothermobacter thermautotrophicus, Methanothermobacter marburgensis, Methanosarcina barkeri, Methanosarcina mazei, Methanobacterium bryantii, Methanothermobacter tenebrarum or Methanosarcina thermophila.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (i), the operating temperature and pressure of the first reactor are respectively lower than 40 degrees C and lower than 250 kPa. In step (iii): the operating temperature and pressure of the second reactor are respectively lower than 39 degrees C, preferably lower than 75 degrees and lower than 250 kPa, preferably lower than 500 kPa; the gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide obtained in step (i) is introduced in at least one between the second reactor and the third reactor; and the introduction of the gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide in at least one between the second reactor and the third reactor occurs, preferably continuously by injecting the gaseous mixture into the second culture medium and/or into the third culture medium. After reaching the stationary growth phase of the hydrogen-producing bacteria step (i) comprises the additional steps of (ia) drawing the gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide from the head space of the at least one first reactor, (ib) unloading from the first reactor a volume of the first fermented culture medium until a concentration of the hydrogen-producing bacteria in the first fermented culture medium of no less than 2 g/l is reached, (ic) loading inside the first reactor a quantity by volume of the first culture medium that is equal to the volume of the first fermented culture medium unloaded in step (ib) and (id) restarting the growth of the hydrogen-producing bacteria until the stationary growth phase of the hydrogen-producing bacteria is reached and repeating steps (ia)-(ic). The process further comprises: the step of (ib') separating the first fermented culture medium unloaded in step (ib) into a liquid component and a solid component; (iv) introducing the gaseous mixture comprising methane obtained in step (iii) in at least one additional second reactor which comprises up to 95 vol.% the culture medium and continuous stirring in anaerobic conditions, obtaining a fermented culture medium and methane; the step of separating the second fermented culture medium and/or the third fermented culture medium into a liquid component and a solid component; and the step of introducing in the second reactor and/or in the third reactor carbon dioxide that originates from sources which are external with respect to the one that originates from step (i). The gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide obtained in step (i) is drawn from the first reactor and stored in at least one accumulation tank. The step of (ii) separating the hydrogen from the gaseous mixture of hydrogen and residual carbon dioxide obtained in step (i), where in step (iii), the residual carbon dioxide separated from the hydrogen in step (ii) is introduced in the second reactor. In step (iv), the operating pressure and temperature of the additional second reactor are respectively lower than 39 degrees C and lower than 250 kPa.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Low-power integrated process-based carbon dioxide mineralization apparatus for removing greenhouse gas/acid gas, has mineralization unit that discharges solid phase containing divalent metal carbonate crystallized by solid-liquid separation.
AU LEE W H
   JUNG S
   BAK K T
   YOUN M
   KIM E Y
   KO Y N
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202162600K
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has electrolysis unit (15) which is provided for generating and discharging chlorine gas and an aqueous alkali metal hydroxide solution by electrolyzing brine. An extraction unit (40) is provided for receiving an aqueous hydrochloric acid solution and inorganic waste containing a divalent metal and discharging an extraction solution containing a divalent metal chloride. The alkali metal hydroxide aqueous solution discharged from the electrolysis unit is branched. The first branch liquid and the gas to be treated containing carbon dioxide and the extraction solution are supplied to produce a crystalline divalent metal carbonate. A mineralization unit (25) is provided for discharging a solid phase containing a divalent metal carbonate crystallized by solid-liquid separation and a residual liquid phase containing an alkali metal chloride.
   USE - Low-power integrated process-based carbon dioxide mineralization apparatus for removing greenhouse gas/acid gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The excellent greenhouse gas/acid gas removal efficiency is achieved, and the generation of waste liquid is minimized, and the excellent process stability is achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide absorption unit (80) is provided for receiving the first treatment target gas containing the alkali metal hydroxide aqueous solution and carbon dioxide discharged from the electrolysis unit and discharging the alkali metal carbonate aqueous solution. A carbon dioxide mineralization apparatus is provided with mineralization unit. The second branch liquid is supplied to the electrode solution of the cathode of the electrolysis unit. The aqueous alkali metal hydroxide solution discharged from the electrolysis unit is mixed with the aqueous alkali metal bicarbonate solution remaining in the mineralization unit. The carbon dioxide mineralizer is fed to the absorber. A gas-liquid separator (5) is provided for separating and discharging the liquid phase of the aqueous alkali chloride solution. The residual alkali chloride aqueous solution discharged from the gas-liquid separator is re-supplied to the electrode solution of the anode of the electrolysis unit. The pH of the aqueous alkali metal carbonate solution discharged from the carbon dioxide absorber is 11.0-12.5. An electrolysis unit is provided to receive brine supplied to the anode side and oxygen-consuming reduction. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon dioxide mineralization method, which involves reacting chlorine produced in the electrolysis step with portion of the aqueous alkali metal hydroxide solution produced in the electrolysis step or an aqueous alkali metal hydroxide solution supplied from outside. The electrode solution containing water is supplied with the brine to the anode side. The mixture of chlorine and residual aqueous alkali chloride solution produced in the electrolysis step is separated by gas-liquid separation, and the separated residual aqueous alkali chloride solution is mixed with the concentrated solution from which divalent metal ions are removed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the low-power integrated process-based carbon dioxide mineralization apparatus.

   Carbon dioxide mineralization unit (25)

PN CN113583111-A; CN113583111-B
TI Extracting phycocyanin useful in field of deep processing of natural products, comprises treating material to be treated e.g. algae containing phycocyanin with high-pressure carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide reactor, and separating supernatant to obtain phycocyanin.
AU MI L
   XU Z
AE HENAN ACAD AGRIC SCI QUALITY STANDARD (CAGS-C)
GA 2021C5505W
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting phycocyanin comprises treating the material to be treated with high-pressure carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide reactor at a pressure of 2-15 MPa for 5-45 minutes, and separating the supernatant to obtain phycocyanin, where the material to be treated is algae containing phycocyanin.
   USE - The method for extracting phycocyanin is useful in field of deep processing of natural products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the high pressure, anaerobic, and blasting effects of high pressure carbon dioxide to break cells, extract phycocyanin, and separate and take the supernatant to obtain a high-quality phycocyanin aqueous solution, which can be directly used for subsequent processing, has a certain selectivity for cell wall breaking, and the phycocyanin aqueous solution obtained has a higher purity, can be suitable for the extraction of phycocyanin from other algae materials, has simple operation, short extraction time, good repeatability, energy saving and environmental protection, good extraction quality, industrial production, and the first application of high-pressure carbon dioxide sterilization technology to protein extraction provides a scientific basis for the extraction research and industrial production of high value-added natural pigments.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The processing temperature of the high-pressure carbon dioxide is 25-35degrees Celsius. The processing pressure of the high-pressure carbon dioxide is 2-7 MPa, and the processing time is 10-30 minutes. In the method, before using high-pressure carbon dioxide for processing, further includes immersing the material to be processed in a soaking liquid, preferably citrate solution for soaking. The separating supernatant refers to taking the supernatant by centrifugation at 3000-10000 revolutions/minute for 10-30 minutes.Preferred Components: The algae containing phycocyanin is blue-green algae, Rhodophyceae, Cryptophyceae and/or Dinophyceae. The blue-green algae is spirulina, Anabaena and bloom Cyanobacteria. The material to be treated is dry powder or wet substance. The citrate is trisodium citrate, and the concentration of the citrate is 0.1-2 wt.%. The mixing ratio of the material to-be-processed and the soaking liquid is 1:10-1:50 (g/ml).

GA 2021A6348F
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a double rectification purification system of carbon dioxide liquid relating to carbon dioxide double rectification purification technology field. The double-rectifying purification system of carbon dioxide liquid a liquid storage tank, a first purifying device and a second purifying device, one side of the bottom of the liquid storage tank is connected with a first purifying device, one side of the top end of the first purifying device is connected with a second purifying device. The double rectification purification liquid of the carbon dioxide, through the liquid dioxide orderly through the first rectifying tank and the second rectifying tank rectifying and purifying, which greatly improves the purification liquid of the carbon dioxide, meets the industrial requirement, through the first rectifying tank and the second rectifying tank bottom is installed with a reboiler, a condenser; a waste liquid tank and a return pipe; the waste liquid in the first rectifying tank and the second rectifying tank are processed again; the return pipe is extracted; the return pipe transmits the return pipe to the first pipeline; the recycling of the resource is realized; it is good for environment protection.

TI Processing facility has hydrogen production system            configured to produce hydrogen and carbon dioxide from            lights stream, and carbon dioxide conversion system            which is configured to produce hydrocarbons from carbon            dioxide, and hydroprocessing system which is configured            to process heavies stream.
AU BALLESTEROS A L
   YOUNES M
   BOURANE A
   JAMAL A

   HARALE A
   HALALADESH
   ABDENUR B
   UNESMURAD
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO AMERICAS (SAOI-C)
GA 202159645P
AB    NOVELTY - The processing facility (100) has a feedstock                separation system (104) which is configured to                separate a feed stream into a lights stream (106)                and a heavies stream (108). A hydrogen production                system (114) is configured to produce hydrogen and                carbon dioxide from the lights stream. A carbon                dioxide conversion system is configured to                FIG.produce synthetic hydrocarbons from the carbon                dioxide. A hydroprocessing system is configured to                process the heavies stream.
   USE - Processing facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The products from the steam cracking system                are improved as steam cracking of materials having                higher hydrogen contents results in better                products. The furnaces are flexible or customized                for some of the feed sent to the steam cracking                system. The purposeful generation of aromatics by                treating the separator tops stream in the reformer                enables the overall yield of aromatics from the                processing facility to be increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - alcohol/water
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a                processing facility that directly converts crude                oil into petrochemicals, including both olefinic                and aromatic petrochemicals.

   Feedstock separation system (104)

The carbon dioxide is                  processed in a carbon dioxide conversion system.                  An ethylene product stream is provided from a                  products separation system. The propylene product                  stream is provided from a products separation                  system. The product stream is comprised of                  benzene, toluene and xylene (BTX) from a products                  separation system.

PN CN112169817-A; CN112169817-B
TI Perovskite type composite oxygen carrier used for            carbon dioxide hydrogenation to generate low-carbon            olefin, is prepared by hydrothermally crystallizing            zinc ferrite, lanthanum ferrite and metal oxide,            calcining, and grinding.
AU GUO Q
   MA L
   ZHANG J
   ZHAO T
   GAO X
   MA Q
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202106207T
AB    NOVELTY - A perovskite type composite oxygen carrier                used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to generate                low-carbon olefin, is prepared by taking zinc                ferrite, lanthanum ferrite and metal oxide,                hydrothermally crystallizing, placing in muffle                furnace, constant temperature calcining, and                grinding.
   USE - The perovskite type composite oxygen carrier                is used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                generate low-carbon olefin (claimed), specifically                ethylene, propylene and butylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The carrier overcomes problem of poor                catalytic activity of single lanthanum ferrite                oxygen carrier, and exhibits good low-carbon olefin                selectivity in carbon dioxide hydrogenation                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A perovskite type composite oxygen carrier is                prepared by taking zinc ferrite, lanthanum ferrite                and metal oxide, hydrothermally crystallizing at                150-200 degrees C for 12-24 hours according to zinc                ferrite of (NaBH4)/(Zn2++Fe3+) molar ratio of                0.5-5, placing in muffle furnace, constant                temperature calcining at 700-900 degrees C for 3-8                hours, and grinding. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for an application of perovskite type                composite oxygen carrier comprising applying                catalyst to carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction                at 2-8 MPa, 1000-4000 /hour and 150-400 degrees C                at hydrogen/carbon dioxide ratio of 1-4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen carrier                  comprises 0.1-30% zinc ferrite, 10-70% lanthanum                  ferrite and balance iron oxide. The metal oxide                  comprises lanthanum oxide, zinc oxide, iron(III)                  oxide and iron(II,III) oxide. The oxygen carrier                  is modified by elements of magnesium, manganese,                  sodium, zinc and potassium, and has optimized                  catalytic activity. Preferred Method: The                  lanthanum ferrite is prepared by taking nitrate                  solution of lanthanum and iron and citric acid in                  water bath at 80 degrees C to form gel, drying at                  105-120 degrees C for greater than or equal to 12                  hours, and calcined in muffle furnace at 700-900                  degrees C.

GA 2020992113
AB    NOVELTY - The valve has a locking component (2) comprising a locking block (21) and an electromagnet (22) that are placed with a valve body (1). The electromagnet is fixedly connected with the valve body. The locking block is attracted by the electromagnet to move. A side of the locking block is provided with a fixing bulge (24) far away from the electromagnet. The fixing bulge limits a valve core (31) such that the valve core blocks connection between an air inlet interface (11) and an outlet (14). The electromagnet attracts the locking block such that the fixing bulge cancels limit of the valve core when the electromagnet is electrified.
   USE - Rail vehicle carbon dioxide air conditioner safety valve.
   ADVANTAGE - The valve ensures passive pressure releasing function and avoids refrigerant carbon dioxide leakage, and is actively opened according to carbon dioxide concentration.

GA 2018090341
AB    NOVELTY - Isothermal process for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce an oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition, comprises contacting a chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst (112) with a reactant feed stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide at a molar ratio of less than 1, where the reaction occurs at a constant temperature of at least 550 degrees C and a pressure of at least 0.5 MPa to produce the oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide at molar ratio of less than 1.2 and methane at least at 1 molar%.
   USE - The isothermal process is useful for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce an oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The isothermal process provides an elegant alternative to the highly endothermic process of methane steam reforming to produce syngas for use in oxo-product synthesis.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the process to produce oxo-synthesis syngas that includes methane using a combined carbon dioxide and hydrogen containing reactant feed gas and the chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst.
   System (100)
   Gas source (102)
   Reactor (104)
   Collection device (106)
   Chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reaction temperature is 550-700 degrees C, 570-590 degrees C, or 625-675 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is 0.5-3 MPa or 1-3 MPa. The process further comprises providing the methane containing syngas composition to an oxy-synthesis process. The reaction occurs at 550-600 degrees C and 0.9-3 MPa, where the hydrogen gas flow rate in the reactant feed stream is 20-50 ml/minutes and the carbon dioxide gas flow rate in the reactant feed stream is 50-70 ml/minute. The reaction occurs at 640-660 degrees C and 2.5-3 MPa, and the hydrogen gas flow rate in the reactant feed stream is 30-50 ml/minute and the carbon dioxide gas flow rate in the reactant feed stream is 50-70 ml/minute. The reaction occurs at 640-660 degrees C and 2.5-3 MPa, and the hydrogen gas flow rate in the reactant feed stream is 20-40 ml/minute and the carbon dioxide gas flow rate in the reactant feed stream is 60-80 ml/minute, and the syngas composition comprises 5-15 mole% carbon monoxide, 70-90 mole% carbon dioxide, 3-10 mole% methane and 3-7 mole% hydrogen. The oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition further includes carbon dioxide and the process further comprises removing the carbon dioxide from the oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition, and providing the oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition to a hydroformylation of olefins reaction, a carbonylation of methanol reaction or a carbonylation of olefins reaction. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 0.1-0.9, 0.2-0.8 or 0.4-0.7. The reactant feed stream does not contain methane. The hydrogen in the syngas composition is present at 1-40 (preferably 5-25) molar%. The carbon monoxide in the syngas composition is present at 1-40 (preferably 10-25) molar%. The molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide is 0.1-1.2, 0.2-1.2 or 0.4-1.2. The methane in the syngas composition is present at 1-10 (preferably 1.5-6.5) molar%. The syngas composition further comprises carbon dioxide at 40-90 molar% or 50-80 molar%. The chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst comprises 5-30 (preferably 13-17) wt.% of chromium. The catalyst comprises 0.2-30 (preferably 2-5) wt.% of at least one member comprising lithium, potassium, cesium or strontium. The carbon dioxide conversion is at least 10%. The syngas composition comprises 15-21 mole% carbon monoxide, 50-60 mole% carbon dioxide, 1.5-2.5 mole% methane and 22-25 mole% hydrogen. The syngas composition comprises 15-25 mole% carbon monoxide, 50-60 mole% carbon dioxide, 5-10 mole% methane and 15-20 mole% hydrogen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Desulfurizing gas having water vapor, carbon            dioxide and sulfur oxides, comprises e.g. adding            carbonate of alkali metal to remaining portion of            acidic aqueous solution, and cooling resultant to            result in the cooled alkaline aqueous solution.
AU LEFEBVRE S

   CLERVEAUX F
   VEILLEUX G
   ZHANG D
AE CO2 SOLUTIONS INC (COTW-Non-standard)
   SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)

GA 201915349P
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurization (P1) of a gas comprising water                vapor, carbon dioxide and sulfur oxides (SOx),                where x is equal to 2 and/or 3 and gas has initial                gas temperature, comprises: contacting, in                treatment unit, gas with cooled alkaline aqueous                solution comprising water and carbonate of alkali                metal and having temperature lower than initial gas                temperature, thus causing cooling of gas,                condensation of some water vapor and absorption of                sulfur oxides in the carbonate-containing solution,                and producing a sulfur oxides-depleted gas and an                acidic aqueous solution comprising sulfate and/or                sulfite ions; recovering the sulfur oxides-depleted                gas from the treatment unit; purging a portion of                the acidic aqueous solution exiting the treatment                unit; adding carbonate of the alkali metal to a                remaining portion of the acidic aqueous solution                exiting the treatment unit to obtain an alkaline                aqueous solution; and cooling the alkaline aqueous                solution to result in the cooled alkaline aqueous                solution.
   USE - The methods and systems are useful for                desulfurization of a gas comprising at least water                vapor, carbon dioxide and sulfur oxides and                removing sulfur oxides contained in the gas (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods and systems: capture sulfur                dioxide and sulfur trioxide from gases that are                produced by various industrial processes, using                carbonate absorbent and further treated for carbon                dioxide removal e.g. flue gases after the                combustion of carbon-based fuels; do no separate                desulfurization unit required for sulfur oxides                removal prior to carbon dioxide capture; utilize                the both desulfurization and cooling of the gas                performed in the same process unit instead of                several process units, thus reducing costs and is                economically beneficial; recycle a waste stream                i.e. absorption solution bleed stream to pre-treat                the gas and selectively remove contaminants;                utilize the carbonate absorption solution in the                main absorption loop that is reduced, thus reducing                the environmental impact of the process and the                operating costs associated with the consumption of                the absorption solution; provide removal of sulfur                dioxide from the gas stream in the pretreatment                loop generates sulfates, a desirable by-product                which may be used in fertilizers; minimize the                sulfate ion concentration in the absorption                solution; control and reduce the nitrate and/or                nitrite levels of the absorption loop.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a system (S1) for removing sulfur oxides                contained in a gas comprising at least water vapor,                carbon dioxide and sulfur oxides, comprising a                treatment unit for contacting the gas with a cooled                alkaline aqueous solution comprising water and a                carbonate of an alkali metal and having a                temperature lower than the initial gas temperature,                where in the treatment unit the gas is cooled, some                water vapor is condensed and the sulfur oxides are                absorbed in the cooled alkaline aqueous solution, a                mixing unit for receiving a first portion of an                acidic aqueous solution comprising sulfate and/or                sulfite ions recovered from the treatment unit and                adding carbonate of the alkali metal so as to                obtain an alkaline aqueous solution, a purge line                for purging a second portion of the acidic aqueous                solution recovered from the treatment unit, and a                cooling unit (18) for cooling the alkaline aqueous                solution to be returned to the treatment unit as                the cooled alkaline aqueous solution;
   (2) use of the alkaline carbonate-containing                solution for desulfurization and cooling of a gas                comprising at least water vapor, carbon dioxide and                sulfur oxides and recovering a cooled sulfur                oxides-depleted gas, where the alkaline                carbonate-containing solution has a temperature                lower than an initial gas temperature and is                obtained by mixing an acidic aqueous solution with                a carbonate of an alkali metal, and the acidic                aqueous solution comprises sulfate and/or sulfite                ions resulting from an absorption of the sulfur                oxides of the gas in the alkaline                carbonate-containing solution;
   (3) removing (P2) carbon dioxide from a gas                comprising at least water vapor, carbon dioxide and                sulfur oxides;
   (4) a system (S2) for removing carbon dioxide                from a gas comprising at least water vapor, carbon                dioxide and sulfur oxides;
   (5) desulfurization (P3) of a gas comprising                at least water vapor, carbon dioxide and sulfur                oxides;
   (6) a system (S3) for removing sulfur oxides                contained in a gas comprising at least water vapor,                carbon dioxide and sulfur oxides;
   (7) removing (P4) carbon dioxide from a gas                comprising at least water vapor, carbon dioxide and                sulfur oxides;
   (8) use of a bleed stream obtained from a                carbon dioxide-capture process and comprising                sodium or potassium carbonate for desulfurization                of a gas comprising water vapor, carbon dioxide and                sulfur oxides, and production of an sulfur                oxides-depleted gas;
   (9) an sulfur oxides absorbent solution                comprising the bleed stream obtained from carbon                dioxide capture process and comprising sodium or                potassium carbonate, and an acidic aqueous solution                obtained from desulfurization of a sulfur                oxides-containing gas;
   (10) a gas pre-treatment solution for                absorbing contaminants from a carbon                dioxide-containing gas, comprising the bleed stream                obtained from a carbon dioxide-capture process and                comprising sodium or potassium carbonate, and an                acidic aqueous solution obtained from                desulfurization of a sulfur oxides-containing gas;                and
   (11) desulfurization (P5) of a gas comprising                at least water vapor, carbon dioxide and sulfur                oxides, comprising contacting, in a treatment unit,                the gas with an alkaline aqueous solution                comprising water and a carbonate of an alkali metal                to cause absorption of the sulfur oxides in the                alkaline aqueous solution, to produce an sulfur                oxides-depleted gas and an acidic aqueous solution                comprising sulfate and/or sulfite ions, withdrawing                the sulfur oxides-depleted gas from the treatment                unit, withdrawing the acidic aqueous solution from                the treatment unit, removing at least a portion of                the sulfate and/or sulfite ions from the acidic                aqueous solution to produce a remaining acidic                aqueous solution, adding carbonate of an alkali                metal to the remaining acidic aqueous solution to                obtain a made-up alkaline aqueous solution, and                reintroducing the made-up alkaline aqueous solution                into the treatment unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a process flow diagram                representing process for treating a flue gas                showing the integration of the sulfur oxide removal                pre-treatment loop with the carbon dioxide capture                loop.
   Flue gas (1)
   Purge line (16)
   Cooling unit (18)
   Cooling fluid (20)
   Alkali metal carbonate (22)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P2)                  comprises a pre-treatment loop for desulfurizing                  the gas and recovering a sulfur oxides-depleted                  gas, and an absorption loop for removing water                  vapor from the sulfur oxides-depleted gas, where                  the process (P2) comprises cooling an alkaline                  aqueous solution comprising water and a carbonate                  and bicarbonate of an alkali metal to obtain a                  cooled alkaline aqueous solution having a                  temperature lower than the initial gas                  temperature, contacting the gas with the cooled                  alkaline aqueous solution in a desulfurization                  unit of the pre-treatment loop, thus causing                  cooling of the gas, condensation of some water                  vapor and absorption of the sulfur oxides in the                  cooled alkaline aqueous solution, and producing                  the sulfur oxides-depleted gas and an acidic                  aqueous solution comprising sulfate and/or                  sulfite ions, purging a first portion of the                  acidic aqueous solution exiting the                  desulfurization unit, supplying the sulfur                  oxides-depleted gas which contains carbon dioxide                  from the desulfurization unit to a carbon dioxide                  capture unit of the absorption loop, in the                  carbon dioxide capture unit, contacting the                  sulfur oxides-depleted gas with an absorption                  solution comprising water and carbonate of the                  alkali metal, thus causing absorption of the                  carbon dioxide in the absorption solution and                  producing a carbon dioxide-depleted gas and a                  carbonate and bicarbonate-rich absorption                  solution, treating the carbonate and                  bicarbonate-rich absorption solution in a                  stripping unit to generate a purified carbon                  dioxide gas and recover a carbonate and                  bicarbonate-lean absorption solution, bleeding a                  fraction of an absorption solution circulating in                  the absorption loop e.g. from the carbonate and                  bicarbonate-lean absorption solution or from the                  carbonate and bicarbonate-rich absorption                  solution as an absorption solution bleed, and                  mixing the absorption solution bleed with a                  second portion of the acidic aqueous solution                  exiting the desulfurization unit of the                  pre-treatment loop to produce the alkaline                  aqueous solution. The process (P3) comprises                  contacting, in a treatment unit, the gas with an                  alkaline aqueous solution comprising water and a                  carbonate of an alkali metal to cause absorption                  of the sulfur oxides in the alkaline aqueous                  solution, to produce an sulfur oxides-depleted                  gas and an acidic aqueous solution comprising                  sulfate and/or sulfite ions, recovering the                  sulfur oxides-depleted gas from the treatment                  unit, withdrawing the acidic aqueous solution                  from the treatment unit, purging a portion of the                  acidic aqueous solution as a purged stream and                  producing a remaining portion of the acidic                  aqueous solution, adding carbonate of an alkali                  metal to the remaining portion of the acidic                  aqueous solution to obtain a made-up alkaline                  aqueous solution, and reintroducing the made-up                  alkaline aqueous solution into the treatment                  unit. The process (P4) comprises a pre-treatment                  loop for desulfurizing the gas and recovering a                  sulfur oxides-depleted gas, and an absorption                  loop for removing carbon dioxide from the sulfur                  oxides-depleted gas, comprising contacting the                  sulfur oxides-depleted gas with an absorption                  solution comprising water and carbonate of the                  alkali metal, thus causing absorption of the                  carbon dioxide in the absorption solution and                  producing a sulfur oxides depleted gas and a                  carbonate and bicarbonate-rich absorption                  solution, treating the carbonate and                  bicarbonate-rich absorption solution in a                  stripping unit to generate a purified carbon                  dioxide gas and recover a carbonate and                  bicarbonate-lean absorption solution, bleeding a                  fraction of the absorption solution circulating                  in the absorption loop as an absorption solution                  bleed, supplying at least a portion of the                  absorption solution bleed to the pre-treatment                  loop as part of a desulfurization solution,                  contacting the gas with the desulfurization                  solution in a desulfurization unit of the                  pre-treatment loop, thus causing absorption of                  the sulfur oxides in the alkaline aqueous                  solution, and producing the sulfur                  oxides-depleted gas and an acidic aqueous                  solution comprising sulfate and/or sulfite ions,                  purging a first portion of the acidic aqueous                  solution exiting the desulfurization unit,                  supplying the sulfur oxides-depleted gas which                  contains carbon dioxide from the desulfurization                  unit to a carbon dioxide capture unit of the                  absorption loop, and mixing the second portion of                  the acidic aqueous solution exiting the                  desulfurization unit of the pre-treatment loop                  with at least a portion of the absorption                  solution bleed, to produce the desulfurization                  solution. In the process (P1): the gas contacted                  with the cooled alkaline aqueous solution has a                  concentration in sulfur oxides of 1-100 ppmv; the                  gas contacted with the cooled alkaline aqueous                  solution further comprises nitrogen oxides (NOy),                  where y is equal to 1 and/or 2; the nitrogen                  oxides comprises 10-150 ppmv in the gas, where                  the gas is a flue gas (1) or a post-combustion                  flue gas; the temperature of the cooled alkaline                  aqueous solution is 5-60 degrees C, preferably                  10-60 degrees C; the cooled alkaline aqueous                  solution has a concentration in carbonate of the                  alkali metal of 1 mM to 1 M, preferably 1 mM to                  700 mM; the cooled alkaline aqueous solution has                  a pH of 7-9.5, preferably 7-9; the treatment unit                  comprises a contacting device comprising a bubble                  column, a packed column with random packing, a                  packed column with structured packing, a venturi,                  a barometric leg, an eductor, a spraying device                  or a demister pad, preferably packed column with                  random packing, a packed column with structured                  packing and/or a spraying device; contacting the                  gas with the cooled alkaline aqueous solution is                  performed under conditions e.g. residence time,                  flow rates of gas and solution, composition of                  gas and solution, and mass transfer properties,                  that produce a sulfur oxides-depleted gas in                  which at least 50%, preferably at least 80% of                  the sulfur oxides has been removed; removing the                  solid particles from the acidic aqueous solution                  is performed using a separation system comprising                  a radial vane separator, a Schoepentoeter device,                  a cyclone or cyclones, a settling system or a                  filtration unit; the purging is performed to                  maintain a mass balance of the process; the                  purging is performed at a flowrate determined by                  a water vapor condensation rate and an alkaline                  aqueous solution flowrate; the alkaline aqueous                  solution is obtained by adding the carbonate of                  the alkali metal to the remaining portion of the                  acidic aqueous solution, in a solid form; the                  alkaline aqueous solution is obtained by adding                  the carbonate of the alkali metal to the                  remaining portion of the acidic aqueous solution,                  dissolved in solution as an alkali metal                  carbonate (22) make-up solution; the alkali metal                  carbonate make-up solution added to the remaining                  portion of the acidic aqueous solution is derived                  from a carbon dioxide-capture process; the alkali                  metal carbonate make-up solution added to the                  remaining portion of the acidic aqueous solution                  is an absorption solution bleed derived from a                  carbon dioxide-loaded stream or a carbon                  dioxide-lean stream of the carbon dioxide-capture                  process; and the alkaline aqueous solution, which                  can also be referred to as a carbonate-containing                  solution, further comprises a bicarbonate of the                  alkali metal. The process further comprises:                  removing solid particles e.g. precipitates                  originating from the solution and/or fly ash                  and/or particulate matter from the gas, from the                  acidic aqueous solution; and adding a bicarbonate                  of the alkali metal to the remaining portion of                  the acidic aqueous solution to obtain the                  alkaline aqueous solution. In the process (P2):                  the gas contacted with the cooled alkaline                  aqueous solution has a concentration in sulfur                  oxides of 1-100 ppmv; the temperature of the                  cooled alkaline aqueous solution is 5-60 degrees                  C, preferably 10-60 degrees C; the cooled                  alkaline aqueous solution has a concentration in                  carbonate of the alkali metal of 1 mM to 1 M,                  preferably 1 mM to 700 mM; the cooled alkaline                  aqueous solution has a pH of 7-9.5, preferably                  7-9; the desulfurization unit comprises a                  contacting device comprising a bubble column, a                  packed column with random packing, a packed                  column with structured packing, a venturi, a                  barometric leg, an eductor, a spraying device or                  a demister pad, preferably packed column with                  random packing, a packed column with structured                  packing or a spraying device; contacting the gas                  with the cooled alkaline aqueous solution is                  performed under conditions to produce a sulfur                  oxides-depleted gas in which at least 50%,                  preferably at least 80% of the sulfur oxides has                  been removed; the purging is performed to                  maintain a mass balance in the pre-treatment                  loop; the purging is performed at a flowrate                  determined by a water vapor condensation rate and                  an absorption solution bleed flowrate; the gas                  contacted with the cooled alkaline aqueous                  solution further comprises nitrogen oxides; the                  nitrogen oxides are present in the gas in a                  concentration of 10-150 ppmv; contacting the                  sulfur oxides-depleted gas with the absorption                  solution in the carbon dioxide capture unit is                  performed in the presence of a carbonic anhydrase                  or its analogues; the carbonic anhydrase or its                  analogues is present in a concentration below 0.1                  wt.% of the absorption solution; and the                  absorption solution in the carbon dioxide capture                  unit has a concentration of 1-5 mol per litre.                  The process (P2) further comprises: removing                  solid particles from the acidic aqueous solution;                  removing solid particles from the acidic aqueous                  solution using a separation system comprising a                  radial vane separator, a Schoepentoeter device, a                  cyclone, a settling system or a filtration unit;                  and adjusting a flow rate of the absorption                  solution bleed to maintain a concentration of                  nitrite and nitrate ions in the absorption loop                  for optimal carbon dioxide capture. In the                  process (P5), the removing of at least a portion                  of the sulfate and/or sulfite ions from the                  acidic aqueous solution comprises purging a                  portion thereof to produce a remaining portion as                  the remaining acidic aqueous solution. Preferred                  Components: The alkali metal comprises potassium                  (claimed) or sodium. The gas: comprises 1-100                  ppmv in sulfur oxides; and further comprises                  10-150 ppmv of nitrogen oxides. The gas before                  desulfurization has a concentration in sulfur                  oxides of 1-100 ppmv. The temperature of the                  alkaline carbonate-containing solution is 5-60                  degrees C, preferably 10-60 degrees C. The                  alkaline carbonate-containing solution has a                  concentration in carbonate of the alkali metal of                  1 mM to 1 M, preferably 1 mM to 700 mM. The                  alkaline carbonate-containing solution has a pH                  of 7-9.5, preferably 7-9. At least 50%,                  preferably at least 80% of the sulfur oxides is                  removed from the gas. The alkaline                  carbonate-containing solution is obtained by                  adding the carbonate of the alkali metal in a                  solid form to the acidic aqueous solution. The                  alkaline carbonate-containing solution is                  obtained by adding the carbonate of the alkali                  metal dissolved in a solution as an alkali metal                  carbonate make-up solution. The alkali metal                  carbonate make-up solution added to the acidic                  aqueous solution is derived from a carbon dioxide                  capture process. The alkali metal carbonate                  make-up solution added to the acidic aqueous                  solution is an absorption solution bleed derived                  from a carbon dioxide capture process. The                  alkaline carbonate-containing solution further                  comprises a bicarbonate of the alkali                  metal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system (S2)                  comprises a pre-treatment loop for desulfurizing                  the gas and recovering a sulfur oxides-depleted                  gas and an absorption loop for removing carbon                  dioxide from the sulfur oxides-depleted gas,                  where the pre-treatment loop comprises a                  desulfurization unit for contacting the gas with                  a cooled alkaline aqueous solution comprising                  water and a carbonate and bicarbonate of an                  alkali metal and having a temperature lower than                  the initial gas temperature, in the                  desulfurization unit the gas is cooled, some                  water vapor is condensed and the sulfur oxides                  are absorbed in the cooled alkaline aqueous                  solution, a mixing unit for mixing a first                  portion of an acidic aqueous solution comprising                  sulfate and/or sulfite ions recovered from the                  desulfurization unit with an absorption solution                  bleed recovered from the absorption loop to                  obtain an alkaline aqueous solution comprising                  water and the carbonate and bicarbonate of the                  alkali metal, a purge line for purging a second                  portion of the acidic aqueous solution recovered                  from the desulfurization unit, and a cooling unit                  for cooling the alkaline aqueous solution to be                  returned to the desulfurization unit as the                  cooled alkaline aqueous solution, where the                  absorption loop comprises a carbon dioxide                  capture unit for contacting the sulfur                  oxides-depleted gas with an absorption solution                  comprising water and carbonate of the alkali                  metal and producing a carbon dioxide-depleted gas                  and a carbonate and bicarbonate-rich absorption                  solution and a stripping unit for treating the                  carbonate and bicarbonate-rich absorption                  solution to recover a purified carbon dioxide gas                  and generate a carbonate and bicarbonate-lean                  absorption solution at least a part of which is                  recirculated back into the carbon dioxide capture                  unit at the absorption solution. The system (S3)                  comprises a treatment unit for contacting the gas                  with an alkaline aqueous solution comprising                  water and a carbonate of an alkali metal, in the                  treatment unit some water vapor is condensed and                  the sulfur oxides are absorbed in the alkaline                  aqueous solution to form an acidic aqueous                  solution comprising sulfate and/or sulfite ions,                  a mixing unit for receiving a first portion of                  the acidic aqueous solution recovered from the                  treatment unit and for adding carbonate of the                  alkali metal so as to obtain a regenerated                  alkaline aqueous solution, a purge line for                  purging a second portion of the acidic aqueous                  solution recovered from the treatment unit, and a                  recirculation line for feeding the regenerated                  alkaline aqueous solution back into the treatment                  unit. The system (S4) comprises a pre-treatment                  loop for desulfurizing the gas and recovering a                  sulfur oxides-depleted gas and an absorption loop                  for removing carbon dioxide from the sulfur                  oxides-depleted gas, where the pre-treatment loop                  comprises a desulfurization unit for contacting                  the gas with an alkaline aqueous solution                  comprising water and a carbonate and bicarbonate                  of an alkali metal, in the desulfurization unit                  sulfur oxides are absorbed in the alkaline                  aqueous solution to produce an acidic aqueous                  solution, a mixing unit for mixing a first                  portion of the acidic aqueous solution comprising                  sulfate and/or sulfite ions recovered from the                  desulfurization unit with an absorption solution                  bleed recovered from the absorption loop to                  obtain an alkaline aqueous solution comprising                  water and the carbonate and bicarbonate of the                  alkali metal, and a purge line for purging a                  second portion of the acidic aqueous solution                  recovered from the desulfurization unit, where                  the absorption loop comprises a carbon dioxide                  capture unit for contacting the sulfur                  oxides-depleted gas with an absorption solution                  comprising water and carbonate of the alkali                  metal and producing a carbon dioxide-depleted gas                  and a carbonate and bicarbonate-rich absorption                  solution, and a stripping unit for treating the                  carbonate and bicarbonate-rich absorption                  solution to recover a purified carbon dioxide gas                  and generate a carbonate and bicarbonate-lean                  absorption solution at least a part of which is                  recirculated back into the carbon dioxide capture                  unit at the absorption solution. In the systems                  (S1 and S2): the treatment unit has a height and                  diameter based on operating conditions to obtain                  sulfur oxides removal efficiency of at least 50%.                  preferably at least 90%; the separation unit                  comprises a radial vane separator, a                  Schoepentoeter device, a cyclone or cyclones, a                  settling system, or a filtration unit; the purge                  line (16) is configured and operable to maintain                  a mass balance in the system; the purge line is                  configured and operable to purge the second                  portion of the acidic aqueous solution at a                  flowrate determined by a water vapor condensation                  rate and an alkaline aqueous solution flowrate;                  the mixing unit comprises a simple line device, a                  static mixer, a Tee pipe joint, a manifold, a                  stirred tank mixer or any other comingling                  device; the mixing unit is configured so that the                  carbonate of the alkali metal is added to the                  mixing unit in a solid form; the mixing unit is                  configured so that the carbonate of the alkali                  metal is added in the mixing unit dissolved in a                  solution as an alkali metal carbonate make-up                  solution; the alkali metal carbonate make-up                  solution added in the mixing unit is derived from                  a carbon dioxide-capture process and the system                  comprises a feed line for supplying the alkali                  metal carbonate make-up solution from the carbon                  dioxide-capture process into the mixing unit; the                  alkali metal carbonate make-up solution added in                  the mixing unit is an absorption solution bleed                  derived from a carbon dioxide-capture process and                  the system comprises a feed line for supplying                  the absorption solution bleed from the carbon                  dioxide-capture process into the mixing unit; and                  the cooling unit comprises a heat exchanger,                  where the cooled water is used as cooling fluid                  (20). The system (S1) further comprises: a                  separation unit for removing solid particles from                  the acidic aqueous solution or its first                  portions, recovered from the treatment unit; and                  a level sensor upstream of the purge line to                  detect a level of liquid in the system. The                  system (S2) further comprises: a separation unit                  for removing solid particles from the acidic                  aqueous solution recovered from the                  desulfurization unit; and a level sensor upstream                  the purge line to detect a level of liquid in the                  system. In the system (S2): the cooling unit                  comprises a heat exchanger where cooled water is                  used as cooling fluid; the carbon dioxide capture                  unit comprises a bubble column, a column with                  random packing, a column with structured packing,                  a rotating packed bed, a liquid film contactor or                  a spray scrubber; the absorption solution bleed                  comprises a fraction of the carbonate and                  bicarbonate-lean absorption solution released                  from the stripping unit; the absorption solution                  bleed comprises a fraction of the carbonate and                  bicarbonate-rich absorption solution released                  from the carbon dioxide capture unit; the                  absorption solution bleed is derived from a                  hotter or a cooler (18) stream of the absorption                  loop. In the system (S4): the absorption solution                  bleed is supplied directly from the absorption                  loop to the pre-treatment loop without any                  intermediate treatment; and the absorption                  solution bleed is treated prior to being supplied                  to the pre-treatment loop.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2292426-B1
TI Continuously separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide involves passing synthesis gas through supply pipe through adsorption tower or another adsorption tower filled with multiple adsorbents capable of selectively adsorbing components.
AU SEOK K H
   LEE H S
   DOH S H
AE ASPE INC (ASPE-Non-standard)
GA 202199293X
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide involves passing synthesis gas through a supply pipe (100) through an adsorption tower (110) or another adsorption tower (120) filled with multiple adsorbents capable of selectively adsorbing components other than hydrogen in the synthesis gas. The hydrogen purified to high purity via the adsorption towers is collected in a hydrogen storage tank (140) through a hydrogen pipe (130). The adsorption tower for cleaning the former adsorbent saturated with residue gas selectively stops the hydrogen production. Another residue gas is discharged from which moisture is removed through a gas dryer (200) formed at the rear end of vacuum pump. The latter residue gas not adsorbed via the third adsorption tower or the fourth adsorption tower is circulated and supplied to the gas dryer through a circulation pipe (240).
   USE - Method for continuously separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide with a purity of 99% from syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to operate hydrogen separation tower without generating a differential pressure continuously, where the adsorbent has strong adsorption to the target product or the target product to selectively separate the target product, and continuously operates cycle consisting of an unnecessary gas adsorption and a PSA adsorbent regeneration for a long period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuously separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide involves passing synthesis gas through a supply pipe (100) through an adsorption tower (110) or another adsorption tower (120) filled with multiple adsorbents capable of selectively adsorbing components other than hydrogen in the synthesis gas. The hydrogen purified to high purity via the adsorption towers is collected in a hydrogen storage tank (140) through a hydrogen pipe (130). The adsorption tower for cleaning the former adsorbent saturated with residue gas selectively stops the hydrogen production. Another residue gas is discharged from which moisture is removed through a gas dryer (200) formed at the rear end of vacuum pump. The latter residue gas not adsorbed via the third adsorption tower or the fourth adsorption tower is circulated and supplied to the gas dryer through a circulation pipe (240). The separated carbon dioxide is stored in the carbon dioxide storage tank (290) through the carbon dioxide pipe (280) at the rear end of the latter vacuum pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of the method for simultaneously separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Supply pipe (100)
   Adsorption tower (110)
   Hydrogen pipe (130)
   Hydrogen storage tank (140)
   Gas dryer (200)
   Circulation pipe (240)
   Carbon dioxide pipe (280)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (290)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The synthesis gas is a gas mixture of other components including 40 vol.% carbon monoxide, 30 vol.% hydrogen, 10 vol.% carbon dioxide and moisture. The gas mixture is converted into a water gas conversion reaction. The adsorption layer is filled with an adsorbent capable of adsorbing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, moisture, nitrogen, hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide, and methane.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Apparatus and method useful for generating and capturing carbon dioxide to generate electric energy, comprises anode electrode and cathode electrode electrically connected to each other, multiple ion exchange membranes and collection unit.

   HWANG K S

   KIM C S

AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2019255029
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises an anode electrode (101) and a cathode electrode (102) arranged to face each other with predetermined interval and electrically connected to each other; multiple ion exchange membranes (300) including at least one carbonate channel in which a first carbonate solution flows, at least one low-concentration solution channel in which low-concentration solution flows are arranged, a first ion exchange membrane (301) closest to the anode electrode side, and a second ion exchange membrane closest to the cathode electrode side, provided between the anode electrode and the cathode electrode; and a collection unit (200) containing a high-concentration solution and a gas containing carbon dioxide are introduced into the space, respectively, and a first flow path for passing the first carbonate solution generated in the process of passing through the space to the carbonate flow path side, and disposed in the space between the first ion exchange membrane and the anode electrode.
   USE - The apparatus and method are useful for generating and capturing carbon dioxide (claimed) to generate electric energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus produces renewable energy without carbon dioxide emission by producing electricity using salinity generation by using solution in which carbon dioxide is captured using solution containing seawater or high purity sodium chloride in atmosphere or exhaust gas, reduces carbon dioxide and energy consumption to produce additional energy, and can store carbon dioxide with very little energy and prevent leakage for long time in stable state.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for generating and capturing carbon dioxide using apparatus, comprising (a) disposing an electrically connected anode electrode and a cathode electrode so as to face each other at a predetermined interval, (b) providing multiple ion exchange membranes including first ion exchange membrane closest to the anode electrode side and a second ion exchange membrane closest to the cathode electrode side between the anode electrode and the cathode electrode, and at least one low concentration solution flow paths through which the low concentration solution flows, and (c) introducing high concentration solution and gas containing carbon dioxide into trapping section provided in the space between the first ion exchange membrane and the anode electrode, and transferring the first carbonate solution generated in the process of passing through the space to the carbonate flow path through the first flow path.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the apparatus for generating and capturing carbon dioxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Anode electrode (101)
   Cathode electrode (102)
   Collection unit (200)
   Multiple ion exchange membranes (300)
   First ion exchange membrane (301)

Preferred Method: In                  step (iii), the temperature includes increasing                  the temperature of the reactant to 90 degrees C                  within 300 minutes, the heat preservation time is                  5000 minutes. In step (iii), the cooling                  comprises reducing the temperature from 90-30                  degrees C within 600 minutes.

PN CN111011365-A
TI Caespitose seeds storage method involves collecting mature caespitose seeds, immersing in disinfectant for disinfection, cleaning and washing seeds, and then drying washed seeds with cool air to obtain dried caespitose seeds.
AU MAO X
   CAO Z
   CAO S
AE ANHUI TAIHU HUALITIANHUA IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202034126G
AB    NOVELTY - Caespitose seeds storage method involves collecting mature caespitose seeds, immersing in disinfectant for disinfection for 6-10 minutes, cleaning and washing seeds, and then drying the washed seeds with cool air at 0-6 degrees C to obtain dried caespitose seeds. The dried caespitose seeds are placed in storage box at -20 to -4 degrees C under 80-90% volume concentration of carbon dioxide. The storage box includes a box body, a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide concentration sensor, a carbon dioxide generator and a controller. The controller is provided in the box, where the humidity sensor, carbon dioxide concentration sensor and carbon dioxide generator are all connected to the controller.
   USE - Method for storing caespitose seeds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method stores the caespitose seeds in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with stable storage and high germination rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The caespitose seeds are placed in a petri dish. The petri dish is placed in the storage box. The layer of fine sand is laid on the bottom of the petri dish, where filter paper is laid on the fine sand layer. The caespitose seeds are placed on the filter paper. The disinfectant comprises 0.8-1.5 pts. wt. allicin, 0.2-0.4 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 0.01-0.03 pts. wt. chlorine cobalt, 0.04-0.07 pts. wt. potassium permanganate and 90-100 pts. wt. water.

PN CN110923776-A
TI Converting carbon dioxide into metal carbide plating and oxygen by molten salt electrochemical method, comprises e.g. taking carbonate, high melting point metal and nickel-based metal and performing electrolysis by passing carbon dioxide.
AU XIAO W
   WENG W
   CAO J
   XIAO H
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202025871B
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide into metal carbide plating and oxygen by molten salt electrochemical method, comprises taking carbonate as the electrolyte, high melting point metal as the cathode, and nickel-based metal as the inert anode, then continuously passing carbon dioxide into the molten electrolyte for electrolysis to obtain a metal carbide coating on the surface of the metal cathode and oxygen at the anode.
   USE - The metal carbide coating material is useful in wear-resistant materials or electrolysis of hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal carbide coating material has high hardness, wear resistance and excellent catalytic hydrogen production performance. The method: consume carbon dioxide and achieves greenhouse gas emission reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for metal carbide coating material obtained by above method, where the thickness of the metal carbide plating layer is 3-10 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate is lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, calcium carbonate and/or magnesium carbonate. The high melting point metal is iron, cobalt, nickel, titanium, zirconium, hafnium, vanadium, niobium, tantalum, chromium, molybdenum, or tungsten. The nickel-based metal is nickel, nickel-iron-copper alloy (Ni11Fe10Cu) alloy or nickel-chromium (NiCr) alloy. Preferred Method: The flow rate of carbon dioxide continuously flowing into the molten electrolyte is 20-100 ml/minute. The electrolysis temperature is 450-950 degrees C, and the electrolysis time is 5 minutes to 12 hours, the method of electrolysis is constant current electrolysis, constant tank pressure electrolysis, and pulse electrolysis, the cathode current density of constant current electrolysis is 0.1-5 Ax cm-2, the voltage of constant tank pressure electrolysis is 2-3.2 V, the value of current density of pulse electrolysis is 0.1-5 Ax cm-2 or the value of voltage is 2-3.2 V, the pulse width is 60-5 ms/second, and the pulse interval is 100-5 ms/second.

PN CN108907218-A; CN108907218-B
TI Preparation of ultrafine tungsten powder by reducing tungsten oxide by carbon monoxide-carbon dioxide mixed atmosphere includes placing raw materials in furnace, calcining, introducing protective gas argon or carbon dioxide and cooling.
AU JIANG P
   LIU W
   XIAO Y
   LIAO C
   QIU T
AE UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
GA 201898530H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ultrafine tungsten powder by reducing tungsten oxide by carbon monoxide-carbon dioxide (CO-CO2) mixed atmosphere comprises placing raw materials in furnace, placing yellow tungsten, blue tungsten or purple tungsten in reaction boat, pushing reaction boat to constant temperature zone of reaction tube in the horizontal tubular furnace, evacuating air in the horizontal tube furnace by argon (Ar) or carbon dioxide (CO2) before the temperature rise, calcining at 500-1000 degrees C for 1-2 hours, cutting off mixed atmosphere, introducing protective gas Ar or CO2 and cooling to room temperature. The CO2 gas flow rate is 50-200 ml/min. The CO gas flow rate is 50-1800 ml/min.
   USE - Method of preparing ultrafine tungsten powder by reducing tungsten oxide by carbon monoxide-carbon dioxide mixed atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has 21% mass loss rate of tungsten.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flow rate of Ar or CO2 is 500 ml/min.

TI Efficient agarwood extraction system has supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle that is provided with partitioning mechanism that is arranged in housing for separating interior space of housing into several extraction zones.

   ZHU B
   LU Q
   FENG Z
   DING Z
AE HAINAN ALOES ISLAND LEISURE AGRIC TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2018378727
AB    NOVELTY - An efficient agarwood extraction system has supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle is provided with a housing, a partitioning mechanism that is arranged in the housing for separating an interior space of the housing into several extraction zones that are arranged longitudinally and connected to an intake pipe at the bottom of the housing. The intake pipe is provided with branch pipes that correspond to the extraction zones one by one.
   USE - Efficient agarwood extraction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The partitioning mechanism separates the interior space of the extraction kettle into several extraction zones, thus makes the distribution of agarwood to be more uniform. Each extraction zone has independent carbon dioxide intake pipe, so that the flow of carbon dioxide along each extraction zone is limited. The extraction yield is significantly improved within the same extraction time, and extraction efficiency is increased by 20-30%.

Preferred Properties: The several extraction zones are parallel to the central axis of the housing. The cross-section of the extraction zone is polygonal, circular, elliptical, irregular or quadrangular.
