PT P
PN CN110615553-A; CN110615553-B
TI Deep processing of heavy oil produced in water with flue gas, comprises e.g. performing crude oil-water separation by cyclone separator, extracting heavy oil produced water, performing oil-water separation and removing oil droplets.
AU QIU H
   QIU S
AE LANZHOU ZHENGFENG PETROCHEMICAL EQUIP CO (LANZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020018017
AB    NOVELTY - Deep processing of heavy oil produced in water with flue gas, comprises e.g. performing crude oil-water separation by cyclone separator, extracting heavy oil produced water from sewage degreasing tank by a sewage pump, a centrifugal pump or a screw pump at 40-65 degrees C, increasing the pressure to 0.8-1 MPa, performing oil-water separation using a cyclone, removing oil droplets and suspended solids with large particle diameters of not greater than 25-30 mu m and stabilization of water quality; mixing the underflow sewage by flue gas with sewage and pre-saturated cyclone separator, at a pressure of 0.4-0.6 MPa, mixing, dissolving and saturating with excess clean flue gas at pressure of 0.5 MPa, injecting 10-200 mu m micro-bubbles into a mixer at a pressure of 0.4 MPa, efficiently mixing with full-flow heavy oil produced water and dissolving saturated gas in a pre-saturator; and allowing the pre-saturated carbon-dioxide supersaturated flue gas to flow into the carbon-dioxide supersaturator.
   USE - The method is useful for deep processing of heavy oil produced in water with flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly and economical; and has high treatment efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Deep processing of heavy oil produced in water with flue gas, comprises (i) performing crude oil-water separation by cyclone separator, extracting heavy oil produced water from sewage degreasing tank by a sewage pump, a centrifugal pump or a screw pump at 40-65 degrees C, increasing the pressure to 0.8-1 MPa, performing oil-water separation using a cyclone, removing oil droplets and suspended solids with large particle diameters of not greater than 25-30 mu m and stabilization of water quality; (ii) mixing the underflow sewage by flue gas with sewage and pre-saturated cyclone separator, at a pressure of 0.4-0.6 MPa, mixing, dissolving and saturating with excess clean flue gas at pressure of 0.5 MPa, injecting 10-200 mu m micro-bubbles into a mixer at a pressure of 0.4 MPa, efficiently mixing with full-flow heavy oil produced water and dissolving saturated gas in a pre-saturator; (iii) allowing the pre-saturated carbon-dioxide supersaturated flue gas to flow into the carbon-dioxide supersaturator, mixing with the micro-bubble generator micro-bubbles, filtering the tail gas carbon-dioxide micro-bubble water by a large bubble separator, mixing, supersaturating and dissolving carbon-dioxide in the form of less than or equal to 10 mu m micro-bubbles in the pre-saturated sewage, mixing a large amount of carbon-dioxide free micro-bubbles with sewage and adsorbing oil droplets and suspending matter in sewage; (iv) performing sewage pressurization and pressure maintenance by pressurizing carbon-dioxide supersaturated sewage to 1.6-4 MPa in a closed hydraulic cylinder or pressure vessel by a hydraulic system, after the fluid is pressurized, maintaining the pressure for 5-30 seconds, which promotes the sewage and oil beads to be fully dissolved by the free carbon-dioxide micro-bubbles and volume of oil beads will swell is 10-100% after dissolved in carbon-dioxide; (v) pressurizing sewage and carbon-dioxide supersaturated wastewater; (vi) injecting a small amount of demulsifier or flocculant into sewage through demulsifier dosing system and flocculant dosing system; (vii) transferring the above sewage into cyclone separator II for oil-water cyclone separation under the pressure control of 0.8-1 MPa and discharging; (ix) conveying exhaust gas carbon-dioxide from the gas-liquid separator into exhaust gas circulation line of micro-bubble air flotation separator, dehumidifying by the exhaust gas dryer, using exhaust gas circulation compressor to inject into the exhaust gas storage tank as the source of the exhaust gas carbon-dioxide micro-bubbles at an operating pressure of 0.6-0.8 MPa, discharging exhaust carbon-dioxide from the exhaust gas storage tank when in use, filtering through a precision exhaust filter to remove solid particles not greater than 2 mu m, after precise filtering, exhaust gas carbon-dioxide is again made into a large number of micro-bubbles, injected into the carbon-dioxide supersaturator and the micro-bubble air floatation separator for recycling; (x) using a high-pressure reflux pump with a head of 0.8-1 MPa to extract a part of the purified water discharged from the micro-bubble air floatation separator with a reflux ratio of 10-15%, exhaust gas carbon-dioxide filtered with precision exhaust gas filter at less than or equal to 0.2 MPa or atmospheric pressure is supersaturated with dissolved gas in the micro-bubble generator, efficiently cutting to prepare a large number of micro-bubbles, high-efficiency swirl gas-liquid separation through large bubble separator, operating pressure is 0.45-0.65 MPa, after the large bubbles are separated, carbon-dioxide micro bubbles with a uniform particle size of less than or equal to 10 mu m are generated; and (xi) using high pressure induced draft fan to extract part of the flue gas from the steam injection boiler flue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The step (v) further comprises pressurizing sewage and carbon-dioxide supersaturated wastewater after holding pressure 1.6-4 MPa in a closed hydraulic cylinder or pressure vessel, reducing the pressure of the sewage through an expander or a throttle valve to a stable tank of 1.2-1 MPa, controlling the pressure difference between the front and back of the expander, micro-bubbles can be analyzed smoothly, adding a capsule accumulator between the hydraulic system and the expander or throttle to stabilize the inlet pressure of the expander or throttle, in the stabilization tank, the gas re-analyzed under the pressure of 1.2-1 MPa, excess gas that has not been dissolved in the sewage when the sewage is pressurized is discharged from the upper part of the stabilization tank, transferring tail gas carbon-dioxide circulation pipeline, and the stable water quality is discharged from the lower part of the stabilization tank. The step (vii) further comprises transferring the above sewage into cyclone separator II for oil-water cyclone separation under the pressure control of 0.8-1 MPa, discharging dirty oil foam by overflow port of the cyclone separator II at 0.2-0.3 MPa, separating dirty oil foam by foaming by the gas-liquid separator under operating pressure of 0.2-0.3 MPa, the gas phase is tail gas carbon-dioxide conveyinging the tail gas circulation line, liquid phase is a high-concentration sludge oil and water de-sludge dehydrating machine and dehydrating until commercial crude oil is obtained; (viii) discharged underflow sewage from the cyclone separator II at 0.4-0.6 MPa, sewage oil less than or equal to 25-20 mg/l, conveying micro-bubble air floatation separator, air-float separation at less than or equal to 0.2 MPa or atmospheric pressure, when oil-containing wastewater with a difference in oil-water density not greater than 0.05-0.1 g/cm3, purified water discharged after the secondary micro-bubble air floatation treatment can reach an oil content of less than or equal to 5 mg/l, contains technical indicators of suspended matter less than or equal to 1 mg/l and suspended particle size less than or equal to 1 mu m, conveying purified water the gas injection boiler feed water system or the environmental drainage system; the dirty oil foam de-sludge dehydrating machine that overflows from the micro-bubble air-float separator is aerated and condensed, after removing traces of solid sludge at atmospheric pressure, after dehydration, it becomes commercial crude oil and conveying tail gas carbon-dioxide analyzed from the micro-bubble air floatation separator into tail gas circulation pipeline. The step (xi) further comprises using high pressure induced draft fan to extract part of the flue gas from the steam injection boiler flue, exhaust gas and excess exhaust gas discharged through heat exchangers and devices exchange heat and cool down, removing the solid suspended particles above 20 mu m through the flue gas filter, compressing into a gas storage tank of 0.8-1 MPa with a flue gas compressor, which becomes the gas source of the device, when the flue gas is used, discharged from the flue gas storage tank, filtered by a flue gas precision filter to remove solid suspended particles above 10 mu m, in order to reduce the secondary pollution of water quality, it becomes clean flue gas, and then conveying the flue gas mixer to mix with sewage, excessive clean flue gas in the pre-saturator and nitrogen, oxygen and other gases are not dissolved in the sewage are used as exhaust gas, excess exhaust gas carbon-dioxide, which is enriched with the device discharged by the regulating valve, is exchanged with the flue gas extracted from the flue of the steam injection boiler through a heat exchanger, and then returned to the boiler flue for incineration and digestion.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))
MC D04-A01F1; D04-A01F3; D04-A01J; D04-A01K2; D04-B03; Q42-D

   KANEOKA L
   KIM D W
AE BKT CO LTD (BKTB-Non-standard)
GA 2021704465
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has an aeration tank (115) for receiving excrement or manure digestion liquid from the outside and aeration. A pump (120) provides air for aeration to the aeration tank. A mixing tank (135) receives the gas generated in the aeration tank and mixes with water. A liquid storage tank (140) stores only the liquid component in the solution mixed in the mixing tank. The gas generated in the aeration tank includes carbon dioxide and ammonia. The mixing tank dissolves carbon dioxide in water. The mixing tank dissolves ammonia with a solution in which the carbon dioxide is dissolved and the pH is lowered. The carbon dioxide gas is additionally introduced into the mixing tank from the outside. The carbon dioxide gas is in the form of micro or nano bubbles. A vortex generator is used to produce micro or nano bubbles.
   USE - Ammonia recovery device for recovering ammonia in livestock manure in liquid state.
   ADVANTAGE - The ammonia recovery device is capable of recovering ammonia in a liquid state in manure at low cost without the use of chemicals.

The carbon dioxide is dissolved in water in the mixing process. The ammonia is dissolved in a solution in which the carbon dioxide is dissolved and the pH is lowered in the mixing process.

PN DE202019100277-U1
TI System with dry ice blasting device, has supply unit which has expansion valve, by which carbon dioxide escaping from gas bottle is expanded with supply unit such that dry ice snow is generated which is introduced into pressing chamber.
AU 
AE MAYER M (MAYE-Individual)
GA 2020487427
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a pressing unit, an extruder die (3) and a punch (7) that is movable against the extruder die. A feed unit is provided by which liquid, cryogenic carbon dioxide is introduced into a press chamber (2) delimited by the extruder die and the punch. The dry ice snow is pressed to a dry ice pellet by guiding the punch against the extruder die, and the supply unit is assigned a storage unit in the form of a gas bottle (10) in which carbon dioxide is stored liquefied at ambient temperature under pressure. The supply unit has a pressure reducing valve (11) in which carbon dioxide escaping from the gas bottle is expanded, so that at the outlet of the pressure reducing valve there is cryogenic liquid carbon dioxide which is fed to the pressing chamber. The supply unit has an expansion valve, by which carbon dioxide escaping from the gas bottle is expanded with supply unit in such a way that dry ice snow is generated which is introduced into the pressing chamber.
   USE - System with dry ice blasting device for producing dry ice pellets used for cleaning in the form of jets from different devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The storage and logistics for providing the carbon dioxide required for the production of dry ice pellets is thus considerably simplified, as a result of which the manufacturing costs of the entire device are reduced. The cryogenic carbon dioxide generated in the pressure reducing valve can be fed directly to the press chamber, which results in a particularly simple construction of the feed unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for producing dry ice pellets for the system.
   Press chamber (2)
   Extruder die (3)
   Punch (7)
   Gas bottle (10)
   Pressure reducing valve (11)

   (2) producing methane which involves 1st                source gas supply process which supplies source gas                so that source gas containing a carbon dioxide may                flow through the inside of reactor into 1st                direction with respect to reactor which                accommodates catalyst (11) containing metallic                element which has methanation catalyst performance,                and metallic compound which has carbon-dioxide                occlusion capacity, and 2nd hydrogen supply process                which supplies hydrogen so that hydrogen may flow                through the inside of reactor into 1st direction                and different 2nd direction.

   Source gas supply unit (40)

The control unit                  supplies the hydrogen with respect to reactor to                  which source gas is supplied. Preferred Process:                  The source gas can be supplied so that source gas                  may flow through the inside of reactor into 2nd                  direction. After each setting the source gas in                  reactor, and the flow of hydrogen to 1st                  direction and supplying the source gas and                  hydrogen, the source gas in reactor and flow of                  hydrogen are each changed to 2nd direction, and                  the source gas and hydrogen are supplied. The 2nd                  direction is reverse direction of 1st direction.                  The cycle which performs 1st hydrogen supply                  process after 1st source gas supply process, and                  the cycle which performs 2nd hydrogen supply                  process after 2nd source gas supply process are                  performed alternately.

GA 202247316C

The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process is: adding the marijuana powder into the extraction kettle of the supercritical extraction apparatus, using supercritical carbon dioxide as the solvent, 60-100 wt.% ethanol solution as the cosolvent, and adding cosolventby continuous pump input, adjusting the relevant parameters of the extraction kettle, extracting at 30-40degrees Celsius preferably 40degrees Celsius under pressure of 25-35 MPa, preferably 30 MPa and flow rate of carbon dioxide of 30-40 l/hour, preferably 35-40 l/hour, preferably 40 l/hour for 3-5 hours, preferably 4 hours adjusting the relevant parameters of the separation kettle, carrying out separation and analysis under pressure of 8-10 MPa, preferably 8 MPa and separating at 20-30degrees Celsius, preferably 30degrees Celsiusto obtain a yellow paste product, where the mass ratio of the cosolvent and the marijuana powder is 1-3:20, preferably 1.5-2.5:20, and 60-80 wt.%, preferably 80 wt.% ethanol is used as the cosolvent.

PN CN109368661-A
TI Alkaline extraction of tungsten using excess ammonium comprises e.g. introducing carbon dioxide into the recovered aqueous ammonia, stopping accessing carbon dioxide, and adding solid ammonium hydrogencarbonate.
AU LI X
   JIANG C
   LEI Y
   PENG Y
   YANG J
   LONG Y
   WANG Q
   CAO Y
   XIAO L
   YANG D
AE HUNAN MAOTIAN CENTURY NEW MATERIAL CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201920939A
AB    NOVELTY - Alkaline extraction of tungsten using excess ammonium comprises introducing carbon dioxide into the recovered aqueous ammonia, real-time monitoring of the free ammonium concentration in the reaction solution, stopping accessing carbon dioxide when the free ammonium concentration is in the range of 1-1.5 mol/l, and adding solid ammonium hydrogencarbonate to obtain stripping agent.
   USE - The method is useful for alkaline extraction of tungsten using excess ammonium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the cost of the purchase ammonium bicarbonate; recovers the aqueous ammonia is completely used; and solves ammonium high concentration ammonia excess and water treatment problems.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of free ammonium in the recovered ammonia water is 2-3 mol/l. The total carbon concentration in the stripping agent is measured, and the amount of solid ammonium hydrogencarbonate added is such that the total C concentration in the stripping agent reached 3-3.5 mol/l. The concentration of free ammonium in the reaction solution was monitored by titration in real time. The total C concentration in the stripping agent is measured using a carbon-sulfur tester. The carbon dioxide is derived from purchased liquid carbon dioxide.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E35; E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.)

PN CN114177771-A
TI Photocatalytic reduction exhaust gas integrated            recycling treatment device has gas fractionator,            sublimation chamber, pressure chamber, liquid-phase            reaction chamber and waste gas recovery device.
AU TENG F
   FAN H
   HU P
   KANG T
   REN Y
   LIU S
   ZHANG M
   YANG X
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 202244092Y
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic reduction exhaust gas                integrated recycling treatment device has a gas                fractionator (1), a sublimation chamber (5), a                pressure chamber (8), a liquid-phase reaction                chamber (12) and a waste gas recovery device (16).                Outlet of the gas fractionator (1) is connected                with the sublimation chamber (5). The sublimation                chamber (5) is connected with the pressure chamber                (8). The pressure chamber (8) is connected with the                liquid-phase reaction chamber (12). The                liquid-phase reaction chamber (12) is connected                with the waste gas recovery device (16). The waste                gas recovery device (16) is connected to the gas                inlet of the gas fractionator (1). The gas                fractionator (1) is also provided with an exhaust                gas inlet (2).
   USE - Photocatalytic reduction exhaust gas                integrated recycling treatment device.
   ADVANTAGE - Photocatalytic reduction exhaust gas                integrated recycling treatment device not only                effectively separate carbon dioxide and nitrogen                oxide from industrial waste gas, so that the carbon                dioxide and nitric acid existing in the solution is                fully reacted in the liquid phase reaction chamber                to generate products such as methanol, nitrogen,                oxygen and the like, but also realize internal                circulation of the carbon dioxide in the device,                separate other gas products generated by the                photocatalytic reduction reaction, and improve the                recovery utilization rate of the carbon dioxide in                the industrial waste gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                method of using the photocatalytic reduction                exhaust gas integrated circulation treatment                device, which involves: i.filling the pressure                chamber (8) with pure water, and keep flowing pure                water; ii. after the industrial waste gas to be                treated is filtered and processed, waste gas inlet                (2) is introduced, and air is introduced, and the                temperature inside the fractionator (1) is kept at                100 degreesC to obtain solid carbon dioxide and                nitrogen dioxide; iii. solid carbon dioxide and                nitrogen dioxide are input into the sublimation                chamber (5), the pressure in the sublimation                chamber (5) is increased during the sublimation                process, and dissolving gaseous carbon dioxide and                nitrogen dioxide into the pure water in the                pressure chamber (8) to form carbon dioxide and                nitric acid solution; iv. after the carbon dioxide                and the nitric acid solution pass through the                liquid-phase reaction chamber (12), the carbon                dioxide is reduced to methanol, and the nitric acid                is reduced to nitrogen and oxygen, and the solution                and the generated gas are input into the waste gas                recovery device (16); and v. inside of the waste                gas recovery device (16) is kept at a low pressure,                and carbon dioxide in the solution is precipitated                and separated in the fractionator (1).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                structure a photocatalytic reduction exhaust gas                integrated recycling treatment device. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
   Gas fractionator (1)
   Waste gas inlet (2)
   Sublimation chamber (5)
   Pressure chamber (8)
   Liquid-phase reaction chamber (12)
   Waste gas recovery device (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Material of the liquid                  phase reaction chamber is quartz glass, double                  helical zinc sulfide/cadmium sulfide                  photocatalyst (13) is installed inside. Preferred                  Method: Double helical zinc sulfide/cadmium                  sulfide (ZnS/CdS) photocatalyst is prepared by                  the following process, which involves: i. mixing                  zinc acetate dihydrate, cadmium acetate dihydrate                  and thiourea and dissolving in deionized water,                  stirring evenly, and then adding with hydrazine                  hydrate, after heating for reaction,                  centrifuging, washing, and drying to obtain                  ZnS/CdS photocatalyst powder, zinc                  sulfide/cadmium sulfide (ZnS/CdS) photocatalyst                  powder is dispersed in water, dispersion is                  obtained, and the dispersion is coated on the                  double helix structure load.

AU CHO S S

   SEO M H
   LEE S Y
AE INST ADVANCED ENG (ADVG-C)
GA 2018999917
AB    NOVELTY - Rotary carbon dioxide mineralization reactor comprises a raw material reservoir (200) for accommodating calcium aqueous solution, a metering pump (120) for discharging the calcium solution in the raw material reservoir, provided at an outlet side of the raw material reservoir, a gas supplying unit (300) for supplying carbon dioxide to the discharged calcium aqueous solution, a discharge tank (500) for temporarily storing the product and unreacted material generated by reaction in a reaction pipe (400), a first feedback line for re-supplying unreacted gas to the gas supplying unit, a filter unit for separating the mixture of calcium carbonate and water into calcium carbonate and water, and a second feedback line for re-supplying water separated from the filter unit to the raw material storage tank.
   USE - Used as rotary carbon dioxide mineralization reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor maximizes contact time between the raw material and the carbon dioxide gas for mineral carbonation, measures reaction state between the raw material and the carbon dioxide gas in real time, adjusts the reaction environment by measuring predetermined target value, and improves carbonate conversion rate, the pH concentration and the carbon dioxide concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rotary carbon dioxide mineralization reactor comprises either: a raw material reservoir (200) for accommodating calcium aqueous solution, a metering pump (120) for discharging the calcium solution in the raw material reservoir, provided at an outlet side of the raw material reservoir, a gas supplying unit (300) for supplying carbon dioxide to the discharged calcium aqueous solution, a discharge tank (500) for temporarily storing the product and unreacted material generated by reaction in a reaction pipe (400), a first feedback line for re-supplying unreacted gas to the gas supplying unit, a filter unit for separating the mixture of calcium carbonate and water into calcium carbonate and water, and a second feedback line for re-supplying water separated from the filter unit to the raw material storage tank; or the raw material reservoir, metering pump, a gas supplying unit, a reaction pipe for reacting the calcium aqueous solution and the carbon dioxide, and moving the aqueous calcium solution and carbon dioxide mixture in zigzag form, and the discharge tank, where the reaction pipe is provided with a first pipe for guiding the mixture in a first direction of the reactor, a second pipe for guiding the mixture in a third direction of the reactor, and a third pipe for guiding the mixture in the second direction of the reactor. The first pipe, the second pipe, and the third pipe is provided with multiple second electrodes arranged in a staggered fashion in the first direction, the third direction and the second direction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the reactor.
   Metering pump (120)
   Raw material reservoir (200)
   Gas supplying unit (300)
   Reaction pipe (400)
   Discharge tank (500)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The raw material reservoir comprises an agitator for stirring the calcium aqueous solution. The reaction pipe is provided with line mixer for mixing the mixture by moving the mixture, observation window for observing flow of the mixture. The reactor further comprises a meter for measuring carbonate conversion, pH concentration and carbon dioxide concentration of the mixture in the reaction pipe, and a controller for controlling the supply amount of the carbon dioxide gas and the aqueous calcium solution to the reaction pipe to bring the carbonate conversion, the pH concentration and the carbon dioxide concentration to a predetermined target value.

TI Sulfur recovery involves e.g. feeding tail gas            stream to hydrogenation reactor to produce hydrogenated            gas stream, feeding hydrogenated stream to quench tower            and nitrogen and feeding first outlet gas stream to            second stage adsorption unit.
AU LITHOXOOS G
   WANG Y
   OTHMAN R
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202049966Q
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfur recovery comprises: feeding a tail gas                stream to a hydrogenation reactor (104) to produce                a hydrogenated gas stream by converting                sulfur-containing compounds to hydrogen sulfide;                feeding the hydrogenated gas stream to a quench                tower (106) to produce a quenched gas stream by                condensing and recovering liquid water via a water                condensate stream, where the quenched gas stream                comprises hydrogen sulfide and at least one of                water, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen; feeding the                quenched gas stream to a first stage adsorption                unit to produce a first outlet gas stream by                separating water via a first byproduct stream from                hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen; and                feeding the first outlet gas stream to a second                stage adsorption unit (112) to produce a second                outlet gas stream by separating carbon dioxide and                nitrogen via a second byproduct stream.
   USE - The method is useful for recovery of                sulfur.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sulfur recovery comprises: feeding a tail gas                stream to a hydrogenation reactor to produce a                hydrogenated gas stream by converting                sulfur-containing compounds to hydrogen sulfide,                where the tail gas stream includes a Claus tail gas                comprising the sulfur-containing compounds, the                hydrogenated gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide,                water, and at least one of carbon dioxide and                nitrogen, and the hydrogenated gas stream is in the                absence of the sulfur containing compounds other                than hydrogen sulfide; feeding the hydrogenated gas                stream to a quench tower to produce a quenched gas                stream by condensing and recovering liquid water                via a water condensate stream, where the quenched                gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide and at least                one of water, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen; feeding                the quenched gas stream to a first stage adsorption                unit to produce a first outlet gas stream by                separating water via a first byproduct stream from                hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen,                where the first outlet gas stream comprises                hydrogen sulfide and at least one of carbon dioxide                and nitrogen, and the first outlet gas stream is in                the absence of water; and feeding the first outlet                gas stream to a second stage adsorption unit to                produce a second outlet gas stream by separating                carbon dioxide and nitrogen via a second byproduct                stream, where the second outlet gas stream                comprises hydrogen sulfide, the second outlet gas                stream is in the absence of carbon dioxide and                nitrogen. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                a Claus tail gas treatment system for sulfur                recovery, comprising a hydrogenation reactor, the                hydrogenation reactor configured to receive a Claus                tail gas and to convert sulfur-containing compounds                to hydrogen sulfide such that a hydrogenated gas                stream is produced, where the Claus tail gas                comprises the sulfur-containing compounds, the                hydrogenated gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide,                water, and at least one of carbon dioxide and                nitrogen, the hydrogenated gas stream is in the                absence of the sulfur-containing compounds other                than hydrogen sulfide, a quench tower, the quench                tower fluidly connected downstream of the                hydrogenation reactor to receive the hydrogenated                gas stream, the quench tower configured to condense                and recover liquid water via a water condensate                stream and to produce a quenched gas stream, where                the quenched gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide                and at least one of water, carbon dioxide, and                nitrogen, a first stage adsorption unit, the first                stage adsorption unit fluidly connected downstream                of the quench tower to receive the quenched gas                stream, the first stage adsorption unit configured                to separate water via a first byproduct stream from                hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen to                produce a first outlet gas stream, where the first                outlet gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide and at                least one of carbon dioxide and nitrogen, the first                outlet gas stream is in the absence of water, and a                second stage adsorption unit, the second stage                adsorption unit fluidly connected downstream of the                first stage adsorption unit to receive the first                outlet gas stream, the second stage adsorption unit                configured to separate carbon dioxide and nitrogen                via a second byproduct stream to produce a second                outlet gas stream, where the second outlet gas                stream comprises hydrogen sulfide, and the second                outlet gas stream is in the absence of carbon                dioxide and nitrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic diagram for                Claus tail gas treatment.
   Hydrogenation reactor (104)
   Quench tower (106)
   First cooler (108)
   First stage adsorption unit (110)
   Second stage adsorption unit (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: heating the tail gas stream to a first                  temperature of 240-260 degrees C; cooling the                  quenched gas stream to a second temperature of                  15-30 degrees C; cooling the first outlet gas                  stream to a third temperature of 15-30 degrees C;                  introducing an air feed to the first stage                  adsorption unit during a regeneration cycle such                  that hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and                  nitrogen desorbs from the first adsorbent to form                  the first outlet gas stream; introducing an air                  feed to the first stage adsorption unit during a                  regeneration cycle such that water desorbs from                  the first adsorbent to form the first byproduct                  stream; introducing an air feed to the second                  stage adsorption unit during a regeneration cycle                  such that hydrogen sulfide desorbs from the                  second adsorbent to form the second outlet gas                  stream; and feeding the second outlet gas stream                  to a Claus unit. The first stage adsorption unit                  comprises at least three first adsorption vessels                  fluidly connected in parallel such that a                  continuous flow of the first outlet gas stream is                  provided by having one of the at least three                  first adsorption vessels in an adsorption cycle,                  one of the at least three first adsorption                  vessels in a regeneration cycle, and one of the                  at least three first adsorption vessels in a                  standby cycle. The first stage adsorption unit                  comprises a first adsorption vessel including a                  first adsorbent, where the first adsorbent                  adsorbs hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and                  nitrogen while passes water to form the first                  byproduct stream during an adsorption cycle. The                  first adsorbent comprises an all silica zeolite                  having a framework comprises MFI and/or CHA. The                  first stage adsorption unit comprises a first                  adsorption vessel including a first adsorbent,                  where the first adsorbent adsorbs water while                  passes hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and                  nitrogen to form the first outlet gas stream                  during an adsorption cycle. The first adsorbent                  comprises a hydrophilic 3 Angstrom molecular                  sieve. The second stage adsorption unit comprises                  at least three second adsorption vessels fluidly                  connected in parallel such that a continuous flow                  of the second outlet gas stream is provided by                  having one of the at least three second                  adsorption vessels in an adsorption cycle, one of                  the at least three second adsorption vessels in a                  regeneration cycle, and one of the at least three                  second adsorption vessels in a standby cycle. The                  second stage adsorption unit comprises a second                  adsorption vessel including a second adsorbent,                  where the second adsorbent adsorbs hydrogen                  sulfide while passes carbon dioxide and nitrogen                  to form the second byproduct stream during an                  adsorption cycle. The second adsorbent comprises                  a zeolite having a framework selected from the                  group consisting of MFI, chabazite, faujasite,                  mordenite, Deca-dodecasil 3R, ferrierite, MWW,                  metal organic frame-work (MOF) and/or zeolite                  imidazolate framework.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The Claus tail gas                  treatment system further comprises: a heater, the                  heater fluidly connected upstream of the                  hydrogenation reactor, the heater configured to                  heat the Claus tail gas to a first temperature of                  240-260 degrees C; a first cooler (108), the                  first cooler fluidly connected downstream of the                  quench tower and fluidly connected upstream of                  the first stage adsorption unit (110), the first                  cooler configured to cool the quenched gas stream                  to a second temperature of 15-30 degrees C; and a                  second cooler, the second cooler fluidly                  connected downstream of the first stage                  adsorption unit and fluidly connected upstream of                  the second stage adsorption unit, the second                  cooler configured to cool the first outlet gas                  stream to a third temperature of 15-30 degrees C.                  The first stage adsorption unit and the second                  stage adsorption unit are configured to receive                  an air feed during a regeneration cycle to desorb                  molecules adsorbed to an adsorbent in an                  adsorption cycle. The first stage adsorption unit                  comprises at least three first adsorption vessels                  fluidly connected in parallel such that a                  continuous flow of the first outlet gas stream is                  provided by having one of the at least three                  first adsorption vessels in an adsorption cycle,                  one of the at least three first adsorption                  vessels in a regeneration cycle, and one of the                  at least three first adsorption vessels in a                  standby cycle. The first stage adsorption unit                  comprises a first adsorption vessel including a                  first adsorbent, where the first adsorbent is                  configured to adsorb hydrogen sulfide, carbon                  dioxide, and nitrogen while passing water to form                  the first byproduct stream during an adsorption                  cycle. The first stage adsorption unit comprises                  a first adsorption vessel including a first                  adsorbent, where the first adsorbent is                  configured to adsorb water while passing hydrogen                  sulfide, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen to form the                  first outlet gas stream during an adsorption                  cycle. The second outlet gas stream is fluidly                  connected upstream of a Claus unit.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109517816-A; CN109517816-B
TI Preparing immobilized carbonic anhydrase comprises e.g. adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate aqueous solution to 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution, stirring, adding carbonic anhydrase solution, stirring, centrifuging, and washing.
AU ZHANG S
   SHEN Y
   DU M
   CHEN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201929461M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing immobilized carbonic anhydrase comprises adding aqueous solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate to 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution, stirring at 30-40 degrees C for 5-6 minutes, adding carbonic anhydrase solution, stirring for 20-25 minutes, centrifuging the mixture, and washing the precipitate with de-ionized water after the reaction is complete to obtain immobilized carbonic anhydrase, where the carbonic anhydrase solution is carbonic anhydrase solution preparing by dissolving carbonic anhydrase in aqueous solution of polyvinylpyrrolidone to make 7.5-15 mg/ml.
   USE - The immobilized carbonic anhydrase is useful in absorbent for capturing the carbon-dioxide in flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The anhydrase: strengthens organic amine solution in absorbent to absorb carbon dioxide in the flue gas; increases carbon-dioxide absorption rate and absorption capacity under the same conditions, where the absorption rate can reach 80-90% of traditional MEA process, and the absorption capacity increased to 40-60%, which has significantly improves economic and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing absorbent from the immobilized carbonic anhydrase, comprising mixing immobilized carbonic anhydrase with tertiary amine and water, where the concentration of the tertiary amine in the absorbent is 1-5 molar/l, and the mass concentration of immobilized enzyme particles is 1.5-15 wt.%; and
   (2) capturing flue gas carbon-dioxide using absorbent, comprising the utility model adopts flue gas carbon dioxide capture system for flue gas carbon-dioxide capture, the flue gas carbon-dioxide capture system includes packed absorption tower, cyclone separator, desorption tower, reboiler and condensing reflux device, the top of packing absorption tower is provided with flue gas outlet, the inlet of absorption liquid has flue gas inlet and absorption liquid outlet at the bottom, the bottom of the cyclone separator is provided with slurry outlet, the top side is provided with liquid inlet, the top is provided with liquid outlet, the top side of desorption tower is provided with desorption liquid inlet, condensed water inlet, the top end is provided with carbon-dioxide mixed water vapor outlet, the bottom side is provided with heat source inlet, the bottom end is provided with desorption liquid outlet, the condensing reflux device is provided with inlet and carbon-dioxide outlet, water outlet, the absorption liquid outlet of packing absorption tower is in communication with liquid inlet of cyclone separator, the liquid outlet of cyclone separator is passed through second pump, passed through heat exchanger to desorption liquid inlet of desorption column, the desorption liquid outlet of desorption column passes through reboiler, the third pump, heat exchanger are connected to absorption liquid inlet of packed absorption tower to form liquid circulation, the slurry outlet of cyclone is in communication with absorption inlet of packed absorption column through first pump, the carbon dioxide mixed water vapor outlet of desorption column is in communication with inlet of condensing reflux unit, the water outlet of condensing reflux device is in communication with condensate inlet of desorption column, at the same time, the heat generated by reboiler enters the desorption column through heat source inlet, the method for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas includes, adding the absorbent from absorption liquid inlet into packing absorption tower, at the same time, introducing the simulated flue gas into the packed absorption tower from the flue gas inlet, treating the absorbent, simulated flue gas by packed absorption tower, and entering the cyclone from outlet of the absorption liquid through liquid inlet, and after being processed by cyclone separator,slurry from the slurry outlet through first pump from the absorption liquid inlet back into packing absorption tower, the liquid treated by cyclone is passed from liquid outlet to second pump, the heat exchanger enters desorption column from desorption liquid inlet, the desorbed liquid passes from desorbing liquid outlet through the reboiler, and then returns from absorption liquid inlet to packing absorption tower through third pump and heat exchanger, the carbon-dioxide and water vapor treated by desorption column are discharged from the carbon-dioxide mixed water vapor outlet through inlet into condensing reflux device, after the condensation treatment, the carbon-dioxide is discharged from carbon-dioxide outlet, the water enters desorption column from water outlet, and the heat generated by reboiler enters desorption column from heat source inlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of 2-methylimidazole in the aqueous solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate and the aqueous solution of 2-methylimidazole in the zinc nitrate aqueous solution is 1:12-25, the amount of carbonic anhydrase in the carbonic anhydrase solution is 5-15 mg/ml based on the volume of aqueous solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate. The concentration of aqueous solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate is 0.4-0.6 molar/l, the concentration of 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution is 0.7-1 molar/l, and the concentration of polyvinylpyrrolidone aqueous solution is 10 mg/ml. The tertiary amine is N,N-dimethylcyclohexyl amine, diethanolamine, diethylaminoethanol, or 3-diethyl -1,2-propylene glycol. Preferred Method: In the method the simulated flue gas is formulated with volume concentration of 15% carbon-dioxide mixed volume concentration of 85% dinitrogen. In the method after adding the absorbent and simulated flue gas to packed absorption tower at 40-60 degrees C, the carbon-dioxide ncapture was carried out under 1 bar conditions, the carbon-dioxide-loaded absorbent was sent to the desorption column along the pipeline, and the carbon-dioxide desorption was carried out at 90-110 degrees C and 3-4 bar, where the ratio of absorbent to simulated smoke in packed absorption tower is 5-15 l/m3. The liquid rate is 100-120 ml/hour, the gas rate is 1.2 l/hour, and the residence time of simulated flue gas in the packed absorption tower is 5-15 seconds.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN WO2022029884-A1
TI Apparatus used for producing product gas            containing carbon monoxide, comprises unit that            supplies raw material gas comprising carbon dioxide,            unit that supplies reducing gas comprising metal oxide            that reduces agent oxidized by contact with carbon            dioxide, and reactors comprising gas merging unit.
AU YUKI N
   DASANAYAKE ALUTHGE R
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 202221703B
AB    NOVELTY - A gas production apparatus (1) comprises a raw                material gas supply unit that supplies a raw                material gas comprising carbon dioxide, a reducing                gas supply unit (3) that supplies a reducing gas                comprising a metal oxide as a reducing substance                that reduces reducing agent oxidized by contact                with carbon dioxide, and reactors (4a,4b)                comprising a reaction unit (4) capable of switching                between raw material gas and reducing gas supplied                to each of the reactors, and a gas merging unit                that merges the gases after passing through the                reactor to generate a mixed gas, connected to raw                material gas supply unit and reducing gas supply                unit. The reducing agent is arranged in each of the                reactors. When a predetermined amount of the raw                material gas is supplied to each of the reactors,                or when efficiency of conversion of carbon dioxide                into carbon monoxide decreases below a                predetermined value, the raw material gas and the                reducing gas to be supplied to each of the reactors                are switched to each other.
   USE - Apparatus is used in system for producing                product gas containing carbon monoxide (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system containing the gas production                apparatus enables efficient, continuous and stable                production of product gas containing carbon                monoxide, by adjusting the switching timing between                the raw material gas and the reducing gas supplied                to the reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) gas production system (100), which                comprises a raw material gas generator that                generates the raw material gas comprising carbon                dioxide, and the gas production apparatus connected                to the raw material gas generator via the raw                material gas supply unit; and
   (2) production of gas (40), which involves                contacting the raw material gas containing carbon                dioxide with the reducing agent containing metal                oxide that reduces carbon dioxide, to convert                carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, reducing                oxidized reducing agent, merging gases after                passing through the reactor to generate mixed gas,                and recovering the mixed gas or purifying carbon                monoxide from the mixed gas as product gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas                production system. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)
   Gas production apparatus (1)
   Reducing gas supply unit (3)
   Reaction unit (4)
   Reactors (4a,4b)
   Product gas (40)
   Gas production system (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The apparatus further                  comprises a heat exchanger that exchanges heat                  between the mixed gas and the raw material gas                  before being supplied to the reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal oxide                  comprises metal elements belonging to group                  3-12.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of supply                  amount (mL/minute) of the raw material gas to the                  reactor with respect to supply amount (mL/minute)                  of the reducing gas to the reactor is 0.9-2. The                  predetermined amount is 0.01-3 mol of carbon                  dioxide per 1 mol of metal element having the                  largest mass ratio in the reducing agent. The                  predetermined value is 50-100%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN CN111810451-A; CN212360316-U
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor self-circulating stable casing includes inlet of the self-circulation channel is arranged on the wheel cover of the centrifugal compressor at the position of the trailing edge of the blade.
AU TONG Z
   SHANG P
   ZHANG C
   JU P
   FU W
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 2020A5632P
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor self-circulating stable casing includes a self-circulating channel (1), and inlet of the self-circulation channel is arranged on the wheel cover of the centrifugal compressor at the position of the trailing edge of the blade, and the outlet is arranged at the inlet of the centrifugal compressor. A guide vane (6) is provided at the inlet end of the self-circulation channel (7), and a control valve (3) is provided in the self-circulation channel. The outlet end of the self-circulation channel is provided with a rotatable guide vane that can adjust the circumferential included angle.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor self-circulating stable casing.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor self-circulating stable casing achieves the purpose of avoiding ''condensation'' at the apex of the leading edge of the main and splitter blades, and broaden the stable working range of the supercritical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor, and make the supercritical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor run efficiently and stably.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a working method of self-circulating and stabilizing casing of a supercritical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor, which involves:
   (A) closing the control valve and opening the control valve, when the working efficiency of the supercritical carbon dioxide (sCO2) centrifugal compressor is high, and when the sCO2 centrifugal compressor is about to condense;
   (B) introducing portion of the downstream high temperature and high pressure sCO2 from the entrance of the circulation channel into the self circulation channel, and changing the rotation direction of sCO2 through the guide vanes;
   (C) passing through the control valve and the rotatable guide vane in turn, it enters the inlet of the sCO2 centrifugal compressor from the outlet of the circulation channel; and
   (D) opening the control valve, and closing the control valve and introduce sCO2 from the outside hen the working efficiency of the sCO2 centrifugal compressor is low or unstable, and when the sCO2 centrifugal compressor is about to condense, and heating sCO2 to the set temperature by the heat source and entering the inlet of the sCO2 centrifugal compressor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of supercritical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor self-circulating stable casing.
   Self-circulating channel (1)
   Control valve (3)
   Heat source (4)
   Guide vane (6)
   Inlet end of the self-circulation channel (7)
DC Q56 (Non-positive displacement fluid machines/pumps/compressors (i.e. for driving fluid) (F04D,F))

PN EP3626861-A1; WO2020057998-A1; KR2021060468-A; CN112912543-A; EP3853396-A1; US2021348286-A1; JP2022500558-W
TI Electrolysis cell comprises cathode half-shell with cathode, gas space connected to first gas feed line for carbon dioxide gas and first gas discharge line for gaseous reaction products, catholyte inlet and catholyte outlet.
AU VOR D E L
   BULAN A
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 202023048R
AB    NOVELTY - Electrolysis cell comprises a cathode half-shell (1) with a cathode, a gas space (4) connected to a first gas feed line (5) for carbon dioxide gas and a first gas discharge line for gaseous reaction products, preferably carbon monoxide, hydrogen and unused carbon dioxide gas, and a catholyte inlet and a catholyte outlet further comprising an anode half-shell (2) and a separator (3) arranged between the anode half-shell and cathode half-shell for separating the anode compartment and cathode compartment. The anode half-shell is provided with at least a second gas discharge line for the anode reaction product, preferably oxygen and possibly carbon dioxide, an anolyte inlet and an anolyte outlet, further comprising electrical power lines for connecting the anode and cathode to DC voltage source.
   USE - The electrolysis cell is useful for the electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide on an industrial scale.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolysis cell can be operated in an industrial scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Electrolysis cell comprises a cathode half-shell (1) with a cathode, a gas space (4) connected to a first gas feed line (5) for carbon dioxide gas and a first gas discharge line for gaseous reaction products, preferably carbon monoxide, hydrogen and unused carbon dioxide gas, and a catholyte inlet and a catholyte outlet further comprising an anode half-shell (2) and a separator (3) arranged between the anode half-shell and cathode half-shell for separating the anode compartment and cathode compartment. The anode half-shell is provided with at least a second gas discharge line for the anode reaction product, preferably oxygen and possibly carbon dioxide, an anolyte inlet and an anolyte outlet, further comprising electrical power lines for connecting the anode and cathode to DC voltage source. The cathode is designed as a gas diffusion electrode for converting carbon dioxide gas and the main dimensions of the cathode, anode and the separator are arranged vertically, and a gap for the passage of the catholyte according to the principle of a falling liquid film is arranged between the separator and the cathode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic vertical cross section of electrolytic cell.
   Cathode half-shell (1)
   Anode half-shell (2)
   Separator (3)
   Gas space (4)
   First gas feed line (5)

PT P
PN CN109824321-A; CN109824321-B
TI Carbon dioxide curing precast permeable concrete comprises recycled aggregate, nickel slag, cement, water, naphthalene water reducing agent, cellulose ether, and welan glue, where cement comprises ordinary Portland cement and fly ash.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG S
   LI B
   PAN Z
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201950055Q
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide curing precast permeable concrete comprises 55-80 pts. wt. recycled aggregate, 7-30 pts. wt. nickel slag, 18-40 pts. wt. cement, 5-25 pts. wt. water, 0.01-2 pts. wt. naphthalene water reducing agent, 0-0.02 pts. wt. cellulose ether, and 0-0.01 pts. wt. welan glue. The cement comprises 70-90 pts. wt. ordinary Portland cement, 5-15 pts. wt. fly ash, 10-30 pts. wt. steel slag, 5-15 pts. wt. lime or white ash, and 0.02-1 pts. wt. nano calcium carbonate.
   USE - Carbon dioxide curing precast permeable concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide curing precast permeable concrete has simple preparation process, fully utilizes the advantages of the permeable concrete structure and the properties of industrial by-products such as nickel slag, steel slag and fly ash, realizes efficient, rapid and environmentally friendly production by using carbon dioxide curing, enables to obtain high-strength, high-durability permeable concrete products, reduces energy consumption, effectively utilizes industrial exhaust gas, smelting waste residue and construction waste, and has good economic, environmental and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a carbon dioxide curing precast permeable concrete, which involves:
   (A) allowing the recycled aggregate to absorb water to the saturated surface;
   (B) proportionally measuring the recycled aggregate, nickel slag sand and 40-60% water, adding into the mixer, and stirring for 0.5-1 minute;
   (C) adding the binder and stirring for 1-2 minutes to obtain concrete;
   (D) adding the remaining water, water reducing agent, cellulose ether and welan glue, and stirring for 1-5 minutes;
   (E) pouring the concrete into the mold, layering and tapping the low-frequency flat vibration machine, and transferring the mold to the closed curing box or curing room;
   (F) filling the closed curing box or curing room with carbon dioxide gas, controlling the relative humidity of the curing box or curing room to be 40-70% and carbon dioxide mass fraction to 20-100%, and carrying out carbon dioxide curing for 6-24 hours to obtain concrete products; and
   (G) and performing finishing and demolding to obtain the final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The recycled aggregate has a particle size of 5-16 mm, which is a crushed recycled concrete aggregate. The nickel slag sand has a particle size of between 0.16 and 5 mm and is air-cooled or water-quenched nickel slag. The fly ash is high calcium fly ash or low calcium fly ash, the specific surface area of fly ash is more than 350 m2/kg, and the sum of the weight contents of calcium oxide and silicon dioxide is more than 50%. The steel slag has a specific surface area of more than 350 m2/kg, and the sum of the weight contents of calcium oxide and silicon dioxide is greater than 50%, where the weight content of calcium oxide is greater than 20%. The lime is slaked lime or quicklime, and its calcium oxide weight content is more than 80%, and the fineness of 0.08 mm square hole sieve is less than 18%. The white ash residue calcium oxide has a weight content of more than 50% and a specific surface area of more than 350 m2/kg.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PT P
PN WO2022075588-A1; KR2022046457-A
TI Oxygen donor particles used for producing hydrogen from water, and producing carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide, comprises core-shell structure comprising core containing metal oxide and shell containing perovskite surrounding portion or all of core.
AU LEE J W
   LEE M
   LIM H S
   KANG D
   LEE J
   LEE M B
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2022516915
AB    NOVELTY - Oxygen donor particles comprises a core-shell structure comprising a core containing a metal oxide and a shell containing perovskite surrounding portion or all of the core.
   USE - The oxygen donor particles is useful for producing hydrogen from water, and producing carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen donor particles has high activity and stability of metal oxide-perovskite, and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the oxygen donating particles, involving (a) mixing and drying the metal oxide nanoparticle suspension and a chelate solution containing a precursor of perovskite, and (b) calcining the dried sample, cooling, and pulverizing into a powder;
   (2) a method for producing hydrogen from water, involving subjecting water to a medium circulation thermochemical decomposition reaction using a reducing agent and the above oxygen donor particles;
   (3) a method for producing carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide, involving subjecting carbon dioxide to a medium circulation thermochemical decomposition reaction using a reducing agent and oxygen donor particles; and
   (4) a method for producing hydrogen and carbon monoxide from water and carbon dioxide, involving subjecting water and carbon dioxide to a medium circulation thermochemical decomposition reaction using a reducing agent and the oxygen donor particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is oxidized comprising metal element comprising a lanthanum group and/or a transition metal. The perovskite has a structure of ABO3. The reducing agent comprises methane, hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide.A = La, Ca or Sr;andB = Mn, Fe, Ni or Co.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the metal oxide to the perovskite is 1:10-10:1. Preferred Method: The method involves (a) dissolving metal oxide nanoparticles in a solvent, leaving still, and obtaining a nanoparticle suspension in the resulting lower layer of separation, (b) adding a chelating agent to the precursor solution of perovskite to obtain a chelating solution, (c) mixing and drying the nanoparticle suspension of step (a) and the chelate solution of step (b) by stirring, and (d) calcining the sample dried in step (c) at 450-900 degrees C, cooling to room temperature, and pulverizing into a powder. The process for the production of hydrogen and carbon monoxide from water and carbon dioxide, involves reducing while generating oxygen defects on the surface of the oxygen donor particles using reducing agents comprising methane, hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide in a reduction reactor, exposing the oxygen donor particles reduced in step (a) to a water environment in an oxidation reactor to reoxidize and obtain hydrogen, exposing the oxygen donor particles reduced in step (a) to a carbon dioxide environment in the oxidation reactor to reoxidize and simultaneously obtain carbon monoxide, and after step (b) or after step (c), (d) re-oxidizing with air or a gas containing oxygen to obtain additionally oxidized oxygen donor particles. Preferred Conditions: In step (a) reduction reactor, the contact time of the reducing agent and the oxygen donor particles is 0.1 to 1000 l/g catalystasteriskhour under the absolute pressure of 0.01-100 atm. In the oxidation reactor of (b) or (c), the contact time of water or carbon dioxide and oxygen donor particles is 0.1-1000 l/g catalystasteriskhour under the conditions of absolute pressure of 0.01-100 atm. The reaction temperature is 100-1200 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.1 minutes to 2 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN113694692-A; CN113694692-B
TI Preparing single atom fluid containing metal            nickel involves dissolving nickel ion salt and            supporting electrolyte in solvent, stirring uniformly            to obtain electrolyte, electrolyzing electrolyte to            obtain solution containing elementary substance.
AU WANG J
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG C
   LI Y
   ZHOU C
   YU Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021E6852G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing single atom fluid containing metal                nickel involves dissolving nickel ion salt and                supporting electrolyte in the solvent, stirring                uniformly to obtain the electrolyte, electrolyzing                the electrolyte to obtain the solution containing                nickel elementary substance, mixing the solution                containing nickel elementary substance, polyaniline                and 2,6 pyridinebis (iminoguanidine) to obtain                product.
   USE - Method for preparing single atom fluid                containing metal nickel used in carbon dioxide                capture and utilization technologies. No biological                data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation process of the single-atom                fluid containing metal nickel is simple; does not                involve high temperature and high pressure                operation; the preparation device is easy to                construct; and the preparation process can be                conveniently prepared by stable safety and the                capturing of carbon dioxide can be realized.                Because the mixed solvent containing polyaniline                and PyBIG has excellent electrochemical activity                and environmental stability based on the reaction                heat generation thermoelectric effect, the single                atom fluid can absorb carbon dioxide in the flue                gas based on reaction heat and electrochemical                effect; and it can absorb and dissolve carbon                dioxide, but also can conduct, in addition, it also                has good performance of absorbing carbon dioxide                and the single atomic fluid absorption process mass                transfer heat transfer resistance is low, is an                ideal carbon dioxide capture solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel ion salt is                  nickel nitrate hexahydrate. The supporting                  electrolyte is sodium nitrate or potassium                  nitrate. The solvent is dimethyl formamide.                  Preferred Conditions: The stirring time is 30                  minutes-1 hour. The electrolyte is used as the                  anode liquid of the electrolytic cell, the                  dimethyl formamide is used as cathode liquid, the                  anode liquid is connected with the anode of the                  power supply through the copper rod, the cathode                  liquid is connected with the cathode of the power                  supply through the carbon rod, the cathode liquid                  and the anode liquid are connected through the                  cation exchange film, the cathode liquid is                  electrolyzed; the cathode liquid after                  electrolysis is precipitated and filtered to                  obtain the solution containing nickel elementary                  substance. The concentration of the nickel ion in                  the anode liquid is 0.1-0.2 mole/L, the                  concentration of the supporting electrolyte is                  0.1-0.2 mole/L, the electrolytic voltage is 5-7                  V, and the current is 3-5 milliAmpere The                  temperature is 20-30degrees Celsius, the pressure                  is 0.1 megaPascal, the electrolytic time is 20-24                  hours. The method involves mixing polyaniline and                  PyBIG at 20-30degrees Celsius for 12 hours under                  the pressure of 0.1 megaPascal, then                  ultrasonically oscillating for 10-30 minutes to                  obtain product. The mass ratio of the polyaniline                  to PyBIG is 20:1:1-25:1.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN112657542-A
TI Preparing p-aminophenol from nitrobenzene by e.g.            adding nitrobenzene, solid acid catalyst, surfactant,            and water into batch reaction autoclave, introducing            carbon dioxide to replace air in reactor, and heating            carbon dioxide-water system.
AU HE M
   ZHANG Y
   QIAN J
   SUN Z
   WU Z
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 202150950E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing p-aminophenol from nitrobenzene                comprises adding nitrobenzene, solid acid catalyst,                surfactant, and water into a batch reaction                autoclave, where the weight ratio of nitrobenzene                to water is 1:10-40, and the weight ratio of solid                acid catalyst and nitrobenzene is 1:20-50, closing                the autoclave, and introducing carbon dioxide to                replace the air in the reactor for 3-5 times,                introducing carbon dioxide pressure is 4-6 MPa to                form the carbon dioxide/water system, then heating                to the temperature of 100-150 degrees C, start                stirring at 700 revolutions/minute, when the                temperature is stable, introducing hydrogen into                the kettle to make the pressure 4.5-6.5 MPa, and                reacting for 4-7 hours; the carrier of the solid                acid catalyst is H form zeolite socony mobil-5                (HZSM-5) molecular sieve, and the HZSM-5 molecular                sieve is loaded with platinum and magnesium, nickel                and chromium.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                p-aminophenol from nitrobenzene.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst solves the problem of sulfuric                acid pollution and post-treatment, and avoids the                corrosion problem of sulfuric acid on the device,                can adjust the acidity of the carrier, and provides                sufficient acidity of the carbon dioxide/water                system and good catalyst stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The solid acid catalyst is                  prepared by firstly placing the HZSM-5 molecular                  sieve powder into a muffle furnace at 5000700                  degrees C for 4-6 hours, taking out and soaking                  in 3-8 mol/l dilute sulfuric acid for 0.5-3                  hours, after soaking, drying, then placing in a                  muffle furnace and calcining at 300-500 degrees C                  for 4-6 hours to obtain acid-modified HZSM-5                  molecular sieve powder, then adding to platinum                  salt, magnesium salt, nickel salt and chromium                  salt in the mixed solution, controlling adding                  HZSM-5 molecular sieve powder and other volume                  impregnation, placing the impregnated material in                  an oven for drying at 400-600 degrees C for 2-4                  hours; finally taking out and placing into a                  tubular furnace and reducing at 400-600 degrees C                  for 3-5 hours, and preparing into the solid acid                  catalyst. Preferred Components: The mass content                  of platinum in the solid acid catalyst is 2-5%,                  and the total mass content of magnesium, chromium                  and nickel is 0.5-2% of the catalyst. The                  silicon-to-aluminum ratio of the HZSM-5 molecular                  sieve is 25-110. The surfactant is hexadecyl                  ammonium bromide, where the mass ratio of                  hexadecyl ammonium bromide to nitrobenzene is                  1:100. The platinum salt is potassium                  chloroplatinate or chloroplatinic acid, and the                  magnesium salt, nickel salt and chromium salt are                  respectively magnesium nitrate, chromium nitrate                  and nickel nitrate. The final mass ratio of                  dilute sulfuric acid and calcined HZSM-5                  molecular sieve powder is 1:9-11.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AU WANG D
   WANG Y
   WANG B
   ZHEN J
AE GUANGZHOU RUIHAI OCEAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
