TI Temperature and salt resistant carbon dioxide            jelly foam liquid-phase gel for selectively block water            under high temperature and high salt formation            conditions, comprises temperature-tolerant            salt-tolerant polymer and phenol or hydroquinone.
AU WANG Y
   LAN J
   JIANG B
   MENG L
   XU N
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 202031427K
AB    NOVELTY - Temperature and salt resistant carbon dioxide                jelly foam liquid-phase gel comprises 0.3-0.9 wt.%                temperature-tolerant salt-tolerant polymer, 0.2-0.9                wt.% phenol or hydroquinone, 0.2-0.9 wt.% aldehyde                compound, 0.2-0.8 wt.% foaming agent, 0.3-0.6 wt.%                organic matter containing phosphonic acid                structure, 0.3-0.7 wt.% auxiliary agent and the                balance is water. The organic substance containing                a phosphonic acid structure is one or more of                aminomethylphosphonic acid, phenylphosphonic acid,                phenylphosphonic acid and hexylphosphonic acid. The                temperature and salt resistant polymerin the                presence of a solvent and an initiator, acrylamide                or acrylamide derivatives and salt-tolerant                monomers, 2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic                acid, N-vinylpyrrolidone are prepared by                polymerization. The salt-tolerant monomer is                isooctyl acrylate or N-vinylamide.
   USE - Temperature and salt resistant carbon dioxide                jelly foam liquid-phase gel used to selectively                block water under high temperature and high salt                formation conditions (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing temperature and salt tolerant carbon                dioxide jelly foam, which involves passing carbon                dioxide gas into the liquid gel, and the gas-liquid                volume ratio of the carbon dioxide to the liquid                gel is 40-50:1-2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hydroquinone is                  catechol, resorcinol or hydroquinone. The                  aldehyde compound is formaldehyde,                  paraformaldehyde or hexamethylenetetramine. The                  auxiliary agent is sodium sulfite, thiourea,                  potassium sulfite or sodium thiosulfate. The                  foaming agent is a nonionic surfactant, which is                  one or more of octylphenol polyoxyethylene ether,                  nonylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, lauryl                  alcohol, and fatty acid polyoxyethylene ester.                  The solvent is acetone. The initiator is selected                  from benzoyl peroxide (BPO),                  azodiisopropylimidazoline hydrochloride (AIBI)                  and potassium persulfate (KPS) or beta                  -dimethylaminopropionine (DMAPN). Preferred                  Process: The temperature and salt resistant                  polymer is prepared by putting acrylamide or                  acrylamide derivatives, salt-tolerant monomers,                  2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid,                  N-vinylpyrrolidone into the reactor together with                  the solvent, passing nitrogen to remove oxygen,                  and then stirring and heating. The molar ratio of                  the acrylamide or acrylamide derivatives,                  salt-tolerant monomers,                  2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid, and                  N-vinylpyrrolidone is                  1:(2.0-5.0):(1.0-2.0):(1.0-2.5). The temperature                  is raised to the reaction temperature, nd added                  an initiator to proceed the polymerization                  reaction. The obtained crude polymer product is                  repeatedly soaked in absolute ethanol to remove                  unreacted salt-tolerant monomers and initiators,                  and then dried to obtain a temperature-tolerant                  salt-tolerant polymer. Preferred Conditions: The                  molar ratio of the acrylamide derivatives,                  salt-tolerant monomers,                  2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid and                  N-vinylpyrrolidone is                  1:(3.0-4.0):(1.0-1.5):(1.0-1.5). The molar ratio                  of the acrylamide derivative to the initiator is                  1:(0.02-0.05). The volume ratio of absolute                  ethanol to crude product is 100:(1-5).

PN CN108836470-A; CN108836470-B
TI Nozzle frosting prevention liquid-based carbon dioxide flash evaporation spray cooling device, has electromagnetic valve whose output end is provided with stainless steel two fluid nozzle that is coated with heat insulation vacuum layer.
AU ZHOU Z
   JIANG Y
   YANG X
   CHEN B
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2018944380
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a high- pressure nitrogen bottle (1) whose outlet is connected with an inlet of a carbon dioxide storage tank (5). An end of the carbon dioxide liquid storage tank is connected with a high pressure hose (6) that passes through a middle transition tube side inlet of a sleeve type heat exchanger (7). A tube side outlet of the sleeve type heat exchanger is connected to an end of the high pressure hose (6). Another end of high-pressure hose is connected with an electromagnetic valve (13). An output end of the electromagnetic valve is provided with a stainless steel straight two- fluid nozzle (14) that is coated with a heat insulation vacuum layer.
   USE - Nozzle frosting prevention liquid-based carbon dioxide flash evaporation spray cooling device for cooling normal epidermal tissue in vascular and pigmentary dermatosis laser surgery.
   ADVANTAGE - The device prevents thermal damage of epidermis, and improves treatment effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a nozzle frosting prevention liquid-based carbon dioxide flash evaporation spray cooling device.
   High-pressure nitrogen bottle (1)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (5)
   High pressure hose (6)
   Sleeve type heat exchanger (7)
   Electromagnetic valve (13)
   Stainless steel straight two-fluid nozzle (14)

PN CN207517166-U
TI Intelligent household environment alarming device, has carbon dioxide generating device connected to intelligent control module, and pipe body provided with high-temperature detector that is connected with control module and light detector.
AU JIANG X
AE WENZHOU XIBEN ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (WENZ-Non-standard)
GA 201851091A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a household intelligent environment alarm device, comprising a pipe body, the pipe body is provided with an exhaust fan, the end of the tube is located at the inner side from inside to outside in turn is provided with a loudspeaker; the intelligent control module, a carbon dioxide generating chamber, a partition plate, on the wall of the pipe body between the partition board and the exhaust fan is provided with an air inlet, outer wall of tube body is further connected with a conical hood, said suction cover corresponding to the air inlet hole is set at the position, the pipe body is provided with a fuel gas leakage probe, the fuel gas leakage probe for gas sensing leakage. the carbon dioxide generating chamber is a closed space, the corresponding carbon dioxide generating chamber position of the pipe wall of the pipe body is provided with an air outlet, the carbon dioxide generating carbon dioxide generating device is set in the chamber, the tube body is provided with a high temperature detector are respectively connected with the intelligent control module and a light detector. The purpose of the utility model is to provide a home-to-home kitchen gas leakage alarming and controlling intelligent environment alarm device.

PN CN108008753-A
TI Indoor air temperature and humidity carbon dioxide index controller for planting greenhouse, has controller base, display circuit board, and control unit base which is provided with new tuyere, connecting stud, and air inlet entrance.
AU YIN C
   REN L
   QIN X
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 2018373884
AB    NOVELTY - The controller (42) has a controller base, an air filter, base gasket, a sensing air connection tube, an electric blower, a main control circuit board, a power supply circuit board, a display circuit board, a control circuit board, a power switch board, a controller cover, a panel, gas pipelines and gas transmission hoods. The control unit base is provided with a new tuyere, a connecting stud, and an air inlet entrance. The area of the air filter is slightly larger than the area of the new tuyere and completely covers the new tuyere.
   USE - Indoor air temperature and humidity carbon dioxide index controller for planting a greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The real-time monitoring and early warning of the indoor air temperature, humidity, and carbon dioxide index in greenhouses can effectively guarantee the indoor air temperature in the greenhouses. The humidity and carbon dioxide index meet the requirements for effective plant growth and improve indoor air quality in planting greenhouses. The controller which has simple structure is easy to manufacture, simple to operate and has wide application in the modern fine agricultural production.

PT P
PN IN201621025611-A
TI Producing biogas involves hydrolyzing biomass in presence of enzymes or microbes producing enzyme selected from esterase, lactase, cellulase or hemicellulose, to obtain hydrolysed residue and gaseous effluent containing carbon dioxide.
AU KUMAR M
   SINGH M P
   SINGH D
   DAS B
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 2018121486
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biogas involves hydrolyzing a biomass in presence of enzymes or microbes producing enzyme selected from esterase, protease, lactase, cellulase or hemicellulose, to obtain a hydrolysed residue and gaseous effluent containing carbon dioxide. Portion of hydrolysed residue is subjected to acidogenesis, to obtain volatile fatty acids (VFAs) enriched with acetic acid of 70% of total VFAs, and the gaseous effluent containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The volatile fatty acids enriched with acetic acid are subjected to electro-assisted methanogenesis.
   USE - Method for producing biogas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing biogas involves hydrolyzing a biomass in presence of enzymes or microbes producing enzyme selected from esterase, protease, lactase, cellulase or hemicellulose, to obtain a hydrolysed residue and gaseous effluent containing carbon dioxide. Portion of hydrolysed residue is subjected to acidogenesis, to obtain volatile fatty acids (VFAs) enriched with acetic acid of 70% of total VFAs, and the gaseous effluent containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The volatile fatty acids enriched with acetic acid are subjected to electro-assisted methanogenesis at a potential of 0.1-1 Volts to obtain biogas containing 95% methane. The gaseous effluent comprises carbon dioxide is transferred and has a total solid loadings of more than 50%. The biomethanogenesis is carried in the presence of neutral red and nanosized iron oxide in a ratio of 2:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The biomass is heated in presence of alkali. The hydrolysis is carried for 1-14 days at 20-70 degrees C. The hydrolysis and the acidogenesis are adapted to be carried out in a single reactor or in two different reactors. The electro-assisted methanogenesis is performed in a single cell reactor with at least one cathode and one anode that are connected to each other by a conductive wire. The electro-assisted bio methanogenesis is carried in the presence of carbonic anhydrase or carbonic anhydrase producing microbes. The process is carried out at 5-65 degrees C with salinity range of 0-10%. Preferred Components: The enzymes used in hydrolysis are produced by microbes selected from Ruminococcus species, Lactobacillus species, Bacillus licheniformis, Bacillus subtilis, Pseudomonas species, Streptococcus species or a Consortium. The microbes are selected from a Bacillus subtlis MTCC 5386, Bacillus species (MTCC 5662), Bacillus cereus (MTCC 5665), Lysinibacillus species (MTCC 5666). The hydrolysis is carried out in a hydrolysis reactor fed with a nutrient system containing 0.5-4.8g/l monopotassium phosphate, 0.5-5 g/l dipotassium phosphate, 0.01-1.0g/l magnesium sulfate, 0.25-0.50g/l ammonium sulfate, 0.15-4.75g/l potassium nitrate, 0.15-4.75g/l urea, 0.15-4.75g/l Di-ammonium phosphate, 0.2-2.1 g/l zinc sulfate, 0.2-10g/l sodium chloride and 2-10ml trace element solution. The acidogenesis is carried by microbes selected from Clostridium aceticum, Acetobacter woodii, Ruminococcus species, Lactobacillus species, Bacillus licheniformis or Clostridium termoautotrophicum. The acidogenesis is carried in an acidogenesis reactor, fed with a nutrient medium containing nutrients that are selected from 4-5g/l monopotassium phosphate, 4.0-15g/l dipotassium phosphate, 0.2-2.0g/l magnesium sulfate, 0.5-10ml trace element solution, 3-10 g/l yeast extract, 5-8g/l ammonium nitrate, 25-300ppm thiamine, 10-20g/l citrate, 5-25ppm sorbitol ester, 100-1000ppm oleic acid and 20-500 ppm pantothenic acid. The cathode and the anode are selected from carbon paper, carbon cloth, carbon felt, carbon wool, carbon foam, graphite, porous graphite, graphite powder, iron, carbon nanotubes, functionalized carbon nanotubes, metals or stainless steel. The electro-assisted methanogenesis is carried by microbes selected from Methanosarcina, Desulfovibrio, Clostridium, Methanobacterium, Brevibacterium, Methanolobus, Methanosaeta, Thermotoga, Methanobacterium bryantii, Methanobacterium formicum, Methanobrevibacter arboriphilicus, Methanobrevibacter gottschalkii or Methanobrevibacter ruminantium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable examples given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC D05-A02C; D05-A02D; D05-A04A; D05-C14; H09-F03
IP C12M-001/00

PN WO2021075425-A1; JP2021552395-X
TI Disinfectant comprises organic solvent-based solution containing hydrogen ultrafine bubbles and carbon dioxide ultrafine bubbles.
AU FUJITA T
   ZHANG L
   DODBIBA G
   MATSUI H
   KUROKAWA H
   SUZUKI C
AE BELLPACK CO LTD (BELL-Non-standard)
   BELL TECHNO SERVICE CO LTD (BELL-Non-standard)
GA 202142065Y
AB    NOVELTY - Disinfectant comprises an organic solvent-based solution containing hydrogen ultrafine bubbles and carbon dioxide ultrafine bubbles.
   USE - As disinfectant.
   ADVANTAGE - The disinfectant has an active oxygen enhancing effect, and is very safe as it does not include chemical substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing the disinfectant, which involves blowing hydrogen into an ultrafine bubble-containing solution comprising carbon dioxide ultrafine bubbles to produce hydrogen ultrafine bubbles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The organic solvent-based solution is a mixed solution of an organic solvent and water. The organic solvent is at least one alcoholic organic solvent chosen from ethanol, ethylene glycol, and isopropanol, preferably ethylene glycol. The solution comprises 25-55% ethylene glycol. The organic solvent-based solution comprises one or more organic solvent chosen from isopropanol and kerosene. The solution comprises 25-35% isopropanol.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN CN114002054-A
TI Measuring and evaluating method for coke high temperature performance for blast furnace iron making, involves measuring initial temperature and specific heat capacity of coke test, and calculating new surface energy of coke after reaction for characterizing reaction strength.
AU WANG D
   WANG L
   WANG M
   DONG J
   YANG S
   LIU Q
AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 202225839D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring the initial temperature and specific heat capacity of the coke test, placing the cooled coke in the electronic pressure test machine, and performing compression test, and placing the infrared thermal imager on the outer side of the electric pressure testing machine, shooting the infrared image in the compression test process by the infrared imager, keeping coke in an atmosphere of 35% carbon dioxide, 5% water and 60% nitrogen at a second set temperature for a second set time to obtain the second process coke, placing the second process coke in an atmosphere of 90% carbon dioxide and 10% water, obtaining fully reacted coke, obtaining the crushing work and heat dissipation of carbon dioxide by electro-pressure tester, calculating consumption work of surface carbon dioxide by crushing and heat-dissipation, and (vi) taking reactivity and consumption works as the measurement evaluation index of the coke high temperature performance
   USE - Measuring and evaluating method for coke high temperature performance for blast furnace iron making.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a measurement and evaluation of coke high temperature performance for blast furnace iron making. The method is simple, the process parameter is stable, the change of the temperature system and atmosphere is consistent with the actual hydrogen-rich blast furnace, the obtained evaluation result is more objective and real.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves measuring the initial temperature and specific heat capacity of the coke test, placing the cooled coke in the electronic pressure test machine, and performing compression test, and placing the infrared thermal imager on the outer side of the electric pressure testing machine, where the axis of the infrared imager lens is perpendicular to the surface of the coal, shooting the infrared image in the compression test process by the infrared imager, keeping coke in an atmosphere of 35% carbon dioxide, 5% water and 60% nitrogen at a second set temperature for a second set time to obtain the second process coke, placing the second process coke in an atmosphere of 90% carbon dioxide and 10% water, obtaining fully reacted coke, obtaining the crushing work and heat dissipation of the carbon dioxide by the electro-pressure tester, shooting the image of the carbon dioxide shot by the heat-insulating imager to obtain the heat dissipation of carbon dioxide, calculating the consumption work of the surface carbon dioxide by the crushing and heat-dissipation, and (vi) taking the reactivity and the consumption works as the measurement evaluation index of the coke high temperature performance, where the consumption power is larger, and the coke is not easy to degrade and greater the reactivity, the coke is easy to degrade.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The average granularity of the coke is 10-30 mm. Preferred Process: The compression test is performed by placing the coke in the electronic pressure test machine, gradually increasing the reduction of the electronic pressure test machine until the coke is crushed.

PN JP2021080202-A; WO2021100767-A1
TI Methanation reactor for synthesizing methane, comprises raw material supply path for supplying hydrogen and carbon dioxide, product extraction path for extracting methane, heat transfer medium circulation path, and thermal storage device.
AU IMADA N
   YOKOYAMA K
   SASAKI G
AE MITSUBISHI POWER LTD (MIBI-C)
   MITSUBISHI POWER LTD (MIBI-C)
GA 202157484U
AB    NOVELTY - A methanation reactor (1) comprises a raw material supply path for supplying hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the reactor, a product extraction path for extracting synthesized methane from the reactor, a heat transfer medium circulation path for circulating heat transfer medium, and a thermal storage device (3) for storing a thermal storage material composition. The circulation path is configured a unit to allow heat exchange between the heat transfer medium and the material composition in combination with the thermal storage device, and to allow heat exchange between the heat transfer medium and the substance existing in the reactor.
   USE - Methanation reactor for synthesizing methane from hydrogen and carbon dioxide (claimed) for electric power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor is compact due to high heat storage amount per unit mass of chemical storage material, and ensures excellent energy efficiency of the methanation reaction. The method does not require supply of heat by surplus electric power or combustion of fossil fuel at the start of operation due to usage of generated and stored heat for initiating the methanation reaction. The heat storage and heat dissipation is easily controlled by a reaction gas such as water vapor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) operation of the reactor, which involves transferring heat from the material composition in the thermal storage device to the heat transfer medium, transferring heat from the heat transfer medium to the substance in the reactor to warm the substance in the reactor to a preset temperature, supplying hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the reactor through the raw material supply path, starting the reaction synthesis of methane from hydrogen and carbon dioxide, continuing the reaction synthesis of methane from hydrogen and carbon dioxide by supplying hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the reactor through the raw material supply path, transferring heat from the substance in the reactor, maintaining the substance in the reactor at a preset temperature by passing to a heat medium, transferring and storing heat from the heat transfer medium to the material composition in the storage device, and stopping the reaction synthesis of methane from hydrogen and carbon dioxide by stopping the supply of hydrogen or carbon dioxide to the reactor; and
   (2) methanation reaction method, which involves dissipating heat from the composition to warm the inside of the reactor, reacting hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the reactor to synthesize methane, and absorbing and storing the heat generated during the reaction in the composition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the reactor.
   Methanation reactor (1)
   Preheater (2)
   Thermal storage device (3)
   Thermal storage material container (4)
   Reactive gas container (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thermal storage material composition contains a chemical thermal storage material containing at least one selected from calcium sulfate, and hydroxides or oxides of magnesium, strontium, barium and calcium. Preferred Apparatus: The reactor further contains a preheater (2) for bringing hydrogen or carbon dioxide to a preset temperature in the middle of the raw material supply path, and a unit in which the heat transfer medium circulation path is combined with the preheater to allow heat exchange between the heat transfer medium and hydrogen or carbon dioxide present in the raw material supply path. Preferred Process: The heat is transferred from the material composition to the heat transfer medium by causing the material composition to absorb water and generate heat. The heat is transferred from the heat transfer medium to the material composition by removing water from the material composition and making the composition to absorb heat. The method further involves transferring heat from the heat transfer medium to hydrogen or carbon dioxide in the raw material supply path in the preheater.

AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 2020A36900
AB    (CN211694808-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of heating, claims a heat pump steam generating device, comprising a carbon dioxide heat pump system and a steam generating system coupled by air cooler heat exchange; the carbon dioxide heat pump system comprises an expander; the heat discharging side outlet of the air cooler is connected with the inlet of the expander; the outlet of the expander is connected with the heat discharging side inlet of the air cooler; the water vapour generating system comprises a water supply pump; the water supply pump is connected with the heat absorbing side inlet of the air cooler; the expander is connected with the water supply pump through the transmission shaft. The device provided by the utility model, carbon dioxide heat pump system uses carbon dioxide as circulating working medium, fully using the latent heat of the phase change, expander can effectively recover expansion work when supercritical carbon dioxide throttling, and transmits it to the water supply pump through the transmission shaft, so as to improve the heat pump efficiency, reducing the power consumption of the water steam generating system to the water pump, improving the energy utilization effect, making the device more clean and environment-friendly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a heat pump steam generating device.
EA    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide heat pump system provided with an expander. An exothermic side outlet of an air cooler is connected with an inlet of the expander. An outlet of the expander is connected with an exothermic side inlet of the air cooler. A water steam generating system is provided with a water supply pump. The water supply pump is connected with a heat absorbing side inlet of the air cooler. An expansion machine is connected with the water supply pump through a transmission shaft.
   USE - Heat pump steam generating device.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide heat pump system utilizes carbon dioxide as circulating working medium, and fully utilizes latent heat of the phase change. The expander can effectively recycle expansion work when ultra-critical carbon dioxide throttling, and transmit the heat to the water supply pump through the transmission shaft so as to improve heat pump efficiency. The device reduces power consumption of the water steam generating system to the water pump, and improves energy utilization effect. The device is clean and environment-friendly.

PT P
PN CN114377677-A
TI New iron-based catalyst used for preparation of            high molecular weight hydrocarbons, by photocatalytic            hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, is a new iron-based            compound containing magnesia and alumina, and is            prepared by using preset amounts of ferric salt,            magnesium salt, aluminum salt and precipitator.
AU LI Z
   ZHANG T
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 202260301U
AB    NOVELTY - An iron-based catalyst is a new iron-based                compound containing magnesia and alumina.
   USE - The new iron-based catalyst is used in the                photocatalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to                produce high molecular weight hydrocarbons                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - When the catalyst is used for preparing high                molecular weight hydrocarbon by photo catalytic                carbon dioxide hydrogenation, the carbon dioxide                used has high conversion rate. The conversion of                methane and 2-4C hydrocarbons, is enabled with high                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An iron-based catalyst is a new iron-based                compound containing magnesia and alumina of formula                Fe/FexOy/MgO-Al2O3In formula                Fe/FexOy/MgO-Al2O3,
   x = 2 or 3; y = 3 or 4.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. preparation of the new iron-based                catalyst.
   2. use of the iron-based catalyst in the photo                catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to form                high molecular weight hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Preparation of the                  new iron-based catalyst involves:1) dissolving ferric salt, magnesium salt                  and aluminum salt in deionized water, adding a                  precipitator, adding the formed mixture into a                  hydrothermal kettle, reacting at                  90-130&#8451; and crystallizing for 8-24                  hours to form a crude product; 2) washing and                  drying the crude product formed in step 1 to form                  a precursor hydrotalcite material; 3) subjecting                  the precursor hydrotalcite material to a                  hydrogen-argon mixed gas atmosphere and raising                  the temperature to 300-700&#8451; at the                  heating rate 1-5&#8451; per minute, keeping                  the temperature for 2-5 hours, changing to                  nitrogen atmosphere after temperature rise, and                  naturally cooling to room temperature.Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  magnesium salt dissolved in the deionized water,                  in step 1 of the iron-based catalyst preparation,                  is 0.2-0.04 mol/l. The concentration of the                  ferric salt dissolved in the deionized water, is                  0.1-0.02 mol/l. The concentration of the aluminum                  salt dissolved in deionized water, is 0.1-0.02                  mol/l. The molar ratio of the magnesium salt,                  iron salt and the aluminum salt used in the                  process, is 3-1: 1: 1. The amount of hydrogen                  contained in the mixed hydrogen-argon gas used in                  step 3, is 10 vol.%.Preferred Components: The magnesium salt                  used is magnesium nitrate, magnesium chloride                  and/or magnesium sulfate. The ferric salt used is                  ferric nitrate, ferric chloride and/or ferric                  sulfate. The aluminum salt used is aluminum                  nitrate, aluminum chloride and/or aluminum                  sulfate. The precipitator used is sodium                  hydroxide having addition mole number 2-8 times                  that of the magnesium salt, ferric salt and                  aluminum salt.The precursor hydrotalcite material formed                  is a magnesium, aluminum and iron containing                  compound of formula [Mg2+ 1-m-nFe3+mAl3+                  n(OH)2](m+n)+(Ax-)(m+n)/x.yH2O.In formula [Mg2+ 1-m-nFe3+ mAl3+                  n(OH)2](m+n)+(Ax-)(m+n)/x.yH2O,m + n = more than or equal to 0.2 and less                  than or equal to 0.33; x = valence number of the                  anion; y = 0.5-9 (quantity of crystal water); Ax                  -= NO3-or CO32-.Preferred Process: In step 2, the washing of                  crude product is performed by using deionized                  water, for 3-6 times. The drying of crude                  product, is performed at 40-90&#8451; for                  5-20 hours.During the use of the iron-based catalyst,                  the reaction is carried out under light                  conditions, preferably full spectrum light                  conditions. The use of the iron-based catalyst                  involves adding the iron-based catalyst into a                  light-permeable closed reaction kettle, adding                  diluted reaction gas, irradiating under                  full-spectrum condition and detecting the change                  of product along with time by using gas                  chromatography.The diluted reaction gas comprises carbon                  dioxide, hydrogen and argon in a volume ratio                  15:60: 25. The amount of iron-based catalyst                  added into the reaction kettle, is 20-120 mg/108                  ml diluted reaction gas.

PN CN113996331-A
TI Preparing a monolithic honeycomb catalyst for desorption of carbon dioxide amine solution comprises preparing carrier, mixing, aging, extrusion, drying and calcination, and modifying cerium modified structure-5 powder by molecular sieve powder.
AU ZHANG Z
   PING Y

   WANG X
   DUAN Y
   YANG H
   LIU H
   DUAN M
   WU T
AE SPIC YUANDA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION EQU (CPIC-C)
GA 202225376T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a monolithic honeycomb catalyst for desorption of carbon dioxide amine solution comprises preparing carrier, preparing cerium-ZMS-5 powder, mixing, aging, extrusion, drying and calcination; It is nano-titanium dioxide powder; modifying cerium modified structure-5 powder by molecular sieve powder; the mixing is specifically: nano-titanium dioxide powder, organic small components, glass fiber, silica sol, and mixing Ce-ZMS-5 powder body and water to make mud.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing monolithic honeycomb catalyst for desorption of carbon dioxide amine solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the device investment cost; The modified cerium modified structure -5 powder can increase the active site in the whole catalyst, so as to increase the catalytic activity of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for monolithic honeycomb catalyst for desorption of carbon dioxide-rich amine solution, where catalyzer is the catalyzer obtained by adopting the preparation method of the carbon dioxide-rich amine solution desorption monolithic honeycomb catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of carrier comprises hydrolyzing titanyl sulfate solution, calcining generated metatitanic acid to obtrain nano-titanium dioixide powder. The hydrolysis of titanyl sulfate solution is a uniform precipitation hydrolysis process, where the concentration of the precipitant is 5-10 g/l, hydrolysis time is 2 hours, the hydrolysis temperature is 100-120degrees Celsius. The calcination temperature of the metatitanic acid is 480degrees Celsius, the heating rate is 3degrees Celsius/minutes, calcination is 5-8 hours. The D50 of the nano-titanium dioxide powder is 1.2-2 mum. The preparation of the cerium -ZMS-5 powder is to immerse the HZMS-5 in a certain concentration of cerium(IV) nitrates solution, After immersion, the modified molecular sieve powder is obtained by drying and grinding at 100degreesC; The mass concentration of the cerium(IV) nitratessolution is 5-10% and dipping for 10-15 minutes. The D50 of the Ce-ZMS-5 powder is 1.2-2 mum. In the mixing step, the addition amount is 1-2 wt.% organic small components, 2-8 wt.% glass fibers, 10-15 wt.% silica sol, and 5-10 wt.% Ce-ZMS-5 powder, and nano-titanium dioxide powder and water. The plasticity of the mud is 0.2-0.6 kfg, and the moisture of the mud is 20-26%. The ageing is to age mud material for many hours . The temperature of ageing is room temperature, and ageing time is 12-48 hours. The extrusion is specifically as follows: extruding and moulding the aged mud to obtain the wet honeycomb blank catalyst; the extruding speed is 1-2.8 m/minutes. The drying is to dry the extruded wet honeycomb green body catalyst; the formed wet green body catalyst is dried at 30-100degreesC for 120-360 hours. The calcining is to calcine and activate the dried catalyst to obtain the honeycomb blank catalyst. calcining the dried catalyst for 15-40 hours at 200-600degrees Celsius .Preferred Components: The precipitator is urea, ammonia water. The organic group is divided into carboxymethyl cellulose, lactic acid, polyethylene glycol or polyethylene oxide. The silica sol is an alkaline silica sol.

PT P
PN WO2021234207-A1
TI Capturing carbon dioxide in a gas intended to be carried out in at least two reactors involves introducing an alkaline solution in the first reactor and bubble or purifying the gas, and absorbing the carbon dioxide in the form of carbonates.
AU COLZI LOPES A
   ESTRADA PEREZ J M
AE TROVANT TECHNOLOGY SL (TROV-Non-standard)
GA 2021D5219Y
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide in a gas intended to be carried out in at least two reactors involves introducing an alkaline solution in the first reactor and bubble or purifying the gas. The carbon dioxide is absorbed in the form of carbonates in the alkaline solution forming a first liquid which includes solubilized carbonates, and as a consequence a clean gas stream is formed in an upper portion of the first reactor. The first liquid is added to the second reactor which in turn includes chemoautotrophic nitrifying microorganisms. A stream of air is introduced into the second reactor and a source of nutrients reduced by nitrogen. The chemoautotrophic nitrifying microorganisms use the solubilized carbonates of the first liquid for its growth thus generating solid biomass within a resulting liquid in the second reactor.
   USE - Capturing carbon dioxide in a gas intended to be carried out in at least two reactors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves resource efficiency of the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further includes using residual nutrients resulting from anaerobic digestion and introducing the residual nutrients into the second reactor thus acting as the nutrient source reduced by nitrogen. The process includes adding one or more nitrogen-reduced nutrients which includes at least ammonium, phosphorus, fertilizers, slurry or urea. The process further includes recirculating the resulting liquid to the first reactor thus acting as an alkaline solution. The process includes adjusting the pH of the resulting liquid recirculated outside the reactor by a device. Preferred Conditions: The gas is a biogas produced from biomass in a previous stage that comprises an anaerobic digestion. The process comprises reusing a portion of the biomass generated in the second reactor in the anaerobic digestion to produce the starting biogas. The stages are carried out at gage pressures between 0-6 bar. The process intended to be carried out in three reactors, where the gas is purified and where the process also includes feeding the first liquid which includes solubilized carbonates to a third reactor where the absorbed carbon dioxide is partially or totally desorbed, regenerating its absorbent capacity. A flashing step of the first liquid is carried out where its pressure is reduced. The process includes recirculation step of a stream rich in CH 4 at the inlet of the first reactor, and mixing it with the gas. The desorption takes place at a temperature between 80 and 150 degrees C. The process includes cooling stage where the recovery of the evaporated water is carried out. The process includes a stage of compensation of a stream purged in stage c, and stage of feeding the compensated current in stage g. The stages are carried out at gage pressures between 2-15 bar.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Method for controlling fresh air function of air            conditioner, involves determining priority of fresh air            function and temperature control function, and            controlling fresh air functions if carbon dioxide            concentration of indoor space is reduced at rate higher            than set rate value.
AU LIU H
   CHEN Z
   LU J
   WANG Y
   LI J
   ZHANG S
AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 2021C9278N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the priority                of the fresh air function and the temperature                control function. The carbon dioxide concentration                of the indoor space is determined when the priority                of new air function is higher than the priority of                the fresh air function. The change rate of the                carbon dioxide in the indoor spaces is determined.                The fresh air functions are controlled if the                carbon dioxide concentration of the indoor space is                reduced at a rate higher than a set rate value. The                fresh air function is controlled to stop if the                carbon dioxide concentration of the indoor space is                reduced at a rate lower than the set rate                value.
   USE - Method for controlling fresh air function of                an air conditioner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining different                scenes of the indoor space in an accurate manner,                and starting fresh air function at a suitable time                according to different scenes.

PT P
PN CN112759195-A; CN214735181-U
TI Treatment of high-ammonia-nitrogen wastewater by adding lime to wastewater, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide to mixed solution, subjecting to solid-liquid separation, anaerobically fermenting, and calcining precipitate with biogas.
AU QIN X
   WANG C
   HUANG M
   FU S
   ZHANG X
AE GUANGXI HONGSHENGYUAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROT (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202154096W
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of high-ammonia-nitrogen wastewater comprises adding lime to wastewater to adjust pH of wastewater to obtain alkali liquor; stirring the alkali liquor to obtain mixed solution and ammonia gas, and discharging ammonia gas; introducing carbon dioxide into the mixed solution to reduce pH of mixed solution to obtain turbid liquid; subjecting turbid liquid to solid-liquid separation to obtain clear liquid and precipitate; anaerobically fermenting the clear liquid to obtain biogas; and calcining the precipitate with biogas to obtain lime and carbon dioxide, and returning lime to first step and carbon dioxide to third step.
   USE - The method is for treating high-ammonia-nitrogen wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids addition of organic acid or inorganic acid, has high anaerobic fermentation efficiency, and recycles carbon dioxide and lime, which reduces cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a processing device comprising pH adjusting device, ammonia removing device, settling device, separation device, anaerobic device, biogas processing device, calcining device, and calcium carbonate enriching device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method includes adding flocculant to wastewater, detecting pH, and adjusting pH of wastewater to 10-13, preferably 10-11, by adding lime; discharging ammonia gas by vacuum suction, air blowing, or mechanical stirring, and recovering ammonia gas and spraying with clean water to obtain ammonia water; reducing pH of mixed solution to 6-8, preferably 7.5; standing turbid liquid for 20-40 minutes, preferably 30 minutes, and filtering; and desulfurizing biogas and calcining the precipitate with desulfurized biogas.

TI Humidity-tolerant device for generating carbon            dioxide used as attractant for biting arthropods in            combination with trap, includes reaction chamber to            receive aqueous acid solution, and feeder reservoir            containing compressed pellets.
AU CLAPSADDLE B
   FREDRICKSON J
   BELL W
   HAANSTAD T
   MARTIN J
AE TDA RES INC (TDAR-Non-standard)
   TDA RES INC (TDAR-Non-standard)
GA 201938825Y
AB    NOVELTY - A humidity-tolerant carbon dioxide (CO2)                generation device (110) comprises a reaction                chamber charged with aqueous acid solution when in                use; a trap (120) for biting arthropods; a gas                outlet (130) connecting the reaction chamber and                the trap; a feeder reservoir containing compressed                pellets which comprise bicarbonate salt; and a                mechanism for controllably adding the pellets from                the feeder reservoir to the reaction chamber. CO2                is generated in the reaction chamber, passed                through the outlet and into the trap for biting                arthropods.
   USE - Humidity-tolerant device for generating carbon                dioxide used as attractant for biting arthropods in                combination with trap (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device is portable and produces steady                stream of CO2 gas over extended period, e.g. up to                12 hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                humidity-tolerant CO2 generation using the                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows perspective view of                humidity-tolerant carbon dioxide generation device                connected to arthropod trap.
   Carbon dioxide generation device (110)
   Trap (120)
   Gas outlet (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bicarbonate salt                  is sodium bicarbonate, lithium bicarbonate,                  potassium bicarbonate, ammonium bicarbonate,                  magnesium bicarbonate, calcium bicarbonate,                  and/or strontium bicarbonate. The binder may also                  be clay. The pelletizing lubricant may also be                  talc, hexagonal boron nitride, or tungsten                  disulfide. The compressed pellets further                  comprise carbonate salt including sodium                  carbonate, lithium carbonate, potassium                  carbonate, ammonium carbonate, magnesium                  carbonate, calcium carbonate, and/or strontium                  carbonate. Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises adding anhydrous salt or partially                  hydrated salt to the aqueous acid solution to                  adjust temperature of the solution prior to                  addition of compressed pellets.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous solution                  comprises acid(s) including acetic acid, ascorbic                  acid, butanoic acid, citric acid, formic acid,                  heptanoic acid, hexanoic acid, 1-octanoic acid,                  lactic acid, octanoic acid, oxalic acid,                  pentanoic acid, propanoic acid, uric acid,                  succinic acid, malonic acid, maleic acid,                  citriconic acid, norbornene dicarboxylic acid,                  gamma -hydroxybutanoic acid, benzoic acid, boric                  acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric                  acid, phosphoric acid, linear or branched 1-20C                  alkane sulfonic acid, linear or branched 2-20C                  alkene sulfonic acid, 6-20C optionally                  substituted aryl sulfonic acid, ammonium                  bisulfate, sodium bisulfate, lithium bisulfate,                  and/or potassium bisulfate. The binder may be wax                  or magnesium stearate. The pelletizing lubricant                  may be magnesium stearate or amide wax.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The compressed pellets                  further comprise binder and pelletizing                  lubricant. The binder is starch, lignin,                  cellulosic binder, or polymeric binder. The                  pelletizing lubricant is PTFE. The compressed                  pellets have mass in 10 wt.% of arithmetic mean                  pellet mass.

TI Conversion of cyclic ethylene urea in manufacture            of ethylene amine, involves contacting water with            compound comprising cyclic alkylene urea moieties, in            which portion of moieties are converted to            corresponding alkylene diamine moieties.
AU TEN KATE A J B
   VENEMAN R
   VAN DAM H
   EDVINSSON R K
   RAAIJMAKERS M J T
   KANTZER E N
   EHLERS I
   JOVIC S
   LAKE K F
   WERNERSSON S M
AE AKZO NOBEL CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (NOUR-Non-standard)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (NOUR-Non-standard)
GA 201915326C
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of cyclic ethylene urea(s) into                corresponding ethylene amine and carbon dioxide                involves contacting (i) water with cyclic alkylene                urea compound(s) comprising cyclic alkylene urea                moieties in a reaction vessel at a temperature of                150-400 degrees C, optionally in the presence of an                amine compound chosen from primary amine, cyclic                secondary amine and bicyclic tertiary amine, in                which the mole ratio of water to cyclic alkylene                urea moieties is 0.1-20, and a portion of the                cyclic alkylene urea moieties are converted to                corresponding alkylene diamine moieties and carbon                dioxide, removing carbon dioxide from the liquid                reaction mixture in a stripping vessel by feeding a                stripping fluid to the stripping vessel, and                removing a carbon dioxide-containing stripping                fluid.
   USE - Conversion of cyclic ethylene urea in                manufacture of ethylene amine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical conversion of                cyclic ethylene urea into corresponding ethylene                amine and carbon dioxide with high cyclic ethylene                urea conversion rate and improved yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The stripping vessel is                  also the reaction vessel, preferably chosen from                  packed vessel and column, vessel or column                  comprising trays, falling film column, spray                  chamber, line mixer, membrane vessel, Venturi                  loop vessel, and liquid jet loop vessel, more                  preferably randomly packed vessel, optionally                  comprising types of packing chosen from Raschig                  ring, Pall ring, Berl saddle and Intalox (RTM:                  High-performance packing), or vessel or column                  with structured packing, optionally comprising                  Mellapak (RTM: structured packing), Kerpak, Pyra                  pack G and F, and Rombapak, or vessel or column                  adapted with plates or trays, optionally                  comprising plate types chosen from valve plate                  (ballast plate), cross-stream plate, sieve plate,                  Kittel polygonal plate, preformed contact plate                  and bubble cap plate. The stripping vessel is a                  rotating packed bed vessel. The stripping vessel                  is adapted with plates, and is configured to                  operate in countercurrent flow or cross-flow mode                  using a liquid or gas continuous phase, or the                  stripping vessel is a packed vessel, e.g. packed                  column, configured to operate in countercurrent                  flow or co-current flow mode, using a liquid or                  gas continuous phase, or the stripping vessel is                  a falling film (wetted wall) column, in which                  liquid is in contact with the column wall, and                  gas phase flows through the center of the column                  in either co-current or countercurrent flow mode,                  or the stripping vessel is a spray chamber, in                  which liquid droplets are contacted with a                  continuous gas phase in either co-current flow,                  countercurrent flow or cross-flow mode, or the                  stripping vessel is a line mixer, in which the                  gas and liquid phases are fed co-currently, with                  a gas or liquid continuous phase. The stripping                  vessel is a membrane vessel, in which carbon                  dioxide transfers across the membrane to a                  permeate side of the membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The contact between the                  stripping fluid and reaction mixture is achieved                  by cross-flow or countercurrent flow. The                  conversion of cyclic ethylene urea is performed                  using more than one type of vessel, operating                  either in parallel or in series, operating in                  batch-mode, semi-batch mode or continuous mode,                  and optionally involving recycling of gas and/or                  liquid phase. The reaction (i) is performed at a                  pressure of 0.5-100 bar absolute for 1 minute to                  24 hours. Preferred Composition: The cyclic                  alkylene urea is of formula (I), preferably                  chosen from ethylene urea, urea of                  diethylenetriamine, urea of triethylenetetramine,                  diurea of triethylenetetramine, diurea of                  tetraethylenepentamine, urea of                  aminoethylethanolamine, urea of hydroxyethyl                  diethylenetriamine, urea of hydroxyethyl                  triethylenetetramine, and diurea of hydroxyethyl                  triethylenetetramine. The alkylene diamine moiety                  is of formula: R4-HN-A-NH-R5. The carbon                  dioxide-containing stripping fluid removed from                  the stripping vessel comprises from 1-60 mol%                  carbon dioxide. The stripping fluid flow rate to                  the stripping vessel is 1 m3 or more per 1 m3                  stripping vessel per hour. The stripping fluid is                  a stripping gas optionally being nitrogen, argon,                  steam and/or helium.R1,R2 = -(A-X1-)qR3;R3 = H or 1-20C alkyl optionally substituted                  with -OH and/or -NH2;X1 = -O-, -NR3-, or group of formula (i) or                  (ii);q = 0-20;R4,R5 = -(A-X2-)qR3;X2 = X1 or -NH-A-NH-;andA = 1-3C alkylene optionally substituted                  with 1-3C alkyl.

AE UNIV ZHONGYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UZYT-C)
GA 201917105W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an intelligent central control system and an ecological greenhouse season detection system. An ecological greenhouse energy supply system is connected with the intelligent central control system. The ecological greenhouse season detection system is included a Carbon dioxide detector. The Carbon dioxide detector is configured with a greenhouse soil for detecting a concentration of Carbon dioxide. The concentration of Carbon dioxide is transmitted to the intelligent central control system. The intelligent central control system is determined according to a relationship between pre-stored Carbon dioxide concentration and a season for processing a greenhouse crop production.
   USE - Agricultural ecological greenhouse intelligent linkage system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can adjust ecological environment of the greenhouse energy according to growth rule of crops in greenhouse so as to improve regulation precision. The system has greenhouse environment is promoted to meet crop growth demand so as to increase crop yield and scientifically shortening crop growth cycle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an agricultural ecological greenhouse intelligent linkage system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN CN114623659-A
TI Method for separating and recycling carbon dioxide            in waste gas, involves pressurizing and desulfurizing            waste gas, entering desulfurized waste gas into drier            dry, and condensing gas to form liquid, and            decompressing residual liquid.
AU WANG Y
   XU B
   JIA H
   YAN S
   DONG X
   TAN S
   LUAN L
   JI S
AE JINING POLYTECHNIC (JINI-Non-standard)
GA 202283655Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using an industrial waste                gas for exchanging heat and removing dust (S1). The                waste gas is pressurized and desulfurized (S2). A                desulfurized waste gas is entered (S3) into a drier                dry. A compressor is pressurized. The pressurized                gas is condensed (S4) to - 15 to -20 degrees                centigrade to form the liquid. The liquid is                decompressed (S5) to 3.3 to 3.5MPa. A residual                liquid is decompressed (S6) to 2.0 to 2.5MPa. The                generated gas is mixed in the waste gas in dry, and                the rest is liquid carbon dioxide. An adsorption                bed is introduced. The sulfur is removed in the                waste gas.
   USE - Method for separating and recycling carbon                dioxide in waste gas. Uses include but are not                limited to industry, agriculture, food, medicine                and fine chemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables directly separating and                recycling the carbon dioxide in the industrial                waste gas after heat exchange. The concentration of                the liquid carbon dioxide obtained by recycling is                high. The method enables reducing the gas                temperature, and consumption of the subsequent                condensing process, and improving the yield of                carbon dioxide. The water content of the waste dry                is less than or equal to 20 ppm, and the gas                generated by the second decompression is                multiplexed, adding to the gas after dry.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for separating and recycling carbon                dioxide in waste gas. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
   Using an industrial waste gas for exchanging                heat and removing dust (S1)
   Pressurizing and desulfurizing waste gas                (S2)
   Entering desulfurized waste gas into drier                dry (S3)
   Condensing pressurized gas to - 15 to -20                degrees centigrade to form the liquid (S4)
   Decompressing liquid (S5)
   Decompressing residual liquid (S6)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-carbon linear alpha -olefins by                carbon dioxide hydrogenation comprises using the                catalyst a mixture of carbon-supported ferrous                oxalate and potassium-containing substances at mass                ratio of 10:0.1-10:5 (10:0.4-10:0.8). The                potassium-containing substance is a                potassium-containing compound, or a                potassium-containing compound is mixed with a                calcium-containing compound or potassium-containing                compounds and magnesium-containing compounds or a                compound containing potassium, a compound                containing calcium and a compound containing                magnesium are mixed together.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-carbon                linear alpha-olefins by carbon dioxide                hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can convert carbon dioxide                efficiently to produce linear alpha olefins,                introduce additives to enhance the carbon chain                growth ability and improve the selectivity of                high-carbon linear alpha -olefin products; and                provides fresh ideas for the efficient conversion                process of selective hydrogenation of carbon                dioxide to produce high value-added chemicals with                a good industrial application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                application of the catalyst in the reaction of                carbon dioxide hydrogenation to produce high-carbon                linear alpha -olefins, comprising loading the                catalyst into a fixed bed reactor, using a                hydrogen/argon mixture with a molar ratio of 1-50%                or a hydrogen/nitrogen mixture with a molar ratio                of 1-50% or pure hydrogen for pretreatment at                350-450 degrees C at pressure 0-2 MPa and                activation time is 1-50 hours, cooling fixed bed                reactor to 300-350 degrees C and feeding the raw                material gas of hydrogen: carbon dioxide with a                molar ratio of 5:1-2:1 into the reactor for                reaction at reaction pressure of 0.5-3 MPa and the                feed space velocity of the reaction is                1000-20000/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron salt                  comprises at least one of iron nitrate, ferric                  sulfate or ferric chloride. The                  potassium-containing compound comprises at least                  one of potassium carbonate, potassium chloride or                  potassium acetate, the calcium-containing                  compound is calcium carbonate, the                  magnesium-containing compound is magnesium oxide                  or basic magnesium carbonate, and the mass ratio                  of the potassium-containing compound to the                  calcium-containing compound is (potassium:                  clacium is 2.5:1-1:1) or, the mass ratio of the                  potassium-containing compound to the                  magnesium-containing compound is (potassium:                  magnesium is 3:1-1:1) or the mass ratio of                  potassium-containing compound to                  calcium-containing and magnesium-containing                  compound is (potassium: calcium is 2.5:1-1:1) and                  (calcium: magnesium is 3:1-1:1). Preferred                  Method: The mixing is mechanical mixing.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of the                  ferrous oxalate comprises adopting one-step                  hydrothermal synthesis method, dissolving sugar                  and soluble iron salt in deionized water,                  stirring, transferring the obtained mixed liquid                  into the reactor, sealing the reactor and                  transferring it into the hydrothermal synthesis                  equipment, where the mass ratio of iron in iron                  salt to carbon element in sugar is controlled at                  1:0.5-1:10, the conditions of the hydrothermal                  synthesis process are 80-180 degrees C for 12-48                  hours, washing the obtained product with                  deionized water and absolute ethanol, and drying                  to obtain carbon-carrying ferrous oxalate. The                  method of application of the catalyst in the                  reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                  produce high-carbon linear alpha -olefins                  comprises filling the catalyst into the fixed bed                  reactor, the pretreatment temperature is 350-400                  degrees C, the pressure is 0-5M Pa, and the                  activation time is 5-10 hours and reaction                  process comprises cooling fixed bed reactor to                  320 degrees C and passing hydrogen: carbon                  dioxide with a molar ratio of 3:1 into the fixed                  bed reactor for reaction at reaction pressure of                  1-1.5MPa, and the feed space velocity of the                  reaction is 2000-4000/hour. Preferred                  Composition: The sugar comprises at least one of                  glucose, sucrose, cellulose or xylose.

PN CN111632470-A; CN212215061-U
TI Composite carbon dioxide desorption tower, has tower body for entering gas-liquid mixed fluid into tower body after desorption through gas-liquid mixing inlet pipe and exitting liquid after desorption of carbon dioxide form reboiler.
AU XUE Q
   CAO B
   WANG R
   ZHANG H
AE XIAN BENQING CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
   XIAN BENQING CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202088354F
AB    NOVELTY - The tower has a tower body fixed at lower unit of a purifying gas outlet (1). A top end of the tower body is provided with a demister (2). A lower unit of the demister is provided with a first liquid inlet distributor (3). A lower unit of the first liquid inlet distributor is provided with a filler layer (5) and an interlayer liquid distributor (6). A tray (4) is fixed between the filler layers. A second liquid inlet distributor (7) is fixed at a lower unit of the filler layer. A bottom unit of a tower kettle is provided with a liquid outlet pipe (10) for entering liquid inot a reboiler to heat and desorb through the liquid outlet pipe. The tower body enters gas-liquid mixed fluid into the tower body by a gas-liquid mixing inlet pipe (9) after desorption, and exits liquid after desorption of carbon dioxide form the reboiler.
   USE - Composite carbon dioxide desorption tower.
   ADVANTAGE - The tower reduces flow rate of rich amine liquid required when actual operating conditions need to produce less carbon dioxide, performs stripping and desorption by a tray unit of the tower body, and reduces amount of carbon dioxide gas carried out from the tower by using a packing unit as a ejection tower. The tower reduces the problems of biased flow and small operating flexibility in a packed tower, and enhances adaptability of alcohol amine solutions with different properties.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a hydraulic circuit diagram of a composite carbon dioxide desorption tower.
   Purifying gas outlet (1)
   Demister (2)
   Liquid inlet distributors (3,7)
   Tray (4)
   Filler layer (5)
   Interlayer liquid distributor (6)
   Gas-liquid mixing inlet pipe (9)
   Liquid outlet pipe (10)

PN US2020248113-A1
TI System for accelerated aging of alcohol spirit, comprises a machine frame, a pressure vessel, a carbon dioxide pressurization source, a heating mechanism and at least one processor.
AU MITCHELL J B
   GEIGER A R
   MITCHELL W H
AE PICOBREW INC (PICO-Non-standard)
GA 202074978P
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a machine frame; a pressure vessel (226); a carbon dioxide pressurization source; a heating mechanism; and at least one processor (208). The method for acelerating the aging of alcohol spirit involves detecting that the pressure vessel comprising unprocessed alcohol spirits is attached to the machine frame; causing the carbon dioxide pressurization source to pressurize the pressure vessel with carbon dioxide; causing the heating mechanism (230) to heat the alcohol spirits in the pressure vessel while pressure with the carbon dioxide; holding the pressure vessel at pressure for a first predefined time; and releasing the carbon dioxide under pressure, after the first predetermined time.
   USE - System for accelerated aging of alcohol spirit (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heating system can apply heat to maintain the contents of the pressure vessel at a constant temperature. The reflux cooling system can cool the top portion of the pressure vessel. Thw wood varieties can impart interesting flavors to the spirits. The characteristics of the aging can be enhanced or minimized at predefined temperatures and pressures. The flavors, mouthfeel, and other tasting characteristics are improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for accelerated aging, which involves receiving unprocessed alcohol spirits into a pressure vessel; pressurizing the alcohol spirits in the pressure vessel with carbon dioxide to a predefined pressure of at least 30 psi; maintaining at least the predefined pressure in the pressure vessel for at least a first predefined time of at least 5 minutes; after the first predefined time, returning the pressure vessel to atmospheric pressure; and removing processed alcohol spirits from the pressure vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic or functional representation of a machine for accelerated aging of alcohol spirits.
   Processor (208)
   Memory (212)
   Controller and monitor (224)
   Pressure vessel (226)
   Pressurization system (228)
   Heating mechanism (230)
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

An                outlet of the low-temperature re-heating inlet                device is connected with a low-pressure turbine (8)                in a supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle                power generation system by a high-temperature                re-heater (64). PT P
PN CN114891588-A
TI Carbon dioxide dipping process that improves the            aroma quality of kiwifruit wine involves selecting            intact fruits with high maturity and no disease, no            injury or damage utilizing sulfur dioxide aqueous            solution to disinfect outer skin of selected fruits,            and draining water for later use.
AU TANG Q
   ZHANG N
   XU Z
   WANG T
AE SICHUAN JIANNANCHUN GROUP CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2022A7620D
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide dipping process that improves                the aroma quality of kiwifruit wine involves                selecting intact fruits with high maturity and no                disease, no injury or damage utilizing sulfur                dioxide aqueous solution to disinfect the outer                skin of the selected fruits, draining the water for                later use, putting the fruit code after screening                and cleaning into the prepared impregnation tank.                The cover of the impregnation tank should have an                exhaust valve to facilitate the overflow of excess                carbon dioxide. At the same time, the capacity of                the impregnation tank should not be too large,                carbon dioxide is introduced from the lower part of                the dipping tank. The amount of gas introduced                should account for 3 to 4 times the volume of the                tank, and then the airflow rate of the introduced                carbon dioxide is reduced. The fruit after                immersion is crushed, and the obtained pulp is                added with sugar and pectinase, and after stirring                uniformly.
   USE - Carbon dioxide dipping process that improves                the aroma quality of kiwifruit wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the fragrance quality of                kiwi fruit wine, which solves the problem that the                traditional brewing method kiwifruit wine has faint                scent and rough smell. The method uses the content                of the characteristic fragrance substance contained                in the Chinese goosebeery wine brewed by the carbon                dioxide dipping fermentation method, which is                obviously better than the traditional fermentation                method. It has important meaning for producing                high-quality Chinese goose beery wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide dipping process that improves                the aroma quality of kiwifruit wine involves                selecting intact fruits with high maturity and no                disease, no injury or damage utilizing sulphur                dioxide aqueous solution to disinfect the outer                skin of the selected fruits, draining the water for                later use, putting the fruit code after screening                and cleaning into the prepared impregnation tank.                The cover of the impregnation tank should have an                exhaust valve to facilitate the overflow of excess                carbon dioxide. At the same time, the capacity of                the impregnation tank should not be too large,                carbon dioxide is introduced from the lower part of                the dipping tank. The amount of gas introduced                should account for 3 to 4 times the volume of the                tank, and then the airflow rate of the introduced                carbon dioxide is reduced. The fruit after                immersion is crushed, and the obtained pulp is                added with sugar and pectinase, and after stirring                uniformly, the activated Saccharomyces cerevisiae                is added, and the pulp is pumped into the                fermentation tank for fermentation. The reducing                sugar content in the fermentation broth is                detected, when the reducing sugar content falls                below 3.0g/l, the fermentation is terminated, and                then the fermentation broth is pressed and filtered                to obtain the original wine. The raw wine obtained                is allowed to stand for physical sedimentation, the                raw wine after the physical sedimentation is                completed, taking the supernatant liquid, add                auxiliary material egg white powder, stirring                uniformly, continuing to stand for physical                sedimentation, finally filter with diatomaceous                earth to remove the precipitate to obtain clear                kiwi wine.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-E; D05-A02
IP C12G-003/024; C12G-003/026; C12H-001/07; C12H-001/22

PI CN10642326    08 Jun 2022
