PT P
PN WO2022015515-A1
TI Offshore power generation for enhanced gas recovery (EGR) for gas reservoirs involves producing a produced gas from gas reservoir to offshore facility, and combusting produced gas in a gas turbine to produce power and a flue gas.
AU WANG R
   AUSBORN M C
   LI Z
   CHANG-TANG E M
AE EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2022138048
AB    NOVELTY - A method for offshore power generation involves providing an offshore facility comprising a gas turbine (110), and a gas separator (116); producing a produced gas from a gas reservoir (102) to the offshore facility; combusting the produced gas in a gas turbine to produce power and a flue gas; partially removing nitrogen from the flue gas in a gas separator to produce a carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas and a nitrogen-enriched flue gas; compressing the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas in a gas compressor (120) to produce a compressed gas; and injecting the compressed gas from gas compressor into the gas reservoir, where greater than or equal to 80 mol.% hydrocarbon in the produced gas is combusted and/or injected into the gas reservoir.
   USE - A method for offshore power generation for enhanced gas recovery (EGR) for gas reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The systems and methods include offshore facilities that produce power as the primary or only product export which is lower cost to transport compared to compressed gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) offshore facility comprising an offshore production unit configured for producing a produced gas from a gas reservoir (102); a gas turbine (110) configured to receive the produced gas for producing power and a flue gas; a gas separator (116) configured to receive the flue gas for at least partially removing nitrogen from the flue gas and for producing a carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas and a nitrogen-enriched flue gas; a gas compressor (120) configured to receive the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas for producing a compressed gas; and an injection unit configured for injecting the compressed gas from the gas compressor into the gas reservoir; and
   (2) method which involves providing an offshore facility comprising a gas turbine, a first gas separator, a reformer (232), a second gas separator, and an ammonia reactor; producing a produced gas from a gas reservoir to the offshore facility; combusting the produced gas in a gas turbine to produce power and a flue gas; at least partially removing nitrogen from the flue gas in the first gas separator to produce a carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas and a nitrogen-enriched flue gas; reforming a portion of the produced gas in the reformer to produce a stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide; at least partially separating the carbon dioxide in the second gas separator from the stream to yield a carbon dioxide stream and a hydrogen stream; reacting the hydrogen stream and the nitrogen-enriched flue gas in the ammonia reactor to yield ammonia; combining and compressing the carbon dioxide stream and the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas in a gas compressor to produce a compressed gas; and injecting the compressed gas from the gas compressor into the gas reservoir, where greater than or equal to 80 mol.% hydrocarbon in the produced gas is combusted, reformed, and/or injected into the gas reservoir.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the method for power generation and carbon dioxide injection.
   Gas reservoir (102)
   Gas turbine (110)
   Gas separator (116)
   Gas compressor (120)
   Reformer (232)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrocarbon-derived exports from the offshore facility consists of the power. The method further comprises purifying the produced gas upstream of the gas turbine to partially remove greater than or equal to 1 of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, a heavy metal, and hydrogen sulfide. Purifying partially removes carbon dioxide, and the method further comprises adding the carbon dioxide to carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas before compressing the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas. The method further comprises diverting a portion of the produced gas upstream of the gas turbine; and adding the portion of the produced gas to the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas before compressing the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas. The method further comprises condensing a water vapor from the flue gas to produce a liquid water; and injecting the liquid water with the compressed gas into the gas reservoir. The power is stored, consumed either on board or by nearby subsea equipment, transmitted via cables to onshore grid and/or transported by battery ship. The method further comprises providing the power to greater than or equal to 1 production platforms within operating range, where greater than or equal to 1 production platforms are floating structures. The method further comprises controlling a position of the low motion floater by a computer-controlled system. The method further comprises cooling the flue gas with sea water. The method further comprises diverting a portion of the produced gas upstream of the gas turbine; and adding the portion of the produced gas to the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas before compressing the carbon dioxide-enriched flue gas. Preferred Components: The produced gas comprises a gas selected from steam, hydrogen, helium, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, greater than or equal to 1 1-4C hydrocarbons, and/or metal-containing compounds. The produced gas comprises a combined carbon dioxide and nitrogen content of greater than or equal to 25 mol.%. The produced gas has a gas volume of greater than or equal to 1 million standard cubic feet per day (MMSCFD), preferably 50-500 MMSCFD. The hydrocarbon-derived exports from offshore facility consists of power and ammonia. Preferred Parameters: The power is 500 megawatts (MW) to 3 gigawatts (GW). The power is comprised of a high voltage alternative current (HVAC), and/or high voltage direct current (HVDC). Preferred Devices: The offshore facility is a low motion floater selected from semi-submersible, and/or ship-shaped monohull.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); ; H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. PT P
PN CN109721054-A
TI Producing large-scale electronic grade high-purity carbon dioxide comprises e. g. taking a large-scale electronic grade high-purity carbon dioxide producing device, and reducing the raw material gas to distillation column.
AU YANG Y
   YAO F
   ZHANG L
   DONG J
AE ZIBO TIANRUN GAS CO LTD (ZIBO-Non-standard)
GA 201942875W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing large-scale electronic grade high-purity carbon dioxide comprises e. g. (i) taking a large-scale electronic grade high-purity carbon dioxide producing device, reducing the raw material gas to distillation column by throttling to 4.2-4.5 MPa using distillation unit, distributing the corrugated board on the regular packing after spraying through the nozzle, exchanging heat with the exhaust of the screw compressor in the heat exchange coil at the bottom of the rectification column, absorbing the liquid component having a lower boiling point by heat, rising to the top of the rectification column , and entering into the dehydrogenation unit, where the liquid component with a higher boiling point settles at the bottom of the distillation column, exhausting a part into the rectification column the re-boiler shell and the tube-type screw compressor, exchanging, and recycling the vaporization return system.
   USE - The method is useful for producing large-scale electronic grade high-purity carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy, stable, and low cost, and ensures high resource utilization rate, saves energy and emission reduction effect, stable product quality and high recovery rate of gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing large-scale electronic grade high-purity carbon dioxide comprises (i) taking a large-scale electronic grade high-purity carbon dioxide producing device, where the device includes a rectification column, the gas phase outlet of the rectification column is connected to the cold source inlet of the pre-heater, the cold source outlet of the heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of the dehydrogenation column, the outlet of the dehydrogenation column is connected to the heat source inlet of the pre-heater, the heat source outlet of the pre-heater is connected to the heat source inlet of the ammonia cooler, distributing the corrugated board on the regular packing after spraying through the nozzle, exchanging heat with the exhaust of the screw compressor in the heat exchange coil at the bottom of the rectification column, absorbing the liquid component having a lower boiling point by heat, rising to the top of the rectification column , and entering into the dehydrogenation unit, where the liquid component with a higher boiling point settles at the bottom of the distillation column, exhausting a part into the rectification column the re-boiler shell and the tube-type screw compressor, exchanging, and recycling the vaporization return system, discharging high-boiling components that cannot be vaporized through the bottom discharge port, and returning the other part into the rectification column for adjusting the rectification column liquid level; (ii) depressurizing the carbon dioxide raw material gas from the top of the dehydrogenation unit rectification column to 3.5-3.8 MPa, introducing into the pre-heater through the cold source inlet, exchanging heat with the high temperature oxidized gas, entering into the dehydrogenation column, reacting with excess oxygen, discharging the gas from the dehydrogenation tower, entering the pre-heater through the heat source inlet to preheat the raw material gas, discharging from the heat source outlet of the pre-heater and entering into the ammonia cooler through the heat source inlet to exchange heat with the low temperature liquid ammonia, and entering the heat exchanged gas into the dehydration unit through the heat source outlet; (iii) entering the dehydration unit feed gas from the dehydrogenation unit sequentially into the first drying tower and the second drying tower for dehydration, and entering the dehydrated gas into the liquefaction unit; (iv) entering the liquefaction unit gas from the dehydration unit through the filter, reducing the throttling pressure to 3-3.5 MPa, and entering the heat-source inlet into the plate-fin type carbon dioxide condenser and condensing the liquid ammonia after throttling into liquid carbon dioxide, entering the liquid carbon dioxide into the carbon dioxide buffer tank, entering into the purification unit, entering the vaporized ammonia gas into the ammonia separator for separation, entering the separated ammonia gas into the screw compressor to inhale, returning the separated liquid ammonia into the plate-fin carbon dioxide condenser through the liquid return pipe, pressurizing the exhaust gas to 1.1-1.5 MPa by the screw compressor, entering into the rectification column and returning to the ammonia storage device after the tube heat exchange of the re-boiler, entering the ammonia gas in the ammonia storage unit into the evaporative condenser, water cooling, condensing into liquid into the siphon tank, dividing the liquid ammonia in the siphon tank into two parts, heating part of the oil cooler entering the screw compressor and vaporizing and returning into the siphon tank, and flowing back the remaining part into the ammonia storage unit; and (v) dividing the liquid carbon dioxide from the liquefaction unit into two parts, depressurizing a part of the solution to 2.5-3 MPa and entering the purification tower through the feed port, making uniform distribution in the structured packing layer of the corrugated board, exchanging heat with the higher temperature gas from the bottom of the bottom re-boiler, absorbing heat by the lower boiling component, vaporizing, and rising into the tube of the overhead condenser, exchanging the heat exchange with the liquid carbon dioxide after the throttling of the shell side with the liquid carbon dioxide after the throttling of the shell side, and condensing the carbon dioxide having a higher boiling point into a liquid and flows back to the bottom of the purification tower, throttling the other part to 2 MPa and entering the shell side of the purification tower overhead condenser from the bottom, exchanging with the gas of the tube, vaporizing, and discharging from the upper part of the top condenser of the purification tower and entering into the recovery unit, refluxing the liquid carbon dioxide that into the bottom of the purification tower, dividing into two parts, entering a portion of the heat source outlet through the heat exchange coil into the carbon dioxide finished buffer tank from the outlet, flowing into the stripping column for use as low pressure re-boiling heat medium, throttling to 2 MPa and entering into the stripping tower from the upper part, entering into the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, passing the remaining portion through the other outlet at the lower part of the purification tower and entering into the tube end of the bottom re-boiler, exchanging heat with the high-temperature exhaust gas of the shell-side pressurized gas heat exchanger, performing endothermic gasification, raising into the middle of the purification tower and used as re-boiling heat source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (vi) entering the flash vapor into the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank and absorbing the carbon dioxide by the ambient temperature, exchanging heat with the shell-side carbon dioxide compressor at high temperature, flowing out from the upper part, the carbon dioxide compressor, compressing and pressurizing to 3.8 MPa, discharging from the exhaust port of the carbon dioxide compressor into the shell side of the pressurized gas heat exchanger, exchanging heat with the low temperature suction of the tube, exchanging the liquid with the liquid carbon dioxide entering the shell of the purifying vessel at the bottom of the purifying column, and discharging from the lower part. The reaction temperature in the dehydrogenation column is 320-350 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device further comprises the heat source outlet of the ammonia cooler is connected to the inlet of the first drying tower, the outlet of the first drying tower is connected to the inlet of the second drying tower, the outlet of the second drying tower is connected to the inlet of the filter, the outlet of the filter is column chamber of the rectification column and the purification column is provided with an overhead condenser, a distillation column and a re-boiler in order from top to bottom, and a re-boiler is a pump set on the extraction line, reducing the raw material gas to distillation column by throttling to 4.2-4.5 MPa using distillation unit. The heat source inlet of the heat exchange coil of the rectification column is provided with a solenoid valve. The liquid level of the distillation column is 1250-1750 mm. The pre-heater, the ammonia cooler and the pressurized gas heat exchanger are both shell and tube heat exchangers. The first drying tower is filled with alumina, and the second drying tower is filled with high-efficiency molecular sieve. The first drying tower is provided with two, and the two first drying towers are connected in parallel with each other. The bottom of the ammonia separator is higher than the top of the plate-fin carbon dioxide condenser by more than 1.5 meters. The screw compressor is provided with two, and the two screw compressors are connected in parallel with each other. The first drying tower is filled with alumina. The second drying tower is filled with high-efficiency molecular sieve. The first drying tower is provided with two. The two first drying towers are connected in parallel with each other.

PT P
PN CN108579754-A
TI Preparing automobile exhaust gas catalyst involves introducing carbon dioxide into a first reactor such that pressure in first reactor reaches first predetermined pressure, adding soluble salt of copper and soluble salt of cerium solution.
AU DONG X
AE SHAOXING DINGTAI ENERGY CONSERVATION (SHAO-Non-standard)
GA 201883842V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing automobile exhaust gas catalyst involves introducing carbon dioxide into a first reactor such that pressure in first reactor reaches first predetermined pressure value i.e 8-15 megaPascal. The mixed solution of a soluble salt of copper, a soluble salt of cerium, a soluble salt of zirconium and absolute ethanol are added to the first reactor, and the mixture is heated to a standstill with stirring at 100 to 160 degrees C. The carbon dioxide is introduced into the second reactor such that the pressure in the second reactor reaches a second predetermined pressure value, the first predetermined pressure value and the second The difference between the preset pressure values is 0.1-0.5 megaPascal, mixed solution of deionized water and citric acid are added to the second reactor at 100-160 degrees C and stirred. The supercritical carbon dioxide is introduced into first reactor, where mixed solution of deionized water and citric acid are slowly added, and first reactor is stirred.
   USE - Method for preparing automobile exhaust gas catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare automobile exhaust gas catalyst in eco-friendly and unique chemical reaction environment manner in which reduces surface tension of supercritical carbon dioxide and amount of surface active agent, where conversion rate of carbon monoxide, hydrocarbon and nitric oxide reaches to 92.3%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing automobile exhaust gas catalyst involves introducing carbon dioxide into a first reactor such that pressure in first reactor reaches first predetermined pressure value i.e 8-15 megaPascal. The mixed solution of a soluble salt of copper, a soluble salt of cerium, a soluble salt of zirconium and absolute ethanol are added to the first reactor, and the mixture is heated to a standstill with stirring at 100 to 160 degrees C. The carbon dioxide is introduced into the second reactor such that the pressure in the second reactor reaches a second predetermined pressure value, the first predetermined pressure value and the second The difference between the preset pressure values is 0.1-0.5 megaPascal, mixed solution of deionized water and citric acid are added to the second reactor at 100-160 degrees C and stirred. The supercritical carbon dioxide is introduced into first reactor, where mixed solution of deionized water and citric acid are slowly added, and first reactor is stirred such that the deionized water and citric acid are thoroughly mixed at 130-160 degrees C, reacted for 5 to 10 hours, filtered, washed to obtain solid product, and dried to obtain calcined catalyst of automobile exhaust. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM for a system, which comprises first reactor (1), second reactor (2), carbon dioxide storage tank (3), booster pump (4), and three-way valve (5), where carbon dioxide output in carbon dioxide storage tank, booster pump is pressurized, divided into two paths at the three-way valve, one of which is input to the first reactor and the other of which is input to the second reactor and the first is connected to the first reactor a mixer and a first transfer pump for forming a mixed solution of soluble salt of copper, soluble salt of cerium, soluble salt of zirconium, and absolute ethanol, second mixer and second transfer pump in communication with the second reactor, second mixer for forming a mixed solution of deionized water and citric acid, and second transfer pump is used to deliver the mixed solution to the second reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system.
   First reactor (1)
   Second reactor (2)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (3)
   Booster pump (4)
   Three-way valve (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the soluble salt of copper, the soluble salt of cerium and the soluble salt of zirconium is (0.05 to 0.15):(0.65 to 0.85):(0.1 to 0.2). The soluble salt of copper, the soluble salt of cerium and the soluble salt of zirconium are copper nitrate, cerium nitrate and zirconium nitrate. The molar ratio of soluble salt of copper, soluble salt of cerium, and soluble salt of zirconium in anhydrous ethanol form mixed solution is (0.05 to 0.15):(0.65 to 0.85):(0.1 to 0.2). The soluble salt of copper, the soluble salt of cerium and the soluble salt of zirconium are copper nitrate, cerium nitrate and zirconium nitrate. Preferred Condition: The first reactor and the second reactor have a pressure difference of 0.1 to 0.5 megaPascal.

PT P
PN US2018008958-A1; KR2018006147-A; US10010861-B2; KR1967508-B1
TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises polymeric amine            consisting of polymer skeleton containing nitrogen            atoms and branched chains bonded to nitrogen atoms of            the polymer skeleton, and porous support on which            polymeric amine is supported.
AU CHOI M K
   CHOI W S
   CHOI M
   CHOI W
AE KOREA ADVANCED INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KOAD-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 201803911U
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide adsorbent (100) comprises                polymeric amine (114) consisting of polymer                skeleton containing nitrogen atoms and branched                chains bonded to nitrogen atoms of the polymer                skeleton, each branched chain containing greater                than or equal to 1 nitrogen atom; and porous                support (112) on which polymeric amine is                supported, where polymeric amine is modified by                substitution of greater than or equal to 1 nitrogen                atoms of polymer skeleton or branched chains with                hydroxyl group-containing carbon chain.
   USE - A carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The core-shell structure of the carbon dioxide                adsorbent including the polymeric amine-supported                porous support coated with the conductive carbon                material can effectively prevent aggregation of the                adsorbent particles caused by the organic material                present on the core surface and the generation of                static electricity while maintaining the ability of                the modified polyethyleneimine to adsorb carbon                dioxide. In addition, the coating can also inhibit                the supported material from leaching from the                carbon dioxide adsorbent particles. Furthermore,                the core-shell particles are prepared in an                environment-friendly and economically efficient                way. The carbon dioxide adsorbents are superior in                high-temperature stability, and particularly, have                high adsorption capacity, selectivity, and                long-term stability. The coating with the                conductive carbon material increases the durability                of the adsorbent particles and suppresses                aggregation of the particles in a reactor,                contributing to an improvement in processing                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent                (100) which involves adding oxide derivative to                solution of raw polyalkyleneimine in a solvent,                stirring the solution such that greater than or                equal to 1 nitrogen atoms of raw polyalkyleneimine                are alkylated with hydroxyethyl group, removing                solvent from reaction solution to obtain                polyalkyleneimine modified by partial substitution                with hydroxyethyl group, and supporting the                modified polyalkyleneimine on a porous support                (112);
   (2) core-shell structured carbon dioxide                adsorbent comprising core (110) which contains                polymeric amine (114) consisting of polymer                skeleton containing nitrogen atoms and branched                chains bonded to the nitrogen atoms of the polymer                skeleton, each branched chain containing greater                than or equal to 1 nitrogen atom, and porous                support on which the polymeric amine is supported,                and shell (120) which contains conductive carbon                material layer surrounding and coated on the core,                where polymeric amine is modified by substitution                of greater than or equal to 1 nitrogen atoms of the                polymer skeleton or the branched chains with a                hydroxyl group-containing carbon chain;
   (3) preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent                which involves adding oxide derivative to solution                of raw polyalkyleneimine in a solvent, stirring                solution such that greater than or equal to 1                nitrogen atoms of raw polyalkyleneimine are                alkylated with hydroxyethyl group, removing solvent                from reaction solution to obtain polyalkyleneimine                modified by partial substitution with hydroxyethyl                group, supporting modified polyalkyleneimine on a                porous support, adding conductive carbon material                to modified polyalkyleneimine-supported porous                support, stirring mixture such that conductive                carbon material is uniformly coated on the surface                of the modified polyalkyleneimine-supported porous                support, to obtain a core-shell structured                adsorbent, and removing conductive carbon material                remaining uncoated on the surface of the modified                polyalkyleneimine-supported support; and
   (4) polymeric amine comprising polymer                skeleton containing nitrogen atoms and branched                chains bonded to the nitrogen atoms of the polymer                skeleton, each branched chain containing greater                than or equal to 1 nitrogen atom.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional diagram                of a core-shell structured carbon dioxide                adsorbent.
   Carbon dioxide adsorbent (100)
   Core (110)
   Porous support (112)
   Polymeric amine (114)
   Shell (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Conductive carbon                  material is selected from carbon black, graphite,                  activated carbon, carbon nanotubes, graphene,                  and/or carbon filaments. Conductive carbon                  material is added in an amount of 0.1-20 pts. wt.                  based on 100 pts. wt. of the modified polymeric                  amine-supported porous support.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Hydroxyl                  group-containing carbon chain has 1-20 carbon                  atoms and contains 1-10 hydroxyl groups. Hydroxyl                  group-containing carbon chain has a structure of                  formula -(CH2)m(OH)nL.m=1-20;n=1=10; andL=H, 1-18C alkyl, 3-10C cycloalkyl, 1-18C                  alkoxy, -CH=CH2, -CH=CHCH2CH3, -CH2CH=CHCH3,                  -CH2CH2CH=CH2, -CH=CHCH2CH2CH2CH3,                  -CH2CH=CHCH2CH2CH3, -CH2CH2CH=CHCH2CH3,                  -CH2CH2CH2CH=CHCH3, -CH2CH2CH2CH2CH=CH2,                  -CH=CHCH2CH2CH2CH2CH2CH3,                  -CH2CH=CHCH2CH2CH2CH2CH3,                  -CH2CH2CH=CHCH2CH2CH2CH3,                  -CH2CH2CH2CH=CHCH2CH2CH3,                  -CH2CH2CH2CH2CH=CHCH2CH3,                  -CH2CH2CH2CH2CH2CH=CHCH3,                  -CH2CH2CH2CH2CH2CH2CH=CH2, -CH2O(CH2)3Si(OCH3)3,                  -CH2O(CH2)3Si(OCH2CH3)3, -CH2O(CH2)2CH3,                  -CH2O(CH2)3CH3, -CH2OCH=CHCH3, or                  -CH2OCH2CH=CH2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Polymeric amine has                  a polyalkyleneimine basic structure, preferably a                  polyethyleneimine basic structure. Polymer                  skeleton has a repeating unit structure of                  formula -((CH2)x-NRy)-, and branched chain has                  alkyleneamine structure of formula -(CH2)x-NH2 or                  alkyleneimine structure of formula -(CH2)x-NR'2.                  Mole ratio of nitrogen atoms present in the raw                  polyalkyleneimine to oxide derivative is                  1:0.2-0.95. About 10-90% of all nitrogen atoms of                  the polyalkyleneimine modified by partial                  substitution are present in the form of                  unmodified primary or secondary amine groups.                  About 10-90% of all nitrogen atoms in the                  polymeric amine are present in the form of                  unmodified primary or secondary amine. Branched                  chain is a primary amine group of formula                  -(CH2)x-NH2, where the repeating unit structure                  bonded with the branched chain is a tert.-amine                  moiety. Branched chain has alkyleneimine                  structure of formula -(CH2)xNR'2, provided that                  (i) when the other R' is hydrogen, the repeating                  unit structure bonded with the branched chain is                  a tert.-amine moiety and the branched chain is a                  sec.-amine group; (ii) when the other R' is an                  alkyleneamine group, the repeating unit structure                  bonded with the branched chain is a tert.-amine                  moiety, the branched chain is a tert.-amine                  group, and the alkyleneamine group is a                  prim.-amine group; and (iii) when the other R' is                  an alkyleneimine group, the repeating unit                  structure bonded with the branched chain is a                  tert.-amine moiety, the branched chain is a                  tert.-amine group, and the alkyleneimine group is                  a sec.- or tert.-amine group.R=H or branched chain;x=2-6;y=5-100, representing the average number of                  repeating units in the linear polyalkyleneimine                  chain; andR'=alkyleneamine or alkyleneimine group and                  the other R' is H, alkyleneamine, or                  alkyleneimine.Preferred Parameter: Polymeric amine has                  weight average molecular weight of 200-150000.                  Preferred Process: Preparation method further                  includes heating the modified                  polyalkyleneimine-supported porous support.

GA 202266488Y

The gas heater is controlled to send out different alarm information and limit the working parameter of the gas water heater according to the concentration level of the carbon dioxide value. Determination is made to check whether the concentration level of the carbon dioxide concentration value is the lowest level.
   USE - Method for controlling an alarm of a gas water heater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preventing the gas water heater in the working process from being in incomplete combustion state to cause danger when the carbon dioxide concentration exceeds a certain value to improve safety and use experience of the water heater, thus increasing customer satisfaction. The method allows an alarm control module to control the water heater to send out different alarm information according to the concentration level of the concentration value, when determining that the water is to be sent out the alarm information, such that the working parameter of the heater can be limited, thus ensuring that the heater has good combustion condition, and hence improving safety and usage experience.

TI Producing a carbonated beverage comprising a blend of water and syrup having a predetermined final carbonation level involves introducing carbon dioxide into a flowing stream of a product blend comprising water, syrup and dissolved oxygen.
AU LUCAS L M
AE BEVCORP LLC (BEVC-Non-standard)
GA 2020879479
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a carbonated beverage comprising a blend of water and syrup having a predetermined final carbonation level involves introducing carbon dioxide into a flowing stream of a product blend comprising water, syrup and dissolved oxygen, such that carbon dioxide is dissolved in product blend, deaerating the carbon dioxide-containing product blend by introducing the blend into a vented atmospheric vessel, the interior of which is at ambient pressure with a headspace maintained above the surface of the liquid within the vessel, where dissolved oxygen is released from the product blend and vented from the vessel, pumping the deaerated product blend from the vessel, where the deaerated blend includes dissolved carbon dioxide at an intermediate carbonation level less than final carbonation level and carbonating the deaerated product blend to final carbonation level downstream of the vented vessel to produce a carbonated beverage for subsequent packaging.
   USE - Method for producing a carbonated beverage comprising a blend of water and syrup having a predetermined final carbonation level.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce throughput or result in deleterious effects on the process and/or the packaged beverage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for producing carbonated beverage, which comprises carbon dioxide injector adapted for controllably injecting carbon dioxide into a flowing stream of a blend of water and syrup containing dissolved oxygen, an atmospheric deaerating vessel located downstream of carbon dioxide injector, vessel vented to the atmosphere and adapted to maintain a liquid level and a headspace above the liquid contained; and
   (2) a method for producing a beverage comprising a blend of water and syrup, which involves introducing nitrogen into a flowing stream of a blend of water and syrup containing oxygen dissolved, such that nitrogen is dissolved in blend, and deaerating the nitrogen containing blend by introducing the blend into a vented atmospheric vessel, the interior of which is at ambient pressure with a headspace maintained above the surface of the liquid within the vessel, where dissolved oxygen is released from the blend and vented from the vessel and pumping the deaerated blend from the vessel, where the deaerated blend includes dissolved nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The intermediate carbonation level is between about 2 and about 4 volumes of carbon dioxide less than final carbonation level. The temperature of the product blend during the steps of injecting and deaerating is between about 50-90 degrees F. The carbon dioxide is injected in the form of bubbles having a size of less than 5 microns in diameter. Preferred Process: The method further involves measuring the temperature of the product blend during deaeration, and adjusting the amount of carbon dioxide introduced during the carbonating step based upon temperature, measuring the temperature of the product blend during deaeration comprises measuring the temperature within the vented atmospheric vessel, measuring the temperature of the product blend entering the vented atmospheric vessel, or measuring the temperature of the product blend leaving the vented atmospheric vessel. Preferred Components: The product blend further comprises dissolved nitrogen, and deaerating step reduces dissolved nitrogen. The vented atmospheric vessel further includes at least one of a coalescer and a foam breaker, and further comprising the step of maintaining the liquid level within the vessel above the coalescer and/or maintaining the liquid level within the vessel below the foam breaker.

PT P
PN EP4019167-A1
TI Production of metal powder for use in additive manufacturing process involves atomizing metal by directing jet of fluid comprising liquid carbon dioxide towards stream of molten metal.
AU RITZEN O
   DUBIEZ S
   KAPLAN B
   DAHLSTROM M
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2022842698
AB    NOVELTY - Production of metal powder (208) involves providing a stream of molten metal (203), and atomizing the metal by directing a jet of fluid (205) comprising liquid carbon dioxide towards the stream of molten metal.
   USE - Production of metal powder for use in additive manufacturing process used for producing components for automotive, industrial, food processing and medical applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows for an enhanced powder flowability and better bulk density which, in turn, will lead to a better quality of the finished product. Smooth round particles possess better packing properties and therefore spread more evenly on the powder bed and form a uniform layer. The high concentration of carbon dioxide around the metal during the atomization process is also believed to promote the formation of spherical particles. By pressurizing the fluid, it is possible to achieve high fluid velocities before colliding the fluid with a molten stream of metal. The use of supercritical carbon dioxide allows to cool down molten metal more efficiently. By supplying both molten metal and the pressurized carbon dioxide through the nozzle having both a divergent and convergent sections allows for better mixing at the point of exit of the two components due to a change in the speed of the.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of liquid carbon dioxide atomizer.
   Atomizer (200)
   Furnace (202)
   Molten metal (203)
   Jet of fluid (205)

   ZHANG H
   LI T
AE SINOPEC HENAN OILFIELD CO EXPLORATION (SNPC-C)

GA 202237111W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting evaluation indexes of carbon-dioxide miscible-phase flooding of the low-permeability oil reservoir of the gravels. The evaluation indexes have crude oil viscosity, crude oil density, oil reservoir depth, oil reservoir temperature, oil reservoir pressure, oil saturation, cracks and permeability variation coefficient, and constructing a factor set according to the evaluation indexes. A fuzzy evaluation set is adopted as an evaluation set of carbon-dioxide miscible flooding screening. A corresponding weight set is constructed aiming at each evaluation index. An evaluation matrix is constructed according to the membership degree of each factor in the evaluation set. A fuzzy comprehensive evaluation model is constructed according to the weight set and the evaluation matrix, and carbon-dioxide miscible flooding screening is performed according to the model calculation result.

   USE - The catalyst structure is useful for                electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction,                converting carbon dioxide into a diatomic carbon or                higher hydrocarbon compound with high conversion                rate, removal and resource conversion of greenhouse                gases, such as carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide                reduction technology according to electrochemical                catalysts, and producing high value-added chemical                substances, such as formate, methane, ethylene, and                alcohol, and various catalyst systems.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of the                  pyridine-like arrangement structure in the carbon                  nanofibers has a higher value than the content in                  the pyrrole-like arrangement or graphite-like                  arrangement. Preferred Properties: The diameter                  of the carbon nanofibers is 100-200 nm. The                  copper particles have a size of 10-40 nm. The                  pyridine-like structure has a value of 50 at% or                  more with respect to the entire nitrogen doped                  structure. Preferred Method: The step (b)                  involves performing heat treatment at 800-900                  degrees C with a partial pressure of oxygen in                  gas atmosphere of 50 m Torr to 1 Torr. Preferred                  Components: The copper precursor comprises copper                  acetate, copper nitrate or copper chloride. The                  nitrogen-containing carbon nanofiber precursor                  comprises polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyaniline,                  polypyrrole, cyanamide and                  polybenzimidazole.

And in response to determining that at least one target is sensed in the space. PN CN111320743-A; CN111320743-B
TI High solids content carbon dioxide-based cationic waterborne polyurethane comprises poly(carbonate-ether) glycol, diisocyanate, small molecule chain extender, hydrophilic chain extender, neutralizer, and catalyst.
AU GONG R
   ZHANG H
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202060870T
AB    NOVELTY - High solids content carbon dioxide-based cationic waterborne polyurethane comprises 85-115 pts. wt. poly(carbonate-ether) glycol, 33-55 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 5-13 pts. wt. small molecule chain extender, 1.6-3.2 pts. wt. hydrophilic chain extender, 0.7-2 pts. wt. neutralizer, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. catalyst, 80-140 pts. wt. solvent, 200-400 pts. wt. deionized water. The neutralizing agent is phosphoric acid or phytic acid. The hydrophilic chain extender is 3-dimethylamino- 1,2-propanediol.
   USE - High solids content carbon dioxide-based cationic waterborne polyurethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The high solids content carbon dioxide-based cationic waterborne polyurethane which is greatly reduces the viscosity of the emulsion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a high solids content carbon dioxide-based cationic aqueous polyurethane, which involves:
   (A) adding 30-70 wt.% poly(carbonate-ether) glycol, diisocyanate, small molecule chain extender and catalyst to the reaction vessel, and adding 60-85 wt.% solvent, reacting and polymerizing to obtain the first intermediate under the protection of nitrogen;
   (B) adding hydrophilic chain extender and remaining catalyst to the first intermediate;
   (C) adding remaining solvent to reduce the viscosity of the system to obtain the second intermediate;
   (D) adding neutralization agent to the second intermediate to complete the reaction; and
   (E) adding deionized water for emulsification, after removing the solvent to obtain a carbon dioxide-based cationic waterborne polyurethane emulsion with high solid content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The diisocyanate is one or more of toluene diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate, 1,6-hexamethylene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, 4,4-dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate. The small molecule chain extender is one of ethylene glycol, propylene glycol and butylene glycol. The catalyst is one of dibutyltin dilaurate and triethylenediamine. The solvent is one of acetone and methyl ethyl ketone. Preferred Conditions: The molecular weight of the poly(carbonate-ether) glycol is 1500-4500 g/mol, and the carbonate content is 35-65%.

   JAIN A

GA 202026660W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving a carbon                sequestration notification from a first computing                system by a receiver of a processing server (102). The                result is a ledger that accurately records                sequestration efforts by entities in a manner that                is immutable and completely transparent and                auditable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for rewarding carbon sequestration.

PN CN110898628-A
TI Preparing polyvinylamine with hydrolysis degree of 40-70% useful for preparing carbon dioxide separation composite membrane comprises adding poly-N-vinylformamide and hydrochloric acid, and performing constant temperature hydrolysis.
AU WANG Z
   YUAN Y
   XING G
   XU R
   SHENG M
   DONG S
   WANG J
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020242511
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyvinylamine with hydrolysis degree of 40-70% comprises (i) adding poly-N-vinylformamide (PNVF) into a solution, adding 8-12 wt.% hydrochloric acid to obtain a reaction solution, stirring, and performing constant temperature hydrolysis at 65-75 degrees C under the protection of nitrogen to obtain polyvinylamine hydrochloride (PVAm.HCl), (ii) adding PVAm.HCl solution to excess ethanol, and precipitating to obtain a white PVAm.HCl solid, and (iii) dissolving the obtained PVAm.HCl solid in deionized water, adding excess of anion exchange resin to the solution, stirring until the solution has a pH of 12-13, and performing suction filtration to obtain a polyvinylamine aqueous solution with a degree of hydrolysis of 40-70%.
   USE - The polyvinylamine is useful for preparing carbon dioxide separation composite membrane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: scrapes the surface of the ultrafiltration membrane to obtain a carbon dioxide separation membrane that has excellent carbon dioxide permeation rate and separation factor; and has strong application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a carbon dioxide separation composite membrane by using a self-provided 40-70% polyvinylamine, comprising coating polyvinylamine aqueous solution having a degree of hydrolysis of 40-70% on the surface of the ultrafiltration membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon dioxide separation composite membrane (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass concentration of the PNVF solution is 9.5-10.5 wt.%. The anion exchange resin comprises a styrene-type anion exchange resin or a polyethylene-type pyridine resin. The ultrafiltration membranes comprise polysulfone ultrafiltration membranes, polyacrylonitrile ultrafiltration membranes, polyethersulfone ultrafiltration membranes, Polyetheretherketone (RTM: Colorless organic polymer thermoplastic) ultrafiltration membranes, polyvinylidene fluoride ultrafiltration membranes or polycarbonate ultrafiltration membrane materials. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the hydrolysis is carried out under the protection of nitrogen at 65-75 degrees C for 4-5 hours. In the step (iii), the PVAm.HCl solid is dissolved in deionized water and prepared into an aqueous solution of 1-3 wt.%.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for biomass-oriented green production                of low-order acid and carbon dioxide, comprises                mixing water and choline chloride in a molar ratio                of 1-8:1 to prepare an ionic liquid, adding lignin                substrate, methyl rhenium trioxide and hydrogen                peroxide or oxygen, and reacting at 160-200 degrees                C for 2-8 hours.

PN CN108822467-A
TI Preparing high-purity grafted olefin polymer involves putting common grafted olefin polymer product in blast dryer, drying under fluidized condition to obtain crude product and putting crude product into carbon dioxide extraction kettle.
AU LI Z
   ZHANG F
   ZHANG X
   JIA J
AE NINGBO NENGZHIGUANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOL (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018946985
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity grafted olefin polymer involves putting common grafted olefin polymer product in blast dryer, drying under fluidized condition to obtain crude product and putting crude product into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle with inner gas circulating device. The carbon dioxide is injected, pressurized to supercritical state, seal-heated and extracted. The carbon dioxide is removed pressure for 1-5 seconds. The product is put in vacuum drying box. The extruder is used for prilling to obtain high-purity grafted olefin polymer.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity grafted olefin polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-purity grafted olefin polymer has high-purity, and in eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The common grafted olefin polymer is one or themixture of PP-g-MAH, PE-g-MAH, PE-g-GMA, POE-g-MAH, POE-g-GMA. The entrainer is one or mixture of acetone, water, dichloromethane, chloroform, methanol, ethanol, pyridine and tetrahydrofuran. Preferred Conditions: The blast drying temperature is 40-50 degrees C. The drying time is 0.5-2 hours. The seal-heating temperature is 5-50 degrees C greater than melting point of polymer. The pressure is 8-45 megapascal. The extracting time is 10-60 minutes. The drying pressure is less than 0.02 mega pascal. The drying time is 3-6 hours.

PN KR2018129129-A; KR1928002-B1
TI Producing syngas from methane, comprises preparing syngas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide by reacting methane with oxygen donor particles and carbon dioxide.
AU LEE J W
   KANG D
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   HANWHA CHEM CORP (HANW-C)
GA 201897906V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing syngas from methane, comprises preparing syngas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide by reacting methane with oxygen donor particles and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing syngas from methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method contributes to reduction of greenhouse gases, controls hydrogen and carbon monoxide ratio of syngas gas by adjusting carbon dioxide supply, and reduces carbon deposition by supplying additional oxygen source through oxygen donor particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen donor particles are iron oxide, nickel oxide, cerium oxide, zirconium oxide, magnesium oxide and/or manganese oxide. The carrier includes zeolite, silica, alumina, calcium oxide and/or titanium oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises carbonizing deposited carbon into oxidizing gas to produce carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen and thermal energy, performing oxygen donor particles oxidation reaction by oxidizing the reduced oxygen donor particles with the oxidizing gas to generate regenerated oxygen donor particles and thermal energy, performing deposited carbon gasification and oxygen donor particle oxidation reaction, and purging before and after the oxygen donor particle oxidation reaction with an inert gas including nitrogen, argon, helium and/or hydrogen. The regenerated oxygen donor particles are re-introduced into the synthesis gas production step. The oxidation reaction is carried out at 800-1000 degrees C in a fixed bed reactor, a mobile bed reactor or a fluidized bed reactor. Preferred Components: The feed molar ratio of methane and carbon dioxide is 0.01-1. The oxygen donor particles are metal active material or metal active material supported on a carrier. The oxidizing gas includes air, oxygen, carbon dioxide and/or water vapor, preferably air and/or oxygen. The methane is mixed with an inert gas including nitrogen, argon and/or helium. The oxidizing gas is mixed with an inert gas including nitrogen, argon and/or helium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN108677179-A; CN108677179-B
TI Performing hydrothermal modification involves            using copper potassium bicarbonate solution tank having            copper modified electrocatalytic properties, modifying            and electrocatalytic reducing carbon dioxide copper            electrode reaction tank.
AU NING H

AB    NOVELTY - Performing hydrothermal modification involves                using copper potassium bicarbonate solution tank                having a copper modified electrocatalytic                properties, modifying and electrocatalytic reducing                carbon dioxide copper electrode reaction tank,                where carbon dioxide tank used to produce the                copper catalyst efficiency and selectivity of                ethylene have been enhanced.
   USE - Method for performing hydrothermal                modification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform hydrothermal                modification which has high value-added                ethylene-productive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The water tank is                  heat-modified copper at 140&#176; C for 4                  hours, 0.5 wt.% potassium bicarbonate solution is                  added under conditions, hydrothermal copper flute                  having electivity to ethylene, Faraday efficiency                  slightly improves, surface-modified copper flute                  Guangsheng copper oxide which helps to improve                  the electrical properties of the catalytic copper                  foil.

PN CN108455935-A
TI Concrete comprises Portland cement, aggregate composition, mineral powder, carbon dioxide foaming agent, and water.
AU LI G
AE BEIJING YISHI CONSTR IND TECHNOLOGY DEV (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201869162R
AB    NOVELTY - A concrete comprises 50-70 pts. wt. Portland cement, 15-30 pts. wt. aggregate composition, 18-27 pts. wt. mineral powder, 25-35 pts. wt. carbon dioxide foaming agent, and 25-35 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete has high strength, excellent performance of sealing and adsorbing carbon dioxide foaming agent, strength, durability, impermeability, apparent compactness and slump, and improved processing performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation method of concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mineral powder comprises 5-10 pts. wt. perlite powder, 2-5 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 3-7 pts. wt. sepiolite powder, 7-12 pts. wt. sodium polyacrylate, and 1-3 pts. wt. colloidal silica. The aggregate composition is prepared using 10-20 pts. wt. modified perlite powder and 5-10 pts. wt. fly ash. Preferred Components: The aggregate composition is composed of a mixture of fly ash and microsilica, perlite, or slag. The perlite is a modified perlite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The modified perlite comprises 2-5 pts. wt. perlite, 1-3 pts. wt. sodium diisopropyl naphthalene sulfonate, 5-8 pts. wt. calcium silicate, and 2-4 pts. wt. sodium chloride.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PT P
PN CN114907894-A
TI Process for removing methanol from natural gas,            comprises e.g. sending raw natural gas containing            methanol to absorption tower, chemically absorbing acid            gas carbon dioxide in raw natural gas by MDEA aqueous            solution, and absorbing methanol in raw natural gas by            water dissolution.
AU JIA Z
   LI W
   WANG H
AE SHANGHAI CIMC TIANZHAO CLEAN ENERGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B2916S
AB    NOVELTY - Process for removing methanol from natural                gas, comprises (1) sending the raw natural gas                containing methanol to absorption tower, chemically                absorbing acid gas carbon dioxide in raw natural                gas by MDEA aqueous solution, at same time,                absorbing methanol in raw natural gas by water                dissolution, and passing natural gas from which                acid gas and methanol have been removed into                molecular sieve adsorption dehydration system from                the top of absorption tower; (2) depressurizing and                flashing MDEA aqueous solution containing carbon                dioxide and methanol at bottom of absorption tower,                then entering into regeneration tower, heating by                reboiler for regeneration, qualified MDEA aqueous                solution flowing out from bottom of regeneration                tower, and gas flowing out from top of regeneration                tower entering the carbon dioxide separation tank                after being air-cooled; and (3) rectifying and                separating methanol-containing aqueous solution at                bottom of carbon dioxide separation tank by double                rectifying tower.
   USE - The method is useful for process for removing                methanol from natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is especially suitable for working                conditions with high methanol content and large                fluctuation range in raw natural gas; and can                actively removes methanol in raw natural gas,                recovers pure water used for dissolving MDEA,                avoids freezing blockage of LNG cold box due to                methanol, and improves the stability and                reliability of production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for removing methanol from natural                gas, comprises (1) sending the raw natural gas                containing methanol to absorption tower, chemically                absorbing acid gas carbon dioxide in raw natural                gas by the MDEA aqueous solution, at same time,                absorbing methanol in raw natural gas by water                dissolution, and passing natural gas from which                acid gas and methanol have been removed into                molecular sieve adsorption dehydration system from                the top of the absorption tower; (2) depressurizing                and flashing the MDEA aqueous solution containing                carbon dioxide and methanol at the bottom of the                absorption tower, then entering into regeneration                tower, heating by the reboiler for regeneration,                qualified MDEA aqueous solution flowing out from                the bottom of regeneration tower, and gas flowing                out from top of regeneration tower entering the                carbon dioxide separation tank after being                air-cooled; (3) rectifying and separating the                methanol-containing aqueous solution at bottom of                the carbon dioxide separation tank by double                rectifying tower, sending methanol steam from the                top of the second rectifying tower to torch tower                for burning, qualified dilute MDEA aqueous solution                flowing out from the bottom of the first rectifying                tower and second rectifying tower, and returning to                the system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (2), the                  diameters of the first rectifying tower and the                  second rectifying tower are both less than                  &#966;300mm, which are atmospheric                  towers.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), feeding                  raw natural gas containing methanol into the                  absorption tower at pressure of 5MPa. In the step                  (2), MDEA aqueous solution containing carbon                  dioxide and methanol at the bottom of the                  absorption tower is depressurized to 0.9MPa. In                  the step (2), MDEA aqueous solution entering the                  regeneration tower after flashing is heated to                  121&#176;C by a reboiler for regeneration.                  The step (3) comprises discharging dilute                  solution of MDEA containing methanol from the                  bottom of the carbon dioxide separator, sending                  to the first rectification tower by a pump,                  heating by reboiler, steam containing methanol                  and MDEA from rectification entering the second                  rectifying tower after being air-cooled into                  liquid, and heating by the reboiler again,                  discharging methanol and small amount of water                  vapor that have been rectified again from the top                  of the second rectification tower to the torch                  tower and burning, and recycling dilute solution                  containing a small amount of MDEA. In the step                  (3), steam containing methanol and MDEA from the                  rectification entering into second rectifying                  tower after being air-cooled into liquid, and                  again heating to 105&#176;C through the                  reboiler. In the step (3), discharging                  methanol-containing MDEA dilute solution from the                  bottom of the carbon dioxide separator, sending                  to the first rectification column by a pump, and                  heating to 105&#176;C by reboiler. In the                  step (2), the gas flowing out from the top of the                  regeneration tower is air-cooled to 42&#176;C                  and then enters the carbon dioxide separation                  tank. Preferred Components: The concentration of                  the MDEA aqueous solution is 40 wt.%.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PT P
PN CN113630085-A
TI Method for recycling carbon dioxide by photovoltaic power in flue gas of power plant, involves attaching two groups of conductive copper sheet when piston plate of movement is large, and electrifying alarm to send alarm.
AU ZHENG Z
   LIN C
AE JIANGSU YIWAN IOT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D5092F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves filtering the flue gas generated by the work of the power plant by the intake pipe and enters the interior of the absorption equipment, where it is operated by the absorption equipment. The rich liquid that absorbs carbon dioxide is introduced into the regeneration equipment. The rich liquid containing carbon dioxide is divided into carbon dioxide and lean liquid and generated carbon dioxide is processed through artificial forests for carbon neutralization. The impurities will be blocked by the screen when the flue gas is filtered through intake pipe. The impurities are made to accumulate on the left side of the screen. The air pressure inside the installation pipe will increase, and the piston plate will move up with the test rod when accumulated impurities block the screen. The two sets of conductive copper sheets will be attached, and the alarm will be energized to give an alarm, and the staff can be notified when the movement range of the piston plate is large.
   USE - Method for recycling carbon dioxide from flue gas of power plant by using photovoltaic power.
   ADVANTAGE - The direct current output by the inverter is boosted by the boosting system and then transmitted to the recycling carbon dioxide device, so that the recycling device can supply the power supply demand by the solar energy, and the loss of the electric energy is reduced. The design of the voltage adjusting unit and the output of the bus box can be adjusted according to the voltage index of the current sunshine and the preset voltage index.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for recycling carbon dioxide from flue gas of power plant by using photovoltaic power.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for recycling carbon dioxide from flue gas of power plant. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for converting the absorbed light energy into electric energy by the photovoltaic component (S1)
   Step for obtaining the voltage index of the current sunshine by the voltage adjusting unit (S2)
   Step for sending a control signal to the pre-connected photovoltaic combiner box according to the relationship between the current sunlight voltage index and the preset voltage index (S3)
   Step for ontrolling multiple switches according to the control signal to adjust the connection mode between multiple sets of photovoltaic modules (S4)
   Step for outputting DC power to the pre-connected inverter through the DC cabinet (S5)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); X12 (Power Distribution/Components/Converters); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)
MC E31-N05C; E11-Q02B; J01-G03B; J01-E; X12-J; X12-H01D; X15-A02C

PT P
PN WO2020131116-A1; CA3123802-A1; EP3898515-A1; EP3898515-A4
TI Treating livestock waste containing ammonia            nitrogen involves treating livestock waste to create            gas and converting all ammonia in waste to gaseous form            in gas without using chemicals, where gas contains            carbon dioxide and water vapor.
AU BASSANI D
   ORENTLICHER M
   SIMON M
   PAGANO S
AE BION ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGIES INC (BION-Non-standard)
   BION ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGIES INC (BION-Non-standard)
GA 2020575480
AB    NOVELTY - Treating livestock waste containing ammonia                nitrogen involves treating livestock waste at a                temperature of 80 degrees C to create a gas and                converting substantially all ammonia in waste to                gaseous form in the gas without using chemicals.                The gas contains carbon dioxide and water vapor.                The method involves treating gas containing gaseous                ammonia, carbon dioxide, and water vapor by using                partial condensation to concentrate the ammonia and                carbon dioxide followed by absorption of                concentrate gases, or total condensation of gas                followed by dewatering by using reverse osmosis, or                a combination of these methods without using                chemicals. The method involves removing gas from a                significant amount of water vapor in liquid                condensate form, and causing formation of a                concentrated gas containing higher concentration of                ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas than before                condensation.
   USE - Method for treating livestock waste containing                ammonia nitrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat livestock waste                containing ammonia nitrogen, which produces an                organic solid containing high concentration of                nitrogen from a wastewater containing ammonia which                is utilized as a component ingredient in a nitrogen                rich, organic fertilizer product, converts NH3-N                into ammonia gas, does not utilize chemical to                increase pH, captures ammonia gas in form of a                stable salt, does not utilize industrial acids to                react with ammonia, and produces a solid-fertilizer                product with minimal use of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating livestock waste containing ammonia                nitrogen involves treating livestock waste at a                temperature of 80 degrees C to create a gas and                converting substantially all ammonia in waste to                gaseous form in the gas without using chemicals.                The gas contains carbon dioxide and water vapor.                The method involves treating gas containing gaseous                ammonia, carbon dioxide, and water vapor by using                partial condensation to concentrate the ammonia and                carbon dioxide followed by absorption of                concentrate gases, or total condensation of gas                followed by dewatering by using reverse osmosis, or                a combination of these methods without using                chemicals. The method involves removing gas from a                significant amount of water vapor in liquid                condensate form, causing formation of a                concentrated gas containing higher concentration of                ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas than before                condensation, treating concentrated gas by using                absorption to form a dissolved ammonium carbonate                and ammonium bicarbonate liquid solution at                saturation concentrations for dissolved ammonium                carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate, and                crystallizing dissolved ammonium carbonate and                ammonium bicarbonate condensate liquid solution at                a temperature of less than 35 degrees C to form                solid ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The livestock waste is                  treated at a temperature of at least 80 degrees C                  by stripping waste of ammonia, and stripping                  waste by using biogas from an anaerobic digester                  used to treat the waste before stripping. The                  livestock waste is treated at a temperature of at                  least 80 degrees C by drying waste using a dryer                  and removing ammonia with water vapor in gaseous                  form. The livestock waste is treated at 80                  degrees C by separating ammonia and other gasses                  by using membrane modules. The substantially all                  gaseous ammonia in gas is converted into ammonium                  carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate by using                  carbon dioxide in gas without addition of carbon                  dioxide not present in digestate. The waste is a                  digested dairy manure. The substantially all                  gaseous ammonia in gas is converted into ammonium                  carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate by using                  carbon dioxide in gas with addition of carbon                  dioxide from another source. The condensation is                  a two stage condensation process comprising                  sequential condensation at two temperatures or                  residence times. The total ammonia and carbon                  dioxide concentration in concentrated gas is at                  least 2 times greater than in the gas before                  treatment with condensation. The dissolved                  ammonium carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate                  concentration in liquid solution after absorption                  comprises 5 times of concentration of dissolved                  ammonium bicarbonate in the digestate. The solid                  crystals of ammonium bicarbonate grown in                  crystallizer are produced by creating a solid                  ammonium-salt. The ammonium-salt is dried,                  pelletized or granulated. The livestock waste                  containing ammonia nitrogen is treated by                  treating livestock waste by using anaerobic                  digestion creating a biogas and a digestate,                  treating biogas to separate and concentrate                  carbon dioxide in biogas from other gasses in                  biogas forming a concentrated carbon dioxide                  biogas, treating digestate at 80 degrees C to                  create a gas, using partial condensation to                  remove a significant amount of water vapor and                  organics in liquid condensate form from gas,                  causing formation of a condenser effluent                  concentrated gas containing higher concentrations                  of ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas than before                  condensation, treating concentrated carbon                  dioxide biogas and condenser effluent                  concentrated gas by using absorption operating at                  20-50 degrees C to form a dissolved ammonium                  carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate liquid                  solution at saturation concentrations for                  dissolved ammonium carbonate and ammonium                  bicarbonate at crystallizer temperature, and                  crystallizing the dissolved ammonium carbonate                  and ammonium bicarbonate condensate liquid                  solution. The method further involves recycling a                  portion of saturated dissolved ammonium carbonate                  and ammonium bicarbonate liquid solution from                  crystallization to absorber to assist with                  absorption.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E35; H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN CN108827989-A; WO2020000757-A1; CN108827989-B
TI Method for determining calcium silicate hydrate gel adsorption, involves mixing sample cement with water, solidifying cement paste and curing after demolding, drying sample, followed by determining and calculating.
AU CHANG H
   JIN Z
   LIU J
   FENG P
   HAN B
   SONG J
   XIE Q
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
   UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 201891777R
AB    NOVELTY - A calcium silicate hydrate (C-S-H) gel adsorption determining method mixing the sample cement with water to make a cement paste, solidifying the cement paste and curing after demolding, taking three samples and marking, drying the sample in absence of carbon dioxide and determining the free chloride ion content and total chloride ion content, combining the sample with chloride ion, placing the samples in a carbon dioxide atmosphere until the Friedel's salt is completely carbonized, taking out the sample and drying in a carbon dioxide-free environment, calculating the combined chloride ion, taking out the sample in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, measuring the free chloride ion content and the total chloride ion content, calculating the combination in the sample chloride ion, simultaneous quantitative X-ray powder diffraction (XRD) phase analyzing and calculating bound chloride ion represented by the expression as given in the specification.
   USE - Method for determining C-S-H gel adsorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining C-S-H gel adsorption in a simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A C-S-H gel adsorption determining method mixing the sample cement with water to make a cement paste, solidifying the cement paste and curing it after demolding, taking three samples from the test piece and marking with S0, S1 and S2 respectively, drying the sample S0 in the absence of carbon dioxide and determining the free chloride ion content cf,0 and total chloride ion content ct,0, combining the sample with chloride ion cb,0 and quantitative XRD phase analyzing to obtain C-S-H gel content csh0, placing the samples S1 and S2 are in a carbon dioxide atmosphere until the Friedel's salt is completely carbonized in the S1 and S2 samples, taking out the sample S1 and drying in a carbon dioxide-free environment to determine the free chloride ion content cf,1 and total chloride ion content, ct,1, calculating the combined chloride ion cb,1, and quantitative XRD phase analyzing to obtain C-S-H gel content C-S-H1, after a period of time, taking out the sample S2 in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, measuring the free chloride ion content cf,2 and the total chloride ion content ct,2, after drying in a carbon dioxide-free environment, calculating the combination in the sample chloride ion cb,2, simultaneous quantitative XRD phase analyzing to obtain C-S-H gel content C-S-H2 and calculating bound chloride ion represented by the expression as given in the specification.
