PN US2022127560-A1
TI Capturing and recycling carbon dioxide from on-island combustion emissions using sustainable solar energized aquaculture of algae involves generating electrical power from power producing systems, receiving carbon dioxide from source of emissions, followed by controlling operational parameters.
AU HABERMAN D
AE HABERMAN D (HABE-Individual)
GA 202256129R
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing and recycling carbon dioxide from on-island combustion emissions using sustainable solar energized aquaculture of algae involves generating electrical power from renewable power producing systems. The renewable power producing systems comprise solar photovoltaic cell and a water turbine (108). The electrical power in a battery array (110) is stored. Carbon dioxide from source of emissions are received. The carbon dioxide is input into algae cultivators (102). The algae cultivators are feed with nutrients derived from a waste source of on-island agriculture (30). Operational parameters of algae cultivators are controlled using controller (106). The controller is configured to be powered by battery array. The operational parameters comprise temperature, humidity, and pH. Responding to contamination in the algae cultivators are detected using a set of countermeasures triggered by multi-modes sensor array.
   USE - Method for capturing and recycling carbon dioxide from on-island combustion emissions using sustainable solar energized aquaculture of algae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables decreasing carbon emissions and overall lifecycle pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing and recycling carbon dioxide from on-island combustion emissions using sustainable solar energized aquaculture of algae involves generating electrical power from renewable power producing systems. The renewable power producing systems comprise solar photovoltaic cell and a water turbine (108). The electrical power in a battery array (110) is stored. Carbon dioxide from source of emissions are received. The carbon dioxide is input into algae cultivators (102). The algae cultivators are feed with nutrients derived from a waste source of on-island agriculture (30). Operational parameters of algae cultivators are controlled using controller (106). The controller is configured to be powered by battery array. The operational parameters comprise temperature, humidity, and pH. Responding to contamination in the algae cultivators are detected using a set of countermeasures triggered by multi-modes sensor array. The responding comprises a compartmentalization of a compromised subsection of the algae cultivators with direct application of biologic neutralizers.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system which has renewable power producing systems, where the renewable power producing systems comprise solar photovoltaic cell and water turbine; a battery array configured to store the electrical power produced by the renewable power producing systems; one or more algae cultivators; a waste source of on-island agriculture configured to provide nutrient for the one or more algae cultivators; a controller configured to monitor operational parameters of the algae cultivators, where the controller is configured to be powered by the battery array.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block logical and physical architecture view of capturing and recycling carbon dioxide from on-island combustion emissions using sustainable solar energized aquaculture of algae.
   Waste source of on-island agriculture (30)
   Algae cultivators (102)
   Controller (106)
   Water turbine (108)
   Battery array (110)

TI System useful for preparing biomass continuous hydrothermal furfural under carbon-dioxide atmosphere, comprises e.g. raw material conveyer includes valve-storage tank, coil pipe type reaction kettle, centrifuge and a discharging valve.
AU CHEN Y
   QU L
   YANG H
   SHAO J
   ZENG K
   LI J
   HU J
   WANG X
   CHEN H
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019394852
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-dioxide atmosphere biomass continuous hydrothermal furfural preparation system comprises a raw material conveyer (6), a coil pipe type reaction kettle (10) and a centrifuge (17). The raw material conveyor comprises a valve (4) and a storage tank (5). The valve is set above outer part of storage tank. The lower outlet of the storage tank is provided with a discharging valve. The discharging valve is fixedly connected by a bent pipe and a slurry conveyor. The slurry conveyer is connected to the coil pipe type reaction kettle equipped with a coil pipe type reactor whose outer part is sleeved with a heating sleeve. The coil pipe type reactor is set in the center of the carbon-dioxide pipe. The side end face of centrifuge is arranged in the system. The centrifuge main body is provided with an air exhaust port and an outlet. The bottom of centrifuge is provided with the solid-phase outlet. An air exhaust port and carbon-dioxide pipe joint achieves continuous hydrothermal reaction.
   USE - The system is useful for preparing biomass continuous hydrothermal furfural under carbon-dioxide atmosphere (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has easy operation procedure and promotes even heating in the continuous reaction, and effectively improves product quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing biomass continuous hydrothermal furfural in carbon-dioxide atmosphere using the system, comprising (a) feeding biomass feedstock and water through the feed valve into the raw material conveyor, uniformly mixing under the agitation of the stirring shaft , and feeding by discharge valve into the slurry conveyor; (b) conveying a set flow of biomass raw material and a water mixed slurry from discharge valve on the raw material conveyor into the coil reactor for hydrothermal reaction, providing heat required for the hydrothermal reaction by the heating jacket, and providing pressure required for the hydrothermal reaction by the carbon-dioxide conduit; (c) conveying hydrothermally reacted product into centrifuge after being cooled in the cooling pipe the tail, and conveying heat-exchanged water as a reactant from the feed valve into the raw material conveyor; and (d) separating the reaction product in the centrifuge, sequentially passing the separated gas product through the exhaust port, using carbon-dioxide pipe, and the flushing carbon-dioxide nozzle re-entering in the coil reactor for recycling, discharging separated liquid product from the liquid outlet which is rich in inorganic mineral elements and target furfural products, and is used for adding nutrient-rich solution and a furfural product, discharging the separated solid product from the solid phase outlet and subsequently preparing a high-quality carbon material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon-dioxide atmosphere biomass continuous hydrothermal furfural preparation system.

PN WO2022096147-A1
TI Breathing gas mixture separating method, involves separating carbon dioxide from breathing gas mixture by separating material, where separated carbon dioxide is temporarily released into environment or breathing gas scavenging system.
AU KREMEIER P
   SCHOBER A
AE LOEWENSTEIN MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY GMBH & CO (LOEW-Non-standard)
GA 202264754V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves guiding a breathing gas mixture under a flow to a separating material, where the material contains oxygen and carbon dioxide. The mixture is separated from a volatile anesthetic. The anesthetic is fed back to a respiratory gas path or remains in the respiratory gas system. The remaining mixture is fed under another flow to another separating material. The carbon dioxide is separated by the latter material, and is temporarily separated or released into the environment or into a breathing air scavenging system. A remaining portion of the mixture is returned to the former material, which is supplied.
   USE - The method is useful for separating a breathing gas mixture containing carbon dioxide and at least one volatile anesthetic and flows in the breathing gas path.
   ADVANTAGE - The method offers a cost-effective and practicable way of separating certain respiratory gases. It offers a regulation of the proportions of specific respiratory gas concentrations in a respiratory gas mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the device for separating a breathing gas mixture.
   Lower portion (13)
   Environment (24)
   Scavenging system (25)

PN US11305229-B1
TI Capturing and purifying carbon dioxide from exhaust gas stream for use in post-combustion coal-fired power plant, industrial boiler exhaust, and natural gas combustion exhaust involves providing carbon dioxide sorbent material, which is selected from lithium aluminate nanowire.
AU SUNKARA M
   NGUYEN T
   HE J
AE ADVANCED ENERGY MATERIALS LLC (ADEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022517039
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing and purifying carbon dioxide from exhaust gas stream involves providing a carbon dioxide (CO2) sorbent material, which is selected from lithium aluminate nanowire, calcium aluminate nanowire, sodium aluminate nanowire, potassium aluminate nanowire or its combination, packing the CO2 sorbent material in a column, heating the CO2 sorbent material to a predetermined temperature, allowing the exhaust gas stream to pass through the CO2 sorbent material in the column while maintaining the sorbent material temperature at the predetermined temperature, continuing the exhaust gas flow through the CO2 sorbent material until the CO2 sorbent material becomes a CO2 saturated adsorbent, venting the exhaust gas stream from the column, and regenerating the CO2 sorbent material by causing the CO2 saturated adsorbent to desorb.
   USE - Method for capturing and purifying carbon dioxide from exhaust gas stream for use in post-combustion coal-fired power plant, industrial boiler exhaust, and natural gas combustion exhaust.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has eco-friendly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - the drawing shows a scanning electron microscope image of the synthesized alumina nanowire.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of regenerating the CO2 sorbent material involves heating of the CO2 saturated adsorbent by exposing the CO2 saturated adsorbent to a dielectric barrier discharge plasma at frequencies ranging from 1 kilohertz - 30 megahertz. Preferred Conditions: The CO2 sorbent material is heating at a predetermined temperature of 15-300degrees Celsius. The desorption of the CO2 saturated adsorbent is achieved by exposing the CO2 saturated adsorbent to a dielectric barrier discharge plasma for about 30 seconds with humid air as carrier gas, and radio frequency (RF) plasma exposure for 3 minutes.

TI Preparing small-sized zinc oxide from carbon dioxide, comprises slurrying inorganic zinc salt and deionized water, adding compatible suspending agent, introducing carbon dioxide into slurry for carbonization treatment, washing zinc salt with deionized water, and calcining.
AU WANG H
   PAN H
AE JIANGYONG XIAOXIANG CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202238050D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing small-sized zinc oxide from carbon dioxide, comprises slurrying inorganic zinc salt and deionized water in a volume ratio of 1:5-10 to form a mixed slurry, and adding a compatible suspending agent, introducing carbon dioxide into the slurry for carbonization treatment for 2-6 hours under the conditions of set temperature and pressure to obtain reaction product zinc salt, washing the zinc salt with deionized water, and placing product in 60-70 degrees Celsius of baking ovens and drying for 8-10 hours, and placing into a muffle furnace for calcination for 1-4 hours to obtain nano-zinc oxide. The inorganic zinc salt is zinc oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing small-sized zinc oxide from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The zinc oxide nano-particle diameter ratio surface area obtained by the method is large, the dispersibility is good, the chemical activity is high, at the same time, the small size zinc oxide has excellent surface effect, small size effect and macroscopic quantum tunnel effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The preparation method of the compatible suspending agent utilizes 10-18 pts. wt. vinyl zinc bromide, 0.5-5 pts. wt. (R)-4-mercapto-2-pyrrolidone, 5-11 pts. wt. sodium dimercaptopropanesulfonate, 100-200 pts. wt. organic solvent, and 2-10 pts. wt. triethylamine. The organic solvent is dimethylformamide or chloroform.

PN CN113025832-A; CN113025832-B
TI Extracting nickel from laterite nickel ore and            simultaneously mineralizing carbon dioxide by grinding            laterite nickel ore, mixing ferrous sulfate            heptahydrate and additives, roasting, leaching solid            product, subjecting leachate to precipitation reaction,            adding aqueous ammonia and reacting.
AU LIU W
   CAO J
   WU H
   GAO Y
   JIANG X
   HE M
   LIU Q
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 202176907G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting nickel from laterite                nickel ore and simultaneously mineralizing carbon                dioxide involves (a) finely grinding laterite                nickel ore to less than 150 mu m, mixing ferrous                sulfate heptahydrate and additives and roasting to                obtain a solid product, (b) leaching the solid                product with water, separating leaching slurry from                solid and liquid to obtain a filter residue                (comprising ferric oxide and silica) and a leaching                solution comprising magnesium sulfate and nickel                sulfate, (c) subjecting the leachate to a                precipitation tank and adding sodium sulfide for                precipitation reaction to obtain a nickel sulfide                product and a precipitation mother liquor, and (d)                adding aqueous ammonia to the precipitation mother                liquor to raise the pH of the mother liquor and                controlling the temperature of the solution to pass                carbon dioxide, reacting to obtain a turbid liquid,                filtering to obtain magnesium carbonate                precipitation and realizing carbon dioxide                mineralization.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting nickel                from laterite nickel ore and simultaneously                mineralizing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additives comprise                  iron sulfide, sulfur, sodium sulfate, and/or                  potassium sulfate. Preferred Method: The step (a)                  involves mixing and roasting the materials at                  500-900 degrees C for 30-240 minutes. The step                  (b) involves leaching the solid product at 25-100                  degrees C for 10-180 minutes, where the                  liquid-solid mass ratio is (0.5-8):1. The step of                  precipitation reaction is carried out at 20-60                  degrees C, in which the molar ratio of sodium                  sulfide to nickel content in the leaching                  solution is (1-3):1. The step (d) involves adding                  aqueous ammonia to adjust the pH of the mother                  liquor to 9-12 at 20-60 degrees C, and reacting                  for 30-120 minutes. Preferred Composition: The                  mass ratio of laterite nickel ore to ferrous                  sulfate heptahydrate is 1:(1-8). The proportion                  of additives is 2.5%-25% of the total material                  mass.

TI System used for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, consists of solid oxide electrolysis cell to receive carbon dioxide and olefin and discharge carbon monoxide and olefin oxide, conduit which is provided to supply olefin to cell.
AU AL S W I
   AMR I T
   FADHEL B A
   HAMMAD A D
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202168333G
AB    NOVELTY - A system (100) consists of a solid oxide electrolysis cell (SOEC) (106) to receive carbon dioxide and an olefin (102) and discharge carbon monoxide and an olefin oxide (104) a conduit (a) to supply the olefin to the SOEC, a control valve (130) arranged along the conduit to modulate an amount of the olefin supplied to the SOEC, and a conduit (b) to supply the carbon dioxide to the SOEC.
   USE - System used for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide for producing oxide anions for upgrading feedstocks such as olefin feedstocks, for producing olefin oxide such as ethylene oxide, acidcyclohexene, propylene oxide, used as precursor for the production of higher value hydrocarbon, such as acrylic acid, alcohols, ethers and esters.
   ADVANTAGE - The system includes the material of the SOEC electrodes having excellent stability through reduction-oxidation (redox) cycles, electrocatalytic activity, and mixed ionic and electronic conductivity in reducing atmospheres.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system.
   System (100)
   Olefin (102)
   Olefin oxide (104)
   Solid oxide electrolysis cell (106)
   Cathode (108)
   Anode (110)
   Solid electrolyte (120)
   Power source (122)
   Control valve (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Components: The SOEC comprises a cathode (108) to electrochemically reduce the carbon dioxide and generate oxygen ions and the carbon monoxide, which is coupled to a power source to receive electric current to the cathode, a solid electrolyte (120) to diffuse the oxygen ions from the cathode to an anode (110), and the anode to oxidize, via the oxygen ions, the olefin into the olefin oxide. The system comprises a controller to modulate an amount of the electric current received at the cathode and a power source (122) to supply the electric current to the cathode. The power source comprises a battery. The cathode electrochemically reduces the carbon dioxide to the carbon monoxide via the electric current. The controller is provided to direct a set point of the control valve. The power source comprises a battery, a power generator, or an electrical grid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid electrolyte comprises a solid oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The system comprises a catalytic reactor to convert the olefin oxide into a hydrocarbon different than the olefin oxide. The catalytic reactor comprises an inlet to receive the olefin oxide. The conduit (a) is to discharge the olefin oxide to a catalytic reactor. The conduit (b) to discharge the carbon monoxide to the catalytic reactor. The feed conduit is to supply a flue gas comprising the carbon dioxide and water to the cathode side.

PN CN112619644-A
TI New ruthenium-based catalyst with tetragonal barium titanate as carrier, useful for catalytic carbon dioxide methanation reaction.
AU DAI W
   HE S
   CHEN X
   FU X
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202140311D
AB    NOVELTY - Ruthenium-based catalyst with tetragonal barium titanate as carrier, is new. The catalyst is supported by tetragonal barium titanate, ruthenium nanoparticles are highly dispersed supported catalysts composed of active components and the content of ruthenium nanoparticles in the catalyst is 1 wt.%.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalytic carbon dioxide methanation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to implement, improves the catalytic efficiency of carbon dioxide methanation, and lowers the reaction temperature, and is conducive to popularization and application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the ruthenium-based catalyst with tetragonal barium titanate as carrier, comprising preparing tetragonal barium titanate as carrier, and performing photoreduction method to load ruthenium nanoparticles on the obtained tetragonal barium titanate; and
   (2) use of ruthenium-based catalyst with tetragonal barium titanate as carrier in catalytic carbon dioxide methanation reaction, comprising taking the ruthenium-based catalyst with tetragonal barium titanate as carrier can promote the conversion of carbon dioxide to methane under the conditions of light and thermal catalysis, where the illumination uses 300 W xenon lamp and the temperature of the thermal catalysis is 200-300 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) adding the barium hydroxide solution dropwise into the water/ethanol solution of tetrabutyl titanate, performing hydrothermal reaction, water washing, drying and calcining, and preparing tetragonal barium titanate; and (ii) adding ruthenium chloride solution into the obtained tetragonal barium titanate, deionized water and anhydrous methanol, centrifuging with 300 W xenon lamp light for 30 minutes in nitrogen atmosphere, washing with deionized water, and drying at 80 degrees C. The temperature of the hydrothermal reaction in step (i) is 200 degrees C, and the time is 24 hours, the calcination temperature is 1000 degrees C, and the time is 2 hours. In the step (ii), adding 2 ml ruthenium chloride solution, 5 ml deionized water and 5 ml anhydrous methanol to every 1 g tetragonal barium titanate, the concentration of Ru4+ in the ruthenium chloride solution used is 5 mg/ml. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of barium hydroxide to tetrabutyl titanate used in step (i) is 1.4:1, and the volume ratio of water and ethanol in the water/ethanol solution is 1:1.

PN CN112076777-A; CN112076777-B
TI Photocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction,            comprises transition metal oxides, transition metal            carbides and graphitic carbon nitride.
AU LUO X
   LI Z
   CHEN M
   LIANG Z
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 2020C7843E
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction                comprises transition metal oxides, transition metal                carbides and graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4),                where the electrons on conduction band of                transition metal oxide and holes on valence band of                graphitic carbon nitride recombine on transition                metal carbide, so that the Z-type heterojunction                formed by the catalyst has a Z-type carrier                transport characteristics, and is prepared by (a)                dispersing graphitic carbon nitride powder into a                mixed solution of absolute ethanol and deionized                water, adding transition metal carbides to the                mixed solution, performing ultrasonic treatment and                stirring treatments to obtain the mixed solution,                and evaporating the mixed solution to dryness under                stirring conditions catalyst precursor, and (b)                grinding the catalyst precursor into powder and                placing in a reaction furnace for high-temperature                calcination while maintaining the oxygen atmosphere                in the reaction furnace to finally obtain the                photocatalyst.
   USE - As photocatalyst for carbon dioxide                reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst improves the efficiency and                stability of photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction, increases the photocatalytic activity,                is beneficial to the reuse of active sites and                enhances the stability of the photocatalyst, has a                excellent application prospect in the of                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, and the                preparation method and operation are simple,                economical and is suitable for large-scale                production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal                  of the transition metal carbide comprises                  titanium, zirconium, tantalum, vanadium, hafnium,                  niobium, chromium, scandium and other metals. The                  reducing agent used in the reduction reaction                  comprises methane, water or hydrogen. Preferred                  Method: The transition metal oxide is prepared by                  oxidizing transition metal carbides under                  high-temperature calcination conditions, and                  adjusting the degree of oxidation of the                  transition metal carbides by controlling the                  calcination temperature and time. Preferred                  Composition: In step (i), the volume ratio of the                  absolute ethanol to deionized water is 0-4. In                  step (i), the mass ratio of the transition metal                  carbide and graphitic carbon nitride is 1:5-1:20.                  In step (ii), the oxygen atmosphere comprises                  oxygen or air, and the volume concentration of                  oxygen is 0-100%. The volume ratio of the carbon                  dioxide to the reducing agent is 1-10. Preferred                  Conditions: In step (i), the ultrasonic and                  stirring time is 0.5-2 hours, and the stirring                  rate is 500-1200 rpm. In step (ii), the                  calcination temperature is 300-600 degrees C, and                  the calcination time is 2-5 hours.

PN KR2020076094-A
TI Producing hydrogen and dimethylether from syngas involves using carbon dioxide (CO2), separating hydrogen by permeating a mixed gas containing hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), and passing carbon dioxide (CO2) through a separator.
AU GI M
   KWON O
   LEE Y C
   YEOUL L J
   LEE J H
AE KOREA GAS CORP (KOAS-C)
GA 202060647E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen and dimethylether from syngas involves using carbon dioxide (CO2), separating hydrogen by permeating a mixed gas containing hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), and passing carbon dioxide (CO2) through a separator. The hydrogen is separated and dimethyl ether is synthesized from the remaining mixed gas to obtain hydrogen and dimethylether.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen and dimethylether from syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrogen and dimethylether from syngas that reduces risk of explosion, and improves corrosion resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a production apparatus for H2 and dimethyl ether, which involves SCR (steam carbon dioxide reformer) for H2 and CO synthesis, separation membrane through which a mixed gas containing H2, CO, and impurity CO2 synthesized in the SCR is permeated, pressure swing adsorption (PSA) device for recovering high purity H2 from the mixed gas passing through the separator, dimethyl ether synthesizer for synthesizing dimethyl ether from the mixed gas remaining after H2 is separated through the separator, dimethyl ether separator for recovering the synthesized dimethyl ether, and CO2 recoverer for recovering CO2 from the PSA apparatus and a dimethyl ether separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The H2 and CO generated through a reforming reaction (steam carbondioxide reformer) is used as natural gas, steam and CO2 are by-product. The temperature of the SCR is 700-800 degrees C, and the pressure is 20-22 bar. The ratio of H2 and CO produced from the SCR (H2/CO) is 1.8-2.2, and amount of CO2 is 12-18%. The H2 content of the gas permeated through the separator is 70-80%. The H2 is separated and the ratio of H2 to CO (H2/CO) in the remaining gas is 1.0-1.2. The synthesis of dimethyl ether is carried out at 230-280 degrees C and a pressure of 40-50 bar. The H2 separated is returned to high purity H2 through a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) process. Preferred Components: The separation membrane is made of one polymer material selected from polysulfone (polysulfone, PS), polyethersulfone and polyimide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110436446-A
TI Preparing graphene comprises reacting mixture of porous iron oxide and iron with carbon dioxide at high temperature.
AU ZHANG X
AE ZHANG X (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201996866F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene comprises reacting a mixture of porous iron oxide and iron with carbon dioxide at a high temperature, where the graphene has a specific surface area of 1800-2000 m2/g, and a conductivity of 6000-8000 S/m.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and excellent, and has excellent reproducibility and easy application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing graphene, characterized in that the method for preparing the graphene comprises (i) uniformly mixing metal iron powder and porous iron oxide into a container to obtain a mixture, (ii) feeding a container with argon gas, heating the container containing the mixture to 1000-1200 degrees C at a heating rate of 16-20 degrees C/minute, and when the temperature in the container reaches 1200 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide gas, (iii) holding the container for introducing the carbon dioxide gas for 90-120 minutes, stopping the introduction of the carbon dioxide gas, continuously passing argon gas, and cooling the container to room temperature to obtain a black powder, and (iv) adding an acid solution to the black powder, sufficiently reacting, then washing with deionized water to be neutral, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The porous iron oxide is prepared by introducing iron sulfate hexahydrate into argon gas at a flow rate of 25 ml/minute while heating iron sulfate hexahydrate to 800 degrees C at a heating rate of 10 degrees C/minute for 30 minutes. The iron sulfate hexahydrate is mixed with 2 mol/l sodium sulfate solution and 2 mol/l ferric chloride solution at a mass ratio of 1:1, then washing with ethanol for 3-5 times, and drying in a vacuum atmosphere at 40-50 degrees C. In the step (i), the mixture of the metal iron powder and the porous iron oxide is prepared by grinding metal iron powder having a mass ratio of 1:6-7 and porous iron oxide in a mortar for 50-60 minutes. In the step (ii), the flow rate of argon gas into the container is 130-150 ml/minute, and after the temperature reaches the highest value, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 150-170 ml/minute, and the flow rate of argon gas is reduced to 80-100 ml/minute. In the step (iv), the drying is carried out in a vacuum drying oven at 50-60 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the acid added to the black powder is 3-5 mol/l sulfuric acid.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PN CN114632406-A
TI Preparing supported carbon dioxide solid adsorbent            involves using high-temperature air as the fluidizing            medium, circulating and fluidizing the porous carrier            particles in the fluidized bed, atomizing active            component solution, and spraying into the fluidized            bed.
AU LIANG C
   PAN S
   BAO X
   LIU D
   CHEN X
   ZHI Y
   MA J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2022873987
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supported carbon dioxide solid                adsorbent involves using high-temperature air as                the fluidizing medium, circulating and fluidizing                the porous carrier particles in the fluidized bed.                The active component solution is atomized, and                sprayed into the fluidized bed from the bottom of                the bed, and immersed the atomized droplets in the                pores of the porous carrier particles. The                high-temperature air acts as a drying medium at the                same time to evaporate the active component                droplets immersed in the pores of the porous                carrier, and the active component is loaded on the                pore walls of the porous carrier particles. The                loaded particles are calcined to obtain a supported                carbon dioxide solid adsorbent.
   USE - Method for preparing supported carbon dioxide                solid adsorbent
   ADVANTAGE - The method makes the carbon dioxide react more                sufficiently with the active components during the                diffusion process in the pore canals, thus                increasing the adsorption capacity, and reducing                the energy consumption. The method controls the                evaporation rate of the active component liquid                drop in the carrier channel by adjusting the                intensity dry so as to control the load depth in                the hole.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                preparation device of the loaded carbon dioxide                solid adsorbent, which includes fluidized bed body,                where a flow guide pipe is arranged in the                fluidized bed body, is coaxially arranged with the                central shaft of the fluidized bed body and is                close to an air distribution plate at the bottom of                the fluidized bed body.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous support                  particles are gamma alumina. The active component                  solution is a mixed solution of sodium carbonate                  and potassium carbonate.

   n = 5-400;
   R1 = organic groups, including propane-2,2-diylbis(cyclohexane-4,1-diyl), aniline-N,N-diyl, methane moiety of formula (VI-VII), or benzene moiety of formula (VIII);
   R2-R4 = organic groups, preferably oxybis(benzene-4-yl), thiobis(benzene-4-yl), 4,4'-(dimethylsilanediyl)diphenyl, carbonylbis(benzene-4-yl), sulfonylbis(benzene-4-yl), or N1-(4-aminophenyl)-N1-phenylbenzene-1,4-diyl;

   X = O or S.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The organic solvent includes tetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane, 1,2 dichloroethane, chloroform, 1,4 dioxane, dimethyl sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylformamide and N,N dimethylformamide, and/or N,N-dimethylacetamide; the carbon dioxide atmosphere is normal pressure carbon dioxide atmosphere; and the purification process comprises dissolving polymerized product in organic solvent, filtering, then adding filtrate into methanol, n hexane or diethyl ether for precipitation, and collecting the precipitate; and the drying is vacuum drying to constant weight. The polymerization reaction temperature is 50-180degrees Celsius; and the polymerization reaction time is 0.25-72 hours.Preferred Components: The molar ratio between bi functional o alkynyl aniline monomer, and bi functional aryl iodide monomer is 0.5-1.8:1. The concentration of bifunctional o alkynyl aniline monomer in the organic solvent is 0.05-1 mol/l. The catalyst includes palladium acetate, palladium chloride, tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0), bis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(II) chloride, bis(acetylacetonato)palladium(II), (1,1' bis(diphenylphosphino)ferrocene)palladium(II) dichloride, and/or tris(dibenzylideneacetone)dipalladium(0); and the dosage of the catalyst is 1-60 mol% of the bifunctional o-alkynyl aniline monomer. The base includes cesium carbonate, cesium fluoride, triphenylphosphine, sodium tert-butoxide, 1,8 diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec 7 ene and/or triethylenediamine; and the dosage of base is 20-500 mol% of the bifunctional oalkynyl aniline monomer.

PN CN111885835-A; CN111885835-B
TI Carbon dioxide laser based printed circuit board manufacturing method, involves forming center of adjacent etching hole, and obtaining adjacent ring etching step preset distance to form continuous etching hole to obtain cavity.
AU SONG J
   XU T
   FENG H
   LIU J
   JIANG Y
   CHEN X
   DU W
AE SHANGHAI MEADVILLE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2067S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a substrate prepared with multiple metal lines. A substrate is exposed between the metal lines and a to-be-grooved region. A to-be-grooved area is etched using Carbon dioxide laser to form a cavity. The first ring etching is performed along contour of the cavity, where the first ring etching comprises Carbon dioxide laser drilling is performed along the contour of the cavity to form first etching holes. A center of the adjacent etching hole is formed at preset distance in the etching process from the first ring to last ring. An adjacent ring etching step preset distance is obtained to form a continuous etching hole to obtain the cavity.
   USE - Carbon dioxide laser based printed circuit board manufacturing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves productivity and working efficiency, and reduces production cost, and realizes small etching hole distance, and ensures smoothness of the cutting surface, has better heat dissipation time so as to prevent the product from burning coke in the cutting process, and prevents dust generated in the laser processing process from polluting the other positions of the product by the secondary protection of the sacrificial film layer, and can effectively ensure uniformity of the green oil in the groove.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide laser based printed circuit board manufacturing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a carbon dioxide laser based printed circuit board manufacturing method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V04 (Printed Circuits and Connectors); V08 (Lasers and Masers)

PN CN211734302-U
TI Essential oil production line comprises a carbon dioxide storage tank, a high pressure pump, an extracting kettle, a separating tank and a controller, where the top of the extracting kettle is provided with a feeding port.
AU YU H
   WANG L
AE MOYAN BIOTECHNOLOGY ZHONGSHAN CO LTD (MOYA-Non-standard)
GA 2020A7001B
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an essential oil production line, belonging to the technical field of essential oil production equipment. It comprises a carbon dioxide storing tank, an extracting kettle, a separating tank and a controller; the top of the extracting kettle is provided with a feeding end cover; the bottom is provided with a discharging end cover; the extracting kettle is provided with an air inlet pipe and a liquid level sensor; the air inlet pipe is connected with the high pressure pump; the high pressure pump is connected with the carbon dioxide storage tank through the pipeline; the kettle wall of the extracting kettle is provided with an electric heating wire I; the extracting kettle is provided with a pressure sensor and a temperature sensor; the extracting kettle is connected with the separating tank through the discharging pipe; the tank wall of the separating tank is provided with an electric heating wire II; the separating tank is provided with a heat conducting plate; the separating tank, the carbon dioxide compressor and the carbon dioxide storage tank are connected in turn. The utility model can automatically finish the extraction work of essential oil, with less manual operation, reducing the labour intensity of the staff; it can monitor the temperature and pressure change condition in the extraction kettle in real time and ensure the material extraction rate; the carbon dioxide is used circularly to reduce the environment pollution.

PN CN111505050-A; CN213181340-U
TI Method for detecting biomass heat share in coal-fired coupled biomass combustion, involves inputting heat share of biomass into boiler during coupled combustion of coal and biomass is obtained, and burning only coal in boiler.
AU YUAN J
   ZHANG H
   LI J
   ZHOU N
   FU J
   DAI B
   WU S
   YOU S
   ZHANG Q
   BAO W
AE STATE POWER INVESTMENT CORP POWER STATIO (POWE-Non-standard)
GA 2020770987
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves burning only coal in the boiler, and measuring the oxygen content and carbon dioxide content in the flue gas at the tail of the boiler. The biomass is burned in the boiler and the oxygen content and carbon dioxide content in the flue gas are measured at the tail of the boiler. The coal and biomass are combined in the boiler, and the oxygen content and carbon dioxide content are measured in the flue gas at the tail of the boiler. The carbon dioxide content in the flue gas based on the standard oxygen content when only coal is burned in the boiler is recorded. The carbon dioxide content in the flue gas based on the standard oxygen content when only biomass is burned in the boiler is recorded. The carbon dioxide content in the flue gas based on the standard oxygen content when coal and biomass are burned in the boiler at the same time is recorded. The heat share of the biomass is inputted into the boiler during the coupled combustion of coal and biomass is obtained.
   USE - Method for detecting biomass heat share in coal-fired coupled biomass combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is relatively simple, and relatively accurately obtains the biomass heat share in coal-fired coupled biomass combustion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for detecting biomass heat fraction in combustion of coal-fired coupling biomass.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for detecting the biomass heat share. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN KR2020045111-A; KR2181993-B1
TI Zeolite for separating carbon dioxide, and for the production of methylamine, has a composition comprises a linear structure, and zeolite is characterized by powder X-ray diffraction.
AU HONGSERKBONG
   LEE H
   HO S J
AE POSTECH ACAD-IND FOUND (UYPO-C)
GA 202038626S
AB    NOVELTY - PST-29 zeolite has a composition comprises a linear structure of 0.1-10 M2O: 1.0 aluminum oxide (Al2O3): 1.0-100 silicon oxide (SiO2), where M is one or more selected from monovalent metal elements. The zeolite is characterized by powder X-ray diffraction, and theta , d, and I are Bragg angles, grid spacing, and the intensity of the X-ray diffraction peak, and all powder X-ray diffraction data including this powder X-ray diffraction pattern were measured using a standard X-ray diffraction method, and the radiation source is copper k alpha ray and 40 kilovolt. An X-ray tube operating at 30 milliampere is used, and measured at a rate of 5 degrees per minute (2 theta ) from a horizontally compressed powder sample. The D and I are calculated from the 2 theta value and peak height of the observed X-ray diffraction peak, where 100 I/I0 is W (weak: 0-20), M (middle: 20-40), S (strong: 40-60), VS (very strong: 60-100).
   USE - PST-29 zeolite used for separating carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide adsorbent and for PST-29 catalyst for the production of methylamine (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The PST-29 zeolite having skeletal structure of approximately 25 angstrom or more is produced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method of separating carbon dioxide which involves contacting a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide with PST-29 zeolite to selectively adsorb carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a method for producing methylamine involves reacting ammonia with methanol, where dehydrated PST-29 zeolite is used as a catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The PST-29 zeolite is a ratio of aluminum oxide (Al2O3) and silicon oxide (SiO2) in a ratio of 1:2-45. The PST-29 zeolite belongs to the space group of Im-3m of the cubic crystal system, and the axial unit cell lengths a, b, and c are all 20 angstrom or more. The crystal axis unit cell lengths a, b, and c are 25 mm 2 angstrom. The PST-29 zeolite comprises 1-20 wt.% water, and 1-20 wt.% of an organic structure-inducing molecule. The ratio is 0.5-7.0 M2O: 1.0 Al2O3: 1-25 SiO2. The PST-29 zeolite is a sodium ions ion exchanged zeolite after calcination at 500 degrees C or higher. Preferred Components: The monovalent metal is selected from one or more groups consisting of lithium (Li), sodium (Na), potassium (K), and cesium (Cs). The organic structure-inducing material is Me2-1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane (DABCO) ion.The PST-29 zeolite is sodium ion (Na+) ion-exchanged zeolite.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
